PT P
PN CN110335765-A; CN110335765-B
TI Method for preparing graphene quantum dot reinforced metal oxide supercapacitor electrode material, involves ultrasonically cleaning current collector material with hydrochloric acid solution, acetone and absolute ethanol.
AU QI J
   JIA H
   CAI Y
   WANG Z
   FENG J
   FEI W
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 2019867096
AB    NOVELTY - A graphene quantum dot reinforced metal oxide supercapacitor electrode material preparing method involves ultrasonically cleaning the current collector material with hydrochloric acid solution, acetone and absolute ethanol to obtain the collected current collector material and hydrothermally depositing nickel oxide, cobalt trioxide or molybdenum trioxide on the surface of the current collector material after cleaning to obtain a metal oxide current collector material. The metal oxide current collector material is placed in a plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition vacuum device and hydrogen gas is introduced after vacuuming. Carbon dioxide gas is passed to the vacuum device, the carbon dioxide gas and argon gas are stopped and the plasma chemical vapor deposition is performed. The vacuum apparatus is evacuated and cooled to room temperature under vacuum to obtain a graphene quantum dot reinforced metal oxide supercapacitor electrode material.
   USE - Method for preparing graphene quantum dot reinforced metal oxide supercapacitor electrode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables solving the problem of lower electrical conductivity and lower specific surface area of metal oxides in existing supercapacitors, which resulting in deterioration of performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A graphene quantum dot reinforced metal oxide supercapacitor electrode material preparing method involves ultrasonically cleaning the current collector material with hydrochloric acid solution, acetone and absolute ethanol for 1-5 minutes to obtain the collected current collector material, where the concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 1-3 mol/liter, and hydrothermally depositing nickel oxide, cobalt trioxide or molybdenum trioxide on the surface of the current collector material after cleaning to obtain a metal oxide current collector material. The metal oxide current collector material is placed in a plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition vacuum device and hydrogen gas is introduced after vacuuming that the hydrogen gas flow rate is adjusted to 10-100 sccm, and argon gas is introduced and the argon gas flow rate is adjusted to 10-100sccm, where the plasma pressure chemical vapor deposition vacuum device has a pressure of 100-1000Pa and the temperature is raised to 200-1000 degrees C. Carbon dioxide gas is passed to the vacuum device and adjusted the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas to 1-100sccm, adjusted the pressure in the plasma chemical vapor deposition vacuum device to 100-1000Pa under the condition of RF power of 20-500W, the temperature of 200-1000 degrees C and the radio frequency of 1-20 min, and the power is turned off after the end of the radio frequency, the carbon dioxide gas and argon gas are stopped and the plasma chemical vapor deposition is performed. The vacuum apparatus is evacuated and cooled to room temperature under vacuum to obtain a graphene quantum dot reinforced metal oxide supercapacitor electrode material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The current collector material is copper foil, nickel foil, copper foam or nickel foam. Preferred Method: The nickel oxide is deposited on the surface of the collected current collector material by mixing nickel chloride hexahydrate, L-valine, ammonia water with a mass percentage of 10-20%, and deionized water and ultrasonically dissolving to obtain a mixed solution A, immersing the cleaned collector material in the mixed solution A at a temperature of 150-200 degrees C for 5-12 hours, naturally cooling the resulting material to room temperature and collecting solid samples, alternately washing the samples with distilled water and absolute ethanol at a temperature of 60-80 degrees C, drying the washed sample for 10-20 hours, and heat treating the dried sample at a temperature of 300-400 degrees C in an air atmosphere for 1-4 hours to obtain a metal oxide current collector material, where the volume ratio of the mass of the nickel chloride hexahydrate to the deionized water is (0.5-5)g:100mL, the volume ratio of the mass of L-valine to deionized water is (0.-0.9)g:100mL and the volume ratio of the ammonia water to the deionized water in the mass percentage of 10-20% is (1-10):100. The cobalt trioxide is deposited on the surface of the collected current collector material by mixing cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, urea, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide and deionized water and sonicating for 1-2 hours to obtain a mixed solution B, immersing the cleaned collector material in the mixed solution B at a temperature of 100-160 degrees C for 6-12 hours, naturally cooling the resulting material to room temperature and collecting solid samples, alternately washing the samples with distilled water and absolute ethanol at a temperature of 60-80 degrees C, drying the washed sample for 10-20 hours, and heat treating the dried sample at a temperature of 350-600 degrees C in an air atmosphere for 2-4 hours to obtain a metal oxide current collector material, where the volume ratio of the mass of the cobalt nitrate hexahydrate to the deionized water is (1-10)g:100mL, the volume ratio of the urea to the deionized water is (0.5-9.5)g:100mL and the volume ratio of the mass of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to deionized water is (0.01-0.5)g:100 mL. The molybdenum trioxide is deposited on the surface of the collected current collector material by mixing ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate, chromium(III) chloride hexahydrate, nitric acid with a mass percentage of 10-65% and deionized water to obtain a mixed solution C, immersing the cleaned collector material in the mixed solution C at a temperature of 150-200 degrees C for 12-36 hours, naturally cooling the resulting material to room temperature and collecting solid samples, alternately washing the samples with distilled water and absolute ethanol at a temperature of 60-80 degrees C and drying the washed sample for 8-16 hours to obtain a metal oxide current collector material, where the volume ratio of the mass of ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate to deionized water is (5-50)g:100mL, the volume ratio of the mass of chromium(III) chloride hexahydrate to deionized water is (0.5-5)g:100mL and the volume ratio of the nitric acid solution to the deionized water is (1-50):100.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)
MC L03-B03G1; L03-B03G2; L03-H04D

GA 202136196P
AB    NOVELTY - A dry water-type material comprises 3-6 pts. wt. hydrophobic nanomaterial and 94-97 pts. wt. inorganic solution.
   USE - Dry water-type material used for preventing explosive dust and explosive harmful gases from blasting.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry water-type material has high capture and storage efficiency for carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and other gases, and can effectively release coated solution to adsorb and absorb the explosive dust and harmful gases.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrophobic nanomaterial is one or more of nano-silicon carbide, nano-aluminum nitride, nano-boron nitride, nano-silicon nitride, and nano-silicon dioxide. The inorganic solution is ferric chloride solution.

PT P
PN KR2032949-B1
TI Producing carbonate, comprises precipitating and separating calcium sulfate by mixing sodium sulfate with industrial wastewater, reacting calcium sulfate with aqueous solution of alkanolamine, separating and purifying.
AU PARK J W
   WOO K
   YOO Y
   KIM I
   CHOI E J
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 201987909U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbonate, comprises (a) precipitating and separating calcium sulfate by mixing sodium sulfate with industrial wastewater generated in salt purification process, (b) reacting the calcium sulfate with aqueous solution of alkanolamine absorbed with carbon dioxide, and (c) separating and purifying the carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be produced high purity carbonate from industrial wastewater generated in the salt purification process, and improves yield of carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises adding sodium sulfate into industrial wastewater, stirring for 1-48 hours to precipitate calcium sulfate, filtering and washing the precipitated calcium sulfate with polar solvent to remove impurities. The step (c) comprises filtering the product of step (b) to separate the carbonate, and purifying the carbonate by washing with polar solvent. The method comprises adding the industrial filtrate and sodium hydroxide generated in the salt refining process into first filtrate in which the carbonate is separated in step (c), separating the obtained product into a precipitate and a second filtrate, and reacting the second filtrate with carbon dioxide to obtain a carbonate. The method comprises adding sodium hydroxide until the pH of the first filtrate is 10-11. Preferred Components: The reaction of step (b) has conversion rate of greater than or equal to 30%. The carbon dioxide is gaseous carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide absorption rate of the second filtrate is greater than or equal to 50% of the carbon dioxide absorption rate of the aqueous alkanolamine solution. The purity of the carbonate is greater than or equal to 90%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

TI Composition used for preparing cigarette, electronic cigarette or brewed beverage, comprises oolong tea and lemon.
AU ZHAN Q
AE FUJIAN LVSEHUANGJIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201979716K
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises oolong tea and lemon.
   USE - The composition is used for preparing cigarette, electronic cigarette or brewed beverage (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides excellent taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the composition, comprising (a) mixing oolong tea and water, leaching, filtering, collecting the filtrate, concentrating, spray-drying, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method, and preparing an oolong tea extract, (b) crushing the lemon, and soaking to obtain a lemon extract, and (c) mixing the oolong tea extract and lemon extract to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 70-99 parts mass oolong tea and 1-30 parts mass lemon. The mass ratio of oolong tea and water in step (a) is 1:(10-20). The concentration ratio of (15 degrees C) in step (a) is 1:(1.2-1.3). Preferred Conditions: The step (a) involves extracting at 51-64 degrees C and 12-15 MPa for 9-12 hours using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method, and spray-drying at 200-230 rpm for 1-2 hours, where the inlet temperature is 90-95 degrees C and the outlet temperature is 45-55 degrees C. The step (b) involves soaking at 80-100 degrees C for 40-90 minutes.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The content of cannabidiol in the industrial hemp flower leaves extract is more than 50%. The composition comprises injection, tablet, granule, oral preparation, dropping pill and so on. The injection is small, powder injection, glucose injection, one of the sodium chloride infusion. The medical accessories include diluent, osmotic pressure regulator, the active component in the industrial hemp flower leaves extract contains 30-150mg/bottle and the rhizoma gastrodiae extract 10-60mg/bottle. PN CN109954449-A
TI New amidine derivative used as surfactant, prepared by subjecting N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal and alkylamine to amide condensation reaction, recrystallizing, dissolving, adding water and introducing excess carbon dioxide.
AU YE Z
   WANG Y
   YANG R
   DONG Z
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2019601510
AB    NOVELTY - Amidine derivative (I) is new.
   USE - The amidine derivative is used as surfactant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product effectively reduces surface tension of the solution; and can be applied in petroleum exploitation, crude oil recovery and emulsion polymerization. The method is mild and has reaction yield of 80%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amidine derivative of formula (I) is new.
   R=octyl or decyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of amidine derivative.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Amidine derivative is prepared by subjecting N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal of formula (Ia) and alkylamine of formula (Ib) to amide condensation reaction, recrystallizing to prepare acetamidine derivative of formula (Id), dissolving acetamidine derivative (Id) in solvent g, adding water and introducing excess carbon dioxide or adding excess dry ice to form target product by salt formation reaction.R=octyl, decyl or dodecyl.Preferred Method: The amide condensation reaction is carried out in the presence of tetrahydropyrrole. The amide condensation reaction is done at 70-90 degrees C for 2-6 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar amount of tetrahydropyrrole is 0.02-0.05 equivalents of N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal. The molar ratio of N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal (Ia) and alkylamine (Ib) is 1-1.5:2-3. The volume of dropwise addition of water is 1% of the volume of solvent g. Preferred Components: The solvent of amide condensation reaction system is methanol, chloroform, tetrahydrofuran or acetone. The solvent used for recrystallization is acetonitrile or acetone. The solvent g is anhydrous diethyl ether.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J02 (Mixing and including dispersing (B01F).)

TI Composting for reducing greenhouse gas emissions, comprises (i) adding alkali lignin into compost material; and (ii) composting the compost material.
AU LIU X
   TANG J
   YU Z
   WANG Y
   WEN P
   ZHOU S
AE GUANGDONG ENVIRONMENT TECH INST (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201939387F
AB    NOVELTY - Composting for reducing greenhouse gas emissions, comprises (i) adding alkali lignin into compost material; and (ii) composting the compost material.
   USE - The composting method is useful for reducing greenhouse gas emission (claimed). The compost product is useful as organic fertilizer, nutritious soil composition and soil conditioning agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces emission of ammonia gas and greenhouse gases (carbon dioxide, methane and nitrous oxide), realizes deodorization effect, is simple and environmentally friendly, and utilizes alkali lignin as raw material which is widely distributed and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the composting is aerobic fermentation, where the aeration amount is 3-6 m3/tx hour. The compost material is turned for every 2-7 days during composting. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of alkali lignin to compost material is 0.01-0.1:1. The alkali lignin has carbon to nitrogen ratio of 200-350:1 and water content of 1-7%. The compost material has carbon to nitrogen ratio of 25-35:1 and water content of 55-65%.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. TI Producing organic zinc catalyst useful to produce polyalkylene carbonate resin, comprises reacting zinc precursor with dicarboxylic acid under liquid medium containing carbon nitride to form zinc decarboxylate catalyst.
AU BANG Y J
   HWANG G H
   BANG J U
   SONG C O
   PARK S Y
   KIM S K
   LEE J W
   BANG Y
   BANG J
   SONG C
   PARK S
   KIM S
   LEE J
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 201836050V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing organic zinc catalyst, comprises reacting zinc precursor with dicarboxylic acid having 3-20 carbon atoms under liquid medium containing carbon nitride to form zinc decarboxylate catalyst.
   USE - The organic zinc catalyst is useful to produce polyalkylene carbonate resin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organic zinc catalyst has excellent catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing polyalkylene carbonate resin comprising polymerizing monomer containing epoxide and carbon dioxide in presence of the organic zinc catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction is carried out by adding 0.5-100 pts. wt. carbon nitride based on the weight of the zinc precursor at 20-110 degrees C for 1-20 hours. Preferred Components: The zinc precursor includes zinc oxide, zinc nitrate, bis(2,4-pentanedionato)zinc, zinc hydroxide, zinc acetate, zinc sulfate and/or zinc chlorate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid medium is solvent including toluene, benzene, xylene, diethyl ether, tetrahydrofuran, 1,4-dioxane, dimethyl carbonate and/or diethyl carbonate. The dicarboxylic acid includes f malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid and/or pimelic acid. The dicarboxylic acid is used in amount of 0.8-2 moles relative to 1 mole of zinc precursor.
   EXAMPLE - 5.30 g Glutaric acid was dispersed in 100 ml toluene and stirred at 55 degrees C for 30 minutes. 0.098 g Carbon nitride CN was added into dispersion solution and mixture was stirred for 30 minutes. 2.72 g Zinc oxide was further added to the dispersion and stirred for 3 hours, obtained solid dispersion was filtered to obtain solid powder, washed for three times with 150 m acetone, and washed solid powder was dried in convection oven at 80 degrees C for 15 hours to finally obtain organic zinc catalyst to which carbon nitride was added.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)

GA 2018234438
AB    NOVELTY - Toner comprises toner base particles comprising binder resin, colorant, and releasing agent, and an external additive for adhering surface of the toner base particle and including quantum dot-inorganic particle composite.
   USE - The toner is useful for electrophotography (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The toner reduces energy consumption and carbon dioxide emissions, and effectively displays discrimination without being restricted by compatibility between labeled substance for discriminating between non-genuine toner and genuine toner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quantum dot-inorganic particle composite: has dispersed form in which quantum dot particles are located between inorganic particles, or dispersed form in which inorganic particles are dispersed between quantum dot particles; is inorganic particle surface-treated with quantum dot, or inorganic particle having quantum dot embedded in inorganic particle. The toner comprises 0.5-2 pts. wt. quantum dot-inorganic particle composite and 100 pts. wt. toner base particles. Average particle size of the inorganic particles in the quantum dot-inorganic particle composite is less than or equal to 200 nm. The quantum dots: are free of lead, mercury, cadmium and chromium; has emission wavelength of 450-750 nm; and is doped with at least one transition metal. The external additive further comprises inorganic particle which is not quantum dot-inorganic particle composite.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107884494-A
TI On-line analyzing of methanol ammoxidation products by gas chromatography comprises e.g. establishing an analysis system connected to the loop 1 between the interface 1 and the interface 8 of the ten-way purge valve V1.
AU JU Y
   TONG M
   PAN R
   LI C
   LI J
   LI Z
AE YANGQUAN COAL IND GROUP CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 201828261M
AB    NOVELTY - On-line analyzing of methanol ammoxidation products by gas chromatography comprises e.g. establishing an analysis system connected to the loop 1 between the interface 1 and the interface 8 of the ten-way purge valve V1, connecting the pre-separation column PC-1 between its interface 2 and interface 6, and connecting the damping pipe CC-1 to its interface 3; (ii) maintaining the ten-way purge valve V1 and the six-way purge valve V2 in a single state before sampling; and (iii) maintaining the ten-pass purge valve V1 initially in the single-line state during the analysis.
   USE - The method is useful for on-line analyzing of methanol ammoxidation products by gas chromatography.
   ADVANTAGE - The relative error of the lowest content is less than 3% and the standard deviation is within the allowable range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - On-line analyzing of methanol ammoxidation products by gas chromatography comprises (i) establishing an analysis system connected to the loop 1 between the interface 1 and the interface 8 of the ten-way purge valve V1, connecting the pre-separation column PC-1 between its interface 2 and interface 6, and connecting the damping pipe CC-1 to its interface 3, where the interface 4 is connected between the hydrogen automatic pressure controller APC-1 through the buffer column DC-1, where the interface 5 of the interface 5 and the six-way purge valve V2 are connected with the packed column MC-1 and its interface 7 is connected with the hydrogen automatic pressure control, where the interface 6 of the APC-1 to its interface 9 is connected with the six-way purge valve V3, and the interface 10 serves as a sample inlet, where the molecular sieve column MC-2 interface is connected between the interface 2 and the interface 3 of the six-way purge valve V2, where the interface 2 and 3 is connected to the molecular sieve column MC-2; the interface 3 is connected with the thermal conductivity detector TCD1 of the L port; the interface 5 and interface 6 are provided between the damping CC-2; the quantitative loop L2 is provided between the interface 1 and the interface 4 of the six-way purge valve V2, and an automatic pressure controller APC-3 is connected to the interface 2 of the six-way purge valve V2, and the interface 3 is connected to the L port of the thermal conductivity detector TCD2 through the packed column MC-3, where the interface 5 is used as the sample outlet; the R port of the thermal conductivity detector TCD1 and the thermal conductivity detector TCD2 are connected to the hydrogen automatic pressure controller APC-2 through the damping tube CC-3 and the damping tube CC-4 respectively, and the thermal conductivity detector TCD2 L is connected to the hydrogen flame ionization detector FID-1; (ii) maintaining the ten-way purge valve V1 and the six-way purge valve V2 in a single state before sampling, where the product gas sample comprises ten valve V1 through interface 10 into the interface 1, which flows into a dosing ring L1, then flows out from the interface 8 into the interface 9, then enters the interface 6 of the six-way purge valve V2, then flows out of the interface 1 into the quantitative loop L1, and then enters the interface 5 from the interface 4 and flows out; and (iii) maintaining the ten-pass purge valve V1 initially in the single-line state during the analysis, and then switching the ten-way purge valve V1 to the double-line state for entering the gas sample in the loop L1 to the analysis system, then passing the sample to the pre-separation column, pre-separating the PC-1 into the packed column MC-1 and reversely blowing the components with carbon dioxide, when carbon dioxide enters the packed column MC-1, initially maintaining the purge valve V2 in the single-line state, when the carbon monoxide flows out of the packed column MC-1 and carbon dioxide has not yet flowed out, then switching the six-way purge valve V2 to the double-line state, and detecting the carbon dioxide CO2 by the thermal conductivity after passing through the damper tube CC-2 and TCD1 detection, after the damping of CC-2 and maintaining the detection of carbon dioxide in the six-way purge valve V2 to the single state, closing the oxygen/nitrogen/carbon monoxide of MC-2 separated by the molecular sieve column, which is detected by the conductivity detector TCD-1, starting the six-way purge valve V3 starts in a single state, switching the six-way purge valve V3 to the line state, maintaining the gas sample quantitative ring L2 in the analysis system, separating the sample by a packed column MC-3 and detecting by thermal conductivity detector TCD2 and a hydrogen flame ionization detector FID1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - COMPUTING AND CONTROL - Preferred Method: The step (ii) includes passing the product gas sample into the concentrated sulfuric acid and then passing through the interface 10 of the ten-way purge valve V1. The basic analyzing chromatographic condition includes chromatographic column temperature of 80 degrees C, hydrogen flame ionization detector temperature of 150 degrees C, thermal conductivity detector temperature of 130 degrees C, high-purity hydrogen is 99.999% of carrier gas, the flow rate is 20 ml/minute, the air flow rate is 400 ml/minute, where under the above chromatographic condition in step (iii) the six-way purge valve V2 is switched to a time-line selection 2 minute and the six-way switching valve V2 is the time of the single selection of 5 minute.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC J04-C01; J04-C04A; S03-E09C1; T01-C07C2; T01-N01D2

AE BD ENERGY SYSTEMS LLC (BDEN-Non-standard)
GA 202293603W
AB    NOVELTY - Low-carbon dioxide emission methanol process                comprises: e.g. (a) mixing a hydrocarbon feed stock                with water to form a mixed feed stream; (b)                reforming the mixed feed stream in a reforming                furnace comprising a radiant section and a                convection section to form a syngas stream; (c)                supplying air and a carbon-lean fuel stream                comprising hydrogen to fire the radiant section;                (d) passing flue gas from the radiant section to                the convection section; (e) cooling the flue gas in                the convection section and optionally removing                particulates from the flue gas; (f) discharging the                cooled flue gas, where the flue gas is lean in                carbon dioxide (less than 3 vol.%, preferably less                than 1 vol.%, dry basis); (g) cooling the syngas                stream; and (h) passing the cooled syngas stream                directly to an absorber-stripper unit to produce a                carbon dioxide rich stream and a carbon                dioxide-lean syngas stream.
   USE - The process is useful for producing low-carbon                dioxide emission methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: reduces or eliminates carbon                dioxide and other emissions and/or ways to recover                waste heat at temperatures below the dew point of                the flue gas; allows bypassing the condensing                section; allows relatively higher flue gas                temperature operation; and improves radiant                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low-carbon dioxide emission methanol process,                comprises: (a) mixing a hydrocarbon feed stock with                water to form a mixed feed stream; (b) reforming                the mixed feed stream in a reforming furnace                comprising a radiant section and a convection                section to form a syngas stream; (c) supplying air                and a carbon-lean fuel stream comprising hydrogen                to fire the radiant section; (d) passing flue gas                from the radiant section to the convection section;                (e) cooling the flue gas in the convection section                and optionally removing particulates from the flue                gas; (f) discharging the cooled flue gas, where the                flue gas is lean in carbon dioxide (less than 3                vol.%, preferably less than 1 vol.%, dry basis);                (g) cooling the syngas stream; (h) passing the                cooled syngas stream directly to an                absorber-stripper unit to produce a carbon dioxide                rich stream and a carbon dioxide-lean syngas                stream; (i) compressing and passing the carbon                dioxide-lean syngas stream to a methanol reactor                loop to produce a methanol-rich stream, where the                methanol reactor loop comprises a methanol                synthesis reactor, a methanol recovery section to                recover a crude methanol stream and a recycle                syngas stream, and a recycle compressor (104) to                compress the recycle syngas stream to a pressure of                the reactor, and the pressure of the reactor is                higher than a pressure of the mixed feed stream in                step (b); (j) processing the crude methanol stream                in a methanol wash unit to recover a methanol                product, a condensate stream, and a fusel oil                stream; (k) supplying a purge stream from the                methanol reactor loop to a pressure swing                adsorption unit to obtain a hydrogen-rich stream                and a hydrogen-lean tail gas stream (110); (l)                supplying the hydrogen-rich stream to the                carbon-lean fuel stream in the step (c); (m)                supplying at least a portion (preferably greater                than or equal to 90% o) of the tail gas stream from                step the (k) to the hydrocarbon feed stock in the                step (a); and (n) purging a remaining portion                (preferably less than or equal to 10%) of the tail                gas stream from the step (1) to the carbon-lean                fuel stream in step (c). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for a low-carbon dioxide emission                methanol production apparatus comprising a mixing                station to mix a hydrocarbon feed stock with water                and form a mixed feed stream, a reformer in a                reforming furnace comprising a radiant section and                a convection section to reform the mixed feed                stream and form a syngas stream, an air intake and                a carbon-lean fuel stream to fire one or more                burners in the radiant section, a flue to pass flue                gas from the radiant section and through the                convection section, at least one convection section                heat exchangers to cool the flue gas in the                convection section, an optional particulate removal                unit in the convection section to remove                particulates from the flue gas, a stack to                discharge the cooled flue gas from the convection                section, where the cooled flue gas is lean in                carbon dioxide (less than 3 vol.%, preferably less                than 1 vol.%, dry basis), at least one process heat                exchangers to cool the syngas stream, an                absorber-stripper unit to directly receive                (preferably without any shift converter) the cooled                syngas stream and produce a carbon dioxide rich                stream and a carbon dioxide-lean syngas stream, a                compressor to recirculate the carbon dioxide-lean                syngas stream in a methanol reactor loop at a                higher pressure than the reformer and produce a                methanol-rich stream, a methanol wash unit to                recover a methanol product, a condensate stream,                and a fusel oil stream from the methanol-rich                stream, a pressure swing adsorption unit to receive                a purge stream from the methanol reactor loop to                produce a hydrogen-rich stream and a hydrogen-lean                tail gas stream, a line to supply the hydrogen-rich                stream from the pressure swing adsorption unit to                the burners, a line to supply at least a portion                (preferably greater than or equal to 90% e.g.                90-95%) of the tail gas stream to the hydrocarbon                feed stock, and a line for purging a remaining                portion (preferably greater than or equal to 10%                e.g. 5-10%) of the tail gas stream to the                carbon-lean fuel stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a block diagram of a                low-carbon dioxide emissions methanol process and                production equipment.
   Hydrocarbon feedstock in line (102)
   Compressor (104)
   Methane reformer (108)
   Tail gas stream (110)
   Turbine (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reforming the step                  (b) comprises: (b-1) pre-reforming the mixed feed                  stream to form a partially reformed feed stream,                  and (b-2) primary reforming of the partially                  reformed feed stream in the radiant section of                  the reformer; and (B-1) supplying the mixed feed                  stream to a first pre-reformer to form a first                  partially reformed feed stream, (B-2) supplying                  the first partially reformed feed stream to a                  second pre-reformer to form a second partially                  reformed feed stream, and (B-3) passing the                  second partially reformed feed stream to the                  radiant section of the reforming furnace to                  produce the syngas stream. The pre-reforming is                  in at least one pre-reformer disposed in the                  convection section in heat exchange relationship                  with the flue gas from the radiant section. The                  pre-reforming is in the pre-reformers disposed                  externally of the convection section and                  receiving the mixed feed stream from a coil                  disposed in the convection section. The reforming                  step has an efficiency of at least 90%,                  preferably at least 94%. The reforming the step                  (b) comprises a crossover temperature of less                  than or equal to 566 degrees C (1050 degrees F)                  and a reformer outlet temperature of at least 902                  degrees C (1655 degrees F). A ratio of air to                  fuel in the step (c) produces a flame temperature                  greater than or equal to 2500 degrees K. The                  process further comprises: (e-1) pre-heating the                  hydrocarbon feed stock in a first heat exchanger                  coil in the convection section, (e-2), where the                  mixing in the step (a) comprises saturating the                  pre-heated hydrocarbon feed stock from the step                  (e-1), (e-3) heating the mixed feed stream from                  the step (e-2) with the flue gas in a second heat                  exchanger coil in the convection section upstream                  of the first heat exchanger coil with respect to                  flue gas flow through the convection section, to                  superheat the mixed feed stream, (e-4)                  pre-reforming the superheated mixed feed stream                  in the pre-reformers disposed in the convection                  section in heat exchange relationship with the                  flue gas, or disposed externally of the                  convection section receiving the mixed feed                  stream from a preheating coil disposed in the                  convection section, upstream of the second heat                  exchanger coil with respect to flue gas flow to                  form a partially reformed stream, (e-5)                  optionally pre-heating the partially reformed                  stream, in a third heat exchanger coil in the                  convection section upstream of the pre-reformers                  with respect to flue gas flow through the                  convection section, and (b-2) reforming the                  partially reformed feed stream in the radiant                  section; (e-6) selective catalytic reduction of                  the flue gas to remove nitrogen oxides, and (e-7)                  pre-heating cold air in a third heat exchanger                  coil in the convection section; (e-8) cooling the                  flue gas to below a dew point to form condensate                  in a condensing portion of the convection                  section, (e-9) collecting and removing the                  condensate from the convection section; (E-1)                  pre-heating the mixed feed stream in a first heat                  exchanger coil in a condensing portion of the                  convection section to cool the flue gas to below                  a dew point and form condensate, (E-2) collecting                  and removing the condensate from the convection                  section, (E-3) heating the pre-heated hydrocarbon                  feedstock from the first heat exchanger coil with                  the flue gas, in a second heat exchanger coil, in                  a non-condensing portion of the convection                  section upstream of the first heat exchanger coil                  with respect to flue gas flow through the                  convection section to superheat the mixed feed                  stream, and (E-4) reforming the superheated mixed                  feed stream in at least the radiant section and                  optionally in a reforming exchanger in the                  convection section upstream of the second heat                  exchanger coil with respect to flue gas flow;                  (E-5) recycling a portion of the collected                  condensate to the convection section to contact                  and scrub the flue gas downstream of the first                  heat exchanger coil, and/or (E-6) collecting the                  recycled condensate with the condensate from the                  first heat exchanger coil, and/or (E-7) cooling                  the recycled condensate prior to contact with the                  flue gas, and/or (E-8) adding a scrubbing agent                  to the recycled condensate prior to contact with                  the flue gas, and/or (E-9) regenerating at least                  a portion of the collected condensate to recover                  a carbon dioxide-rich stream and carbon                  dioxide-lean condensate for the recycling to the                  convection section, and/or (E-10) passing the                  scrubbed flue gas through a demister to remove                  entrained liquid; (E-11) preheating the                  hydrocarbon in a third heat exchanger coil                  located downstream of the first heat exchanger in                  the condensing portion of the convection section,                  (E-12) further preheating the hydrocarbon from                  the third heat exchanger coil in a fourth heat                  exchanger coil upstream of the second heat                  exchanger coil in the noncondensing portion of                  the convection section, and (E-13) exchanging                  heat between the preheated hydrocarbon from the                  fourth heat exchanger coil and the preheated                  hydrocarbon from the third heat exchanger coil,                  and (E-14) combining the hydrocarbon from the                  fourth heat exchanger coil with the water to form                  the mixed feed stream for heating in the first                  heat exchanger coil, and (E-15) desulfurizing the                  preheated hydrocarbon from the fourth heat                  exchanger coil prior to the mixing with the water                  in the step (a); (E-16) inducing a draft of the                  flue gas downstream of the second heat exchanger                  coil and upstream of the condensate collection                  and first heat exchanger coil, and discharging                  the flue gas downstream from the first heat                  exchanger into a stack, and/or (E-17) diverting                  at least a portion of the flue gas, from a                  location i.e. downstream of the draft induction                  and upstream of the condensate collection and                  first heat exchanger coil, into the stack, and                  bypassing the condensate collection and first                  heat exchanger coil, and/or (E-18) controlling                  the portion of the diverted flue gas using at                  least one flow damper to regulate the flow of the                  diverted portion, and/or (E-19) passing the flue                  gas downstream of the draft induction through a                  first portion of the stack, across the first heat                  exchanger coil, and then into a second portion of                  the stack located above the first portion, where                  the dampers is located in the stack between the                  first and second portions of the stack;                  preheating air in an air preheat coil in the                  convection section and supplying the preheated                  air to a combustion burner in the radiant section                  in the step (c); and condensate stripping from                  the cooled syngas stream from the step (g)                  upstream from the absorber-stripper unit in the                  step (h), preferably where the fusel oil stream                  from the step (j) is supplied to the condensate                  stripping step. The entire convection section is                  maintained at a temperature above a dew point to                  inhibit condensate formation. One of the first                  and second heat exchangers is located in an                  essentially vertical convection flue section, and                  the other one of the first and second heat                  exchangers is located in an essentially                  horizontal convection flue section. The step (g)                  comprises generating steam, preheating boiler                  feed water, heat exchange with air and/or cooling                  water. The step (h) comprises: contacting the                  cooled syngas with a carbon dioxide solvent,                  preferably an alkanolamine i.e. methyl                  diethanolamine; and contacting the cooled syngas                  with the carbon dioxide solvent in an absorber,                  where the carbon dioxide is stripped from the                  solvent in a stripper, and the stripper is                  operated at a lower pressure and/or a higher                  temperature than the absorber. The carbon-lean                  fuel stream in the step (c) comprises the                  hydrogen-rich stream from step (1) and the                  remaining portion of the tail gas stream from                  step (n). The hydrocarbon feed stock from the                  step (a) and the portion of the tail gas stream                  from the step (m) are supplied to the reforming                  the step (b) at a rate, where the reforming the                  step (b) produces excess hydrogen for step (1) in                  an amount, together with the tail gas purge in                  step (n), that matches the fuel requirements in                  step (c). Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon                  feed stock comprises natural gas, where the mixed                  feed stream comprises a molar ratio of steam to                  carbon 1-5, preferably 1.8-2.5. The carbon                  dioxide-lean syngas stream in the step (h)                  comprises less than 0.1 vol.% carbon dioxide or                  is preferably essentially free of carbon dioxide.                  The step (i) comprises methanol synthesis with a                  carbon efficiency of greater than or equal to                  95%, preferably greater than or equal to 97%. The                  reformer comprises: the pre-reformers receiving                  the mixed feed stream to form a partially                  reformed feedstock; and a primary reformer                  disposed in the radiant section to receive the                  partially reformed feedstock to produce the                  syngas stream. The pre-reformers are: disposed in                  the convection section in heat exchange                  relationship with flue gas from the radiant                  section; and disposed externally of the                  convection section, and further comprising a                  preheat exchange coil disposed in the convection                  section to pre-heat the partially reformed stream                  from the pre-reformers for supply to the primary                  reformer. The reformer is: operable with an                  efficiency of at least 90%, preferably at least                  94%; and operable with a crossover temperature of                  less than or equal to 566 degrees C (1050 degrees                  F) and a reformer outlet temperature of at least                  902 degrees C (1655 degrees F). A ratio of air to                  fuel produces a flame temperature of greater than                  or equal to 2500 degrees K. The entire convection                  section is free of condensate. The apparatus                  further comprises: a first heat exchanger coil in                  the convection section for pre-heating the                  hydrocarbon feed stock, a saturator for                  saturating the pre-heated hydrocarbon feed stock,                  a second heat exchanger coil in the convection                  section upstream of the first heat exchanger coil                  with respect to flue gas flow through the                  convection section to superheat the mixed feed                  stream, the pre-reformers disposed in heat                  exchange relationship with the convection                  section, or disposed externally of the convection                  section and receiving the mixed feed stream from                  a feed preheat coil disposed in the convection                  section, upstream of the second heat exchanger                  coil with respect to flue gas flow for                  pre-reforming the superheated mixed feed stream                  and forming a pre-reformed feed stream, an                  optional third heat exchanger disposed in the                  convection section upstream from the                  pre-reformers with respect to flue gas flow to                  pre-heat the pre-reformed feed stream, and a line                  to supply the pre-reformed feed stream to the                  reformer; a selective catalytic reduction unit                  downstream from the first heat exchanger coil                  with respect to flue gas flow through the                  convection section to remove nitrogen oxides from                  the flue gas, and a cold air preheater disposed                  downstream from the selective catalytic reduction                  unit with respect to flue gas flow through the                  convection section; a heat exchanger in the                  convection section for cooling the flue gas to                  below a dew point to form condensate in a                  condensing portion of the convection section, and                  a line for removing the condensate from the                  convection section; a first heat exchanger coil                  in a condensing portion of the convection section                  to pre-heat the mixed feed stream and cool the                  flue gas to below a dew point and form                  condensate, a reservoir to collect and a line to                  remove the condensate from the convection                  section, a second heat exchanger coil disposed in                  a non-condensing portion of the convection                  section upstream of the first heat exchanger coil                  with respect to flue gas flow through the                  convection section, to heat the pre-heated                  hydrocarbon feed stream from the first heat                  exchanger coil with the flue gas and superheat                  the mixed feed stream, where the reformer                  comprises a pre-reformer in the convection                  section upstream of the second heat exchanger                  coil with respect to flue gas flow, or disposed                  externally of the convection section and                  receiving pre-heated mixed feed from a pre-heat                  coil disposed in the convection section upstream                  of the second heat exchanger coil with respect to                  flue gas flow, and a primary reformer in the                  radiant section; a line for recycling a portion                  of the collected condensate to the convection                  section to contact and scrub the flue gas                  downstream of the first heat exchanger coil,                  and/or a reservoir for collecting the recycled                  condensate with the condensate from the first                  heat exchanger coil, and/or a heat exchanger for                  cooling the recycled condensate prior to contact                  with the flue gas, and/or a scrubbing agent added                  to the recycled condensate prior to contact with                  the flue gas, and/or a regenerator to regenerate                  at least a portion of the collected condensate to                  recover a carbon dioxide-rich stream and carbon                  dioxide-lean condensate for the recycling to the                  convection section, and or a demister to receive                  the scrubbed flue gas and remove entrained                  liquid; a third heat exchanger coil located                  downstream of the first heat exchanger in the                  condensing portion of the convection section for                  preheating the hydrocarbon, a fourth heat                  exchanger coil upstream of the second heat                  exchanger coil in the non-condensing portion of                  the convection section for preheating the                  hydrocarbon from the third heat exchanger coil,                  an intercooler for exchanging heat between the                  preheated hydrocarbon from the fourth heat                  exchanger coil and the preheated hydrocarbon from                  the third heat exchanger coil, where the mixing                  station combines the hydrocarbon from the fourth                  heat exchanger coil with the water to form the                  mixed feed stream for heating in the first heat                  exchanger coil, and a desulfurizer for                  desulfurizing the preheated hydrocarbon from the                  fourth heat exchanger coil prior to the mixing                  with the water; a fan for inducing a draft of the                  flue gas downstream of the second heat exchanger                  coil and upstream of the condensate collection                  and first heat exchanger coil, and discharging                  the flue gas downstream from the first heat                  exchanger into a stack, and or a diverter flue                  for diverting at least a portion of the flue gas,                  from a location that is downstream of the draft                  induction and upstream of the condensate                  collection and first heat exchanger coil into the                  stack, and bypassing the condensate collection                  and first heat exchanger coil, and/or a damper                  for controlling the portion of the diverted flue                  gas using the flow dampers to regulate the flow                  of the diverted portion and pass the flue gas                  downstream of the draft induction through a first                  portion of the stack, across the first heat                  exchanger coil, and then into a second portion of                  the stack located above the first portion, where                  the dampers is located in the stack between the                  first and second portions of the stack; an air                  preheat coil in the convection section and a line                  for supplying preheated air from the air preheat                  coil to a combustion burner in the radiant                  section; and a condensate stripper for stripping                  condensate from the cooled syngas stream from the                  process heat exchangers, and preferably a line to                  supply the fusel oil stream from the methanol                  wash unit to the condensate stripper. One of the                  first and second heat exchangers is located in an                  essentially vertical convection flue section, and                  the other one of the first and second heat                  exchangers is located in an essentially                  horizontal convection flue section. The process                  heat exchangers generate steam, preheat boiler                  feed water, exchange heat with air and/or cooling                  water. The carbon dioxide-lean syngas stream                  comprises less than 0.1 vol.% carbon dioxide or                  is preferably essentially free of carbon dioxide.                  The absorber-stripper unit comprises a carbon                  dioxide solvent, preferably an alkanolamine, i.e.                  methyl diethanolamine. The stripper is operated                  at a lower pressure and/or a higher temperature                  than the absorber. The methanol reactor loop                  operates with a carbon efficiency of greater than                  or equal to 95%, preferably greater than or equal                  to 97%. The carbon-lean fuel stream comprises the                  hydrogen-rich stream from the pressure swing                  adsorption unit and the remaining portion of the                  tail gas stream. The hydrocarbon feed stock and                  the remaining portion of the tail gas stream                  match the fuel requirements for reforming at a                  rate that produces excess hydrogen to meet the                  firing requirements of the reformer.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of reaction                conditions that do not support the claims.

TI Nasal respiratory device for sampling            orally-exhaled end tidal carbon dioxide, has end tidal            connector being complementary to connector on portion            of air chamber such that sampling attachment is            connected to exterior of air chamber.
AU KANE D M
   REDFORD R
AE PNEUMA THERAPEUTICS INC (PNEU-Non-standard)
GA 2022B2493X
AB    NOVELTY - The device (2500) has a gas conduit being in                fluid communication with an air chamber. A blade                opening is oriented to face a mouth of a patient                for receiving end tidal carbon dioxide expelled                from the mouth of the patient. A blade port is                arranged in a wall of a tube for allowing fluid                communication with a blade channel (2662) through                the blade port to provide sample end tidal gasses                from the blade opening to an end tidal sample line                port. An end tidal connector is complementary to a                connector on a portion of an air chamber such that                a removable oral end tidal sampling attachment is                connected to an exterior of the air chamber.
   USE - Nasal respiratory device for sampling                orally-exhaled end tidal carbon dioxide along with                nasally-exhaled end tidal carbon dioxide during                ventilation of a patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The removable oral end tidal sampling                attachment can be connected to the exterior of the                air chamber to sample the oral end tidal gasses                during ventilation of the patient in an efficient                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for sampling oral end tidal gasses during                ventilation of a patient through a nasal                respiratory device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                device for sampling orally-exhaled end tidal carbon                dioxide along with nasally-exhaled end tidal carbon                dioxide.

TI Building control system, has intermediate relay            connected to collector so that intermediate relay is            turned on based on output current of collector to make            building control device work, and carbon dioxide bottle            arranged inside electric control cabinet for filling            carbon dioxide gas into cabinet.
AU DENG Y
   MO Z
   HUANG M
   PENG H
   SHEN W
AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 2022B2636Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an electric control cabinet                including an intermediate relay, a building control                device and a carbon dioxide bottle. The                intermediate relay is connected to a collector so                that the intermediate relay is turned on based on                the output current of the collector to make the                building control device work. The carbon dioxide                bottle is arranged inside the electric control                cabinet for filling carbon dioxide gas into the                electric control cabinet according to instruction                of the collector. The collector controls on-off of                the intermediate relay according to the working                parameters of the electric control cabinet and                opening and closing of a valve of the carbon                dioxide bottle according to the working parameters                of the electric control cabinet.
   USE - Building self-control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The collector obtains relevant parameters of                the electric control cabinet, so as to identify                whether there is an abnormality, quickly makes a                protective response in the event of an abnormality,                which reduces loss of equipment and property and                ensures safe operation of the building control                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a collector;
   (2) an electric control cabinet;
   (3) a building control system safety                protection controlling method; and
   (4) a computer readable storage medium                comprises a set of instructions for realizing                building control system safety protection                controlling process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a building control system. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN WO2021207857-A1
TI Electrolyzing bicarbonate by applying electrical potential between anode and electrode of electrochemical cell comprising ion exchange membrane separating anode/electrode, dissociating water into hydrogen and hydroxide ions, and reacting.
AU BERLINGUETTE C
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV BRITISH COLUMBIA (UYBR-C)
GA 2021B86266
AB    NOVELTY - Bicarbonate is electrolyzed by applying electrical potential between anode and electrode of electrochemical cell comprising an ion exchange membrane which separates anode and electrode comprising porous metallic material having pores distributed throughout the electrode; dissociating water, within the ion-exchange membrane, into hydrogen ions and hydroxide ions; permeating hydrogen and the hydroxides ions out of the ion exchange membrane, where hydrogen ions permeate towards the electrode, and hydroxide ions permeate towards the anode; reacting hydrogen ions with biccarbonate in a solution to form carbon dioxide; and reducing carbon dioxide, at the porous metallic material of the electrode, to form one or more carbon-containing resulting products.
   USE - The method is useful for electrolyzing carbon-containing solutions, such as a solution containing bicarbonate, useful for capturing atmospheric carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cell facilitates the capture of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere or a point source to form useful products without requiring high temperature or pressurization processes. The current efficiency of the electrolysis reaction is optimized by adjusting one or more of the porosity of the porous metallic electrode and/or the electrochemical surface area of the electrode or the specific type of metal used for the electrode and the temperature of the carbon-containing solution being supplied to the electrode. The cell is capable of producing useful products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) system for electrolyzing bicarbonate;
   (2) method of capturing carbon dioxide;
   (3) system for capturing carbon dioxide;
   (4) method of electrolyzing a carbon-containing ion; and
   (5) electrochemical cell for electrolyzing a carbon ion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: carbon-containing resulting products comprises carbon monoxide. Concentration of bicarbonate in the bicarbonate solution is 0.1-6 M, preferably 4-6 M. Metallic material comprises greater than or equal to 1 transition metals. Metallic material comprises silver (Ag), preferably in the form of a free standing silver foam. Electrode is made of a foam material. Nanosized catalyst comprises nanowires, nanorods, nanoparticles, or nanocubes. Preferred Properties: Surface of the electrode is hydrophilic. Preferred Processes: Etching the electrode comprises contacting the electrode with an acid. Step of increasing the electrochemically active surface area of the electrode comprises depositing a nanosized catalyst on a surface of the electrode. The method further comprises heating the bicarbonate solution to 60-80 degrees C prior to the reacting step.

PN CN112717725-A; CN112717725-B
TI Preparation of mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve film doped with porous nitrogen-containing microsphere used for separating carbon dioxide/nitrogen, involves adding porous nitrogen-doped microsphere into polyamic acid film-making liquid.
AU LI J
   WANG Y
   ZHANG X
   LI X
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2021473288
AB    NOVELTY - A mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve film doped with porous nitrogen-containing microsphere is prepared by hydrothermal layer-by-layer self-assembly method, activating to obtain porous nitrogen-doped microsphere as additive, selecting polyamic acid as polymer precursor of carbon molecular sieve film, adding porous nitrogen-doped microsphere into polyamic acid film-making liquid, stirring uniformly, standing and defoaming, scraping and drying to obtain polyamic acid mixed matrix film doped with porous nitrogen-containing microsphere; putting polyamic acid mixed matrix film of doped porous nitrogen-containing microsphere in carbonization furnace of protective atmosphere for high temperature carbonization, cooling to room temperature under protection atmosphere, and taking out to obtain mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve film doped with porous nitrogen-containing microsphere.
   USE - The method is used for preparing mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve membrane doped with porous nitrogen-containing microsphere, which is useful for separating carbon dioxide/nitrogen, carbon dioxide/methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve membrane prepared by taking the carbon material as doping filler has good carbon dioxide adsorption performance; the preparation technique of the mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve membrane is simple and easy to operate; the prepared mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve membrane has good chemical stability and thermal stability; the mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve film is used for the separation of carbon dioxide/nitrogen, carbon dioxide/methane, and breaking the polymer film to sacrifice selectivity as cost to improve the trade-off effect of permeability, with excellent comprehensive performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method involves preparing porous nitrogen-containing microsphere:dissolving the glucose and melamine according tomass ratio of 1: (0.2 ~ 5) in water, ensuring the massfraction of glucose in water is 10-15%, pouring the solution into the hydrothermal reaction kettle containing polytetrafluoroethylene lining , putting in theblast drying box of 110-200 degrees C, reacting for 3-4 hours, collecting the obtained product,washing with distilled water for 3-10 times, putting inthe blast drying box at 60-110 degrees C, the obtained product is namedas melamine-glucose microsphere, marking as substance A, dispersing the substance A and activatoraccording to mass ratio of 1: (0.1 ~ 5) in water, mixing uniformly, putting in vacuum dryingbox of 80 to 150 degrees C for drying for 6 to 24h, taking dry sample to activate for 0.5 to 5 hours under theprotection of 500 to 800 degrees C nitrogen to obtain the porous nitrogen-containing microsphere, named asporous nitrogen-doped microsphere, reparingpolyamic acid mixed substrate film doped with porousnitrogen-containing microsphere, weighing polyamicacid; adding the porous nitrogen-containingmicrosphere into the solvent, ensuring that thepolyamic acid accounts for 5-20%, mass ratio of the polyamic acid andporous nitrogen-containing microsphere is 1: (0.005-0.1); the solution is in the frequency range of 20 ~ 50KHz; 20-80 degrees C of temperaturerange for ultrasonic 0.5-12 h, at the temperaturerange of 25-60 degrees C, stirring for 2-24h on the magnetic stirrer at the rotating speed range of 20-2000 rpm, uniformly dispersing, standing for 12-24 h at constant temperature of 25 degrees C to obtain the casting solution B, uniformly coating the casting solution B on the clean glass plate, using the film scraper to control the thickness of the wet film to be 100-300 m, putting in the vacuum drying box at 30-40 degrees C for drying for 6-24 h,then drying for 12- 36 h in the vacuum drying box at 40-80 degrees C obtaining the polyamic acid mixed substrate film doped with porous nitrogen-containing microsphere.

TI Preparation of anhydrous phosphorus gypsum-based product for carbon dioxide maintenance, involves mixing anhydrous phosphorus gypsum, excitation reagent, cement clinker, active admixture and gypsum whisker, adding water reducing agent.
AU SU Y
   XIONG G
   WANG Y
   FU Z
   FAN J
   HE X
   LI Y
   CHEN S
   HUANG Z
   LIU Q
AE UNIV HUBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHI-C)
GA 202101526S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anhydrous phosphorus gypsum based product of carbon dioxide maintenance comprises (i) taking 70-100 pts. wt. anhydrous phosphorus gypsum, 5-20 pts. excitation agent, 2-14 pts. wt. cement clinker, 5-15 pts. wt. active admixture, 2-6 pts. Wt. gypsum whisker, 0.1-0.95 pts. water reducing agent, and 0.25-0.65 pts. wt. water, mixing and stirring uniformly to obtain the mixed material, (ii) slowly adding water in the obtained mixed material, at the same time, adding water reducing agent, stirring uniformly to disperse to obtain the flowing slurry, and (iii) quickly injecting the obtained slurry into the mold, placing into carbon dioxide curing box to maintain, maintaining to the regular age, removing the mold to obtain the anhydrous phosphorus gypsum based product of carbon dioxide maintenance, where the excitation reagent is powder material comprising quicklime, alkali slag and/or white mud; and the active admixture is fly ash, slag powder, silicon ash and/or coal gangue powder.

   ADVANTAGE - The anhydrous phosphorus gypsum-based product has good mechanical property, large surface hardness, good water resistance, water resistance coefficient can reach more than 0.8, not only effectively using industrial waste, low cost, realizing high additional value utilization of waste, but also uses waste greenhouse effect gas-carbon dioxide, reducing greenhouse gas emission, protecting environment, and choking greenhouse effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gypsum whiskers are phosphogypsum dihydrate whiskers. The content of calcium sulfate greater than or equal to 98%. PN CN112090252-A; CN112090252-B
TI Modified desulfurized ash used for fixing carbon dioxide, comprises desulfurized ash, nitrogen-containing silane coupling agent, ethanol and deionized water at given weight ratio.
AU YANG H
   FENG Q
   LIU Y
   LIANG H
   TAO H
   CHEN D
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2020C8858F
AB    NOVELTY - A modified desulfurized ash comprises desulfurized ash, nitrogen-containing silane coupling agent, ethanol and deionized water at weight ratio of 1:0.3-2:0.3-2:0.5-3.
   USE - The modified desulfurized ash is used for fixing carbon dioxide, particularly as filling component of abandoned mines after mining to realize fixing and permanent sealing of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The modified desulfurized ash is environment-friendly, has low carbon content, can realize dry desulfurized ash resource utilization, and changes traditional method of simply using calcium-based material to fix carbon dioxide. Modification of desulfurized ash with nitrogen-containing silane coupling agent increases chemical adsorption effect of carbon dioxide, thus improving capability of modified desulfurized ash to fix carbon dioxide. Preparation method is simple and convenient to operate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of modified desulfurized ash by washing desulfurized ash with ethanol and deionized water, drying to constant weight at 100-110 degrees C, sieving, and heating to 700-850 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours to obtain activated desulfurized ash; dissolving nitrogen-containing silane coupling agent in ethanol and water, and mechanically stirring for 1-2 hours or ultrasonically treating for 30 minutes at 10-60 degrees C to prepare hydrolysate; mixing activated desulfurized ash with hydrolysate, mechanically stirring at 10-40 degrees C for 2-4 hours, spreading obtained product on flat glass, and naturally air-drying until solvent is completely evaporated; and hydrothermally crystallizing dried product at 90-115 degrees C for 1-2 days, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing, drying to constant weight at 100-110 degrees C, grinding, and sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The desulfurized ash is the product after dry desulfurization of coal-fired flue gas. Preferred Method: The dried desulfurized ash is passed through 100-400-mesh sieve. Mechanical stirring is performed at 100-400 revolutions/minute. Ultrasonic treatment is carried out at 200-800 W and 40 kHz.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silane coupling agent is gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane, gamma -aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, gamma -aminopropylmethyldiethoxysilane, and/or gamma -aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane.

PN US2020017422-A1; WO2020013908-A1; US10882800-B2; CA3106310-A1; EP3820969-A1; JP2021531376-W
TI Production of methane used for integrated gasification for producing renewable natural gas, involves contacting carbonaceous feed with oxygen-containing gasifier feed to form effluent, and reacting electrolysis hydrogen with effluent.
AU KRAMER A
   SEABA J
   LAMONT D
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 2020059425
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methane involves contacting a carbonaceous feed (10) with an oxygen-containing gasifier feed to provide a gasifier effluent (16) comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas, reacting electrolysis hydrogen (18), obtained from an electrolyzer, with at least a portion of the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide to form methane, adjusting an operation of the process, and affecting a methanation reactor inlet carbon monoxide concentration or a methanation reactor inlet carbon dioxide concentration, in response to a makeup quantity of the electrolysis hydrogen.
   USE - Production of methane used for integrated gasification for producing pipe quality renewable natural gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of methane having improved efficiency, and reduced the overall carbon footprint associated with the production of natural gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for integrated gasification, methanation, and electrolysis process, which involves contacting biomass with an oxygen-containing gasifier feed to provide a gasification zone effluent comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas, converting at least a portion of the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in the gasification zone effluent to methane, obtaining the renewable natural gas as, or recovering the renewable natural gas from, a methanation zone effluent, the gasification zone or the methanation zone comprises at least one operation affecting carbon monoxide concentration or carbon dioxide concentration, and adjusting the operation in response to a makeup quantity of electrolysis hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of illustrating the operation in gasification zone.
   Carbonaceous feed (10)
   Gasification zone effluent (16)
   Electrolysis hydrogen (18)
   Methanation zone feed (20)
   Methanation reactor effluent (25)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further involves obtaining a methane product as, or recovering the methane product from, a methanation reactor effluent. The process of affecting the methanation reactor inlet carbon monoxide concentration or the methanation reactor inlet carbon dioxide concentration is a sour shift operation or an acid gas removal operation which consumes or produces carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide in the process, and is adjusted by passing at least a portion of a feed to the operation. The electrolysis oxygen and the air separation unit oxygen provide a makeup quantity of oxygen that is determined at least partly on the basis of a makeup quantity of the carbonaceous feed. The process further involves operation(s), downstream of the gasifier and upstream of the methanation reactor, performing processing step(s) on the gasifier effluent, the processing step(s) involves converting tar, recovering heat, filtering/scrubbing, supplemental cleaning, and removing sulfur. The methanation zone in addition to a methanation reactor comprises operation(s) such as sour shift operation, sulfur removal operation, acid gas removal operation, and methanation reactor heat recovery operation. Preferred Composition: The ratio of methanation reactor inlet satisfying the relation: (H2)/3(CO)+4(CO2) is 0.7-1.5. Preferred Components: The carbonaceous feed comprises coal or biomass. The oxygen-containing gasifier feed comprises electrolysis oxygen, air separation unit oxygen, or water and/or carbon dioxide. Preferred Property: The electrolysis hydrogen increases the yield of methane based on carbon present in the biomass, by at least 10% relative to a baseline process performed without addition of the electrolysis hydrogen.
