PT P
PN CN110339672-A; CN110339672-B
TI Functionalized ionic liquid/organic solvent            composite system for capturing carbon dioxide in mixed            gas, comprises functionalized ionic liquid            tetraethylenepentamine-2-methylimidazolium salt,            n-propanol and ethylene glycol.
AU LI W
   LIU F
   LI S
   SUN C
   SHEN L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201988659Q
AB    NOVELTY - A functionalized ionic liquid/organic solvent                composite system comprises functionalized ionic                liquid tetraethylenepentamine-2-methylimidazolium                salt (I), n-propanol and ethylene glycol.
   USE - Functionalized ionic liquid/organic solvent                composite system is used for capturing carbon                dioxide in mixed gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic liquid absorbs carbon dioxide in                n-propanol solution to precipitate solid insoluble                matter. By adding ethylene glycol, the carbon                dioxide absorption product can be prevented from                being precipitated in the solution to form a turbid                liquid, and the volatility of the solution can be                lowered. The introduction of amino functional                groups effectively increases the carbon dioxide                absorption load of the system. An organic solvent                with lower heat capacity is used instead of water                to dissolve the ionic liquid, thus reducing the                regeneration energy consumption of the                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A functionalized ionic liquid/organic solvent                composite system comprises functionalized ionic                liquid tetraethylenepentamine-2-methylimidazolium                salt of formula (I), n-propanol and ethylene                glycol. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of functionalized ionic                liquid/organic solvent composite system, which                involves mixing tetraethylenepentamine,                2-methylimidazole and water, obtaining a mixed                solution, reacting the mixed solution at the                rotation speed of 1200-2000 rpm for 12-36 hours,                distilling under reduced pressure to remove                unreacted raw materials and excess deionized water                in the ionic liquid, drying in vacuum, obtaining                functionalized ionic liquid                tetraethylenepentamine-2-methylimidazolium salt,                mixing n-propanol and ethylene glycol as solvent,                and adding functionalized ionic liquid                tetraethylenepentamine-2-methylimidazolium salt to                the solvent; and
   (2) use of functionalized ionic liquid/organic                solvent composite system for capturing carbon                dioxide in a mixed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The functionalized                  ionic liquid                  tetraethylenepentamine-2-methylimidazolium salt                  has concentration of 0.5-1 mol/L. The volume                  ratio of n-propanol with respect to ethylene                  glycol is 50-80:50-20, with respect to 100 parts                  volume of n-propanol and ethylene glycol. The                  molar ratio of tetraethylenepentamine with                  respect to 2-methylimidazole is 0.8-1.2:1.                  Preferred Process: The mixed gas containing                  carbon dioxide is introduced into the                  functionalized ionic liquid/organic solvent                  composite system, and absorption is carried out                  at 30-60 degrees C, followed by regeneration by                  heating. The carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas                  comprises 10-15 vol.% carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Conditions: The mixed solution is reacted at the                  stirring speed of 1400-1800 rpm for 18-30 hours.                  The regeneration process is carried out at                  100-120 degrees C for 30-60 minutes.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN108424359-A; CN108424359-B
TI Preparation of formate/formic acid via catalytic reduction carbon dioxide in water phase by ruthenium complex involves pumping nitrogen, and taking potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution and ruthenium complex catalyst.
AU WANG W
   KE J
   HAN D
   BAO M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2018667263
AB    NOVELTY - A formate/formic acid via catalytic reduction carbon dioxide in water phase by ruthenium complex (I) or (II) is prepared by pumping nitrogen, taking 1-2 mol/L potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution and 0.01-0.1 mu mol ruthenium complex catalyst, pumping 1:5-5:1 volume ratio of carbon dioxide/hydrogen gas mixed gas under 1-4 MPa, reacting at 90-130 degrees C, stirring for 24 hours, obtaining 3380 ton 14.5 mmol/L formate solution, using deoxygenated water, and reacting under same conditions as preparation of formate to obtain 920 ton of 3.3 mmol/L formic acid solution.
   USE - Method for preparing formate/formic acid via catalytic reduction carbon dioxide in water phase by ruthenium complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, low-cost and highly efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A formate/formic acid via catalytic reduction carbon dioxide in water phase by ruthenium complex of formulae (I) or (II) is prepared by pumping nitrogen, taking 1-2 mol/L potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution and 0.01-0.1 mu mol ruthenium complex catalyst, pumping 1:5-5:1 volume ratio of carbon dioxide/hydrogen gas mixed gas under 1-4 MPa, reacting at 90-130 degrees C, stirring for 24 hours, obtaining 3380 ton 14.5 mmol/L formate solution, using deoxygenated water, and reacting under same conditions as preparation of formate to obtain 920 ton of 3.3 mmol/L formic acid solution.
   R1=H, OH or OCH3; and
   R=Ph, tBu or iPr.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ruthenium complex is prepared by taking PNN-tridentate ligand and tris(triphenylphosphine)ruthenium(II) dichloride catalyst precursor, dissolving in anhydrous toluene according to molar ratio of 1:1, pumping nitrogen gas, reacting for 12 hours, filtering, collecting filter cake, washing with diethyl ether, drying to Compound (I); taking PNN-tridentate ligand and tris(triphenylphosphine)ruthenium(II) dichloride catalyst precursor, dissolving in anhydrous tetrahydrofuran according to molar ratio of 1:1, pumping nitrogen gas, stirring at 40 degrees C for 12 hours, cooling, evaporating, taking residue, and washing with diethyl ether to obtain Compound (II).

GA 2018321598
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing a board (110) manufacturing process (S100). A mold is made for the board in which an aspherical surface of a groove portion (110a) is formed in an upper side. A housing (120) installation process is performed. The housing is connected with a metal material. The metal material is fixed on the board for the mold. Beam of a carbon dioxide laser is irradiated (S400). Lens raw material melt phase is determined, where a lens raw material is fused. An aspherical surface of the mold and a lens are formed corresponded to the groove portion (S500).
   USE - Method for manufacturing a metal housing insert lens by using a carbon dioxide laser.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables making the mold with iron alloy so as to save manufacturing cost of a lens unit while diminishing cost of the mold, so that the lens raw material can be fused by using the beam of the carbon dioxide laser and the lens of the aspherical surface can be manufactured, thus avoiding separate impurity removal process and saving processing expense and time period of the mold.

PT P
PN KR1831348-B1
TI Catalyst composition useful for producing syngas from methane using media circulation type gas reforming, comprises oxidation transition metal and perovskite metal oxide complex.
AU KANG D
   LEE J W
   LIM H S
AE KOREA ADVANCED INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KOAD-C)
GA 2018178528
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst composition comprises oxidation transition metal and perovskite metal oxide complex. The transition metal includes copper oxide (CuxOy), iron oxide (FexOy), nickel oxide (NixOy), cobalt oxide (CoxOy) and manganese oxide (MnxOy).
   USE - The composition is useful for producing syngas from methane using media circulation type gas reforming (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition decomposes carbon from methane which is accumulated on the surface of the metal catalyst to deactivate the catalyst in conventional chemical looping reforming reaction, reduces carbon dioxide by accumulating carbon on the surface of the catalyst by injecting the decomposed carbon with carbon dioxide instead of air, produces carbon monoxide, partially oxidizes reduced metal, and the reduced metal partially oxidized. The method produces synthesis gas from methane without additional process for capturing the carbon dioxide, shortens production time, improves production efficiency, and reduces reduces methane and carbon dioxide in greenhouse gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing syngas from methane using media circulation type gas reforming, comprising (a) producing synthesis gas by partial oxidation of methane using metal oxide catalyst containing oxidation transition metal and perovskite metal oxide complex, (b) decomposing methane produced in step (a) to accumulate carbon on the catalyst surface to produce carbon monoxide, (c) partially oxidizing the catalyst by injecting carbon dioxide into the catalyst in which the carbon is accumulated in the step (b), and producing carbon monoxide by reacting carbon on the catalyst surface with carbon dioxide, and (d) reoxidizing the partially oxidized catalyst in the step (c) using an oxidizing gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The perovskite metal oxide has ABO3 structure, where A is calcium, lanthanum or strontium, and B is manganese, iron, nickel or cobalt. The complex has structure in which the oxidation transition metal is supported on the perovskite metal oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methane includes pure methane, hydrocarbon having 2-5 carbon atoms including methane or methane-containing hydrocarbon having 5-10 carbon atoms. The methane is subjected at pressure of 0.1-10 atm with contact time of 0.1-1000 l/g catalyst/hr. The oxidizing gases includes pure oxygen, pure carbon dioxide, air, waste gas containing oxygen, and exhaust gas containing air and carbon dioxide, and is subjected at pressure of 0.1-10 atm with contact time of 0.1-1000 l/g catalyst/hr. Preferred Method: The reactions are carried out at 500-1500 degrees C for 1 minute to 5 hours for each step.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2021086643-A1; CA3155975-A1; AU2020375635-A1; EP4051655-A1; CN115052834-A; IN202227030173-A; KR2022141783-A
TI Producing synthetic graphite by feeding reaction            mixture comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen into            reactor, feeding catalyst, loading vessel into vacuum            furnace, starting vacuum pump, increasing furnace            temperature, and cooling.
AU SMITH R
AE SEERSTONE LLC (SEER-Non-standard)
   SEERSTONE LLC (SEER-Non-standard)
   SEERSTONE LLC (SEER-Non-standard)
GA 202147913H
AB    NOVELTY - A synthetic graphite is produced by feeding                reaction mixture comprising carbon dioxide and                hydrogen into reactor to convert portion of                reaction mixture to methane; feeding catalyst into                reactor; maintaining reaction process to produce                solid carbon reaction product; removing solid                carbon reaction product from reactor and cooling;                condensing out any water and other gaseous                impurities from solid carbon reaction product;                placing solid carbon reaction product into reaction                vessel; loading reaction vessel into vacuum furnace                equipped with vacuum pump; closing furnace and                beginning to heat furnace to increase temperature                of reaction vessel; starting vacuum pump;                increasing furnace temperature; maintaining                temperature of reaction vessel; increasing furnace                temperature until reaction vessel temperature                reaches predetermined temperature; maintaining                reaction vessel temperature at the predetermined                temperature; cooling; and removing processed solid                carbon reaction product from reaction vessel.
   USE - Production of synthetic graphite.
   ADVANTAGE - Treatment of carbon feedstock can be                customized to produce a carbon product with desired                characteristics, i.e., carbon produced can be                thermally treated to take out impurities, as well                as increase the graphitization of the carbon                product. This results in increased conductivity of                the carbon product. Furthermore, these processes                use carbon dioxide (such as that scrubbed from                refinery flue gas) to make a carbon pitch for                production of synthetic graphite, or a dry coke                from those very refinery reactor flue gas. This dry                carbon pitch or coke is produced not from petroleum                tar or other such wet feedstock as previously                known. Furthermore, dry coke can then be used to                make synthetic graphite and typically has a                significant carbon fiber content, which fiber                content may be increased or reduced based on the                operating parameters of the production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A synthetic graphite is produced by feeding                reaction mixture comprising carbon dioxide and                hydrogen at a 1st predetermined feed rate into                reactor at a predetermined temperature and a                predetermined pressure, a portion of the reaction                mixture being converted to methane as the reaction                mixture is being fed into the reactor; feeding                catalyst into the reactor at 2nd predetermined feed                rate; maintaining the reaction process for a                predetermined time to produce a solid carbon                reaction product; removing the solid carbon                reaction product from the reactor and cooling the                solid carbon reaction product; condensing out any                water and other gaseous impurities from the solid                carbon reaction product; placing a quantity of the                solid carbon reaction product into a reaction                vessel; loading the reaction vessel into a vacuum                furnace equipped with a vacuum pump; closing the                furnace and beginning to heat the furnace to                increase the temperature of the reaction vessel;                starting the vacuum pump; increasing the furnace                temperature at a 1st predetermined rate;                maintaining the temperature of the reaction vessel                at a 1st predetermined temperature for a 1st                predetermined time; increasing the furnace                temperature at a 2nd predetermined rate until the                reaction vessel temperature reaches a 2nd                predetermined temperature; maintaining the reaction                vessel temperature at the 2nd predetermined                temperature for a 2nd predetermined time; cooling                the reaction vessel at a rate sufficient to ensure                that the processed solid carbon reaction product                will not oxidize upon opening of the furnace;                opening the furnace and allowing the reaction                vessel to cool to a handling temperature; and                removing the processed solid carbon reaction                product from the reaction vessel. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) treatment of mixture of amorphous and                fibrous carbon to increase order and conductivity                which involves placing a quantity of carbonaceous                material comprising amorphous or fibrous forms of                carbon or their combination into a reaction vessel,                loading the reaction vessel into a vacuum furnace                equipped with a vacuum pump, closing the furnace                and beginning to heat the furnace to increase the                temperature of the reaction vessel, starting the                vacuum pump, increasing the furnace temperature at                a 1st predetermined rate, maintaining the                temperature of the reaction vessel at a 1st                predetermined temperature for a 1st predetermined                time, increasing the furnace temperature at a 2nd                predetermined rate until the reaction vessel                temperature reaches a 2nd predetermined                temperature, maintaining the reaction vessel                temperature at the 2nd predetermined temperature                for a 2nd predetermined time, cooling the reaction                vessel at a rate sufficient to ensure that                processed carbonaceous material will not oxidize                upon opening of the furnace, opening the furnace                and allowing the reaction vessel to cool to a                handling temperature, and removing the carbonaceous                material from the reaction vessel; and
   (2) production of carbon pitch which involves                feeding reaction mixture comprising carbon dioxide                and hydrogen at a 1st predetermined feed rate into                a reactor at a predetermined temperature and a                predetermined pressure, a portion of the reaction                mixture being converted to methane as the reaction                mixture is being fed into the reactor, feeding                catalyst into the reactor at 2nd predetermined feed                rate, maintaining the reaction process for a                predetermined time to produce a predetermined                amount of carbon pitch reaction product, removing                the reaction product from the reactor and cooling                the reaction product, condensing out any water and                other gaseous impurities from the reaction product,                and collecting the reaction product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further                  comprises introducing a flow of a relatively                  inert gas into the furnace at a predetermined                  rate of flow. The method further comprises                  introducing a flow of a relatively inert gas into                  the furnace at a predetermined rate of flow.                  Preferred Conditions: The 1st predetermined rate                  is approximately 20 degrees C per minute. The 1st                  predetermined temperature is approximately 2000                  degrees C, and the 1st predetermined time is                  approximately 30 minutes. The 2nd predetermined                  temperature is approximately 2400 degrees C, and                  the 2nd predetermined rate is approximately 20                  degrees C a minute. The 2nd predetermined time is                  approximately 4 hours. Predetermined temperature                  is 340-540 degrees C. Predetermined pressure is                  14-610 pounds per square inch. Predetermined time                  is 1-1250 hours. Preferred Compositions: Reaction                  mixture has a hydrogen content of 2-89.2% and a                  carbon dioxide content of 2-60%. Methane                  converted comprises 0.5-65.7% of the reaction                  mixture after the reaction mixture has been fed                  into the reactor.

PN KR1840340-B1
TI Purifying biomethane, comprises e.g. compressing biogas, separating compressed biogas into first residual stream and first permeate stream, and separating into second residual sub-stream and second permeate stream.
AU KIM J H
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 201824713M
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying biomethane, comprises e.g. compressing biogas, separating compressed biogas into first residual stream and first permeate stream by introducing into a first polymer separation membrane (51), separating into second residual sub-stream and second permeate stream by introducing first residual sub-stream into a second polymer separation membrane (52), and separating second residual sub-stream into third residual sub-stream and third permeate stream by selectively introducing second residual sub-stream into a third polymer separation membrane or injecting into a methane recovery unit.
   USE - The method and device are useful for purifying biomethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and device provide stable high purity biomethane, and are economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying biomethane, comprises (a) compressing the biogas, (b) separating the compressed biogas into first residual stream and first permeate stream by introducing into a first polymer separation membrane, (c) separating into second residual sub-stream and second permeate stream by introducing the first residual sub-stream into a second polymer separation membrane, (d) separating the second residual sub-stream into third residual sub-stream and third permeate stream by selectively introducing the second residual sub-stream into a third polymer separation membrane or injecting into a methane recovery unit, (e) separating the second permeate stream into fourth residual stream and fourth permeate stream by injecting the second permeate stream into a fourth polymer separation membrane, and (f) selectively recycling the fourth residual sub-stream into the fifth polymer separation membrane or recycling the biogas. The step (f) comprises recycling the fourth residual sub-stream before compressing when the content of carbon dioxide in the fourth residual stream is not less than 0.01 vol.% to less than 5 vol.%;and injecting the fourth residual sub-stream into the fifth polymer separation membrane when the content of carbon dioxide in the fourth residual stream is not less than 5 vol.% to not greater than 15 vol.%. The pressure ratio of the permeable portion to the remaining portion of the fifth polymer separation membrane is 0.4-0.7. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for biomethane purification system comprising a biogas supply unit (10), a compressing unit (41) for compressing the biogas supplied from the biogas supply unit, and a purification unit (50) having a polymer separation membrane for separating gas and connected to the compressing unit or a residual secondary stream of the fourth polymer separation membrane may optionally be separated from the fifth polymer separation membrane for removing carbon dioxide from the compressed biogas. The residual secondary stream of the fourth polymer separation membrane is optionally separated from the fifth polymer separation membrane. The residual secondary stream of the second polymer separation membrane is selectively connected to a methane recovery unit or a third polymer separation membrane. The permeate stream of the second polymer separation membrane is connected to the fourth polymer separation membrane. The residual secondary stream of the first polymer separation membrane is connected to the second polymer separation membrane. The purification unit comprises 4-1 residual sub-stream flow path for connecting the fourth polymer separation membrane and the fifth polymer separation membrane and conveying feeding the residual sub-stream of the fourth polymer separation membrane into the fifth polymer separation membrane, 4-2 residual sub-stream flow path for connecting the fourth polymer separation membrane and the compressing unit and connecting the residual sub-stream of the fourth polymer separation membrane into the compressing unit, a third flow path wastewater stage arranged on the 4-1 residual sub-stream flow path for regulating opening and closing of the 4-1 residual sub-stream flow path, a fourth flow path wastewater stage provided on the 4-2 residual sub-stream flow path for regulating opening and closing of the 4-2 residual sub-stream flow path, first measuring sensor positioned between the fourth polymer separation membrane and the third flow path opening/ closing unit of the 4-1 residual sub-stream flow path, and a first controller connected to the first measurement sensor, the third flow path opening/closing unit and the fourth flow path opening/closing unit. The first measuring sensor detects the amount of carbon dioxide in the residual sub-stream of the fourth polymer separation membrane, which is transferred from the fourth polymer separation membrane through the 4-1 residual sub-stream flow path and converts the content into electrical signals. The first controller controls the opening and closing of the third flow path converting unit and the fourth flow path converting unit by determining the electrical signal transmitted from the first measurement sensor according to the preset reference. The first controller controls opening and closing of third flow path opening/closing unit and the fourth flow path opening/closing unit, when the signal indicates that the carbon dioxide content in the residual sub-stream of the fourth polymer separator from the first measuring sensor is less than 0.01 vol.% to less than 5 vol.%. The pressure ratio of the permeable portion to the residual portion of the fifth polymer separation membrane is adjusted 0.4-0.7.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device.
   Biogas supply unit (10)
   Compressing unit (41)
   Purification unit (50)
   First polymer separation membrane (51)
   Second polymer separation membrane (52)

AU ZHANG Y
   LIU Z
   QIN S
   MIAO L
   MAO H
   SUN Y
   YAN R
AE UNIV BEIJING FORESTRY (UYBF-C)
GA 201931006H
AB    NOVELTY - Use of Arthrobacter species in fixing atmospheric carbon dioxide and inducing formation of carbonaceous mineral, is claimed.
   USE - The strain is useful in fixing atmospheric carbon dioxide and inducing formation of inorganic carbonaceous mineral, preferably carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The strain reduces atmospheric greenhouse effect and alleviates global warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for biosynthesis of carbonaceous mineral, comprising inoculating Arthrobacter species into liquid culture medium, culturing for a period of time, separating and purifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The Arthrobacter species is Arthrobacter species 201806YC02 (CGMCC No.16019). The Arthrobacter species strain converts carbon source in culture medium and carbon dioxide in atmosphere to carbonaceous mineral, when cultured using organic carbon source containing culture medium. The liquid culture medium comprises 5 g/l yeast extract, 10 g/l peptone, 5 g/l sodium chloride and 2 g/l calcium chloride, where the pH of medium is 7.6. Preferred Method: The culturing is performed under dark at 25 degrees C. The liquid culture medium is placed in 250 ml triangular flask and the amount of liquid culture medium is 100-150 ml.

TI Culturing mesenchymal stem cell without exogenous            component comprises primary culturing, collecting cell            suspension, adding centrifugal tube to mix with            original cell suspension, centrifuging and removing            supernatant, adding sodium chloride injection            suspension cell precipitation and centrifuging.

   GE C

AE DARCELO SHANGHAI LIFE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DARC-Non-standard)
GA 2022973071
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing mesenchymal stem cell without                exogenous component comprises (a) primary culture                by (a1) cutting the left and right umbilical cord                tissue into culture dish, using hemostatic forceps                to extrude residual blood in umbilical cord,                cutting into a new culture dish filled with sodium                chloride injection, (a2) using sodium chloride                injection for cleaning Warpass rubber tissue, using                pasteurization suction pipe to absorb Huatong glue                tissue slurry in culture bottle, and uniformly                paving tissue on bottom of culture bottle by using                pipetting, (a3) adding primary culture medium into                the culture bottle, (b) absorbing more than half of                supernatant in culture bottle to centrifuge to                obtain centrifugal supernatant, (b2) adding                recombinant enzyme digestion cell into culture                bottle, (b3) transferring cell suspension to                centrifugal tube, adding sodium chloride injection                into culture bottle for cleaning, and (b4)                inoculating the uniformly mixed cell suspension to                culture bottle.
   USE - The method is useful for mesenchymal stem cell                culture method of without exogenous                component.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, shortens the sample                processing time, reduces the cost, ensures the                temperature and clinical safety of the culture                process, improves the stability and survival rate                of stem cell morphology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mesenchymal stem cell culture method of                without exogenous component comprising (a) primary                culture: (a1) cutting the 10 cm left and right                umbilical cord tissue into the culture dish, using                hemostatic forceps to extrude the residual blood in                the umbilical cord, cutting into a new culture dish                filled with sodium chloride injection, and peeling                the umbilical cord blood vessel and the outer film                obtain the Huatong glue tissue, (a2) using sodium                chloride injection for cleaning the Warpass rubber                tissue for 8-10 times, transferring into a                centrifugal tube, using ophthalmic scissors to cut                into small blocks with volume of 1-2 mm3, using the                pasteurization suction pipe to absorb the Huatong                glue tissue slurry in the culture bottle, and                uniformly paving the tissue on the bottom of the                culture bottle by using the pipetting, and (a3)                adding primary culture medium into the culture                bottle, slowly shaking the bottle body after                adding, the culture medium is completely covered on                the bottom surface, and placing the culture bottle                into 37.0&#176;C, and culture box to culture in                a 5.0% Carbon dioxide, (b) subculture (b1) to (a3)                more than 50% of tissue block around the cell and                the cell fusion degree reaches more than 80%,                absorbing more than half of supernatant in the                culture bottle to centrifuge to obtain centrifugal                supernatant, and removing the redundant culture                supernatant and tissue block and washing by sodium                chloride injection, (b2) adding recombinant enzyme                digestion cell into the culture bottle, adding                centrifugal supernatant to obtain cell suspension,                (b3) transferring the cell suspension to the                centrifugal tube, and adding sodium chloride                injection into the culture bottle for cleaning,                collecting the cell suspension, adding centrifugal                tube to mix with the original cell suspension,                centrifuging and removing the supernatant, adding                sodium chloride injection suspension cell                precipitation, centrifuging and removing the                supernatant again, adding subculture medium                re-suspending, and mixing uniformly, and (b4)                inoculating the uniformly mixed cell suspension to                the culture bottle, placing 37&#8451;, culture                box to culture in a 5.0% carbon dioxide and to                obtain-passage cell, when the fusion degree of the                cell to be passaged reaches more than 80%,                continuing subculture based on the method ant to                obtain the third generation cell.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                subculture of human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem                cell obtained by above method.

In the step (b3),                  the subculture medium is mesenchymal stem cell                  for serum-free culture medium.

PN CN114689742-A
TI Stable isotope fractionation of coffee bean            involves calcining green coffee bean to different            degrees to obtain pre-calcined, calcining moderately            and calcining heavily coffee bean, grinding green            coffee bean, lightly calcined coffee beans, medium            calcined coffee bean.
AU CHENG M
   ZHOU H
   WU H
   ZHANG A
   TIAN L
AE SHENZHEN POLYTECHNIC (SHPN-C)
GA 2022904276
AB    NOVELTY - Stable isotope fractionation of coffee bean                involves calcining green coffee bean to different                degrees to obtain pre-calcined, calcining                moderately calcining heavily coffee bean, grinding                green coffee bean, lightly calcined coffee beans,                medium calcined coffee bean and heavily calcined                coffee bean into powder, burning, reducing them at                a temperature of 1000-1100degrees Celsius,                converting them into carbon dioxide, nitrogen and                sulfur dioxide, dehydrating with anhydrous                magnesium perchlorate, performing chromatographic                column separation at a temperature of 65-75degrees                Celsius, entering the IRMS (stable isotope ratio                mass spectrometer) ion source to measure the                isotopic content of 15N, 13C and 34S element.
   USE - Method for stable isotope fractionation of                coffee bean.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be applied not only to green                coffee bean, but also to coffee bean of different                calcining degree, and the prediction accuracy can                reach 100%, whether it is for the sale market or                customs inspection, and it has very good assistance                to improve the market situation of counterfeiting                coffee bean.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Stable isotope fractionation of coffee bean                involves calcining green coffee bean to different                degrees to obtain pre-calcined, calcining                moderately calcining heavily coffee bean, grinding                green coffee bean, lightly calcined coffee beans,                medium calcined coffee bean and heavily calcined                coffee bean into powder, burning, reducing them at                a temperature of 1000-1100degrees Celsius,                converting them into carbon dioxide, nitrogen and                sulfur dioxide, dehydrating with anhydrous                magnesium perchlorate, performing chromatographic                column separation at a temperature of 65-75degrees                Celsius, entering the IRMS (stable isotope ratio                mass spectrometer) ion source to measure the                isotopic content of 15N, 13C and 34S element,                wrapping, drying green coffee powder, light                calcined coffee powder, medium calcined coffee                powder and heavily calcined coffee powder, standing                until it reached equilibrium with the atmospheric                pressure, decomposing coffee powder into hydrogen                and carbon monoxide at a high temperature of                1200-1400degrees Celsius, separating on a                chromatographic column at 80-90degrees Celsius,                enters the IRMS ion source to determine the                isotopic content of 18O and 2H, using inductively                coupled plasma optical emission spectroscopy                (ICP-OES) to measure the metal element content in                coffee bean, and performing above steps in no                particular order or simultaneously.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for simultaneously identifying the origin of                calcined and green coffee bean, which involves: (a)                establishing a database based on method of stable                isotope fractionation of coffee bean to determine                the content of isotopes and elements in raw,                moderately and heavily calcined coffee bean;
   (b) determining the element and content data                of green coffee beans from the same origin and                coffee beans with different roasting degree, and                train the data;
   (c) dividing randomly the data set into 2                portion, of which 70% of the total data set is used                as the training set, and 30% of the total data set                is used as the test set;(c)comparing the accuracy                of calculation and prediction of green coffee bean,                medium and dark calcined coffee bean;
   (d) classifying the data that reaches the                required accuracy rate as valid data in the trained                dataset, obtaining the element database of                different origins to construct a mathematical model                for simultaneously determining the origin of green                coffee bean and calcined coffee beans;
   (e) taking the coffee beans to be tested,                measuring stable isotopes and multi-elements in the                coffee bean with specific above method, inputting                the measured data into the discriminant                mathematical model, and outputting the                result.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The helium purge flow                  rate in the IRMS ion source is 170-180                  ml/minutes, and the reference gas flow rate is                  40-60 ml/minutes. The caramelization color value                  of the lightly calcined coffee bean is 85-90, the                  caramelization color value of the medium calcined                  coffee beans is 65-75, and the caramelization                  color value of the heavily calcined coffee bean                  is 40-50. The random forest tree method                  (Randomforest, RF) is used to train data and                  construct a discriminant model, where the random                  forest tree method ranks the importance of each                  element according to the average precision                  reduction rate, the importance of the elements is                  sorted from large to small with specific                  proportion of the content from more to less, the                  element importance level of the green coffee bean                  is sodium (Na), hydrogen (H2), aluminum (Al),                  nickel (Ni), manganese (Mn), barium (Ba),                  chromium (Cr), calcium (Ca), oxygen (O18),                  nitrogen (N15), copper (Cu), magnesium (Mg), zinc                  (Zn), iron (Fe), sulfur (S34), potassium (K) and                  carbon (C13) in descending order, the element                  levels of the heavily calcined coffee beans are,                  in descending order, Mn, H2, Na, Ni, Al, Ba, Cr,                  N15, Ca, Mg, Cu, Zn, O18, Fe, K, S34 and C13 the                  element importance levels of the heavily calcined                  coffee beans are Mn, H2, Ni, Ba, Na, Cr, Al, Ca,                  N15, O18, Zn, Cu, Mg, Fe, K, S34 and C13 in                  descending order, when the step of measuring                  metal element content in coffee bean is                  determined simultaneously, the element importance                  of coffee beans is Na, Ni, Al, Ba, Cr, Mn, O18,                  Ca, H2, N15, Cu, Fe, Zn, S34, Mg, K and C13 in                  descending order, and the element are introduced                  into the corresponding discriminant equation in                  order.

PN KR2339722-B1
TI Foam for shielding radon generated inside and outside building, is prepared using polyether-based polyol, bromine-based polyol, metal foaming catalyst, curing agent, filler, flame retardant e.g. bromine-based flame retardant, and foaming agent containing 1,1,1,3,3-pentafluoropropane and formic acid.
AU PARK J G
AE RPT INC (RPTR-Non-standard)
GA 2021F01294
AB    NOVELTY - A radon shielding foam is prepared using a composition comprising 100 pts. wt. polyether-based polyol, 20-80 pts. wt. bromine-based polyol, 0.05-10 pts. wt. metal foaming catalyst, 50-200 pts. wt. curing agent, 0.1-20 pts. wt. granular filler, 0.01-10 pts. wt. mixed flame retardant, and a foaming agent comprising a physical foaming agent which is carbon dioxide, 1,1,1,3,3-pentafluoropropane or a mixture comprising 1,1,1,3,3-pentafluorobutane and 1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane, and 0.01-10 pts. wt. chemical foaming agent comprising water and formic acid. The composition comprises 5-200 vol.% physical foaming agent. The mixed flame retardant comprises a bromine-based flame retardant, a phosphorus-based flame retardant and a chlorine-based flame retardant. The foam has a thickness of less than 3 mm, and includes a microstructure in which open cells with an average particle diameter of 200-800 mu m and closed cells with an average particle diameter of 150-600 mu m are mixed.
   USE - Foam e.g. polyurethane foam or polyurea foam (all claimed) is used for shielding radon generated inside and outside building. Uses include but are not limited to private house, apartment, school, hospital, subway, research institute and various public facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam has high skin density and low core density by controlling closed cell ratio, and has excellent tensile strength, compressive strength and radon shielding effect, even if the thickness is reduced to less than 3 mm compared to the existing foam installed with a thickness of 3-200 mm. The foam can be prepared by economical method, and can solve the problem of reduction of the internal space due to the existing thick foam. The thin foam minimizes damage to human body by implementing safe indoor environment by simply and effectively shielding radon generated inside and outside building.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A radon shielding foam is prepared using a composition comprising 100 pts. wt. polyether-based polyol, 20-80 pts. wt. bromine-based polyol, 0.05-10 pts. wt. metal foaming catalyst, 50-200 pts. wt. curing agent, 0.1-20 pts. wt. granular filler, 0.01-10 pts. wt. mixed flame retardant, and a foaming agent comprising a physical foaming agent which is carbon dioxide, 1,1,1,3,3-pentafluoropropane (HFC-245fa) or a mixture comprising 1,1,1,3,3-pentafluorobutane and 1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane (HFC-365/227) in a volume ratio of 1:0.1 to 5:0.1, and 0.01-10 pts. wt. chemical foaming agent comprising water and formic acid which are mixed in a weight ratio of 1:0.1-1.5. The composition comprises 5-200 vol.% physical foaming agent. The mixed flame retardant comprises a bromine-based flame retardant, a phosphorus-based flame retardant and a chlorine-based flame retardant in a ratio of 1:0.5-1:0.5-1. The foam has a thickness of less than 3 mm, and includes a microstructure in which open cells with an average particle diameter of 200-800 mu m and closed cells with an average particle diameter of 150-600 mu m are mixed. The foam has 95% or more closed cells with an average particle diameter of 300 mu m or less.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The granular filler includes spherical particles having an average particle diameter of 5 nm to 100 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The foam has a self-adhesive force of 5 N/cm2 or more measured according to ASTM D1623 (Type C sample). The foam has a radon blocking rate of 90% or more, when measuring radon emission concentration for 24 hours using a radon meter.

PT P
PN CN112661192-A; CN112661192-B
TI Manufacturing lanthanum manganite/cobalt oxide composite photocatalyst used for purification of high-concentration volatile air pollutants, involves preparing surface defect-rich cobalt oxide catalyst, stirring, calcining, and refluxing.
AU WANG X
   BURENBAYAER
   DING L
   YANG X
   MA Y
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA (UNMO-C)
GA 202148317A
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing lanthanum manganite (LaMnO3)/cobalt oxide (Co3O4) composite photocatalyst involves preparing surface defect-rich Co3O4 catalyst by precipitation method; adding 15 mL ultrapure water and 35 mL ethylene glycol solution into 100 mL polytetrafluoroethylene liner, stirring fully at room temperature for 30 minutes, and then adding 0.004 molar cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, and continuing at room temperature to stir for 30 minutes; weighing 9.6 g sodium hydroxide solid, slowly adding to upper beaker and fully dissolved, transferring to stainless steel autoclave, and hydrothermally reacting at 180 degrees C for 12 hours, drying at 75 degrees C for 10 hours, calcining precursor at 550 degrees C in air atmosphere in tube furnace for 5 hours to obtain final black Co3O4 powder; adding 0.003 molor lanthanum chloride hexahydrate and 0.003 molar manganese acetate tetrahydrate solid to 50 mL ultrapure aqueous solution, stirring at room temperature for 30 minutes until solid is completely melted.
   USE - Method for manufacturing LaMnO3/Co3O4 composite photocatalyst used for purification of indoor and outdoor high-concentration volatile air pollutants, such as toluene into harmless carbon dioxide and water (H2O) under drive of full-spectrum energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for manufacturing photocatalyst is low in cost, environment friendly, easy to be recycled and reused, and has high stability, which meets requirements of environmental purification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing lanthanum manganite (LaMnO3)/cobalt oxide (Co3O4) composite photocatalyst involves preparing surface defect-rich Co3O4 catalyst by precipitation method; adding 15 mL ultrapure water and 35 mL ethylene glycol solution into 100 mL polytetrafluoroethylene liner, stirring fully at room temperature for 30 minutes, and then adding 0.004 molar cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, and continuing at room temperature to stir for 30 minutes; weighing 9.6 g sodium hydroxide solid, slowly adding to upper beaker and fully dissolved, transferring to stainless steel autoclave, and hydrothermally reacting at 180 degrees C for 12 hours, drying at 75 degrees C for 10 hours, calcining precursor at 550 degrees C in air atmosphere in tube furnace for 5 hours to obtain final black Co3O4 powder; adding 0.003 molor lanthanum chloride hexahydrate and 0.003 molar manganese acetate tetrahydrate solid to 50 mL ultrapure aqueous solution, stirring at room temperature for 30 minutes until solid is completely melted, adding to beaker, weighing 9.6 g sodium hydroxide solid, slowly adding to beaker and fully dissolve, slowly adding to solution contains lanthanum and manganese ions, and continuing to stir for 1 hour before transferring to single-mouth circle in bottom flask, refluxing at 90 degrees C in oil bath until black product is formed, then continuing to reflux for 2 hours, drying, and preparing lanthanum manganate photocatalyst; dispersing cobalt tetroxide catalyst in 50 mL ultrapure water, and after being fully dispersed with aid of ultrasound, preparing LaMnO3/Co3O4 composites with different composite ratios according to synthesis method of lanthanum manganate photocatalyst; providing sunlight purification treatment of gaseous toluene; weighing 30 mg composite catalyst, using absolute ethanol to fully dispersed on watch glass, drying sufficiently to remove ethanol, and placing into reactor; heating reactor and each connecting pipe at 80 degrees C with heater to ensure toluene gaseous atmosphere; sending certain amount of gaseous toluene in reactor with air through gas distribution system, and built-in gas pump of photoacoustic infrared detector achieves purpose of gas circulation, where light source is 300 Watt xenon lamp with vertical irradiation mode from top to bottom; and detecting generated degradation concentration of toluene gas and amount of carbon dioxide (CO2) gas automatically by photoacoustic infrared detector and data is output every 10 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The cobalt nitrate hexahydrate ethylene glycol, absolute ethanol, lanthanum chloride hexahydrate, manganese acetate tetrahydrate, sodium hydroxide, and toluene solutions are all analytically pure. The volume-to-volume ratio of ethylene glycol is key to generation of oxygen defects in Co3O4 sample, and also key to regulation of morphology of Co3O4 sample, improves catalytic activity and adsorption performance of pure phase Co3O4 catalyst; and increase of reflux time enables to increase crystallinity of LaMnO3 sample, making more stable in catalytic oxidation reaction. The reaction conditions simulate real environment of indoor and outdoor volatile harmful gases. The reaction conditions of composite catalyst are simulating sunlight to oxidize high-concentration toluene into harmless carbon dioxide and water, degradation rate of toluene and rate of CO2 generation reached 100% and 94.33%, respectively. The two-way catalyst processing indoor gaseous toluene, which is configured to simulate indoor, and outdoor volatile gas environment, where initial concentration of toluene is 2800 ppm, and catalyst input, which is 30 mg; 300 Watt xenon lamp light source irradiates surface of catalyst vertically; providing 500 mL volume reaction vessel; maintaining temperature of reaction vessel, and gas pipeline, which is 80 degrees C; providing photoacoustic spectrometer to simultaneously detect volatile organic contents (VOCs) concentration, and CO2 generation amount, which is supplemented by built-in air circulation of photoacoustic spectrometer pump, and after 2 hours of light, degradation rate of toluene reached 100%, and generation rate of CO2, which is 94.3%.

PN CN112048544-A
TI Detection of carbonic anhydrase and alkaline phosphatase, by designing new structure of DNA nanodevices as probes and combining with catalytic reaction, and measuring carbonic anhydrase protease and alkaline phosphoprotease.
AU YANG Z
   QING M
   WU H
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV CHONGQING TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYSS-C)
GA 2020C4837L
AB    NOVELTY - Detection method of carbonic anhydrase and alkaline phosphatase related to growth of algae that are target protein based on self-catalytic properties of the target protease, involves designing new structure of DNA nanodevices as probes and combining with the catalytic reaction of the protease, converting the enzyme-catalyzed product into single-stranded DNA, realizing the detection of protease by the measurement of single-stranded DNA, and measuring carbonic anhydrase protease and alkaline phosphoprotease. The determination method of carbonic anhydride protease, involves designing equation of the catalytic reaction of carbonic anhydrase based on the catalytic reaction of carbonic anhydrase, designing DNA probe responsive to hydrogen ion as hydrogen ion capture probe, catalyzing carbonic anhydrase to conversion of carbon dioxide in the test substrate and using the electrochemical biosensor to indirectly measure the amount of carbonic anhydrase.
   USE - The method is useful for detection of carbonic anhydrase and alkaline phosphatase (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method detects carbonic anhydrase and alkaline phosphatase with high sensitivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detection method of carbonic anhydrase and alkaline phosphatase related to growth of algae that are target protein based on self-catalytic properties of the target protease, involves designing new structure of DNA nanodevices as probes and combining with the catalytic reaction of the protease, converting the enzyme-catalyzed product into single-stranded DNA, realizing the detection of protease by the measurement of single-stranded DNA, and measuring carbonic anhydrase protease and alkaline phosphoprotease. The determination method of carbonic anhydride protease, involves designing equation of the catalytic reaction of carbonic anhydrase based on the catalytic reaction of carbonic anhydrase, designing DNA probe responsive to hydrogen ion as hydrogen ion capture probe, catalyzing carbonic anhydrase to conversion of carbon dioxide in the test substrate into bicarbonate and produce hydrogen ion at the same time when carbonic anhydrase is present, stimulating the generated hydrogen ion with designed DNA nanoprobe, preparing DNA nanoprobe needle undergoing conformational change, releasing two DNA single strands RS1 and RS2 with partial sequences identical, thus converting the determination of carbonic anhydrase protease to the determination of single DNA strands, constructing the sensitive interface of the electrochemical biosensor based on the capture probe and enzyme-free DNA double-cycle amplification strategy, and using the electrochemical biosensor to indirectly measure the amount of carbonic anhydrase through the output electrochemical signal. The method for determining alkaline phosphoprotease involves designing equation of the hydrolysis reaction of alkaline phosphoprotease based on the hydrolysis reaction of alkaline phosphoprotease, selecting 5' end phosphate monoester of the single-stranded TS in the DNA nanoprobe as catalytic substrate of alkaline phosphatase, selecting 5' end of other single-stranded DNA in the DNA nanoprobe in advance phosphorylation treatment, when there is no alkaline phosphatase, generating T4 ligase by phosphodiesterase bond between TS and RS in the DNA structure, where S2 does not cause conformational changes in the DNA nanoprobe, and the TS and RS are two single-stranded DNAs in the presence of alkaline phosphatase, catalyzing alkaline phosphatase by hydrolysis of the 5'phosphate of RS to obtain dephosphorylated single-stranded DNA TS, undergoning corresponding DNA nanoprobe with conformational change in the presence of S2, releasing two single-stranded DNAs, TS and RS, successfully converting the measurement of alkaline phosphatase to the quantitative measurement of single-stranded DNA, constructing the sensitive interface of the electrochemical biosensor based on the capture probe and enzyme-free DNA double-cycle amplification strategy, and indirectly measuring amount of alkaline phosphatase by the size of the electrochemical signal output by the electrochemical biosensor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves measuring carbonic anhydride protease through explosive growth of algae, dissolving inorganic carbon in the water, dissolving dissolved inorganic carbon (DIC) to promote the algal cells to secrete a large amount of carbonic anhydrase to ensure the effective use of carbon dioxide and maintain a faster growth rate of the algae, catalyzing carbonic anhydrase with conversion of intracellular carbon dioxide to avoid the loss of carbon dioxide from the intracellular carbon dioxide during this catalytic reaction, and producing hydrogen ion as a by-product. The method involves designing capture probe based on the principle of the proximity touch reaction, hybridizing three single DNA strands with each other, utilizing codability of DNA bases and resulting DNA nanoprobe to endow the designed DNA nanoprobe with response to alkaline phosphatase/carbonic anhydrase catalytic products. The method involves designing different numbers of complementary bases in the DNA nanoprobe, using electrochemical method as an auxiliary means to optimize the DNA nanoprobe through acrylamide gel electrophoresis analysis, chemical calculation, atomic force microscope and projection electron microscope to characterize the DNA nanoprobe. The method involves electrodepositing the pre-processed glassy carbon electrode layer of gold nanoparticles on the electrode, using gold-sulfide bond to assemble a DNA nanoprobe labeled with an electroactive substance on the electrode surface as a DNA capture probe, which simultaneously captures the converted RS1 and RS2 are single-stranded, causing capture of a single DNA strand with conformational change of a DNA nanoprobe, which promotes the two molecules of electroactive substances to leave the electrode surface and realizing 1:2 conversion of input DNA and output electroactive substances. The method involves combining electrochemical biosensor with a toehold-mediated chain displacement reaction, simultaneously cycling the RS1 and RS2 single chains, increasing the conversion ratio of input DNA to output electroactive substances to 1:N, where N less than 2, and indirectly measuring amount of carbonic anhydrase by the size of the output electrochemical signal. The construction content of the sensitive interface of the electrochemical biosensor is the same as that of the electrochemical biosensor. The amount of alkaline phosphatase indirectly measured by the size of the output electrochemical signal is same as method in which the amount of carbonic anhydrase which is measured indirectly by the size of the output electrochemical signal.

PN CN111392861-A; CN111392861-B
TI Processing kitchen garbage and sewage by crushing kitchen garbage and sewage by crushing roller of processing device, separating and precipitating by filter fence, and anaerobic microorganisms hydrolyzing large molecule insoluble substances.
AU SHANG Z
AE LAIXI XINZHE ENG TECHNOLOGY SERVICE CENT (LAIX-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI LANCHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020667197
AB    NOVELTY - Processing kitchen garbage and sewage involves crushing kitchen garbage and sewage, separating and precipitating crushed solid garbage and waste water, settling in filter fence, clockwise rotating filter fence to burning box, burning, clockwise rotating to leave solid waste from filter fence, and counterclockwise rotating filter fence to purifying box; anaerobic microorganisms hydrolyzing insoluble substances of large molecules into soluble substances of small molecules, anaerobic microorganism absorbing, and primarily purifying sewage; and passing anaerobic preliminary treated sewage through aerobic layer of processing device, forming large surface area, reducing pollutants concentration, transporting external air into purifying box through air duct when passing through aerobic layer, adsorbing organic pollutants as nutrients through aerobic microorganisms, and converting into carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - The method is used for processing kitchen garbage and sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates solid waste and sewage, ensures that sewage and solid waste are separated before entering sewer, and solves blockage of sewers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing kitchen garbage and sewage involves:
   (A) crushing kitchen garbage and sewage by crushing roller of processing device at speed of 300-500 revolutions/minute (rpm), passing through filter fence, separating and precipitating crushed solid garbage and waste water, settling in filter fence for 1-5 minutes, clockwise rotating filter fence to burning box, burning for 5-10 minutes, clockwise rotating for certain angle to leave solid waste from filter fence, and counterclockwise rotating filter fence to purifying box;
   (B) passing filtered sewage through anaerobic layer of processing device, anaerobic microorganisms hydrolyzing insoluble substances of large molecules into soluble substances of small molecules, anaerobic microorganism absorbing, absorbing portion of organic pollutants in water for growth, and primarily purifying sewage; and
   (C) passing anaerobic preliminary treated sewage through aerobic layer of processing device, forming large surface area during growth and reproduction of aerobic microorganisms on filler, reducing pollutants concentration by higher concentration of bio-membrane that can absorb most of organic pollutants in water, air inlet fan transporting external air into purifying box through air duct when passing through aerobic layer, adsorbing organic pollutants as nutrients through aerobic microorganisms, and converting into carbon dioxide and water.
   The processing device comprises water inlet pipe. The water inlet pipe is fixedly connected with crushing box. The crushing box is movably connected with crushing roller two ends. The crushing box is fixedly connected with gear box at left side. The crushing roller close to gear box is fixedly connected with first gear. The first gear is meshed with second gear. The second gear is fixedly connected with left side of motor. The motor close to right side is fixedly connected with gear box. The crushing box close to crushing roller is fixedly connected with inclined plate. The bottom of crushing box is fixedly connected to left side of bottom of purifying box. The crushing box is close to left of movable connection filter fence. The purifying box is close to filter fence bottom. The purifying box close to anaerobic layer is fixedly connected with bottom of aerobic layer. The right side of purifying box close to left side filter fence is fixedly connected to burning box. The bottom of bottom of burning box is fixedly connected with gas cylinder. The burning box upper portion is fixedly connected with nozzle. The burning box is fixedly connected with discharging port. The bottom of purifying box is fixedly connected with supporting column top. The bottom of purifying box close to supporting column is fixedly connected with water outlet pipe. The purifying box is fixedly connected to left side of air inlet fan. The air inlet fan is fixedly connected with wind pipe end. The air duct other end is fixedly connected to purifying box.

PN CN110934797-A
TI Preparing perfume useful for e.g. soothing sleep, by planting pre-treated lavender seeds on plot, spraying e.g. magnesium fertilizer, mixing oil mixture with anhydrous ethanol, filtering and filling with ultrafiltration membrane.
AU WANG Q
   QIANG G
   TAO S
AE WUHU MAOWANG AROMATIC PLANT TECHNOLOGY (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020264918
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing lavender soothing sleep perfume, involves leveling and deep turning the plot, backfilling, adding pentachloronitrobenzene, leveling soil surface, soaking the lavender seeds in water bath, adding plant leaf extract to the water, soaking to complete seed pretreatment, planting the pre-treated lavender seeds on the plot after raking, performing conventional planting, spraying magnesium fertilizer with foliar concentration, zinc fertilizer every other week and manganese fertilizer, sterilizing once a month, carbendazim and chlorothalonil, selecting manual harvesting on a sunny day when 10% of the lavender flowers are bloomed, extracting the lavender for four times using a four-stage extraction method to obtain lavender pure dew, mixing lavender hydrosol, chamomile hydrosol, lily hydrosol, linden hydrosol, marjoram hydrosol, and elderflower hydrosol, and mixing absolute oil mixture with anhydrous ethanol, filtering and filling with ultrafiltration membrane.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lavender soothing sleep perfume (claimed) which is used for calming nerves.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing lavender soothing sleep perfume, involves leveling and deep turning the plot, where the depth of deep turning is 10-15 cm, and the mass ratio of deep turned soil to humus leaves, machine-made sand and wood chips is 8:2.3-2.6:0.8-1.2:0.8-1.2, backfilling, adding 3.5-4 kg pentachloronitrobenzene on the plot to be planted, leveling soil surface at 4-5 cm, soaking the lavender seeds in water bath of 15-25 degrees C for 3 hours, adding plant leaf extract to the water, soaking for 2 hours to complete seed pretreatment, where the amount of the plant leaf extract is 10-15% of clean water quality, planting the pre-treated lavender seeds on the plot after raking, where the plant spacing is 50-60 cm and row spacing is 80-100 cm, performing conventional planting, when more than 60% of lavender seedlings show bud stage or flowering period, spraying magnesium fertilizer with 0.1-0.2% foliar concentration, 0.1-0.2% zinc fertilizer every other week and 0.1-0.2% manganese fertilizer, sterilizing once a month, carbendazim and chlorothalonil, selecting manual harvesting on a sunny day when 10% of the lavender flowers are bloomed, extracting the lavender for four times using a four-stage extraction method to obtain lavender pure dew, where the specific operation of the four-time extraction method is as follows: entering lavender raw material into first-level extraction kettle and feed carbon dioxide into the first-stage extraction kettle, adding the raw materials into second-stage extraction kettle after separation I and separation II, transferring the raw materials into rectification column after separation I and separation II, further transferring the raw materials into three-stage extraction kettle, adding carbon dioxide to the three-stage extraction kettle, rectifying through rectification column, separation I and separation II, entering the four-stage extraction kettle, separating through separation I, rectification column, separating II to obtain lavender hydrosol, mixing lavender hydrosol, chamomile hydrosol, lily hydrosol, linden hydrosol, marjoram hydrosol, and elderflower hydrosol in mass ratio of 15:1.6-2:3-5:0.8-1:1.1-1.3:1.4-1.7 to obtain essential oil mixture, and mixing absolute oil mixture with anhydrous ethanol in ratio of 1:9-9.5, stirring the mixture in dark for several days, filtering and filling with ultrafiltration membrane to obtain lavender soothing sleep perfume.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The plant leaf extract is prepared by mixing residual lavender leaves and clove leaves in ratio of 10:3-4, sequentially extracting with water and ethanol-water mixture to obtain filter residue and filtrate, obtaining filtrates obtained from the two extractions are combined, and obtaining plant leaf extract by concentration under reduced pressure. Preferred Method: The method involves mixing water with lavender residual leaves and clove leaves for 6-8 times, where the extraction time is 1-1.5 hours, the ethanol-water mixture comprises anhydrous ethanol and water in mass ratio of 8-10:3, and extracting ethanol-water mixture when the ethanol-water mixture is extracted. The microbial inoculant comprises Bacillus subtilis and B.licheniformis and the number of effective viable bacteria is 200 million/g. The ratio of lavender hydrosol, chamomile hydrosol, lily hydrosol, linden hydrosol, marjoram hydrosol, and elderflower hydrosol is 15:1.8:4:0.8:1.2:1.5.
   ACTIVITY - Hypnotic; Neuroprotective.

PN CN110680024-A; CN110680024-B
TI Preparing fructus cannabis electronic tobacco shreds useful for quitting smoking, by processing fructus cannabis, extracting volatile oil, preparing electronic tobacco embryo and synthesizing electronic tobacco shreds.
AU GUO C
   ZHANG W
   ZHAO J
   LI G
   HUANG W
   GUO M
   SHAO W
AE HEILONGJIANG HEIKE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2020075075
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing fructus cannabis electronic tobacco shreds involves (a) processing fructus cannabis by sterilizing fructus cannabis by UV lamp for 30-35 minutes, performing microwave treatment, rinsing with water, drying and baking to obtain detoxified roasted hemp kernels, mixing with water, beating, adjusting solid content of slurry with a vacuum rotary evaporator to solid concentration of material and liquid, adding emulsifier to feed liquid, stirring uniformly, emulsifying with a shearer, using two-stage pressure homogenizer to homogenize emulsion and spray-drying the emulsion using a pressure spray dryer, (b) extracting volatile oil, (c) preparing electronic tobacco embryo, and (d) synthesizing electronic tobacco shreds by soaking embryo in volatile oil for 48-96 hours, washing, adding 0.3-1.2 wt.% cellulase, reacting for 3-6 hours, adding 7-19% ethanol solution, immersing at 20-25 degrees C for 15-17 hours, subjecting to solid-liquid separation and drying at 39 degrees C for 15 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fructus cannabis electronic tobacco shreds for preparing cartridge (claimed) for quitting smoking.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly. The prepared electronic tobacco shreds have unique taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing fructus cannabis electronic tobacco shreds involves (a) processing fructus cannabis by sterilizing fructus cannabis by UV lamp for 30-35 minutes, performing microwave treatment, rinsing with water, drying and baking to obtain detoxified roasted hemp kernels, mixing with water, beating, adjusting solid content of slurry with a vacuum rotary evaporator to solid concentration of material and liquid, adding emulsifier to feed liquid, stirring uniformly, emulsifying with a shearer, using two-stage pressure homogenizer to homogenize emulsion and spray-drying the emulsion using a pressure spray dryer, (b) extracting volatile oil by weighing 1 kg refined powder, crushing, passing through 1000 mesh sieve, adding into sample kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, extracting at 25 MPa, 50 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate of 30 l/hour, performing first analysis at 55 degrees C and 9 MPa, and then at 6 MPa and 60 degrees C, combining resolved oil, degreasing and subjecting to dehydration treatment, (c) preparing electronic tobacco embryo by weighing 40-50 pts. wt. stem and 40-60 pts. wt. leaves modified and detoxified by microwave, mixing with 50-70 degrees C water in ratio of 4-12 pts. wt.:300-600 pts. wt., washing for 60-75 minutes, subjecting to solid-liquid separation, drying the solid phase, mixing the solid phase with water in weight ratio of 1:150-400, cutting using fiber cutter to obtain slurry with beating degree of 30-35 degrees SR, adding 3-4 pts. wt. adhesive, 15-20 pts. wt. smoking agent, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. diammonium hydrogen phosphate and 1 pt. wt. seed powder to 100 pts. wt. slurry, stirring for 25 minutes, vacuum degassing, spreading on steel belt, air-drying at 40 degrees C, forming pulp into flakes, drying the flakes at 65 degrees C until moisture content reaches 10% of total weight of flakes, cooling to room temperature, regaining moisture to 13.5% of total weight, and cutting into 0.8-25 mm electronic tobacco embryo using cutting machine, and (d) synthesizing electronic tobacco shreds by soaking embryo in volatile oil for 48-96 hours, washing, adding 0.3-1.2 wt.% cellulase, reacting for 3-6 hours, adding 7-19% ethanol solution, immersing at 20-25 degrees C for 15-17 hours, subjecting to solid-liquid separation and drying at 39 degrees C for 15 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for electronic cigarette cartridge, which is produced by winding the tobacco shreds and compressing the shreds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The stem and leaves are modified by weighing 40-50 stems, 40-60 leaves and 6-15 modifier, soaking at room temperature for 30 minutes, heating to 50-55 degrees C, adding 3-5% 15-20% cobalamin to modifier, adding to vacuum reactor, subjecting to vacuum immersion treatment at 50-60 degrees C for 60 minutes, filtering, washing using deionized water to a pH of 7-7.5 and drying to constant weight. The modifier comprises Acidothermus cellulolyticus, Bacillus subtilis, B.stearothermophilus in weight ratio of 1-3:2:2-4, 0.5-1% sucrose, 0.5-1% peptone and water. The microwave treatment is performed at 4.375 w/g, 50 degrees C and water vapor flow of 2 m/second for 6 minutes. The adhesive is chosen from one of guar gum, chitosan, carboxymethyl cellulose, modified starch, sodium alginate or shellac. The smoking agent is chosen from one of propylene glycol, glycerol, glyceryl monoacetate, glyceryl diacetate or glyceryl triacetate.
   ACTIVITY - Antismoking.

PT P
PN CN110592606-A
TI Preparing gold-iron nano-alloy catalyst useful for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide comprises adding surfactant to high boiling point organic solvent, adding e.g. gold salt compound, adding nanocarbon material.
AU WANG Z
   SUN K
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 2019A75483
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a gold-iron nano-alloy catalyst comprises (a) adding the surfactant to a high boiling point organic solvent, stirring until completely dissolved to obtain a surfactant solution, (b) adding a gold salt compound, an iron salt compound and a reducing agent to surfactant solution, stirring, mixing under 30-80 degrees C and argon atmosphere to obtain a mixture, (c) heating the mixture to 250-290 degrees C in argon atmosphere, maintaining the temperature at 250-290 degrees C in argon atmosphere for 0.5-3 hours, cooling to room temperature to obtain a reaction product, adding ethanol to reaction product, centrifuging to remove the supernatant to obtain a solid product, (d) washing the solid product 2-5 times with ethanol-n-hexane mixed solution to obtain washed product, dispersing washed product in n-hexane to obtain a dispersion and (e) adding nano-carbon material to dispersion, ultrasonically mixing, centrifugally separating, removing supernatant to obtain a solid product and drying.
   USE - The catalyst is used as a raw material for preparing a working electrode for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the amount of gold and the cost of industrial application of gold-based catalysts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a gold-iron nano-alloy catalyst comprises (a) adding the surfactant to a high boiling point organic solvent, stirring until completely dissolved to obtain a surfactant solution, where the volume ratio of the mass of the surfactant to the high boiling point organic solvent is (0.006-0.6) g:(1-100) ml, (b) adding a gold salt compound, an iron salt compound and a reducing agent to the surfactant solution, stirring, mixing under 30-80 degrees C and an argon atmosphere to obtain a mixture, where the mass ratio of the mass of the gold salt compound to the volume of the surfactant solution is (0.0093-0.93) g: (1-100) ml, the mass ratio of the iron salt compound to the volume of the surfactant solution is (0.0088-0.88) g: (1-100) ml, the mass ratio of the reducing agent to the volume of the surfactant solution is (0.064-0.64) g: (1-100) ml and the reducing agent is an alcohol compound, (c) heating the mixture to 250-290 degrees C in argon atmosphere, maintaining the temperature at 250-290 degrees C in argon atmosphere for 0.5-3 hours, cooling to room temperature to obtain a reaction product, adding ethanol to the reaction product, centrifuging to remove the supernatant to obtain a solid product, where the volume ratio of the reaction product to ethanol is 1:1-3, (d) washing the solid product 2-5 times with the ethanol-n-hexane mixed solution to obtain the washed product;, dispersing the washed product in n-hexane to obtain a dispersion, where the mass ratio of the washed product to the volume ratio of n-hexane is (0.0028-0.985) g: (5-500) ml and (e) adding nano-carbon material to the dispersion, ultrasonically mixing, centrifugally separating, removing the supernatant to obtain a solid product, drying in a vacuum drying box to obtain gold-iron nano-alloy catalyst, where the mass ratio of the nano-carbon material to the volume of the dispersion is (0.84-84) mg: (1-100) ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of ethanol to n-hexane in the ethanol-n-hexane mixture is 1:2. Preferred Components: The surfactant is at least one of oleylamine, oleic acid and polyvinylpolypyrrolidone. The high-boiling organic solvent is octyl ether or octadecene. The gold salt compound is gold acetate or chloroauric acid tetrahydrate. The iron salt compound is iron acetylacetonate or iron chloride. The alcohol compound is 1,2-hexadecanediol, ethylene glycol or glycerol. Preferred Method: In the step (a), adding the surfactant to high-boiling organic solvent and stirring at 300-800 revolutions/minutes for 10-20 minutes to obtain a surfactant solution. In the step (b), stirring is at 500-1200 revolutions/minutes for 30-120 minutes. In the step (c), increasing the temperature of the mixture at 3-6 degrees C/ in argon atmosphere and centrifuging at 8000-15000 revolutions/minutes for 1-5 minute and removing the supernatant to obtain the solid product. In the step (e), adding the nano-carbon material to the dispersion, ultrasonically mixing for 20-40 minutes, centrifuging at 8000-15000 revolutions/minutes, removing the supernatant to obtain the solid product, drying in vacuum drying box at 150-200 degrees C for 8-24 hours.
