PN CN110054169-A
TI Preparing long-acting magnetic mesoporous carbon comprises e.g. providing cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to water, stirring, filtering and calcining to obtain porous silica, mixing porous silica and mixed treatment liquid and filtering.
AU WU P
AE WU P (WUPP-Individual)
GA 201966978L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing long-acting magnetic mesoporous carbon comprises e.g. providing the ratio of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to water, stirring, filtering and calcining to obtain porous silica, mixing porous silica and mixed treatment liquid, filtering, washing to obtain a pretreated porous silica, mixing, filtering, washing and drying to obtain pre-modified silica, mixing pre-modified silica and polyallylamine hydrochloride solution, filtering to obtain filter cake, mixing, stirring and filtering to obtain pre-treatment cake, filtering to obtain a pre-modified filter cake, adding pre-modified filter cake mass of hydrochloric acid, stirring the reaction under a carbon dioxide atmosphere, filtering and drying to obtain modified filter cake, mixing modified filter cake and ethylene glycol, adding modified filter cake with sodium acetate and modified cake, stirring, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain a modified silica.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing long-acting magnetic mesoporous carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good magnetic properties, and has broad prospects in development of the porous material technology industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing long-acting magnetic mesoporous carbon comprises (1) providing the ratio of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to water is 1:15-1:18, and the amount of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide is 5-7 times of aqueous ammonia, where a mixture of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide having a mass of 2-3 times of orthosilicate, stirring, filtering and calcining to obtain porous silica, (2) mixing porous silica and mixed treatment liquid at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:12, filtering, washing to obtain a pretreated porous silica, where the pretreated porous silica and aqueous ammonia mixture are mass ratio 1:6-1:10, mixing, filtering, washing and drying to obtain pre-modified silica, (3) mixing pre-modified silica and polyallylamine hydrochloride solution at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:15, filtering to obtain filter cake, where the mass ratio of filter cake to ferric acid solution is 1:5-1:12, mixing, stirring and filtering to obtain pre-treatment cake, where the pretreatment filter cake and ferric acid are mixed at a mass ratio of 1:5-1:8, filtering to obtain a pre-modified filter cake, where the pre-modified filter cake and ethanol are mixed at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:12, adding the pre-modified filter cake mass of 1-2 times of hydrochloric acid, stirring the reaction under a carbon dioxide atmosphere, filtering and drying to obtain modified filter cake, (4) mixing modified filter cake and ethylene glycol at a mass ratio of 1:10-1:20, adding modified filter cake with 2-3 times of sodium acetate and modified cake has a mass of 0.4-0.5 times of polyethylene glycol, stirring the reaction, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain a modified silica and (5) mixing modified silica with water at a mass ratio of 1:5-1:10, adding a concentrated sulfuric acid mixture having a mass of the modified silica of 0.2-0.3 times, stirring the reaction, concentrating by rotary evaporation, heating to obtain a concentrate, mixing the concentrate with water at a mass ratio of 1:3-1:6, adding a concentrated sulfuric acid mixture with a mass of 0.2-0.3 times of the concentrate, stirring the reaction, concentrating by rotary evaporation to obtain pretreated concentrate, and calcining the pretreated concentrate, mixing long-acting magnetic mesoporous carbon blank with long-acting magnetic mesoporous carbon billet and sodium hydroxide solution by a mass ratio of 1:10-1:15, stirring the reaction, filtering, washing and drying to obtain long-acting magnetic mesoporous carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1) the tetraethyl orthosilicate mixture is prepared by mixing ethyl orthosilicate and absolute ethanol in a mass ratio of 5:1-5:2 to obtain a mixture of tetraethyl orthosilicate. In the step (2) the mixed treatment liquid is obtained by mixing concentrated sulfuric acid having a mass fraction of 95-98% with a mass fraction of 20-30% hydrogen peroxide at a volume ratio of 7:3 to obtain a mixed treatment liquid; the aqueous ammonia mixture is obtained by mixing aqueous ammonia having a mass fraction of 20-26% with hydrogen peroxide having a mass fraction of 20-25% by a volume ratio of 3:1 to obtain aqueous ammonia mixture. In the step (4) the polyethylene glycol is a polyethylene glycol having a molecular weight of 10000-12000; the concentrated sulfuric acid mixture is obtained by mixing sulfuric acid and sucrose having a mass fraction of 90-95% by mass ratio of 3:1-4:1 to obtain a concentrated sulfuric acid mixture; calcination temperature at 750-900 degrees C.

PN CN109868507-A
TI Preparing TYGG magneto-optical crystal involves            calculating, weighing and mixing terbium oxide, gallium            oxide and yttrium oxide, calcining, pressing powder            calcined into polycrystal block material, putting seed            crystal.
AU ZENG F
   JIN W
   LI C
   LIN H
   LIU L
   ZHOU Y
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYCY-C)
GA 201954499E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing TYGG magneto-optical crystal                involves calculating, weighing and mixing terbium                oxide with purity greater than or equal to 99.999%,                gallium oxide and yttrium oxide, calcining for 8                hours at 1200-1300 degrees C, pressing powder                calcined into polycrystal block material of 100x 20                millimeter, putting seed crystal into desired seed                crystal rod, putting pressed and formed polycrystal                block material into 120x 100 millimeter iridium                crucible, adjusting concentricity of coil, heat                insulation system, seed crystal rod, quartz                cylinder, iridium crucible, and vacuumizing, when                furnace vacuum degree reaches 8-10 pascal, slowly                filling 80% nitrogen + 20% carbon dioxide mixed gas                with purity greater than or equal to 99%, starting                intermediate-frequency induction crystal pulling                furnace, so that heating system in hearth starts to                work, after raw material in crucible iridium is                completely melt, melt flow line is clear and                stable, slowly adding seed crystal, contacting seed                crystal with surface of melt.
   USE - Method for preparing TYGG magneto-optical                crystal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare TYGG                magneto-optical crystal that is stable, has good                integrity, excellent magneto-optical performance                and wide application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing TYGG magneto-optical crystal                involves calculating, weighing and mixing terbium                oxide with purity greater than or equal to 99.999%,                gallium oxide and yttrium oxide, calcining for 8                hours at 1200-1300 degrees C, pressing powder                calcined into polycrystal block material of 100x 20                millimeter, putting seed crystal into desired seed                crystal rod, putting pressed and formed polycrystal                block material into 120x 100 millimeter iridium                crucible, adjusting concentricity of coil, heat                insulation system, seed crystal rod, quartz                cylinder, iridium crucible, and vacuumizing, when                furnace vacuum degree reaches 8-10 pascal, slowly                filling 80% nitrogen + 20% carbon dioxide mixed gas                with purity greater than or equal to 99%, starting                intermediate-frequency induction crystal pulling                furnace, so that heating system in hearth starts to                work, after raw material in crucible iridium is                completely melt, melt flow line is clear and                stable, slowly adding seed crystal, contacting seed                crystal with surface of melt, performing necking                technique, reducing diameter of seed crystal by 1-2                millimeter, adjusting temperature, starting crystal                growth, crystal growing process mainly includes 4                stages, shouldering, pulling speed when shouldering                is 0.6-0.8 millimeter/hour, crystal rotating rate                is 13-15 revolution per minute, shouldering angle                is controlled at 35-45 degrees C, when size of                crystal shouldering place and target size are                substantially equal, starting constant temperature,                growing at equal diameter, keeping cooling speed,                pulling speed and rotating speed unchanged, along                with reduction of S-L interface, increasing of                crucible radiation. The crystal size is thinned,                adjust temperature to keep crystal grow at equal                diameter, pulling speed in TYGG crystal                equal-diameter growth is 0.8-1.2 millimeter/hour,                rotating speed is 10-12 revolution per minute, when                crystal grows to certain length, enter ending                stage, ending is just opposite to shouldering                process, ensure integrity of crystal, gradually                increase temperature, thinned crystal, reduced                pulling and rotating speed to 0, end crystal                growth, reduce internal stress of grown crystal and                avoid crystal cracking which leads to crystal                growth failure, use slower cooling rate and longer                annealing time to reduce internal stress as much as                possible, take out crystal, reduce internal stress                of crystal growth, avoid crystal cracking and                crystal growth failure, adopt slower cooling rate                and longer annealing time to reduce internal stress                of crystal as much as possible, and finally take                out crystal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The raw material                  purity is more than 99.999%. The prepared TYGG                  crystal is new type magneto-optical crystal. The                  inert gas is nitrogen+ carbon dioxide mixed gas                  with purity greater than or equal to 99%.

PN CN109845577-A; CN109845577-B
TI Method for cultivating Morchella secondary nutrition-free bag, involves preparing culture package of mushroom package is kernel, wood chips, rice, soil, gypsum and light calcium carbonate and uniformly stirring culture material with water.
AU LUO X
   LI R
   ZHAO Q
   ZHANG J
   LU Q
   ZI L
   CHEN M
   LUO P
AE YUNNAN MUSHROOM WORLD BIO-TECHNOLOGY CO (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2019534860
AB    NOVELTY - A Morchella secondary nutrition-free bag cultivating method involves preparing a culture package of the mushroom package is 50% kernel, 12% wood chips, 17% rice, 18% soil, 1% gypsum, 1% light calcium carbonate, 0.5% sugar and 0.5% potassium dihydrogen phosphate. The culture material is uniformly stirred with water and the water content of the culture material is controlled to 38-42%, and bagged into a cylindrical bacteria bag with a high-temperature resistant polypropylene plastic bag, where the plastic bag is a long bag with a size of 150 mmx 550 mm and the wet weight of each bag is1.5 kg and sterilized at 121-123 degrees C, 0.15 MPa pressure for 3 hours. The Morchella inoculation is carried out, the inoculation method is inoculated in the middle of the bacteria bag and sealed. The inoculated bacteria bag is placed in the culture room at 17-19 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 500-2500 ppm, the humidity is 50-60%, and the dark culture is carried out for 10 days.
   USE - Method for cultivating Morchella secondary nutrition-free bag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating Morchella secondary nutrition-free bag with rich nutrition, in simple, rapid and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A Morchella secondary nutrition-free bag cultivating method involves preparing a culture package of the mushroom package is 50% kernel, 12% wood chips, 17% rice, 18% soil, 1% gypsum, 1% light calcium carbonate, 0.5% sugar and 0.5% potassium dihydrogen phosphate. The culture material is uniformly stirred with water and the water content of the culture material is controlled to 38-42%, and bagged into a cylindrical bacteria bag with a high-temperature resistant polypropylene plastic bag, where the plastic bag is a long bag with a size of 150 mmx 550 mm and the wet weight of each bag is1.5 kg and sterilized at 121-123 degrees C, 0.15 MPa pressure for 3 hours. The Morchella inoculation is carried out, the inoculation method is inoculated in the middle of the bacteria bag and sealed. The inoculated bacteria bag is placed in the culture room at 17-19 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 500-2500 ppm, the humidity is 50-60%, and the dark culture is carried out for 10 days. Greenhouse is constructed and the greenhouse is covered with a 6-pin shade net, the pre-treatment of the land is to clean the ground and removed the debris and weeds, during the tillage process, a thin layer of lime is disinfected on the ground, and the sun is exposed for 3 days. The soil preparation is performed by using a lime powder with a width of 0.8 m, a spacing of 0.3 m between the adjacent carriage surface compartment surface and dig a trench has a depth of 0.3 m between adjacent carcasses. The bacteria bag of the cultured species cut along the middle inoculation position along the section, the cut surface of the bacteria bag is placed downward and close to the carcass to allow the hyphae to fully contact the soil. Two rows of cut bacteria bags are placed on each side of the car, and the adjacent bacteria bags are separated by 30 cm, covered the black mulch after the bacteria package is placed, during the mushrooming period, the soil temperature is controlled at 3-18 degrees C, and the soil moisture is 50-65%, the air temperature at 5-20 degrees C, the air humidity is 80-95% and the ventilation in the greenhouse is strengthened to prevent mold, disease and deformed mushrooms.

TI Separation and purification of product from high            pressure system during conversion of carbonaceous            materials e.g. biomass, involves cooling converted feed            mixture at preset temperature, and recovering organic            compounds from aqueous phase.
AU IVERSEN S B
   RODRIGUEZ G J K

GA 201942210W
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and purification of products from                high pressure processing system involves converting                (2) feed mixture comprising carbonaceous                material(s) at operating conditions in presence of                homogeneous catalyst in form of potassium and/or                sodium and liquid organic compounds for producing                converted feed mixture which is cooling (2) to                50-250 degrees C and depressurizing (2) to at 1-150                bar, separating (3) into gas phase comprising                carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and methane, oil phase                comprising liquid organic compounds and aqueous                phase comprising liquid organic compound, dissolved                salts and optionally suspended particles, at least                partly recovering the aqueous phase liquid organic                compounds and dissolved homogeneous catalyst to                produce aqueous phase stream (I) and (II),                recycling the aqueous phase (I) to feed mixture to                providing portion(s) of liquid organic component                and homogeneous catalyst in feed mixture and                discharging bleed stream from water which is prior                to recycling of recycle stream (S1).
   USE - Separation and purification of products from                high pressure processing system for conversion of                carbonaceous materials e.g. woody biomass, wood                chips, saw dust, forestry thinning, road cuttings,                bark, branches, garden and park wastes and weeds,                grasses, straw, stems, stover, husk, cobs and                shells from e.g. wheat, rye, corn rice, sunflowers,                empty fruit bunches from palm oil production, palm                oil manufacturers effluent, residues from sugar                production such as bagasse, vinasse, molasses,                greenhouse wastes, energy crops like coppice,                willow, Miscanthus, switch grass, Sorghum,                Jatropha, macroalgae, microalgae, cyano bacteria,                animal beddings and manures such as fiber fraction                from livestock production, municipal and industrial                waste streams such as black liquor, paper sludge,                off-spec fibers from pulp and paper production,                residues and byproducts from food production such                as juice or wine production, vegetable oil                production, sorted municipal solid waste, source                sorted household wastes, restaurant wastes,                slaughter house waste, sewage sludge, and/or                plastics (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and effective                separation and purification of products with high                oil yield, high energy efficiency, excellent                recovery and recirculation rate of homogeneous                catalyst and liquid organic compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation and purification of products from                high pressure processing system involves processing                feed mixture comprising carbonaceous material(s) at                pressure of 150-400 bar and at 300-430 degrees C in                presence of homogeneous catalyst in form of 0.5                wt.% potassium and/or sodium and 5-40 wt.% liquid                organic compounds, thereby producing converted feed                mixture which is cooled at 50-250 degrees C and                depressurized at 1-150 bar, separating converted                feed mixture into gas phase comprising carbon                dioxide, hydrogen, and methane, oil phase                comprising liquid organic compounds and aqueous                phase comprising aqueous phase liquid organic                compounds, dissolved salts and optionally suspended                particles, at least partly recovering aqueous phase                liquid organic compounds and dissolved homogeneous                salts in form of potassium and/or sodium from                aqueous phase, thereby producing aqueous phase                stream (I) which is enriched in aqueous phase                liquid organic compounds and dissolved homogeneous                catalyst and aqueous phase stream (II) which are                depleted in aqueous phase liquid organic compounds                and dissolved homogeneous catalyst, at least partly                recycling the aqueous phase (I) to feed mixture to                providing at least portion of liquid organic                components and homogeneous catalyst in feed mixture                and discharging bleed stream from aqueous phase                which is enriched in liquid organic compounds and                homogeneous catalyst in form of potassium and                sodium which is prior to recycling of recycle                stream to feed mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of                continuous high pressure process.
   Preparing feed (1)
   Converting feed mixture (2)
   Cooling and expanding (3)
   Separating feed mixture (4)
   Upgrading (5)

PN CN109652271-A
TI Preparing health care wine involves picking fresh,            mature, non-rotating jujube fruit and washing with            water, peeling fruit, mixing disinfected water, yellow            bell wood flower, fruit and rock sugar into glass            bottle, and sealing.
AU LIN D
   HUANG Z
AE LIN D (LIND-Individual)
GA 201939357A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing health care wine involves picking                fresh, mature, non-rotating jujube fruit and                washing with water, peeling fruit, mixing                disinfected water, yellow bell wood flower, fruit                and rock sugar into glass bottle, and sealing the                fermentation, stirrring once every 10 days to mix,                after 3 months, all the flesh float up, water                clear, opening the seal, and using the principle of                siphon to suck the wine into another container for                storage. The core of slurry in the original bottle                is presased to remove the core, and the extruded                juice is stored together with the wine. The core is                finely crushed, and then enzymatic hydrolysis is                added to enzymatic hydrolysis for 2-5 hours, and                then placed in an extractant, and the extraction                solution is obtained by microwave-assisted                extraction for 2-3 times. The extracted core is                placed in a supercritical extraction kettle, and                supercritical carbon dioxide is extracted to obtain                a core oil.
   USE - Method used for preparing health care wine                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts a natural plant                bacteriostatic agent for sterilization before                fermentation, and has good sterilization effect,                and extracts the core active ingredient by using an                extracting agent with high dissolution extraction                effect, thus improves the utilization value of                core.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing health care wine involves picking                fresh, mature, non-rotating jujube fruit and                washing with water, peeling fruit, mixing                disinfected water, yellow bell wood flower, fruit                and rock sugar into glass bottle, and sealing the                fermentation, stirrring once every 10 days to mix,                after 3 months, all the flesh float up, water                clear, opening the seal, and using the principle of                siphon to suck the wine into another container for                storage. The core of slurry in the original bottle                is presased to remove the core, and the extruded                juice is stored together with the wine. The core is                finely crushed, and then enzymatic hydrolysis is                added to enzymatic hydrolysis for 2-5 hours, and                then placed in an extractant, and the extraction                solution is obtained by microwave-assisted                extraction for 2-3 times. The extracted core is                placed in a supercritical extraction kettle, and                supercritical carbon dioxide is extracted to obtain                a core oil. The extracting agent comprises                petroleum ether, chloroform, ethyl acetate,                n-butanol, ethanol and water in a mass ratio of                1-2:2-4:5-7:6-8:6-8:10. The ratio of the core to                the extractant is 10 g:20 ml. The extraction                solution is mixed with the fruit wine, core oil,                anethole, hop acid, syringol extract, and ginkgo                leaf alcohol extract for 2-3 hours, and added with                25 degrees of pure rice wine in 10:0.1-0.5 ratio as                a fermentation species, and again sealed                fermentation for 25-35 days to obtain a                fermentation broth. The fermentation broth is                allowed to stand, and after the broth is turbid and                clear, the mixture is mixed with fermentation broth                at a mass ratio of 1-3:5 with 50 degrees of pure                rice wine. The seabuckthorn and kiwi fruit meat are                added again and sealed the fermentation for 25-35                days to obtain mixed fermentation broth. The upper                layer of the mixed fermentation broth is cleared,                and sealed with double-layer disinfectant gauze                under vacuum. Then, packing, sealing and packing                are done to obtain the finished product. The                alcohol content of jujube fruit wine is 20-22                degrees.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: Preparing health                  care wine involves adding the extraction solution                  with fruit wine, core oil, anethole, hop acid,                  syringol extract, and ginkgo leaf alcohol extract                  in 0.1-1:100:0.1-1:0.1-0.5:0.1-1:0.1-1:0.05-0.1                  ratio. The mass ratio of water, yellow bell wood                  flower, fruit and rock sugar is                  7-10:0.1-1:1-3:2-5. The 20-30 wt.% sea-buckthorn                  and 20-30 wt.% Chinese goosebeery slurry are                  added. Preferred Conditions: The extracted core                  is placed in a supercritical extraction kettle                  with an equal mass ratio of chloroform, petroleum                  ether and ethyl acetate as an entrainer at an                  extraction pressure of 25 kPa and 25-30 degrees C                  extraction temperature. The core oil is obtained                  by extracting for 20-30 minutes under the                  condition of a carbon dioxide flow rate of 25                  L/minute. The microwave extraction is done with                  500 W microwave power for 5-15 minutes. Preferred                  Components: The enzymolysis liquid contains                  laccase, cellulase, beta -amylase and                  phosphorylase.

PN CN109570227-A; CN109570227-B
TI Repairing acidized arsenic polluted soil by            biochar loaded nano zero-valent iron synergistic            bacteria comprises e.g. inoculating Pseudomonas putida            strain MnB1 into Pseudomonas putida enrichment medium            and aerobic enrichment culturing.
AU WANG H
   WU Y
   WANG Y
   SUN Y
   GAO Y
   HAN H
AE UNIV QINGDAO TECHNOLOGY (UQDT-C)
   UNIV QINGDAO TECHNOLOGY (UQDT-C)
GA 201933601V
AB    NOVELTY - Repairing acidized arsenic polluted soil by                biochar loaded nano zero-valent iron synergistic                bacteria comprises e.g. inoculating Pseudomonas                putida strain MnB1 intoPseudomonas putida                enrichment culture medium, performing aerobic                enrichment culture, adding Pseudomonas putida                bacterial liquid enriched into Pseudomonas putida                culture medium B, adding manganese carbonate at the                same time, culturing under aerobic condition, and                generating a brownish black flocculent body in the                culture medium to obtain bacterial metabolite,                placing crushed agricultural biomass into                pyrolyzing furnace, introducing carbon dioxide,                protecting the gas, heating, cooling, grinding,                sieving, adding the prepared black powder into                hydrochloric acid solution, stirring, filtering,                washing with deionized water, and freeze-drying in                a freeze-drying machine to obtain charcoal powder                and adding charcoal powder into ferrous sulfate or                ferric chloride solution.
   USE - The method is useful for repairing acidized                arsenic polluted soil by biochar loaded nano                zero-valent iron synergistic bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method immobilizes arsenic in soil,                increases soil pH, solves soil acidification,                achieves the dual purpose of simultaneously solving                soil acidification and arsenic pollution repair,                has short repair time, has stable effect and large                processing range and has no secondary                pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Repairing acidized arsenic polluted soil by                biochar loaded nano zero-valent iron synergistic                bacteria comprises (1) inoculating Pseudomonas                putida strain MnB1 intoPseudomonas putida                enrichment culture medium an according to the                volume ratio of 5-10%, performing aerobic                enrichment culture for 1-5 days at 15-35 degrees C                under the horizontal oscillation (100-180                revolutions/minute), (2) adding Pseudomonas putida                bacterial liquid enriched into Pseudomonas putida                culture medium B with the pH of 6.5-7.5 according                to the volume ratio of 5-10%, adding 0.2-1.8 g/l                manganese carbonate at the same time, culturing at                15-35 degrees C for 3-10 days under the aerobic                condition, and generating a brownish black                flocculent body in the culture medium to obtain                bacterial metabolite, (3) placing 5-100 g crushed                agricultural biomass into pyrolyzing furnace,                introducing carbon dioxide, protecting the gas,                heating at 400-700 degrees C for 5-12 hours,                cooling, grinding, and sieving by a 60-300-mesh                sieve, adding the prepared black powder into 0.2-1                mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, stirring for 8-24                hours, filtering, washing with 20-300 ml of                deionized water until the pH is 8-9, and                freeze-drying in a freeze-drying machine for 12-36                hours to obtain charcoal powder, (4) adding 5-20 g                charcoal powder into 0.1-0.5 mol/l ferrous sulfate                or ferric chloride solution, stirring at the                temperature of 200-400 revolutions/minute for 10-30                minutes at the temperature of 30-80 degrees C in                the water bath, adding a green tea extracting                solution with the volume ratio of 20-80%, stirring                for 10-40 minutes in a nitrogen environment,                filtering and freeze-drying in a freeze-drying                machine for 12-36 hours to obtain biochar-loaded                zero-valent iron material and (5) adding 20-100 ml                metabolite and 0.5-8 g biochar loaded zero-valent                iron material into 100 g arsenic-polluted acidified                soil, adjusting the moisture content of the soil to                50-70%, stirring for 10-30 minutes, placing the                mixture at the room temperature 15-35 degrees C,                allowing to stand for 3-10 days, enabling the                active manganese oxide and biochar loaded                zero-valent iron and generating a series of                physical-chemical reactions with trivalent arsenic                or pentavalent arsenic in the soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The culture medium A                  comprises 0.3-0.8 g yeast extract, 0.2-0.8 g                  hydrolyzed casein, 0.3-0.8 g glucose, 0.1-0.4 g                  calcium chloride, 0.1-0.6 g magnesium sulfate,                  1-5 ml microelements and 1 liter deionized water.                  The culture medium A comprises 0.05-0.45 g                  ammonium ferrous sulfate, 0.1-0.5 g sodium                  citrate, 0.01-0.3 g yeast extract powder,                  0.01-0.05 g sodium pyrophosphate and 1 liter                  deionized water. Preferred Method: In the step                  (1) the selected bacterial strain is from the                  American model collection center, (Peservation                  number is ATCC 23483). In the step (3) the carbon                  dioxide shielding gas inlet speed is 200-600                  ml/minute, and the pyrolysis furnace has a                  heating rate of 10-20 degrees C/minute; the                  agricultural biomass is straw, wheat straw and                  bamboo.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

AB    NOVELTY - Toilet collection tank comprises a stirring rod (2) for stirring the excrement and a strain ball arranged in an excrement collection box body (1), a motor (3) for driving the stirring rod to rotate outside the excrement collection box body, an air vent (4) for extracting gas after the excrement is degraded and vaporized set on the one side of excrement collection box body, a slag discharge port (5) for discharging the slag after the gasification of the feces set on the opposite side of the motor on the excrement collection box body and an electric control box for controlling the operation of the stirring rod provided on excrement collection box body.
   USE - The toilet collection tank is useful for underground construction work.
   ADVANTAGE - The toilet collection tank is environmentally-friendly and solves the problem of large amount of excrement underground.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of toilet collection tank comprising (a) placing the toilet collection tank horizontally and fixing the whole of the toilet collection tank with a universal wheel, (b) preparing a multi-element mixed septic antifungal agent by (b1) physical and chemical analysis of excrement by collection the three groups of excrement samples at different locations, carrying out physical and chemical analysis, where the unit mass of excrement water content is 58%, containing 29% crude fiber and hydrocarbons, containing 9% of organic nitrogen compounds and 4% minerals, (b2) quickly decomposing crude fiber and hydrocarbons to obtain mildew, purifying the fibrolytic enzyme from the bovine ruminant, separating and selecting the wood decay fungi from forest dead wood, separating and screening out multi-enzymes from koji and mash, concentrating the three molds separately and purifying by medium culture, making into egg yolk-sized bulbs to form a ternary compound hydrocarbon degrading strain ball 1, decomposing the crude fiber and hydrocarbons in the excrement into poly alkylglycosides, carbon dioxide and water vapor, (b3) quickly decomposing the organic nitrogen compound, separating and screening the odorless and non-toxic enzymes from squid and stinky eggs, carrying out the same culture and purification (as in the step b) and making into a strain ball 2, where the function strain ball 2 is quickly decomposing the nitrogenous compound into ammonia nitrogen gas and water vapor by using the underground normal temperature, (c) adding mixed septic mildew agent, when the liquid level of the collection tank exceeds 1/3, adding mixed septic mildew agent, according to the volume size of the collection tank, adding mixed septic mildew agent according to component by volume fraction, adding strain ball 0.01-0.02 parts of strain ball 1 and 0.02-0.03 parts of strain ball 2, (d) stirring the strain ball (1 and 2), stirring the mixture in the collection tank by a motor-driven stirring rod for 3-4 minutes every half hour, (e) exhausting exhaust excrement under the action of strain ball 1, quickly decomposing crude fiber and part of hydrocarbons in excrement into poly alkylglycosides and carbon dioxide, degrading nitrogen compounds in the excrement to ammonia nitrogen gas under the action of strain ball 2, discharging through the exhaust vent of the collection tank and (e) gasification and deslagging processing excrement, reducing the cycle of slagging, when the liquid level in the collection tank is greater than 2/3 and discharging the slag through the slag discharge port of the collection tank.

PT P
PN CN108718916-A
TI Cultivating wild mountain black Boletus edulis, by inoculating wild mountain black Boletus edulis in culture medium, culturing medium, until medium is full of mycelium, covering soil and culturing until mycelium grows to soil surface.
AU SHU B
   SHU X
   SHU D
   SHU W
AE GUIZHOU WUCHUAN ZHAOSHI AGRIC & FORESTRY (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018874114
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating wild mountain black Boletus edulis, involves (a) preparing culture medium, selecting wild mountain black Boletus edulis, cleaning the wild mountain black B.edulis, washing top of B.edulis mushroom, cleaning the stem of the B.edulis with the first cleaning brush on the side panel, draining and sterilizing mushroom, inoculating B.edulis mushroom in sterilized medium, culturing medium, until the culture medium is full of mycelium, covering the soil, culturing mycelium, until the mycelium grows to the surface of the soil layer.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating wild mountain black B.edulis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases yield of wild mountain black B.edulis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cultivating wild mountain black Boletus edulis, involves (a) preparing culture medium by using 20-30 pts. wt. maple wood sheets, 20-30 pts. wt. red pine wood chips, 10-15 pts. wt. peanut shells, 10-15 pts. wt. corn cobs, 20-30 pts. wt. dark brown soil, 0.6-1.6 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 0.5-1 part monopotassium phosphate, 0.8-1.4 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 10-20 pts. wt. bagasse, and 6-10 pts. wt. licorice extract and the pH of the culture substrate is 4.8-5.8, (b) selecting wild mountain black Boletus edulis, cleaning the wild mountain black B.edulis using cleaning device, washing top of B.edulis mushroom with the salt water sprayed from the flushing gun, and the salt water is recovered into the circulation hole under the guidance of the support plate, the vertical reciprocating movement of the rack can not only clean the rod portion of B.edulis mushroom through the second cleaning brush, but also drives the piston plate to reciprocate along the path of the piston tube, the brine in the circulation hole is ejected from the flushing hole through the groove under the action of the piston structure, a part of the brine is used to clean the ground of the top of the B.edulis mushroom, a part of the brine drives the circular shaft through the side plates, cleaning the stem of the B.edulis with the first cleaning brush on the side panel, the washed B.edulis mushroom is drained and sterilized and use the clean wild B.edulis mushroom to cultivate the strain, and obtain the B.edulis mushroom, (c) inoculating B.edulis mushroom in the sterilized medium, culturing medium at 25-30 degrees C under carbon dioxide concentration of 900-1200 ppm, and humidity of 50-70%, and performing shading culture until the culture medium is full of mycelium, (d) covering the soil, then placing shaker at a ratio of 1:3 to mix the dark brown soil and the sterile soil, and spraying the water to make the soil moisture content 60-75%, stacking for 6-11 days and covered on the medium full of mycelium, (e) culturing mycelium in the soil layer, after covering the soil under temperature of 25-30 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration of 1000-1200 ppm, the humidity of 80-90%, and culturing mycelium until the mycelium grows to the surface of the soil layer, and (f) culturing mushroom at 25-28 degrees C under carbondioxide concentration 600-900 ppm and humidity of 90-95%, and daily light for 6-9 hours with illuminance of 120-220 LX, until the mushroom harvest.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (d), the sterilization method is ethylene oxide gas sterilization, moist heat sterilization or dry heat sterilization process. The medium soil is vegetable garden soil or lime sand soil. The soil is further added with 6-9% organic fertilizer, where the organic fertilizer is animal dung, animal residue or straw.

TI Apparatus comprises low temperature vacuum drying device and one pulverizing device, where low temperature vacuum drying device comprises drying cylinder, and front end of drying cylinder is provided with open front cover.
AU LIU L
   LIU H
   CHENG B
   ZHAO Q
AE SICHUAN SHANGHAO AGRIC DEV CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018849481
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises a low temperature vacuum drying device and one pulverizing device (3), where the low temperature vacuum drying device comprises a drying cylinder (1), and the front end of the drying cylinder is provided with an open front cover (2), and a rear cover, which is fixed at the rear end. The drying cylinder includes an outer heat insulating cylinder disposed on the outer layer and an inner heat conducting cylinder nested in the outer heat insulating cylinder, an oil chamber which is provided for heat conducting oil and is disposed between the inner heat conducting cylinder and the outer heat insulating cylinder, an electric heating rod is disposed in the oil chamber, and the drying cylinder is provided with an evacuation tube that communicates with the drying chamber of the inner heat conducting cylinder and refrigeration tube (15), and an oil inlet pipe (5) communicating with the oil chamber.
   USE - Apparatus for preparing mulberry powder at a low temperature vacuum.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a mulberry tea powder by apparatus at low-temperature vacuum involves cleaning mulberry bud, taking buds of 3-4 buds on the mulberry branches of the spring silkworm period as raw materials, washing by spraying and soaking, removing stains on the surface of the sprouts;#draining the washed mulberry buds, removing the moisture of the surface of the mulberry bud by a fan, spreading on the paving mechanism in the drying and dehydrating station in the drying chamber, paving#freezing, through the dry ice refrigerator connected to the refrigeration tube, injecting carbon dioxide gas with a temperature of (-30)-(-40) degrees C into the drying chamber rapidly, where the freezing time is 30 minutes;#dehydrating and drying, closing the refrigeration tube, opening the vacuum tube, vacuuming the drying chamber by a vacuum pump connected to the vacuum tube to make the drying chamber, where the vacuum of drying chamber is 8-9 Pascals, and the vacuum state is maintained for 12 hours, subjecting the ice crystal formed by the moisture in the frozen mulberry buds to sublimation dehydration, heating the heat conducting oil in the oil chamber to maintain the temperature of the heat conducting oil at 40-50 degrees C, forming a hot atmosphere in the drying chamber, subjecting the dehydrated mulberry bud to a hot atmosphere baking, where the baking time is 3-5 hours;#discharging, shutting down the electric heating rod, stopping heating of the heat coducting oil, cutting off the vacuum pump connected to the vacuum tube simultaneously, passing the refrigeration tube to the drying chamber, injecting carbon dioxide gas at 5-8 degrees C to cause the drying chamber, dewatering and drying mulberry buds on the paving mechanism, cooling the mulberry collecting mechanism and the paving mechanism, restoring the pressure in the drying chamber to atmospheric pressure, opening the front cover, driving the paving mechanism to pour the dehydrated and dried mulberry bud into the mulberry bud collecting mechanism, transferring manually to the operation between the front cover and the pulverizing device;#pulverizing, transferring the dehydrated dried mulberry bud to the pulverizing device for pulverization;#loading the mulberry bud powder after crushing the quantitative mulberry bud powder according to the bagging standard, encapsulating and disinfecting; and#storing the disinfected mulberry powder in a refrigerator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of apparatus.
   Drying cylinder (1)
   Open front cover (2)
   Pulverizing device (3)
   Oil inlet pipe (5)

PN CN108341420-A; AR108604-A1; CN108341420-B
TI Preparation of lithium hydroxide and lithium            carbonate involves reducing brine after extraction of            potassium, electrodialyzing, refining, desalting,            producing lithium hydroxide solution, evaporating and            treating in gas-liquid reactor.
AU MA P
AE MA P (MAPP-Individual)
   MA P (MAPP-Individual)
   GUO Y (GUOY-Individual)
GA 201864447Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of lithium hydroxide and lithium                carbonate involves reducing brine after extraction                of potassium, reducing total concentration of                sodium and potassium ions, concentrating,                performing coarse filtration, acidifying, cooling,                removing boron to form a lithium brine,                centrifuging, heating, producing pure boric acid,                passing lithium brine to electrodialyzer,                filtering, producing secondary refining liquid,                removing calcium and magnesium ions, producing                three refined liquids, feeding the refining liquids                into a desalting chamber, passing lithium ion in                the refining solution through the anode membrane,                forming lithium hydroxide solution, performing                forced evaporation, discharging mixture of lithium                hydroxide monohydrate and mother liquid, filtering,                washing, recrystallizing to form battery-grade                lithium hydroxide, mixing mother liquid with                washing liquid, passing through a gas-liquid                reactor, reacting with carbon dioxide gas to form                lithium carbonate sediment, filtering and                drying.
   USE - Preparation of lithium hydroxide and lithium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The recovery rate of lithium ions can be                improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of lithium hydroxide and lithium                carbonate involves reducing brine after the                extraction of potassium by natural evaporation in a                stable pool, reducing total concentration of sodium                and potassium ions, generating potash raw material                in a regulating tank, concentrating to form a                concentrated boron-lithium brine, performing coarse                filtration, mixing hydrochloric acid, acidifying,                cooling, removing boron to form a lithium brine,                centrifuging, heating, producing purified boric                acid, passing lithium brine to electrodialyzer,                intercepting divalent ions by an ion selective                membrane, separating magnesium and lithium,                filtering by a nanofiltration membrane,                intercepting more than 90% magnesium ions and                sulfate ions, producing secondary refining liquid                and concentrated water, which is recycled to                lithium brine, removing calcium and magnesium ions                by passing through a calcium magnesium ion                selective cationic resin, producing three refined                liquid having calcium, magnesium and sulfate                concentration of less than 20 ppm, feeding the                refining liquids into a desalting chamber of a                bipolar membrane electrodialyzer, passing lithium                ion in the refining solution through the anode                membrane, forming lithium hydroxide solution,                passing anions of refining solution through the                negative membrane to the acid chamber, performing                forced evaporation of lithium hydroxide solution by                an evaporation crystallizer, discharging a mixture                of lithium hydroxide monohydrate and mother liquid,                filtering, washing, recrystallizing to form                battery-grade lithium hydroxide and washing liquid                containing lithium hydroxide solution, mixing the                evaporation mother liquid with the washing liquid,                passing through a gas-liquid reactor, reacting with                carbon dioxide gas in the reactor to form lithium                carbonate sediment, filtering, washing, and drying                to obtain battery grade lithium carbonate. The                bipolar membrane electrodialyzer has anode chamber,                an alkali chamber, an acid chamber, a desalting                chamber and a cathode chamber. The special cation                exchange membrane, alkali chamber, a bipolar                membrane, acid chamber, anion exchange membrane,                desalting compartment, and cation exchange membrane                are stacked to form a membrane group. An anode                plate is provided in the anode chamber, and a                cathode plate is provided in the cathode chamber.                The membrane group is sandwiched between the anode                chamber and the cathode chamber. During the                continuous operation of the bipolar membrane                electrodialyzer, four solutions chosen from liquid,                acid, lye and polar solution are circulated. A                stable circulation operation of the feed liquid in                the desalination chamber of the bipolar membrane                electrodialyzer is realized by the liquid-liquid                balance buffer tank, and the continuous                replenishment of the liquid amount and the balanced                discharge of the diluent. The acid circulation in                the acid chamber is stabilized by the acid balance                buffer tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The brine is further                  concentrated in a stable pool to form a                  low-potassium sodium borohydride brine. The total                  amount of potassium and sodium ions is less than                  5 g/L. The product of lithium ion concentration                  and sulfate concentration is less than 150                  g2/L2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The base layer of the                  special cation exchange membrane is made of a                  corrosion-resistant material, preferably                  polytetrafluoroethylene.

PT P
PN CN108164382-A; CN108164382-B
TI Comprehensive extraction of flavone compound from hop comprises extracting hop with methanol/ethanol, separating solid and solution, resin absorption of solution, dissolving solid in dichloromethane and nylon powder column chromatography.
AU LIU Q
   MA W
   MENG L
   FAN Y
AE YUMEN TUOPU TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (YUME-Non-standard)
GA 201849206C
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive extraction of flavone compound from hops comprises extracting the hop residue with methanol/ethanol under carbon dioxide, filtering the solution, distilling off methanol/ethanol under reduced pressure, clarifying the remaining solution, separating the solid and solution, treating the solution in an adsorption resin column having medium polarity adsorption resin, washing with water, eluting with an alcohol solution, distilling, retaining the remaining aqueous phase, spray drying to obtain a brown powder containing rutin, hyperoside and astragalin, drying the solid, dissolving in dichloromethane, filtering, subjecting to nylon powder column chromatography, eluting with dichloromethane, collecting eluate, eluting with ethyl acetate, separately concentrating the five fractions of the eluate, recovering dichloromethane solvent, concentrating the ethyl acetate eluate, evaporating to dryness, allowing to stand to precipitate green crystals, and silica gel column chromatography.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive extraction of flavone compound from hops.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables extraction, purification and enrichment of xanthohumol; enriches rutin, hyperin and astragalin; and recycles hop resource.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive extraction of flavone compound from hops comprises (a) extracting the hop residue with 40-80 vol.% methanol or ethanol under carbon dioxide, where the solvent dosage is 3-10 times of the amount of hop residue, extracting for 0.5-3 hours from room temperature to 60 degrees C, extracting for 1-3 times under the same conditions, (b) collecting the solution by filtration, distilling off methanol or ethanol under reduced pressure at -0.06 to -0.09 MPa, clarifying the remaining solution at 0-10 degrees C, and separating the solid and solution, (c) treating the solution in an adsorption resin column, where the adsorption resin is a medium polarity adsorption resin, washing the adsorbent resin column with water, eluting with an alcohol solution, distilling the eluted alcohol solution under reduced pressure at -0.06 to -0.09 MPa to recover the alcohol, retaining the remaining aqueous phase, spray drying to obtain a brown powder containing rutin, hyperoside and astragalin, (d) completely drying the solid obtained in the step (b) at less than 80 degrees C, dissolving the dried solid in dichloromethane (2-20 times), and filtering to obtain a clear solution, (e) subjecting the dichloromethane solution to nylon powder column chromatography, where the volume of the nylon powder column is 1-4 times the volume of the dichloromethane solution, eluting the column with dichloromethane, collecting dichloromethane effluent, where the volume of the effluent is 2-3 times the volume of the column, eluting with ethyl acetate, collecting ethyl acetate eluent, where the volume of ethyl acetate effluent is 2-3 times the volume of the column, and (f) separately concentrating the five fractions of the eluate obtained in the step (e), and completely recovering dichloromethane solvent from dichloromethane eluate, concentrating the ethyl acetate eluate to 1-2 vol.%, concentrating the methanol elution ribbon, evaporating to dryness, concentrating and evaporating the effluent from the front part of the methanol elution ribbon to dryness, eluting the ribbon with methanol, concentrating the latter part of the effluent to 0.03-0.2% by volume, and allowing to stand to precipitate green crystals, and (g) solid treatment of the partial portion of the methanol ribbon and the front portion of the methanol ribbon, performing 2-5 times of silica gel column chromatography, eluting silica gel column with ethyl acetate eluent to obtain isoxanthohumol, enrichment of demethylated xanthohumol by silica gel column elution with ethyl acetate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises extracting hops residue using 50-70% alcohol as solvent (5-7 times) under carbon dioxide. The step (b) comprises solid drying at 60 degrees C, dissolving the obtained solid in dichloromethane (2-4 times), drying the solid after dissolution. Preferred Components: The non-polar adsorption resin is AB-8 adsorption resin. The nylon powder is nylon 66 or nylon 6 powder (preferred). The nylon powder is of 50-300 meshes. The volume of the nylon column is 1.5-2 times the volume of the dichloromethane solution.

PN CN108102876-A; CN108102876-B
TI Microalgae continuous harvesting apparatus comprises microalgae incubator which is provided with oxygen recovery device at left end, and U-shaped tube is provided on outer wall on right side, U-shaped tube extends to incubator.
AU JIANG H
   LI Y
   TIAN L
   MA T
AE JIANG H (JIAN-Individual)
   RIZHAO MARINE & FISHERIES RES INST (RIZH-Non-standard)
GA 201844982E
AB    NOVELTY - Microalgae continuous harvesting apparatus comprises microalgae incubator (1) which is provided with oxygen recovery device (2) at left end, and U-shaped tube (3) is provided on the outer wall on the right side. The other end of the U-shaped tube extends to the top of the cavity of the microalgae incubator, and the right side of the top of the microalga incubator is provided with a culture solution supply tank (4). Water pump (5) is installed on the left outer wall of the culture solution supply tank through a connecting pipe, and a liquid supply pipe is provided at the liquid outlet of the water pump, and the culture liquid supply pipe. The other end extends into the lumen of the microalgae incubator and is connected to the shower dish. Dive light is arranged on the left wall of the inner cavity of the microalgae incubator, and a reflective plate matching the dive light is provided on the left outer wall of the microalga incubator.
   USE - Microalgae continuous harvesting apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The microalgae continuous harvesting apparatus uniformly sprinkle culture solution, promote growth of the culture solution, and improve the purity of harvested microalgae.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for microalgae continuous harvesting, which involves:
   (A) starting water pump on inoculum box of microalgae, sending culture solution in culture solution supply tank to shower dish through culture solution supply pipe and spraying, spilling culture solution from nozzle on the bottom of the tray effectively increasing survival rate of the microalgae;
   (B) opening first valve and second valve, introducing appropriate amount of carbon dioxide and air into the inoculation box of the microalgae, forming tiny bubbles of gas through the influent by aeration device greatly increasing gas-liquid contact area not only efficiently replenish carbon dioxide into the microalgae incubator;
   (C) promoting dissolved oxygen in the culture solution, collecting precipitated oxygen in the oxygen recovery device through the U-shaped tube, and is further processed to be utilized, blocking higher quality impurities by U-shaped tube and increasing purity of oxygen collected by the oxygen recovery unit;
   (D) starting power supply to make the dive lamp work, to provide uniform and controllable lighting conditions for the microalgae incubator and to set the brightness adjustment device on the dive light according to the natural lighting, selecting intensity and brightness of the dive light where reflector reflects the light on the other side of the dive light into the microalgae incubator to increase the light utilization efficiency of the dive light;
   (E) starting one-way water pump on the microalgae harvesting tube, transporting microalgae incubator to the microalgae filtration box, passing the microalgae through the first-class filter and secondary filter, removing liquid, performing step-by-step filtration, and removing microalgae from the collection of microalgae recovery pipe, falling removed culture liquid into the microalgae filter box cavity bottom; and
   (F) starting one-way water pump on the culture solution recovery pipe, recovering microalgae culture liquid at the bottom of the inner cavity of the microalgae filtration box through the culture solution recovery pipe as an algae species, and completing cyclic culture, and microalgae continuous harvest operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of microalgae continuous harvesting apparatus.
   Microalgae incubator (1)
   Oxygen recovery device (2)
   U-shaped tube (3)
   Culture solution supply tank (4)
   Water pump (5)

PT P
PN CN107647406-A; CN107647406-B
TI Extracting tea polyphenols comprises e.g. picking fresh tea leaves, freezing rapidly, crushing, concentrating centrifugal solution, mixing desorption solution, washing concentrate twice with ethyl acetate and freeze drying.
AU MEI G
AE XUNWU GANLIN YANGTIAN TEA CO LTD (XUNW-Non-standard)
GA 201811671V
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting tea polyphenols comprises e.g. (i) picking fresh tea leaves beginning with a bud, drying, freezing rapidly and thawing; (ii) crushing thawed tea, dissolving resulting tea powder in ethanol and extracting; (iii) centrifuging; (iv) concentrating centrifugal solution by evaporation and adsorbing; (v) adsorbing eluted caffeine with water; (vi) desorbing decaffeinated NKA-2 adsorption resin with 80% ethanol; (vii) mixing desorption solution from step (v) and (vi) and concentrating; and (viii) washing concentrate twice with ethyl acetate and freeze drying.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting tea poly phenols.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces caffeine content of the tea polyphenols extracted from the tea thus improves extraction rate of the tea polyphenols and improves the purity of caffeine and tea poly phenols.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting tea polyphenols comprises (i) picking fresh tea leaves beginning with a bud, displaying a bud with two leaves, drying the surface until there is no visible moisture, freezing rapidly at -3to 5 degrees C for 30-50 minutes and thawing by microwave; (ii) crushing thawed tea, screening machine, dissolving resulting tea powder in 80% ethanol and extracting for 1-3 hours, where the mass ratio of the tea powder and 80% ethanol is 1:6-10; (iii) placing mixture at 35-45 degrees C, at pressure 0.4-0.6Mpa, centrifuging for 1-3 minutes, discarding precipitate, taking centrifuge fluid for the next step; where centrifuging is carried out at 5000-7000 turns/minute; (iv) concentrating centrifugal solution by evaporation, diluting with water to adjust the pH of 6-7, controlling the solution flow rate at 1-3 bed volume/hour , adsorbing under NKA-2 adsorption resin, where mass ratio of the centrifugal solution and NKA-2 adsorption resin is 1:1; (v) adsorbing eluted caffeine with 10-15 degrees C water on NKA-2, where the mass ratio of NKA-2 adsorption resin and water is1:1-3, eluting for 1-3 times, where flow rate is 1-2 bed volume/hours, collecting eluent, flowing through the polyethylene terephthalate (PET) resin at a rate of 2 bed volume/hour with a mass ratio of eluent to PET resin is 1: 3, adsorbing PET resin desorbed with 80% ethanol, where the mass ratio of the PET resin and 80% ethanol is 1:1, the flow rate is 1 bed volume/hour to obtain desorption solution, heating eluent to 190 degrees C, cooling the distillate to remove tar, filtering, concentrating to obtain a crude product of caffeine, adding remaining eluent into the desorption solution, mixing, adding the crude product into 3 times the weight of distilled water, dissolving, treating with activated carbon, recrystallizing, drying under 75 degrees C and crystallizing caffeine; (vi) desorbing decaffeinated NKA-2 adsorption resin with 80% ethanol, where the mass ratio of the NKA-2 adsorption resin to 80% ethanol is 1: 1 for 2-4 times and the flow rate is 1-2 bed volume/hour to obtain desorption solution; (vii) mixing desorption solution from step (v) and (vi), concentrating by evaporation to obtain concentrated solution, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide by the extraction condition, where the extraction conditions are carbon dioxide flow rate 1100-1300 liter/hour, extraction pressure at 26-30Mpa at 60-80 degrees C for 3-5 hours and separation pressure is 9-12Mpa at 25- 35 degrees C for 2-4 hours; and (viii) washing concentrate twice with ethyl acetate, mass ratio of concentrated and ethyl acetate is 1: 5-8, whichever in ester is freeze drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The rapid freezing in step (i) is carried out at -4 degrees C for 40 minutes. The tea powder in step (ii) is dissolved in 80% ethanol and leaching for 2 hours, where the mass ratio of the tea powder and 80% ethanol is 1:8. The mixture in step (iii) is placed at 40 degrees C, at pressure of 0.5Mpa, centrifuging for 2 minutes, discarding precipitate and collecting centrifuge solution for the next step of operation. The centrifuging in step (iii) is carried out at 6000 revolutions/minute. The pH in step (iv) is adjusted to 7, flowing solution through the NKA-2 adsorption resin at a rate of 2 bed volume/hour. The adsorbed NKA-2 adsorption resin eluted caffeine with water in step (v) is carried out at 13 degrees C, mass ratio of the NKA-2 adsorption resin to water is 1:2 and the number of elution times is 2 and the flow rate is1 bed volume/hour. The deadsorbing de-caffeinated NKA-2 on adsorption resin in step (vi) with 80% ethanol, the mass ratio of the NKA-2 adsorption resin and 80% ethanol is 1:1, desorption for 3 times and the flow rate of 1bed volume/ hours to obtain desorption solution. The extraction of carbon dioxide in step (vii) is carried out at 1200 liter/hours, extraction pressure at 28Mpa at 70 degrees C, extracting for 4 hours, separation at 11Mpa at 25 degrees Cfor 3 hours. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the concentrate to the ethyl acetate in step (viii) is preferably 1:7.

PN CN107586830-A; CN107586830-B
TI Detecting influence of cigarette smoke on expression of aquaporin 5 cell mRNA by preparing bionic suction liquid, digesting cells, culturing, extracting RNA, reverse transferring and performing fluorescent quantitative PCR analysis.
AU GAO Q
   LIU X
   HUANG H
   XIANG H
   LI X
   KONG W
   YANG G
   YAO J
   LI J
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 2018068652
AB    NOVELTY - Method of detecting influence of cigarette smoke on expression of aquaporin 5 cell mRNA involves (a) infiltrating simulated artificial oral mucosa wall of the device with Dulbecco's modified essential medium and Ham's F-12 cell culture medium, selecting cigarettes as test sample to obtain bionic suction liquid, (b) digesting salivary adenoid cystic carcinoma cells and culturing, (c) removing the culture supernatant obtained in step (b), adding bionic suction liquid and culturing to obtain RNA, (d) synthesizing complementary DNA and (e) performing fluorescent quantitative PCR analysis.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting influence of cigarette smoke on expression of aquaporin 5 cell mRNA.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is rapid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of detecting influence of cigarette smoke on expression of aquaporin 5 cell mRNA involves (a) infiltrating the simulated artificial oral mucosa wall of the device with Dulbecco's modified essential Medium (DMEM) and Ham's F-12 (DMEM/F12) cell culture medium, selecting 10-20 cigarettes with the same weight and resistance as the samples to be tested, weighing, suctioning, removing the liquid in the body of the biomimetic absorption device to obtain bionic suction liquid, (b) digesting the salivary adenoid cystic carcinoma cells with 0.25% ml trypsin, seeding the cells on a cell culture plate containing DMEM/F12 cell culture medium, culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 22-26 hours, and adding 6 ng/ml bone morphogenetic protein 6 and culturing for 2-3 days, (c) removing the culture supernatant obtained in step (b), adding 4-8 times bionic suction liquid obtained in step (a) as culture medium and culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 22-26 hours, (d) after culturing, discarding the supernatant, digesting the cells with 0.25% trypsin and extracting total cellular RNA, (e) using RNA as template for reverse transcription PCR to synthesize cDNA, (f) performing fluorescent quantitative PCR analysis using the cDNA obtained in step (e) as template, where performing fluorescent quantitative PCR analysis using SYBR Green I (RTM: cyanine dye), where PCR reaction system (20 mu l) comprises 10 mu l 2 x mix, 6.8 mu l double distilled water, 200 nmol/l 0.4 mu l upstream primer, 200 nmol/l 0.4 mu l downstream primer, 0.4 mu l ROX (RTM: 6-carboxy-X-rhodamine) dye, 2 mu l template and beta -actin as internal reference, and performing PCR reaction by predenaturing at 95 degrees C for 5 minutes, denaturing at 96 degrees C for 10 seconds, annealing/extending at 60 degrees C for 30 seconds, and repeating for 40 cycles, where AQP5 primer comprises upstream primer having a base pair sequence of SEQ ID NO: 1 and downstream primer having a base pair sequence of SEQ ID NO: 2 and internal reference primer comprises upstream primer having a base pair sequence of SEQ ID NO: 3 and downstream primer having a base pair sequence of SEQ ID NO: 4 and (g) analyzing the fluorescence quantitative PCR result obtained in step (f) by 2 Delta Delta (C)t method and obtaining relative expression level of aquaporin 5 cell mRNA of the sample to be tested. 5'-actgggttttctgggtaggg-3' (SEQ ID NO: 1), 5'-gtggtcagctccatggtctt-3' (SEQ ID NO: 2), 5'-ggagattactgccctggctccta-3' (SEQ ID NO: 3) and 5'-gactcatcgtactcctgcttgctg-3' (SEQ ID NO: 4).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves weighing at 22 plus minus 1 degrees C, humidity of 60 plus minus 2% for 48 hours and suctioning using fully automatic carousel machine, where the suction frequency is 1 port/minute for 2 seconds and the suction capacity is 35 plus minus 0.15 ml/port. The step (b) and (d) involves digesting the cells for 1-2 minutes. The step (f) involves determining the purity of RNA before performing reverse transcription, where the RNA purity is determined by measuring the RNA sample at a wavelength of 260 nm and optical density at 280 nm, when the ratio of the optical density value at a wavelength of 260 nm to the optical density value at a wavelength of 280 nm is 1.8-2.1, thus indicating the purity of RNA and performing reverse transcription, where if the purity of DNA is unqualified then cannot be reverse transferred. The method involves performing culture in culture plates, where repeating detection thrice for each test sample. Preferred composition: In step (a), the total content of DMEM/F12 cell medium used to infiltrate the artificial oral mucosal wall is 5 ml.

   MA X
   LIU Y
   WANG L
AE LUOYANG RUICHANG ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CO (LUOY-Non-standard)
GA 202120075X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a tail gas processing system of sulphur recycling device, comprising a tail gas burning unit; a waste heat recycling unit; the tail gas burning unit comprises a burner and an incinerator; the burner is connected with the incinerator; the incinerator is connected with the waste heat recycling unit; it further comprises a catalytic reaction device; the catalytic reaction device is connected with the waste heat recycling unit; the flue gas after burning by the incinerator enters into the catalytic reaction device after passing through the waste heat recovery unit; the catalytic reaction device fully oxidised the hydrocarbon and/or CO in the flue gas to CO2 and water and then discharge. The utility model under the condition of not improving the burning temperature, through low temperature catalytic technology, the unburned hydrocarbon and/or CO oxidation into CO2 and water in the flue gas after burning, reducing pollutant discharge, at the same time, setting the waste heat recovery system, the catalytic reaction to generate heat recycling, reducing fuel consumption, obtaining good effect .
