TI Producing methanol using submerged arc gas            comprises e.g. removing dust and tar impurities in            submerged arc furnace by electric trapping or            adsorption filtration, and sending submerged furnace            gas after de-dusting to gas cooling device.
AU ZHOU E
   LIU J
   WANG Y
   WU M
AE SEDIN ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 2018900245
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol using submerged arc gas                comprises e.g. (i) removing dust and tar impurities                in the submerged arc furnace by electric trapping                or adsorption filtration, and sending submerged                furnace gas after de-dusting to the gas cooling                device, (ii) reducing the temperature of the                submerged arc gas, and entering the cooled ore                furnace gas the buffer and stabilized gas cabinet,                (iii) controlling buffer time, and sending ore                furnace gas of the gas outlet cabinet to the gas                compression device, (iv) entering the pressure of                the submerged coal gas the gas compressor, and                sending to the fine desulfurization device, (v)                removing organic sulfur in the ore furnace gas by                hydrolysis or hydroconversion, and removing                inorganic sulfur in the ore furnace gas by the zinc                oxide desulfurization technology, and controlling                treated gas, and sending submerged furnace gas                after desulfurization to the converter, and (vi)                carrying out medium temperature or low temperature                conversion process technology.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol                using submerged arc gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has reliable process, low                comprehensive energy consumption; and is                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing methanol using submerged arc gas                comprises (i) removing dust and tar impurities in                the submerged arc furnace by electric trapping or                adsorption filtration, and sending submerged                furnace gas after dedusting to the gas cooling                device, (ii) reducing the temperature of the                submerged arc gas to 35-40 degrees C, and entering                the cooled ore furnace gas the buffer and                stabilized gas cabinet, (iii) controlling buffer                time for 20-30 minutes, and sending ore furnace gas                of the gas outlet cabinet to the gas compression                device, (iv) entering the pressure of the submerged                coal gas the gas compressor is 0.003-0.02 MPaG, and                the final outlet pressure of the compressor is                1.8-4 MPaG, sending to the fine desulfurization                device, (v) removing organic sulfur in the ore                furnace gas by hydrolysis or hydroconversion, and                removing inorganic sulfur in the ore furnace gas by                the zinc oxide desulfurization technology, and                controlling treated gas, where total sulfur content                is less than 0.1 ppm, and sending submerged furnace                gas after desulfurization to the converter, (vi)                carrying out medium temperature or low temperature                conversion process technology to transform the                carbon monoxide and steam in the gas to form                hydrogen and carbon monoxide where operating                temperature range of the converter is 210-450                degrees C, and the conversion catalyst is                iron-chromium or copper, where the zinc-based                catalyst controls the carbon monoxide conversion                rate to be 60-80%, firstly recovering the shift gas                by the waste heat recovery device, and by-product                is 0.5-3.9 MPaG saturated steam, and introducing                the feed gas into the shift furnace through the                inlet and outlet heat exchangers, reducing                temperature of the shift gas to 170-200 degrees C,                recovering converter gas by the boiler feed water                pre-heater and the desalted water preheater, and                cooling by an air cooler and a circulating water                cooler, and lowering temperature to 40 degrees C,                and sending to decarburization device, (vii)                removing carbon dioxide in the shift gas by the wet                decarburization technology, in the absorption                tower, the shift gas is in countercurrent contact                with the decarburization solution, and the carbon                dioxide in the shift gas is absorbed by the                solution, and removing carbon removal gas, sending                to the syngas compression device, where the molar                composition of the decarburization purification gas                satisfies (hydrogen-carbon dioxide)/( carbon                monooxide +carbon dioxide)=1.60-2.05, and the                decarburized rich liquid after absorbing carbon                dioxide is recycled by heating to remove carbon                dioxide, and recycling, (vii) purifying and                compressing the decarburized rich liquid by heating                to remove liquefied carbon dioxide product with                purity greater than or equal to 99%, (ix)                compressing methanol synthesis fresh gas and                recycle gas to the pressure required for methanol                synthesis, and forming the methanol synthesis fresh                gas by mixing the decarbonization purification gas                and the hydrogen recovered by the hydrogen recovery                device, and where the gas molar composition is                satisfied (hydrogen-carbon dioxide)/( carbon                monooxide +carbon dioxide)=2.05-2.15, the fresh gas                pressure entering the syngas compressor is                1.3-3.2MPaG, the circulating gas pressure is 5.2-7                MPaG, and the final outlet pressure of the                compressor is 6-8 MPaG, sending gas compressed                outlet gas to the methanol synthesis reactor, (x)                using low-pressure methanol synthesis process,                where the synthesis tower operating temperature                range is 180-300 degrees C, the operating pressure                range is 5.5-8 MPaG, and sending crude methanol                produced by the methanol synthesis reactor to the                methanol rectification unit without reaction,                sending most of the gas to the syngas compression                unit as recycled gas, and sending small part to the                hydrogen recovery unit as a purge gas, (xi)                recovering The hydrogen in the purge gas by                membrane separation or pressure swing adsorption,                where the hydrogen recovery rate is greater than or                equal to 85%, and the recovered hydrogen is sent to                the synthesis gas compression device to form a                methanol synthesis fresh gas mixed with the                decarbonization purification gas, (xii) carrying                out rectification by a three- or four-tower                rectification process, where methanol recovery rate                is greater than or equal to 98%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The utility model                  comprises total content of dust and tar in the                  submerged coal gas entering the dust removing                  device in the step (i) is 20-50 mg/Nm3, the                  temperature range is 150-180 degrees C, the                  pressure range is 5-20 kPa, and the dust control                  is controlled, and total content of dust and tar                  in the ore furnace gas is less than 5 mg/Nm3. In                  the step (ii), ore furnace gas is indirectly                  cooled, and the heat is exchanged by the                  demineralized water and the submerged furnace                  gas, and the secondary reverse flow of the                  circulating water and the submerged furnace gas                  to meet the subsequent sequence. The temperature                  requirements of the coal mine gas inlet cabinet.                  In the step (iii), buffering and stabilizing gas                  cabinet adopts a low-pressure vertical gas                  cabinet to buffer and stabilize the ore furnace                  gas. The compressor is pressurized by a                  reciprocating compressor or a centrifugal                  compressor, and the compressor is driven by a                  motor or a steam turbine. In the step (v),                  hydrolysis method is carried out at a temperature                  of 150-250 degrees C, the hydroconversion method                  is carried out at a temperature of 300-400                  degrees C, and the zinc oxide desulfurization is                  at 200-400 degrees C under temperature                  conditions. In the step (vi), when the catalyst                  of is a iron-chromium catalyst, the main                  component comprises 80-90 wt.% ferric oxide, 7-11                  wt.% Chromium(III) oxide, when the copper-zinc                  catalyst is used, the main component is 15.3-42                  wt.% cupric oxide, 32-62.2 wt.% zinc oxide, and                  0-40.5 wt.% aluminum oxide The compression of the                  step (ix) is carried out by using a centrifugal                  compressor or a reciprocating compressor. In the                  step (x), methanol synthesis catalyst can use the                  RK-5 type catalyst of Dalian Rick Technology Co.,                  Ltd., the C307 type catalyst of the Nanhua Group                  Research Institute, the XN-98 type catalyst of                  the Southwest Chemical Research and Design                  Institute or the support, one of Psso's MK-121                  type catalysts. In the step (x), the methanol                  synthesis reactor adopts an isothermal reactor                  with a by-product steam, and the molar ratio of                  the recycle gas to the methanol synthesis fresh                  gas is 5-6. The hydrogen pressure recovered by                  the hydrogen recovery unit in the step (xi) is                  1.5-3.4 MPaG. In the step (xii), the three-column                  rectification process is a pre-rectification                  column, a pressurized rectification column and an                  atmospheric distillation column, and the crude                  methanol is added to the pre-removing column to                  sufficiently remove the low-boiling component                  pressurization rectification tower, pressurized                  distillation column operating pressure 0.6 MPaG                  to 0.7MPa, operating temperature 130-140 degrees                  C, the top gas of the pressurized rectification                  column as the heat source of the atmospheric                  distillation column, atmospheric distillation                  column the operating temperature is 105-115                  degrees C. The mass content of water in the                  refined methanol product is less than or equal to                  0.1%, the boiling range is less than or equal to                  0.8 degrees C under the condition of 0 degrees C                  under 101.3 kPa, and the mass of the evaporation                  residue is less than or equal to 0.001%.

PN CN109666642-A; CN109666642-B
TI Isolating and purifying oligodendrocyte precursor            cells from the cerebral cortex of the tree shrew            comprises e.g. cutting cerebral cortex of tree shrew in            fuzzy shape, adding trypsin, digesting and blowing            mixture in cell suspension.
AU SUN X
   LI M
   LU C
   DAI X
   TONG P
   LI X
   WANG X
   YUAN Y
   DAI J
AE INST MEDICAL BIOLOGY CHINESE ACAD MEDICA (MSUM-C)
GA 2019399543
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating and purifying oligodendrocyte                precursor cells from the cerebral cortex of the                tree shrew comprises e.g. cutting cerebral cortex                of tree shrew into fuzzy shape, adding trypsin and                DNase I, digesting, blowing mixture into cell                suspension, placing the cell suspension into                culture box and carbon dioxide, adding mixed                culture solution, stopping digestion, blowing and                mixing uniformly, filtering, centrifuging filtrate,                discarding the supernatant, adding mixed culture                solution to precipitate for heavy suspension cell                precipitation, inoculating obtained cell suspension                into culture bottle, inoculating cell suspension                into culture flask, and culturing, sucking cell                suspension into cell culture bottle which is coated                by poly-D-lysine bag, placing the mixture into                culture box, where the mixed culture solution is                fetal calf serum, B27 and double-antibody                Dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture                F-12 culture medium and preparing                poly-D-lysine-coated cell culture bottle.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and                purifying oligodendrocyte precursor cells from the                cerebral cortex of the tree shrew.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation steps, is fast                cell adherence and high survival rate, exhibits                high cell purity in the subsequent identification                experiment, has high credibility, meets the                requirements of medicine, biology, pharmacy and                life science and is suitable for popularization and                application in general laboratories.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Isolating and purifying oligodendrocyte                precursor cells from the cerebral cortex of the                tree shrew comprises (i) cutting cerebral cortex of                tree shrew in first 48 hours into fuzzy shape,                adding 3 ml 0.0.25% trypsin and 1ml 500-1000 U/ml                DNase I, digesting, blowing mixture into cell                suspension, placing the cell suspension into                culture box with temperature of 37 degrees C and 5%                carbon dioxide for 5-10 minutes, adding 6 ml of the                mixed culture solution, stopping digestion, blowing                and mixing uniformly, filtering in 70-mesh cell                screen, centrifuging filtrate, discarding the                supernatant, adding mixed culture solution to                precipitate for heavy suspension cell                precipitation, inoculating obtained cell suspension                into culture bottle, inoculating cell suspension                into culture flask, and culturing at 37 degrees C                and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 1-2 hours,                sucking cell suspension into cell culture bottle                which is coated by poly-D-lysine bag, placing the                mixture into culture box with temperature of 37                degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator culture,                where the mixed culture solution is 10% fetal calf                serum, 2% B27 and 1% of double-antibody Dulbecco's                modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 culture                medium, preparing PDL-coated cell culture bottle by                adding 0.1-0.5 mg/ml poly-D-lysine to the cell                culture flask, uniformly shaking up bottle bottom,                allowing to stand for 5 minutes, sucking out                liquid, placing cell culture bottle into                temperature of 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide                incubator culture, drying culture box overnight,                and washing with phosphate buffered saline before                inoculation, (ii) culturing for 5 days, adding                multiplication culture of proliferation culture                solution after the original culture solution is                removed, replacing the solution every day,                culturing for 2 days, removing culture solution,                washing cells by phosphate buffered saline,                removing the residual proliferation culture                solution, adding 2 ml of separation liquid, placing                the mixture in temperature of 37 degrees C, and 5%                carbon dioxide incubator culture, incubating for                10-15 minutes, blowing cell surface, forming cell                suspension, sucking the cell suspension into                40-mesh sieve, filtering for one time,                centrifuging, discarding supernatant, adding mixed                culture solution to precipitate for heavy                suspension cell precipitation to obtain cell                suspension, inoculating to culture plate, covering                with poly-D-lysine bag to be cultured for 1-2                minutes, sucking the cell suspension on culture                plate into another common culture plate and                culturing for 1-2 hours in culture box with                temperature of 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide                incubator culture, inoculating the suction-out cell                suspension into culture plate which is covered by                poly-D-lysine, placing mixture into culture box                with the temperature of 37 degrees C and 5% carbon                dioxide incubator culture, cultivating for                overnight, replacing the culture solution into                proliferation culture solution and continuously                culturing culture solution, where the proliferation                culture solution comprises 10 ng/ml basic                fibroblast growth factor, 5 mu g/ml insulin, 1%                fetal bovine serum, 2% B27 and 1% double antibody                in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient                mixture F-12 culture medium, the separating liquid                is Dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient                mixture F-12 culture medium containing 0.02% EDTA,                5 mu g/ml insulin, 0.5% DNase I and 1% double                antibody, the mixed culture solution is Dulbecco's                modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 culture                medium containing 10% fetal calf serum, 2% B27 and                1% double antibody, the poly-D-lysine-coated                culture plate prepared by adding 1 ml of 0.1-0.5                mg/ml poly-D-lysine to the culture plate, and                uniformly spreading the culture plate in shaking                manner, allowing to stand for 5 minutes, sucking                out liquid, and placing culture plate into                temperature of 37 degrees C, and 5% carbon dioxide                incubator culture, drying overnight, and washing                with phosphate buffered saline before inoculation                and (iii) culturing for 3 days, adding                differentiation culture solution for directional                differentiation, and finally removing the culture                solution and stopping culturing, where the                differentiation culture medium is Dulbecco's                modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 culture                medium containing 30 ng/ml tri-iodothyronine, 10                ng/ml ciliary neurotrophic factor, 5 mu g/ml                insulin, 2% B27 and 1% double antibody.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The cerebral cortex                  obtained by taking regenerated brain of tree                  shrew within 48 hours of the newborn, and placing                  it in sterile culture dish containing D-Hanks                  liquid with pre-cooling to 4 degrees C, and                  washing, removing bloodstain, removing meningitis                  and blood vessels from the ventral side and edge                  of brain and removing the brainstem and                  cerebellum, removing the cerebral cortex and                  washing it into another sterile dish containing                  D-Hanks with pre-cooling temperature of 4 degrees                  C. In the method, washing tree shrew twice in                  D-hanks liquid, where the cerebral cortex is                  washed for one time in D-hanks liquid. In the                  step (i) and (ii) centrifuging the parameters at                  1000 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes. In the                  step (i) the culture bottle is 25 cm2 culture                  bottle that has been coated with poly-D-lysine.                  In the step (ii) washing the phosphate buffered                  saline twice; the culture plate is 6-well culture                  plate that has been coated with                  poly-D-lysine.

PN CN114522513-A
TI Method for capturing low partial pressure carbon            dioxide, involves entering flue gas after washing and            purification into induced draft fan through pipeline            from top of washing tower, where pressure is raised to            specified value by induced draft fan.
AU WANG Q
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
GA 202274293P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves conveying factory smoke to                a washing tower (2) from a chimney through a                pipeline. Washing water is pressurized by a washing                water pump (8), conveyed to a washing water cooler                for cooling, and returned to a lower section for                recycling. An upper section is washed with sodium                carbonate to remove sulfur in the flue gas, and the                lye is absorbed in a cycle. The sulfur-containing                waste liquid is discharged to replenish the new lye                after saturation. The flue gas after washing and                purification is entered into an induced draft fan                (6) through the pipeline from the top of the                washing tower, and the pressure is raised to 8                Kpa.g by the induced draft fan.
   USE - Method for capturing low partial pressure                carbon dioxide. Uses include but are not limited to                synthesize high-purity carbon monoxide, tobacco                shred puffing, fertilizer production, supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction, beverage additive, food                fresh-keeping and storage, welding protection gas,                fire extinguisher, pulverized coal conveying, and                synthetic degradable plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The low partial pressure carbon dioxide                capture process is capable of efficiently and                economically capturing the carbon dioxide with high                carbon dioxide trap rate and purity, reduced energy                consumption and reduced back end solvent loss and                improved maintenance system balance, and reduces                the whole energy consumption to less than 2.54                GJ/TCO2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram                illustrating a low partial pressure carbon dioxide                capture process. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
   Acidic water heat exchanger (1)
   Washing tower (2)
   Acidic water pump (3)
   Lye pump (4)
   Lye tank (5)
   Induced draft fan (6)
   Washing water pump (8)
   Washing liquid storage tank (9)
   Pregnant solution pump (11)
   Mechanical filter (12)
   Active carbon filter (13)
   Barren liquid cooler (16)
   Barren liquid pump (17)
   Process gas reboiler (18)
   Process gas compressor (19)
   Steam reboiler (21)

PN WO2022066868-A1
TI Carbonated, oral rehydration beverage used to treat or prevent dehydration, comprises sodium, potassium, chloride, dextrose, and carbon dioxide.
AU BRICKER G
   LAPLANTE T
   LOWE K
   DEWILLE N
   ZEVCHIK D
AE ABBOTT LAB (ABBO-C)
GA 202247623F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonated, oral rehydration beverage (B1) comprises 25-75 mEq/l sodium, 15-40 mEq/l potassium, 20-55 mEq/l chloride, dextrose, and at least 2 volumes of carbon dioxide. The beverage further comprises stevia leaf extract and monk fruit extract, and 1-6 g/l sucrose. The beverage is in packaged form. The pH of the beverage is greater than 3, preferably 3- 5. The molar ratio of dextrose to sodium is 1:1-3:1.
   USE - The beverage is useful for treating or preventing dehydration in an individual (claimed). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The aqueous, carbonated, oral rehydration beverage: is ready-to-drink; and has a less salty flavor as compared to non-carbonated or lightly carbonated electrolyte beverages (less than 2 volume of carbon dioxide) having the same sodium concentration. The carbon dioxide is dissolved in or injected into the beverage before or at the time of packaging such that the beverage requires no additional preparation by the consumer before consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a carbonated, oral rehydration beverage (B2) comprising 25-75 mEq/l sodium, 15-40 mEq/l potassium, 20-55 mEq/l chloride, at least 2 volumes of carbon dioxide, and at least one of dextrose, maltodextrin and maize syrup solids;
   (2) a concentrated syrup that when combined with a predetermined quantity of carbonated water, provides the oral rehydration beverage;
   (3) an aqueous, carbonated, oral rehydration beverage (B3) comprising essentially of water, dextrose, citric acid, potassium citrate, sodium chloride, sodium citrate, at least one sweetener, a source of zinc, and carbon dioxide, where the beverage comprises 30-60 mEq/l sodium, 20-40 mEq/l potassium, 30-50 mEq/l chloride, and 2-3.5 volumes of carbon dioxide; and
   (4) preventing or treating dehydration in an individual, comprising administering the oral rehydration beverage to a dehydrated individual or an individual at risk of becoming dehydrated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The beverage (B1) further comprises: 5-15 mg/l zinc; citrate; and at least one of maltodextrin and maize syrup solids. The beverage (B1) comprises: 15-30 g/l sugars; at least one source of sodium comprising sodium chloride, sodium phosphate, sodium citrate, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate or sodium hydroxide; at least one source of chloride comprising sodium chloride, potassium chloride, calcium chloride or magnesium chloride; at least one source of potassium comprising potassium chloride, potassium phosphate, potassium citrate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate or potassium hydroxide; a source of zinc comprising zinc gluconate, zinc sulfate, zinc chloride, zinc citrate, zinc bicarbonate, zinc carbonate, zinc hydroxide, zinc lactate, zinc acetate, zinc fluoride, zinc bromide or zinc sulfonate; 10-50 mEq/l, 15-45 mEq/l or 20-40 mEq/l citrate; 2-3.5 volumes of carbon dioxide; 30-60 mEq/l, 35-60 mEq/l or 40-50 mEq/l sodium; 20-40 mEq/l or 20-30 mEq/l potassium; 30-50 mEq/l chloride; 25-225, 30-170 mmol/l, 35-150 mmol/l or 70-140 mmol/l dextrose, where the molar ratio of dextrose to sodium is 1:1 to 3:1, 2:1 to 3:1, or 2:1 to 2.5:1; at least 2 volumes, preferably 2.6-3.5 volumes of carbon dioxide; at least one of stevia leaf extract and monk fruit extract, where the combined content of stevia leaf extract and monk fruit extract is 0.0037-0.037 wt.%; stevia leaf extract and monk fruit extract, where the ratio of stevia leaf extract to monk fruit extract is 1:1 to 3:1, 1.5:1 2.5:1, or 2:1; and 3-6 g/l sucrose. The beverage (B3) further comprises essentially of sucrose. Preferred Components: The sweetener comprises essentially of stevia leaf extract in combination with monk fruit extract.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Typical carbonated, oral rehydration beverage comprised of: 2.17 wt.% dextrose monohydrate; 0.54 wt.% sucrose; 0.428 wt.% citric acid; 0.23 wt.% potassium citrate monohydrate; 0.214 wt.% sodium chloride; 0.113 wt.% sodium citrate dihydrate; 0.0151 wt.% stevia; 0.00755 wt.% monk fruit; 0.0075 wt.% flavor; 0.00637 wt.% zinc gluconate; and 96.3 wt.% water.

TI Device for continuous monitoring of blood characteristic quantities in external cardiovascular supporting circuit, has acquisition unit that acquires quantity relating to amount of carbon dioxide removed from patient lung (V COiNL).
AU PETRALIA A
   GHELLI N
   BELLIATO M
AE EUROSETS SRL (EURO-Non-standard)
   EUROSETS SRL (EURO-Non-standard)
   EUROSETS SRL (EURO-Non-standard)
   EUROSETS SRL (EURO-Non-standard)
GA 2019708477
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a circuit (2) comprising a venous line (3) which conveys blood from the patient to an oxygenator (4), and along which a pump (5) is arranged, and an arterial line (6) which returns blood from the oxygenator to the patient. A microprocessor (15) is programmed to display in numerical form on a display the values of all the quantities measured and calculated and in graphic form values that comprises an acquisition unit for acquiring the quantity relating to the amount of carbon dioxide removed from the patient lung (V'COiNL) and by the fact that microprocessor is provided with means adapted to calculate the quantity relating to the amount of total removed carbon dioxide (V?CO2totai).
   USE - Device for continuous monitoring of blood characteristic quantities in external cardiovascular supporting circuit.
   ADVANTAGE - The ratio is particularly useful in case of cardiopulmonary bypass (CPB) treatments, where the amount of carbon dioxide removed by the oxygenator corresponds to the amount of total removed carbon dioxide inasmuch as the patient is bypassed and does not therefore make any contribution to the elimination of the carbon dioxide from the blood, where provides an important parameter in identifying the presence of any kidney problems in the patient. The fact is underlined that the graphic representation relating to the amount of carbon dioxide removed by the oxygenator and to the amount of oxygen transferred by the oxygenator, enables the medical staff to have immediate and intuitive information on the operation of the oxygenator and on how extracorporeal circulation is proceeding.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the monitoring device.

PN WO2018074638-A1; KR2018043903-A; KR1877920-B1
TI Method for storing carbon dioxide involves adding            solvent which is chelating reagent having preset            stabilization constant to alkali industrial            by-products, separating obtained eluate into calcium            carbonate and solvent, and reusing solvent.
AU KIM M
   JUNG S
   KIM D
   JEON J
   CHO T
   KIM M J
   JUNG S S
   KIM D M
   JEON J H
   CHO T Y
AE UNIV KOREA MARITIME IND ACADEMIC COOP (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA MARITIME IND ACADEMIC COOP FO (UYKR-C)
GA 2018332277
AB    NOVELTY - Method for storing a carbon dioxide (CC)                through the production of high-purity calcium                carbonate, involves (a) adding a solvent (FF) to                the alkali industrial by-products (DO) to elute                calcium, (b) separating the calcium eluate (GG)                from the residual by-product (HH), (c) performing a                carbonation reaction (II), (d) separating the                calcium eluate into the calcium carbonate (JJ) and                a solvent, and (e) reusing (KK) the solvent                obtained from the step (d). The solvent is a                chelating reagent having a stabilization constant                with calcium less than the stabilization constant                of calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for storing carbon dioxide through                production of high-purity calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of effective reusing of                solvent for eluting calcium from industrial                by-products, improving calcium elution and                carbonation efficiency and reducing waste                discharge. The method is economical and has high                industrial application. The calcium carbonate                obtained using the method has improved                purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for storing a carbon dioxide through                the production of high-purity calcium carbonate,                involves (a) adding a solvent to the alkali                industrial by-products to elute calcium, (b)                separating the calcium eluate from the residual                by-product, (c) performing a carbonation reaction                by introducing carbon dioxide into the calcium                eluate to obtain a calcium carbonate, (d)                separating the calcium eluate into the calcium                carbonate and a solvent after the carbonation                reaction, and (e) reusing the solvent obtained from                the step (d) as the solvent of the step (a). The                solvent is a chelating reagent having a                stabilization constant with calcium less than the                stabilization constant of calcium carbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the method for storing carbon dioxide through                production of high-purity calcium carbonate.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide (CC)
   Industrial by-products (DO)
   Solvent (FF)
   Calcium eluate (GG)
   Residual by-product (HH)
   Carbonation reaction (II)
   Calcium carbonate (JJ)
   Solvent reuse (KK)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The beads are ceramic                  beads. The content of bead added in the method is                  20-30 vol.% with respect to 100 vol.% of the                  mixture in step (a).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves adding a bead to step (a) for removing                  the surface layer of the industrial by-product,                  thereby improving calcium dissolution efficiency.                  The step (a) is carried out at least twice                  consecutively in the presence of same or                  different kinds of solvents. The method involves                  eluting calcium using the chelating agent and                  continuously injecting filtered industrial                  by-product with ammonium chloride to further                  elute calcium. The solvent is reused at least                  three times. Preferred Composition: The solvent                  is an ammonium salt. The ammonium salt is chosen                  from ammonium chloride, ammonium acetate, and                  ammonium nitrate. The alkali industrial                  by-products are chosen from paper sludge ash,                  cement kiln dust, fuel fly ash, bottom fly,                  de-oil fly ash, steel making slag and waste                  concrete. Preferred Property: The purity of the                  calcium carbonate is 99.5-99.7%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The chelating agent                  is chosen from citrate salt, malonate salt,                  adipate salt, maleate salt, fumarate salt,                  iminodiacetate salt, and nitrilotriacetate                  salt.

PN CN107746743-A
TI Carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of bitter gourd oil from bitter gourd seed used e.g. for treating osteoarthritis involves obtaining bitter gourd seed powder, setting temperature and pressure of cylinders, extracting and weighing.
AU HE C
AE BAOZHONG HAOFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BAOZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018216875
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of bitter gourd oil from bitter gourd seed comprises removing shells of bitter gourd seeds, washing, drying, and grinding to obtain bitter gourd seed kernel powder; setting temperature of extracting and two separating cylinders; placing barrel containing bitter gourd seed kernel powder into extraction cylinder; starting carbon dioxide circulating system, and regulating pressures of extraction and separating cylinders; starting recording time; and periodic discharging of bitter gourd oil from discharge port of separating cylinders, weighing and recording.
   USE - Carbon dioxide supercritical method of extracting bitter gourd oil from bitter gourd seed used for relieving muscle pain, healing wounds, removing blood stasis, diminishing inflammation, treating osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis and diabetic leg and hand, preparing bitter gourd polypeptide or protein powder and as industrial raw material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy, uses non-toxic, and non-residual carbon dioxide, has stable and recyclable air supply, high extracting efficiency and low production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of bitter gourd oil from bitter gourd seed comprises removing shells of bitter gourd seeds, washing, drying, and grinding to obtain bitter gourd seed kernel powder; setting temperature of extracting cylinder and two separating cylinders (I, II); placing barrel containing bitter gourd seed kernel powder into extraction cylinder; starting carbon dioxide circulating system, and regulating pressures of extraction cylinder and separating cylinders (I,II); starting recording time at preset temperature and pressure values of cylinders; and periodic discharging of bitter gourd oil from discharge port of separating cylinders, weighing and recording. The extraction cylinder is set at 32-40 degrees C and 35-40 MPa. The separating cylinder I is set at 50-55 degrees C and 5.0-6.0 MPa, while separating cylinder II is set at 40-45 degrees C and 4.0-5.0 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide supercritical extraction is carried out for 6-12 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Analgesic; Thrombolytic; Anticoagulant; Antiinflammatory; Antiarthritic; Osteopathic; Antirheumatic; Antidiabetic; Vulnerary.

TI Production of oral rotavirus vaccine using a            disposable sheet-shaped carrier reaction bag comprises            e.g. seeding, culturing adherent cell, inoculating,            calculating cell viability, observing cell morphology            and dispersion degree and carrying out ultrafiltration            and concentration.
AU FAN Z
AE SHANGHAI BOAODE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B62007
AB    NOVELTY - Production of oral rotavirus vaccine using a                disposable sheet-shaped carrier reaction bag                comprises e.g. (1) adding the adherent cell culture                medium to the sheet carrier bag and making to reach                the working volume, passing the mixed gas of 5-10%                carbon dioxide (CO2), adding the sheet carrier bag                into the shaker, where the equilibration time is                more than 5 hours or overnight, Infiltrating the                sheet carrier, and setting the shaker to the                temperature of 37&#177;0.5&#8451;, the                swing speed of 8-12rpm, and the shaker angle of                5-10&#176;, (2) seeding and culturing adherent                cells, (3) inoculating 1-9E+5 cells/ml cells, (4)                inoculating with inoculation temperature of                37&#177;0.5&#8451;, rocking speed of                2-55rpm, shaking angle of 2-15&#176;, mixed gas                of 3-8% carbon dioxide (CO2), and (5) inoculating                cells into the balanced sheet carrier bag, where                the sheet carrier bag is cloudy, observing large                number of non-adherent cells under the                microscope.
   USE - The method is useful for production of oral                rotavirus vaccine using a disposable sheet-shaped                carrier reaction bag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to scale up; has simple and                airtight in operation, which significantly reduces                the risk of contamination and improve                batch-to-batch consistency, and real-time detection                and control of key process parameters.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of oral rotavirus vaccine using a                disposable sheet-shaped carrier reaction bag                comprises (1) adding the adherent cell culture                medium to the sheet carrier bag and making to reach                the working volume, passing the mixed gas of 5-10%                carbon dioxide (CO2), adding the sheet carrier bag                into the shaker, where the equilibration time is                more than 5 hours or overnight, Infiltrating the                sheet carrier, and setting the shaker to the                temperature of 37&#177;0.5&#176;C, the                swing speed of 8-12rpm, and the shaker angle of                5-10&#176;, (2) seeding and culturing adherent                cells, (3) inoculating 1-9E+5 cells/ml cells, (4)                inoculating with inoculation temperature of                37&#177;0.5&#176;C, rocking speed of                2-55rpm, shaking angle of 2-15&#176;, mixed gas                of 3-8% carbon dioxide (CO2), (5) inoculating cells                into the balanced sheet carrier bag, where the                sheet carrier bag is cloudy, observing numerous                non-adherent cells under the microscope, placing                the sheet carrier bag on the shaking table, passing                the mixed gas of 3-8% CO2, mix for 2-100 minutes,                where the shaker settings are swing speed is 2-55                revolutions/minute, temperature is                37&#177;0.5&#176;C, shaker angle is                2-15&#176;, (6) mixing, allowing to stand or                culturing at a low rotation speed for 0.5-4 hours                to observe the liquid in the bag being clear, (7)                setting the shaker to the swing speed of 10                revolutions/minute, the temperature of                37&#177;0.5&#176;C, and the shaker angle of                5-10&#176; after the bag is clarified, passing                mixed gas of 3-8% CO2 through, starting the medium                perfusion from the second day, where the perfusion                volume is 0.5-3 working volumes per day, taking                samples every day to measure the sugar                concentration, and calculating the daily sugar                consumption to maintain the sugar concentration                above 1 g/l, and culturing for 2-7 days, (8)                performing 1-5 long-term digestions on the cells in                the sheet carrier bag when the cells have grown to                2-7 days, (9) adding Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered                saline (DPBS), mixing well, discharging, repeating                1-3 times, and discharge DPBS, (10) adding trypsin                digestion solution, infiltrating the flaky carrier,                draining the pancreatin after digestion for 1-60                minutes, adding serum-free medium, beating the bag                to make the cells fall off from the flaky carrier,                draining and collecting the cell suspension,                repeating this step 1-3 times, taking samples,                counting, calculating the cell viability, and                observing the cell morphology and dispersion degree                under a microscope, (11) inoculating the cells                collected and combining after digestion into 2-20                times the volume of the sheet carrier bag that has                been equilibrated overnight in advance at a certain                cell density, (12) subjecting the cells in the                sheet-shaped carrier bag to 1-15 short-term                digestions, where the digestion time of each                digestion is preferably 1-40 minutes, (13)                repeating steps 2)-7) until cell production reaches                late logarithmic growth or plateau phase, (14)                introducing the number of cells to the late                logarithmic growth phase or the plateau phase,                taking the sheet-shaped carrier and counting it                with the citrate crystal violet method, (15)                preheating the phosphate buffered saline to room                temperature to 37&#177;0.5&#176;C, draining                the medium in the bag, adding 1 working volume of                phosphate buffered saline, mixing well, draining,                repeating washing and changing 1-3 times, draining                the phosphate buffered saline, replacing with the                maintenance solution containing 0.5-10                &#956;g/ml trypsin that has been incubated in                advance, and calculating the inoculation amount                according to the virus multiplicity of infection                (MOI) of 0.001-0.1, (16) adding trypsin to make the                final concentration 5-25 &#956;g/ml, incubating                at 37&#177;0.5&#176;C for 0.5-2 hours, and                shaking the virus solution 3-6 times during this                period, (17) adding the activated virus liquid into                the bag, mixing, closing the liquid inlet and                outlet, where the operating parameters are rotation                speed is 2-20rpm, angle is 2-12&#176;,                temperature is 37&#177;0.5&#176;C,                dissolved oxygen is 20-80%, pH is 7.0-7.8, (18)                opening the maintenance fluid perfusion culture on                the second day after receiving the poison, where                the perfusion volume is 0.5-3 working volumes/day,                (19) harvesting the maintenance liquid into the                harvest liquid bucket, and placing in an                environment of 2-8 &#176;C, (20) stopping virus                culturing when culturing until the cells fall off                more than 60%, disconnecting the other pipes of the                bag, adding together with the harvest liquid bucket                into an environment below minus 15 &#8451; for                freezing, and thawing it at room temperature,                repeating this for 1-3 times to lyse the cells and                release the virus, (21) carrying out clarification                filtration, filtering with a 1.0-0.8 &#956;m                sleeve filter to remove cell debris, (22) carrying                out ultrafiltration and concentration according to                the virus content and the composition of the                medium, concentrating the clarified virus liquid by                10-20 times, adding virus protective agent after                concentration, or adding it to the harvest liquid,                and (23) adding the virus protective agent, which                is virus stock solution, where the pentavalent live                attenuated rotavirus vaccine contains five main                circulating serotypes of G1, G2, G3, G4 and G9, and                covers 90% of the main circulating strains of group                A rotavirus. The active ingredients of the vaccine                are: G1, G2, G3, G4, G9 antigens, and the titer of                each serotype is 1-5X106active units/ml. The virus                protective agent includes 50-75 g/l sucrose, 1-1.5                g/l dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, 0.4-0.6g/l                potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.8-1 g/l glutamic                acid monosodium salt, and 10-20 mMg/l calcium                chloride.

PT P
PN EP4056893-A1; WO2022189423-A1
TI Method for performing steam cracking for producing            olefins by using steam cracking arrangement, involves            adiabatically expanding superheated high pressure steam            at first pressure level to second pressure level.
AU HOERENZ M
   ZELLHUBER D M
   STEGEMANN R
   SCHMIDT G
   ZELLHUBER M
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 2022B6448K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using steam generation                arrangements (30) with superheated high pressure                steam (HP) at first pressure level between 30 and                175 bar absolute pressure and at a first                temperature level and no steam at higher                temperature level than the first temperature level.                The superheated high pressure steam at the first                pressure level is adiabatically expanded to a                second pressure level below the first pressure                level such that temperature level is lowered to a                second temperature level. The first temperature                level is selected such that the intermediate                temperature level reached at intermediate pressure                levels of more than 20 bar during the adiabatic                expansion is between 5 and 120 Kelvin above the dew                point of steam at the respective intermediate                pressure level during the adiabatic                expansion.
   USE - Method for performing steam cracking for                producing base chemicals i.e. olefins, by using a                steam cracking arrangement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables allowing desired cracking                reactions to take place inside the cracking coils,                so that process gas can be continuously enriched in                cracking products and local carbon dioxide                emissions of industrial processes can extend to                operation of steam cracking plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for performing steam cracking.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system                for performing steam cracking for producing base                chemicals.
   HPSuperheated high pressure steam
   21Additional water-boiling primary quench                exchanger
   22Primary quench exchangers
   23Secondary quench exchanger
   30Steam generation arrangements

PT P
PN CN115007976-A
TI Welding repair method for cast iron crack defects,            involves using nickel-based welding core with            de-oxidation layer to repair welding at welding gap            based on manual argon tungsten-arc welding.
AU ZHOU G
AE ZHOU H (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 2022B9422H
AB    NOVELTY - Welding repair method involves (s1) processing                a repair welding groove on an iron casting along                the extending direction of the crack defects,                deoxidizing the surface of the repair welding                groove and the surface around the groove to obtain                the iron casting without the oxygen atoms, (s2)                deoxidizing the outer surface of a prepared                nickel-based core wire to obtain the nickel-based                welding core with a deoxidized layer, (s3) using                nickel-based welding core with a deoxidized layer                for repairing the welding gap until the repair                welding of the cast iron crack defect is completed,                based on manual argon tungsten-arc welding. The                repair welding process is carried out at the                no-load voltage of 50-70 V, the welding voltage of                25-30 V, and argon gas flow rate of 10-15                L/minutes.
   USE - Welding repair method for cast iron crack                defects.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the technical problem that                the crack defect repair welding defect of the cast                iron is easy to introduce oxygen atoms, which cause                difficult welding, low welding quality, and easy                generation of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide                gas hole and crack defect. The addition of thorium                oxide to the cerium thorium tungsten electrode has                low work function, so that the welding arc can burn                stably, so as to improving the quality of repair                welding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The manual argon                  tungsten-arc welting utilizes a tungsten                  electrode comprising cerium tungsten electrode or                  a cerium thorium tungsten electrode.Preferred Property: The diameter of the                  tungsten electrode is &#966;1.5mm or                  &#966;2.0mm.Preferred Composition: The content of                  thorium oxide in the cerium thorium tungsten                  electrode is 1-2%.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); X24 (Electric Welding)
MC M23-D01B4; M23-B02; X24-B06; X24-B04
IP B23K-009/167; B23K-009/235; B23K-009/32

TI Attenuating opioid induced ventilatory and/or            respiratory depression and/or augmenting opioid induced            analgesia, involves administering therapeutically            effective amount of composition comprising cystine            ester or its adduct, tautomer.
AU LEWIS S J
   GASTON B M
AE UNIV CASE WESTERN RESERVE (UCWR-C)
GA 2022B0570C
AB    NOVELTY - Attenuating opioid induced ventilatory and/or                respiratory depression and/or augmenting opioid                induced analgesia in a subject in need involves                administering to the subject a therapeutically                effective amount of a composition comprising a                cystine ester or its adduct, pharmaceutically                acceptable salt, tautomer, or solvate.
   USE - Method for attenuating opioid induced                ventilatory and/or respiratory depression and/or                augmenting opioid induced analgesia in a subject,                where the opioid comprises at least one of                alfentanil, buprenorphine, butorphanol,                carfentanil, codeine, diamorphine, dextromoramide,                dezocine, dihydrocodeine, fentanyl, hydrocodone,                hydromorphone, levorphanol, meperidine, meptazinol,                methadone, morphine, nalbuphine, nalorphine, opium,                oxycodone, oxymorphone, pentazocine, propoxyphene,                remifentanil, sufentanil, tapentadol, and tramadol,                and their pharmaceutically acceptable salts (all                claimed) for stimulating ventilatory and/or                respiratory drive, used for treating breathing                diseases and/or disorders associated with impaired                ventilatory and/or respiratory drive.
Test details are described but no results                given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is effective at reversing the                narcotic induced respiratory depression, utilizes                the composition that is effective to elicit                sustained reversal of opioid elicited disturbances                in ventilatory parameters (e.g., decreases in                frequency of breathing, tidal volume, and minute                ventilation), Arterial Blood Gas (ABG) chemistry                (e.g., decreases in pH, partial pressure of oxygen                (PO2), oxygen saturation (sO2) with increases in                partial pressure of carbon dioxide (PCO2)) and                alveolar-arterial (A-a) gradient while maintaining                or augmenting opioid sedation and analgesia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Process: The therapeutically                  effective amount is an amount effective to                  stimulate the ventilatory and/or respiratory                  drive of the subject and/or augment opioid                  induced analgesia. The opioid administration                  elicits disturbances in ventilatory parameters,                  arterial blood gas (ABG) chemistry, and A-a                  gradient while causing sedation and analgesia.                  The administration of the therapeutically                  effective amount of the composition is effective                  to elicit sustained reversal of opioid elicited                  disturbances in ventilatory parameters, ABG                  chemistry, and A-a gradient while augmenting                  opioid induced sedation and/or analgesia. The                  opioid is administered systemically by                  intravenous infusion. The composition is                  administered concurrently with opioid                  administration and/or up to about 120 minutes                  before or after initiation of opioid                  administration.Preferred Components: The cystine ester is                  cystine ester of formula (I). The cystine ester                  is a cystine dialkyl ester. The cystine dialkyl                  ester is a D-cystine dialkyl ester or its adduct,                  pharmaceutically acceptable salt, tautomer, or                  solvate. The cystine dialkyl ester is selected                  from cystine dimethyl ester, and/or cystine                  diethyl ester, or their adduct, pharmaceutically                  acceptable salt, tautomer, or solvate. The                  cystine dialkyl ester is D-cystine dimethyl ester                  or their adduct, pharmaceutically acceptable                  salt, tautomer, or solvate. The opioid comprises                  morphine and/or fentanyl.R1and R2=H, optionally substituted 1-24C                  alkyl, 2-24C alkenyl, 2-24C alkynyl, 3-20C aryl,                  heterocycloalkenyl containing from 5-6 ring                  atoms, heteroaryl, and heterocyclyl containing                  from 5-14 ring atoms, where at least one of R1and                  R2 is not a H.                                                                                                                              
   ACTIVITY - Respiratory-Gen; Analgesic .
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition is administered to the subject                systemically, parenterally, intravenously,                intramuscularly, intrapleurally, intravascularly,                intrapericardially, intraarterially, intrathecally,                intracapsularly, intraorbitally, intracardiacly,                intradermally, intraperitoneally, transtracheally,                subcutaneously, subcuticularly, intra-articularly,                subcapsularly, subarachnoidly, intraspinally and by                intrastemal injection and infusion in a dosage of                0.01-300 mg/kg body weight.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.)

TI Device useful for preparing glutathione liposome,            comprises wall material chamber whose side wall is            provided with wall material feeding pipe, and pressure            reducing tank connected with horizontal reaction tank,            where wall material chamber and core chamber are            provided with ultrasonic wave generator.
AU CHEN L
   YANG X
   XIE L
   GAN H
   SUN Y
AE CHENGDU KEJIAN BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B49979
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a horizontal reaction tank                whose inner part is divided into a wall material                chamber and a core material chamber. The side wall                of the wall material chamber provided with a wall                material feeding pipe. The side wall of the core                material chamber is set with a core material                feeding pipe. The wall material feeding pipe and                core material feeding pipe are Y-shaped pipeline                which is set with two fluid inlets and one fluid                outlet. The fluid outlet is located in the                horizontal reaction tank. The supercritical carbon                dioxide production device connected with a fluid                inlet of the wall material feeding pipe. The core                material feeding pipe is connected with the other                fluid inlet of the wall material feeding pipe. The                core material constant flow pump connected with the                other fluid inlet of the core material feeding                pipe. A pressure reducing tank connected with the                horizontal reaction tank. The wall material chamber                and the core chamber are provided with an                ultrasonic wave generator.
   USE - The device is useful for preparing glutathione                liposome.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can realize the large-scale                production of glutathione, and has less solvent                residue.

   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic diagram of the                device for preparing glutathione liposome.

TI Gas concentration detecting device e.g.            non-dispersive infrared (NDIR) gas sensor for detecting            concentration of carbon dioxide gas in air, has optical            gas chamber whose surface is coated with plating layer            for reflecting light beam.
AU FAN H
   ZHOU Z
AE SHENZHEN HOTO INTELLIGENT CONTROL TECHNO (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B4471N

PN CN114602422-A
TI Lithium silicate base bifunctional material            comprises capture layer, isolation layer and catalytic            layer, isolation layer supported on the capture layer            and the catalytic layer supported on isolation            layer.
AU RAN J
   LV Z
   QIN C
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2022833415
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium silicate base bifunctional material                comprises capture layer, isolation layer and                catalytic layer. The isolation layer is supported                on the capture layer and the catalytic layer is                supported on the isolation layer. The capture layer                is lithium silicate. The isolation layer is an                isolation metal oxide. The catalytic layer is                catalytic metal, where the mass fraction of lithium                silicate is 80-95%, the mass fraction of isolation                metal is 2.5-10% and the mass fraction of catalytic                metal is 2.5-10%.
   USE - Used as a lithium silicate base bifunctional                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high carbon dioxide                adsorption capacity, stable cyclic adsorption and                desorption/in-situ conversion performance, high                conversion rate and good product selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing lithium silicate base bifunctional                material comprising (a) loading isolated metal                oxide precursor onto lithium silicate by                impregnation method and calcining in air at                400-600&#176;C for 2-6 hours to obtain sample                loaded with isolated metal oxide, (b) loading                catalytic metal precursor onto the sample prepared                by (a) by impregnation method, calcining in air at                400-600&#176;C for 2-6 hours to obtain lithium                silicate based bifunctional material, where the                isolating metal oxide precursor is nitrate or                soluble organic acid salt containing isolating                metal element and catalytic metal precursors are                nitrates or soluble organic acid salts containing                catalytic metal elements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The isolating metal                  oxide is titanium dioxide, yttrium oxide,                  gadolinium (III) oxide, cerium(IV) oxide or                  calcium Oxide. The catalytic metal is nickel or                  cobalt. In the step (a), the addition amount of                  isolated metal oxide is 5-10 wt.% of material. In                  the step (b), the addition amount of catalytic                  metal is 2.5-7.5 wt.% of material. The silicon                  dioxide is hydrophilic gas-phase silicon                  dioxide.Preferred Method: The preparation of lithium                  silicate step (a) comprises (a1) taking powder                  material lithium nitrate, gas phase silicon                  dioxide and levoglucose in a molar ratio of                  40-41:10:16-36, blending and adding deionized                  water to dissolve the blend completely, uniformly                  mixing at 60-95&#8451; for 4-8 hours and                  drying for 24-48 hours to obtain a blend gel, (b)                  uniformly heating mixed gel obtained in step (a)                  to 400-650&#8451; under nitrogen, converting                  nitrogen into air, heating to 700-850&#8451;                  and insulating for 1-6 hours, (c) cooling to room                  temperature, taking out the product, i.e. powdery                  lithium silicate and grinding via 80-200 mesh                  sieve to obtain lithium silicate powder                  adsorption material.

Microalgae are grown in a photobioreactor with a wedge-shaped bottom, on a Tamiya nutrient medium with the addition of 0.01% ASD F-2 preparation, with constant aeration and stirring of the suspension using an aquarium pump and a compressor with simultaneous supply of 0.05 l/min carbon dioxide, at a temperature of 30C and continuous illumination with 5000 Lux fluorescent lamps. The photobioreactor for growing microalgae has a rectangular cross-section, the distance between the large walls is 10 cm. The wedge-shaped bottom has ascending and descending parts, the angle between which, as well as the angles between each part and the adjacent vertical wall, are equal to 120. The length of the working vertical wall connected to the descending part of the bottom is L, the projection of the descending part of the bottom on the horizontal base is 2/3 L, and the projection of the ascending part of the bottom on the horizontal base is 1/3 L. This bottom structure improves the mixing of the microalgae suspension.
   USE - Biotechnology.
   ADVANTAGE - Increase in the growth of biomass and the preservation of the required quality with minimal time and energy costs, as well as the simplicity and accessibility of the method.

PN WO2022060281-A1; NL2026492-B1
TI Preparing animal for slaughter involves subj ecting animal to gaseous muscle relaxant without inducing unconsciousness of animal, and stunning animal in space using gas composition suitable for stunning.
AU KIEZEBRINK H
AE KIEZEBRINK H (KIEZ-Individual)
   KIEZEBRINK H (KIEZ-Individual)
GA 202242959J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves subjecting the animal to a gaseous muscle relaxant without inducing unconsciousness of the animal and the animal is stunned in a space using a gas composition suitable for stunning. The animal is subjected to the relaxant before being unconscious. The relaxant is introduced as foam. The ambient air is expelled from the container using first foam comprising the relaxent. The gas composition comprises relaxant and oxygen. The first foam is broken down before the second foam is introduced. The gases contain nitrogen, argon, carbon monoxide, nitrous oxide, or carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing animal for slaughter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares an animal for slaughter with a reduced chance of damage to the meat. The muscle relaxant reduces the strength and/or number of convulsions, reducing the risk of bones breaking or becoming dislocated. The method allows for multiple animals to be stunned simultaneously, which helps to reduce stress, for example in pigs which show a strong stress reaction when separated from their mates. The oxygen concentration is preferably at least 10 (preferably at least 15) vol.% so as to reduce discomfort by the lack of oxygen. The animal effectively is contacted with the gas composition suitable for stunning and saves on the amount of gas composition used. The foam expels ambient air around the animal and could even be used to expel substantially all air from the space.
DC D12 (Butchering, meat treatment, processing poultry or fish (A22).)

PT P
PN US2022085428-A1
TI Battery thermal runaway detection sensor system for use within battery enclosure, has microcontroller that determines power management and signal conditioned output on concentration of specific battery venting gases.
AU ROBERTS N
   ENGLE B A
AE AMPHENOL THERMOMETRICS INC (AMPJ-C)
GA 2022396172
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a gas sensor for detecting a venting condition of a battery cell of hydrogen, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide, and providing a sensed output in real time. A microcontroller determines power management and signal conditioned output on the concentration of specific battery venting gases based on the sensed output from the gas sensor. A secondary sensor detects a secondary condition of the battery and provides information on a rate of progression of the cell venting and thermal runaway. A sensor housing encloses the sensor and the secondary sensor, and a hydrocarbon sensor.
   USE - Battery thermal runaway detection sensor system for use in a battery enclosure.
   ADVANTAGE - The detection system addresses the challenges of fast, robust thermal runaway detection within a battery enclosure that is generally agnostic to electrochemistry, cell packaging, as well as battery configuration (series/parallel) by identifying attributes of initial cell venting that are shared between numerous design types and responding to venting gases of a failing cell. The risk of explosion can be reduced by design to incorporate a controlled venting location in the cell, but risk of fire and explosion due to thermal runaway has not been eliminated in most liquid electrolyte lithium-based batteries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for detecting battery cell venting.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a battery thermal runaway detection sensor system.
DC T01 (Digital Computers); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

TI Poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant used for forming polymer polyol, characterized in that molecular structure of poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant contains unsaturated bond, where molecular structure of poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant contains carbonate ether bonds.
AU WANG X
   LI Z
   MIAO Y
   QIAO L
AE CHANGCHUN FAWAY ADIENT AUTOMOTIVE SYSTEM (FAWG-C)
   CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202235738R
AB    NOVELTY - Poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant, characterized in that the molecular structure of the poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant contains only one unsaturated bond, where the molecular structure of the poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant contains carbonate ether bonds.
   USE - The poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant is useful for forming polymer polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The dispersant ensures that each macromolecule in the prepared macromolecular dispersant contains only one unsaturated bond, avoids the possibility of crosslinking and gelation in the subsequent reaction, and ensures the stability of the product, and has excellent compatibility with the basic polyether polycarbonate ether polyol, and the prepared polymer polyol has lower viscosity and high storage stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) A preparation method of poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant, involving subjecting carbon dioxide and an epoxy compound to a copolymerization reaction, in the presence of a double metal cyanide catalyst and an unsaturated bond-containing carboxylic acid compound initiator, to obtain the dispersant.
   (2) A polymer polyol is prepared from raw materials comprising the above dispersant.
   (3) A preparation method of the polymer polyol, involving mixing the polycarbonate ether polyol base polyether, compound containing unsaturated double bonds, free radical initiator, chain transfer agent and a macromolecular dispersant, and reacting to obtain the polymer polyol, where the macromolecular dispersant comprises the poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The number-average molecular weight of the poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant is 1000-20000. The content of carbon dioxide in the poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant is 1-35 %mass. The temperature of the copolymerization reaction is 5-120degrees Celsius, and the time of the copolymerization reaction is 1-48 hours. The temperature of the reaction is 110-145degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 1-10 hours.Preferred Components: The poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant is a macromolecular dispersant for preparing polymer polyols, where the mass ratio of the poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant to the polymer polyol is 1-20%. The base polyether of the polymer polyol is a polycarbonate ether polyol, where the content of carbon dioxide in the poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant is 1-35 %mass, and the mass content of carbon dioxide in the polycarbonate ether polyol is 1-35 %mass, and the carbon dioxide mass content in the poly(carbonate-ether) polyol dispersant is within plus minus5% of the carbon dioxide mass content in the polycarbonate ether polyol. The initiator for preparing the polycarbonate ether polyol comprises 1-10C polyol compounds, hydroxyl-containing polymers with a molecular weight of less than 2000 g/mol, aliphatic saturated carboxylic acids, alicyclic saturated carboxylic acids and/or aromatic family of carboxylic acids.Preferred Composition: The content of the macromolecular dispersant in the polymer polyol raw material is 1-20 %mass. Preferred Method: The step of mixing involves adding portion of the polycarbonate ether polyol to a reaction device, and dripping the mixed solution formed by the remaining polycarbonate ether polyol, unsaturated double bond-containing compound, free radical initiator, chain transfer agent and macromolecular dispersant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The double metal cyanide comprises Zn3(Co(CN)6)2, Zn3(Ni(CN)6)2 and/or Zn3(Fe(CN)6)2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound comprises ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide, cyclohexane oxide, epichlorohydrin and/or styrene oxide, where the mass ratio of the epoxy compound to the unsaturated bond-containing carboxylic acid compound initiator is (40-200):1, and the mass ratio of the epoxy compound to the catalyst is (100-10000): 1, and the pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.01-12 MPa. The unsaturated bond-containing carboxylic acid compound starter comprises maleic acid, fumaric acid, itaconic acid, ricinoleic acid, aconitic acid and/or trans-aconic acid. The compound containing unsaturated double bonds comprise styrene, acrylonitrile, methacrylonitrile, alpha-methylstyrene, butylstyrene, methacrylic acid, butyl methacrylate, acrylate, maleate, hydroxyethyl methacrylate and/or acrylic acid. The free radical initiator comprises azobisisobutyronitrile, dimethyl azobisisobutyrate, benzoyl peroxide, tert-butyl peroxy(2-ethylhexanoate) and/or tert-butyl peroxydiethyl acetate. The chain transfer agent comprises isopropanol, dodecyl mercaptan, 2-butanol, methanol, isobutanol and/or isopropanol..

TI Fuel supply system for ship, has compressor that is connected to third pipe to pressurize boil-off gas generated in storage tanks, and aftercooler that condenses and liquefies boil off gas pressurized through compressor.
AU WOO C D
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 202229920V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first pipe (L1) which is connected to the storage tanks (100) to supply fuel to the engine. The suction drum (101) is connected to the first pipe. A third pipe (L3) is configured to connect the storage tanks and the suction drum. A compressor (201) is connected to the third pipe to pressurize the BOG generated in the storage tanks. An aftercooler (202) is configured to condense and liquefy the BOG pressurized through the compressor. A tenth pipe is configured to connect the rear ends of the compressor and the aftercooler and the economizer (204). A heat recovery unit (205) is connected to the tenth pipe between the rear end of the compressor and the aftercooler and the economizer.
   USE - Marine fuel supply system for ship (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces emission of carbon dioxide by supplying ammonia as a fuel. The system re-liquefies ammonia boil-off gas continuously generated in the storage tank of the ammonia fuel without using a separate compressor for a re-Liquefaction equipment, so that economical fuel supply system for ships can be constructed for supplying the fuel and reliquefying the boil off gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of fuel supply system.
