PN CN114057355-A
TI Recycling domestic sewage comprises e.g. injecting domestic sewage into filtering device, performing acidification and methane fermentation treatment, discharging processed domestic sewage into aerobic tank and pumping domestic sewage in temporary pool and aerobic pool for sterilizing treatment.
AU ZHOU Z
   ZHANG Z
AE HUANDE FUJIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022369095
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling domestic sewage comprises e.g. injecting the domestic sewage into the filtering device for filtering, adding hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring, allowing to stand for 1-3 hours, then adding flocculating agent in domestic sewage, stirring uniformly, allowing to stand and settling, pumping the upper clear liquid into the first anaerobic reaction tank, discharging the mixed liquid of the bottom sludge and sewage into the second anaerobic reaction tank, adding anaerobic acid producing bacteria and methane producing bacteria in the first reaction tank, performing acidification and methane fermentation treatment, hydrolyzing the mixed solution of sludge and sewage, discharging processed domestic sewage into the aerobic tank, adjusting the pH of the domestic sewage, and pumping the domestic sewage in the temporary pool and the aerobic pool into the sterilizing pool for sterilizing treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling domestic sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can convert the organic matter in the sewage is into methane by methane generating bacteria, which reduces the discharge of carbon dioxide, before the domestic sewage is hydrolyzed, can settling the insoluble macromolecular substance in the domestic sewage in water, and hydrolyzing the mixed liquid after sedimentation, and the supernatant of the upper layer is carried out to the next processing step, and improve the processing efficiency of the sewage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flocculating agent is aluminum polychloride, polymeric aluminum sulfate, polymeric ferric chloride, and/or polymeric ferric sulfate. The methane generating bacteria is methane bacteria, methane bacteria and/or methane eight azide.Preferred Method: The method further comprises (iv) hydrolyzing the mixed solution of sludge and sewage; (v) after the mixed solution is hydrolyzed, adding anaerobic acid bacteria and methane to generate bacteria in the second reaction tank, performing acidification and methane fermentation treatment; (vi) further adding flocculating agent in the mixed solution, stirring uniformly, allowing to stand and settling, pumping the upper clear liquid into the temporary placing tank, discharging the sludge at the bottom into the sludge tank; (vii) discharging the domestic sewage processed in step (iii) into the aerobic tank, further decomposing the pollutant in the domestic sewage; (viii) adjusting the pH of the domestic sewage, controlling the pH at 6-8; and (ix) pumping the domestic sewage in the temporary pool and the aerobic pool into the sterilizing pool for sterilizing treatment.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

PT P
PN WO2022034519-A1
TI Increasing yield from oxidative dehydrogenation            reactor, comprises controlling temperature of feed gas            composition, flowing feed gas composition through a            feed preheater to form heated feed gas, and flowing            into the reactor.
AU GOODARZNIA S
   SIMANZHENKOV V
   OLAYIWOLA B
   GENT D
   KLUTHE J
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 202226728F
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing yield from oxidative                dehydrogenation (ODH) reactor, comprises:                controlling a temperature of a feed gas composition                at less than 250 degrees C; flowing the feed gas                composition through a feed preheater to form a                heated feed gas, where in the feed preheater the                feed gas composition is heated to between 150                degrees C and 250 degrees C; and flowing the heated                feed gas into the ODH reactor for less than 15                seconds after leaving the feed preheater.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing yield from                oxidative dehydrogenation reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: minimizes side reactions; avoids                side reactions; maximizes carbon dioxide output                upstream minimizes content of inert diluent that                would need to be added to the stream before the                next reactor; and increases yields from oxidative                dehydrogenation reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) feed preheater for ODH reactor, where the                feed preheater is configured to heat a reactor feed                to between 150 degrees C and 250 degrees C and flow                the reactor feed into the ODH reactor within 15                seconds of the reactor feed reaching a maximum                temperature; and
   (2) ODH reactor comprising a feed preheater,                where the feed preheater is used to heat a reactor                feed to 150 degrees C and 250 degrees C, and to                introduce the reactor feed into the ODH reactor                within less than 15 seconds of the reactor feed                reaching a maximum temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Composition: The feed preheater                  comprises effluent piping selected in length to                  flow the reactor feed into the ODH reactor within                  15 seconds of the reactor feed reaching a maximum                  temperature. The ODH reactor comprises a second                  feed preheater for heating a second reactor feed                  to 150 degrees C and 250 degrees C, and to                  introduce the second reactor feed into the ODH                  reactor within less than 15 seconds of the                  reactor feed reaching a maximum temperature.                  Preferred Components: In the feed preheater: the                  feed preheater is attached to the ODH reactor;                  the feed preheater is used to be heated by excess                  heat from the ODH reactor; the feed preheater                  comprises tubing incorporated into the ODH                  reactor; length of the tubing incorporated into                  the ODH reactor is selected to adjust a residence                  time and a temperature of the reactor feed; and                  the feed preheater is used to flow the reactor                  feed into the ODH reactor within 13 seconds of                  the reactor feed reaching a maximum temperature.                  The ODH reactor comprises a feed tube disposed                  within the ODH reactor, where the feed tube is                  heated by contents of the ODH reactor, and a                  length of the feed tube is selected to heat the                  reactor feed to between 150 degrees C and 250                  degrees C. In the ODH reactor, the second reactor                  feed comprises acetic acid, ethanol and/or                  recycle stream.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  heating the feed preheater with heat from the ODH                  reactor; flowing the feed gas composition into                  tubing within the ODH reactor as the feed                  preheater; controlling a residence time and the                  temperature of the feed gas composition by                  selecting a length of the tubing; residence time                  and the temperature of the feed gas composition                  by selecting a length of piping coupling the                  preheater to the reactor; controlling the                  temperature of the feed gas composition to less                  than 200 degrees C before flowing the feed gas                  composition through the feed preheater; flowing                  the heated feed gas into the ODH reactor within                  13 seconds of reaching less than 250 degrees C;                  forming the feed gas composition by blending                  steam, a light hydrocarbon, and oxygen in a                  flooded blending tank; controlling temperature of                  interstage feed stream at less than 250 degrees                  C, flowing the interstage feed stream through an                  interstage feed preheater to form a heated                  interstage feed stream, where the interstage feed                  stream is heated to 150 degrees C and 250 degrees                  C, and injecting the heated interstage feed                  stream into the ODH reactor in less than 13                  seconds after the interstage feed stream is                  heated; forming the interstage feed stream in a                  second ODH reactor; cooling effluent from the                  second ODH reactor in effluent cooler.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of carbon                dioxide-ethane-oxygen empty tube experiment at 300                degrees c and preheater residence time of 13                seconds that do not support the claims.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-E03
IP B01J-004/00; C07C-011/04; C07C-005/48

   (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The concentration of the artemisinin solution is 0.00001-40 g/l, preferably 0.4-40 g/l. In the step (i), the flowers and leaves of industrial hemp are dried and crushed, and passing through a 40-60 meshes sieve to obtain the crushed product. In the step (i), the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 0.8-1.2 kg/hour, and the extraction is carried out at 45-55degreesC under 15-25 MPa for 4-5 hours.

PN CN113816346-A
TI Process for utilizing modified metal organic            framework to purify hydrogen selenide for e.g.            manufacturing infrared optical material, involves            filtering crude hydrogen selenide product to remove            solid particles, removing impurities, hydrogen sulfide            and micron-scale solid particles, and            pressurizing.
AU ZHOU Z
   LIU D
   CHEN Q
   SUN M
   YIN Z
AE SUZHOU JINHONG GAS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202205620Q
AB    NOVELTY - Process for utilizing modified metal organic                framework to purify hydrogen selenide involves (1)                adding crude hydrogen selenide product to                micron-scale screen filter to remove solid                particles, (2) removing impurities and other                components in the filtered hydrogen selenide                product through rectifying tower (r1) equipped with                mixed adsorbent (a1), (3) feeding the obtained                hydrogen selenide product into rectifying tower                (r2) equipped with mixed adsorbent (a2) to remove                hydrogen sulfide, (4) passing the obtained hydrogen                selenide product into micron-scale screen filter to                further remove micron-scale solid particles, and                (5) pressurizing the hydrogen selenide product by a                booster pump. The mixed adsorbent (a1) comprises 4A                molecular sieve and/or 13X molecular sieve. The                impurity gas components comprise moisture, oxygen,                nitrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide. The                mixed adsorbent (a2) comprises modified metal                organic framework and/or activated carbon.
   USE - Process for utilizing modified metal organic                framework to purify hydrogen selenide for                manufacturing infrared optical material,                photovoltaic solar battery and doped gas in LED and                integrated circuit manufacturing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses modified metal organic                framework material which solves the aperture                problem of the common metal organic framework                material, and improves desulfurization effect. The                product has high stability and long-term service                life when compared with the existing adsorption                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of 4A                  molecules to 13X molecules in the mixed adsorbent                  (a1) is 1:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The pore size of the                  modified metal organic framework is 0.140 nm.                  Preferred Composition: The mixing ratio of                  modified metal organic framework and activated                  carbon in mixed adsorbent (a2) is 1:5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The inside component of                  the filter is a micron stainless steel                  mesh.

GA 2022030432
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synergistic removal of cyanide from                metal heat treatment residues by sensitizer and                microwave, which involves: i. adding mineral                particles with an iron oxide content of not less                than 50% as a sensitizer to the solid metal heat                treatment residue, and control the weight ratio of                the sensitizer to the residue to be 0.1-1, and                fully stirring and mixing to obtain a solid initial                material; ii. treating the obtained solid initial                material with microwave in aerobic state, the                microwave treatment power is 600-900 Watt, and the                microwave treatment time is 120-240 seconds, and                the solid initial material is first converted into                cyanate and ferricyanate under microwave treatment,                cyanate and ferricyanate are further oxidized to                form carbonate, nitrogen, carbon dioxide and iron                oxide.
   USE - Method for cooperatively removing cyanide in                metal heat treatment residue by using sensitizer                and microwave used in the fields of metal                processing, building materials, metallurgy,                chemical engineering and the like.
   ADVANTAGE - Method for synergistic removal of cyanide from                metal heat treatment residues by sensitizer and                microwave not only realizes the harmless removal of                the cyanogen-containing residues in a solid phase                system, but also has the advantages of wide raw                material source, high cyanogen removal efficiency,                simple process, low treatment cost, safety,                environmental protection and the like.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Mineral particles with                  iron oxide content not less than 50% are natural                  iron ore particles is magnetite, hematite,                  maghemite and/or limonite; preferably magnetite                  or hematite; most preferably hematite. Preferred                  Condition: Particle size of the iron oxide-rich                  mineral particles is 100-400 mesh, and the                  optimal particle size is 200-300 mesh. Preferred                  Composition: Weight ratio of the sensitizer to                  the residue is preferably 0.2-0.6, most                  preferably 0.3-0.4. Preferred Process: Fully                  stirring and mixing is carried out by a dry                  mixer, the speed of the mixer is more than 200                  revolutions per minute, and the mixing time is                  more than 10 minutes, the microwave power is                  700-800 Watt, and the microwave processing time                  is 120-180 seconds, the microwave treatment is                  700 Watt for 180 seconds or 800 Watt for 120                  seconds.

PN WO2021226525-A1; US2021348840-A1
TI Method for increasing reliability and availability            of cryogenic fluid reliquefaction system, involves            disengaging motor from variable speed system and            connecting to existing power grid when motor and            compressor is at or near design capacity.
AU GUERIF P
   GUILLARD A
   FLAVIEN G
   FOLLACA V
   KONG P
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE LARGE IND US LP (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGIES US LLC (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE LARGE IND US LP (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGIES US LLC (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2021C5891B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting a                re-liquefaction system to a liquid cryogenic fluid                user which is then supplied a liquid cryogenic                fluid. The liquid cryogenic fluid is vaporized                within the liquid cryogenic fluid user. The                vaporized cryogenic fluid sent back to the main                cryogenic tank (102). The first motor is disengaged                from the variable speed system and connected to an                existing power grid, thus freeing the variable                speed system, the variable speed system is engaged                to a second motor and compressor, the second motor                and compressor is then started when the first motor                and compressor with a variable speed system is at                or near design capacity. The cryogenic fluid is                selected from the group consisting of nitrogen,                helium, argon, oxygen, krypton, xenon, carbon                dioxide, methane, ethane, propane, hydrogen, and                combinations.
   USE - Method for increasing reliability and                availability of cryogenic fluid re-liquefaction                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the reliability and                availability of a cryogenic fluid reliquefaction                system. The method allows the liquid cryogenic                liquid stream to be recondensed to avoid losses of                nitrogen product and cold energy                (refrigeration).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view                illustrating the method for increasing the                reliability and availability of a cryogenic fluid                re-liquefaction system.
   Main cryogenic tank (102)
   Liquid nitrogen stream (103)
   Vaporized nitrogen stream (104)
   Recirculation control valve (109)
   Buffer tank transfer control valve                (113)

GA 202198914T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves identifying a region on a                first surface of a photonic platform (120). The                photonic platform comprising a heating element                (140) and an optical waveguide (130) within a                dielectric. The region is removed to produce a                trench in the first surface of the photonic                platform. The first surface of the photonic                platform is bonded to a second surface of a                substrate to define an airtight cavity between the                trench and the substrate. The airtight cavity                contains a predefined atmosphere at a predefined                pressure. The predefined atmosphere is selected                from a group consisting of air, nitrogen gas,                carbon dioxide gas, carbon monoxide gas, sulfur                hexafluoride gas, neon gas and argon gas.
   USE - Method for fabricating silicon                photonics.
   ADVANTAGE - The substrate of the bonded wafer that is                removed to change the thermal conductivity for the                formed device is improved. The thermal isolation                elements are therefore defined in the material of                the opto-electronic device to interrupt the                transfer of heat from the heating element away from                the associated waveguide, thus, reducing the                secondary effects of the heating element on other                components of the opto-electronic device and                concentrating the primary effects of the heating                element on the intended waveguide.

PN CN113154395-A
TI System useful for removing chemical oxygen demand            in waste water cracking and burning, comprises various            subsystems for removal of COD by preheating industrial            wastewater through heat exchange subsystem, and            discharging flue gas.
AU CAO L
   HUANG X
   ZENG L
AE SICHUAN ZOMAIN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202191464W
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a wastewater conveying                subsystem, a high temperature cracking reactor                subsystem, a flue gas subsystem and a heat exchange                subsystem. The process of COD removal through                various subsystems is transporting COD-containing                industrial wastewater to the heat exchange                subsystem by the wastewater conveying subsystem,                preheating industrial wastewater through heat                exchange subsystem, allowing the preheated                industrial wastewater to enter the high-temperature                pyrolysis reactor subsystem, spraying industrial                wastewater into waste water droplets in the                high-temperature pyrolysis reactor subsystem with                the waste water droplets in a boiling state                instantaneously to start cracking reaction, where                products after pyrolysis reaction of waste water                droplets include carbon dioxide, water and other                compounds, reducing COD of generated water to                required index, discharging flue gas that meets the                standard to atmosphere, and allowing washing                wastewater to enter sewage treatment system.
   USE - The system is useful for removing chemical                oxygen demand in waste water cracking and                burning.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can remove the COD removal rate of                the industrial waste water to more than 99%, which                can realize the industrial waste water chemical                oxygen demand is not more than 50 mg/l, meeting the                discharge standard and waste water treatment                process technical requirement of other steps.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating                the process of COD removal through various                subsystems (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method utilizes                  sodium hydroxide solution.
DC Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))
MC Q73-A05; Q73-T07; Q73-U20

TI Catalytic converter for use with cyclone dust collecting tower, washing tower, and buffer tower connected in main combustion tower of incinerator, has catalytic converter whose catalyst removes high-temperature gas from gas.
AU CHEN F
AE ENERGY ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ENER-Non-standard)
GA 2021D9234J
AB    NOVELTY - A catalytic converter, which is matched with the cyclone dust collecting tower, washing tower, and buffer tower connected in the main combustion tower of the incinerator. In the catalytic converter, there are assembled a large number of catalyst parts, and the catalyst parts themselves It is made of adsorbent material and has a large number of apertures for the flow of high-temperature gas after combustion. A digital combustion controller is installed next to the catalytic converter, so that the flame tube in the combustion controller goes deep into the catalytic converter In the furnace chamber, the flame tube is located under the catalyst. When the combustion controller is activated, the flame tube will heat the furnace chamber to generate high temperature after spraying, so as to provide the required temperature operation of the catalytic converter, and A sensor is used to monitor the temperature of the furnace room; a sensor is installed on the side of the catalytic converter to monitor the air quality level of the catalytic converter after treatment; the combustion material is burned into acid gas at high temperature to interact with The urea water is combined to form liquid fertilizer, while the foamed gas and fine particles are processed by the washing tower, buffer tower and catalytic converter to form carbon dioxide and water vapor before being discharged to achieve the real purification purpose.

PN WO2021133595-A1; KR2022113823-A; CA3162835-A1; CN115003405-A; EP4081330-A1; BR112022012500-A2
TI Preparation of microcapillary carbon molecular            sieve membrane for gas separation, involves extruding            polyvinylidene chloride polymer, pre-treating extruded            polymeric micro capillary film, and pyrolyzing            pre-treated polymeric film.
AU LIU J
   KOPPI K A
   MILLAR D M
   MIRAL D M
   MIRA D M
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 202174177D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves extruding a poly                vinylidene chloride (PVDC) polymer to a thickness                from 10 pm to 1,000 pm to form an extruded                polymeric microcapillary film. The extruded                polymeric microcapillary film is pre-treated at a                temperature from 100 degrees C to 200 degrees C for                a time from 1 hour to 48 hours to form a                pre-treated polymeric microcapillary film. The                pre-treated polymeric microcapillary film is                pyrolized at a temperature from 200 degrees C to                1,500 degrees C for a time from 15 minutes to 5                hours to form the microcapillary carbon molecular                sieve membrane. The microcapillary carbon molecular                sieve membrane comprises micropores having an                average micropore size from 3 A to 5 A, and one or                more microcapillaries each have a diameter from 1                pm to 500 pm. The microcapillary fluid comprises                oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, argon,                helium.
   USE - Method for preparing microcapillary carbon                molecular sieve membrane for gas seperation.
   ADVANTAGE - The microcapillary CMS membranes are able to                be extruded into various micro capillary articles.                The retain microcapillaries during pyrolysis and                form permselective CMS membranes capable of gas                separation without the problems associated with the                fragility of the typical CMSs.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                microcapillary carbon molecular sieve membrane                produced.
   Batch (2)
   Microcapillary carbon molecular sieve                membrane (100)
   First end (110)
   Second end (120)
   Microcapillary (130)

PN KR2257706-B1
TI Preparation of biomass-based porous carbon support-supported metal catalyst for forming tetrahydrofuran, involves pyrolyzing biomass with inert gas to form support having preset average pore diameter, and supporting ruthenium and rhenium.
AU LEE J
   HYUN L Y
AE UNIV AJOU IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYAJ-C)
GA 2021591792
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of biomass-based porous carbon support-supported metal catalyst involves pyrolyzing biomass containing potassium and sodium at 300-600 degrees C in the presence of an inert gas containing nitrogen or carbon dioxide to form a porous carbon support, and supporting ruthenium and rhenium binary metals on the porous carbon support, in which potassium and sodium remain in the porous carbon support. The porous carbon support has average pore diameter of 10-20 nm, and specific surface area of 20-60 m2/g.
   USE - Preparation of biomass-based porous carbon support-supported metal catalyst used for preparing tetrahydrofuran from furan (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and enables preparation of biomass-based porous carbon support-supported metal catalyst having excellent activity, using high value-added compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) the biomass-based porous carbon support-supported metal catalyst, which comprises a porous carbon support containing potassium and sodium formed from pyrolysis of biomass containing potassium and sodium, and ruthenium and rhenium binary metals supported on the porous carbon support, and has average pore diameter of 4-7 nm, and specific surface area of 80-150 m2/g; and
   (2) production of tetrahydrofuran, which involves hydrogenating furan in the presence of a metal catalyst supported on a biomass-based porous carbon support.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rice straw is used as the biomass containing potassium and sodium. The precursor is chosen from perrhenic acid and ruthenium chloride. Preferred Composition: The mixed solution comprises 10-80 wt.% precursor, and 10-90 wt.% porous carbon support. The catalyst comprises 1-7 wt.% ruthenium, and less than 1 wt.% rhenium. Preferred Process: The step (2) involves impregnates the porous carbon support in an aqueous solution containing ruthenium, and rhenium precursor, drying the mixed solution under reduced pressure, and reducing the precursor by heat-treating the dried material under reduced pressure in the presence of hydrogen gas. Preferred Conditions: The step (1) is performed for 1-60 minutes, and increases temperature at a rate of 1-20 degrees C/minute, and maintains temperature at 300-600 degrees C. The hydrogenation reaction is performed for 1-10 hours at a pressure of 1-5 MPa and a temperature of 100-200 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Treating waste salt resource involves: (1) mixing waste salt and coal into a coal-salt mixture; (2) combusting the mixture in a coal gasifier, reacting at 1200-1600 degrees C for 2-10 seconds to produce a high-temperature mixture, which includes crude gas, molten ash and waste salt products including molten salt and gaseous sodium chloride generated from the waste salt; (3) directly quenching the mixture at 1200-1600 degrees C with water, collecting the crude gas, processing, utilizing, rapidly curing the molten ash into a coarse slag in a vitreous state, introducing the waste salt product into an aqueous phase during quenching, flowing out in the form of black water, and obtaining atmospheric pressure gray water after temperature reduction, pressure reduction and filtration; and (4) evaporating and crystallizing the gray water at atmospheric pressure to obtain regenerated sodium chloride salt. The crude gas includes carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, nitrogen and hydrogen sulfide.
   USE - The method is useful for treating waste salt resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes coal gasification process to completely decompose the organic matter at the decomposition temperature of more than 1200 degrees C, and effectively prevents the formation of dioxin and other substances by rapidly cooling. Crude water gas is used for further chemical utilization without exhaust gas emissions, which solves the problem of secondary pollution in the process of industrial waste salt disposal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The coal gasifier is an interconnected integrated pressure vessel device with a quenching chamber, which is filled with water for quenching. A gas washing tower is connected to the quenching chamber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The steps (1)-(3) further involve: (1) dissolving the waste salt in an aqueous phase of the coal water slurry with a pH of 7-9, and mixing to obtain a coal-water slurry-salt mixture, which is a coal-salt mixture; (2) introducing the coal-water slurry-salt mixture into the coal gasifier for combustion, reacting at a high temperature of 1200-1500 degrees C for 3-10 seconds or 1300-1600 degrees C for 2-6 seconds to generate the high-temperature mixture; and (3) directly quenching the high-temperature mixture with water at 1200-1500 degrees C or 1300-1600 degrees C. The method further involves (5) rinsing the obtained regenerated sodium chloride salt by evaporation and crystallization with water, and spin drying to obtain the refined sodium chloride salt, so that the total organic carbon content is reduced to less than 100 mg/kg. The coal-salt mixture is continuously sprayed into the coal gasifier through a nozzle to directly continuously perform partial combustion, cracking, hydration and other reactions to produce water gas. The atmospheric pressure gray water is filtered to remove coal ash, and then evaporated and crystallized to obtain the regenerated sodium chloride salt.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The waste salt is a salt composed of alkali metal ions discarded in industrial production, preferably sodium chloride, sodium sulfate, potassium chloride, potassium sulfate, sodium acetate, sodium sulfide and/or sodium phosphate. The waste salt is solid waste salt or salt-containing wastewater.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PN CN111893238-A; CN111893238-B
TI Production of corrosion-resistant reinforcing steel bar comprises e.g. selecting high-quality waste steel, smelting it into molten steel in electric furnace, blowing out solid waste slag under oxygen-free conditions.
AU CHEN F
   LIN Y
   CHEN C
AE SICHUAN PROVINCE GUANGHAN DESHENG IRON (SICH-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN GUANGHAN DESHENG IRON & STEEL CO (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2020B4600A
AB    NOVELTY - Production of corrosion-resistant reinforcing steel bar comprises (1) selecting high-quality waste steel, smelting it into molten steel in electric furnace, blowing out the solid waste slag under oxygen-free conditions, to obtain pure molten steel; (2) orderly adding the recarburizer, silico-manganese alloy, and high-carbon ferromanganese into the obtained molten steel, melting into liquid, and blowing out the non-melting slag under oxygen-free conditions to obtain mixed molten liquid; (3) alloying the mixed molten liquid, melting into primary molten steel; adding lime powder to the obtained primary molten steel, at the same time, heating to complete melting under oxygen-free conditions, when the composition to be smelted meets the tapping requirements, tapping the steel at 1700-1750 degrees C to form steel ladle.
   USE - The method is useful for production of corrosion-resistant reinforcing steel bar (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can achieves high cleanliness, oxidation and corrosion resistance; can uses electricity to remove oxygen and carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen to achieve high cleanliness; can uses phlogopite flakes, chromium nitride iron, tungsten-iron alloy to achieve oxidation and corrosion resistance; can uses recarburizer, silico-manganese alloy, and high-carbon ferromanganese to achieve oxidation and corrosion resistance; and pass mixed gas and electric field to realize oxidation and corrosion resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (4) hoisting the formed steel ladle into the container, controlling the superheat of the molten steel in the steel ladle to less than or equal to 10 degrees C, carrying out casting and forming, at the same time, electrifying to remove oxygen under oxygen-free conditions to form crude steel bars; and (5) acid washing the obtained crude steel bars and then alkaline washing, where two stages are carried out in an electric field in an oxygen-free condition, to obtain the finished corrosion-resistant steel bar. In the method, alloying comprises adding phlogopite flakes and ferrochromium nitride to the mixed molten liquid in order to melt, adding tungsten-iron alloy for alloying, and blowing out the solid waste slag under oxygen-free conditions. In the method, the electric field in the oxygen-free condition is the crude steel bar, the two ends are respectively connected to 20 V power supply through wires, the current is 10 A, and the conductive liquid is 15% aluminum chloride solution. Preferred Components: The ratio of the weight components of the phlogopite flakes, ferrochromium nitride and tungsten-iron alloy is 5:8:9:4, and their total weight accounts for 6% of the mixed molten liquid. The weight component ratio of the recarburizer, silico-manganese alloy, and high-carbon ferromanganese is 15:6:3:3, and its total weight accounts for 5 wt.% of molten steel. The weight of each element in recarburizer comprises 95.45 wt.% C, 0.075 wt.% S, 1.6 wt.% volatile content, 0.45 wt.% moisture content, and difficult to remove impurities (remaining amount). The oxygen-free condition is that mixed gas of argon and krypton with volume fraction ratio of 6:4.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); M27 (Ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PN CN111676191-A
TI Three-dimensional culturing of bone marrow            mesenchymal stem cells, by preparing collagel gel            culture medium, obtaining separated bone marrow            mesenchymal stem cells, culturing, and placing            cell-collagel suspension in incubator.
AU WANG D
   SHI B
   LIU F
   CAO S
   SUN S
AE SHANDONG MEDICINAL BIOTECHNOLOGY CENT (SDME-C)
GA 202094419C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for three-dimensional culturing of bone                marrow mesenchymal stem cells based on the collagel                gel scaffold method, involves preparing collagel                gel culture medium, where the medium comprises                reagent A: 5X minimum essential medium (MEM),                reagent B: fetal bovine serum (FBS), reagent C: 1 M                4-hydroxyethylpiperazine ethanesulfonic acid                (Hepes), reagent D: a mixture of 0.1 M sodium                hydroxide and 5X MEM, and collagel gel: 2-10 mg/ml                type I collagen (COL1) and 0.01 M hydrochloric acid                mixture, obtaining separated bone marrow                mesenchymal stem cells, preparing single cell                suspension, counting, uniformly mixing the cells                and the collagel gel culture medium to obtain a                cell-collagel suspension, obtaining cell culture                plate, coating the surface of the cell culture                plate with a surface coating material, culturing                overnight, adding cell-collagel suspension to the                coated cell culture plate, and placing in carbon                dioxide incubator for cell culture.
   USE - The method is useful for three-dimensional                culturing of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells                (claimed), and for intervertebral disc tissue                repair and fiber tissue regeneration and other                medical tissue engineering field and research of                biomechanics field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes growth, proliferation,                adhesion, differentiation and other biological                functions of bone marrow mesenchymal stem                cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cultured                bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells, which is                prepared by collagel gel scaffold method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves mixing                  reagent A, reagent B and reagent C, obtaining                  another reagent D and collagel, adjusting the pH                  of the mixture to 7-7.2, and mixing the                  materials. The volume ratio of reagent A, reagent                  B, reagent C, reagent D and Collagel is                  6-10:2-8:0.5-2:1-3:30-40. The method further                  involves adjusting the cell concentration so that                  the average Collagel, where the cell                  concentration in the medium substrate is 2x 105                  cells/ml. The method further involves performing                  solid-liquid separation of the cell-collagel                  suspension, collecting the cells and resuspending                  in the collagel culture medium. The surface                  coating material comprises elastin, laminin and                  ProNectin (RGD). The surface coating material                  comprises 2-10 mu g/ml elastin, 5-30 mu g/ml                  laminar adhesion protein and 5-30 mu g/ml                  ProNectin (RGD). The method involves placing cell                  culture at 35-40 degrees C and 3-8% carbon                  dioxide constant temperature culture. The method                  further involves replacing on the 3rd, 5th and                  7th day of culture, placing the culture medium in                  the cell culture plate and observing the                  morphology of cultured cells.

PN CN111560557-A
TI Controlling titanium and reducing titanium of non-oriented silicon steel comprises smelting route comprising hot metal pre-treatment, converter treatment, Ruhrstahl-Heraeus refining, and continuous casting.
AU KANG W
   LIAO X
   SHANG D
   CHANG G
   KANG L
   LV C
   LI D
   ZHANG W
   ZHANG R
AE ANGANG STEEL CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 202083672E
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling titanium and reducing titanium of non-oriented silicon steel comprises smelting route comprising hot metal pre-treatment, converter treatment, Ruhrstahl-Heraeus (RH) refining, and continuous casting, specifically comprises (i) selecting low-titanium molten iron for silicon steel smelting, where titanium in molten iron is less than or equal to 0.02 wt.%, (ii) using 0.05-0.07 wt.% oxygen at the time of tapping, controlling the slag of the converter so that the titanium dioxide in the top slag of the refining is less than or equal to 0.3 wt.%, (iii) the RH ladle enters the station, adding 2-4 kg refined slag modifier per ton of steel, carrying out RH alloying, switching the lifting gas to carbon dioxide, maintaining vacuum degree at 2-5 kpa, where the treatment time is 10-15 minutes, and (iv) loading the treated molten steel on a casting machine for casting.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling titanium and reducing titanium of non-oriented silicon steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes titanium in the non-oriented silicon steel molten steel, lowers titanium content of low-grade non-oriented silicon steel to not less than 0.001 wt.%, ensures quality of non-oriented silicon steel products, has simple operation and strong practicability, less cost increase, and remarkable titanium reduction effect, and provides basic technical platform for the smelting of low-titanium steel products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The refined slag modifier comprises 50-70 pts. wt. calcium oxide, 10-20 pts. wt. fluorite, 5-10 pts. wt. cerium oxide, and 10-20 pts. wt. alumina.

TI Method for calibrating networks of environmental            sensors, involves calibrating first sensor in network            of sensors collecting data from first sensor and            collecting data from a proxy reference representing            good estimate for true concentration.
AU HENSHAW G S
   WILLIAMS D E
   MILES E
   MISKELL G
   WEISSERT L
AE AEROQUAL LTD (AERO-Non-standard)
   AEROQUAL LTD (AERO-Non-standard)
   AEROQUAL LTD (AERO-Non-standard)
   AEROQUAL LTD (AERO-Non-standard)
GA 202071357K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calibrating a first sensor                in a network of sensors collecting data from the                first sensor and collecting data from a proxy                reference that represents a good estimate for the                true concentration at the site of the first sensor.                A gain and offset of the first sensor is adjusted                so that over a selected period of time first                probability distributions of adjusted first sensor                measurements closely matches second probability                distribution of the proxy reference. The selected                period of time over which the first and second                probability distributions are matched is from 1                hour to 1000 hours. The first sensor measures air                pollutants includes ozone, nitrogen dioxide, sulfur                dioxide, carbon monoxide, benzene, particulate                matter, hydrogen sulfide, ammonia, nitric oxide,                carbon dioxide, methane.
   USE - Method for calibrating networks of an                environmental sensors.
   ADVANTAGE - An air quality regulatory monitors requires                frequent, rigorous calibration using standard                reference materials in order to provide accurate                and traceable environmental data, and provides a                new approach that is economical to use, does not                assume sensor stability, does not rely on training                data and can update in real-time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus comprising a server.

TI Generating and separating gases involves passing            first stage in which a gas phase fuel stream, rising            combustible substances whose oxidation to gaseous            products, and an oxygen-rich inlet stream through two            ceramic separation membrane.
AU SERRA ALFARO J M
   HERRAIZ CARDONA I
   CATALAN MARTINEZ D
   CATALAN M D
   HERRAIZ C I
   SERRA A J M
AE KERIONICS SL (KERI-Non-standard)
   KERIONICS SL (KERI-Non-standard)
   KERIONICS SL (KERI-Non-standard)
GA 202062169M
AB    NOVELTY - Generating and separating gasses involves                passing first stage in which a gas phase fuel                stream, rising combustible substances whose                oxidation to gaseous products, and an oxygen-rich                inlet stream through at least two ceramic                separation membrane modules oxygen so that the two                streams come into contact through the membranes and                a heat exchange takes place between, second stage                of selective oxygen diffusion from the oxygen-rich                stream into the fuel stream, and such that the                membrane module output streams are oxygen-depleted                or completely oxygen-free stream, partially                oxidized or completely oxidized stream, recovering                two separate outlet streams, two gasses selected                from oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen.
   USE - Method for generating and separating                gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to generate and separate                gasses improves selectivity of these                reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is incluided for an                installation for carrying out generate and separate                gases, which comprises two ceramic oxygen                separation membrane modules, gas compressors that                drive the inlet currents to the membrane modules,                heat exchanger devices between incoming or outgoing                gas currents in membrane modules, turbine arranged                to receive at least gas from one of the membrane                modules and produce work, two separate individual                exhaust gas stream lines from the oxygen separation                membrane modules and in which each membrane module                oxygen separation comprises a set of parallel flat                ceramic membranes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen-rich input                  stream is selected from air, water vapor. The                  second stage the oxidation of the fuel is                  complete. The oxygen-rich inlet stream is air and                  the two separate outlet streams are nitrogen and                  carbon dioxide. The oxygen-rich inlet stream is                  water vapor and the two separate outlet streams                  are hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The three                  modules selected from first membrane module in                  which the gasified fuel stream is partially                  oxidized, second membrane module in which                  completely oxidizes the gasified fuel stream, and                  third membrane module in which oxygen extraction                  is carried out. The inlet stream contains                  combustible materials, comprises selected from                  carbonmonoxide, hydrogen, hydrogen sulfide,                  methane, liquefied petroleum gases, alcohols,                  olefins, peroxides, aromatics, organic acids,                  organic amines, naphtha, asphalt, bituminous,                  diesel, vegetable, animal or mineral oils or                  fats, coals, and mixtures. Preferred Conditions:                  The gas exchange between the two inlet streams to                  the membrane modules is carried out at a                  temperature between 600-1500 degrees C. The                  method involves pressurizing the inlet currents                  in a first gas compressor devic at absolute                  pressures between 2-15 bar to obtain pressurized                  inlet currents, and slow preheating of the                  pressurized inlet streams with heat given off by                  the outlet streams to obtain pre-heated                  pressurized inlet streams. The flows of the fuel                  gas stream and the oxygen-rich stream are                  arranged in countercurrent. The oxygen-depleted                  output stream is introduced into a turbine for                  its energy revaluation. The first gas compressor                  device and the turbine are connected to the same                  axis. The second membrane module, cross flows,                  co-current flows or counter current flows are                  used. The carbon monoxide is produced and liquid                  water from the oxidized fuel gas stream leaving                  the second module, the water is condensed through                  a condenser-separator arranged at the output of                  the second module, from which two output streams                  leave an oxidized fuel gas stream comprises                  carbondioxide and a stream of liquid water. The                  recirculation portion of the carbon dioxide or                  nitrogen obtained in the process is carried out,                  so that a dilution of the combustible gas stream                  occurs and the heats of oxidation. The three                  membrane modules are used, all the oxygen-rich                  input stream from the second heat exchanger is                  fed into the second membrane module, the stream                  depleted in outlet oxygen from the second                  membrane module is dosed by a valve system that                  partially circulates this current, the first                  membrane module as a stream supplying oxygen as                  an oxygen-depleted stream containing an amount of                  oxygen less than the stoichiometric amount to                  fully oxidize the gasified fuel stream, and to                  the third membrane module as a stream supplying                  oxygen to the third module and the turbine. The                  second and third diaphragm modules are used, the                  heated pressurized gasified fuel stream from the                  first heat exchanger is introduced into the                  second diaphragm module, and the oxygen rich                  input stream coming from the second heat                  exchanger is introduced into the second membrane                  module. The first and second membrane modules are                  used, and - the oxygen depleted output stream                  from the second membrane module is recirculated                  to the turbine as the turbine inlet current. The                  carbonmonoxide output current is obtained and an                  oxygen-depleted stream leaving the modules where                  partial oxidation occurs, and both outlet streams                  are pressurized after cooled. The first, second                  and third membrane modules in the additional                  modules the gas streams produced in the first                  membrane module and the second module are                  purified membrane removing oxygen from them, and                  the fuel gas stream enters the add-on modules as                  a entrainment stream. The membrane modules have a                  countercurrent flow distribution to ensure the                  complete exchange of oxygen from the streams to                  be purified to the fuel streams. The exhaust gas                  streams are purified by polymeric oxygen                  permeation membranes cooled to room temperature                  and pressurized. The source of the combustible                  gasified stream is biomass is gasified by                  thermochemical reactor, the input currents to the                  thermochemical reactor biomass, entrainment                  stream consisting of an inert gas and,                  optionally, steam, and oxygen-rich input                  stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   (1) tumor cell proliferation inhibitor                medicine, comprising (I) as active ingredient and                excipient;
   (2) anti-tumor medicine, comprising (I) as                active ingredient, and excipient; and
   (3) preparing phenolic compound, comprising                (i) carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical                extraction and ethanol extraction of hemp plant                inflorescence to obtain crude extract, and (ii)                dissolving obtained crude extract, and passing                through normal phase silica gel column                chromatography, macroporous adsorption resin column                chromatography, one-stage medium pressure normal                phase silica gel column chromatography, two-stage                medium pressure normal phase silica gel column                chromatography, three-stage medium pressure normal                phase silica gel column chromatography, high                pressure reverse phase high-performance liquid                chromatography for separation to obtain final                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The medicine includes                  (I) with dose of 650 ng/time. The medicine is in                  the form of oral agent or injection agent, where                  oral agent includes pill, tablet, capsule,                  granule or oral liquid; and injection agent                  includes injection or powder injection. Preferred                  Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction conditions are: pressure of extraction                  kettle is 20-30 MPa, temperature of extraction                  kettle is 35-60 degrees C; pressure of separation                  kettle I is 8-11 MPa, temperature of separation                  kettle I is 35-65 degrees C; pressure of                  separation kettle II is 3-6 MPa, and temperature                  of separation kettle II is 30-40 degrees C; the                  amount of ethanol used for ethanol extraction is                  15-25% the mass of hemp plant inflorescence, and                  extraction time is 30-60 minutes; the crude                  extract is completely dissolved in petroleum                  ether; the normal phase silica gel column                  chromatography utilizes n-hexane/ethyl acetate in                  a ratio of 98:2 as eluent for isocratic elution;                  the macroporous adsorption resin column                  chromatography adopts D101 macroporous adsorption                  resin column, and gradient elution is performed                  with 30-100% ethanol/water as eluent; the                  one-stage medium-pressure normal-phase silica gel                  column chromatography utilizes                  dichloromethane/ethyl acetate as eluent for                  gradient elution; the two-stage medium-pressure                  normal-phase silica gel column chromatography                  utilizes n-hexane/diethyl ether as eluent for                  gradient elution; the three-stage medium-pressure                  normal-phase silica gel column chromatography                  utilizes n-hexane/diethyl ether as eluent for                  gradient elution; and the high-pressure                  reversed-phase high-performance liquid                  chromatography chromatography utilizes                  acetonitrile/water as eluent for gradient                  elution.

AU ZHENG D

   WU B
   LU Z
   ZHENG X
AE LIUZHOU WINNER NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (LIUZ-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG WINNER MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   LIUZHOU WINNER NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 202048705V
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene oxide/polypropylene foamed bead comprises 86-92 wt.% copolymerized polypropylene, 0.5-2 wt.% graphene oxide masterbatch, 1-5 wt.% black mother, 0.5-5 wt.% nucleating agent and 0.5-2 wt.% foaming auxiliary, where the graphene oxide masterbatch comprises 1-5 wt.% graphene oxide, 5-15 wt.% maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene and 80-94 wt.% copolymerized polypropylene.
   USE - As graphene oxide/polypropylene foamed bead.
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene oxide/polypropylene foamed bead has excellent antistatic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the graphene oxide/polypropylene foam beads comprising (a) melting graphene oxide, maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene and antioxidant to form a mixed melt, and mixing the mixed melt with a copolymer polypropylene melt to prepare graphene oxide masterbatch, (b) mixing copolymer polypropylene, graphene oxide masterbatch, black masterbatch, nucleating agent and foaming aid, and extruding to produce polypropylene particles, (c) adding water, dispersant, dispersing aid and the polypropylene particles to the autoclave in sequence, passing high-pressure carbon dioxide fluid and heating the autoclave body, and when the temperature reaches the predetermined temperature and the pressure reaches the predetermined pressure, releasing the pressure from the autoclave, crushing to prepare foamed beads, dehydrating and drying, and (d) taking the expanded beads into the autoclave, passing high-pressure air load, and passing the loaded expanded beads into a secondary foaming machine for secondary foaming to obtain secondary foamed beads.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene comprises 0.5-5 wt.% maleic anhydride. The melt index of the polypropylene is 10-50 g/10 minutes. The ratio of carbon to oxygen atoms in the graphene oxide is 5-10. The carbon to oxygen atom concentration ratio in the graphene oxide is 6-8. Preferred Method: The method further involves injecting the secondary foamed beads into a steam forming machine, and heating and cooling the steam to prepare a foamed beads shaped element. Preferred Condition: The melting temperature in step (a) is 160-180 degrees C, and the melting time is 1-10 minutes. The expansion ratio of the expanded beads in step (c) is 15-30 times. Preferred Property: The pressure release rate of the autoclave in step (c) is 0.3-2 MPa/second.

PN CN111019704-A
TI Hydrogenation system for production of refined oil, has cooler that cools oil and gas after reaction, and fractionating tower that is utilized for desulfurization of oil and gas to get national hexagasoline.
AU JIANG W
AE NINGXIA TAIFU ENERGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202034499R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a buffer tank used to store and buffer the fractionated gasoline. A hydrogenation feed pump transports hydrogen. A circulating hydrogen compressor increases the pressure of hydrogen and to recycle hydrogen. A seat exchanger is used for heat exchange treatment. The reaction oil is used for reacting with hydrogen in hydrogenation reactor. A heat conduction unit guides the heat generated during the operation of the hydrogenation reactor into the fractionation tower. A cooler cools the oil and gas after the reaction. A high sub-tank stores reactive oil and gas. A fractionating tower is used for desulfurization of oil and gas to get national hexagasoline. A mixing silo mixes the national sixth gasoline with methyl tert-butyl ether, xylene and isooctane in proportion. The carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide contained in the hydrogen are removed in a pressure swing adsorption mode.
   USE - Hydrogenation system for production of refined oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogenation system recycles hydrogen repeatedly, reduces the hydrogen manufacturing cost in the production process and improves the hydrogen utilization rate. The heat generated by reaction in the hydrogenation reactor is brought into the fractionating tower by heat conducting liquid in the circulating copper pipe, so that the utilization rate of heat energy is reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the hydrogenation system for production of refined oil. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN110791088-A
TI Polyamide/thermoplastic polyurethane supercritical composite material comprises polyamide, silicone-modified polyurethane, maleic anhydride grafted high-density polyethylene, glass fiber, crosslinking agent and nucleating agent.
AU DING Y
   DING X
   LIN Q
AE FUJIAN ANDAFU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202015412V
AB    NOVELTY - A polyamide/thermoplastic polyurethane supercritical composite material comprises 200-240 pts. wt. polyamide, 160-180 pts. wt. silicone-modified polyurethane, 70-77 pts. wt. maleic anhydride graft modifier, 45-52 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted high-density polyethylene, 22-26 pts. wt. glass fiber, 15-18 pts. wt. crosslinking agent and 5-7 pts. wt. nucleating agent. The polyamide is mixture of polysebacyl adipamide and adipic adipamide-terephthaloyl adipamide copolyamide in a mass ratio of 1:(0.5-0.7). The silicone-modified polyurethane is silicone-modified polyurethane elastomer synthesized by prepolymer method using 2,4-toluene diisocyanate, tetrahydrofuran propylene oxide copolyether polyol, 3,3'-dichloro-4,4'-diaminodiphenylmethane and silicone diol. The maleic anhydride graft modifier is obtained by reacting epoxy group-containing polysiloxane and maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene in a mass ratio of 1:(2-3), and then grafting with silicon group-containing copolymer.
   USE - Polyamide/thermoplastic polyurethane supercritical composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The lightweight polyamide/thermoplastic polyurethane supercritical composite material has excellent foaming effect, external appearance, mechanical property, tensile strength, tear strength and durability, medium cell size and low density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the composite material, which involves mixing polyamide, silicone-modified polyurethane, maleic anhydride graft modifier, maleic anhydride grafted high-density polyethylene and glass fiber, adding crosslinking agent and nucleating agent, mixing at 130-135 degrees C, pressing into sheet, pre-heating, placing in mold, and infusing carbon dioxide gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The nucleating agent is nano zinc oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The crosslinking agent is mixture of dicumyl peroxide and benzoyl peroxide in a mass ratio of 1:(0.55-0.65).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polyamide/thermoplastic polyurethane supercritical composite material comprises 220 pts. wt. polyamide, 170 pts. wt. silicone-modified polyurethane, 74 pts. wt. maleic anhydride graft modifier, 48 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted high-density polyethylene, 24 pts. wt. glass fiber, 17 pts. wt. crosslinking agent and 6 pts. wt. nucleating agent. The mass ratio of polysebacyl adipamide and adipic adipamide-terephthaloyl adipamide copolyamide is 1:0.62. The mass ratio of epoxy group-containing polysiloxane and maleic anhydride grafted polyethylene is 1:2.4.

PN CN110540805-A
TI Adhesive comprises sodium metasilicate,            nanocellulose, polyacrylamide, cross-linking agent,            initiator, thickener, filler, deionized water, flame            retardant and emulsion.
AU ZHONG Y
AE JINJIANG QIANSHAN NEW MATERIALS SCI (JINJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5037M
AB    NOVELTY - Adhesive comprises 50-60 pts. wt. sodium                metasilicate, 5-8 pts. wt. nanocellulose, 15-20                pts. wt. polyacrylamide, 20-30 pts. wt.                cross-linking agent, 4-6 pts. wt. initiator, 8-10                pts. wt. thickener, 10-15 pts. wt. filler, 70-80                pts. wt. deionized water, 10-12 pts. wt. flame                retardant and 3-5 pts. wt. emulsion.
   USE - As adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The adhesive improves viscosity, is free of                formaldehyde, environmentally-friendly and improves                the fire and water resistance performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the adhesive involving (i) adding                sodium metasilicate and deionized water to a                reaction kettle and mixing, adding polyacrylamide,                maintaining the temperature at 60-65 degrees C and                stirring for 30 minutes to obtain a modified sodium                metasilicate, (ii) adding the cross-linking agent,                nanocellulose and initiator to the modified sodium                metasilicate, raising the temperature at 70-80                degrees C by heating in a water bath, and stirring                600 rpm for 20-40 minutes, dripping 25-30                drops/minute thickener until viscous liquid is                obtained, (iii) adding emulsion, filler and flame                retardants to the viscous liquid, rotating for                30-50 minutes at 1500 rpm, microwave heating at                110-125 degrees C for 50-110 minutes to obtain the                mixture, and (iv) placing the mixture in the                ultrasonic equipment and conducting the over                frequency oscillation at 20-25 KHz for 15-20                minutes, and introducing carbon dioxide to the                ultrasonic equipment, and leaving still for 1-2                hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is                  sodium persulfate. The filler comprises silicone                  powder, asbestos powder, and/or mica powder. The                  flame retardant comprises phosphorus flame                  retardant, inorganic flame retardant, intumescent                  flame retardant, magnesium hydroxide, aluminum                  hydroxide and/or zinc borate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cross-linking                  agent comprises acrylic acid, methacrylic acid                  hydroxyalkyl group, and/or methacrylic acid                  ester. The thickener comprises erythritol,                  pentaerythritol, sorbitol, and/or hydrogenated                  maltose.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The nanocellulose is                  prepared by hydrolyzing bagasse pulp or straw                  fiber using sodium hydroxide and hydrochloric                  acid solution and stirring using ultrasonic                  vibration for 30 minutes, washing, and performing                  centrifugation for 4 hours to obtain the product.                  Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the                  sodium hydroxide and hydrochloric acid is 40-50%,                  and the temperature of the hydrolysis is 55-65                  degrees C. Preferred Components: The emulsion                  comprises polydimethylsiloxane or                  cyclomethylsiloxane and alkylphenol                  polyoxyethylene ether (7) (OP-07                  emulsifier).

PT P
PN CN110217769-A; CN110217769-B
TI Producing calcium ammonium nitrate byproduct            feed-grade dicalcium phosphate, by mixing phosphate            rock, reacting, adding limestone for defluorination,            adding lime milk, washing solids, adding ammonia to            liquid, concentrating and drying.
AU ZHANG Z
   XU D
   YANG X
   WANG X
   YANG L
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201979566T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing calcium ammonium nitrate                byproduct feed-grade dicalcium phosphate, involves                (i) mixing phosphate rock with 20-70 wt.% nitric                acid solution, reacting at 30-70 degrees C for                0.3-2 hours to obtain a solid-liquid mixture, where                the phosphate rock is in the form of powder or                slurry, (ii) adding limestone to the mixture for                primary defluorination, controlling the pH to 1.5-2                and carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain                a acid hydrolysis liquid (a), (iii) adding                limestone to the liquid (a) for secondary                defluorination, controlling the pH to 2-3.2 and                carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain a                acid hydrolysis liquid (b), (iv) adding lime milk                to liquid (b), controlling the pH to 4-6, carrying                out solid-liquid separation to obtain a liquid and                solids, washing the solids and drying to obtain a                feed-grade calcium hydrophosphate, and (v) adding                ammonia to the liquid, controlling the pH to 6-7,                carrying out solid-liquid separation, concentrating                and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for producing calcium                ammonium nitrate byproduct feed-grade dicalcium                phosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the consumption of large                amount of limestone resources, carbon dioxide                emissions, energy consumption, operation difficulty                and production cost, is simple to operate, does not                produce solid waste such as gypsum, and has                excellent performance and economic and social                benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The use amount of                  nitric acid in step (i) is 0.9-1.2 times,                  preferably 0.95-1.05 times based on the                  theoretical acid consumption of the complete                  reaction of the phosphate rock. Preferred Method:                  The method further involves filtering the                  solid-liquid mixture before step (ii) after step                  (i) and substituting the obtained filtrate for                  the solid-liquid mixture in the step (ii) to                  carry out primary defluorination. In step (ii),                  the solid obtained by solid-liquid separation is                  prepared to obtain a fertilizer grade calcium                  hydrogen phosphate. In step (iii), the solid                  obtained by solid-liquid separation is returned                  to step (ii), and carrying out primary                  defluorination instead of limestone. In step                  (iii), the acid hydrolysis liquid (a) has greater                  than or equal to 230 phosphorus                  pentoxide/fluorine. In step (v), the solid                  obtained by solid-liquid separation is returned                  to step (iii). Preferred Components: The ammonia                  in step (v) is ammonia gas, ammonium carbonate or                  ammonium bicarbonate.

PN US2019270068-A1; US10549261-B2
TI Selectively separate oxygen from air, comprises contacting gas stream containing oxygen at a first concentration with a metal-organic framework material at a first temperature and first pressure and desorbing oxygen from the material.
AU NENOFF T M
   SAVA G D F
   PARKES M V
   GREATHOUSE J
AE NAT TECHNOLOGY & ENG SOLUTIONS SANDIA (HONE-C)
GA 201975752X
AB    NOVELTY - Selectively separate oxygen from air, comprises: contacting a gas stream containing oxygen at a first concentration with a metal-organic framework material (I), at a first temperature and first pressure to adsorb oxygen from the gas stream into the metal-organic framework and desorbing oxygen from the metal-organic framework material at a second temperature and a second pressure.
   USE - The process is useful for separating oxygen from air.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: reduces the expense and increase purity in oxygen separation technology; selectively separates oxygen from air under mild conditions of low pressure and temperature; improves capacity and competitive (or better) selectivity than zeolites, that reduce energy intensity, greenhouse gas footprint, and costs (anticipated reduction of 50% of 2.5 million MWh of energy) over existing purified oxygen streams; and reduce the carbon dioxide and energy footprint of stationary energy production facilities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Selectively separate oxygen from air, comprises: contacting a gas stream containing oxygen at a first concentration with a metal-organic framework material having the formula m-(1,3,5-BTC) (I), at a first temperature and first pressure to adsorb oxygen from the gas stream into the metal-organic framework and desorbing oxygen from the metal-organic framework material at a second temperature and a second pressure.
   BTC = benzene-1,3,5-C6H3(CO2H)3; and
   m = Sc, Ti or V.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The oxygen is desorbed by decreasing pressure from the first temperature to the second pressure. The oxygen is desorbed by increasing temperature from the first temperature to the second temperature. Preferred Components: The first temperature is ambient temperature. The second pressure is less than the first pressure. The second temperature is greater than the first temperature. The first and second temperatures are equal. The metal-organic framework material is of formula (Eu(TTB)(DEF)(H2O).(DEF)5), where TTB is 3,3 0,4,4 0-tetra (trimethylsilylethynyl)biphenyl, and DEF is N,N'-diethylformamide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
