TI Preparing high-efficiency long-life silicon-based anode material for use in lithium ion battery, involves calcining the silicon oxynitride under protection of inert gas to obtain calcined material, and mixing calcined material and sodium.
AU WANG H
   WANG Q
   LIN S
   ZHOU Y
   XU J
   CHEN L
AE HEFEI GUOXUAN HIGH TECH POWER SOURCE CO (HEFG-C)
GA 2019739042
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-efficiency long-life silicon-based anode material involves calcining the silicon oxynitride under the protection of an inert gas to obtain a calcined material, and uniformly mixing calcined material with sodium metal, and then carrying out the reduction reaction in carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain reduced material. The reduced material is pickled and removed, washed to neutral, and dried to obtain a dry material. The dry material is placed in an inert gas, and a cracking gas is introduced for chemical vapor deposition to obtain a carbon coating material. The carbon coating material is dispersed in the deionized water-alcohol system, and a surfactant, a high conductivity nano metal and a polymer monomer are added and mixed uniformly, and then an initiator is added to carry out self-polymerization, and dried to obtain conductive polymer coating material.
   USE - Method for preparing high-efficiency long-life silicon-based anode material for use in a lithium ion battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-efficiency long-life silicon-based anode material that has an excellent application prospect and improves coulombic efficiency and the cycle stability of the battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The conductive polymer coating material is broken into powdery particles, and the high-effect long-life silicon-based anode material is obtained by mixing with graphite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: An acid is used for the pickling. The acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or nitric acid. The high conductivity nano metal is silver, gold or copper. The initiator is hydrogen peroxide, ferric chloride or ammonium persulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cracking gas is methane or its homolog, acetylene or its homolog, or benzene or its homolog. The deionized water-alcohol system is deionized water-methanol, deionized water-ethanol, deionized water-propanol, deionized water-isopropyl alcohol, or deionized water-methanol. The surfactant is polyvinylpyrrolidone, sodium lauryl sulfate or sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate. The polymer monomer is pyrrole, dopamine, acrylonitrile or aniline. The graphite is natural graphite, artificial graphite, mesocarbon microbeads, hard carbon, or soft carbon.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

TI Preparing an ionic liquid microcapsule for removing a low concentration gas, involves preparing an ionic liquid and a polyol into a solution, and then performing high-speed stirring with nano silica.
AU ZHANG F
   ZHANG Z
   MENG W
   WANG X
   HU J
   CHEN M
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 201957356D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing an ionic liquid microcapsule involves preparing an ionic liquid and a polyol into a solution, and then performing high-speed stirring with nano silica in a high-speed mixer to obtain the ionic liquid microcapsule, and in the solution, the mass concentration of the ionic liquid is 5-90%.
   USE - Method for preparing an ionic liquid microcapsule is used for removing a low concentration gas(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare an ionic liquid microcapsule, that have small volume, large specific surface area and fast absorption speed, and can quickly and efficiently remove low concentration gas in the raw material gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process for removing low-concentration gas by using the ionic liquid microcapsule, which involves introducing a feed gas containing a low concentration gas from the gas inlet of the venturi mixer into the venturi mixer, the ionic liquid microcapsules entering through the liquid inlet of the venturi mixer, and forming a gas-solid mixture with the ionic liquid microcapsules, where the low concentration gas is absorbing by the ionic liquid microcapsules, where the low concentration gas is one or more of a low concentration of carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, hydrogen sulfide or volatile organic compound (VOC), and the volume ratio of the ionic liquid microcapsule to the raw material gas is 0.001-0.5:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is an imidazole or quaternary ammonium salt ionic liquid, preferably tetramethylamine-glycine ionic liquid, 1-ethyl 3-methylimidazolium-glycine ionic liquid, 1-ethyl 3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate ionic liquid or tetraethylamine-alanine ionic liquid. The polyol is one or both of ethylene glycol or glycerol. The feed gas also includes one or more of methane, hydrogen, oxygen, or an inert gas. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the solution to the silica is 4-9:1. The molar concentration of the low concentration gas in the raw material gas is 0.0001-3%. The raw material gas has a pressure of 0.001-5 megapascal and a temperature of 5-40 degrees C.

TI Automatically stackable and tillable cradle for performing incubation process of eggs in hatcheries, has inner structure provided with number of positions for receiving eggs, such that cradle comprises stackable outer edge unit.
AU VAN DE LOO P
   VAN DE LOO P K M
   LOO P V D
AE COLUMBUS BV (COLU-Non-standard)
   COLUMBUS BV (COLU-Non-standard)
   COLUMBUS BV (COLU-Non-standard)
   COLUMBUS BV (COLU-Non-standard)
GA 2019458400
AB    NOVELTY - The cradle (1) has an inner structure (3) provided with a number of positions for receiving eggs, such that the cradle comprises a tiltable inner structure and a stackable outer edge unit. The inner structure is tiltable in relation to two sidewalls (2) of the outer edge unit, where the outer edge unit of the cradle remains in fixed position and is not tilted, such that the outer edge units are stably stacked. The cradle is positioned horizontally with the outer edge unit, where the cradle is provided for setting eggs and hatching chicks, and a tilting point (4) of the cradles inner structure is located in a middle part of the sidewalls.
   USE - Cradle e.g. automatically stackable and tillable cradle (all claimed), for performing incubation process of eggs in hatcheries.
   ADVANTAGE - The cradle ensures that a large part of waste production can be prevented as the chicks can be taken out of a cradle-room at an early stage, such that extra stress caused by high carbon-dioxide content is prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a process for incubating eggs in a cradleroom
   (2) a method for incubating eggs in a cradleroom and hatching chicks.

TI Greenhouse system for cultivation of crops in facility horticulture industry, has auxiliary greenhouse portion that encloses portion of greenhouse space, and auxiliary greenhouse movable portion which moves auxiliary greenhouse portion.
AU HOON I Y
   WON Y S
   CHANG C K
   LIM Y
   SI-WON Y
   CHANG K C
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 201944949M
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a greenhouse frame. A main light transmitting surface assembly (2) is mounted on the greenhouse frame. An auxiliary greenhouse portion (3) is formed to enclose a portion of the greenhouse space (1a) provided inside the greenhouse frame. An auxiliary greenhouse movable portion (4) is connected to auxiliary greenhouse portion to move the auxiliary greenhouse portion. A utility supply unit is connected to the auxiliary greenhouse portion, for supplying a substance necessary for cultivation of the plant.
   USE - Greenhouse system for cultivation of crops e.g. strawberry in facility horticulture industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The auxiliary greenhouse portion for locally sealing the plant cultivation area inside the greenhouse is provided, so that the auxiliary greenhouse operates the east portion and forms double greenhouse if necessary. The system provides warmth, cold air, humidity and carbon-dioxide quickly and efficiently. Waste is reduced due to unnecessary supply to the entire greenhouse, thus the farming cost and the production cost are reduced. The dew condensation phenomenon due to the difference between secondary temperature and external temperature is prevented. The pest insects such as the strawberry gray mold disease which occurs easily when the strawberry grows are prevented. Thus the productivity and the quality of the product is improved.

   Main light transmitting surface assembly (2)

PN CN109566941-A
TI Water-soluble ginger drink comprises white granulated sugar, concentrated ginger juice, monohydrate citric acid, sodium citrate, potassium sorbate, and rest is water and concentrated ginger juice is water extracting solution.
AU ZHANG J
   HUANG J
   WEN S
   NI J
   LU Z
AE GUANGZHOU GUANGSHI FOOD CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU HUATANG FOOD CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201934014X
AB    NOVELTY - Water-soluble ginger drink comprises 8-11 wt.% white granulated sugar, 1-3.2 wt.% concentrated ginger juice, 0.04-0.1 wt.% monohydrate citric acid, 0.04-0.1 wt.% sodium citrate, 0.01-0.05 wt.% potassium sorbate, and rest is water. The concentrated ginger juice is water extracting solution which is obtained by ultrasonic extraction and biological enzymolysis.
   USE - Water-soluble ginger drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The water-soluble ginger drink is clear liquid, has good stability, healthcare and refreshing effect, no pungent smell, rich nutrition and high accepted degree.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing water soluble ginger drink, which involves:
   (A) dissolving white granulated sugar into water, stirring and dissolving at 80-85oC, adding accessories in solution;
   (B) adding concentrated ginger juice, citric acid monohydrate, potassium sorbate and citric acid, stirring to dissolve, then raising temperature to 85-95 degrees C, heat preservation for 30 minutes;
   (C) filtering slurry, reducing temperature to normal temperature;
   (D) adding rest of sterile water, where obtained material liquid constant volume till soluble solid content is 8.0-12.0% and pH value is 3.5-4.5;
   (E) pouring material liquid mixed with carbon dioxide gas obtained; and
   (F) sending into filling machine in constant pressure perfusion, finally sealing, code spraying and packing to obtain water-soluble ginger drink.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The concentrated ginger juice is prepared by crushing dry ginger slice into 20-40 mesh ginger powder, mixing gingerpowder mixed with water according to solid-liquid in ratio 1:6, ultrasonic extracting for 20 minutes, adding neutral proteinase and cellulose, stirring uniformly, enzymolysis for 3 hours, raising temperature to 75-80 degrees C for 45-55 minutes, adding -amylase, performing enzymolysis for 3-4 hours;#raising temperature to 95-100 degrees C, keeping temperature for 20 minutes and inactivating enzyme, filtering, concentrating filtrate to obtain concentrated ginger juice. Peferred Conditions: The power of ultrasonic extraction is 800-1000 watts. The activity of neutral protease is 50000u/g. The amount of ginger powder is 2.2-2.6 wt.%. The activity of cellulase is 11000u/g. The ratio of liquid to carbon dioxide gas is 1:(1.5-2.5).
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D03-D03B; D03-H01T2B; D05-A02; D05-A04D

TI New non-naturally occurring Wood-Ljungdahl            bacterium comprising disrupted genes and having            improved carbon flux, useful for producing a product,            e.g. acetone, isopropanol, butanol, chorismate,            para-hydroxybenzoic acid, and salicylate.
AU DANIELL J
   DANIEL J
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZA TECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 201930529E
AB    NOVELTY - A non-naturally occurring Wood-Ljungdahl                bacterium comprising a heterologous thiolase and a                disruptive mutation in genes encoding NAD-dependent                electron-bifurcating (FeFe)-hydrogenase, glutamate                synthase, citramalate synthase, acetolactate                decarboxylase, lactate dehydrogenase, acetate                kinase, phosphate transacetylase, and aldehyde                dehydrogenase, where the non-naturally occurring                bacterium has improved carbon flux through                acetoacetyl-CoA compared to a parental bacterium,                is new.
   USE - The non-naturally occurring Wood-Ljungdahl                bacterium is useful for producing a product. The                product is selected from acetone, isopropanol,                3-hydroxyisovaleryl-CoA, 3-hydroxyisovalerate,                isobutylene, isopentenyl pyrophosphate,                dimethylallyl pyrophosphate, isoprene, farnesene,                3-hydroxybutyryl-CoA, crotonyl-CoA,                3-hydroxybutyrate, 3-hydroxybutyrylaldehyde,                1,3-butanediol, 2-hydroxyisobutyryl-CoA,                2-hydroxyisobutyrate, butyryl-CoA, butyrate,                butanol, caproate, hexanol, octanoate, octanol,                1,3-hexanediol, 2-buten-l-ol, isovaleryl-CoA,                isovalerate, or isoamyl alcohol. The product is                selected from chorismate, para-hydroxybenzoic acid,                salicylate, 2-aminobenzoate, dihydroxybenzoate,                4-hydroxycyclohexane carboxylic acid, and their                salts and ions (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The genetically engineered microorganisms have                improved characteristics and enable the production                of useful fuels and chemicals with high efficiency,                high yield and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing                a product by culturing the non-naturally occurring                bacterium defined above in the presence of a                gaseous substrate comprising carbon monoxide (CO),                carbon dioxide (CO2), and hydrogen gas (H2).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Bacterium: Expression of the genes                  is decreased or eliminated compared to the                  parental bacterium. The non-naturally occurring                  bacterium produces a product selected from                  acetone, isopropanol, 3-hydroxyisovaleryl-CoA,                  3-hydroxyisovalerate, isobutylene, isopentenyl                  pyrophosphate, dimethylallyl pyrophosphate,                  isoprene, farnesene, 3-hydroxybutyryl-CoA,                  crotonyl-CoA, 3-hydroxybutyrate,                  3-hydroxybutyrylaldehyde, 1,3-butanediol,                  2-hydroxyisobutyryl-CoA, 2-hydroxyisobutyrate,                  butyryl-CoA, butyrate, butanol, caproate,                  hexanol, octanoate, octanol, 1,3-hexanediol,                  2-buten-1-ol, isovaleryl-CoA, isovalerate, or                  isoamyl alcohol. The parental bacterium is                  selected from Acetobacterium woodii,                  Alkalibaculum bacchii, Blautia producta,                  Butyribacterium methylotrophicum, Clostridium                  aceticum, Clostridium autoethanogenum,                  Clostridium carboxidivorans, Clostridium                  coskatii, Clostridium drakei, Clostridium                  formicoaceticum, Clostridium ljungdahlii,                  Clostridium magnum, Clostridium ragsdalei,                  Clostridium scatologenes, Eubacterium limosum,                  Moorella thermautotrophica, Moorella                  thermoacetica, Oxobacter pfennigii, Sporomusa                  ovata, Sporomusa silvacetica, Sporomusa                  sphaeroides, and Thermoanaerobacter kiuvi. The                  parental bacterium is C. autoethanogenum and: (a)                  the NAD-dependent electron-bifurcating                  (FeFe)-hydrogenase is selected from CAETHG1576,                  CAETHG1578, CAETHG3569, CAETHG3570, and                  CAETHG3571, (b) the glutamate synthase is                  selected from CAETHG0477, CAETHG1580, CAETHG3850,                  and CAETHG3851, (c) the citramalate synthase is                  CAETHG2751, (d) the acetolactate decarboxylase is                  CAETHG2932, (e) the lactate dehydrogenase is                  CAETHG1147, (f) the acetate kinase is CAETHG3359,                  (g) the phosphate transacetylase is CAETHG3358,                  or (h) the aldehyde dehydrogenase is selected                  from CAETHG1819, CAETHG3287, and CAETHG1830. A                  non-naturally occurring Wood-Ljungdahl bacterium                  comprises a disruptive mutation in genes, where                  the non-naturally occurring bacterium has                  improved carbon flux through chorismate compared                  to a parental bacterium. The genes encode                  purine-nucleoside phosphorylase, lactate                  permease, cystathionine gamma-lyase, adenine                  phosphoribosyltransferase,                  5'-nucleotidase/3'-nucleotidase/exopoly                  phosphatase, small conductance mechanosensitive                  channel, arginine deiminase, LL-diaminopimelate                  aminotransferase apoenzyme, and                  phosphopentomutase. Expression of the genes is                  decreased or eliminated compared to the parental                  bacterium. The non-naturally occurring bacterium                  produces a product selected from chorismate,                  para-hydroxybenzoic acid, salicylate,                  2-aminobenzoate, dihydroxybenzoate,                  4-hydroxycyclohexane carboxylic acid, and their                  salts and ions. The parental bacterium is C.                  autoethanogenum and the genes encode CAETHG0160,                  CAETHG0248, CAETHG0498, CAETHG1270, CAETHG1371,                  CAETHG2107, CAETHG3021, CAETHG3510, and                  CAETHG3924, (b) the parental bacterium is C.                  ljungdahlii and the genes encode CLJUc20750,                  CLJUc21610, CLJUc24380, CLJUc33720, CLJUc34740,                  CLJUc42810, CLJUc09270, CLJUcl4280, and                  CLJUcl8150, or (c) the parental bacterium is C.                  ragsdalei and the genes encode CLRAG19250,                  CLRAG31200, CLRAG25120, CLRAG24560, CLRAG14800,                  CLRAG25620, CLRAG09600, or CLRAG00520.                  Preparation: No preparation method given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109481808-A; CN109481808-B
TI Repetitive breathing apparatus comprises breathing apparatus, gas connecting pipe, intake pipe, gas storage tank, adjusting device, and main controller, where breathing device is placed on face of user for covering mouth.
AU SUN Y
   XIE A
   WANG X
   HAN F
   LV C
AE UNIV BINZHOU MEDICAL (UYBI-Non-standard)
GA 201927291S
AB    NOVELTY - The repetitive breathing apparatus comprises breathing apparatus, gas connecting pipe, intake pipe, gas storage tank (3), adjusting device, and main controller, where breathing device is placed on the face of the user for covering the mouth and nose of the user. The breathing device is connected to the first end of the intake pipe through the gas connecting pipe. The second end of the intake pipe is provided with two vents for a gas storage port and a straight port, the intake pipe is open to the outside air of the repetitive breathing device through the through port, the intake pipe passes through the gas storage port Into the gas storage tank (4). The gas storage tank is configured to store the gas exhaled by the user, the adjusting device is located at the second end of the air intake pipe. The adjusting device receives an adjustment signal of the main controller (5), and adjusts an opening degree of the gas storage port.
   USE - Repetitive breathing apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The repetitive breathing apparatus utilizes own repeated breathing to improve the patient's own carbon dioxide level, effectively improves patient's breathing state during sleep state, eliminates sleep apnea, and improves user comfort.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of repetitive breathing apparatus.
   Air filter (1)
   Pipe inside the gas tank (2)
   Gas storage tank (3)
   Gas storage tank (4)
   Main controller (5)

PN CN109473099-A
TI Office environment intelligent detecting voice control method, involves fixing air conditioning working state through infrared module, fed back data in monitoring control process to controller and adjusting preset value by voice command.
AU HE D
   ZHU J
   HU H
   ZHOU C
AE JIANGXI GUOTAI LIMIN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201927932N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves presetting an indoor environment parameter by a controller. Determination is made to check whether an indoor carbon dioxide solubility parameter exceeds a preset value. A voice prompt prompted a user to open a window for ventilating or control a motor to open the window. Determination is made to check whether an indoor humidity is within the preset value range. A working state of the air conditioner is fixed to a dehumidifying mode. Determination is made to check whether a temperature is within the preset value range. An appropriate mode of the air conditioning working state is obtained and fixed the air conditioning working state through the infrared module, if not. Data in monitoring control process is fed back to the controller in real time. The preset value is adjusted by a voice command. A current state parameter is passed through a Wireless fidelity (WIFI) module by an environment detecting module and a pressure detection module in real time.
   USE - Office environment intelligent detecting voice control method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables controlling the adjustment of the office working environment through the environment detection module and the pressure detection module, thus avoiding waste of energy caused by improper setting of the air conditioner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating an office environment intelligent detecting voice control method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN CN208597419-U
TI Energy-saving rice breeding shed, has shed body whose front side is provided with blower, where back side of shed body is provided with exhaust fan, and storage battery and solar panel fixed with each other.
AU QUE Z
AE UNIV YICHUN (UYYC-C)
GA 201926847S
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an energy-saving rice breeding shed, comprising a shed, ground inside the shed is provided with a seedling bed, surface of the seedling bed is provided with an infrared electro-thermal film. the upper part of the infrared electric heating film is provided with a seedling substrate, the body left and right two side of under the ground are provided with a water tank, the bottom of the water tank is provided with a water pump, the output end of said water pump is connected with a water pipe, upper end of the water pipe is connected with a spraying pipe, the middle part of the spraying pipe is set with a nozzle; outer surface of the shed body of the left side is equipped with a solar panel, the front side of the canopy is provided with a blower, the back side of the roof is provided with an exhaust fan. The utility model is provided with a blower and an exhaust fan, the blower and the exhaust fan through using solar energy into power to work, so that the air in the greenhouse is circulation, reduces the content of carbon dioxide in the greenhouse and guarantee the health growth of rice seedlings, effectively enhances the yield of the rice.

PT P
PN US2019039016-A1; US11202986-B2
TI Plasticization-resistant polyurethane membrane useful for gas separation, comprises soft segment including polyol compound, hard segment including diisocyanate and chain extender, and covalent ester crosslinks.
AU POURNAGHSHBAND I A
   SADEGHI M
   GHALEI B
   BAGHERI R
AE SADEGHI M (SADE-Individual)
   UNIV ISFAHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYIS-Non-standard)
   POURNAGHSHBAND I A (POUR-Individual)
   GHALEI B (GHAL-Individual)
   BAGHERI R (BAGH-Individual)
GA 201913613L
AB    NOVELTY - Plasticization-resistant polyurethane membrane comprises: a soft segment including a polyol compound; a hard segment including a diisocyanate and a chain extender; and covalent ester crosslinks.
   USE - The membrane is useful for gas separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane: exhibits a plasticization resistance to plasticizing gases including carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, or hydrocarbon gases; has a plasticization resistance to plasticizing gases at least more than 25 bar, at 25-50 degrees C; has a thermal stability enhancement up to 100 degrees C at T-50%; and increases in the Young's modulus by at least more than 1300 MPa (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Plasticization-resistant polyurethane membrane comprises: a soft segment including a polyol compound; a hard segment including a diisocyanate and a chain extender; and covalent ester crosslinks. The polyol compound comprises a block copolymer of formula ((polyethylene glycol)a-(polypropylene glycol)b-(polyethylene glycol)a) (I), or ((polypropylene glycol)b-(polyethylene glycol)a-(polypropylene glycol)b) (II).
   a, b = 3-50.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fabricating the plasticization-resistant polyurethane membrane, comprising (i) synthesizing a polyurethane pre-polymer, (ii) synthesizing a polyurethane polymer, where the polyurethane polymer comprises carboxylic acid groups from the synthesized polyurethane pre-polymer, (iii) obtaining an acyl chloride polyurethane polymer by functionalizing the synthesized polyurethane polymer, and (iv) preparing a plasticization-resistant polyurethane membrane by simultaneously crosslinking and casting the acyl chloride polyurethane polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The soft segment comprises a polyether and a polyester, where the polyether and the polyester includes at least one of poly (tetramethylene ether) glycol, polypropylene glycol, polyethylene glycol, polydimethylsiloxane hydroxyl terminated, and polycarprolactone. The plasticization polyurethane membrane comprises 60-80 wt.% of the soft segment. The block copolymer comprises an average molecular weight of at least 1000. The diisocyanate includes at least one of isophorone diisocyanate, toluene diisocyanate, hexamethylene diisocyanate, dicyclohexylmethane 4,4'-diisocyanate, methylene diphenyl diisocyanate, diisocyanate-terminated polyester, and diisocyanate-terminated polyether. The chain extender comprises: a diol and a diamine; or substituted phenyl compounds of formulae (a1) or (a2).X1 = -NH2-; andY1 = -OH-.The diol comprises at least one of 1,4-butanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid and/or pentiptycene. The diamine comprises at least one of 1,6-hexanediamine, 1,8-diaminooctane, 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid, and/or pentiptycene. The thickness of the plasticization-resistant polyurethane membrane is 0.05-500 mu m. Preferred Method: The step (i) of synthesizing the polyurethane pre-polymer comprises preparing a mixture of a diisocyanate and a polyol compound by adding the diisocyanate to the polyol compound; fabricating a micro diisocyanate by adding a catalytic compound to the mixture of the diisocyanate and the polyol compound, where the catalytic compound comprises of at least one zinc-based or amine-based catalyst including tin 2-ethylhexanoate, tin octanoat, dibutyltin 2-ethylhexanoate, dibutyltin diacetate, dioctyltin dimereaptide, N,N-dimethylaIkylamines, N-alkylpyrrolidines, bis (dimethylaminopropyl) amine, 1,8-diazabicyclo-5,4,0-undecene-7, and/or 2-methyl-2-azanorbornane dibutyltin dilaurate; and forming a polyurethane pre-polymer by aging the micro diisocyanate. The step (ii) of synthesizing the polyurethane polymer comprises synthesizing the polyurethane polymer with an average molecular weight of at least 40000 by adding a chain extender to the polyurethane pre-polymer. The step (iii) of obtaining the acyl chloride polyurethane polymer by functionalizing the synthesized polyurethane polymer comprising: preparing a first solution by dissolving the synthesized polyurethane polymer in a solvent, where the solvent comprises at least one tetrahydrofuran, N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethyl acetamide, acetone, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, 1,3-dioxolane, ethanol, n-butanol, isopropyl, toluene, and chloroform; preparing a second solution by adding a thionyl chloride to the first solution; and obtaining an acyl chloride polyurethane polymer by distilling out excess amounts of solvent and thionyl chloride from the second solution. The step (iv) of preparing a plasticization-resistant polyurethane membrane by crosslinking and casting of the polyurethane polymer comprises: preparing a third solution by dissolving the acyl chloride polyurethane polymer in anhydrous N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone; preparing a fourth solution by adding a crosslinking agent to the third solution; and obtaining a crosslinked polyurethane membrane by casting a layer of the fourth solution on a substrate, where the substrate includes a fabric substrate, a clean glass plate, Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene) Petri-dish or a relatively porous membrane support, the obtained crosslinked polyurethane membrane is a plasticization-resistant polyurethane membrane, and the crosslinking agent comprises at least one of 1,4-butanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,6-hexanediamine, 1,8-octanediol, or 1,8-diaminooctane.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A28 (Condensation polymers in general.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC A05-G01E; A10-D; A11-B04; A11-C02; A12-W11A; J01-E03E
IP B01D-053/22; B01D-071/48; B01D-071/52; B01D-071/54; B01D-071/80; C08G-018/12; C08G-018/20; C08G-018/24; C08G-018/32; C08G-018/34; C08G-018/72; C08G-018/83; B01D-069/02; C08G-018/75; C08G-018/18; C08G-018/76; C08G-018/48; C08G-018/73

PN CN109123395-A
TI Potato noodles is rich in nutrients, high in protein content, easy to absorb and good in elasticity comprises starch, protein, fat, vitamins, minerals and salt.
AU ZHAO J
   YUAN J
   YANG L
   YANG X
   ZHANG N
AE DINGXI SHUBAO NONGKE HALAL FOOD CO LTD (DING-Non-standard)
GA 201904847E
AB    NOVELTY - Potato noodles comprises starch, protein, fat, vitamins, minerals and salt.
   USE - Used as potato noodles.
   ADVANTAGE - The noodles is rich in nutrients, high in protein content, easy to absorb, good in elasticity, good in taste, good in color and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Potato noodles comprises starch, protein, fat, vitamins, minerals and salt, where the protein is plant protein, animal protein, the enzyme as noodle quality improver, the amount of protein is 13-20%, the starch is potato starch and wheat starch, the amount of starch is 65-85% and the preparation of noodles comprises (i) washing, peeling, and bud-treating potatoes, spraying with aqueous solution of coloring agent having pH of 3.5-5, breaking into small pieces of single volume of no more than 2 cm3 to obtain first treated potato, (ii) treating the first treated potato with high pressure carbon dioxide process to obtain second treatment of potato, (iii) adding 80-280 g wheat flour per 100 g of second treated potato, 2-8 g salt, 10-50 g nutritional supplements and 0.008-0.35 g enzyme and mixing to obtain premix, (iv) performing first calendering combination on the premix to obtain first dough in which the small particle size of the potato is not greater than 1 mm, (v) aging first dough for 20-40 minutes in an environment at 20-45 degrees C to obtain second dough, and (vii) calendering to form sheet and cutting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The ratio of the plant protein to the animal protein is 1:0.2-1. The enzyme is glucose oxidase, transglutaminase, lipoxygenase or lipase. The color agent is malic acid, citric acid, ascorbic acid or L-cysteine. The nutritional additive is soy protein, peanut protein, almond protein, sesame protein, gluten powder, egg, casein and whey protein. The starch does not comprise gelatinized starch. Preferred Method: The high pressure carbon dioxide treatment condition is; the temperature 30-45 degrees C, the pressure is 30-150 MPa and the holding time is 20-50 minutes. The first calendering combination comprises (a) calendaring premix to form first sheet having thickness of 3-10 mm, (b) synthesizing a dough, rolling pellet again to form first sheet of 3-10 mm thick and repeating for 2-4 times, (c) reciprocally folding into 3-5 layers at 10-30% pressure ratio and calendering to form second sheet, (d) reciprocally folded into 3-5 layers at 10-30% pressure ratio and calendering to form second patch and repeating for 3-7 times, and (e) kneading to synthesize the first dough. The method comprises subjecting second treated potato to dehydration treatment and the moisture content is not less than 40% and subjecting potato noodles to non-heat sterilization treatment and vacuum packing.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN108722421-A; CN108722421-B
TI Catalyst used for preparing heterogeneous diesel, comprises alumina as carrier, and metal elements including specific group metal elements, alkali metal element, alkaline earth-metal element, and has desorption peak at preset temperature.
AU JIN C
   WU Y
   LI M
   XIA G
   YAN Z
   ZHANG R
   HOU C
   SUN X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 201887340T
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises a carrier and metal elements (a1) and (a2) supported on the carrier. The metal element (a1) is group VIII metal element(s). The metal element (a2) is group IVB metal element, and optionally alkali metal element, alkaline earth-metal element. The carrier is alumina. The valence state of portion(s) of the group VIII metal element is lower than the highest oxidation state of the metal element. In carbon dioxide-temperature-programmed desorption of the catalyst, the catalyst has high-temperature desorption peak at 300-500 degrees C.
   USE - Catalyst is used for preparing heterogeneous diesel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst enables preparation of heterogeneous diesel with high selectivity and conversion rate of carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) reduction activation of catalyst precursor, which involves reducing catalyst precursor containing the carrier and the elements (a1) and (a2) in gas e.g. hydrogen or mixture of hydrogen and inert gas, and reducing and activating resultant product in gas e.g. hydrocarbon or mixture of hydrocarbon and inert gas;
   (2) preparation of catalyst, which involves calcining precursor including carrier, compound containing element (a2) and oxide of group VIII metal element, reducing and activating resultant product; and
   (3) preparation of heterogeneous diesel, which involves contacting hydrogen and carbon monoxide with the catalyst under Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The group VIII metal element is iron, cobalt and/or nickel. The group VIII metal element is iron. The catalyst contains 6-20 wt.%, preferably 8-15 wt.% group VIII metal element. The group IVB metal element is zirconium and/or titanium. The alkali metal element is lithium, sodium and/or potassium. The alkaline earth-metal element is magnesium and/or calcium. The catalyst contains 0.5-10 wt.%, preferably 2-6 wt.% element (a2). The inert gas is nitrogen and/or argon. The molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide is 0.4-2.5:1, preferably 0.8-2.2:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of inert gas and hydrocarbon is 1-200:1, preferably 10-30:1. The hydrocarbon is 1-4C alkane and/or 2-4C olefin, preferably methane, ethane, ethylene, propylene, propane, butane and/or butene.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Method for making magnetic filter, involves wrapping magnetic belt around external surface of filter housing, and joining ends of belt to each other to form endless loop which is fixed in place around external surface of filter housing.
AU DOWNIE S
AE ADEY HOLDINGS 2008 LTD (ADEY-Non-standard)
GA 201883881J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves assembling a modular magnetic belt from several modules, where each module having a magnet. The modules of the belt are connected to each other. Each module is hinged to adjacent modules. The number of modules in the belt is selected according to a length of a path around an external surface of the filter housing (12). The magnetic belt is wrapped around an external surface of the carbon dioxide air-and-did separator. The ends of the belt are joined to each other to form an endless loop which is fixed in place around the external surface of the filter housing.
   USE - Method for making magnetic filter of central heating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The magnetic filters of various different sizes are made, without the need to manufacture large numbers of custom-molded portions. The magnetic filter is cleaned without removing the belt from the filter main portion. The unwanted snapping of modules against each other when the belt is detached is prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a magnetic filter for a central heating system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the magnetic filter in the form of air and dirt separator and is fitted with a modular magnetic belt.

   Modular magnetic belt (18)
   Outlet port (44)

PN CN108669493-A
TI Preparing sweet and crisp persimmon involves selecting smooth and lustrous pale yellow and fresh single persimmon, having uniform size, complete appearance and no injury, and that is not rotten, and has no pests and diseases.
AU YU Y
   YANG D
   LIANG Y
AE GUILIN GONGCHENG FENGSHENGYUAN AGRIC PROD DEV CO LTD (GUIL-Non-standard)
GA 2018846335
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sweet and crisp persimmon involves selecting smooth and lustrous pale yellow and fresh single persimmon with a weight of more than 250g, having uniform size, complete appearance and no injury, and that is not rotten, and has no pests and diseases, and no obvious defects. The fresh persimmons that have been screened out are washed with water, dried, placed in cans and processed in baskets, which is followed by putting persimmons into decanting tank, arranging and packing neatly, filling the decanting tank with carbon dioxide, observing the pressure gage and temperature controller data along with making a record, and calculating the production time, where pressure in the untwisting tank reaches 0.1 megapascal, the temperature is controlled at 22-30 degrees C decarburization is completed in 18-20 hours, optimum temperature in the decanting tank is 28 degrees C, and the optimal time is 18 hours. The sealing valve is then covered, and the bolt is tightened.
   USE - Method for preparing sweet and crisp persimmon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare sweet and crisp persimmon, which is environmental friendly, and simple and easy to prepare, maintains color, fragrance, sweetness and crispness of fresh persimmon, has excellent taste, and can be widely used in industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The sweet and crisp persimmons that have been produced are taken out and immediately vacuum packed with a vacuum bag.

PT P
PN CN108448081-A
TI Preparation method of high-nickel ternary lithium battery material of germanium oxide aerogel network, involves mixing germanium chloride, aluminum chloride and solvent, adjusting pH, adding carbon nanotube fiber, mixing, aging, and drying.
AU CHEN Q
   ZENG J
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201868029B
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of high-nickel ternary lithium battery material involves (a) mixing germanium chloride, aluminum chloride and solvent in a weight ratio of (25-7):(15-10):3, and adjusting pH to less than 5 to form sol, adding carbon nanotube fiber, uniformly mixing, aging at 20-40 degrees C for 2-4 days, and carbon dioxide supercritical drying to obtain germanium oxide-carbon nano-tube composite aerogel, where the solvent comprises water, ethanol, and propylene oxide, (b) adding 5-10 pts. wt. nickel salt to 1.8-2.8 pts. wt. germanium oxide-carbon nano-tube composite aerogel, and adding alkali solution to obtain a homogeneous dispersion of nickel precursor, adding 1-5 pts. wt. cobalt salt, 1-5 pts. wt. manganese salt and 8-15 pts. wt. lithium salt, and uniformly mixing to obtain precursor, (c) calcining the precursor under the protection of inert gas at 300-600 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and oxygen-enriched sintering at 900-1000 degrees C for 1-2 hours.
   USE - Preparation method of high-nickel ternary lithium battery material (claimed) of germanium oxide aerogel network.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of high-nickel ternary lithium battery material with excellent cycle stability, and capacity retention rate increased from 68% before coating to 97.8% after 50 cycles of 1C constant current charge and discharge cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (a), the weight ratio of germanium chloride, aluminum chloride and solvent is 10:5:21. In the step (c), the oxygen-rich atmosphere is an atmosphere having oxygen greater than 25 vol.%. Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the aging is performed at 30 degrees C for 3 days. Preferred Components: In step (b), the nickel salt is nickel sulfate, nickel nitrate or nickel acetate. The cobalt salt is cobalt sulfate, cobalt nitrate or cobalt acetate. The manganese salt is manganese sulfate, manganese nitrate or manganese acetate. The lithium salt is lithium sulfate, lithium acetate or lithium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (a), the solvent comprises 20-40 wt.% ethanol, 10-30 wt.% propylene oxide, and balance amount of water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Composition: In step (a), the adding amount of the carbon nanotube fiber is 30-60 wt.% of sol.

PN CN108330098-A
TI Recovering frozen cells involves obtaining            cleaning solution by adding modified Dulbecco's            Modified Eagle's serum-free medium, fetal bovine serum            in first centrifuge tube then mixing upside down,            putting in water bath and heating.
AU LI J
   LIU Z
   XU H
   WANG W
   CHENG F
AE ANHUI GUYI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018608360
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering frozen cells involves obtaining                cleaning solution by adding 10 ml modified                Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's serum-free medium,                10-30 vol.% fetal bovine serum in first centrifuge                tube then mixing upside down, and putting in water                bath and heating. The frozen cells preserved in the                freezing tube are quickly taken out from liquid                nitrogen, then transferred to a water bath in 30-60                seconds, and the freezing tube is shaken                twice/second, and the cells are completely melted                within 1 minute to obtain molten frozen cells. The                molten frozen cells are transferred to second                centrifuge tube, and then all the washing liquid is                absorb gradually absorbed into the cell freezing                liquid, for one drop at a time in the previous                period, shaken slowly and added with 1-2ml cleaning                solution and gradually increasing the filling rate                to obtain cell filtrate.
   USE - Method for recovering frozen cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recover frozen cells,                which significantly improves cell survival.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The cell filtrate is centrifuged after the                addition of the washing solution and transferred to                second centrifuge tube and further centrifuged to                obtain supernatant. The supernatant is transferred                to a T-175 flask, and the temperature is maintained                to 35-40 degrees C, and incubated with 3-7 vol.%                carbon-dioxide to obtain frozen-free cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The Dulbecco's                  Modified Eagle's serum-free medium comprises                  265.00 mg/L calcium chloride dihydrate, 0.10 mg/L                  ferric nitrate nonahydrate, 400 mg/L potassium                  chloride, 92.67 mg/L sodium chloride, 6400.00                  mg/L glycine, 109.00 mg/L L-Arginine                  hydrochloride, 75.00 mg/L L-Cystine                  dihydrochloride, 100.00 mg/L L-Glutamine, 84.00                  mg/L L-Histidine hydrochloride monohydrate, 63.00                  mg/L L-Isoleucine, 30.00 mg/L L-Leucine, 42.00                  mg/L L-Lysine hydrochloride, 105.00 mg/L                  L-Methionine, 105.00 mg/L L-Phenylalanine, 146.00                  mg/L L-Serine, 30.00 mg/L 66.00 mg/L L-Threonine,                  42.00 mg/L L-Tryptophan, 95.00 mg/L L-Tyrosine                  disodium salt, 16.00 mg/L L-Valine, 72.00 mg/L                  choline chloride, 94.00 mg/L                  D-Calcium-Pantothenate, 4.00 mg/L folic acid,                  7.20 mg/L nicotinamide, 4.00 mg/L pyridoxal                  hydrochloride, 0.40 mg/L riboflavin, 0.40 mg/L                  thiamine hydrochloride, 4.00 mg/L i-Inositol,                  4.00 mg/L D-Glucose, and 9.30 mg/L phenol red                  sodium salt. Preferred Conditions: The water bath                  temperature is mantained to 35-40 degrees C,                  particularly 37 degrees C. The capacity of first                  centrifugal tube and second centrifugal tube is                  12-18 ml. The temperature of the centrifuge used                  for first centrifugation is 35-40 degrees C,                  where centrifugal force is 400-600 g, and the                  centrifugation time is 3-7 minutes. The                  supernatant is transferred to a T-175 flask, and                  the temperature is maintained to 37 degrees C,                  and incubated with 5 vol.% carbon-dioxide.                  Preferred Process: The recovery of frozen cells                  involves obtaining cleaning solution by adding 12                  ml, particularly 18 ml modified Dulbecco's                  Modified Eagle's serum-free medium, 10 vol.%                  fetal bovine serum (FBS) in first centrifuge tube                  then mixing upside down at 40 degrees C, and                  putting in water bath and heating. The frozen                  cells preserved in the freezing tube are quickly                  taken out from liquid nitrogen, then transferred                  to a water bath in 35 seconds, particularly 60                  seconds, and the freezing tube is shaken                  twice/second, and the cells are completely melted                  within 1 minute to obtain molten frozen cells.                  The molten frozen cells are transferred to second                  centrifuge tube at a temperature of 35 degrees C,                  particularly 40 degrees C, and then all the                  washing liquid is absorb gradually absorbed into                  the cell freezing liquid, for one drop at a time                  in the previous period, shaken slowly in water                  bath for 30 seconds, for 2 times/second, and the                  cells melt is controlled to 40 seconds and                  further added with 1-2ml cleaning solution and                  gradually increasing the filling rate to obtain                  cell filtrate. The cell filtrate is centrifuged                  after the addition of the washing solution to                  12ml, particularly 18 ml second centrifuge tube,                  so that the cleaning solution is slowly mixed                  into the freezing solution, and adding 1 ml                  cleaning solution, so that the filling rate is                  gradually accelerated. The filtrate is                  transferred to second centrifuge tube and further                  centrifuged to obtain supernatant. The cell                  frozen liquid after adding the washing solution,                  the temperature of the centrifuge is set to 35                  degrees C, particularly 40 degrees C, then                  centrifugal force is maintained to 400 g,                  particularly 600 g, and the centrifugation is                  performed for 3 minutes. The supernatant is                  transferred to a T-175 flask, and the temperature                  is maintained to 35 degrees C, particularly 40                  degrees C, and incubated with 3 vol.%,                  particularly 7 vol.% carbon-dioxide to obtain                  frozen-free cells.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN JP2018115295-A; JP6860358-B2
TI Manufacture of polystyrene-type resin extrusion foaming board used as building materials, involves extruding foamable molten resin composition obtained by kneading polystyrene resin, physical foaming agent and brominated flame retardants.
AU HIYAMA T
   SUGIMOTO K
   NISHIYAMA N
AE JSP CORP (JASY-C)
GA 2018580626
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polystyrene-type resin extrusion foaming board involves extruding and foaming a foamable molten resin composition obtained by kneading a polystyrene resin, a physical foaming agent and a brominated flame retardant to a plate shape. The physical foaming agent comprises one or more premature dissipative foaming agent chosen from carbon dioxide, water, dimethyl ether, and ethanol, and 3-5C hydrocarbon. The polystyrene-type resin comprises polystyrene-type resin (A) and polystyrene-type resin (B). The polystyrene-type resin (A) has weight average absolute molecular weight of 100000-300000 as measured by gel permeation chromatography with multi-angle light scattering detection, and weight average value of shrinkage factors of 0.90-1.0. The polystyrene-type resin comprises 40-70 pts. wt. polystyrene-type resin (A) and 30-60 pts. wt. polystyrene-type resin (B).
   USE - Manufacture of polystyrene-type resin extrusion foaming board used as building materials such as heat insulating material in construction applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and stable manufacture of polystyrene-type resin extrusion foaming board having excellent mechanical strength, thermal insulation property, flame retardance, compressive strength, and bending strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The polystyrene-type resin (B) has weight average absolute molecular weight of 300000-450000 as measured by gel permeation chromatography with multi-angle light scattering detection, and weight average value of shrinkage factors of less than 0.90.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The addition amount of the physical foaming agent is 0.8-2.0 mol with respect to 1 kg polystyrene-type resin. The total content of the physical foaming agent is 100 mol%. The physical foaming agent comprises 30-60 mol% 3-5C hydrocarbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polystyrene-type resin extruded foam board has apparent density of 20-50 kg/m3 or less and width of 800 mm or more.

GA 201846704A
AB    NOVELTY - An ethylene epoxidation catalyst comprises a fluoride-mineralized carrier containing silver and a rhenium promoter deposited on the carrier. The ratio of total fluorine content and water-extractable fluorine content is 10-110.
   USE - Catalyst is used in epoxidation of ethylene (claimed) during production of ethylene glycol. Uses include but are not limited to antifreeze of automobile engine, hydraulic brake fluid, resin, fibers, solvent, paint, plastic, film, household and industrial cleaner, pharmaceutical preparation, and personal-care product, such as cosmetics and shampoo.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has improved selectivity, catalytic activity, and stability as compared with the epoxidation catalyst which does not comprise the fluoride-mineralized carrier, and enables the epoxidation of ethylene with high productivity.

   (1) method for epoxidation of ethylene, which involves contacting an inlet feed gas (2) comprising ethylene and oxygen with the catalyst; and

   Inlet feed gas (2)
   Epoxidation reactor (4)
   Ethylene oxide separation system (6)
   Recycle gas compressor (9)
   Carbon dioxide separation system (10)

The ratio of total fluorine content and water extractable fluorine content is more than 15 to less than 70. Preferred Properties: The carrier has total fluorine content of more than 2000 ppm to less than 4500 ppm, water extractable fluorine content of more than 50 ppm to less than 180 ppm, water absorption rate of at least 50 wt.%, and surface area of 0.5-5 m2/g, preferably 0.7-3 m2/g.

AB    NOVELTY - A cerium-iron-zirconium composite oxide                catalyst is a solid solution catalyst comprising                cerium oxide, iron oxide and zirconium oxide. The                molar ration of cerium, iron and zirconium in the                catalyst composition is 1-30:30-70:30-70.
   USE - Cerium-iron-zirconium composite oxide catalyst                is used for preparing dimethyl carbonate                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cerium-iron-zirconium composite oxide                catalyst has high catalytic activity and                selectivity, and is economically prepared by                environmentally-friendly process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of cerium-iron-zirconium                composite oxide catalyst, which involves preparing                a mixed solution of iron salt, zirconium salt and                strontium salt in a concentration of 0.1-1 mol/L,                adding 0.1-1 mol/L citric acid ethanol solution,                stirring at room temperature, evaporating solvent,                drying at 50-120 degrees C, and calcining obtained                catalyst precursor at 300-900 degrees C in air                atmosphere. The volume ratio of salt mixed solution                and citric acid ethanol solution is 1:2-2:1;                and
   (2) usage of cerium-iron-zirconium composite                oxide catalyst, which involves adding the                cerium-iron-zirconium composite oxide catalyst,                methanol and a dehydrating agent to an autoclave,                discharging carbon dioxide, performing synthesis                reaction by heating to a reaction temperature under                stirring, separating dehydrating agent and catalyst                from the reaction product, and obtaining dimethyl                carbonate. The usage amount of catalyst is 0.5-10                wt.% and dehydrating agent is 5-200 wt.%, with                respect to methanol.

The dehydrating agent                  is a physical dehydrating agent or a chemical                  dehydrating agent. The physical dehydrating agent                  is calcium chloride, molecular sieve or                  silica.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The chemical                  dehydrating agent is 2,2-dimethoxypropane,                  2-cyanopyridine, dicyclohexylcarbodiimide or                  acetonitrile.

PT P
PN CN108069674-A
TI Preparation of diatomaceous earth capable of efficiently absorbing formaldehyde involves dissolving e.g. titanium tetrachloride in water, adding concentrated sodium hydroxide solution to form colloid, adding e.g. quicklime, and stirring.
AU CHEN Q
   GUAN D
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201842323K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of diatomaceous earth capable of efficiently absorbing formaldehyde involves dissolving titanium tetrachloride or titanium sulfate in water, adding reduced amount of dilute hydrochloric acid or dilute sulfuric acid to adjust the pH value obtain acidic solution, stirring and dissolving, adding concentrated sodium hydroxide solution into titanium tetrachloride or titanium sulfate acidic solution to form titanium hydroxide colloid having solid content of 10-25%, weighing 10-25 pts. wt. sodium carbonate solid solution in colloidal solution, taking dried 38-60 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth and placing in a mechanical stirrer for 30 minutes at uniform speed, spraying the colloid into the mixer, adding quicklime, heavy calcium powder, latex powder, and wood fiber, and completely stirring. The mass ratio of the sodium carbonate to the colloid is 1:2. The dilute acid is inorganic weak acid or organic acid, or the dilute acid is either fruit acid or citric acid.
   USE - Preparation of diatomaceous earth capable of efficiently absorbing formaldehyde.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the adsorption capacity of diatomaceous earth. Titanium dioxide in the diatomaceous earth micropores adsorbs formaldehyde in the air under the light condition and quickly decomposes. The carbon dioxide gas generated during the decomposition process reacts with quicklime to form calcium carbonate, which can effectively increase the strength of diatomaceous earth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for construction method of diatomaceous earth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The colloid comprises 20-50 pts. wt. quicklime, 25-35 pts. wt. heavy calcium powder, 30-45 pts. wt. latex powder, and 5-15 pts. wt. wood fiber.

PT P
PN US2018073153-A1; JP2018043193-A
TI Oxygen generation catalyst useful in an electrochemical or photoelectrochemical device, comprises a graphene oxide layer, and a nickel-iron layered double hydroxide layer supported on the surface of the graphene oxide layer.
AU YAMADA A
   MIKOSHIBA S
   ONO A
   KUDO Y
   TAMURA J
   KITAGAWA R
   TSUTSUMI E
   YAMAGIWA M
   SUGANO Y
   MOTOSHIGE A
   KANNO Y
   TAMURA A
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
GA 201821147E
AB    NOVELTY - Oxygen generation catalyst (100) comprises a graphene oxide layer (103), and a nickel-iron layered double hydroxide (104) layer supported on the surface of the graphene oxide layer, where the graphene oxide layer has an average thickness of 0.33-4 nm.
   USE - The oxygen generation catalyst is useful in an electrochemical or photoelectrochemical device.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen generation catalyst exhibits excellent optical transparency, catalytic activity and durability, and provides a highly efficient and highly durable electrochemical or photoelectrochemical device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a chemical reactor comprising the oxygen generation catalyst which comprises the graphene oxide layer and the nickel-iron layered double hydroxide layer supported on the surface of the graphene oxide layer, a reduction catalyst comprising a carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, and a power supply element connected to the oxygen generation catalyst and the reduction catalyst; and
   (2) producing the oxygen generation catalyst comprising forming a graphene oxide layer by coating and then drying a graphene oxide-containing composition on a substrate surface, and electrodepositing a nickel-iron layered double hydroxide on the surface of the graphene oxide layer using an aqueous solution containing iron ions and nickel ions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic cross-sectional view of the oxygen generation catalyst.
   Oxygen generation catalyst (100)
   Substrate (101)
   Conductive layer (102)
   Graphene oxide layer (103)
   Nickel-iron layered double hydroxide (104)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The oxygen generation catalyst is formed on the semiconductor layer which performs charge separation with light energy. The graphene oxide-containing composition is coated by spin-coating. Preferred Components: The graphene oxide layer contains nitrogen, and has a thickness variation of less than or equal to 2 nm in an area of 1 mu m2. The catalyst further comprises a substrate (101) to support the graphene oxide layer, and a conductive layer (102) between the substrate and the graphene oxide layer. The conductive layer contains a conductive polymer. The substrate is a conductive substrate, and comprises a semiconductor layer which performs charge separation with light energy. The power supply element comprises a semiconductor layer which performs charge separation with light energy.
   EXAMPLE - 0.02 wt.% Poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene):poly(ethylene glycol) was spin-coated on an indium tin oxide substrate which had been washed with water and acetone. 0.5 mg/l Graphene oxide was spin-coated on it to form a graphene oxide layer. The spin coating was performed at 500 rpm for 3 seconds, 3 second sloping, and then at 1500 rpm for 60 seconds. The surface of the thus formed graphene oxide layer was treated with ammonia. On this graphene oxide layer, a nickel-iron layered double hydroxide layer was electrodeposited as an oxygen generation catalyst under the following conditions. Solution in which equal amounts of an 80 mM aqueous nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate solution and a 20 mM aqueous iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate solution were mixed was prepared as an electrolyte solution. The electrodeposition on the graphene oxide layer was performed using this electrolyte solution. Using Kapton tape, only a prescribed area (8 mm psi ) of the graphene oxide layer surface of a sample was exposed. The sample was arranged as a working electrode in one chamber of an H-type cell, a platinum wire was arranged as a counter electrode in the other chamber, and a glass filter was installed between these two chambers. Further, a silver/silver chloride electrode was arranged as a reference electrode. Using this apparatus, electrodeposition was performed at a voltage of -1.2 V (versus normal hydrogen electrode) for 10 seconds to prepare a catalyst. In the thus obtained catalyst, the thickness of the graphene oxide layer was 2-4 nm and the thickness variation in an area of 1 mu m2 was less than or equal to 2 nm.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dividing gas phase flashed by the four-stage flash tank (4) into first and second gas phase streams. The first gas phase stream is introduced into the second stage. A gas phase stream (2) is introduced into the secondary flash tank by a compressor (7). An outlet of the injector (6) is connected to the compressor. The sulfur-enriched absorbent is sent to the downstream through the stripper (5) for gas stripping and the gaseous phase is sent to the downstream as the tail gas.
   USE - Semi-lean absorbent regeneration method.
   ADVANTAGE - The content of carbon dioxide in the semi-lean absorbent is effectively reduced. The absorption capacity of the semi-lean absorbent is increased. The circulation of the semi-lean absorbent is reduced, thus the system energy consumption is reduced. The semi-lean absorbent regeneration effect is significantly improved with the use of the ejector. The compressor load is reduced to a certain extent. The arrangement is simple in structure. The number of flashing devices is reduced which saves floor space and reduces the construction difficulty.

PN CN113122868-A
TI Preparing carbon monoxide by electrochemical catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises preparing electrolytic cell, using hydrogen ion exchange membrane to divide the electrolytic cell into cathode chamber and anode chamber.
AU CAO X
   CAO H
AE JINZHOU JUNREN MINGYANG NEW TECHNOLOGY (JINZ-Non-standard)
GA 202185337C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon monoxide by electrochemical catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises preparing electrolytic cell using hydrogen ion exchange membrane to divide electrolytic cell into cathode chamber filled with sodium bicarbonate solution and anode chamber filled with sulfuric acid solution; ensuring tightness of cathode chamber and anode chamber in electrolytic cell, and adding graphite electrodes; keeping argon in cathode chamber, heating, passing in carbon dioxide, and recovering unabsorbed carbon dioxide; connecting positive and negative electrodes of direct current power supply to graphite electrode in anode chamber and graphite electrode in cathode chamber; supplying power to graphite electrodes, using collecting tube to collect gas released from cathode chamber, and stopping power supply; and passing gas collected from cathode chamber to gas scrubber filled with sodium hydroxide solution, concentrated sulfuric acid and oxygen absorbent to collect gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon monoxide by electrochemical catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method discharges air rich in solution by introducing argon gas in battery cell, and increases discharge of temperature auxiliary impurity of electrolytic cell, realizes reduction of conversion rate caused by interference of impure gas in electrolytic cell when discharging carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbon monoxide by electrochemical catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises preparing electrolytic cell using hydrogen ion exchange membrane to divide electrolytic cell into cathode chamber and anode chamber, where cathode chamber is filled with sodium bicarbonate solution, and anode chamber is filled with sulfuric acid solution; ensuring tightness of cathode chamber and anode chamber in electrolytic cell, adding graphite electrodes to solution inside cathode chamber and anode chamber; keeping argon in cathode chamber, raising temperature of electrolytic cell to 60-80 degrees C, passing in carbon dioxide into cathode chamber, and recovering unabsorbed carbon dioxide; connecting positive and negative electrodes of direct current power supply to graphite electrode in anode chamber and graphite electrode in cathode chamber; turning on DC power supply to supply power to graphite electrodes located in anode chamber and cathode chamber, using collecting tube to collect gas released from cathode chamber, and stopping power supply; and passing gas collected from cathode chamber to gas scrubber filled with sodium hydroxide solution, concentrated sulfuric acid and oxygen absorbent to collect gas after scrubbing.

PN CN111329836-A; CN111329836-B; WO2021203606-A1
TI Preparing compound amino acid injection for animals comprises (i) preparing base fluid, preparing intermediate solution, adding intermediate solution with activated carbon, decolorizing, and filtering.
AU LI F
   CHEN S
   ZHAO S
   GUO Y
   LI Y
   WANG W
   XING J
AE HEBEI KEXING PHARM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
   HEBEI KEXING PHARM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202061266R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing compound amino acid injection for animals comprises e.g. boiling and sterilizing injection water, introducing carbon dioxide gas, controlling the water temperature at 75-100 degrees C, adding antioxidant disodium ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and the antioxidant sodium bisulfite to dissolve to obtain base liquid A, adding Group B amino acid to base liquid A, dissolving, reducing the temperature, adding Group D amino acid to solution C, dissolving, lowering the temperature to obtain solution E, adding gamma -aminobutyric acid to the solution E, dissolving at 20-35 degrees C, adjusting the pH value to 5.7-7, set the volume to obtain the intermediate solution F, under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, adding intermediate solution F with activated carbon, decolorizing, and filtering to obtain amino acid injection for animals.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing compound amino acid injection for animals.
   ADVANTAGE - The injection is suitable for body repair during the treatment of livestock diseases and the recovery period after illness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing compound amino acid injection for animals comprises (i) taking injection water, boiling and sterilizing, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and controlling the water temperature at 75-100 degrees C, under the protection of carbon dioxide gas flow, adding antioxidant disodium ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and the antioxidant sodium bisulfite to dissolve to obtain base liquid A, (ii) under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, adding Group B amino acid to base liquid A, dissolving at 70-100 degrees C, and then reducing the temperature to 60-70 degrees C, where Group B amino acids include arginine, histidine, leucine, isoleucine, lysine, phenylalanine, threonine, valine, methionine, glycine, alanine, proline, serine, aspartic acid and glutamic acid, under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, adding Group D amino acid to solution C, dissolving at 60-70 degrees C, and lowering the temperature to 20-35 degrees C to obtain solution E, where Group D amino acids include tryptophan, tyrosine and cysteine hydrochloride, under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, adding gamma -aminobutyric acid to the solution E, dissolving at 20-35 degrees C, adjusting the pH value to 5.7-7, set the volume to obtain the intermediate solution F, and (iii) under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, adding intermediate solution F with activated carbon, decolorizing, and filtering to obtain amino acid injection for animals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The prepared compound amino acid injection for animals is sterilized. The weight-volume ratio of activated carbon to intermediate solution F is 0.5-1 kg:1000 l. Preferred Method: In the method, before sterilization, the amino acid injection for animals is filled under the protection of carbon dioxide gas. The decolorization is carried out at 20-35 degrees C for 30-40 minutes. The filtration includes coarse filtration and fine filtration in this order. During the filling process, the residual oxygen content in the container is less than or equal to 3%. The sterilization is carried out at 110-121 degrees C for 15-40 minutes.

TI Carbonating fine fraction resulting from dissociation of deconstruction concrete, comprises passing carbonation material through carbonation reactor and bringing material in contact with carbon dioxide or gas containing carbon dioxide.
AU CORDONNIER A
AE FIVES FCB (FIVE-C)
GA 202228161A
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonating fine fraction resulting from dissociation of deconstruction concrete comprises (i) passing carbonation material through the carbonation reactor, and (ii) bringing carbonation material into contact with carbon dioxide or a gas containing at least 5% carbon dioxide in the carbonation reactor. The fine fraction comprises sand and hydrated cement paste whose proportion by mass is at least 1.25 times higher than that of the hydrated cement paste contained in the deconstruction concrete from which it is derived, and fine fraction is referred to as carbonation material, where carbonation process is implemented by means of an installation comprising a carbonation reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for carbonating fine fraction resulting from dissociation of deconstruction concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces residence time of carbonation material in carbonation reactor, ensures optimum carbonation of carbonating material, is environmentally-friendly and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonation material passing through the carbonation reactor has dimensions less than 4 mm. The relative humidity of the gases in the carbonation reactor is 40-100%. The moisture content of carbonation material entering the carbonation reactor is 3-7%. Preferred Method: The temperature within carbonation reactor is 20-90 degrees C. The temperature inside the carbonation reactor is 40-80 degrees C. The residence time of carbonation material within carbonation reactor is 20-120 minutes. The carbonating step is carried out at a pressure substantially equal to atmospheric pressure. The method comprises dissociating deconstruction concrete comprises breaking up the deconstruction concrete in a machine for breaking up by compression in a bed of material under the effect of a fragmentation force. The machine comprises a tank forming an inner fragmentation track around a longitudinal axis of the machine. A hub forming an outer fragmentation track around a longitudinal axis of the machine, hub being placed inside the tank. At least one vibrator rotated about a longitudinal axis of the machine. A hub forms an outer fragmentation track around a longitudinal axis of the machine the hub being placed inside the tank. At least one vibrator rotated around a longitudinal axis of the machine, and connected to one or the other of the tank and the hub. A system controls at least one parameter of the fragmentation force from among the speed of rotation of the vibrator(s) and angle of phase shift between at least two vibrators. The dissociation comprises rotation of vibrator or vibrators of the machine of fragmentation, so that the tank performs a movement in a transverse plane of the machine relative to the hub, feeding deconstruction concrete fragmentation machine, fragmentation of deconstruction concrete between the track outside and the track inside fragmentation, prior dissociation process in which the control system adjusts at least one parameter of the rotation of the vibrators so as to generate a breaking force by the machine allowing the dissociation of the carbonation material from the other constituents deconstruction concrete.

PN PL434817-A1
TI Method for separation of carbon dioxide in syngas production plant, involves introducing mixture of gases into carbonate anodes of fuel cells, and reacting hydrogen on anodes of cells with carbonate ion taken from electrolyte.
AU BERNAT R
   MILEWSKI J
   SMOLKA W
   ZDEB J
AE TAURON WYTWARZANIE SA (TAUR-Non-standard)
GA 2022813476
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using carbonate fuel cells in systems with an electrolyzer and a methanation reactor for every 1 kW of installed power of the carbonate cathode cell fuel cells. A mixture of gases selected in appropriate amounts is introduced. Carbon dioxide and oxygen react on the cathodes to form a carbonate ion that penetrates the electrolyte and from the cathodes of carbonate fuel cells. A mixture of gases with a temperature of 528-650 degrees C in specified amounts flows out. A mixture of gases is introduced into the carbonate anodes of fuel cells. The hydrogen reacts on the anodes of the cells with the carbonate ion taken from the electrolyte to form carbon dioxide and water vapor.
   USE - Method for separation of carbon dioxide in syngas production plant.
