GA 2021E34881
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oxidized graphite alkyne nanosheet                by supercritical carbon dioxide involves dispersing                graphite and lithium hexafluorosilicate into water                in mass ratio of 1:(2-8) to obtain dispersion,                transferring dispersion solution to supercritical                device, injecting carbon dioxide in supercritical                equipment, stirring and reacting for 3-6 hours                under supercritical conditions, naturally cooling                to room temperature and then releasing pressure,                separating system after supercritical treatment,                washing and drying to obtain oxidated                graphite-alkyne nano sheet.

PT P
PN CN110357837-A; CN110357837-B
TI Epoxidation of ethylene includes performing epoxidation reaction on ethylene, oxygen, carbon dioxide, chlorine containing components and stabilizing gas in the presence of highly selective silver catalyst.
AU SUN X
   REN D
   LI J
   DAI W
   TANG Z
   WANG S
   HAN H
   LIN Q
   WANG H
   LIN W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 2019888319
AB    NOVELTY - Epoxidation of ethylene comprises performing epoxidation reaction on feed gas in the presence of epoxidation catalyst. The feed gas is ethylene, oxygen, carbon dioxide, chlorine containing components and stabilizing gas. The epoxidation catalyst is highly selective silver catalyst. When the concentration of ethylene oxide at the outlet is more than 2 mol% and maintained for more than 60 hours, reduce the concentration of chlorine component and ethylene. When the temperature of the reactor intermediate bed is constant for more than 60 hours, increase the concentration of carbon dioxide. When the temperature of the reactor intermediate bed is constant for more than 60 hours, restore the concentration of chlorine component, ethylene and carbon dioxide to the normal starting concentration.
   USE - The method is for epoxidation of ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves selectivity of catalyst without increasing energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The highly selective silver catalyst is prepared by impregnating alpha -alumina support in the solution of silver compound, organic amine compound and additive, filtering and performing activation heat treatment. Preferred Components: The additive is alkali metal additive, alkali earth metal additive, optional rhenium additive and optional rhenium co additive. The highly selective silver catalyst comprises 10-20 wt,% silver, 10-3000 ppm alkali metal element, 100-10000 ppm alkaline earth metal element and 2000 ppm platinum element system.

PT P
PN GC10391-A
TI Producing soda ash and calcium chloride by ammoniating brine with gaseous ammonia, heating limestone, reacting ammoniated brine with carbon dioxide, calcining sodium bicarbonate, reacting calcium oxide with ammonium chloride and recycling.
AU ABU-SHARKH B F
AE ALOTAISHAN M A (ALOT-Individual)
GA 201996050F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing soda ash and calcium chloride, involves (a) ammoniating brine with gaseous ammonia to form ammoniated brine, heating limestone to produce calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, and reacting the ammoniated brine with the carbon dioxide to produce sodium bicarbonate, ammonium chloride and brine effluent, (b) calcining the sodium bicarbonate, and decomposing to produce soda ash and gaseous carbon dioxide, (c) reacting the calcium oxide with the ammonium chloride to produce calcium chloride, water and ammonia, and (d) recycling the ammonia to be used in the initial step of ammoniating the brine, and recycling the water and brine effluent to be used as the brine in the initial step.
   USE - The method is useful for producing soda ash and calcium chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and does not produce waste and hazardous byproducts.

PN US2019070109-A1; TW686214-B1; TW202017563-A
TI Pharmaceutical composition useful as oral chemotherapy platform includes poorly water-soluble drug, acid initiator, lipophilic or ampiphilic solvent, and foaming agent producing carbon dioxide bubbles.
AU SUNG H
   LIN P
   CHEN K
AE UNIV NAT TSING HUA (UNTH-C)
GA 201921699U
AB    NOVELTY - A pharmaceutical composition comprises a poorly water-soluble drug; a lipophilic or ampiphilic solvent; an acid initiator; and a foaming agent producing carbon dioxide bubbles when the acid initiator is dissolved in the intestinal fluid to form an acidic environment. The poorly water-soluble drug attaches to the solvent to form a self-assembled monolayer carrier system with a bile salts surrounding the carbon dioxide bubbles in water when the composition is dispersed in an intestinal tract, and lipid oil drops containing the poorly water-soluble drug form when the carbon dioxide bubbles burst at the air-liquid interface in the intestinal tract.
   USE - Composition in the form of tablet or capsule useful as oral chemotherapy platform. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has a very high drug release efficiency, and good bioavailability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The foaming agent comprises carbonates or bicarbonates.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The solvent comprises capric acid. The acid initiator comprises organic acid anhydrides or organic acids. The acid initiator comprises diethylene triaminepentaacetic dianhydride, citric acid anhydride, or citric acid. The poorly water-soluble drug comprises curcumin, paclitaxel, doxorubicin, or their derivatives. The poorly water-soluble drug is present at 1-3 mg. The lipophilic or amphiphilic solvent is present at 15-60 mg. The acid initiator is present at 2-25 mg. The foaming agent is present at 1-20 mg.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The enteric coating comprises methacrylic acid copolymer, hypromellose phthalate, hydroxypropyl cellulose acetate, hydroxypropyl cellulose succinate, or carboxymethyl ethyl cellulose.
   ACTIVITY - None given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration is oral (claimed). No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN US2018264173-A1
TI System useful for decellularizing tissue e.g. porcine aorta, comprises pretreatment chamber comprising solution comprising surfactant, decellularization solution comprising carbon dioxide, ethanol and water, and environmental chamber.
AU CASALI D M
   MATTHEWS M A
AE UNIV SOUTH CAROLINA (USCL-C)
GA 2018779274
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a pretreatment chamber comprising a pretreatment solution, where the pretreatment solution comprises a surfactant, a decellularization solution comprising carbon dioxide, ethanol, and water, and an environmental chamber comprising a treatment chamber, where the environmental chamber is maintained at a temperature greater than 31.1 degrees C and the carbon dioxide is maintained at a pressure greater than 7.38 megapascals to form supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - The system is useful for decellularizing tissue e.g. porcine aorta, which is used in tissue engineering and removing cellular matter from tissue.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively decellularizes tissue within a short period of time without using harsh chemicals or solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for decellularizing tissue; and
   (2) two-part decellularization solution for removing cells from tissue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred System: The treatment chamber receives the tissue. The environmental chamber comprises a presaturation chamber, where the decellularization solution is mixed in the presaturation chamber. The system further comprises a pump, where the pump compresses the carbon dioxide. The system facilitates removal of cells from the tissue so that tissue treated with decellularization solution contains less than 0.05 mu g of DNA per mg of dry tissue after the tissue is exposed to the decellularization solution. Preferred Solution: The decellularization solution is deliverable from the presaturation chamber to the treatment chamber. The decellularization solution is delivered to the treatment chamber at a flow rate of 0.1-5 ml/minute. Preferred Component: The surfactant comprises sodium dodecyl sulfate.

PT P
PN CN108299298-A; CN108299298-B
TI Extracting norisoboldine comprises e.g. crushing and sieving Lindera aggregata root, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, adding ionic liquid solution, and carrying out ultrasound-assisted extraction.
AU WANG L
   BAI M
   QIN Y
   LIU B
   WANG Y
AE ZHEJIANG ACAD FORESTRY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018590698
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting norisoboldine comprises (i) crushing and sieving Lindera aggregata root, and adding into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and receiving the extractor from the separation vessel to obtain extract, (ii) adding the above supercritical carbon dioxide extract into the ionic liquid solution, and carrying out ultrasound-assisted extraction to obtain extract, and filtering the extract to obtain filtrate and a solid material, (iii) adding the ionic liquid extraction filtrate into organic solvent for extraction, allowing to stand and layering to obtain upper organic phase extract and the lower aqueous layer, concentrating the upper organic extract under reduced pressure to obtain norisoboldine.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting norisoboldine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high extraction rate, high quality and purity ion liquid can be recycled, convenient to operate; and is environmentally friendly and suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction conditions are: the temperature is 40-60 degrees C, the pressure is 20-30 MPa, the static hold time is 30-60 minutes, and the dynamic extraction time is 30-120 minutes. In step (iii), concentrating lower aqueous layer under reduced pressure to remove the residual organic solvent in the upper layer, diluting to the appropriate concentration with water and continue to use; or the concentrating lower aqueous layer under reduced pressure to completely remove water and organic solvents, drying, and storing in a dry environment. Preferred Components: In step (iii), the organic solvent is ethyl acetate or n-butanol.

TI Calculating minimum miscible pressure of carbon            dioxide flooding in low permeability reservoir, by            obtaining minimum miscibility pressure error of sample,            establishing augmented matrix and fitting basic            parameters of reservoir.
AU ZHENG Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 201857501W
AB    NOVELTY - Calculating minimum miscible pressure of                carbon dioxide flooding in low permeability                reservoir involves obtaining minimum miscible                pressure and basic parameters of samples by                capillary displacement experiment method,                calculating minimum miscible pressure of sample by                theoretical formula method to calculate the basic                parameters of samples, comparing capillary                displacement experiment method and theoretical                formula method to obtain minimum miscibility                pressure error of sample, establishing augmented                matrix and fitting formula of minimum miscible                pressure and basic parameters of low-permeability                reservoir by the equation given in the                specification.
   USE - Calculating minimum miscible pressure of                carbon dioxide flooding in low permeability                reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical and                accurate calculation of minimum miscible pressure                of carbon dioxide flooding in low permeability                reservoir with high calculation accuracy.

PT P
PN CN108097070-A; CN108097070-B
TI Preparing polyether block amide gas separation membrane for separating carbon dioxide and nitrogen, involves using solvent, heating zeolite imidazole ester material, subjecting to dry phase conversion method to obtain final product.
AU ZHANG X
   WANG Y
   ZHANG T
   LIU C
   LI H
   ZONG Z
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201844438U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyether block amide gas separation membrane involves using solvent, heating zeolite imidazole ester material, subjecting to dry phase conversion method to obtain doped zeolite imidazole ester of polyether block amide gas separation membrane. The prepared film is homogeneous membrane. The separating matrix is polyphenylene ether-polyamide block copolymer. The additive is zeolite imidazole ester material. The thickness of wet film is 50-500 micrometer.
   USE - Method for preparing polyether block amide gas separation membrane for separating carbon dioxide and nitrogen(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare polyether block amide gas separation membrane that has good flexibility, excellent comprehensive performance, high carbon dioxide permeation flux and carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity, resists acid and alkali, and in simple and eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The zeolite imidazole ester material is Zeolitic imidazolate frameworks (ZIFs)-8 or zinc/nickel-ZIF-8. The content of zeolite imidazole ester material is 5-20 wt.%. Preferred Condition: The particle size of the ZIF-8 material is 45-55 nanometer. The grain diameter of zinc/nickel-ZIF-8 is 38-46 nanometer. The ultrasonic frequency is 20-50 kilohertz. The ultrasonic temperature is 20-70 degrees C. The solid content of solution C is 3-25 wt.%. The amount of polyether block amide is 5-20 wt.%. Preferred Process: The zinc nitrate hexahydrate is dissolved in methanol solution to obtain 0.01-0.1 mol/L solution. The 2-methyl imidazole is dissolved in 100 mL methanol solution to obtain 0.04-7 mol/L solution. The solution is placed in 4A molecular sieve for 6-24 hours to remove residual water to obtain methanol solution of hexahydrate zinc nitrate and 2-methyl imidazole methanol solution. The methanol solution of hexahydrate zinc nitrate is named as product A. The 2-methyl imidazole methanol solution named as product B. The product B and the product A are added into three-mouth flask, stirred for 12-48 hours at 20-80 degrees C, centrifuged for 10-120 minutes, rotating at speed of 8000-12000 revolution per minute. The lower layer in centrifugal tube is precipitate which is washed for three times with methanol, then placed in vacuum oven for drying at 60-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain white ZIF-8 powder. The ratio of zinc nitrate hexahydrate and methylimidazole is 1-10:4-700. The 100 mL methanol solution is added in 5-30 g 4 A molecular sieve. The polyether block amide is dissolved in N,N-dimethyl acetamide to obtain 30-200 g/L solution as solution C. The solution C is poured, ultrasonically dispersed for 0.5-4 hours and magnetic stirrer is used to rotate at speed of 100-500 revolution per minute at 20-90 degrees C and stirred for 12-48 hours to dissolve to form homogeneous casting film liquid as product D. The product D is defoamed for 12-24 hours at 25 degrees C with polyether block amide and ZIF-8 powder in ratio 3-20:0.15-5. The two product D of scraper is uniformly scrap. The thickness of the wet film is controlled at 50-500 micro. The glass plate under the normal temperature, solvent is volatilize for 24-48 hours, then dried for 24-48 hours to remove the residual solvent in vacuum drying oven at 40-90 degrees C. The effective area of the membrane permeation is 1-12.56 cm2. The sweeping gas is hydrogen. The gas flow rate is 10-60 mL/minute. The flow rate of feed gas is 10-60 mL/minute. The flow rate of the raw material side and the permeate side is measured by the soap-film flowmeter for penetrating side, gas phase chromatography. The testing temperature is room temperature. The pressure difference is 0.1-0.8 megapascal.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN107974690-A; CN107974690-B
TI Electrode used in catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide producing formic acid, comprises electrode substrate and nano-crystalline material coating set on electrode substrate surface, and bismuth nano-material coating on electrode substrate.
AU ZHANG R
   LV W
   WANG W
   BEI J
   SHAO L
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2018351240
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode comprises electrode substrate and nano-crystalline material coating, which is disposed on the surface of the electrode substrate, and bismuth nano-material coating is adhered to the electrode substrate by an binder.
   USE - Electrode used in catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode effectively reduces carbon dioxide reduction for preparing formic acid and improves the reduction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing electrode, which involves mixing nano-crystalline material coating, binder, and solvent to obtain suspension and adding suspension droplets to surface of bare electrode substrate to obtain desired product; and
   (2) preparing formic acid using electrode involves reducing activated electrode catalytically and passing into carbon dioxide in the electrolytic cell to produce formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The bismuth nano material coating mainly comprises nano-sized bismuth compound, preferably, bismuth compound is selected from bismuth oxide, bismuth oxide, bismuth oxychloride, bismuth carbonate, or bismuth subnitrate. The binder is a resin binder, which is perfluorinated sulfonic acid resin. Preferred Conditions: The suspension liquid is dispersed under ultrasonic condition, so that the nano material coating layer of the bismuth nanometer material is dispersed and mixed with the binder. The bare electrode substrate is polished before dropping the suspension. The activation of electrode is performed in an alkaline solution at a potential of -1.1 Volt to -1.4 Volt for 2-30 hours. The formic acid production is performed in cathode tank of electrolytic cell, which is a bicarbonate solution, where the electrode is placed in the cathode tank, anode tank of the electrolytic cell has strong alkali solution, and surface coating is placed in the anode tank and electrode is coated with titanium-based iridium ruthenium coating.

TI Producing urea formaldehyde concentrate from hydrocarbon feedstock by producing synthesis gas, splitting, subjecting to high temperature water gas shift, removing carbon dioxide, adding nitrogen, and catalytically converting.
AU SYMRENG M
   HAN P A
   SPETH C H
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202093836H
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing a urea formaldehyde concentrate from a hydrocarbon feedstock, involves (a) producing a synthesis gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide by partial combustion, (b) splitting the synthesis gas, (c) subjecting the second synthesis gas in series to a high temperature water gas shift and a medium temperature water gas shift conversion, (d) removing carbon dioxide from the second synthesis gas, (e) adding nitrogen into the carbon dioxide depleted second synthesis to obtain hydrogen to nitrogen ratio of 2.8-3.2, (f) catalytically converting the nitrogen and hydrogen in the purified second synthesis gas, (g) passing ammonia containing effluent to a urea synthesis stage, (h) catalytically converting the carbon monoxide, (i) recycling effluent, (j) subjecting the effluent containing raw methanol to distillation, (k) oxidizing, and (l) absorbing the formaldehyde in water and an aqueous urea solution prepared from the urea product.
   USE - The process is used for producing a urea formaldehyde concentrate from a hydrocarbon feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple, robust and cost-effective, and requires a minimum of equipment and unit operations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for producing a urea formaldehyde concentrate from a hydrocarbon feedstock, involves (a) producing a synthesis gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide by partial combustion and steam reforming the hydrocarbon feedstock in an autothermal reforming stage, (b) splitting the synthesis gas from step (a) into a first synthesis gas and a second synthesis gas, (c) subjecting the second synthesis gas from step (b) in series to a high temperature water gas shift and a medium temperature water gas shift conversion, (d) removing at least part of the carbon dioxide from the second synthesis gas from step (c) to obtain a carbon dioxide depleted second synthesis gas, (e) adding nitrogen into the carbon dioxide depleted second synthesis to obtain a molar ratio hydrogen to nitrogen of 2.8-3.2, (f) catalytically converting the nitrogen and hydrogen in the purified second synthesis gas from step (e) in an inert free ammonia synthesis stage and withdrawing an effluent containing ammonia, (g) passing at least part of the ammonia containing effluent to a urea synthesis stage and converting the ammonia in the effluent to urea product by reaction with at least part of the carbon dioxide being removed from the synthesis gas in step (d), (h) catalytically converting the carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the first synthesis gas from step (c) in a once through methanol synthesis stage and with drawing an effluent containing raw methanol and an effluent containing unconverted first synthesis gas, (i) recycling the effluent containing the unconverted first synthesis gas to the medium temperature shift conversion in step (c) and/or as fuel to a fired heater and/or to step (e), (j) subjecting the effluent containing raw methanol to distillation and withdrawing purified methanol from the distillation, (k) oxidizing at least a part of the purified methanol from step (j) to formaldehyde, and (l) absorbing the formaldehyde from step (k) in water and an aqueous urea solution prepared from the urea product in step (g) to obtain the urea formaldehyde concentrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process comprises: adding the nitrogen into carbon dioxide depleted second synthesis gas in step (f) by a nitrogen wash; recycling the effluent containing the unconverted first synthesis gas by an ejector; and performing the methanol synthesis stage in step (i) in at least one methanol reactor containing one or two fixed bed methanol catalyst. Preferred Composition: The steam carbon ratio in the synthesis gas in step (a) is 0.4-1.

AU LACKNER K S
   DAHLGREN E
AE AIRCELA INC (AIRC-Non-standard)
GA 202272471K
AB    NOVELTY - A fuel production system (1000) includes a first portion in which carbon dioxide is extracted from a gas volume received by the system, a second portion in which hydrogen is generated from a water volume received by the system, and a third portion in which a fuel is produced from synthesis of the extracted carbon dioxide and generated hydrogen.
   USE - System used for producing fuels e.g. gasoline fuels, diesel fuels and jet fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is extremely flexible regarding its design, both in terms of structural modifications and changes in operating procedures, and configured for operation in generally modular or granular fashion. The system is configured to be operated based on user preferences pertaining to price of electricity and/or availability of low-to-no carbon electricity, for allowing the user to control carbon footprint of fuel produced via system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram illustrating the fuel production system.
   Gas capture (100)
   Carbon dioxide regeneration (200)
   Hydrogen generation (300)
   Fuel synthesis (400)
   Water pre-treatment (500)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The extraction of carbon dioxide in the first portion involves aqueous alkaline sorbent gas capture and a gas contactor of generally tubular shape. The generation of hydrogen in the second portion involves alkaline electrolysis. The pre-treatment of the water volume in the fourth portion involves ion exchange-based pre-treatment. The third portion involves methanol-to-gasoline (MTG) synthesis or Fischer-Tropsch synthesis. Preferred Components: The gas volume comprises ambient air. The gas volume comprises an output of an exhaust system. The gas contactor has a square or rectangular cross-section. The gas contactor has a circular or elliptical cross-section. The system further includes a fourth portion in which the water volume received by the system is pre-treated before hydrogen is generated. The system is further configured to receive input power from at least one of a power grid, an energy storage unit, an energy generating unit, and a chemical source of energy. The energy generating unit is configured to harness at least one of solar energy and wind energy. The system is modular. The system occupies a space of 5 m3 or less, preferably 1 mx1 mx2 m. The system has mass of 300 kg or less. In the second portion, hydrogen additionally is generated from water produced by the system. The gas contactor is configured to change shape during its operation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fuel comprises a liquid fuel. The fuel comprises at least one of gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel. The gas volume comprises a biogas. The fuel comprises dimethyl ether (DME).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The gas contactor comprises a fabric. The fabric comprises nylon.

PN KR2022066580-A; KR2457128-B1
TI Producing nitrogen-supported mesoporous carbon            structure useful e.g. for separating carbon-dioxide,            comprises e.g. polymerizing COP precursor and            nitrogen-containing carbon monomer by microwaves, and            calcining organic polymer structure.
AU YAVUZ C T
   JUNGEUISUK
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202273572J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing nitrogen-supported mesoporous carbon                structure, comprises (a) heating and polymerizing a                mixture including a COP precursor, a                nitrogen-containing carbon monomer, a crosslinking                agent, an initiator, and a metal nanoparticle                dispersion using microwaves to obtain an organic                polymer structure; (b) calcining the organic                polymer structure; and (c) removing the metal                nanoparticles by acid-treating the calcined organic                polymer structure.
   USE - The nitrogen-supported mesoporous carbon                structure is useful for separating carbon-dioxide                from a mixture containing molecule of methane,                nitrogen, ethylene, ethane, propene and propane and                carbon-dioxide (claimed), and for gas storage using                porosity and separation of molecular monomers; and                as an electrochemical catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product with pores of                easily controllable uniform size within a short                time; reduces the manufacturing time and reaction                time (at least about 4 hours vs. 30 minutes);                facilitates simple synthesis; and evaluates the                multi-purpose catalytic activity ability according                to the type of metal nanoparticles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                method for separating carbon-dioxide from a mixture                containing molecule of methane, nitrogen, ethylene,                ethane, propene and propane and carbon-dioxide,                comprising using the nitrogen-supported mesoporous                carbon structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a) heating is                  performed at 80-150 degrees C for 10-30 minutes.                  The method further comprises washing and drying                  the organic polymer structure obtained in step                  (a) with alcohol, or washing and drying after                  step (c). In the step (b) sintering is performed                  at 450-550 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and acid                  treatment is performed at 50-70 degrees C for 3-5                  hours. Preferred Components: The metal                  nanoparticles are iron(II,III) oxide, iron                  manganese oxide, iron cobalt oxide, iron nickel                  oxide and/or zinc iron oxide. The COP precursor                  is polyvinyl alcohol or polyvinyl acetate. The                  carbon monomer containing the nitrogen element is                  acrylonitrile The crosslinking agent is                  divinylbenzene, 1,4-butanediol dimethacrylate                  (BDDMA), 1,4-butanediol divinyl ether (BDDVE),                  diallyl carbonate (DAC),                  diethyleneglycoldivinylether,                  ethyleneglycoldiacrylate,                  ethyleneglycoldimethacrylate and/or methylene                  bisacrylamide. The initiator is                  azobisisobutyronitrile (AIBN) or benzoyl                  peroxide.
   EXAMPLE - Polyvinyl alcohol (25 ml, 1 wt.%) aqueous                solution, acrylonitrile (1 ml, 15.25 mmol) and                1,4-divinylbenzene (3 ml, 21.04 mmol) were added                together, and then, an initiator AIBN (25 mg, 0.15                mmol) and iron oxide nano-dispersion (hexane, 10                ml) were added, and then heated in a microwave oven                at 90 degrees C for 30 minutes. The obtained black                product was washed with methanol and ethanol using                filter paper, and then dried in an oven at 120                degrees C for 12 hours to obtain                COP-122-iron(II,III) oxide structure.

GA 202263755S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gas diffusion electrode involves pulverizing the carbon material through a 500-4000 mesh sieve to obtain a carbon material powder, mixing and beating the carbon material powder and a solvent at 8000-12000 revolutions/minute to obtain a carbon material slurry with a particle size of less than 15 micrometer. A hydrophobic polymer and a pore-forming agent are added to the carbon material slurry, and then beat at 16,000-22,000 revolutions/minute for 3-5 hours to obtain a microporous layer coating with a particle size of less than 15 micrometer. The microporous layer coating is coated on the base layer and dried to obtain a microporous layer, then disposed a catalyst layer on the upper surface of the microporous layer to obtain a gas diffusion electrode.
   USE - Method for preparing gas diffusion electrode for carbon dioxide electroreduction reaction
   ADVANTAGE - The method optimizes the preparation step of the microporous layer, the prepared gas diffusion electrode has excellent gas transmission rate, when it is used for carbon dioxide electroreduction reaction, the diffusion transmission of carbon dioxide in the gas diffusion electrodes is fast, reducing the concentration polarization, so as to improve the conversion rate of the carbon dioxide.

PN CN112355488-A; CN112355488-B
TI Method for preparing soft-edge diaphragm resistant to laser damage, involves irradiating fused quartz surface by using focused carbon dioxide laser pulse, and using arrangement density of pits to control transmittance of light.
AU ZHANG C
   ZHANG L
   JIANG X
   LIAO W
   CHEN J
   FANG Z
   WANG H
   LUAN X
   ZHU Q
AE LASER FUSION RES CENT CHINA ACAD ENG PHY (CAEP-C)
GA 202118266T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves debugging the power and pulse width of the focused carbon dioxide laser pulse. The fused quartz surface is irradiated by using the focused carbon dioxide laser pulse. The fused quartz is strongly absorbed by the CO2 laser energy fast heating to generate phase explosion ablation. The crack-free ablation pit point is formed. The determination is made to check whether residual stress of the ablation pit point is 0. The arrangement density of pits is used to control the transmittance of light, so that the density of the pits ablated by carbon dioxide laser phase explosion at the edge of the soft-sided aperture gradually increases toward the edge of the soft-sided aperture.
   USE - Method for preparing soft-edge diaphragm resistant to laser damage.
   ADVANTAGE - The problem of high cost of preparing the soft edge diaphragm by ultra-short laser pulses is solved, and the problem of thermal stress of the carbon dioxide laser preparation of the soft edge diaphragm is solved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the method for preparing a soft-edged aperture resistant to laser damage.

PT P
PN EP3760663-A1; WO2021004814-A1; CN114026148-A; EP3994198-A1; SG11202111459-A; US2022235176-A1
TI Preparation of polyether ester carbonate polyol            used for foam, involves performing catalytic addition            of alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide to            hydrogen-functional starter substance, and metering in            alkylene oxide and in lactide into reactor.
AU MUELLER T E
   GUERTLER C
   SUBHANI M A
   LEITNER W
   GLUTLE C
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 202104825P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyether ester carbonate                polyol involves performing catalytic addition of                alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide to an                hydrogen-functional starter substance in the                presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst                by (a) submitting of a subset of                hydrogen-functional starter substance and/or a                suspending agent which excludes hydrogen-functional                groups into the reactor, optionally together with                DMC catalyst, (b) metering in alkylene oxide and                optionally carbon dioxide into the reactor during                the reaction, and metering in lactide into the                reactor.
   USE - Production of polyether ester carbonate polyol                used for polyurethane foam and polyurethane block                foam. Can also be used for washing and cleaning                agent formulation, drilling fluid, fuel additive,                ionic and non-ionic surfactant, lubricant, process                chemicals for paper or textile production, and                cosmetic formulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and enables                production of polyether ester carbonate polyol                having high molecular weight of 400-10000 g/mol,                preferably 500-6000 g/mol, functionality of at                least 1, preferably 2-4, improved hydroxyl number,                and low viscosity, and utilizes the excess of                carbon dioxide based on the calculated amount of                built-in carbon dioxide in polyether ester                carbonate polyol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Lactide is of formula                  (I), preferably 1,4-dioxane-2,5-dione,                  (S,S)-3,6-dimethyl-1,4-dioxane-2,5-dione,                  (R,R)-3,6-dimethyl-1,4-dioxane-2,5-dione,                  meso-3,6-dimethyl-1,4-dioxane-2,5-dione,                  3-methyl-1,4-dioxane-2,5-dione,                  3-hexyl-6-methyl-1,4-dioxane-2,5-dione, and                  3,6-di(but-3-en-1-yl)-1,4-dioxane-2, 5-dione. The                  suspending agent is chosen from                  4-methyl-2-oxo-1,3-dioxolane and                  1,3-dioxolan-2-one.R1-R4 = H, linear or branched 1-22C alkyl                  radical optionally containing heteroatoms,                  linear, branched, mono-or-polyunsaturated 1-22C                  alkenyl optionally containing heteroatoms, mono-                  or polysubstituted 6-18C aryl, or optionally                  saturated 4-7 membered ring or polycyclyl                  optionally containing heteroatoms and/or ether                  groups;andn,o = 1 or more, preferably 1-4.(i) When n is more than 1, R1 and R2 are                  repeating units, (ii) When o is more than 1, R3                  and R4 are repeating units.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The step (a) is performed                  in the absence of the lactide. In the step (b),                  the starter substance, alkylene oxide, and                  lactide are continuously metered into the reactor                  in the presence of carbon dioxide, the metering                  of the hydrogen-functional starter substances is                  ended before the addition of alkylene oxide                  and/or lactide, hydrogen-functional starter                  substance, alkylene oxide, lactide, and double                  metal cyanide catalyst are continuously metered                  into the reactor and the obtained reaction                  mixture is continuously removed from the reactor.                  The method is performed in which the double metal                  cyanide catalyst suspended in the                  hydrogen-functional starter substance is added                  continuously. The method involves continuously                  removing the reaction mixture, and transferring                  mixture comprising 0.05-10 wt.% alkylene oxide to                  a post-reactor, and performing post-reaction, in                  which the content of free alkylene oxide is                  reduced to less than 0.05 wt.% in the reaction                  mixture. Preferred Composition: The method is                  performed using 5-40 wt.%, preferably 10-30 wt.%                  lactide based on the total amount of alkylene                  dioxide used. Preferred Components: The                  hydrogen-functional starter substance is chosen                  from alcohol, amine, thiol, amino alcohol,                  thioalcohol, hydroxy ester, polyether polyol,                  polyester polyol, polyester ether polyol,                  polycarbonate polyol, polyether carbonate polyol,                  polyethylene imine, polyetheramine,                  polytetrahydrofuran, polyacrylate oil thiol,                  castor oil, mono- or diglyceride of ricinoleic                  acid, monoglycerides of fatty acids, chemically                  modified mono-, di-, and/or triglycerides of                  fatty acids and 1-24C alkyl fatty acid esters                  containing hydroxyl groups per molecule, ethylene                  glycol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol,                  1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol,                  2-methylpropane-1,3-diol, neopentyl glycol,                  1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, glycerin,                  trimethylolpropane, di- and trifunctional                  polyether polyol in which the polyether polyol,                  propylene oxide, di-, or tri- hydrogen-functional                  starter substance has molecular weight of 62-4500                  g/mol, and functionality of 2-3.

AU LIU H
   BEI P
   YAO H
   GAO Y
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 2019740574
AB    NOVELTY - A graft-type ionic liquid polyimide film comprises substituted isoindole-1,3-dione compound.
   USE - Graft-type ionic liquid polyimide film for separating a mixed gas system of carbon dioxide/nitrogen, carbon dioxide/methane, carbon dioxide/oxygen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graft-type ionic liquid polyimide film improves the selectivity of carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A graft-type ionic liquid polyimide film comprises substituted isoindole-1,3-dione compound of formula (I).
   n = 100-200;
   R1 = methylimidazole, butylimidazole or octyl imidazole; and
   R2 = benzene moiety of formula (a) or biphenyl moieties of formulae (b-f).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing a graft-type ionic liquid polyimide film, which involves adding an aminated functional ionic liquid to a casting solution formed of polyimide, heating and stirring in a water bath, and grafting the aminated functional ionic liquid to the polymer main chain in the form of a side group through the characteristic structure of the Schiff base to obtain an aminated casting liquid, blade-coating the aminated casting liquid into a film, using solvent evaporation method to obtain modified film, drying and curing the film, after freezing, and peeling off the film to obtain a graft type ionic liquid polyimide film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The amination functional ionic liquid is prepared by heating N-methylimidazole, N-butylimidazole, and N-octyl imidazole with bromoethylamine hydrobromide, refluxing with ethanol, after drying to obtain an intermediate, adding potassium hydroxide to remove potassium bromide, carrying out ion exchange reaction with potassium hexafluorophosphate and ethanol is dissolved, heating in a water bath to remove ethanol to obtain an aminated functional ionic liquid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amination functional ionic liquid is 1-(aminoethyl)-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate, 1-(aminoethyl)-3-butylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate, 1-(aminoethyl)-3-octyl imidazolium hexafluorophosphate.

TI Electrocatalyzed carbon dioxide reduction            electrode preparation involves mixing cobalt nitrate            hexahydrate and tetrakis carboxyphenyl porphyrin in            dimethylformamide under nitrogen protection.
AU GAO Y
   LU Z
   CHEN X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201895431V
AB    NOVELTY - Electrocatalyzed carbon dioxide reduction                electrode preparation involves mixing cobalt                nitrate hexahydrate and                tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl)porphyrin in a ratio of                1:1 in dimethylformamide under nitrogen protection.                The reaction mixture is cooled at room temperature,                and then mixed with zinc nitrate hexahydrate at 80                degrees C. The reaction mixture is cooled at room                temperature, and then centrifuged and washed to                obtain a red solid. The carbon paper is washed with                concentrated nitric acid, ethanol and ultrapure                water to remove organic matter and metal impurities                on the surface of the carbon paper. 2.5 mg obtained                red solid is added to the mixed solution with                stirring for 30 minutes to obtain uniform                dispersion. 0.1 ml dispersion is uniformly dropped                onto carbon paper having an active area of 1 cm2,                and then dried overnight in air to obtain an                electrode. The mixed solution is ethanol or                polyethylene.
   USE - Method for preparing electrocatalyzed carbon                dioxide reduction electrode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the electrocatalyzed                carbon dioxide reduction electrode in a simple,                cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with                excellent catalytic activity in electrocatalytic                carbon dioxide reduction system with 0.5 M cesium                hydrogen carbonate aqueous solution as                electrolyte.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural                diagram of the full battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The obtained electrode                  is applied to carbon monoxide electrocatalytic of                  carbon monoxide.

PT P
PN CN108422125-A
TI Carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire for welding steel comprises carbon, manganese, silicon, nickel, chromium, molybdenum, vanadium, lanthanum, cerium, tantalum, sulfur, phosphorus, and rest is iron and unavoidable impurities.
AU LIU Y
   YE D
   LI T
   ZHENG L
   LIU X
AE UNIV GUILIN AEROSPACE TECHNOLOGY (UGAT-C)
GA 201866774C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire comprises 0.01-0.05 wt.% carbon, 0.50-1.80 wt.% manganese, 0.40-0.80 wt.% silicon, 0.50-2.0 wt.% nickel, 0.10-0.40 wt.% chromium, 0.1-0.5 wt.% molybdenum, 0.1-0.5 wt.% vanadium, 0.01-0.20 wt.% lanthanum, 0.01-0.20 wt.% cerium, 0.01-0.20 wt.% tantalum, sulfur is less than or equal to 0.005 wt.%, phosphorus is less than or equal to 0.01 wt.%, and the rest is iron and unavoidable impurities and the components quality percentage sum of the components is 100%.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire for welding steel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire for welding steel has stable electric arc, welding seam structure has excellent wear resistance, corrosion resistance, toughness, and hardness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire, which involves subjecting the drawn wire to surface treatment, cleaning the welding wire ultrasonically, performing copper plating, and finally performing passivation polishing to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The carbon dioxide gas shielded welding wire preferably comprises 0.02-0.04 wt.% carbon, 1.0-1.50 wt.% manganese, 0.50-0.60 wt.% silicon, 1.0-1.5 wt.% nickel, 0.20-0.30 wt.% chromium, 0.2-0.4 wt.% molybdenum, 0.2-0.4 wt.% vanadium, 0.1-0.15 wt.% lanthanum, 0.05-0.1 wt.% cerium, 0.01-0.20 wt.% tantalum, sulfur is less than or equal to 0.005 wt.%, phosphorus is less than or equal to 0.01 wt.%, and the rest is iron and unavoidable impurities and the components quality percentage sum of the components is 100%. The cleaning agent comprises 3-5g/L sodium hydroxide, 1-3g/L sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, 0.5-1.0g/L octyl phenol polyoxyethylene ether, 5.0-10g/L sodium gluconate, 0.2-0.5g/L alkyl polyoxyethylene sulfate, and 25-30g/L sodium carbonate. The copper plating solution comprises 85-100g/L copper sulfate, 40-80g/L sulfuric acid, 10-20g/L triethanolamine, 5-15g/L trisodium citrate, 4-15g/L thiourea, 4-15g/L potassium ferrocyanide, 1-5g/L sodium hypophosphite, and 4-15g/L sodium hydroxide. The passivation polishing liquid comprises 20-30g/L methanol, 5-10g/L sulfuric acid, 2-5g/L hydrofluoric acid, 5-10g/L hexamethylenetetramine, 0.5-2g/L magnesium fluoride, 0.5-2g/L acetic anhydride, 0.1-1g/L dodecyldimethylammonium chloride, and 0.1-1g/L cyclohexanol. Preferred Processes: The method for performing ultrasonic cleaning involves placing the welding wire in an ultrasonic cleaning machine, providing the ultrasonic cleaning machine is provided with an aqueous detergent solution, where the aqueous detergent solution has a temperature of 50-55 degrees C, the ultrasonic cleaner has a sound frequency of 0.5-1 kilo hertz, and the cleaning time is 40-50 seconds. Preferred Condition: The copper plating is formed by washing and drying the ultrasonically cleaned wire and then placing it in a plating solution for copper plating. The passivating polishing is performed after the copper-plated welding wire is washed with water and placed in a passivating polishing liquid.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); M12 (Chemical cleaning and degreasing - including cleaning and pickling.); M14 (Other chemical surface treatments - including etching, brightening, forming non-metallic layers, passivation, cathodic protection and corrosion inhibitors, but not processes specifically for semiconductor production (C23F, C25). This Section also covers multistage processes.); M27 (Ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).); ; P55 (Soldering, welding metal (B23K).)

AU CHENG J
   ZHAO X
   JIANG J
   ZHANG Z
   GUAN R
   ZHAO K
   DING Q
   SU W
   ZHANG H
AE HENAN LINGRUI PHARM CO LTD (FUXN-C)
   WUHAN JINGSHICHENG PHOTOELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201857915E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves converting optical signal detected by probe of radio frequency carbon dioxide laser into a signal recognizable by control card through photoelectric converter. The input duty cycle signal pulse width modulation (PWM) identified by the control card is used to adjust the radio frequency (RF) power source to control the power of the laser. The reflected light needle quickly sweeps the light beam emitted by the laser to reflect the light beam to the energy, under the protection of nitrogen. The energy of the reflected power can be calculated by the photoelectric converter connected to the probe, in the measuring probe and then converted into the energy of the entire beam. The size of the input signal can be adjusted by setting the difference between the pulse width modulation signal and the measured actual laser energy. The adjustment of the input signal can complete the stability control of the power output.
   USE - Method for stabilizing carbon dioxide laser.
   ADVANTAGE - The output power stability of the radio frequency carbon dioxide laser is greatly improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a method for stabilizing carbon dioxide laser. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN107790142-A; CN107790142-B
TI Cobalt hydroxide/tin niobate composite material comprises tin niobate having single layer nano material structure, and cobalt hydroxide having particle size of specified range and uniformly distributed on each layer of tin niobate.
AU LIANG S
   WANG Z
   CHEN W
   WEI Z
   ZHU S
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2018245270
AB    NOVELTY - A cobalt hydroxide/tin niobate composite material comprises cobalt hydroxide having particle size of 0.5-5 nm, and tin niobate having single layer nano material structure. The cobalt hydroxide is uniformly distributed on each layer of tin niobate.
   USE - Cobalt hydroxide/tin niobate composite material used for constructing efficient catalytic conversion of photocatalytic reduction carbon dioxide system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and convenient, and has low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the cobalt hydroxide/tin niobate composite material comprising using coordination-electrostatic absorption and self-assembly strategy, using cobalt nitrate as precursor, and modifying in situ formed cobalt hydroxide on tin niobate nanosheet composites under alkaline condition; and
   (2) constructing efficient catalytic conversion of photocatalytic reduction carbon dioxide system comprising adding reactor with 10 mg cobalt hydroxide/tin niobate composite material, 0.5-10 mL triethanolamine, water and acetonitrile mixed solution with volume ratio of 1:2:3 and 0.5-5 mg dichloropyridine ruthenium, introducing carbon dioxide to remove other gas solution, maintaining carbon dioxide partial pressure at 1 bar, using visible light photo-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, and offline detecting by gas chromatography.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The composite material is prepared by weighing 3 g niobic acid and tin chloride mixed sample with molar ratio of 1:1, adding to 70 ml deionized water, hydrothermal reacting at 150-220 degrees C for 48 hours, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain layered tin niobate, ultrasonically processing 2 g sample in 100 mL water and 1 g methyl pyrrolidone mixed solution for 6 hours, centrifuging at5 speed of 4000 revolutions/minute (rpm) to remove non-peeling layered tin niobate to obtain single lamella tin niobate uniformly dispersed suspension, placing 1 g suspension in beaker, adding 0-0.5 g polyvinyl pyrrolidone and 0-0.5 g methyl pyrrolidone, uniformly stirring and mixing, adding 200-500 mu L cobalt nitrate solution, sealing, stirring for 1 hour, dropwise adding sodium hydroxide solution, sealing and stirring, transferring into 100 mL PTFE reaction kettle, reacting at 100-200 degrees C for 5-12 hours, naturally cooling, taking out sample, centrifugally washing, and fully drying.

TI Environment-friendly flameless combustion            greenhouse heating carbon-increasing and            water-replenishing system for plant greenhouse, has            methanol catalytic device for generating carbon            dioxide, water, heat, carbon dioxide and water.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a plant                greenhouse comprising claim-friendly flameless                combustion greenhouse heating carbon-increasing and                water replenishing system.

PT P
PN CN112791707-A; CN112791707-B
TI Preparing nano carbon dioxide capture agent useful as fertilizer or in fertilizer for enhancing plant photosynthesis, comprises preparing organic modified mineral soil and modified chitosan solution and chitosan and graphene oxide powder.
AU SU B
   SU W
AE BEIJING JINXIU NEW TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202156836N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano carbon dioxide capture agent comprises e.g. (i) dissolving mineral soil and cationic surfactant respectively in the de-ionized water, adding it into reaction kettle, heating, stirring and adjusting pH to obtain organic modified mineral soil solution; (ii) dissolving the chitosan in acetic acid, adding into reaction kettle, stirring, dissolving, dropping cationic surfactant to dissolve and adjusting pH to obtain the modified chitosan solution; (iii) dropping modified chitosan solution under the stirring condition into the organic modified mineral soil solution, stirring, homogenizing, dispersing ultrasonically, adjusting the pH value, filtering, drying and grinding to nano level; and (iv) adding the graphene oxide into de-ionized water, stirring and dispersing, suction filtering, adding into nano level organic modified mineral soil loaded modified chitosan nano-powder into graphene oxide dispersion liquid, reacting, filtering, drying and grinding to nano-grade powder.
   USE - The nano carbon dioxide capture agent is useful as fertilizer or in fertilizer for enhancing plant photosynthesis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prepares capture agent with high photocatalytic effect which captures carbon dioxide in the polymerized air and enriches the leaf stems for crops to absorb and use thus enhances photosynthesis, inhibits photorespiration at night, synthesizes chlorophyll for crop growth, accumulates the three elements of carbon, hydrogen and oxygen necessary for crop growth and absorbs, synthesizes and transforms organic components e.g. nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium in the soil, promotes the gestation, growth and maturity of crops to increase production and income and can be used for both facility crops and field crops.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano carbon dioxide capture agent comprises (i) dissolving the mineral soil and cationic surfactant respectively in the de-ionized water, adding it into the reaction kettle, heating, stirring and adjusting pH to obtain organic modified mineral soil solution, where the mass ratio of the mineral soil and cationic surfactant is 3:1-1:1; (ii) dissolving the chitosan in acetic acid, adding into the reaction kettle, stirring, dissolving, dropping cationic surfactant to dissolve completely and adjusting pH to obtain the modified chitosan solution, the mass ratio of the chitosan and the cationic surfactant is 9: 1-11:1; (iii) dropping the modified chitosan solution under the stirring condition into the organic modified mineral soil solution, stirring, homogenizing, dispersing ultrasonically, adjusting the pH value to obtain organic modified mineral soil load modified chitosan solution, filtering, drying and grinding to nano level, where the mass ratio of the modified chitosan solution and the organic modified mineral soil solution is 0.05-0.1; (iv) adding the graphene oxide into de-ionized water, stirring and dispersing to obtain graphene oxide dispersion liquid, suction filtering the organic modified mineral soil loaded modified chitosan solution to the nano level, adding the obtained organic modified mineral soil loaded modified chitosan nano-powder into the graphene oxide dispersion liquid, reacting in the reactor, filtering, drying and grinding to nano-grade powder to obtain organic modified mineral soil load modified chitosan and graphene oxide powder, where the mass ratio of the organic modified mineral soil-supported modified chitosan nano powder to the graphene oxide is 8:1-15:1; and (v) dispersing the hydrotalcite in de-ionized water to dissolve fully, adding nano-grade powder slowly, adjusting pH to precipitate in the mixed solution, stirring magnetically, homogenizing and dispersing to obtain nano carbon dioxide capture at mass ratio of the nano-powder to the hydrotalcite is 2:1-4:1
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (v), the hydrotalcite co-precipitation method comprises mixing N2CO3, potassium carbonate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate are 3.5:0.5:1.2:1, dispersing uniformly, dissolving in deionized water at a mixture ratio of 100 g/liter and stirring to dissolve fully. The mineral soil comprises at least one of rare earth, diatomite, attapulgite, bentonite, kaolin or montmorillonite, and the fineness reaches nano level, the concentration of the mineral soil and the cationic surfactant respectively dissolved in deionized water is 10-15 g/liter and the stirring speed in the reactor is 400-600 revolutions/minute at 60-80 degrees C, heating and stirring for 1-3 hours. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the method comprising adding the modified chitosan solution, stirring at 400-600 revolutions/minute, adding drop wise into the organic modified mineral soil solution at 50-80 degrees C, stirring for 2 hours, introducing the homogenizer, homogenizing, dispersing for 6-15 minutes at 12000-16000 revolutions/minute, introducing ultrasonic for 2-8 minutes, adjusting the pH to 7.5-8.2 to obtain the organically modified mineral soil loaded modified chitosan solution, suction filtering, drying at 80-120 degrees C for 1-2 hours, introducing a nano mill to grind to nano level or, in the step (iv), the mass concentration of the graphene oxide dispersed in deionized water is 1-2 g/liter, carrying out ultrasonic dispersion for 1.5-2.5 hours, in the microwave reactor, the microwave power is 800-1000 w power, and the microwave radiation time is 15-25 minutes, the drying is carried out for 80-120 degrees C for 1-2 hours and the grinding nano-grinding machine grinding for 1-2 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cationic surfactant comprises at least one of dodecyltrimethylammonium bromide (DTAB), polydimethyldiallylammonium chloride (PDMDAAC), dodecyl dimethyl benzyl ammonium chloride, cetyl trimethyl ammonium chloride (CTAC), cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB), tetradecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide, octadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide (CTMAB) or octadecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride (OTAC). In steps (i)-(iv), the pH adjusting agent for adjusting the pH is an organic amine or an organic alcohol amine and in the step (v), the pH adjusting agent comprises potassium carbonate or potassium hydroxide. The organic amine or organic alcohol amine comprises at least one of diethylene triamine (DETA), triethylenetetramine (TETA), tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), pentaethylene hexamine (PEHA), N-methyl-1,3-propanediamine (MAPA), N,N-dimethylformamide, 2-picolylamine, 3-picolylamine, 4-picolylamine, N'-(pyridine-4-ylmethyl)ethane-1, 2-diamine, AMP-95 (RTM: 2-Amino-2-methyl-1-propanol), ethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA), triethanolamine (TEA), tertiary amine N-methyl glycol amine (MDEA), N,N-Diethylethanolamine (DEEA) or N-ethylethanolamine. In the step (ii), the mass concentration of the acetic acid is 3-8 wt.%, and the mass concentration of the chitosan dissolved in the acetic acid is 50-200 g/liter and the stirring speed in the reaction kettle is 400-600 revolutions/minute, heating and stirring at 50-80 degrees C to completely dissolve. Preferred Method: The method comprises a powdering, transferring the nano carbon dioxide capture agent mixed solution obtained in step (v) to a jacketed reactor, carrying out hydrothermal reaction at 100-120 degrees C for 30-40 hours, washing thoroughly with absolute ethanol and deionization, filtering with suction, freeze-drying and introducing the nano-grinding machine to grind for 1-2 hours to obtain powdery nano-composite.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN110898776-A
TI Preparing basic zinc carbonate from aqueous ammonia, involves adding zinc sulfate powder and water, introducing carbon dioxide gas, discharging solid-to-liquid mixture, filtering and returning filter residue, drying cleaned filter residue.
AU ZHANG X
   LIU Y
AE ANHUI HANSAN JINHUA ZINC OXIDE PLANT (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202024247C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing basic zinc carbonate from aqueous ammonia, involves (a) adding zinc sulfate powder and water to reaction kettle (5) with stirring paddle, drying and supplementing heating air at top of the reaction kettle, (b) continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas in gas reaction chamber (2), (c) passing carbon dioxide gas under the liquid level of obtained ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution, when the liquid level of ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution generated by reaction, passing ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution to the reaction kettle using a overflow pipe, (d) detecting concentration of each substance in the reaction solution at discharge port at bottom end of the reaction kettle, and maintaining the liquid temperature, (e) discharging solid-to-liquid mixture from discharging opening at bottom of the reaction kettle, filtering and returning the filter residue to reaction kettle through cleaning, drying and grinding cleaned filter residue to obtain powder.
   USE - Method for preparing basic zinc carbonate from aqueous ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recovers carbon dioxide tail gas generated by reaction of basic zinc carbonate, and promotes the reaction speed and degree of reaction of ammonium bicarbonate and zinc sulfate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing basic zinc carbonate from aqueous ammonia, involves (a) adding zinc sulfate powder and water to reaction kettle with stirring paddle, when the liquid level reaches 1/2 position of reaction kettle, arranging an air draft tube at top of the reaction kettle, generating gas using reaction pumped away, drying and supplementing heating air at top of the reaction kettle, (b) continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas in gas reaction chamber, when the pressure in gas reaction chamber reaches 0.5-1.2 MPa, simultaneously spraying water mist and ammonia gas using a spray pipe, and reacting at room temperature for 5-10 minutes to obtain an ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution, (c) passing carbon dioxide gas under the liquid level of obtained ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution, when the liquid level of ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution generated by reaction exceeds half the gas reaction chamber, passing ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution to the reaction kettle using a overflow pipe, stirring the paddle of reaction kettle and air heater at a speed of 200-230 rpm, controlling air inlet temperature at 60-80 degrees C, reacting for 15-30 minutes, and generating the gas generated by reaction, which is pumped back into the gas reaction chamber, (d) detecting concentration of each substance in the reaction solution at discharge port at bottom end of the reaction kettle, when the concentration of zinc ions is lower than 100 g/l, continuously adding zinc sulfate powder at top end, when the liquid level of reaction kettle is lower than 1/2, continuously adding water at the top end, when the liquid temperature in the reaction kettle is not 40-50 degrees C, the air inlet temperature of air is increased or decreased using air heater, and maintaining the liquid temperature at 40-50 degrees C, (e) discharging solid-to-liquid mixture from discharging opening at bottom of the reaction kettle when the reaction exceeds 1 hour, filtering and returning the filter residue to reaction kettle through cleaning, drying and grinding cleaned filter residue to obtain powder of finished basic zinc carbonate, where the powder is white fine powder having whiteness of more than 75%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for production of basic zinc carbonate from aqueous ammonia.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for production of basic zinc carbonate.
   Liquid reactor (1)
   Gas reaction chamber (2)
   Motor (3)
   Driving shaft (4)
   Reaction kettle (5)

PN CN111396028-A
TI Performing an equivalent quantitative assessment method for gas drainage compliance based on liquid carbon dioxide fracturing enhancement involves investigating average content of carbon dioxide and methane in coal seams in test area.
AU WEN H
   CHENG X
   FAN S
   GUO H
   JIN Y
   FEI J
   YU Z
   GUO J
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV XIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYXS-C)
GA 202068837J
AB    NOVELTY - Performing an equivalent quantitative assessment method for gas drainage compliance based on liquid carbon dioxide (CO2) fracturing enhancement and phase change displacement and replacement involves investigating average content of CO2 and methane (CH4) in the coal seams in test area which are denoted as WCH4 and WCO2 respectively. The coal samples are collected from injection holes and extraction holes during drilling process, and adsorption and desorption experiments and industrial analysis are conducted in laboratory to obtain adsorption constants a and b values, coal ash Ad and coal moisture Mad, where non-desorbable gas volume Wb is determined in accordance with the Interim Regulations on Compliance with Coal Mine Gas Drainage. The volume V1 of pressure-injected liquid CO2 is tested statistically, and volume Vq is calculate after gasification according to temperature and pressure characteristics of the stored liquid CO2.
   USE - Method for performing an equivalent quantitative assessment method for gas drainage compliance based on liquid carbon dioxide (CO2) fracturing enhancement and phase change displacement and replacement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform an equivalent quantitative assessment method for gas drainage compliance based on liquid carbon dioxide fracturing enhancement and phase change displacement and replacement, which realizes quantitative evaluation after injecting liquid CO2, improves gas drainage and reduces mine gas disaster.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing an equivalent quantitative assessment method for gas drainage compliance based on liquid carbon dioxide (CO2) fracturing enhancement and phase change displacement and replacement involves investigating average content of CO2 and methane (CH4) in the coal seams in test area which are denoted as WCH4 and WCO2 respectively. The coal samples are collected from injection holes and extraction holes during drilling process, and adsorption and desorption experiments and industrial analysis are conducted in laboratory to obtain adsorption constants a and b values, coal ash Ad and coal moisture Mad, where non-desorbable gas volume Wb is determined in accordance with the Interim Regulations on Compliance with Coal Mine Gas Drainage. The volume V1 of pressure-injected liquid CO2 is tested statistically, and volume Vq is calculate after gasification according to temperature and pressure characteristics of the stored liquid CO2. The total CH4 and CO2 drainage volume of the drainage holes are counted and calculated in time (t) including average mixed gas flow Qih of the drainage holes per day in the drainage holes, borehole CH4 concentration CiCH4 is drained every day and borehole CO2 concentration CiCO2 is drained every day. The amount of CH4 drilled per day is ViCH4, and amount of CO2 drilled per day is ViCO2. The total CH4 and CO2 drainage volume of the drainage holes in time t are recorded as VCH4 and VCO2 respectively. The method for evaluating the equivalent radius of liquid CO2 fracturing and phase change drive gas drainage to meet the standard is proposed on basis of desorbable gas content, where coal seam is derived on basis of liquid CO2 fracturing and phase change. The perforation hole is taken as an example assuming that injection hole is center of circle, and equivalent radius of standard is Ri, and then coal volume Vc of the standard area is calculated. The indicators that desorbable gas volume reach under the corresponding production conditions are obtained according to the daily volume of mine working face, and through Interim Regulations on Coal Mine Gas Drainage Standards, and then combined equations are used to obtain the standard equivalent radius. The coal body is in standard range, and then displacement displacement ratio and gas displacement efficiency mu are evaluated, and corresponding displacement displacement ratio and gas displacement efficiency are obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves calculating the non-desorbable gas amount Wb by an equation, where Wb is non-desorbable gas amount of coal under standard atmospheric pressure, where a is gas adsorption constant of coal, limit adsorption capacity of coal at the test temperature, b is gas adsorption constant of coal (MPa), Ad is ash content, Mad is moisture of coal, and gamma is bulk density of coal. The method involves calculating the volume of CO2 entering the injection hole by an equation. The method involves calculating daily CH4 volume of drainage hole, calculating the daily CO2 volume of the drainage hole, and calculating the total CH4 and CO2 drainage volume of drainage hole in the time t, which are recorded separately as VCH4 and VCO2. The method involves deriving remaining desorbable gas content of coal seam based on liquid CO2 fracturing and phase change, where Vf is amount of gas discharged by air in test area during extraction time t, calculating coal volume in the area meeting the standard by an equation, where HC is thickness of coal seam, L is distance between exit of borehole casing and bottom of the hole, and calculating equivalent radius of compliance. The method involves calculating displacement replacement ratio in target area, and calculating gas displacement efficiency mu in target area.
DC Q49 (Mining (E21)); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC Q49-A; Q49-V28; Q49-V35; S03-F06B; T01-J03; T01-J04

AU INVENTOR U
AE CHIFENG ZHENHUAN GAS TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHIF-Non-standard)
GA 201882156K
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises washing tower (1), water washing pump (2), air bag (3), compressor (4), water dividing tank (5), purification column, purification column, condenser, freezer, purification tower and product tank, where the process comprises collecting carbon dioxide gas after defoaming from the beer fermenter, entering in washing tower, spraying water by washing pump, washing off impurities e.g. ethanol and foam, entering in airbag for storage, entering in compressor for pressurization, dividing by water tank, entering in purification column to remove hydrogen sulfide, ethyl acetate, fusel alcohol and residual ethanol, entering in purification column to remove methanol, formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, moisture and odor impurities, entering in condenser, liquefying refrigerant supplied by the refrigerator, entering in purification tower to remove non-condensable gases e.g. oxygen, methane, nitrogen, and carbon monoxide.
   USE - The device is useful for purifying carbon dioxide in a brewery by using a gas purification technique (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes more than 99.99% purity of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises washing tower (1), water washing pump (2), air bag (3), compressor (4), water dividing tank (5), purification column, purification column, condenser, freezer, purification tower and product tank, where the process comprises collecting carbon dioxide gas after defoaming from the beer fermenter, entering in washing tower, spraying water by washing pump, washing off impurities e.g. ethanol and foam, entering in airbag for storage, entering in compressor for pressurization, dividing by water tank, entering in purification column to remove hydrogen sulfide, ethyl acetate, fusel alcohol and residual ethanol, entering in purification column to remove methanol, formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, moisture and odor impurities, entering in condenser, liquefying refrigerant supplied by the refrigerator, entering in purification tower to remove non-condensable gases e.g. oxygen, methane, nitrogen, and carbon monoxide, extracting liquid carbon dioxide from the bottom of the purification tower having greater than or equal to 99.9% purity, when meets national food additive grade quality standard, then storing in the product tank for filling beer or loading and unloading bottles, where the gas inlet at the lower end of the washing tower is directly connected to the raw material gas pipe, the upper end outlet of the washing tower is connected to the air bag by a pipe, the outlet of the air bag is connected to the inlet of the compressor, the outlet of the compressor is connected to the inlet of the water dividing tank, the upper outlet of the water separator tank is connected to the lower inlet of the purification column, the upper end outlet of the purification column is connected to the upper end of the purification column, the lower end of the purification column is connected to the inlet of the condenser, the outlet of the condenser is connected to the intermediate inlet of the purification column, the lower end outlet of the purification tower is connected to the inlet of the product tank, the outlet of the product tank is connected to the pipeline of the user, the upper end of the purification tower is connected to the pipeline of the purification column regeneration system, the piping of the chiller system is connected to the conduit associated with the condenser.

   Water washing pump (2)
