TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The water washing tower is a packed tower. The washing liquid is water or potassium permanganate solution having 1-10% concentration, which is sent to the tower for recycling by centrifugal pump, and is periodically discharged or recycled. The airbag is made up of non-metallic material e.g. nylon, polypropylene or tarpaulin. The airbag volume rises and contracts with the amount of gas entering and is set with drain port at bottom or airbag is made up of metal material e.g. carbon steel or stainless steel having fixed volume. The compressor is an oil-free, lubricated piston compressor of class 2-4 and is driven by the motor, the valve, piston ring and cylinder liner of the compressor are made up of stainless steel or other corrosion-resistant materials. The pressure of the compressed gas is 1.0-7.0 MPa. The purification column comprises two drums of the same small size with equal diameter or variable diameter. The upper part of drums is set with flanged head. The inlet and outlet port are set on drum. The bottom cover is set with inlet and outlet port. The solid hydrogen sulfide purifying agent, fuser purifying agent, ethyl acetate purifying agent and ethanol purifying agent prepared by granular activated carbon material are filled in drum. Each filler is separated by a screen. The bottom and upper parts are 100-400 cm long tubular gas inlet and outlet pipes with sieve holes. The bottom inlet and outlet pipes are transversely placed, and the outer surface of the pipe is wrapped with 10-80 meshes stainless steel sieve which prevents leakage of solid scavengers. The upper and lower inlet and outlet ports of the two drums are connected in parallel by two pipes and four valves. The inlet and exhaust ports are opened in the middle of the two valves. According to different impurity components in the gas source, the purifying agent in the purification column is one type or multiple layered filling. The purifying column is one set or multiple sets of series. The valve is manual, pneumatic or electric valve. The purification column comprises two drums of the same small size with equal diameter or variable diameter. The upper part of drums is set with flanged head. The inlet and outlet port are set on drum. The spherical or strip-shaped solid methanol purifying agent, formaldehyde purifying agent, acetaldehyde purifying agent and odor purifying agent prepared using alumina or molecular sieve is filled in the drum. Each purified agent is isolated by a screen. The bottom and upper parts are 100-400 cm long tubular gas inlet and outlet pipes with sieve holes. The bottom inlet and outlet pipes are transversely placed, and the outer surface of the pipe is wrapped with 10-80 meshes stainless steel sieve which prevents leakage of solid detergent. The upper and lower inlet and outlet ports of the two drums are connected in parallel by two pipes and four valves. The inlet and exhaust ports are opened in the middle of the two valves. The purifying agent in the purification column is one type or multiple layered filling. The purifying column is one set or multiple sets of series. The valve is manual, pneumatic or electric valve. The freezer system is made up of a chiller, condenser, reservoir, pressure regulating valve, sump and piping. The main engine of the refrigerator is piston or screw type, which is driven by the motor. The refrigerant is liquid ammonia, freon or propylene. The condenser is a water-cooled or evaporative condensation type of the column. The pressure after compression of the refrigerant gas is 1.0-3.0 MPa. The temperature of the refrigerant condensed into a liquid at -10 to -50 degrees C. The purification tower is packed tower, which is provided with a corrugated plate or a mesh-type high-efficiency structured packing, and a filler supporting plate is arranged under the packing. There is a liquid distributor on top and a feed distributor in the middle. The bottom of the purification tower is set with reboiler, the heat is supplied by the hot gas at the outlet of the refrigerator, or by process gas dried behind the purification system. The top of the purification tower is set with a full condenser. The amount of cooling is provided by the liquid refrigerant of the liquid reservoir after being depressurized. The reboiler and full condenser of the purification tower re integrally installed. The full condenser is vertical tube type, which is directly placed on the top of the purification tower. The reboiler is connected to the bottom of the purification tower, and the gas heating tube is coil or a tube. There is an inlet and outlet pipe at the top of the purification tower. The purification tower system can also omitted non-condensable gases e.g. nitrogen, oxygen, and carbon monoxide which are discharged from the top of the product tank. The product tank is vertical or horizontal cylindrical shape with a head, or spherical shape. The light component gas discharged from the top of the purification tower and product tank and is heated by heat exchanger as regeneration gas of the purification column. The extracted impurity gas is taken out and discharged into the gas main pipe. The heat exchanger is steam, hot oil heat exchanger or electric heater.

AE CHINA DATANG CORP SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES (CDAT-C)

GA 2022A3092U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an absorption tower (2), a                two-phase absorbent system (6), a forward osmosis                device (8), a regeneration tower (9) and MVR heat                pump system. The forward osmosis device is                connected with the regeneration tower. The                regeneration tower is connected with a reboiled                (12). The MVR heat pump system comprises a flash                tank (10) and a compressor (11). The lean liquid                outlet of the regeneration tower is connected with                the flash tank. The flash tank is connected with                the high temperature and high pressure gas inlet of                the regeneration tower through the compressor. The                barren liquid outlet at the bottom of the flash                evaporation tank is connected with the lean/rich                liquid heat exchanger.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide based on                two-phase absorbent rich liquid concentrated phase                desorption in coal-fired power plant and for carbon                capture and use and sealing (CCUS) technical                research in power industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide capture system reduces                absorbent regeneration energy consumption, improves                regeneration heat efficiency angle, and reduces the                energy consumption of the flue gas CO2 capture                process. The system reduces the content of the                solvent water after concentrating, improves the CO2                balance partial pressure, and strengthens the rich                liquid regeneration mass transfer pushing force, so                as to reduce the latent heat of vaporization of                solvent water and evaporation sensible heat in the                regeneration process. At the same time, rich liquid                flow entering the regeneration tower is                correspondingly reduced, which can improve                regeneration tower inlet rich liquid temperature,                strengthening the waste heat recovery.

AB    NOVELTY - Chemical grouting solution comprises first                grouting liquid, second grouting liquid, and third                grouting liquid. The second grouting liquid comprises 100                pts. wt. water, 25-10 pts. wt. MgCl2, and                8x10-5-8x10-1 pts. wt. second catalyst. The third                grouting liquid comprises 100 pts. wt. water, 25-10                pts. wt. MgCl2, and 5x10-3-3x10-2 pts. wt. third                catalyst.
   USE - Chemical grouting solution that is used for                repairing carbon dioxide leakage in deep formation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical grouting solution is                eco-friendly, cost-effective and simple to operate,                can overcome the limitation of the sedimentation                kinetic of the acid-resistant magnesite particle,                and catalyze the rapid precipitation of the                acid-resistant magnesite particles in the carbon                dioxide leakage area of the deep formation,and it                has low viscosity and wide penetration range, which                enables it to enter into small pores and cracks and                become a more promising reactive grouting                fluid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a chemical grouting liquid for                repairing carbon dioxide leakage in deep formation,                which involves: (a) mixing uniformly MgCl2 and                water with specific weight proportion;(b)stirring                in a container until it is completely dissolved;(c)                adding first catalyst to the MgCl2                solution;(d)stirring until the particles are                completely dissolved to obtain first slurry, or,                mixing uniformly MgCl2 and water with specific                weight proportion;(d)stirring in a container until                it is completely dissolved, adding second catalyst                to the MgCl2 solution;(e)stirring until the                particles are completely dissolved to obtain a                second slurry, or mixing uniformly MgCl2 and water                with specific weight proportion;(f)stirring in a                container until it is completely dissolved, adding                third catalyst to the MgCl2 solution and dispersing                uniformly to obtain the third slurry.

   USE - The system is useful for brewing coffee (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: utilizes total submersion of the ground coffee directly below the filter that allows carbon dioxide to be withdrawn from the ground coffee and removed from the system without the ground coffee being exposed to air and water simultaneously, during the brew process, while the carbon dioxide gas is escaping the ground coffee; utilizes carbon dioxide that becomes trapped in coffee molecules during the bean roasting process, and this trapped carbon dioxide prevents ground coffee from becoming fully saturated with brew water, which prevents a full and balanced extraction of flavor compounds existing in the coffee molecules; and provides coffee that utilizes detrimental oxidation during the brewing process by removing a portion of the oxygen from a sealed brewing container through a one way valve via a vacuum, thus, the coffee is brewed within a vacuum, and the impacts of oxidation are reduced, which results in a more desirable taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for brewing coffee.

PN CN111650007-A
TI Online sampling and analysis method for carbon            dioxide, involves entering gas chromatography packed            column, passing detector to detect and record data,            performing multiple measurements again and taking            average value as final detection value.
AU ZHANG M
   LIU S
   HUANG M
   YANG X
   LIU J
AE YUNNAN ENERGY INST CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202094315R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves installing two sampling                valves in the discharge pipeline of the raw                material tank, desulfurization equipment,                dehydration equipment, super cooler equipment and                finished product tank equipment. The sampling valve                closer to the processing equipment is first opened                (S1), when sampling samples processed in different                processes. The sample flowing out of the sampling                valve enters the heater and undergoes (S2)                temperature-rising treatment, so that the liquid                becomes a normal temperature gas. The moisture of                the sample that becomes gas through an adsorber is                removed (S3). The sample with moisture removed                enters the detection equipment, the sample enters                the gas chromatography packed column for                separation, and the hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen,                methane, and carbon monoxide enter the molecular                sieve, and finally passes (S4) the detector to                detect and record the data. Multiple measurements                are performed again, and taken (S5) the average                value as the final detection value.
   USE - Online sampling and analysis method for carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The operation steps are simple and convenient,                the analysis time is short, and the normal                operation of the processing equipment is not                affected, and the impurity content of each                processing equipment after processing is detected.                The method is convenient for online real-time                analysis of the impurity content of carbon dioxide,                which improves convenience, is convenient for                practical use, and improves work efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the online sampling and analysis method for carbon                dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
   Step for installing two sampling valves in                the discharge pipeline of the raw material tank,                desulfurization equipment, dehydration equipment,                super cooler equipment and finished product tank                equipment (S1)
   Step for entering sample flowing out of the                sampling valve and undergoing temperature-rising                treatment (S2)
   Step for removing moisture of the sample that                becomes gas through an adsorber (S3)
   Step for passing the detector to detect and                record the data (S4)
   Step for performing multiple measurements                again, and taking the average value as the final                detection value (S5)

TI Removing harmful substances from protective clothing of fire-fighters involves isolating used fire-fighting clothing in soluble laundry bag, pre-sterilizing, decontaminating, drying, disinfecting, ironing and repairing if damage is found.
AU CAO W
AE BEIJING YINGTELAI SCI & TECH CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020055126
AB    NOVELTY - Removing harmful substances from protective clothing of fire-fighters involves packing used fire-fighting clothing in soluble laundry bag immediately after fire-fighting rescue, sealing, isolating, classifying based on degree of pollution of hazardous substances, pre-sterilizing using disinfectants and stain removers, decontaminating outer fabrics, performing comprehensive decontamination and sterilization, drying, putting into dry cleaning device, decomposing and removing harmful substances in waterproof and breathable PTFE layer of clothing using liquid carbon dioxide as solvent by utilizing it's excellent dissolving ability and penetrability of non-polar substances in combination with mechanical action of washing tank, using finishing machine and finishing agent to waterproof clothing after three times of liquid carbon dioxide dry cleaning, disinfection, anti-oiling, anti-fouling and other three finishing process, ironing finished clothing and repairing if damage is found.
   USE - Removing harmful substances from protective clothing of fire-fighters.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively remove more than 99% of harmful substances in protective clothing of fire-fighters.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing harmful substances from protective clothing of firefighters involves packing used fire-fighting clothing in soluble laundry bag immediately after fire-fighting rescue, sealing, isolating, sending to washing center, classifying firefighter's protective clothing based on degree of pollution of hazardous substances, pre-sterilizing clothing using disinfectants and stain removers, decontaminating outer fabrics that are locally heavily polluted by soaking and washing, performing comprehensive decontamination and sterilization using water washing process and ozone automatic sterilization method, drying with automatic room temperature air dryer, putting into dry cleaning device, decomposing and removing harmful substances in waterproof and breathable PTFE layer of clothing using liquid carbon dioxide as solvent by utilizing it's excellent dissolving ability and penetrability of non-polar substances in combination with mechanical action of washing tank and dissolving action of additives, using finishing machine and finishing agent to waterproof clothing after three times of liquid carbon dioxide dry cleaning, disinfection, Anti-oiling, anti-fouling and other three finishing process, ironing finished clothing and repairing if damage is found.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ozone concentration in water in ozone automatic sterilization process is 0.3-2 mg/L, preferably 0.8-1.5 mg/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The disinfectant is p-chloro-m-xylenol and is used in an amount of 0.5-4 wt.% of the protective clothing, where the effective concentration is 0.05-0.16% (w/v). The amount of stain remover used is 0.5-4 wt.% of the weight of protective clothing. The stain remover preferably comprises 6-8 wt.% fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, 6-8 wt.% fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate, 6-8 wt.% sodium citrate, 4-5 wt.% propylene glycol, 0.6-0.8 wt.% biological enzyme and 100 wt.% deionized water. Preferred Apparatus: The dry cleaning device refers to a liquid carbon dioxide dry cleaning machine for dry cleaning fire protection clothing. Preferred Condition: When the liquid carbon dioxide dry cleaning machine is used to dry clean fire protection clothing, the temperature is set to 25-50 degrees C, preferably 30-40 degrees C, the pressure is set to 5-8 MPa, preferably 5-6 MPa, and the duration is set to 20-60 minutes, preferably 30-50 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The finishing agent comprises 60-80 wt.% fluorocarbon polymer finishing agent and balance deionized water. The amount of the finishing agent used is 0.5-4 wt.% of the protective clothing weight.

TI Assessing anti-inflammatory activity of active substances involves e.g. obtaining peritoneal exudate of peritoneal macrophage by administering to laboratory animal solution of lectin from Phytolacca americana, washed and suspended in medium.
AU PANTEA V
   CORETCHI I
   GHINDA S
   GUDUMAC V
   TAGADIUC E
AE UNIV MOLD MEDICAL & PHARM (UYML-Non-standard)
   CHIRIL DRAGANIUC INST (CHIR-Non-standard)
GA 201941395F
AB    NOVELTY - Assessing the anti-inflammatory activity of active substances, comprises: obtaining peritoneal exudate comprising peritoneal macrophages by intraperitoneally administering to laboratory animal solution of lectin from Phytolacca americana, washed and suspended in cell culture medium, comprising fetal bovine serum, gentamicin and fluconazole; transferring suspension to plate with wells and incubated in carbon dioxide incubator; removing non-adherent cells and adding each well in test samples with cell culture medium, comprising fetal bovine serum, gentamicin, fluconazole, lipopolysaccharide, ammonium metavanadate and test compounds in various dilutions, prepared control and comparative samples; and mixing samples and incubating in carbon dioxide incubator, mercury(II) chloride, vanadium chloride solution and Griss reagent transferred into wells of the photometric plate, mixed, after measured optical absorption, determined nitric oxide production inhibiting activity.
   USE - The method is useful for assessing the anti-inflammatory activity of active substances.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the anti-inflammatory activity of active substances that is assessed in relation to the comparative sample and the greater the nitric oxide production inhibiting activity, greater the anti-inflammatory activity of active substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Assessing the anti-inflammatory activity of active substances, comprises: obtaining peritoneal exudate comprising peritoneal macrophages by intraperitoneally administering to a laboratory animal a solution of lectin from Phytolacca americana, after 72 hours cells obtained, washed and suspended in a cell culture medium, additionally comprising fetal bovine serum, gentamicin and fluconazole; transferring a suspension to a plate with wells and incubated in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 2-4 hours; removing non-adherent cells and adding each well in the test samples with a cell culture medium, additionally comprising fetal bovine serum, gentamicin, fluconazole, lipopolysaccharide, ammonium metavanadate and test compounds in various dilutions, prepared the control and comparative samples; and mixing samples and incubating in the carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24-48 hours, after which supernatant, mercury(II) chloride, vanadium chloride solution and Griss reagent transferred into the wells of the photometric plate, mixed, left in the dark at 25 degrees C, for 30 minutes, after measured the optical absorption at a wavelength of 540 nm and 630 nm, determined the nitric oxide production inhibiting activity.

PN CN108727872-A
TI Preparation method of low-oil absorption nano-calcium carbonate for adhesive, involves calcining limestone, performing digestion process, coarse filtering, sieving, refining, carbonizing, performing surface treatment, separating and drying.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG S
   CHEN N
   SHAO X
   MA J
AE WUHU ZHUOYUE NANO NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201887237Q
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of low-oil absorption nano-calcium carbonate involves calcining limestone into calcium oxide and a kiln gas containing carbon dioxide, placing calcium oxide in a digesting machine, and adding water to digest to obtain calcium hydroxide emulsion, coarse filtering the calcium hydroxide emulsion, performing hydrocyclone separation and sieving and refining to obtain calcium hydroxide fine emulsion, mixing with sucrose crystal regulator, adding calcium hydroxide suspension to a carbonization reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, carbonizing, continuously spraying, carbonizing kiln gas after foam dust removal and purification, and grinding the carbonized slurry to obtain uniformly dispersed nano-calcium carbonate slurry, coating nano-calcium carbonate adhesive material with surface treatment agent, performing liquid phase coating process, filtering nano-calcium carbonate slurry, drying and depolymerizing, measuring, packing, and warehousing.
   USE - Preparation method of low-oil absorption nano-calcium carbonate for adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of low-oil absorption nano-calcium carbonate with reduced equipment investment, reduced energy consumption, increased viscosity, thixotropy, sag resistance, extrusion resistance and oil absorption value, by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of low-oil absorption nano-calcium carbonate involves (1) calcining limestone into calcium oxide and a kiln gas containing carbon dioxide through a lime kiln, (2) placing calcium oxide in a digesting machine, and adding water to digest to obtain calcium hydroxide emulsion, (3) coarse filtering the calcium hydroxide emulsion, performing hydrocyclone separation and sieving and refining to obtain calcium hydroxide fine emulsion, (4) mixing the calcium hydroxide fine emulsion with sucrose crystal regulator, adding the obtained calcium hydroxide suspension to a carbonization reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, carbonizing, continuously spraying, carbonizing the kiln gas after foam dust removal and purification, and grinding the carbonized slurry to obtain uniformly dispersed nano-calcium carbonate slurry, (5) coating the nano-calcium carbonate adhesive material with surface treatment agent, and carrying out liquid phase coating process, and (6) filtering the nano-calcium carbonate slurry, drying and depolymerizing to obtain low-oil absorption nano-calcium carbonate product, measuring, packing, and warehousing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (1), the obtained kiln gas containing carbon dioxide is further purified. The step (2) further comprises removing slag from the undigested limestone and removing the lime residue. After obtaining the fine emulsion of calcium hydroxide in the step (3), the fine emulsion of calcium hydroxide is poured into an aging tank for aging. The step (4) specifically involves adding calcium hydroxide fine emulsion with concentration of 8.5-9.5 wt.% to a reactor, adding the dissolved sucrose crystal regulator, carbonizing by introducing kiln gas containing 28 vol.% carbon dioxide, controlling the reaction temperature at 27-30 degrees C and the kiln gas flow rate at 0.7 m3/hour, carbonizing for 22 minutes, continuously passing the kiln gas at a flow rate of 1.4 m3/hour, carbonizing for 50 minutes, if the pH of the slurry falls below 7, attaining the carbonization end point, stopping carbonization, grinding the carbonized slurry using a vertical ball mill for 30 minutes. The step (5) adopts a process of synchronously performing strong shear emulsification dispersion and composite coating by dissolving lauric acid, polyethylene glycol and stearic acid in warm water at 90 degrees C, adding a liquid base to adjust the pH of the coating liquid to 12 to prepare coating emulsion, adding the coating emulsion to the nano-calcium carbonate slurry, starting high-shear emulsifier dispersing slurry, treating the solution with a constant temperature coating for 20 minutes for liquid phase coating. The step (6) specifically comprises transferring the nano-calcium carbonate slurry to a press through a pressure filter pump, dehydrating the slurry by heat filtration to form filter cake, drying at 100 degrees C, unloading by a press filter after being pressed, transferring to an extruder by the filter cake belt machine, extrusion molding to obtain low-oil absorption nano-calcium carbonate product, measuring and packaging.

TI Culturing microalgae in culturing solution while            gas containing carbon dioxide is supplied to culturing            solution, involves obtaining acquired value of            concentration of hydrogen carbonate ions in culturing            solution, adjusting temperature or pH of culturing            solution.
AU MACHIDA K
   SHIOZAKI S
   DOI M
   TAKANO F
   KINOSHITA S
   SHIOBARA N
   GOTO M
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2022C7853A
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing microalgae in culturing solution                while gas containing carbon dioxide is supplied to                culturing solution, involves obtaining an acquired                value of a concentration of hydrogen carbonate ions                in the culturing solution. In a case that the                acquired value does not reside within a set                concentration range that is set beforehand.                Temperature or a pH of the culturing solution is                adjusted, and by adjusting the concentration of                hydrogen carbonate ions in the culturing solution                to reside within the set concentration range.
   USE - Method for culturing microalgae in culturing                solution while gas containing carbon dioxide is                supplied to culturing solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables maintaining the                concentration of hydrogen carbonate ions in the                culturing solution within an appropriate range that                is suitable for culturing the microalgae.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                culturing device (10) configured to culture                microalgae in a culturing solution while a gas                containing carbon dioxide is supplied to the                culturing solution, which has a culturing tank (12)                configured to contain the culturing solution and                the microalgae, and to which the gas from a gas                supplying unit is supplied; an ion concentration                acquisition unit (54) configured to obtain an                acquired value of a concentration of hydrogen                carbonate ions in the culturing solution inside the                culturing tank; a temperature adjustment unit (22)                configured to adjust a temperature of the culturing                solution; a pH adjustment unit (24) configured to                adjust a pH of the culturing solution; and an ion                concentration adjustment unit configured to compare                the acquired value and a set concentration range                that is set beforehand, and to, when the acquired                value does not reside within the set concentration                range, control a temperature adjustment by the                temperature adjustment unit or a pH adjustment by                the pH adjustment unit, and by perform an                adjustment of the concentration of hydrogen                carbonate ions in the culturing solution inside the                culturing tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                culturing device.
   10Culturing device
   12Culturing tank
   22Temperature adjustment unit
   24pH adjustment unit
   54Ion concentration acquisition unit
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step of adjusting the                  pH of the culturing solution, adding hydrochloric                  acid to the culturing solution. The step of                  culturing microalgae further involves                  determining, prior to the ion concentration                  adjustment step, whether the acquired value                  resides within the set concentration range; in a                  case it is determined that the acquired value                  does not reside within the set concentration                  range, a further determination is made whether                  the acquired value exceeds or falls below the set                  concentration range; and in a case it is                  determined in the determination step that the                  acquired value exceeds the set concentration                  range, the set temperature is set to an upper                  limit value of a set temperature range that is                  set beforehand; and in a case it is determined in                  the determination step that the acquired value                  falls below the set concentration range, the set                  temperature is set to a lower limit value of the                  set temperature range.Preferred Condition: The acquired value is                  obtained by a calculation based on a carbon                  dioxide concentration measurement value of the                  gas supplied to the culturing solution, a pH                  measurement value of the culturing solution, and                  a temperature measurement value of the culturing                  solution.

The                catalytic combustion process of air and hydrogen                can be carried out at normal temperature and normal                pressure, without additional other conditions. PN KR2236823-B1
TI Air purification device for reducing ultra-fine dust and carbon dioxide, has solenoid valve that opens disinfectant supply pipe and spray pump 830 and driven, such that disinfectant in disinfectant container is discharged to spray nozzle.
AU AN S
AE GANGYANG HTS CO LTD (GANG-Non-standard)
GA 202136903V
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a scrubber unit (100) for purifying the air introduced through an air inlet (101) connected to a first indoor duct (110) in communication with one side of multi-use facility room (R). A water tank (200) is installed under the scrubber unit, for storing washing water for purifying air, and accommodating washing water falling from the scrubber unit. A solenoid valve that opens a disinfectant supply pipe and a spray pump is driven, such that the disinfectant in disinfectant container is discharged to spray nozzle for a predetermined time under the control of a controller. A polling element has a purification layer formed on outer or inner surface of the polling element. The purification layer includes mordenite, talc, perlite, diatomaceous earth, sericite, cryolite, peat moss, silvite, vermiculite, bentonite, zeolite, and tourmaline components.
   USE - Air purification device for reducing ultra-fine dust and carbon dioxide, and for sterilization and disinfection in multi-use facility such as school classroom, gymnasium, auditorium, libraries, catering room, daycare center, churches, and indoor golf driving ranges.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is capable of collecting and purifying ultrafine dust in multiple facilities, reducing the concentration of indoor carbon dioxide, and sterilizing and sterilizing harmful bacteria or viruses by automatically controlled according to a one-touch or touch-type setting mode. The concentration of ultrafine dust and carbon dioxide in the air in the multi-use facility is reliably reduced, the indoor air quality is managed economically and conveniently, and the spread of coronavirus in multi-use facilities is effectively blocked. The function of sterilizing and disinfecting harmful bacteria or viruses through indoor disinfection is performed in one device through an automatic control method through the one-touch control mode when the indoor air is circulated, thus the device user is allowed to conveniently and effectively operate various functions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air purification device.
   Scrubber unit (100)
   Air inlet (101)
   First indoor duct (110)
   Water tank (200)
   Multi-use facility room (R)

PT P
PN WO2021053244-A1; EP3795237-A1; EP4031269-A1; IN202227016707-A; US2022324785-A1
TI Producing formic acid comprises carbon capture            step by contacting source of carbon dioxide with amine            solution in amine scrubber, inducing crystallization in            ammonium bicarbonate solution, subjecting solution to            hydrogenation and heating.
AU GOETHEER E
   FEENSTRA M
   CHATZISAKOULA S
   GOETHEER E L V
   FEENSTRA M S
AE NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
GA 2021297567
AB    NOVELTY - Producing formic acid comprises: (a) a carbon                capture step by contacting a source of carbon                dioxide with an amine solution in an amine                scrubber, to obtain an ammonium bicarbonate                solution, where the carbon capture uses a chilled                amine solution having a temperature of 0-20 degrees                C; (b) inducing crystallization in the ammonium                bicarbonate solution to obtain a concentrated                ammonium bicarbonate solution; (c) subjecting the                concentrated ammonium bicarbonate solution to a                hydrogenation step to obtain an ammonium formate;                and (d) heating the ammonium formate to 50-150                degrees C, to obtain a gaseous product containing                the amine and a liquid product stream containing                formic acid.
   USE - The process is useful for producing formic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is highly efficient and durable                process; and allows for efficient heat                integration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                modular system for performing the process,                comprising (a1) a carbon capture module comprising                an amine scrubber having a first inlet for                receiving an amine solution, a second inlet for                receiving a source of carbon dioxide, an gas outlet                in the top part of the scrubber and an outlet in                the bottom part of the scrubber for discharging an                ammonium bicarbonate solution, where the scrubber                further contains means for cooling at least one of                the amine solution, and the source of carbon                dioxide and the scrubber to a temperature of 0-20                degrees C, (b1) a crystallization module comprising                means to crystallize ammonium bicarbonate,                preferably where the means include a cooler                configured to cool the ammonium bicarbonate                solution to 0-15 degrees C, and an outlet for                discharging an ammonium bicarbonate slurry, (c1) a                hydrogenation reactor comprising an inlet for                receiving the slurry, an inlet for receiving                hydrogen gas, and an outlet to discharge an                ammonium formate solution, and (d1) a stripper or                distillation column comprising an inlet for                receiving the ammonium formate solution, an gas                outlet at the top part of the stripper for                discharging a gaseous product containing the amine                and a liquid outlet at the bottom part of the                stripper for discharging a liquid product stream                containing formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crystallization of                  step (b) is induced by lowering the temperature                  of the ammonium bicarbonate solution to a                  temperature of 0-15 degrees C, preferably 3-5                  degrees C, where the slurry is heated to a                  temperature of 50-100 degrees C, preferably 70-90                  degrees C before it is subjected to the                  hydrogenation of the step (c). The hydrogen gas                  required in the step (c) originates from                  electrolysis of water. The ammonium formate                  originating from the step (c) is subjected to the                  step (e) to remove residual hydrogen gas,                  preferably in a flash drum, before it is                  subjected to heating of the step (d), where the                  hydrogen gas is recycled to the hydrogenation of                  the step (c). The ammonium bicarbonate                  concentration of the concentrated ammonium                  bicarbonate solution is 40-60 wt.% when it is                  subjected to step (c). The heating of step (d) is                  performed in a stripper column, where the gaseous                  product is obtained as top gas and the liquid                  product stream as bottom effluent. The process                  further comprises (g) subjected the formic acid                  to a hydrogenation step to obtain formaldehyde.                  The gaseous product obtained in the step (d) is                  subjected to condensation in the step (f), where                  residual formic acid and water are removed from                  the amine, where the amine is recycled to the                  step (a) and/or the mixture of formic acid and                  water is recycled back to the step (d). The amine                  obtained in the step (d), optionally after                  condensation the step (f) is recycled back to the                  step (a). Preferred Components: The amine is                  ammonia (preferred), monomethylamine,                  dimethylamine or trimethylamine. The amine                  solution comprises 20-55 wt%, preferably 20-40                  wt% the amine. The amine solution (in addition to                  the amine) comprises water and a water-miscible                  organic solvent in a weight ratio water to                  organic solvent of 5/95-95/5. The source of                  carbon dioxide contains a flue gas, preferably a                  waste incinerator flue gas and/or a power                  generation flue gas. The system further comprises                  at least one module including: (e1) a                  vapour-liquid separator for separating hydrogen                  gas from the ammonium formate solution from                  module (c1), to obtain an ammonium formate                  solution depleted in hydrogen gas to be subjected                  to module (d1) and hydrogen gas; (f1) a condenser                  for subjecting the gaseous product from module                  (d) to condensation, to obtain a liquid stream                  containing formic acid and water and a gaseous                  stream containing the amine; and (g1) a second                  hydrogenation reactor comprising an inlet for                  receiving the liquid stream containing formic                  acid from module (d1) or (f1), an inlet for                  receiving hydrogen gas and an outlet for                  discharging formaldehyde.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Fruit and vegetable cold chain high-strength plastic box comprises plastic box component with evenly spaced vents, photocatalyst material coating, carbon dioxide gas-sensitive material coating and superhydrophobic dustproof coating.
AU LI X
   JIA X
   SHAO C
   SHAO N
   CHEN Z
AE TIANJIN JIESHENG DONGHUI PRESERVATION (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C0800M
AB    NOVELTY - A fruit and vegetable cold chain high-strength plastic box comprises plastic box component with evenly spaced vents on surface, photocatalyst material coating, carbon dioxide gas-sensitive material coating and superhydrophobic dustproof coating. The inner wall of the plastic box is provided with carbon dioxide gas-sensitive material coating and outer wall of the plastic box is provided with a super-hydrophobic dust-proof coating and photocatalyst material coating from the inside to outside.
   USE - Fruit and vegetable cold chain high-strength plastic box.
   ADVANTAGE - The plastic box is dustproof and mildew-proof, and has high strength, excellent wear resistance and hydrophobic property, and can solve the problems of corrosion, aging and difficulty in management of fruits and vegetables in the box.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of the fruit and vegetable cold chain high-strength plastic box, which involves mixing high-density polyethylene and linear low-density polyethylene materials in ratio of 9:1, adding 1.5% maleic anhydride and dicumyl peroxide in ratio of 1:1, mixing, pretreating at 70 degrees C for 50 minutes by ultrasonic combined irradiation crosslinking method under ultrasonic frequency of 20 kHz, ultrasonic power of 160 W, ultrasonic time of10 minutes and irradiation power of 200 W/m2 to obtain modified pretreated raw material, adding UV absorber, light stabilizer, antioxidant, and stearic acid modified nano-calcium carbonate to modified pretreatment raw material, mixing, pouring mixture into plastic injection molding machine, controlling barrel temperature of molding machine to 220 degrees C and injection pressure to 80-100 MPa and high injection speed, molding at 60-70 degrees C and holding the pressure for 30 seconds into desired shape, leaving still for 50-60 minutes in air at 50-60 degrees C, cooling and demolding, taking out plastic box, sequentially applying super-hydrophobic dust-proof coating and photocatalyst material coating on the outer wall of the plastic box from inside to outside, setting carbon dioxide gas-sensitive material coating on the inner wall of the plastic box, and installing radio frequency identification (RFID) tag on outer wall of the plastic box.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The nano-calcium carbonate has average particle size of 40-50 nm and Brunauer-Emmett-Teller specific surface area of 42 m2/g. Preferred Composition: The photocatalyst material coating comprises 1.0 %mass nano-titanium dioxide and 0.8 %mass silicon dioxide and ethanol mixture. Preferred Process: The lanthanum-calcium-iron oxide nanocrystalline powder material is prepared by sol-gel method by dissolving 2.5 g lanthanum nitrate, 3 g calcium nitrate and 2 g ferric nitrate in 100 mL deionized water, mixing, adding 2 g citric acid to obtain solution, placing solution in water bath, heating and stirring, adding 10 mL polyethylene glycol, continuously stirring until gel is formed, drying and sintering gel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The light stabilizer is mixture of UV absorber and light stabilizer in ratio of 0.2:0.1. The UV absorber is benzotriazole. Preferred Composition: The antioxidant comprises 0.15 %mass antioxidant 1010 (pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3',5'-di-tert-butyl-4'-hydroxyphenyl)propionate)) and 0.1 %mass antioxidant 168 (tris(2,4-di-t-butylphenyl)phosphite). Preferred Process: The stearic acid modified nano-calcium carbonate is prepared by pouring 250 mL deionized water into 500 mL beaker, slowly adding 150 g nano-calcium carbonate to deionized water, placing beaker in water bath, heating to 85 degrees C, adding 50 mL of 6 %mass anhydrous ethanol solution of stearic acid, suing slow shearing apparatus to shear continuously at rate of 400 rpm for 3 hours, filtering, drying filter cake in drying oven at 85 degrees C for more than 24 hours, and grinding dried stearic acid modified nano-calcium carbonate. The hydrophobically modified nano-silica is prepared by dissolving 1.0 g cetyltrimethylammonium bromide and 1.0 g gelatin in 100 mL deionized water, adding 100 mL absolute ethanol and 13 mL aqueous ammonia, stirring for 20 minutes, adding 1.7 g ethyl orthosilicate, stirring at room temperature for 3 hours, washing with deionized water, vacuum drying at 45 degrees C, calcining at 680 degrees C for 5 hours to obtain modified nano-silicon dioxide white powder, activating 2 g modified silicon dioxide at 110 degrees C for 5 hours, dispersing in 100 mL absolute ethanol, transferring to three-necked flask equipped with magnetic stirrer, condenser and reflux, heating and stirring at 70 degrees C, adding hexamethyldisilazane and dripping 5-6 drops of dibutyltin dilaurate, refluxing for 6 hours, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, washing with absolute ethanol for 4 times and vacuum drying at 40 degrees C for 48 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The plastic box component comprises 90 %mass high-density polyethylene, 10 %mass linear low-density polyethylene, 0.2 %mass UV absorber, 0.1 %mass light stabilizer, 0.16 %mass antioxidant and 0.8 %mass stearic acid modified nano-calcium carbonate. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide gas-sensitive material coating comprises carbon dioxide gas-sensitive capsule wall and carbon dioxide gas-sensitive capsule core in ratio of 4:1. The super-hydrophobic dust-proof coating is formed by mixing binder, polydimethylsiloxane and hydrophobically modified nano-silica. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide gas-sensitive material coating is prepared by adding 0.3% homogeneous emulsifier polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate to microcapsule wall material, core material and emulsifier, stirring at 400 rpm for 120 minutes, standing at 4 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain white viscous substance, adding white viscous substance to 3% corn prolamine (alcohol-soluble protein) solution, stirring and filtering. The carbon dioxide gas-sensitive capsule wall material is prepared by adding 5 g soy protein isolate and 5 g corn starch to 100 mL distilled water, adding 100 mL glycerin, mixing, adjusting pH to 8.0, magnetically stirring in water bath for 40 minutes, cooling to room temperature, adding 10 mg of 2-6 U/g microbial transglutaminase (MTGase) enzyme, homogenizing at 4000 rpm for 30 seconds, adding 1 g lanthanum-calcium-iron oxide (La1Ca2FeO3) nanocrystalline powder material, homogenizing at 4000 rpm for 60 seconds and leaving still at room temperature to form gel. The capsule core material is prepared by mixing garlic essential oil and cinnamon essential oil in volume ratio of 2:3 to form compound essential oil, adding 3 g starch nanoparticles and 5 mg chlorine dioxide powder as bacteriostatic agent to distilled water to obtain 3% nano-starch solution, dripping compound essential oil to 100 mL of 3% nano-starch solution, stirring at 30 degrees C under speed of 450 rpm for 1 hour to obtain emulsion, filtering, centrifuging at 4 degrees C and speed of 10000 rpm for 30 minutes and washing precipitate with distilled water for 3 times. The setting process of carbon dioxide gas-sensitive material coating involves spraying carbon dioxide gas-sensitive material on inner surface of the plastic box by ultrasonic spraying machine at flow rate of 50 mL/minute, frequency of 130 kHz and spraying width of 100-200 mm.

AB    NOVELTY - The cabinet has a glass tube (3) whose inner                side is filled with carbon dioxide gas. An upper                end of a bottom plate (1) is opened with multiple                distributed through holes. An outer end of a                connecting rod (4) is fixedly connected with a pair                of strip-shaped limiting blocks. An outer end of                the connecting rod is fixedly connected with a pair                of strip-shaped limiting blocks. A limiting groove                is matched with a strip-shaped limiting block. A                rolling groove is connected with a rolling ball. An                outer end of the rolling ball is contacted with the                strip-shaped limiting block. An inner wall of a                T-shaped cylinder is fixedly connected with a PVC                film. The inner side of the T-shaped cylinder is                filled with inorganic salt. A lower end of a                pressing plate is fixedly connected with a                needle.

   ADVANTAGE - The cabinet can fix strip-shaped limiting                block in the limiting groove through a ball so that                the fixing plate is stably slid downwards, presses                a pressing plate by the fixing plate, breaks a                storing bag to pass foaming agent to separating                film by the binding needle so that the foaming                agent, aluminum sulfate and sodium bicarbonate                solution generates large amount of foam and carbon                dioxide, sprays foam and carbon dioxide from the                discharge hole position so as to cover open fire.                The cabinet meets high temperature melting by film                when the fixed plate meets the open fire to                generate high temperature so as to allow inorganic                salt in the T-shaped cylinder to fall into the                reaction cavity so that the inorganic salt and the                water in the reaction cavity is mixed to generate                heat absorption phenomenon to cool an outer wall of                the electric cabinet.

TI Light-weight and portable apparatus for hands-free measurement of concentration or partial pressure of carbon dioxide, has retention device ensures that expirated air does not leak out of enclosure and apparatus can be used hands-free way.
AU WITTKOPP J A
   MEADOWS R M
AE CAPNOGRAPHY SOLUTIONS LLC (CAPN-Non-standard)
GA 202097252R
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has an enclosure (102) that fits over the mouth and nose of a patient. An expiratory lumens (104) for directing expirated air to exit the enclosure. A measurement lumen (106) is connected to the expiratory lumen. The measurement lumen is parallel to the patient's face when the enclosure is positioned over the nose and mouth. A capnography device (108) is connected to the measurement lumen for measuring concentration or partial pressure of carbon dioxide in expiratory air output by a patient. The capnography device is parallel to the patient face when the enclosure is positioned over the nose and mouth. A retention device is for securing the enclosure to the patient's face. The retention device ensures that expirated air does not leak out of the enclosure except through the expiratory lumen. The retention device ensures that the apparatus can be used in a hands-free way.
   USE - Light-weight and portable apparatus for hands-free measurement of concentration or partial pressure of carbon dioxide in expiratory air output by non-intubated patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The enclosure creates a seal around the patient mouth and nose that prevents expirated air from freely exchanging with atmospheric air. The measurement lumen is very short, which ensures air that is measured by the measurement device and is the same as the expirated air, which improves the accuracy of the measured end tidal CO2. The capnography device is preferably connected to the mask with the display facing outward away from the patient face, so that the medical provider can easily see the information on the display by looking at the patient's face.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a mask configured to connect to a portable capnography device for performing a mainstream end tidal carbon dioxide (CO2) measurement on a patient; and
   (2) a method for monitoring a patient's breathing.

   Expiratory lumen (104)

PN US2020299595-A1
TI Producing syngas, comprises e.g. co gasifying feedstock having low rank coal and microalgae biomass with gasification agent having oxygen, steam, carbon dioxide gasified intermediate and reforming intermediate mixture and removing dioxide.
AU HOSSAIN M M
   ADNAN M A
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
   KING ABDULAZIZ CITY SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KIAB-C)
GA 202094548E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a syngas, comprises: co-gasifying a feedstock comprising low rank coal and a microalgae biomass at a temperature of 700-850 degrees C with a gasification agent comprising at least 90 vol.% oxygen, steam and carbon dioxide (CO2) based on a total volume of the gasification agent to produce a gasified intermediate; reforming the gasified intermediate to produce a syngas mixture; and removing CO2 from the syngas mixture to thus producing the syngas, where the syngas comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide and is substantially free of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process is useful for producing syngas from Indonesian coal or equivalent low grade coal and from Nannochloropsis oculata microalgae biomass or equivalent biomass and apparatus (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: efficient and improved to produce co-gasifying or gasifying low grade coal and biomass; utilizes thermodynamic simulation exhibited good optimum experimental operating conditions with high accuracy, when compared to experimental values obtained under same operating conditions; and utilizes low-value coal and biomass feedstock to produce high value syngas and contributes to minimization of greenhouse gas emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1) apparatus configured to perform the process, comprising at least one input line for oxygen (O2), water, carbon dioxide, and biomass and coal feed, a gasifier (GSR), a cyclone (CYL), a reformer (RFM), CO2 absorber (ABR), and an output line for syngas, where the O2, CO2 and water input lines are configured to input oxygen, CO2, and water into the gasifier and the and biomass and coal input line is configured to feed biomass and coal to the gasifier, and where the gasifier is connected to the cyclone which is connected to the reformer, which is connected to the CO2 absorber which is connected to the output line for syngas from which CO2 has been removed; and (2) #producing a syngas from Indonesian coal or an equivalent low grade coal and from Nannochloropsis oculata microalgae biomass or an equivalent biomass comprising feeding a mixture of the coal and the biomass and a gasifying agent comprising oxygen, water and/or CO2 into the apparatus, gasifying the mixture, reforming the mixture, and removing CO2 from the reformed mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The low rank coal is Indonesian coal. The biomass is Nannochloropsis oculata microalgae biomass. The biomass/coal (B/C) ratio in the feedstock is 0.75-1. The co-gasifying occurs in the presence of no more than 5 vol.% nitrogen, and/or at least 95 vol.% O2, based on a total volume of the gasification agent. The co-gasification occurs in the presence of oxygen, water or carbon dioxide. The co-gasification and reforming occur at a pressure of 1-50 bar. The co-gasification and reforming occur at a pressure of 1 bar. The O2 equivalence ratio (ER) is 0-0.4, a (B/C) ratio is 0.75-1 and the gasification agent is at least 90 vol.% O2 containing less than 5 vol.% nitrogen each based on a total volume of the gasification agent. The (B/C) ratio, pressure, a steam: carbon (S/C) O2 ratio, COQ to fixed carbon in the biomass (carbon dioxide: carbon molar ratio), and O2 ER provide a gasification system efficiency (GSE) of 0.8-0.99. The co-gasification is performed at 0.9-1.1 bar with a (S/C) ratio of 0.9-1.1, a carbon dioxide: carbon (CO2:C) molar ratio of 0.9-1.1, and an O2 ER of 0.00-0.40. The co-gasification is performed at 1 bar with a (S/C) ratio of 1, a (CO2:C) molar ratio of 1 and an O2 ER of 0.00. The B/C ratio, pressure, S/C ratio, (CO2:C) ratio, and ER provide cold gas efficiency of 0.3-0.5. The (B/C) ratio of 0.00, at a pressure of 1-2 bars with an S/C ratio of 0.00 a CO2:C molar ratio of 0.9-1.1 and an O2 equivalence ratio ER ratio of 0.31-0.41. The (B/C) ratio is 0.00, at a pressure of 1 bar with an S/C ratio of 0.00 a (CO2:C) molar ratio of 1, and an O2 ER ratio of 0.36. The co-gasifying further comprises removing ash and unconverted char from products of the co-gasification. The CO2 input line feeds through a CO2 compressor (CP1), the water input line is configured to feed water through a boiler (BL1), the O2 input line is configured to feed O2 through a compressor (CP-2) and/or the biomass and coal input line is configured to feed biomass and coal into the GSR, and/or the ABR is configured to receive syngas from the RFM and is configured to feed CO2 to a cooler (CR1) and to a compressor configured to receive CO2 product (CP3) which is linked to a second cooler (CR2) that is configured to provide an outward CO2 feed, and the ABR is configured to receive syngas from the RFM and configured to feed non-CO2 components of the syngas to a turbine, which is configured to feed syngas to a third cooler (CR3). Preferred Method: In the method step of removing the CO2 from the syngas comprises contacting the syngas with a membrane that separates CO2 from the syngas mixture or by using a chemical adsorbent for carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Air-conditioner for an air-conditioning system,            comprises a return-air inlet and a supply-air outlet,            which are in communication with a predetermined space,            and a exhaust ventilation channel allows air to flow in            from the return-air inlet.
AU VANDAELE S
   YAMAGUCHI T
AE DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN EURO NV (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN EURO NV (DAIK-C)
GA 2020501957
AB    NOVELTY - The air-conditioner (300) comprises a                return-air inlet (321) and a supply-air outlet                (322), which are in communication with a                predetermined space. A first main air channel (331)                allows the air to flow in from the return-air inlet                towards the supply-air outlet. A first heat                exchanger (341) is arranged in the first main air                channel to cause a heat-exchange between carbon                dioxide (CO2) refrigerant flowing in and air                passing through it. A second main air channel (332)                allows the air to flow in from the outside-air                inlet (323) towards the exhaust-air outlet (324). A                supply ventilation channel (333) allows the air to                flow in from the outside-air inlet towards the                supply-air outlet. An exhaust ventilation channel                (334) allows air to flow in from the return-air                inlet towards the exhaust-air outlet. A controller                acquires CO2information that indicates a CO2level                in the predetermined space and controls at least                the first fan (345) and the second fan (346)                according to the CO2information.
   USE - Air-conditioner for an air-conditioning system                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The friction loss in the ventilation exhaust                air is reduced, and the ventilation amount of the                predetermined space is increased. Both of the first                fan and the second fan can be operated to increase                ventilation amount of the predetermined space                according to carbon dioxide level in the                predetermined space, thus, it is possible to                prevent that the carbon dioxide level becomes high                in the space to be air-conditioned and/or                ventilated by the air-conditioner even when, for                instance, a leakage of the carbon dioxide                refrigerant is occurred.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                air-conditioning system comprises a refrigerant                compressor system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                an air-conditioner.

PN CN110986708-A; CN211425226-U
TI Device for safely handling coal bin blockages in a coal mine underground, comprises a cross beam arranged in the coal bunker upper tunnel and a hoisting winch is installed on cross beam, and a water injection pump is provided in water tank.
AU ZHOU H
   LEI W
   ZHENG C
AE UNIV LONGDONG (UYLO-Non-standard)
GA 2020313283
AB    NOVELTY - The device comprises a cross beam (13) arranged in the coal bunker upper tunnel (16) of the coal bunker (19) and the hoisting winch (12) installed on the cross beam and used for suspending the coupled blasting positioning tube (9). The coupling blasting positioning tube extends into the coal bunker. A water tank (15) is provided in the coal mine upper tunnel, and a coupling blaster (20) extends into the coupling blasting positioning tube. The coupling blaster includes a liquid carbon dioxide blaster and a blasting capsule wrapped outside the liquid carbon dioxide blaster. The coupling blaster is connected to the bottom of the beam through the suspension rope (4). The blasting capsule is provided with prefabricated blasting fractures along the circumference of the blasting capsule. The water injection port of the blasting capsule is connected to a water injection pump through a water injection tube (6), and the water injection pump is provided in the water tank.
   USE - Device for safely handling coal bin blockages in a coal mine underground.
   ADVANTAGE - The coupling blasting positioning tube is used to locate the position of the coupling blaster, provides space for the lowering and moving of the coupling blaster, and wraps the liquid carbon dioxide blaster with a blasting capsule for containing water. At the instant of carbon dioxide phase change blasting, water is used to seal the gas, and the gas cannot overflow with the pores and holes, pushing the filling water to work, and realize the coupled blasting of the coal bunker. The blasting does not produce flames and high-temperature gas, and thus, gas explosion is caused.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using the device for safely handling coal bin blockages in a coal mine underground.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the usage state of the coupled blaster in the device.

PN CN110841432-A
TI Method for drying carbon dioxide, involves closing seventh switching valve, sub switching valve and twelfth switching valve after regeneration operation of main drying tower is completed, and opening fifth and eleventh switching valves.
AU WU J
   XU M
   SHEN J
AE HANGZHOU KUAIKAI HI TECH CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202019509R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves opening a fifth switching valve (V5) and an eleventh switching valve (V11) for drying operation. The raw material gas is pressurized and heated by a compressor (1). The raw material is entered from the upper portion of a first drying tower (4) after cooling through a final cooler (2). The regeneration gas of first drying tower is opened by a first switching valve (V1), a twelfth switching valve (V12) and a third switching valve (V3). The raw material gas is pressurized and heated by compressor, and is entered into a pre-drying tower (3). A seventh switching valve (V7) and a second switching valve (V2) are opened by a first drying tower. The seventh switching valve, second switching valve and twelfth switching valve are closed after the regeneration operation of the first drying tower is completed. The fifth switching valve and eleventh switching valve are opened and switched to the first drying tower to perform the drying operation to complete the cycle operation.
   USE - Method for drying carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly saves heat energy and greatly reduces energy consumption for regeneration. The regenerated and blown gases are return to the inlet of the final cooler at the same time without discharge of exhaust gas and without loss of carbon dioxide during the drying process. The method improves the utilization rate of CO2, reduces production costs, is safe and environmentally friendly and realizes the short cooling time and short regeneration cycle. The regeneration fan is not used, which greatly reduces the area of the heater, reduces the equipment investment, and simplifies the process. The method has simple process flow, low operation energy consumption, low equipment investment, high degree of automation, and simple operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the method for drying carbon dioxide.
   Compressor (1)
   Final cooler (2)
   Pre-drying tower (3)
   First drying tower (4)
   First switching valve (V1)
   Second switching valve (V2)
   Third switching valve (V3)
   Fifth switching valve (V5)
   Seventh switching valve (V7)
   Eleventh switching valve (V11)
   Twelfth switching valve (V12)

PN US2020046256-A1
TI System for aiding in diagnosis of respiratory            dysfunction, has sensors for detecting parameters            related to content and flow of patient exhaled air and            control system that measures temperature of inhaled air            and exhaled air.
AU ALTOBELLI D E
   JONES B W
   KANE D G
   LANIER G R
   MARQUIS P R
   SOEDERBERG E M
AE DEKA PROD LP (DKAP-C)
GA 202012719X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has sensors that detect multiple                parameters related to the content and flow of a                patient exhaled air. A control system measures the                temperature of inhaled air and exhaled air. A                controller unit is coupled to the sensors. The                control system receives data related to the content                and flow of a patient exhaled air and data related                to the temperature of inhaled and exhaled air. The                controller unit determines a concentration ratio of                oxygen to carbon dioxide in the exhaled air based                upon the temperature of the inhaled air and exhaled                air. The sensors include a flow sensor (22), an                oxygen sensor (24) and a carbon dioxide sensor                (34). The oxygen sensor and the carbon dioxide                sensor are optical sensors.
   USE - System for aiding in diagnosis of respiratory                dysfunction in hospital environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The more accurate and consistent measurements                of the necessary parameters are provided, by                minimizing the volume necessary to induce                performance of the sensors. The contours of the                leading edge and the trailing edge are selected in                order to maximize the efficient flow of air about                the light restrictor while minimizing any                associated pressure drop along the trailing edge.                By eliminating the need for light restrictors, the                overall volume of the airway is lessened, thus                requiring less volume per breath in order to                properly and consistently operate the sensors. The                design and implementation of the system are                simplified by obviating the need for mechanical                light restrictors. More accurate measurement of the                concentration of the gas is provided within a                volume of air, by knowing the functional                relationship between the temperature output and the                gas sensor output permits a user to program or                otherwise control the system to bias or vary the                output of the gas sensor to provide a                temperature-dependent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for occluding transmission of light                through a passage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                medical device for aiding in the diagnosis of                respiratory dysfunction.
   Medical device (10)
   Housing (12)
   Flow sensor (22)
   Oxygen sensor (24)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (34)

PT P
PN CN110620276-A; CN110620276-B
TI Method for recovering and reusing used lithium ion battery electrolyte, involves placing used lithium ion battery into liquid nitrogen kettle to fully discharge and disassembling battery followed by passing tail gas and distilling extract.
AU LIU Q
   CHEN Y
   HUA C
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2020016968
AB    NOVELTY - An used lithium ion battery electrolyte recovering and reusing method involves placing the used lithium ion battery into the liquid nitrogen kettle to fully discharge, disassembling the battery, separating the battery case from the battery cell, collecting the frozen electrolyte debris and firmware containing the electrolyte, putting the cell and the collected frozen electrolyte debris and the electrolyte-containing firmware into the extraction kettle of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device under the protection of an inert gas, adding acetone as an entrainer, carrying out co-extraction of supercritical carbon dioxide and an entrainer, separating the electrolyte in the electrolyte, and obtaining an extract, passing the tail gas generated in the extraction process into a tail gas treatment device, filling the tail gas absorption device with calcium oxide, distilling the extract and adding missing components through subsequent component analysis to prepare an electrolyte.
   USE - Method for recovering and reusing used lithium ion battery electrolyte.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An used lithium ion battery electrolyte recovering and reusing method involves placing the used lithium ion battery into the liquid nitrogen kettle to fully discharge, disassembling the battery in the glove box, separating the battery case from the battery cell, collecting the frozen electrolyte debris and the firmware containing the electrolyte, putting the cell and the collected frozen electrolyte debris and the electrolyte-containing firmware into the extraction kettle of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device under the protection of an inert gas, adding acetone as an entrainer, carrying out co-extraction of supercritical carbon dioxide and an entrainer under conditions of a pressure of 10-40 MPa and a temperature of 31-50 degrees C, separating the electrolyte in the electrolyte, and obtaining an extract under the extraction time of 30- 70 minutes, passing the tail gas generated in the extraction process into a tail gas treatment device, filling the tail gas absorption device with one or a combination of two or three of calcium oxide, sodium hydroxide, and calcium hydroxide, distilling the extract under reduced pressure to remove the acetone, which is the entrainer in the extract, and adding missing components through subsequent component analysis to prepare an electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The used lithium ion battery is power type and digital type lithium ion battery.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The missing component comprises conductive salts and carbonates, where the conductive salts are lithium hexafluorophosphate, lithium tetrafluoroborate, lithium perchlorate, lithium hexafluoroarsenate(V), lithium nitride sulfuryl fluoride compound (LiN(SO2F)2), lithium-carbonate compound (LiC2O4BC2O4), and the carbonates are ethylene carbonate, ethylene propyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate, propylene carbonate, dimethyl carbonate, or ethyl methyl carbonate.

PN CN109675573-A
TI Catalyst for hydrogenating carbon dioxide,            comprises alkali metal comprising sodium, potassium,            and rubidium, iron, and other metals comprising            manganese, zinc, copper, and cobalt.
AU HAN Y
   XU J
   ZHANG C
   CAO C
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201940788V
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst is composite metal oxide catalyst                comprises 0.01-15.0 %mass alkali metal comprising                sodium, potassium, and rubidium, and iron to other                metals comprising manganese, zinc, copper, and                cobalt in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:0.05.
   USE - Catalyst used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide                to produce high carbon alpha -olefins                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has catalytic activity,                selectivity of high-carbon alpha -olefin, and                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of catalyst involves (I)                dissolving iron salt in alcohol solvent, adding                other metal salt comprising one or more of cobalt                salt, zinc salt, manganese salt, and copper salt,                dissolving by ultrasonic processing, stirring to                obtain solution (A), (II) preparing mixed solvent                comprising alcohol, and water in a ratio of                0.5-10.0, adding compound which is equivalent to                alkali metal, stirring at constant temperature to                obtain solution (B), (III) adding solution (B)                dropwise to the solution (A), maintaining pH at                8-12, stirring for 2-24 hours to prepare solution                (C), (IV) adding deionized water to the prepared                solution in a volume ratio of 5-15:1, leaving still                to obtain precipitate in the formed solution,                centrifuging, controlling the residual amount of                alkali metal in the precipitate to 0.1-15%, drying                at 40-70 degrees C with vacuum degree of 0.1 MPa to                obtain a precursor, and (V) grinding the prepared                precursor, and calcining under air at 450 degrees C                for 2-24 hours; and
   (2) hydrogenating carbon dioxide involves                subjecting to activation process by packing                catalyst in reactor, pretreating with carbon                monoxide/argon mixture in a molar ratio of 1-45% or                carbon monoxide/hydrogen gas in a molar ratio of                1:0.1-10 at 200-500/ degrees C with 0-2.0 MPa                pressure for 1-100 hours, decreasing temperature to                280-350 degrees C, carrying out reaction under                carbon dioxide to hydrogen gas in a molar ratio of                1:1-1:5, and having pressure of 0.5-8.0 MPa, gas                hourly space velocity (GHSV) of 1000-50000                h-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst                  comprises 1.37-1.49 mol%, preferably 1.45 mol%                  sodium, and iron to zinc in a molar of                  1:0.5-1:0.2, preferably 1:0.5. The other metal                  salt used in step (1) comprises any one of                  nitrate, chloride, sulfate or sulfite hydrate                  which is soluble in alcohol solvent. The molar                  ratio of iron salt to other metal salt in step                  (1) is 1:1-1:0.05, and the mixed salt solution                  having concentration of 0.05-1.0 mol/L. The step                  (1) involves using alkali metal is chosen from                  any one of hydroxide or carbonate of sodium,                  potassium, and rubidium, and the solution having                  concentration of 0.1-2.0 mol/L, and solution (B)                  having dropping volume of 1-5 times in solution                  (A). Preferred Process: The activation process                  preferably involves packing catalyst in reactor,                  pretreating at 350 degrees C with pressure of 0.1                  MPa for 5 hours under 10% carbon monoxide/argon                  mixture or carbon monoxide to hydrogen in a molar                  ratio of 1:10, decreasing temperature to 330                  degrees C, carrying out reaction under carbon                  dioxide to hydrogen in a molar ratio of 1:3 in                  reactor with 1.5 MPa pressure, and gas hourly                  space velocity (GHSV) of 15000 h-1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alcohol comprises                  any one of ethanol, ethylene glycol, and                  glycerin.
