TI Acetylene conveying pipeline anti-explosion inflaming retarding device, has acetylene alarm fixed in shell that is connected with acetylene delivery pipeline and flange, where lower end of shell is connected with exhaust pipe.
AU LI Y
AE YICHANG DEXIN PRACTICAL GAS CO LTD (YICH-Non-standard)
GA 201829334Q
AB    (CN207213646-U)   NOVELTY - An acetylene conveying pipeline anti-explosion inflaming retarding safety device, comprising a carbon dioxide cylinder and a PLC controller, a carbon dioxide gas cylinder connected with the shell through the air inlet pipe, the air inlet pipe is installed with a first electromagnetic valve; the shell is set with alarm of acetylene, the acetylene alarm the monitored signal is transmitted to the PLC controller, the PLC control the first electromagnetic valve, the shell is sleeved in the acetylene conveying pipeline flange, the lower end of the shell is connected with an exhaust pipe. The utility model not only can effectively prevent the acetylene pipeline flange leakage is ignited, but also can prevent the flame in the pipeline from spreading to acetylene and calcium carbide crusher.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of an acetylene conveying pipeline explosion-proof flame-retardant device.
   Carbon dioxide cylinder (1)
   Shell (2)
   Acetylene conveying pipeline (7)
   Exhaust pipe (21)
   Connecting pipe (23)
   Primary electromagnetic valve (61)
   Acetylene alarm (63)
   Air inlet pipe (71)
   Flange (78)
EA    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide cylinder (1) connected with a shell (2) through an air inlet pipe (71). The air inlet pipe is arranged with a primary electromagnetic valve (61). An acetylene alarm (63) is arranged in the shell to transmit a monitoring signal to the PLC controller. The PLC controller controls the primary solenoid valve. The shell is sleeved in an acetylene conveying pipeline (7) that is connected with an outer side of a flange (78). A lower end of the shell is connected with an exhaust pipe (21). The shell comprises a cannula that comprises a cover body that is connected with a connecting pipe (23).
   USE - Acetylene conveying pipeline explosion-proof flame-retardant device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can effectively prevent leakage of pipeline flange acetylene, and avoids flame in the pipeline from spreading to acetylene and calcium carbide crusher.

TI Production of synthesis gas from carbonaceous            feedstock e.g. petroleum coke, involves gasifying            carbonaceous feedstock in presence of oxygen and/or            steam to obtain gaseous mixture and gasifying gaseous            mixture with steam, and/or hydrogen.
AU DAGGUPATI S
   KEELER C G
   MANDAL S
   DAS A K
   MAJHI S K
   GUPTA A
   SAPRE A V
AE RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
   RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
   RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
GA 201811155W
AB    NOVELTY - Production of synthesis gas (52,53) involves                gasifying a carbonaceous feedstock (13) in the                presence of at least one of oxygen (17) and steam                (18,42) to obtain a gaseous mixture comprising                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, carbon dioxide,                water, tar, and char, and gasifying the gaseous                mixture with at least one of steam, carbon dioxide,                carbon monoxide and hydrogen, in the presence of a                catalyst comprising alkali metal salt and at                600-850 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-60 bar, to                obtain synthesis gas having the molar ratio of                hydrogen to carbon monoxide of 1.5-6.
   USE - Production of synthesis gas from carbonaceous                feedstock e.g. petroleum coke, coal, petroleum                residue, biomass and wood (all claimed), used for                generating power, producing chemicals, and                synthesizing liquid fuels, hydrogen and natural                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces synthesis gas                with increased molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon                monoxide. The method efficiently converts more than                90% char and tar to the synthesis gas in the                reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of synthesis gas involves gasifying                a carbonaceous feedstock in a reactor (100), in the                presence of at least one of oxygen and steam, at                850-1800 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-60 bar to                obtain a gaseous mixture comprising hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, methane, carbon dioxide, water,                tar and char, and gasifying the gaseous mixture in                a reactor (300) with at least one of steam, carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen, in the                presence of a catalyst comprising alkali metal salt                impregnated on silica-alumina support and at                600-850 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-60 bar, to                obtain synthesis gas having the molar ratio of                hydrogen to carbon monoxide of 1.5-6. The molar                ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the gaseous                mixture is less than 1.2. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for system (A) for producing synthesis gas                from the carbonaceous feedstock.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system for producing the synthesis gas from the                carbonaceous feedstock.
   Carbonaceous feedstock (13)
   Oxygen (17)
   Steam (18,42)
   Synthesis gas (52,53)
   Reactors (100,300)
   System (A)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method captures                  heavy metals comprising vanadium and nickel of                  the gaseous mixture with the catalyst. The alkali                  metal salt is potassium carbonate Preferred                  Composition: The method uses 10-60 wt.% potassium                  carbonate, based on the total catalyst                  composition. The molar ratio of steam to the                  carbonaceous feedstock in the reactor (100) is                  1:1-1:4. The mass ratio of catalyst to gaseous                  mixture in the reactor (300) is 5:1-60:1.

PN DE102016211971-A1; WO2018002129-A1; KR2019025687-A; CN109526211-A; BR112018077237-A2; EP3478429-A1; US2019255599-A1; MX2018016358-A1
TI Aqueous alkaline binder composition useful as binder for molding material mixture to produce foundry mold body, comprises e.g. a phenol group-containing, negatively charged or uncharged phenol-aldehyde resin including resole and novolac.
AU LADEGOURDIE G
   EGELER N
   EGLE N
AE HUETTENES ALBERTUS CHEM WERKE GMBH (ALBV-C)
   HUETTENES ALBERTUS CHEM WERKE GMBH (ALBV-C)
   HUETTENES ALBERTUS CHEM WERKE GMBH (ALBV-C)
GA 201800555X
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous alkaline binder composition for curing with carbon dioxide gas comprises a phenol group-containing, negatively charged or uncharged phenol-aldehyde resin including resoles and mixture comprising at least one resole and at least one novolac, an oxyanion including borate ions, aluminate ions, stannate ions, zirconium ions and/or titanate ions to form a stable complex with the resol-phenol-aldehyde resin and 0.5-11 wt.%, preferably 2.5-10 wt.% epoxy silane including 3-(glycidyloxy)propyl-triethoxysilane and 3-(glycidyloxy)propyltrimethoxysilane.
   USE - The composition is useful as binder for molding material mixture to produce foundry mold body, preferably in a process in which the binder is cured by gassing with carbon dioxide gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition produces stable sized foundry mold body with high strength and improved surface finish and/or improved edge hardness, and has increased flowability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Aqueous alkaline binder composition for curing with carbon dioxide gas comprises a phenol group-containing, negatively charged or uncharged phenol-aldehyde resin including resoles and mixtures comprising at least one resole and at least one novolac, an oxyanion including borate ions, aluminate ions, stannate ions, zirconium ions and/or titanate ions to form a stable complex with the resol-phenol-aldehyde resin and 0.5-11 wt.%, preferably 2.5-10 wt.% epoxy silane including 3-(glycidyloxy)propyl-triethoxysilane and 3-(glycidyloxy) propyltrimethoxysilane. The molar total amount of the phenol groups of the phenolaldehyde resin in the aqueous alkaline binder composition is 1-3 mol/kg, preferably 1.8-2 mol/kg, based on the total mass of the aqueous alkaline binder composition. The phenol-aldehyde resin has an average molecular weight of 750-1200 g/mol as determined by gel permeation chromatography. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) molding material mixture for producing a foundry mold body, comprising the aqueous alkaline binder composition and a foundry mold material; and
   (2) producing a foundry mold body, comprising providing the molding material mixture, molding provided molding material mixture and curing the molded molding mixture by gassing with carbon dioxide gas; and
   (3) foundry mold body produced by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises 0.5-11 wt.%, preferably 3.5-6 wt.% at least one silane, 1-40 wt.%, preferably 1-15 wt.% at least one compound including polyalkylene glycols, phenylalkylene glycol ethers, propylene glycol alkyl ethers, substituted or unsubstituted pyrrolidones, monoethylene glycol and polyethylene glycol and/or reaction products of the above mentioned compounds, 0.1-5 wt.%, preferably 0.8-1.5 wt.% at least one compound including 4-20C saturated or unsaturated aliphatic carboxylic acid and alkali metal salts of acids, 0.1-5 wt.%, preferably 0.8-1.5 wt.% isononanoic acid and alkali metal salts of isononanoic acid, 3-10 wt.% preferably 3-6 wt.% phenoxyethanol, butyldiglycol and/or monoethylene glycol and 0.1-5 wt.%, preferably 0.5-1.5 wt.% 1,3,5-trioxacyclohexane. Preferred Components: The phenol-aldehyde resin has an average molecular weight of 750-1000 g/mol, preferably 850-980 g/mol, as determined by gel permeation chromatography. The silane is 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, N-(2-aminoethyl)-3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane and phenyltrimethoxysilane and/or 0.5-11 wt.%, preferably 4-6 wt.% epoxy silane including 3-(glycidyloxy)propyl-triethoxysilane and 3-(glycidyloxy) propyltrimethoxysilane. The negatively charged or uncharged phenol-aldehyde resin is a negatively charged or uncharged resole-phenol-aldehyde resin for curing with carbon dioxide gas in the phenol-resol carbon dioxide process. The composition has pH of 12-14, preferably 13-14 at 20 degrees C. The molar amount of the phenol groups in the aqueous alkaline binder composition is 1.5-2.5 mol/kg, preferably 1.8-2 mol/kg. The viscosity of the alkaline binder composition at 20 degrees C of 100-1000 mPas, preferably 150-500 mPas, determined according to DIN EN ISO 3219: 1994. The molar ratio of the total amount of alkali metals to phenol groups is of 1:1-2.5:1, preferably 1.7:1-1.9:1. The molar amount of the alkali metals in the aqueous alkaline binder composition is 1-7.5 mol/kg, preferably 3-4 mol/kg. The molar ratio of the total amount of potassium cations to the total amount of sodium cations is 47:1-59:1, preferably 52:1-55:1.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Embedded metal-oxide interface                  structure forms an anchoring interface                  architecture that prohibits sintering of metal                  particles and resists carbon deposition. Anode is                  configured to conduct non-oxidative                  dehydrogenation of ethane to ethylene. Cathode is                  configured to produce hydrogen gas. Embedded                  metal-oxide interface structure is exsolved in                  situ and at formed at a nanoscale.

GA 2021C63640
AB    NOVELTY - preparation of two-dimensional in-plane heterogeneous copper sulfide/copper oxide comprises dissolving copper acetate in water, adding n-propylamine dropwise, stir-reacting at 25-35degreesC for 25-35 minutes, heating up to 100-150degreesC, and reacting with stirring for 5-10 hours to obtain a precursor; and adding the precursor to water to disperse, dropwise adding vulcanizing agent, stirrng, and reacting at 25-35degreesC for 10-200 seconds.

GA 2020C2277H
AB    NOVELTY - A hypochlorous acid disinfectant comprises 70-80 pts. wt. electro deionization (EDI) ultra-pure water, 15-30 pts. wt. hypochlorous acid propellant, 6-12 pts. wt. stabilizer and 4.5-9 pts. wt. carbon dioxide generating agent.
   USE - The hypochlorous acid disinfectant is used for air sterilization, object surface disinfection, hand disinfection, skin disinfection and medical appliance for killing bacteria and virus.
   ADVANTAGE - The disinfectant is high level disinfectant, and has has low use concentration, low odor, no corrosiveness and very fast new coronavirus killing effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for preparing hypochlorous acid disinfectant comprising opening mixing tank, setting temperature to 20-40 degrees C, adding EDI ultra-pure water, starting stirring, orderly adding sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate, fully dissolving component, adding 10% dilute hydrochloric acid solution, fully stirring for 2-3 hours, adding potassium bromide and sodium bromide into mixing tank, fully dissolving, adding sodium hypochlorite into mixing tank, adjusting pH value of solution to 5.5-6.5, and preparing 85-220 ppm hypochlorous acid solution in mixing tank; and
   (2) a method for preparing sterilizing wet tissue comprising anti-oxidation treating melt-blown cloth for making wet tissue by antioxidant, drying at 35-37 degrees C, fully-closed spraying using spraying device, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide into sealed space.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hypochlorous acid propellant comprises 5-10 pts. wt. hydrochloric acid and 10-20 pts. wt. sodium hypochlorite. The stabilizer comprises 3-6 pts. wt. potassium bromide and 3-6 pts. wt. sodium bromide. The carbon dioxide generating agent comprises 4-6 pts. wt. sodium carbonate and 0.5-3 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate. The antioxidant is mixture of tea polyphenol and phytic acid at adding ratio of 2-4:1. The antioxidant is mixed with absolute ethanol to dissolve. The ratio of ethanol and antioxidant is 2-3:1. Preferred Method: The method further involves putting sterilized wet tissue in outer package, and spraying layer of silver ion spraying film on inner wall of outer package.

PN CN109847712-A
TI Preparation of ZIF-8/cellulose composite membrane            for carbon dioxide adsorption in air, involves mixing            zinc oxide powder and 2-methylimidazole, grinding to            obtain ZIF-8 nanoparticles, and adding cotton cellulose            nanofiber aqueous solution.
AU GE F
   YIN L
   AO Y
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 201953434D
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of ZIF-8/cellulose composite                membrane involves mixing zinc oxide powder and                2-methylimidazole in a ratio, grinding at room                temperature until white fine powder is obtained to                obtain mixture of zinc oxide powder and                2-methylimidazole, adding mixture of zinc oxide                powder and 2-methylimidazole into reaction kettle,                heating in an oven at 160-240 degrees C for 12-20                hours to obtain ZIF-8 nanoparticles, dissolving                cotton cellulose nanofibers in deionized water,                stirring for 5 hours, taking 5 ml obtained cotton                cellulose nanofiber aqueous solution, adding ZIF-8                nanoparticles, stirring for 5-10 hours,                vacuum-filtering cotton cellulose nanofiber and                ZIF-8 mixture to obtain ZIF-8/cellulose composite                membrane containing small amount of water, and                freeze-drying. The molar ratio of zinc oxide and                2-methylimidazole is (5-15):(9.5-28.5). The mass                ratio of cotton cellulose nanofiber to deionized                water is (0.0250-0.250):(10-100). The mass of the                ZIF-8 nanoparticles is 0.1-0.7 g.
   USE - Preparation of ZIF-8/cellulose composite                membrane for carbon dioxide adsorption in air                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of                ZIF-8/cellulose composite membrane which reduces                the concentration of carbon dioxide in the air,                using wide source of raw materials, by short and                economical process.

AU WANG Y
AE DAQING YUANSHI ELECTROMECHANICAL TECHNOL (DAQI-Non-standard)
GA 201917675N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an evaporator connected to a working substance generator. The working substance generator is connected to a condenser. The condenser is connected to a working substance pump for forming a power generating main loop. An absorption-type heat pump is connected to a heat source for supplying heat. The absorption-type heat pump is set to cool condensed water in the condenser and directly driven by industrial waste heat to be conveyed back to a factory. Working medium is formed as carbon dioxide (CO2). The working substance pump forms a circulation of the carbon dioxide in the system. The working substance generator is formed as a carbon dioxide generator set.
   USE - Absorption-type condensed industrial waste heat recycling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses low-temperature surplus heat cycle power generation for coupling the working medium and the absorption-type heat pump, uses industrial waste heat as a power source of the generator and makes the condenser to use the absorption-type heat pump to cool down the heat after generating power enters into the condenser. The absorption-type heat pump is driven by industrial waste heat to obtain high working medium temperature for realizing heating process and producing power.

TI Preparation of methanol from landfill gas involves            preparing hydrogen-deficient synthesis gas from            landfill gas in reforming reactor filled with reforming            catalyst, adding hydrogen for synthesis gas regulation,            and separating methanol and unreacted synthesis            gas.
AU WANG Q
   WANG L
   ZHANG L
   GAO R
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202282920Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of methanol from landfill gas                involves (s1) preparing hydrogen-deficient                synthesis gas from landfill gas and steam through                reforming reaction in a reforming reactor filled                with a reforming catalyst, (s2) adding hydrogen for                synthesis gas regulation, and preparing methanol                synthesis reaction in a reactor equipped with a                methanol synthesis catalyst, (s3) separating                methanol and the unreacted synthesis gas, recycling                one portion of the unreacted synthesis gas to the                methanol reactor, and using another portion of                unreacted syngas as purge gas, mixing with external                natural gas and air, and transferring to                burner.
   USE - Preparation of methanol from landfill                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the exhaust gas                circulation and the internal energy of the process                system comprehensively, and the energy efficiency                and carbon dioxide equivalent emission reduction                rate of the landfill gas-to-methanol process are                effectively improved, under the condition that the                landfill gas treatment capacity is 4577.29m3/hour                and the circulation ratio is 0.95. The whole                process energy efficiency of landfill gas to                methanol is 88.50%, and the carbon dioxide                equivalent emission reduction rate is 95.32%. The                process for producing methanol from landfill gas                reduces emission of greenhouse gases e.g carbon                dioxide and methane, and produces methanol as a                high value-added product, which is an effective                path to achieve the goal of carbon peaking and                carbon neutralization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Condition: The reforming reactor                  in step (s1) operates at 700-900&#8451; in a                  pressure of 0.3-0.7MPa, and in step (s3) operates                  at 230-280&#8451;, in a pressure of 4-6 MPa.                  The circulation ratio of unreacted gas is                  0.6-0.95. The purge gas, air and additional                  natural gas are combusted in the burner at                  900-1000&#176;C to obtain a high-temperature                  exhaust gas, and obtained exhaust gas provides                  heat for the reforming reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  methane to carbon dioxide to water is                  1:(0.5-0.9):(1-1.5).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The addition amount                  of hydrogen is to adjust the hydrogen-carbon                  molar ratio of synthesis gas [H / (2CO+3CO)] is                  0.9-1.1.

PN CN113463114-A
TI Synthesis of nano-alloy cubic hollow-shell structure electrocatalyst used in electroreduction of carbon dioxide, involves reacting palladium chloride, copper chloride and ethylene glycol, mixing with L-glutamic acid, adjusting pH, adding multi-walled carbon nanotubes, reacting and heating.
AU YANG L
   BAI Z
   MIAO R
   ZHANG Q
   WANG C
   CHANG F
AE UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
GA 2021B7321X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano-alloy cubic hollow-shell structure electrocatalyst involves mixing 0.5-1.5 mL of 2 mg/mL palladium chloride solution and 0.245-1.334 mL of 6 mg/mL copper chloride dihydrate solution, dispersing in ethylene glycol, obtaining a solution (A), mixing with 16.67 mg L-glutamic acid, obtaining a solution (B), adjusting the pH of the solution (B) to 11 using 8 wt.% potassium hydroxide in ethylene glycol solution under vigorous stirring, obtaining a solution (C), adding 5 mg multi-walled carbon nanotubes, ultrasonically treating for 30 minutes, stirring for 2 hours, transferring into a 25 mL autoclave lined with PTFE, heating to 160degrees Celsius at the rate of 5degrees Celsius per minute, heat-preserving for 6 hours, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, washing the product 3-4 times with secondary water, and drying at 40degrees Celsius for 24 hours under vacuum.
   USE - Synthesis of nano-alloy cubic hollow-shell structure electrocatalyst used in electroreduction of carbon dioxide for fuel cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables synthesis of the catalyst with simple operation under mild reaction conditions, high reaction efficiency and low energy consumption. TI Detecting drug distribution comprises e.g. taking            different physiological stages of experimental rat,            feeding same number of radiolabeled drug for rat,            placing rat with different dosing time in carbon            dioxide and killing medicine rat.

   DI Y
   WANG Z
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 2021271769
AB    NOVELTY - Method involves taking different physiological                stages of experimental rat. The same number of                radiolabeled drug is fed for rat. The rat with                different dosing time is placed in carbon dioxide.                The rat sample is placed into hexane-carbon dioxide                dry ice to freeze for 20-30 minutes. The slice                thickness is uniform. The slice layer is packaged                using Mylar (RTM: Biaxially-oriented polyethylene                terephthalate) gum paper. The slice sample is                placed on the imaging plate. The fluorescence                portion is observed by the microscope so as to                observe the distribution condition of the                medicine.
   USE - Analysis method for detecting drug                distribution.

PN CN111450832-A
TI Preparing calcium oxide modified nickel-alumina            catalyst by microwave-assisted co-precipitation            comprises e.g. mixing calcium chloride, nickel(II)            nitrate hexahydrate, sodium hydroxide, and            polyvinylpyrrolidone, and microwave reacting.
AU SONG F
   XU Q
   YUE R
   WU A
   QI C
   CAO Y
   YANG B
   YAN J
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2020728031
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium oxide modified                nickel-alumina catalyst by microwave-assisted                co-precipitation comprises (i) mixing calcium                chloride, nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate, sodium                hydroxide, gamma -alumina and polyvinylpyrrolidone,                adding deionized water, stirring evenly to ensure                that each group of substances is fully dissolved,                transferring to a 100 ml PTFE liner, and tightly                sealing the lid, (ii) placing the reaction kettle                into a microwave reactor, setting the microwave                power at 400-600 W, and temperature of 80-120                degrees C, reacting at a constant temperature under                the self-generated pressure of the reaction kettle                for 10-30 minutes, (iii) cooling the above obtained                product to room temperature, centrifuging, drying                it, and calcining it at 400-600 degrees C for 4                hours to obtain precursor nickel(II) oxide/calcium                oxide-alumina, (v) subjecting the obtained                precursor to reduction treatment under the                hydrogen/nitrogen mixed atmosphere at 400 degrees C                to obtain nickel/ calcium oxide-alumina                catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in catalytic adsorption                of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: shortens reaction time compared                with common co-precipitation method; improves the                problem of uneven dispersion of the active                component nickel and adsorption assistant calcium                oxide. The catalyst has improved selective                adsorption of carbon dioxide and activity in the                catalytic hydrogenation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of catalyst in catalytic adsorption of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), nickel(II)                  nitrate hexahydrate and gamma -alumina are mixed                  based on 10-30 wt.% nickel: gamma -alumina,                  calcium chloride, gamma -alumina are mixed based                  on 5-15 wt.% calcium: gamma -alumina, pouring                  into a PTFE reactor equipped with a pressure                  sensor inside, adding 50 ml solution of 2 mol/l                  sodium hydroxide and 0.1 mol/l                  polyvinylpyrrolidone concentration, and                  magnetically stirring for 10 minutes to fully                  dissolve it. In step (ii), the pressure in the                  kettle is controlled to be less than or equal to                  10 MPa during the microwave reaction process. The                  reduction time in step (iv) is 40-60 minutes, and                  the volume ratio between hydrogen and nitrogen in                  the mixed gas is 2:3. Preferred Components: In                  the use method, the conversion rate in the                  catalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction                  reaches 50%, the corresponding temperature is 218                  degrees C when the loading amounts of nickel and                  calcium oxide are 0.2 and 0.15,                  respectively.

AB    NOVELTY - The gas turbine engine (100) has a primary flowpath fluidly connecting a compressor section (110), a combustor section (120), and a turbine section (130). A heat exchanger (150) is mounted in the primary flowpath downstream of the turbine section. The heat exchanger has a first inlet (172) for receiving fluid from the primary flowpath and a first outlet for expelling fluid received at the first inlet. The heat exchanger has a second inlet (192) fluidly connected to a supercritical carbon dioxide (sCO2) bottoming cycle and a second outlet connected to the sCO2 coolant circuit, where sCO2 bottoming cycle is a recuperated Brayton cycle.

PT P
PN CN110935420-A
TI Preparing porous activated carbon adsorbent, involves adding sawdust into tube furnace, heating, carbonizing, mixing primary carbon materials with activator, adding deionized water, mixing activated carbon with modifier and sonicating.
AU LIN R
   ZHOU J
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202026477N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing atmospheric pressure carbon dioxide multi-stage porous activated carbon adsorbent, involves (a) adding a sawdust raw material into a tube furnace, venting the furnace with nitrogen at a flow rate of 0.1-0.5 l/minute, heating the furnace at 600-800 degrees C at a rate of 10-25 degrees C/minute, carbonizing for 60-100 minutes and cooling at room temperature to obtain primary carbon materials, (b) mixing the primary carbon materials with activator, adding deionized water, stirring at room temperature for 30-60 minutes, leaving still for 8-36 hours and drying at 100-115 degrees C, (c) adding the above mixture into the furnace, passing nitrogen gas into the furnace and activating, cooling to room temperature, washing with deionized water until neutral and drying, and (d) mixing the activated carbon in step (c) with modifier, adding deionized water, sonicating in a water bath and drying at 100-115 degrees C to obtain a modified activated carbon.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing atmospheric pressure carbon dioxide multi-stage porous activated carbon adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has adsorption of carbon dioxide by thermogravimetry at room temperature and pressure, excellent equilibrium adsorption capacity of 6.20 mmol/g, and is prepared by simple preparation method and cost-effective. The method adopts easily available raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sawdust raw material is chosen from discarded cedar, pine, cypress or camphor sawdust. The activator is potassium hydroxide, ammonium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide or calcium hydroxide. The modifier is potassium chloride, sodium chloride, magnesium chloride hexahydrate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate, sodium nitrate or potassium nitrate. Preferred Method: The step (b) involves mixing the primary carbon materials and the activator in an alkali-to-carbon mass ratio of (0.2-10):1. The step of activation is carried out at 600-800 degrees C for 60-100 minutes. The step (d) involves sonicating at 70-100 degrees C for 30 minutes in a water bath for 80-100 minutes. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of activated carbon and the modifier is (0.2-10):1.

TI Cleaning method for finished chip on flexible (COF) substrates, involves neutralizing and treating with static electricity at static eliminator after COF product is dried, and removing from cleaning device through winding end.
AU MENG Q
   JI X
   SUN B
   SHEN H
   LI X
AE JIANGSU LEADER-TECH ELECTRONICS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020207736
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves directly entering the chip on flexible (COF) product (2) from the take-out end (1) into the spray tank of the two-fluid spray device (7). The cleaning liquid is treated with carbon dioxide device (8) to form carbonic acid, so as neutralizing the charged charge on the surface of the COF product. The COF product is spray-washed into the air knife section (3) and the drying area (4) for blow drying treatment. The static electricity is neutralized and treated at the static eliminator (5) after the COF product is dried. The cleaning device is removed through the winding end (6) after the electrostatic treatment of the COF product.
   USE - Cleaning method for finished chip on flexible (COF) substrates of fine circuit used in electronic devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The water used for cleaning is treated by the carbon dioxide device to form carbonic acid, so as neutralizing the charged charge on the surface of the COF product and achieving the cleaning of the COF substrate. The use of two fluids can moderately reduce the water consumption of pure water and respond to the problem of insufficient static elimination.

PN CN110669154-A; CN110669154-B
TI Preparing large-diameter latex involves placing emulsifier, electrolyte, initiator and demineralized water in reaction kettle, using nitrogen to replace air in kettle, and turning on stirring device to completely dissolve all additives.
AU LIU B
   LUO X
   ZHANG M
   ZHANG H
   TAN Z
   DU S
   HU Y
   LIU X
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 202006182S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing large-diameter latex involves placing 0.2-5 pts. wt. emulsifier, 0.2-8 pts. wt. electrolyte, 0.2-5 pts. wt. initiator and 60-80 pts. wt. demineralized water in a reaction kettle, using nitrogen to replace the air in the kettle 2-3 times, and turning on the stirring device to completely dissolve all the additives, and then injecting the reaction monomer into the kettle, adjusting the speed of the reactor to 50-400 revolutions per minute, raising the temperature of the reactor to 55-85 degrees C, and starting the polymerization reaction for 2-18 hours to prepare a small particle size polymer latex. The temperature of the reactor is maintained at 55-85 degrees C, carbon dioxide gas is passed into the small particle size polymer latex in the reactor, and the pressure of the reactor is maintained at a pressure of 0.1-5kg for 10-30 minutes, and then the carbon dioxide gas is recovered.
   USE - Method for preparing large-diameter latex by carbon dioxide agglomeration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare large-diameter latex in a simple, safe, eco-friendly and cost-effective manner on industrial scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The heating of the reaction kettle is stopped, the reaction kettle is opened, and the large particle size polymer latex is discharged.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymerization reaction monomer is butadiene, isoprene, butyl acrylate, isooctyl acrylate, styrene, acrylonitrile, methyl methacrylate, butyl methacrylate or isooctyl methacrylate. The emulsifier is disproportionated rosin acid soap, fatty acid soap, potassium oleate, sodium oleate, sodium lauryl sulfate, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, polyoxyvinyl ether, or sodium alkylnaphthalenesulfonate. The electrolyte is potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, or potassium bicarbonate. The initiator is ammonium persulfate, potassium persulfate, sodium persulfate, azobisisobutylcyanide, benzoyl peroxide, or cumene hydroperoxide.

TI Carbon dioxide laser processing method for micropores on microwave ceramic substrate used as semiconductor chip, involves polishing, ultrasonic cleaning and drying microwave ceramic substrate to complete micropore process of substrate.
AU WANG Y
   LIU J
   SONG X
   GUO Y
   QIU Y
AE CHINA ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY GROUP CORP (CETC-C)
   NO 38 RES INST CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TE (CETC-C)
GA 201868422X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pre-treating of the microwave ceramic substrate (1) to smooth the surface of the microwave ceramic substrate for polishing, ultrasonic cleaning and drying. A metal microporous template is prepared using a metal plate having a thickness of 0.1 to 0.3 mm as a metal microporous template. The metal microporous template is mounted to apply the metal microporous template to the microwave ceramic substrate. The carbon dioxide laser drilling is performed through the metal mark (4) on the metal microporous template. The holes to-be processed are processed on the microwave ceramic substrate. The post-treatment degumming of the microwave ceramic substrate is performed to remove the metal microporous template. The polishing, ultrasonic cleaning and drying of the microwave ceramic substrate are performed to complete micropore process the microwave ceramic substrate. The metal microporous templates are ultrasonic cleaned to reuse the metal microporous template.
   USE - Carbon dioxide laser processing method for micropores on microwave ceramic substrate used as semiconductor chip of electronic component package.
   ADVANTAGE - The micropore processing with smaller apertures and lower taper is achieved. The high quality, easy operation and good repeatability of the microwave ceramic substrate are ensured.

The weight                  ratio of spinach powder and distilled water is                  1:10. The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and                  nitrogen is 1:1. Preferred Condition: The                  irradiation treatment is carried out at                  irradiation dose of 1-3 kGy.

PN CN107903292-A; CN107903292-B
TI Stilbene glycoside extraction involves providing Polygonum multiflorum powder, adding aphid agent to Polygonum multiflorum powder in ultrapure water, and then extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain product.
AU XIANG W
AE UNIV SICHUAN ARTS & SCI (UYSI-Non-standard)
GA 2018301024
AB    NOVELTY - Stilbene glycoside extraction involves providing Polygonum multiflorum powder, adding an aphid agent to the Polygonum multiflorum powder in ultrapure water, and then extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain a product. The extraction is carried out at 48-52 degrees C. The extraction product is separated by kettle I and separation kettle II in order to obtain stilbene crude glycosides. The separated kettle I is placed at 43-47 degrees C. The separation kettle II is placed at 28-32 degrees C.
   USE - Method for extracting stilbene glycoside.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts stilbene glycoside in a simple and eco-friendly manner with an extraction rate of 5.25% and a purity of 98%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Stilbene glycoside extraction involves providing Polygonum multiflorum powder, adding an aphid agent to the Polygonum multiflorum powder in ultrapure water, and then extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain a product. The extraction is carried out at 48-52 degrees C. The extraction product is separated by kettle I and separation kettle II in order to obtain stilbene crude glycosides. The separated kettle I is placed at 43-47 degrees C. The separation kettle II is placed at 28-32 degrees C. The crude stilbene glucoside is dissolved in anhydrous ethanol and then filtered to obtain a filtrate. The obtained filtrate is dried to obtain the finished stilbene glucoside.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The Polygonum multiflorum powder is obtained by drying roots in shade and then crushed into powder of 6-9 meshes. The carrying agent and the Polygonum multiflorum powder are provided in a weight ratio of 1.5-2.2:5. The carbon dioxide flow rate is 10-11 liter/hour. The filtration is performed by using macroporous resin filtration.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN US2018179559-A1
TI Biological and chemical process for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide and/or other sources of inorganic carbon into organic compounds, comprises fixing carbon dioxide and/or inorganic carbon via chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction.
AU REED J S
AE KIVERDI INC (KIVE-Non-standard)
GA 201850616Q
AB    NOVELTY - Biological and chemical process (I) for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into organic compounds, comprises: introducing carbon dioxide gas, either alone and/or dissolved in a mixture or solution further comprising carbonate ion and/or bicarbonate ion, and/or introducing inorganic carbon contained in a solid phase into an environment for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and/or chemoautotroph cell extracts; and fixing the carbon dioxide and/or inorganic carbon into organic compounds within the environment via at least one chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing obligate and/or facultative chemoautotrophic microorganisms. The chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is driven by chemical and/or electrochemical energy provided by electron donors and electron acceptors that have been generated chemically and/or electrochemically and/or or are introduced into the environment from at least one source external to the environment, biomass and/or biochemicals are produced by the chemosynthetic reaction. The biomass and/or biochemicals are separated from the environment and are processed into a product comprising an animal feed, a fertilizer, a soil additive, a soil stabilizer, a carbon source for large scale fermentations, and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms.
   USE - The process is useful for biological and chemical process for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into organic compounds. The biomass and/or biochemicals are processed into a nutrient source for the growth of fish (claimed). The process is useful for formation and recovery of useful organic and/or inorganic chemical products comprising biodegradable/biocompatible plastics, as sources of pharmaceutical, medicinal or nutritional substances; soil additives and soil stabilizers.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: enable the effective capture of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere or from a point source of carbon dioxide emissions for the production of liquid transportation fuel and/or other organic chemical products, which can help address greenhouse gas induced climate change and contribute to the domestic production of renewable liquid transportation fuels without any dependence upon agriculture; utilizes the integration of chemoautotrophic microorganisms into a chemical process stream as a biocatalyst, as compared to other lifeforms; provide renewable energy technologies with the capability of producing a transportation fuel having significantly higher energy density than if the renewable energy sources are used to produce hydrogen gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for capture and conversion (II) of carbon dioxide and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into organic compounds, comprising introducing a carbon source in the form of flue gas comprising carbon dioxide and/or in the form of an aqueous solution comprising inorganic carbon into an environment in a bioreactor that is for maintaining chemoautotrophic microorganisms, introducing an electron donor that is separate from the carbon source into the environment in the bioreactor, fixing the carbon dioxide in the flue gas and/or inorganic carbon in the aqueous solution into the organic compounds within the environment in the bioreactor via at least one chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing chemoautotrophic microorganisms and using at least one electron donor and at least one electron acceptor and the electron donor and/or the electron acceptor are generated and/or refined from at least one inorganic chemical, where the electron donor is generated separately from the carbon source and externally to the bioreactor using a renewable, alternative, or low carbon dioxide emission power source, the electron donor is molecular hydrogen that is generated using the power source, through electrolysis of water, biomass and/or biochemicals are produced by the at least one chemosynthetic reaction, and the biomass and/or biochemicals are separated from the environment and are processed into a product comprising an animal feed, a fertilizer, a soil additive, a soil stabilizer, a carbon source for large scale fermentations, and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The biomass and/or biochemicals are processed into a carbon source for large scale fermentation and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms, where the large scale fermentation and/or the other microbes or organisms produce at least one of commercial enzymes, antibiotics, amino acids, vitamins, or bioplastics. The biomass and/or biochemicals are processed into a nutrient source for the growth of fish. The process for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide or inorganic carbon results in a net reduction of gaseous carbon dioxide released to the atmosphere. The biomass and/or biochemicals are processed into a carbon source for a large scale fermentation and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms, where the large scale fermentation and/or the other microbes or organisms produce at least one of commercial enzymes, antibiotics, amino acids, vitamins, and bioplastics. Preferred Components: The animal feed product is a feed for cattle, sheep, chickens, pigs, and/or fish. The chemoautotrophic microorganisms comprise at least one e.g: Acetoanaerobium sp., Achromobacter sp.; Acidianus sp.; Acinetobacter sp., methanogens, consortiums of microorganism that include chemoautotrophs; chemoautotrophs native to at least one of hydrothermal vents, geothermal vents, hot springs, cold seeps, underground aquifers, salt lakes, saline formations, mines, acid mine drainage, mine tailings, oil wells, refinery wastewater, coal seams, deep sub-surface, waste water and sewage treatment plants, geothermal power plants, sulfatara fields, and soils; and extremophiles selected from at least one of thermophiles, hyperthermophiles, acidophiles, halophiles, and psychrophiles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The environment comprises a bioreactor, where the microorganisms are maintained in a culture medium in the bioreactor. The bioreactor is formed at least in part by a microbial culture apparatus selected from: an airlift reactor; a biological scrubber column; a bubble column; a continuous stirred tank reactor; a counter-current, upflow, expanded-bed reactor; a digestor for a sewage and/or waste water treatment or bioremediation system; at least one filters; a fluidized bed reactor; a gas lift fermenter; an immobilized cell reactor; a membrane biofilm reactor; a mine shaft; a Pachuca tank; a packed-bed reactor; a plug-flow reactor; a static mixer; a tank; a trickle bed reactor; a vat; and/or a vertical shaft bioreactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molecular hydrogen acts as an electron donor. The hydrogen is generated through electrolysis of water and/or thermochemical splitting of water. The electrolysis of water comprises at least one of proton exchange membranes, liquid electrolyte, high-pressure electrolysis or high temperature electrolysis of steam. The electrolyte comprises potassium hydroxide. The thermochemical splitting of water comprises at least one of iron oxide cycle, cerium(IV) oxide-cerium(III) oxide cycle, zinc zinc-oxide cycle, sulfur-iodine cycle, copper-chlorine cycle, calcium-bromine-iron cycle or hybrid sulfur cycle. The electron donors and/or electron acceptors are generated or recycled using renewable, alternative, or conventional sources of power that are low in greenhouse gas emissions, and where the sources of power comprise at least one of photovoltaic, solar thermal, wind, hydroelectric, nuclear, geothermal, enhanced geothermal, ocean thermal, ocean wave, and tidal power sources. The electron donor comprises at least one of: ammonia; ammonium; carbon monoxide; dithionite; elemental sulfur; a hydrocarbon; hydrogen; a sulfide; a sulfite; a thionate; a thionite; a transition metal and/or its sulfide; an oxide; a chalcogenide; a halide; a hydroside; an oxyhydroxide; a phosphate; a sulfate; a carbonate; and a conduction or valence band electron in a solid state electrode material. The electron donor is generate within or recycled to the environment through non- or low-carbon dioxide emitting chemical reactions with hydrocarbons, comprising at least one of: thermochemical reduction of sulfate reaction (TSR); the Muller-Kuhne reaction for the production of hydrogen sulfide or reduced sulfur; and methane reforming-like reactions utilizing metal oxides in place of water, where the metal oxides comprise at least one of: iron oxide, calcium oxide, and magnesium oxide, and where the hydrocarbon is reacted to form solid carbonate with little or no emission of carbon dioxide gas along with hydrogen electron donor product. The electron acceptor comprises at least one of: carbon dioxide; oxygen; a nitrite; a nitrate; a transition metal ion; a sulfate; and a valence or conduction band hole in a solid state electrode material. Preferred Process: The hydrogen is generated through at least one of electrolysis of hydrogen sulfide, thermochemical splitting of hydrogen sulfide and the half-cell reduction of hydride ion to hydrogen accompanied by the half-cell oxidization of electron sources comprising ferrous iron oxidized to ferric iron and/or the oxidation of sulfur compounds, where the oxidized iron or sulfur is recycled to back to a reduced state through additional chemical reactions with minerals comprising at least one of a metal sulfide, hydrogen sulfide, and a hydrocarbon. The hydrogen is generated through an electrochemical or thermochemical process known to produce hydrogen with low- or no-carbon dioxide emissions, comprising at least one of carbon capture and sequestration enabled methane reforming, carbon capture and sequestration enabled coal gasification, the Kvaerner process and a process that generates a carbon-black product and carbon capture and sequestration enabled gasification or pyrolysis of biomass. The fixing step is followed by at least one process steps in which unused nutrients and/or process water left after removal of chemoautotrophic cell mass and/or chemical co-products of chemosynthesis and/or waste products or contaminants of the process stream produced during the fixing step are recycled back into a reactor system in which the chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is performed to support further chemosynthesis. The chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is performed by chemoautotrophic microorganisms that have been improved, optimized or engineered for the fixation of carbon dioxide and/or other forms of inorganic carbon and the production of organic compounds. The electron donor is generated from pollutants or waste products of: process gas; tail gas; enhanced oil recovery vent gas; biogas; acid mine drainage; landfill leachate; landfill gas; geothermal gas; geothermal sludge or brine; metal contaminants; gangue; tailings; sulfides; disulfides; mercaptans selected from at least one of methyl mercaptan, dimethyl mercaptan, and ethyl mercaptan; carbonyl sulfide; carbon disulfide; alkanesulfonates dialkyl sulfides; thiosulfate; thiofurans; thiocyanates; isothiocyanates; thioureas; thiols; thiophenols; thioethers; thiophene; dibenzothiophene; tetrathionate; dithioite; thionate; dialkyl disulfides; sulfones; sulfoxides; sulfolanes; sulfonic acid; dimethylsulfoniopropionate; sulfonic esters; hydrogen sulfide; sulfate esters; organic sulfur; and sour gases. The delivery of reducing equivalents from the electron donor to the chemoautotrophic microorganisms for the chemosynthetic reaction during the fixing step is kinetically and/or thermodynamically enhanced by at least one of: introduction of hydrogen storage materials into the environment in the form of a solid support media for microbial growth that facilitates bringing absorbed or adsorbed hydrogen electron donors into close proximity with the chemoautotrophic organisms; introduction of electron mediators comprising at least one of: cytochromes, formate methyl-viologen, NAD+/NADH, neutral red (NR), and quinones to help transfer reducing power from a poorly soluble electron donor comprising hydrogen gas or electrons in solid state electrode materials into chemoautotrophic culture media in the environment; and introduction of electrode materials in the form of a solid growth support media directly into the environment to facilitate bringing solid state electrons into close proximity with the chemoautotrophic microorganisms. The process further comprises prior to the fixing step, reacting carbon dioxide with minerals to form a carbonate or bicarbonate product, which is then used in the fixing step. The process comprises fixing the carbon dioxide and/or inorganic carbon into the organic compounds via at least one chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction within a reactor system, where the electron donor utilized in the chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is produced via a non-biological process in the reactor system. The chemosynthetic microorganisms are obligate anaerobes.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparation method for pituitary tissue three-dimensional cell culture fluid design and growth hormone by performing mixed cell design, selecting human leukocyte antigen gene and T cell receptor gene knockout T cells, and setting artificial nest device parameters and starting device for culture.
AU ZHAO K
   WANG J
   ZHANG J
   ZHU M
   SUN N
   ZHU T
   LIU B
   WAN J
   HUA Z
AE HUA Z (HUAZ-Individual)
GA 2022348479
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method for pituitary tissue three-dimensional (3D) cell culture fluid design and growth hormone involves (a) performing mixed cell design in accordance with the main cell types of human pituitary tissue and selecting human leukocyte antigen (HLA) gene and T cell receptor (TCR) gene knockout T cells, (b) using angiotensin, threonine, lysine, tryptophan, serine, lecithin, hyaluronic acid, folic acid, vitamin B6, vitamin B12, vitamin C, potassium ion, sodium ion, calcium ion, zinc ion, iron ion, magnesium ion and copper ion tissue to prepare cell culture nutrient solution, (c) setting artificial nest device parameters and (d) starting the circulation device (the assembly and culture parameter settings have been completed in advance) for mixed cell culture.
   USE - The preparation method is useful for preparing pituitary tissue 3D cell culture fluid design and growth hormone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids potential problems of exogenous DNA toxicity, transfection efficiency and expression efficiency in host cells during the process of preparing growth hormone by gene recombination and cytotoxicity caused by the ineffective balance of the cytokine network, hence is an effective improvement on the traditional method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation method for pituitary tissue three-dimensional (3D) cell culture fluid design and growth hormone involves (a) performing mixed cell design in accordance with the main cell types of human pituitary tissue and if T cells and other cell sources are not from the same donor, selecting human leukocyte antigen (HLA) gene and T cell receptor (TCR) gene knockout T cells, (b) using angiotensin, threonine, lysine, tryptophan, serine, lecithin, hyaluronic acid, folic acid, vitamin B6, vitamin B12, vitamin C, potassium ion, sodium ion, calcium ion, zinc ion, iron ion, magnesium ion and copper ion in accordance with the microenvironment of pituitary tissue to prepare cell culture nutrient solution and optionally adding traditional Chinese medicinal components such as: radix salviae miltiorrhizae and radix codonopsitis in accordance with different cell types and ratios, (c) setting artificial nest device parameters: pressure of 90-220 mmHg, pH of 7.35-7.45, temperature of 36.2-37.5degrees Celsius, oxygen content of (including dissolved oxygen and bound oxygen) 15-50 ml/100 ml culture medium and carbon dioxide content of (including dissolved state and combined state) of 30-80 ml/100 ml culture medium and (d) starting the circulation device (the assembly and culture parameter settings have been completed in advance) for mixed cell culture. The step (d) involves (i) ensuring that no nutrient solution is present in the spleen area before the operation of the equipment, opening the first control valve of the liver area so that the fresh nutrient solution is actively transported from the stomach area to the spleen area and reaches the minimum set value of the liquid level in the spleen area, the nutrient solution reaches the heart area (the first peristaltic pump) and enters the lung area driven by the power of the heart area (the first peristaltic pump), feeding the nutrient solution enters the stem cell nest after receiving the oxygen and carbon dioxide from the gas permeable membrane in the lung area, providing nutrients and oxygen to stem cells wrapped in a simulated extracellular matrix, powering the gas exchange by the second peristaltic pump in conjunction with the second control valve and providing oxygen and carbon dioxide by sterile air, (ii) allowing the nutrient solution to flow out from the stem cell nest through the third control valve and enters the spleen area, (iii) allowing a portion of the nutrient solution in the spleen area to enter the heart area with the fresh nutrient solution from the liver area and then enter the stem cell nest after passing through the lung area to supply nutrients and oxygen to the cells, where the other part is driven into the kidney region through the third peristaltic pump and return to the spleen region through the fourth control valve, a specific dialysis membrane is provided in the kidney area, which can filter out metabolic wastes such as urea generated during the growth of stem cells to avoid metabolic wastes affecting the growth of stem cells and feeding the dialysate into the kidney region through the fourth peristaltic pump and then discharging through the fifth control valve and (d) allowing the control platform to obtain relevant instructions from the cloud data center to control the cell culture, uploading the cell culture data to the cloud data center, where the control platform is also responsible for providing the patient's life field for future application of cell therapy for cell growth, maintaining the culture temperature of the system by a constant temperature system and monitoring the system state using a sensor, which is connected to the control platform through a data interface.

PN CN110226450-A; CN110226450-B
TI Edible mushroom factory formula, comprises bean straw, maize cob, rice bran, bran, beer grains, soybean hull, shell powder, light weight calcium carbonate, cottonseed hulls and beet pulp in specific weight parts.
AU SHANG D
   XIANG G
AE ZHAOQING LVJIANN AGRIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (ZHAO-Non-standard)
   XIANG G (XIAN-Individual)
GA 2019805878
AB    NOVELTY - An edible mushroom factory formula comprises 4-6 pts. wt. bean straw, 32-36 pts. wt. maize cob, 25-30 pts. wt. rice bran, 10-14 pts. wt. bran, 4-6 pts. wt. beer grains, 5-8 pts. wt. soybean hull, 1-3 pts. wt. shell powder, 1-3 pts. wt. light weight calcium carbonate, 6-8 pts. wt. cottonseed hulls and 5-7 pts. wt. beet pulp.
   USE - Edible mushroom factory formula.
   ADVANTAGE - The formula solves the problem of the price of raw materials for supplying nitrogen sources is rising and the supply shortage is beginning to appear in the market with the rapid development of the edible fungus factory by adding bean straw as a part of raw materials and improving the overall cost of industrialized cultivation of edible fungi.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An edible mushroom factory formula comprises 4-6 pts. wt. bean straw, 32-36 pts. wt. maize cob, 25-30 pts. wt. rice bran, 10-14 pts. wt. bran, 4-6 pts. wt. beer grains, 5-8 pts. wt. soybean hull, 1-3 pts. wt. shell powder, 1-3 pts. wt. light weight calcium carbonate, 6-8 pts. wt. cottonseed hulls and 5-7 pts. wt. beet pulp. The edible mushroom factory formula is prepared by mixing bean straw, maize cob, rice bran, bran, beer lees, soybean hulls, shell powder, light calcium carbonate, cottonseed hulls and beet pulp into a culture material according to a prescribed ratio. The culture material is subjected to feeding - dry mixing - wet mixing that the culture material is evenly mixed, where the moisture content of the test culture material is controlled at 67-69% and the pH is 6.2-6.8 to obtain the medium. The prepared medium is filled into the cultivation bottle by a fully automatic bottling machine, where the cultivation bottle capacity is 1200 ml and the loading amount of each bottle is 960-1050 g that the one basket per 16 cultivation bottles. The hole is punched in the middle and the periphery of the material through the automatic punching machine after the filling is completed. A total of 5 holes are formed and the hole extended to the bottom of the bottle, the cover is tightly covered by the automatic cover machine after the hole is punched. The cultivation basket containing the cultivation bottle is placed on the turnover car and pushed into a vacuum steam sterilizer for sterilization, where the sterilization temperature is 121 degrees C and the sterilization time is 90 minutes. The culture bottle is pushed into the cooling chamber for strong refrigeration after the sterilization is finished and the inoculum is inoculated when the temperature of the material is lowered to 15-23 degrees C, the liquid mushroom of the Flammulina velutipes is inserted into the cultivation bottle under the laminar flow cover by the automatic inoculation machine, where the inoculum size of the bottle is 25-35ml. The culture bottle is transferred to the culture chamber for mycelial culture after the inoculation at 13-15 degrees C, with air humidity 75-85%, light protection and better air circulation. The mycelium is overgrown after 22-24 days of culture. The cultivation bottle filled with mycelium is transferred into the sputum fungus workshop and the sputum sterilization treatment is carried out by an automatic sterilizing machine to remove the aged hyphae and at the same time form mechanical stimulation to promote the formation and differentiation of the primordium of the velutipes fruit body. The bacteria are finished and 10-15ml water is sprayed on the surface to keep the surface moist. The cultivation bottle is transferred to the breeding room and the bud treatment is carried out under the condition of room temperature is 15-17 degrees C, air humidity is 85-95% and carbon dioxide concentration is 3000-4000 ppm. The surface of the material is covered with mushroom buds, the mushroom buds are photo-suppressed and wind-suppressed and the carbon dioxide concentration is controlled at 3000-8000ppm to make the mushroom buds grow neatly. The plastic bag mushroom is wrapped around the bottle mouth to increase the carbon dioxide concentration in the film tube to 10000ppm or more when the mushroom bud grows out of the culture bottle mouth 1-2cm, which promotes the elongation of the mushroom stipe and appropriately illuminates according to the uniformity of the mushroom and harvested when the Flammulina velutipes grows to 16-17cm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The culture bottle is a polypropylene plastic culture bottle.

TI Enriching and purifying atmospheric xenon comprises e.g. removing moisture, oxygen, carbon dioxide and part of nitrogen in air, performing three-stage concentration on the treated gas and separating and purifying concentrated gas.
AU CHEN Z
   CHANG Y
   HEI D
   LIU S
AE BEIJING RADIONUCLIDE LAB (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   NORTHWEST INST NUCLEAR TECHNOLOGY (NWNT-C)
GA 201939797U
AB    NOVELTY - Enriching and purifying atmospheric xenon comprises e.g. (i) removing moisture, oxygen, carbon dioxide and part of nitrogen in the air using a membrane dryer and a membrane separator to initially concentrate the helium in the air, where membrane dryer adopts a hollow fiber water removal membrane module, and the membrane separator is connected in series and/or in parallel by a hollow fiber nitrogen-rich membrane module of the same type and/or different types; (ii) performing three-stage concentration on the treated gas and subjecting gas treated to three-stage normal temperature adsorption high-temperature desorption to achieve stepwise concentration; (iii) separating and purifying the concentrated gas to achieve efficient separation of xenon and radon; and (iv) collecting sample finally, adsorbing xenon gas flowing out, desorbing by fifth-stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, and collecting desorbed xenon.
   USE - The method is useful for enriching and purifying atmospheric xenon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes monitoring atmospheric radiation environment and safe operation of nuclear facilities; and produces not less than 5 ml xenon in 24 hours at volume ratio of more than 20%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enriching and purifying atmospheric xenon comprises (i) removing moisture, oxygen, carbon dioxide and part of nitrogen in the air using a membrane dryer and a membrane separator to initially concentrate the helium in the air, where membrane dryer adopts a hollow fiber water removal membrane module, and the membrane separator is connected in series and/or in parallel by a hollow fiber nitrogen-rich membrane module of the same type and/or different types and M is greater than or equal to to 2; (ii) performing three-stage concentration on the treated gas, where concentration process comprises three stages of carbon molecular sieve packed columns and subjecting gas treated to three-stage normal temperature adsorption high-temperature desorption to achieve stepwise concentration; (iii) separating and purifying the concentrated gas to achieve efficient separation of xenon and radon; and (iv) collecting sample finally, adsorbing xenon gas flowing out, desorbing by fifth-stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, and collecting desorbed xenon. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) atmospheric xenon enrichment and purification device, comprising a gas source system, A sampling unit and a B sampling unit, a purification source unit and a vacuum pump, where gas source system comprises a filter, a compressor, a membrane dryer, a membrane separator, and a buffer tank which are sequentially connected through a pipeline, where the membrane dryer uses a hollow fiber water removal membrane module, the membrane separator is connected and/or connected in series by M fibers of the same type and/or different types of hollow fiber nitrogen-rich membrane modules, M greater than or equal to 2, A sampling unit and the B sampling unit are arranged in parallel in parallel, and the A sampling unit and the B sampling unit both includes first stage carbon molecular sieve packed column and a second stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, the outlet of the first stage carbon molecular sieve packed column is connected to the inlet of the second stage carbon molecular sieve packed column through a pipe and a valve, the purification source unit comprises a third-stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, a fourth-stage molecular sieve packed column, a fifth-stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, a diaphragm pump and a sample source box which are sequentially connected in series through a pipeline and a valve, the outlet of the second stage carbon molecular sieve packed column is connected to the inlet of the third stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, the sample source box is connected to the vacuum pump through a valve and a four-way, the first stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, the second stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, the third stage carbon molecular sieve packed column, the fourth stage molecular sieve packed column, and the fifth stage carbon molecular sieve packed column are connected with an exhaust valve connected to the outside and the pipeline is provided with a mass flow controller for controlling the gas flow rate and a pressure sensor for measuring the pressure of the gas passage; and
   (2) preparing the carbon molecular sieve, comprising preparing a material, activating material, carrying out carbonization treatment and carbon deposition, where material activation and carbonization treatment comprises placing prepared material in a furnace, heating to 300 degrees C, introducing nitrogen gas, heating to 850?Oc at constant temperature for 60 minutes, charging the material and forming pores, using nitrogen to bring hydrocarbons or alcoholic organic compounds into the furnace, controlling carrier time of nitrogen is 1-2.5 hours, forming carbon deposits on the surface of the material to block holes and removing after cooling to normal temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hollow fiber water removal membrane module is a UM series water removal membrane of Ube UBE comprises UMS-B10 and UM-C10 and hollow fiber nitrogen-rich membrane module is NM-C05A, NM-B10A, NM-C07F, NM-C10F or NM-510F and deadsorbing gas after pretreatment in step (i) comprises using columns of parallel first-stage carbon molecular sieves to alternately adsorb and desorb the column in the first stage of concentration and using two columns of parallel second-stage carbon molecular sieves are used to alternately adsorb and desorb the columns in the second-stage concentration process. The carbon molecular sieve filled in the carbon molecular sieve packed column is a cylindrical particle, where column diameter is 1.3-1.8 mm, and the column height is 2.5 mm; carbon molecular sieve has a specific surface area of 380 m2/g , an apparent density of 0.6 g/cm3 , a micropore mean pore diameter of 0.5 nm (HK), and a micropore volume of 0.15 cm3/g and the membrane separator is sequentially connected in series using NM-510F, NM-C10F and NM-B10A. The hollow fiber water removal membrane module of membrane dryer, model IDG series water removal membrane of Japan SMC comprises IDG100 and IDG75 and hollow fiber nitrogen-rich membrane module comprises NM-C05A, NM-B10A, NM-C07F, NM-C10F or NM-510F. The carbon molecular sieve is filled in the carbon molecular sieve packed column is a cylindrical particle, the column diameter is 1.3-1.8 mm, and the column height is 2.5 mm, where carbon molecular sieve has a specific surface area of 380 m2/g , an apparent density of 0.6 g/cm3 , a micropore mean pore diameter of 0.5 nm (HK), and a micropore volume of 0.15 cm3/g. Preferred Method: The method comprises using first stage carbon molecular sieve to desorb the column, controlling gas flow rate at 900-1000 ml/min, and the temperature of the first stage carbon molecular sieve packed column to 260-300 degrees C, using second stage carbon molecular sieve to desorb the column, controlling gas flow rate to 40-50 ml/min, and controlling temperature of the second stage carbon molecular sieve packed column to 260-300 degrees C, desorbing column by the third-stage carbon molecular sieve and controlling gas flow rate at 30-35 ml/min, and the temperature of the third-stage carbon molecular sieve packed column to 260-300 degrees C and using fifth stage carbon molecular sieve to solve the packed column suction and controlling gas flow rate at 10-20 ml/min, and the temperature of the fifth stage carbon molecular sieve packed column to 180-300 degrees C.
