PN US2021016216-A1
TI Holistic protection and virus transmission            suppression, by using synergy between enclosure            infrastructure and individual protective systems that            e.g. relies on keeping clean safe air and has portable            system of control and data acquisition.
AU POPA-SIMIL V
   POPA-SIMIL I L
   POPA-SIMIL A
   POPA-SIMIL L
AE POPA-SIMIL V (POPA-Individual)
   POPA-SIMIL I L (POPA-Individual)
   POPA-SIMIL A (POPA-Individual)
   POPA-SIMIL L (POPA-Individual)
GA 202108638X
AB    NOVELTY - Holistic protection and virus transmission                suppression, involves using synergy between                enclosure infrastructure and individual protective                systems that: (a) relies on keeping clean safe air                to breathe above, the wrist and pushing the exhaled                air, potentially contaminated down at the foot                level; (b) is using a modified building HVAC                system; (c) uses an additional vacuumed drain                system deployed on the floor level; (d) relies on                an individual modular protection system; (e) has a                portable system of control and data acquisition for                quality assurance, and communication comprising:                (i) electronics box-Wi-Fi; (ii) dynamic pressure                control loops for inhalation and exhalation; (iii)                microphone, thermometer; (iv) exhalation sensors                (temperature; airflow speed, humidity, and carbon                dioxide (CO2)); (v) RFID labels on exhaust tube;                and (vi) direct display on mask of check                parameters; and (f) has a fixed system, at                enclosure level to check presence and protection                equipment operation status.
   USE - The method is useful for holistic protection                and virus transmission suppression (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the safety of each person                by separating the air spaces and destroying all                organic matter by a combination of technologies and                a redundant number of layers, which finally                collects all fluid effluents emitted by a person                and sterilizes them continuously maintaining the                person in a fresh, safe environment, and cleaning                even more in-depth the environment for the next                person. It is intended to prevent the spread of                COVID-19 and other viruses while also being as                comfortable as possible for users, but most of all                be redundant, fail safe and assure a high level of                protection. The work to keep people inside safe is                done by both the enclosure that may provide vacuum                lines to collect the exhaled air, and by the people                who will use correctly the infrastructure. The                system is designed for smart communities for                everybody to wear for about 3 weeks when a covid-19                or other virus infection occurs to suppress its                multiplication rate, without perturbation in their                activity and then returning to normality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Holistic protection and virus transmission                suppression, involves using synergy between                enclosure infrastructure and individual protective                systems that: (a) relies on keeping clean safe air                to breathe above, the wrist and pushing the exhaled                air, potentially contaminated down at the foot                level; (b) is using a modified building HVAC system                that: (i) has the clean air coming from ceiling and                the return air being collected near the flow; (ii)                has a UV-C sterilization unit embedded into the                return duct; (iii) uses chemical and                anti-bacteriological filters to stop and kill                viruses and germs; (c) uses an additional vacuumed                drain system deployed on the floor level that: (i)                directly collects the exhaled air from people via a                exhaled air tube connected to the drain tube; (ii)                collects exhaled air near floor by the mobile                people that cannot plug their exhaled air tube into                a fixed drain tube connector; (iii) uses RFID                technology to identify who is in proximity and if                it is connected or not and transmits it by Wi-Fi to                a computer; (d) relies on an individual modular                protection system comprising: (i) top air intake                modulus containing: (I) Primary solid particulate                pre-filter protection grid; (II) coarse hydrophobic                filter; (III) chemical and sterilization filter;                (IV) hydrophilic filter-desiccator; (V) UV                reflective chamber with bio-active wall; (VI) UV-C                light source; (VII) fan and batteries with                electronic control; (ii) inhalation air preparation                modulus, containing inside: (I) intake HEPA filter;                (II) carbon dioxide stopper; (III) air preparation                filter with medicine applicator for nasal inhalers;                (IV) ultrasonic water nebulizer, humidity control,                fan and batteries; (V) compressed air or oxygen                port; (iii) transparent mask with sense valves                comprising: (I) transparent mask; (II) air intake                connection tube with one-way air valve; (III) straw                hole with lid for liquid sipping via a straw; (IV)                exhaling airflow, connection tube with one-way air                valve; (V) electronics box, with display and Wi-Fi                communication; (VI) tube for air to protective                glasses; (iv) exhaled air cleaning modulus,                containing inside: (I) desiccator and hydrophilic                filter; (II) chemical and anti-bacteriological                filter; (III) supplementary active charcoal filter;                (IV) UV-C light sources; (V) heat exchanger; (e)                has a portable system of control and data                acquisition for quality assurance, and                communication comprising: (i) electronics                box-Wi-Fi; (ii) dynamic pressure control loops for                inhalation and exhalation; (iii) microphone,                thermometer; (iv) exhalation sensors (temperature;                airflow speed, humidity, and carbon dioxide (CO2));                (v) RFID labels on exhaust tube; and (vi) direct                display on mask of check parameters; and (f) has a                fixed system, at enclosure level to check presence                and protection equipment operation status. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a protection                system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method has its quality                  assurance procedures that have to be enforced and                  aimed for maximum protection in order to leave                  room for reasonable failures that may occur, and                  fail safe. It requires a downwards air flow                  imposed inside enclosures to assure protection;                  requires exhaled air sterilization as a                  bio-hazard before being dumped in environment or                  recirculated; or considers that there is no                  absolute protection and favors complementary                  measures to stop virus propagation, as covering                  exposed skin, tests and quarantines, to eliminate                  the community hazard in short time, keeping                  everybody safe and not interrupting or disturb                  their activities. Preferred System: The system is                  modular, distributed and has to be used by the                  entire community simultaneously for a short                  period of time, immediately after a pandemic                  source was identified, until the threat is                  removed. It relies on intense cooperation between                  people, wearable technology and enclosure                  technology in order to produce synergy. It has                  independent protective structures, where the                  total failure has a small probability, being                  mainly the result of a conscientious                  self-sabotage action, that will be immediately                  detected by the monitoring systems and corrective                  action triggered. It may be used for open space                  protection too, by simply using the mask and the                  two air-tubes correctly placed. The protection                  system uses the fact the virus is traveling in                  watery droplets, able to locally dissolve and                  react with salts and other chemicals that kill                  the virus, working in synergy with UV-C light,                  and then seals the virus remains after water                  evaporates and chemical re-crystalizes.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN111097340-A
TI Coupling reactor useful for preparing high crystallinity alumina dry adhesive, comprises liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone set with tower plate, liquid collecting plate, plate hole and gas cylinder, and rotational flow settling zone.
AU PENG C
   FANG X
   SUN S
   CAO Z
   DU Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
GA 202041615N
AB    NOVELTY - Coupling reactor comprises liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone, rotational flow settling zone and liquid-liquid collision reaction zone. The liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone is provided with tower plate, liquid collecting plate, plate hole, gas cylinder, mesh plate, cap and downcomer pipe. The gas cylinder is fixed on liquid collecting plate. The position of air cylinder corresponds to position of plate hole. The mesh plate is provided on gas cylinder, and a cap is provided on top of gas cylinder. The liquid collecting plate is set with lower end of air cylinder and downcomer pipe. The rotational flow settling zone is set below the liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone. The liquid-liquid collision reaction zone is provided below the rotational flow settling zone. The top of liquid-liquid collision reaction zone is directly connected to bottom of the rotational flow settling zone, and the liquid-liquid collision reaction zone is set with 1-3 pairs of accelerating tubes.
   USE - The coupling reactor is useful for preparing high crystallinity alumina dry adhesive (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The coupling reactor: enhances the transfer characteristics of the gas-liquid two-phase and liquid-liquid two-phase. The dry adhesive: has high crystallinity, complete crystal grains and concentrated particle size distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coupling reactor comprises liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone, rotational flow settling zone and liquid-liquid collision reaction zone. The liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone is provided with tower plate, liquid collecting plate, plate hole, gas cylinder, mesh plate, cap and downcomer pipe. The gas cylinder is fixed on liquid collecting plate. The position of air cylinder corresponds to the position of plate hole. The mesh plate is provided on the gas cylinder, and a cap is provided on the top of the gas cylinder. The liquid collecting plate is connected with lower end of air cylinder and downcomer pipe. The rotational flow settling zone is set below the liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone. The liquid-liquid collision reaction zone is provided below the rotational flow settling zone. The top of liquid-liquid collision reaction zone is directly connected to the bottom of the rotational flow settling zone, and the liquid-liquid collision reaction zone is provided with 1-3 pairs of accelerating tubes on the side walls symmetrically on both sides of the top. One end of the acceleration tube is used as the inlet or air inlet, and the other end is used as the outlet or outlet to extend into the gas-liquid reaction zone. A pressure atomizing nozzle is installed at the outlet to atomize the liquid, and a porous hole is installed at the outlet Gas nozzle. The liquid-liquid collision reaction zone further comprises a precipitating cylinder and a stirring paddle. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) using the coupling reactor for preparing high crystallinity alumina dry adhesive, comprising (i) adding reaction liquid at the bottom of the coupled reactor, heating to a specific temperature, and starting the bottom stirring paddle, (ii) introducing aqueous solution of alkali metal aluminate into the reactor from the top of the coupled reactor and carbon dioxide gas stream from the upper part of the coupled reactor, (iii) contacting alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution by carbon dioxide gas flowing upwardly in liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone, spraying alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution by carbon dioxide gas into fine droplets through the gas cylinder to generate aluminum hydroxide crystal nucleus, (iv) precipitating aluminum hydroxide crystal nucleus obtained in step (iii) into the rotational flow settling zone, (v) passing materials through rotational flow settling zone, simultaneously adding acidic aluminum salt aqueous solution and alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution or alkaline precipitant simultaneously with the feed ports at the lower end of the reactor to form three impinging streams, adjusting pH value, and carrying out neutralization reaction, (vi) impinging three streams of liquid on the reaction stream, reacting, entering the free precipitating zone, and completing the stable phase of the neutralization reaction, and (vii) aging the reaction material obtained in the step (vi), filtering, washing and drying to obtain the product; and
   (2) alumina dry adhesive prepared using above method, where the crystallinity of alumina dry adhesive is greater than or equal to 90%, the maximum particle size range of alumina dry adhesive is 70-80 mu m, and the minimum particle size range is 1-5 mu m, and in the particle size distribution, the proportion of less than 20 mu m particle size is 85-90%, more than 20-40 mu m particle size is 8-13%, and more than 40 mu m particle size is less than 2%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the coupling reactor is useful for preparing high crystallinity alumina dry adhesive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Preferred Method: In the step (i), the heating temperature is 30-120 degrees C, and the rotation speed of the stirring paddle is 1000-3000 revolutions per minute. In the step (ii), taking alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution from sodium aluminate or potassium aluminate aqueous solution, and the concentration of the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is 8-100 g/l in terms of aluminum oxide, and the flow rate of the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is 5-30 l/hour and flow rate of the carbon dioxide is 1-30 l/hour. In the step (vii), aging pH range is 8-10, the stabilization time range is 5-20 minutes, and the aging temperature range is 70-160 degrees C and aging time is 0.5-2 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (iii) the number of components of the liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone is 3-10. In the step (v), the acidic aluminum salt aqueous solution is an aqueous solution of aluminum chloride, aluminum sulfate or aluminum nitrate. The alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is sodium aluminate or potassium aluminate aqueous solution. The alkaline precipitant is sodium hydroxide, ammonium carbonate or ammonium hydroxide aqueous solution. The concentration of the acidic aluminum salt solution in terms of aluminum oxide is 5-20 g/100 ml. The concentration of the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is 10-35 g/100 ml in terms of aluminum oxide. The pH range is 6-8. The time of the neutralization reaction is 6-25 minutes. The flow rate of the acidic aluminum salt aqueous solution and the alkali metal aluminate aqueous solution is 0.5-1.5 l/minute. The crystallinity of alumina adhesive is 93-98%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The number of layers of mesh plates provided on each gas cylinder in the liquid-solid mass transfer reaction zone is one layer. The shape of the mesh plate corresponds to the shape of the cap, and the mesh structure is a circular hole, a strip hole or a diamond hole. The downward baffles are provided around the mesh plate, and the angle between the baffle plate and the mesh plate is 15-60 degrees . The gas cylinder is in the shape of a cuboid, a cube, a straight cylinder, or a cone bottom structure. The gas cylinder has multiple holes on the wall of the cylinder above the liquid collecting plate, and the opening rate is larger than the tower plate. The opening rate of the cylinder wall of the gas cylinder/plate opening rate is more than 1.2.

PN CN110921099-A; WO2021103519-A1; CN110921099-B; US2022282037-A1
TI Preparation of self-supporting bag in food            packaging involves using e.g. poly(lactic-co-glycolic            acid) copolymer, polybutylene succinate,            poly(N-propionylethyleneimine), phthalic anhydride,            erucamide, nano silicon dioxide, and white oil.
AU SI P
   LIU Z
   WU W
   LIANG P
   LI G
AE JIANGSU JINZHIHONG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU GOLDEN RAINBOW NEW MATERIAL CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU GOLDEN RAINBOW NEW MATERIAL CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202027076S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a high-barrier biodegradable                self-supporting bag involves: (1) preparing a                high-barrier biodegradable self-sealing seal by                (1.1) preparing raw material formula comprising                5-10 pts. wt. poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA)                copolymer, 60-70 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based                fully degradable plastic, 20-30 pts. wt.                polybutylene succinate (PBS), 1 pt. wt.                poly(N-propionylethyleneimine), 0.5 pt. wt.                phthalic anhydride, 0.2 pt. wt. erucamide, 0.5 pt.                wt. nano silicon dioxide, and 0.3 pt. wt. white                oil, (1.2) evenly mixing the above-mentioned                components, then adding to a parallel twin-screw                extruder, melt blending, cooling and pelletizing to                prepare a high-barrier biodegradable self-sealing                modified materials, and (1.3) adding the modified                materials prepared in step (1.2) to a bone extruder                for melt extrusion, water-cooling and shaping, and                then producing a high-barrier biodegradable                self-sealing tape; and (2) preparing a five-layer                composite high-barrier biodegradable film                roll.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a                high-barrier biodegradable self-supporting bag,                which is useful in high-barrier food                packaging.
   ADVANTAGE - The biodegradable self-supporting bag has                excellent physical and mechanical properties,                barrier performance and heat-sealing                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a high-barrier biodegradable                self-supporting bag involves: (1) preparing a                high-barrier biodegradable self-sealing seal by                (1.1) preparing raw material formula comprising                5-10 pts. wt. poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA)                copolymer, 60-70 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based                fully degradable plastic (PPC), 20-30 pts. wt.                polybutylene succinate (PBS), 1 pt. wt.                poly(N-propionylethyleneimine), 0.5 pt. wt.                phthalic anhydride, 0.2 pt. wt. erucamide, 0.5 pt.                wt. nano silicon dioxide, and 0.3 pt. wt. white                oil, (1.2) evenly mixing PLGA, PPC, PBS,                poly(N-propionylethyleneimine), phthalic anhydride,                erucamide, nano-silicon dioxide and white oil, then                adding to a parallel twin-screw extruder, melt                blending, air cooling and pelletizing to prepare a                high-barrier biodegradable self-sealing modified                materials, and (1.3) adding the high-barrier                biodegradable self-sealing modified materials                prepared in step (1.2) to a bone extruder for melt                extrusion, water-cooling and shaping, and then                producing a high-barrier biodegradable self-sealing                tape; (2) preparing a five-layer composite                high-barrier biodegradable film roll by (2.1)                uniformly mixing polybutylene adipate terephthalate                (PBAT), organically modified montmorillonite, HyPer                C181 (RTM: Hyperbranched polyester) and white oil,                adding to the parallel twin-screw extruder, melting                and blending, and carrying out air-cooling and                pelletizing to prepare a bonding layer modified                material, (2.2) uniformly mixing polyglycolic acid                (PGA), acetyl tributyl citrate, polyvinyl acetate                and tris(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)phosphite, then                adding to the parallel twin-screw extruder, and                carrying out melt blending, air-cooling and                pelletizing to prepare a barrier layer modification                material (A), (2.3) uniformly mixing the carbon                dioxide-based fully biodegradation plastic, maleic                anhydride, pentaerythritol                tetrakis(3-(3',5'-di-tert-butyl-4'-hydroxyphenyl)propionate),                HyPer C100 (RTM: Hyperbranched polyester) and white                oil, adding the mixed mixture to the parallel                twin-screw extruder, and carrying out melt                blending, air-cooling and pelletizing to prepare a                barrier layer modification material (B), and (2.4)                combining the modified materials prepared in steps                (2.1), (2.2) and (2.3) with PBS, adding the four                materials to four extruders corresponding to the                four-layer co-extrusion casting machine, starting                four extruders in sequence, preparing four layers                of the co-extruded cast films with different                thicknesses by adjusting the speed of each                extruder, where the film is a heat seal layer, a                barrier layer (B), a barrier layer (A) and a                bonding layer in order from top to bottom, after                the melt flow is stable, coating the film on a                single-gloss white kraft paper, contacting the                rough side of the single-gloss white kraft paper                with the adhesive layer of the melt, cutting the                edge into a five-layer composite high-barrier                biodegradable film roll, and using, to prepare                high-barrier biodegradable bags, a printing layer,                the adhesive layer, the barrier layers (A) and (B)                and the heat seal layer from outside to inside; and                (3) using the high-barrier biodegradable                self-sealing strip prepared in step (1.3) and the                five-layer composite high-barrier biodegradable                film roll prepared in step (2.4) to prepare the bag                through a bone-sticking three-edge sealing bag                mechanism. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                high-barrier biodegradable self-supporting bag                prepared by the method, which comprises                high-barrier biodegradable self-sealing strip, and                a five-layer composite high-barrier biodegradable                bag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The PLGA in step (1.1)                  has -OCH2CO- repeating unit of 80-90%, and a                  weight average molecular weight of 100000-200000.                  The weight average molecular weight of the PGA in                  step (2.2) is 150000-300000, and the residual                  amount of glycolide is not greater than 0.3 wt.%.                  In the cast film extruded by the extruder in step                  (2.4), the thickness of the adhesive layer is                  5-10 mu m. The thickness of the barrier layer (A)                  is 20-40 mu m. The thickness of the barrier layer                  (B) is 20-40 mu m. Preferred Components: In step                  (2.1), the raw material formula used for                  preparing the adhesive layer modified material                  comprises 92 pts. wt. PBAT, 7 pts. wt.                  organically modified montmorillonite, 1 pt. wt.                  HyPer C181 (RTM: Hyperbranched polyester), and                  0.3 pt. wt. white oil. In step (2.2), the raw                  material used for preparing the barrier layer                  modified material (A) comprises 90 pts. wt. PGA,                  5 pts. wt. acetyl tributyl citrate, 5 pts. wt.                  polyvinyl acetate, and 0.5 pt. wt.                  (2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)phosphite. In step                  (2.3), the raw material used for preparing the                  barrier layer modified material (B) comprises 100                  pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based fully degradable                  plastic, 0.5 pt. wt. maleic anhydride, 0.3 pt.                  wt. pentaerythritol                  tetrakis(3-(3',5'-di-tert-butyl-4'-hydroxyphenyl)propionate),                  0.5 pt. wt. HyPer C100 (RTM: Hyperbranched                  polyester), and 0.3 pt. wt. white oil.

PN CN110615579-A
TI Treating biogas slurry produced after anaerobic digestion of organic matter involves passing biogas slurry from original pool into coagulation sedimentation tank, and adding coagulant and coagulant to coagulation sedimentation tank.
AU JIANG L
   CAO B
   YANG J
   LIU D
AE JIANGSU HONGRUN BIOMASS ENERGY TECHNOLOG (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202003547A
AB    NOVELTY - Treating biogas slurry produced after anaerobic digestion of organic matter involves passing the biogas slurry from the original pool into the coagulation sedimentation tank, and adding coagulant and coagulant to the coagulation sedimentation tank through the dosing equipment, after the rapid mixing and reaction is completed, solid-liquid separation is performed. The supernatant biogas slurry is sent to the catchment tank for subsequent treatment, and chemical sludge containing pollutants and chemicals is discharged after biochemical treatment reaches the standard. The obtained supernatant biogas slurry is sent from the catchment tank to the catchment tank. The raw biogas slurry is pumped into the preheater through the raw water lift pump, and the superbiogas slurry is preheated and heated by using a heat source in the preheater.
   USE - Method for treating biogas slurry produced after anaerobic digestion of organic matter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat biogas slurry produced after anaerobic digestion of organic matter is improves the efficiency and effectiveness of subsequent biochemical treatments, removes biochemical organic matter and ammonia nitrogen from biogas slurry to ensure the quality of the effluent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating biogas slurry produced after anaerobic digestion of organic matter involves passing the biogas slurry from the original pool into the coagulation sedimentation tank, and adding coagulant and coagulant to the coagulation sedimentation tank through the dosing equipment, after the rapid mixing and reaction is completed, solid-liquid separation is performed. The supernatant biogas slurry is sent to the catchment tank for subsequent treatment, and chemical sludge containing pollutants and chemicals is discharged after biochemical treatment reaches the standard. The obtained supernatant biogas slurry is sent from the catchment tank to the catchment tank. The raw biogas slurry is pumped into the preheater through the raw water lift pump, and the superbiogas slurry is preheated and heated by using a heat source in the preheater. The preheated and warmed up biogas slurry is injected into the analytical reaction tower, and the temperature range in the analytical reaction tower is maintained at 65-70 degrees C to analyze the carbon dioxide in the supernatant biogas slurry and calcium and magnesium ions in the wastewater. The analyzed supernatant liquid is sent to the stripping deamination tower, and saturated steam is charged in the stripping deamination tower. The analyzed supernatant liquid is from top to bottom, and the ammonia gas in the methane liquid is removed, and then flowed into the bottom of the tower, and then entered the outlet pool after being cooled by the cryocooler. The biogas slurry flowing out of the stripping deammonia tower is sent to the anoxic zone, and the denitrification and denitrification reaction is performed in the hypoxic zone. The biogas slurry entering the hypoxic zone is mixed with the returning sludge in the sedimentation tank to form The reflux mixed liquid, the denitrifying bacteria in the reflux sludge use organic matter entering the biogas slurry as a carbon source, and reduce a large amount of nitrate nitrogen in the reflux mixed liquid to nitrogen. The refluxing mixed solution enters the aerobic zone for biological oxidation of organics, ammoniation of organic nitrogen, and nitrification of ammonia nitrogen. The sedimentation tank is provided behind the aerobic zone. The ammonia gas stripped by the stripping deamination tower is sent to the condenser. The small amount of ammonia gas is condensed and returned to the stripping deamination tower through the gas-liquid separation tank. The large amount of ammonia gas is sent to the carbonization recovery tower through an ammonia pumping mixer through a gas-liquid separation tank, and a small amount of carbon dioxide is added to the carbonization recovery tower through a carbon dioxide storage tank to generate ammonium bicarbonate solution. The concentration of ammonium bicarbonate solution becomes higher and higher. The ammonium bicarbonate crystals precipitate to form a mixed solution of ammonium carbonate brine. The mixed solution is driven into the crystal slurry tank through a discharge pump to be buffered, and then sent to a centrifuge to separate the mother liquor, and then the packaging equipment is used to package and store the ammonium bicarbonate crystals in the warehouse. The non-condensable gas in the carbonization recovery tower enters the exhaust gas recovery tower, and is then sent to the exhaust gas evolution tower through the induced draft fan, and is discharged in the exhaust gas purification tower through an acid addition device to deodorize and purify it at high altitude. The exhaust suction hood is installed above the slurry tank, centrifuge and packaging equipment, and the exhaust gas generated in the slurry tank, centrifuge and packaging equipment is introduced into the exhaust gas purification tower with an induced draft fan and discharged at high altitude after being deodorized and purified by the acid adding device in the exhaust gas purification tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The coagulant is polyaluminum chloride (PAC), and the coagulant is polyacrylamide (PAM). The ratio of the dosing mass of the coagulant to the volume of the biogas slurry is 1000-2000 milligram per litre, and the ratio of the dosing mass of the coagulant to the volume of biogas slurry is 10-50 milligram per litre.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

AB    NOVELTY - Application of human leukemia cells in antitumor agent involves suspending human leukemia cell line THP-1 in sterile fetal calf serum RPMI 1640 medium, culturing, and centrifuging; dissolving o-topolin riboside (oTR), dichloroacetate sodium (DCA), and fludarabine (Flu) with dimethylsulfoxide, dissolving with sterile RPMI 1640 medium, dissolving cytarabine (Ara-C) with sterile RPMI 1640 medium, filtering, and storing; plating THP-1 cells in 96-well plates containing RPMI 1640 medium, setting 18 experiments groups, adding drug to each group, incubating, adding cell counting kit-8 solution, incubating, and measuring absorbance; and treating THP-1 cells in with drugs, taking THP-1 cells in 6-well plates containing RPMI 1640 medium, setting 9 experiments groups, incubating, diluting binding buffer with water, washing samples, suspending with staining buffer, adding annexin V-FITC, incubating, mixing with propidium iodide, upflow cytometry analyzing, and calculating apoptosis rate.
   USE - Method for applying human leukemia cells in antitumor agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has very significant in vitro proliferation inhibitory activity against human tumors in 9 tumor lines of 60 tumors and strong drug sensitivity of leukemia cell lines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Application of human leukemia cells in antitumor agent comprises:
   (A) suspending human leukemia cell line THP-1 in sterile inactivated fetal calf serum RPMI 1640 medium, culturing with 5% carbon dioxide at relative humidity of 90% and temperature of 37 degrees C for 48 hours, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, and discarding supernatant to get cell culture;
   (B) dissolving o-topolin riboside (oTR), dichloroacetate sodium (DCA), and fludarabine (Flu) with 0.1% dimethylsulfoxide, dissolving with sterile RPMI 1640 medium to concentration of 0.01-50 mu L, dissolving cytarabine (Ara-C) with sterile RPMI 1640 culture group to concentration of 0.01-50 mu M, filtering, and storing at room temperature;
   (C) plating 5x 103-1x 104 THP-1 cells in logarithmic growth phase in 96-well plates containing 100 mu L RPMI 1640 medium and setting 18 groups of experiments having 3 duplicates in each group, where group 1 is blank control group, group 2 is 0.1% dimethylsulfoxide group, group 3 is 0.01 mu M oTR group, group 4 is 0.1 mu M oTR group, group 5 is 1 mu M oTR group, group 6 is 10 mu M oTR group, group 7 is 0.01 mu M Ara-C group, group 8 is 0.1 mu M Ara-C group, group 9 is 1 mu M Ara-C group, group 10 is 10 mu M Ara-C group, group 11 is 0.01 mu M DCA group, group 12 is 0.1 mu M DCA group, group 13 is 1 mu M DCA group, group 14 is 10 mu M DCA group, group 15 is 0.01 mu M Flu group, group 17 is 0.1 mu M Flu, group 17 is 1 mu M Flu group, and group 18 is 10 mu M Flu group, adding corresponding drug to each group, incubating with 5% carbon dioxide to relative humidity of 90% at 37 degrees C for 1-3 days, adding 10 mu L cell counting kit-8 solution to each well, incubating for 2.5 hours, and measuring absorbance at 450 nm to obtain cell proliferation inhibition rate; and
   (D) treating THP-1 cells in logarithmic growth phase with drugs oTR, Ara-C, DCA, and Flu, taking 1-3x 105 units THP-1 cells in 6-well plates containing 3 mL RPMI 1640 medium, setting 9 groups of experiments, where group 1 is blank control group, group 2 is 0.1 mu M oTR group, group 3 is 1 mu M oTR group, group 4 is 0.1 mu M Ara-C group, group 5 is 1 mu M Ara-C group, group 6 is 0.1 mu M DCA group, group 7 is 1 mu M DCA group, group 8 is 0.1 mu M Flu group, and group 9 is 1 mu M Flu group, adding each group with 5% carbon dioxide, incubating at relative humidity of 90% and temperature of 37 degrees C for 1-3 days, preparing staining buffer according to annexin V-FITC apoptosis detection kit instructions, diluting 4 times binding buffer to 1 times binding buffer with distilled water, washing control samples and experimental cells with phosphate-buffered saline, suspending each group with 195 mu L diluted staining buffer, adding 5 mu L annexin V-FITC, incubating at room temperature for 10-15 minutes, washing, resuspending with 200 mu L diluted staining buffer, adding 10 mu L propidium iodide, mixing, performing upflow cytometry analysis within 4 hours for 3 times, and calculating apoptosis rate.
TF ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

AB    NOVELTY - Culture method of high proliferation ability                and high cytotoxicity natural killer (NK) cell                involves coating anti-human cluster of                differentiation (CD)16 monoclonal antibody with a                concentration of 0.1-2 mg/ml in a cell culture                flask, where the coating condition is                25&#176;C, and the incubation time is 1-5                hours, obtaining the processed cell culture bottle,                adding NK cell culture medium, centrifuging and                obtaining the single nuclear cell from peripheral                blood into the cell culture bottle, adjusting the                cell density to 1-2&#215;106/ml, where the NK                cell culture medium comprises basic culture                solution and activating culture solution, the                adding amount of the activating culture solution is                3-5% of the basic culture solution, continuously                culturing the cell culture bottle in incubator of                carbon dioxide with concentration of 2% at                37&#176;C for 9-15 days, and adding 2-100                &#956;g/ml interleukin (IL)-2 for every 3                days.
   USE - The method is useful for culture of high                proliferation ability and high cytotoxicity NK cell                which is used for treating tumor, infection or                immune system disease (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to culture NK cells with                high purity, high amplification efficiency and high                cytotoxicity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culture method of high proliferation ability                and high cytotoxicity natural killer (NK) cell                involves coating anti-human cluster of                differentiation (CD)16 monoclonal antibody with a                concentration of 0.1-2 mg/ml in a cell culture                flask, where the coating condition is                25&#176;C, and the incubation time is 1-5                hours, obtaining the processed cell culture bottle,                adding NK cell culture medium, centrifuging and                obtaining the single nuclear cell from peripheral                blood into the cell culture bottle, adjusting the                cell density to 1-2&#215;106/ml, where the NK                cell culture medium comprises basic culture                solution and activating culture solution, the                adding amount of the activating culture solution is                3-5% of the basic culture solution, and the                activating culture solution comprises 1.2-1.5 g/l                human serum albumin, 1.5-2 g/l human serum protein,                120-160 mg/l vitamin C, 220-240 mg/l vitamin E,                120-150 mg/l coenzyme A, 0.5-5 mg/l recombinant                human insulin, 280-330 mg/l L-glutamine, 420-530                &#181;g/ml di-mercaptoethanol, 20-30                &#181;g/ml indomethacin, 0.1-10 g/ml sodium                ion, 50-100 ng/ml monoclonal antibody CD2, 50-100                ng/ml NKp46 antibody, 0.01-0.06 KE/ml OK432 and                2-100 &#181;g/ml cytokine interleukin (IL)-2,                continuously culturing the cell culture bottle in                incubator of carbon dioxide with concentration of                2% at 37&#176;C for 9-15 days, and adding 2-100                &#956;g/ml IL-2 for every 3 days. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for NK cell, which is                prepared by the culture method.

PN CN114456875-A
TI Preparing Camellia oil comprises cleaning and removing impurities from Camellia seeds, taking out the Camellia seeds and drying, storing, performing steam distillation of secondary hair tea oil, slowly cooling, performing solid fat crystallization, and static pressure filtration.
AU HU Y
   WANG P
   ZHOU X
AE ANHUI SHENGSHI BAICHUAN AGRIC & FORESTRY TECHNOLOGY DEV CO L (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202273739V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Camellia oil comprises (i) selecting Camellia oleifera seeds with fresh and high-quality seeds with large grain size, plumpness, dark brown seed shell, no disease spots and gloss, (ii) cleaning and removing impurities from camellia seeds, taking out the Camellia seeds and drying, and storing, (iii) sun-drying and shelling the Camellia oil seeds of step (ii) until the kernel and the shell, and stirring-frying the clean tea seeds, (iv) grinding the fried tea seeds through a grinder, mixing powdered tea seed with purified water, (v) shaking the powdered tea seeds mixed with water with a shaker, placing the fully shaken powdered tea seeds in the centrifuge for centrifuging, (vi) placing primary tea oil into supercritical equipment, feeding carbon dioxide, extracting and separating into secondary tea oil, and (vii) performing steam distillation of secondary hair tea oil, slowly cooling, performing solid fat crystallization, and static pressure filtration.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Camellia oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has light yellow color, bright, pure fragrance, good taste, and unique flavor; and can enhance appetite; has high oil yield; reduces the production cost; has simple production method, practical and high productivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Camellia oil comprises (i) selecting Camellia oleifera seeds with fresh and high-quality seeds with large grain size, plumpness, dark brown seed shell, no disease spots and gloss, (ii) cleaning and removing impurities from camellia seeds, drying and crushing the shell of the high-quality camellia seed cones selected in the step (i), taking out the Camellia seeds and drying, and storing them in a low temperature and low humidity environment, using the biochemical principle of plant seeds' own post-ripening to promote the after-ripening of Camellia oleifera seeds and increase the oil yield, (iii) sun-drying and shelling the Camellia oil seeds of step (ii) until the kernel and the shell are separated to obtain clean tea seeds, and stirring-frying the clean tea seeds to obtain the fried tea seed, (iv) grinding the fried tea seeds through a grinder to obtain powdered tea seed, mixing powdered tea seed with purified water to obtain the powdered tea seed mixed with water, (v) shaking the powdered tea seeds mixed with water with a shaker, placing the fully shaken powdered tea seeds in the centrifuge for centrifuging, making the pulp residue of tea seed, layering Camellia oil and purified water, absorbing and collecting the clear oil layer of Camellia oil to obtain primary Camellia oil, (vi) placing primary tea oil into supercritical equipment, feeding carbon dioxide, extracting and separating into secondary tea oil, and (vii) performing steam distillation of secondary hair tea oil, slowly cooling, performing solid fat crystallization, and static pressure filtration to obtain essential tea oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), storing in an environment of 0-10degreesC low temperature and 5-10% low humidity. In the step (iii), the amount of tea seed husks contained in the tea seed kernel is less than or equal to 1 wt.%. In the step (iv), mixing the powdered tea seeds with purified water of 1:(2-5) by volume to obtain powdered tea seeds mixed with water. In the step (v), when the oscillator is working, it is 160 revolutions/minutes, oil extraction time is 150 minutes, and the water temperature is 75degreesC when the oscillator was oscillating. In the step (v), the operating time of the centrifuge is 4000 revolutions/minute, and the centrifugation time is 10 minutes. In the step (vi), introducing carbon dioxide of 20-30 MPa, the flow rate is 5-10 l/minute, the extraction temperature is 50-55degreesC, and after extraction for 2.5 hours, adjusting the separation pressure to be 15-20 MPa, and the secondary camellia oil is separated. In the step (vii), the secondary hair tea oil is steam distilled to make the free fatty acid in the secondary hair tea oil less than 0.5% under the condition of 230-240degreesC and the vacuum greater than 200 Pa, steam distilling, slow cooling to 4-5degreesC, performing solid fat crystallization, static pressure filtration to obtain essential camellia oil.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

TI Multifunctional self-adhesive label comprises            adhesive layer, which located between surface layer and            bottom paper layer, and surface layer comprises starch,            wood fiber, alginic acid fiber, polyhydroxy fatty acid            ester, acetic acid, and tributyl citrate.
AU HUANG S
   YU J
AE NINGBO ZHUOYUE PRINTING CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0158K
AB    NOVELTY - Multifunctional self-adhesive label comprises                a surface layer, an adhesive layer and a bottom                paper layer, which is in sequence from top to                bottom. The adhesive layer is located between the                surface layer and the bottom paper layer. The                surface layer comprises 40-60 pts. wt. starch, 5-8                pts. wt. wood fiber, 3-6 pts. wt. alginic acid                fiber, 10-15 pts. wt. polyhydroxy fatty acid ester,                10-20 pts. wt. acetic acid, and 3-7 pts. wt.                tributyl citrate. The adhesive layer comprises                20-30 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer polyol,                30-50 pts. wt. amino acids, 3-5 pts. wt. cashew                phenolic aldehyde amine, 5-10 pts. wt. linoleic                acid, 1-3 pts. wt. nano-cellulose crystal whisker,                2-5 pts. wt. nano-montmorillonite, and 1-3 pts. wt.                nano titanium dioxide. The bottom paper layer                comprises glassine bottom paper coated with                silicone oil.
   USE - Used as multifunctional self-adhesive                label.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the multifunctional self-adhesive label,                comprising (i) adding wood fiber and alginic acid                fiber to 20-30%, preferably 25 vol.% acetic acid                solution according to the above-mentioned                composition for acid leaching for 1-2 hours,                preferably for 1.5 hours and using distilled water                to wash to obtain mixed fiber, (ii) adding starch,                polyhydroxy fatty acid ester and tributyl citrate                into a mixing reaction kettle according to the                above mentioned composition, stirring and mixing at                60-90degrees Celsius, preferably 85degreesC under                500-800 revolutions/minute (rpm), preferably                500-700 rpm for 20-40 minutes, preferably 35                minutes, adding the mixed fiber obtained in step                (i), continuously stirring and carrying out the                reaction at 300-500 for 15-35 minutes, preferably                30 minutes and drying to obtain the surface layer                material, (iii) mixing the carbon dioxide copolymer                polyol, amino acid and linoleic acid according to                the above mentioned composition, stirring and                carrying out the reaction at 50-75degreesC,                preferably 60degreesC under 400-600 rpm for 15-25                minutes, preferably 20 minutes to obtain mixture A,                adding nano-montmorillonite and nano-titanium                dioxide to mixture A, and continuously carrying out                the reaction at 600-800 rpm for 20-40 minutes,                preferably 35 minutes, adding nano cellulose                crystal whisker and cashew phenolic aldehyde amine,                and continuously stirring at 300-500 rpm for 10-20                minutes, preferably 15 minutes to obtain the                adhesive material, (iv) using silicon coating                device to coat silicone oil on the surface of the                bottom paper, and drying to obtain the bottom                material, and (v) coating the adhesive material                obtained in step (iii) to the surface of the                surface layer material in step (ii), and drying to                obtain a glued surface layer material, pressing the                glued surface material and the bottom material                obtained in step (iv) and cutting by a rubber                roller and a roller.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The adhesive layer is                  located between the surface layer and the bottom                  paper layer. The surface layer comprises 50 pts.                  wt. starch, 7 pts. wt. wood fiber, 5 pts. wt.                  alginic acid fiber, 13 pts. wt. polyhydroxy fatty                  acid ester, 15 pts. wt. acetic acid, and 5 pts.                  wt. tributyl citrate. The adhesive layer                  comprises 25 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer                  polyol, 40 pts. wt. amino acids, 4 pts. wt.                  cashew phenolic aldehyde amine, 7 pts. wt.                  linoleic acid, 2 pts. wt. nano-cellulose crystal                  whisker, 3 pts. wt. nano-montmorillonite, and 2                  pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide. Preferred Method:                  In the step (iv), the method specifically                  comprises coating silicone oil on the surface of                  the bottom paper with a silicon coating device,                  and the coating amount of the silicone oil is                  1-1.5 g/m2. In the step (v), the method                  specifically comprises coating the adhesive                  material obtained in step (iii) on the surface of                  the surface layer material in step (ii), where                  the glue amount is 31-37 g/m2, and drying at                  100degreesC to obtain a glued surface layer                  material, and pressing and cutting the glued                  surface material and the bottom paper obtained in                  step (iv) by the rubber roller and a roller to                  form a multifunctional self-adhesive label.

PN BR102020006049-A2
TI Packing fish, molluscs and fresh crustaceans involves maintaining and controlling water received from supply network and stored in water tank, and continuously injecting ozone into gas through micro-bubbles.
AU TAMADA H
AE TAMADA H (TAMA-Individual)
GA 202209599K
AB    NOVELTY - Packing fish, molluscs and fresh crustaceans involves maintaining and controlling water received from supply network and stored in a water tank which residual chlorine concentration is 0.2-2 ppm, and continuously injecting ozone into gas through micro-bubbles generated by appropriate equipment. The water tank water comprises 0-80% carbon dioxide, 0-1% carbon monoxide, and 20-100% nitrogen. The products are taken to the preparation room where they are washed in running water cooled with ice to keep the water at 0 degrees C. Excess water is removed with previously sterilized towels with ozonated water and dried inside the sterile preparation room. The products are packed in Styrofoam trays coated with plastic film with high gas barrier on a moisture absorber, and are conducted to the equipment where the protective atmosphere is inserted and capped with high gas barrier plastic film. The packaging is sealed with high gas barrier film.
   USE - Method for packing fish, molluscs and fresh crustaceans.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the packing of fish, molluscs and fresh crustaceans in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with bacterostatic characteristics and protective atmosphere that allows the packaged product without preservatives to maintain its natural conditions such as texture, color and protein properties as well as conditions suitable for consumption for a longer time, allowing a longer exposure time in hygiene conditions without the possibility of contamination by the direct touch of the consumers with the fish, maintains the concept of self service, and makes possible the commercialization of fish, molluscs and crustaceans without the need to mount a fish inside the supermarket.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Packing fish, molluscs and fresh crustaceans involves maintaining and controlling water received from supply network and stored in a water tank which residual chlorine concentration is 0.2-2 ppm, and continuously injecting ozone into gas through micro-bubbles generated by appropriate equipment. The water tank water comprises 0-80% carbon dioxide, 0-1% carbon monoxide, and 20-100% nitrogen. The products are taken to the preparation room where they are washed in running water cooled with ice to keep the water at 0 degrees C. Excess water is removed with previously sterilized towels with ozonated water and dried inside the sterile preparation room. The products are packed in Styrofoam trays coated with plastic film with high gas barrier on a moisture absorber, and are conducted to the equipment where the protective atmosphere is inserted and capped with high gas barrier plastic film. The packaging is sealed with high gas barrier film. The packages are packed in carton packs for transport, and equipped with holes for circulation of the cold air from the cold room to the transport. The packages are transported in refrigerated trucks at 0 degrees C to the place of their commercialization, which can be done in 7-21 days.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN113307279-A
TI Preparing food-grade silicon powder involves            adopting natural quartz stone, cleaning and drying            natural quartz stone, detecting purity of silicon            dioxide of natural quartz stone, and selecting natural            quartz stone with high purity.
AU HUO Z
AE ZHUHAI HONGJIA INVESTMENT CO LTD (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 2021A2608S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing food-grade silicon powder involves                adopting natural quartz stone, cleaning and drying                the natural quartz stone; detecting the purity of                silicon dioxide of the natural quartz stone, and                selecting the natural quartz stone with high purity                of silicon dioxide; crushing all high-purity                natural quartzite by using a food-grade crusher to                generate quartzite powder; pouring quartz stone                powder and food-grade alkali into a reaction                kettle, heating, stirring and mixing to react to                generate sodium silicate and carbon dioxide;                conveying the sodium silicate from a discharge port                of the reaction kettle to a pure water cooling tank                for quenching to form a finished product of silicon                element crystals; fully grinding the silicon                crystal in a food-grade grinder to prepare a                silicon powder finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing food-grade silicon                powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare food-grade                silicon powder, which is safe, nontoxic and                harmless and it not only discharge vivotoxin, but                also activate stem cells and promote self-repair of                organisms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing food-grade silicon powder involves                adopting natural quartz stone, cleaning and drying                the natural quartz stone; detecting the purity of                silicon dioxide of the natural quartz stone, and                selecting the natural quartz stone with high purity                of silicon dioxide; crushing all high-purity                natural quartzite by using a food-grade crusher to                generate quartzite powder; pouring quartz stone                powder and food-grade alkali into a reaction                kettle, heating, stirring and mixing to react to                generate sodium silicate and carbon dioxide;                conveying the sodium silicate from a discharge port                of the reaction kettle to a pure water cooling tank                for quenching to form a finished product of silicon                element crystals; fully grinding the silicon                crystal in a food-grade grinder to prepare a                silicon powder finished product. The method                particularly involves soaking natural quartz stone                in 3-5% oxalic acid solution for 3-4 hr to show                that most of the attachment falls off; then soaking                the mixture for about 1 hour by using clear water,                and finally washing the mixture by using a brush or                a steel wire ball while washing the mixture by                using the clear water; putting the cleaned natural                quartz stone into a dryer to be dried for 15-30                minutes until the natural quartz stone is                completely dried. A portion of the dried quartz                stone is taken, and another portion of the dried                quartz stone is put into a composition detector,                then detecting the content of silicon dioxide, and                selecting natural quartz stone with the purity of                the silicon dioxide higher than 90%. The quartz                stone powder and food-grade alkali are poured into                a reaction kettle, heated to 1600-1700&#176; C,                stirred for 3-5 hours, and fully mixed to complete                the reaction. The step after quenching silicon                element crystal is obtained through cooling,                filtering by a food-grade filter and natural air                drying.

PN CN112811447-A
TI Manufacture of lithium carbonate involves calcining lithium polymer, cooling, grinding, performing acid addition reaction, cooling, adding calcium salt slurry, leaching, adding salt lake ore, causticizing, carbonizing and drying.
AU HE K
   HE D
   WU Z
   WANG L
   JIANG Y
   DAI D
   YANG X
   TAN P
   HUANG J
AE GUANGXI TIANYUAN NEW ENERGY MATERIALS CO (FCET-C)
GA 202158538V
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of lithium carbonate involves calcining a lithium polymer, cooling, grinding, performing acid addition reaction, cooling to 90 degrees C or less, adding water, adding a calcium salt slurry, stirring, leaching, adding calcium oxide, filtering, adding salt lake ore, purifying mixed liquid with lithium hydroxide mother liquor or calcium oxide, adjusting pH to 9-12, removing impurities, filtering, causticizing, freezing at -5 degrees C to -20 degrees C, separating sodium sulfate decahydrate and lithium hydroxide solution, concentrating, purifying, removing crystal water by heating to obtain anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering, concentrating, crystallizing, centrifuging, reheating, filtering, concentrating to obtain a fine lithium hydroxide slurry, centrifugally separating, supplying carbon dioxide gas, carbonizing, centrifugally separating to produce solid lithium carbonate, conveying centrifugal solid phase to the disc dryer, and transporting resulting material to raw material distribution system.
   USE - Manufacture of lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - Lithium carbonate having high quality can be produced efficiently and economically.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of lithium carbonate involves calcining a lithium polymer, cooling, grinding, performing acid addition reaction, cooling to 90 degrees C or less, adding water to produce slurry having solid content of 10-70%, adding a calcium salt slurry, stirring, leaching at 60 degrees C or less, adjusting pH to 5 or more, leaching for 20 minutes, adding calcium oxide to adjust the pH to 8-9, filtering with a filter press, adding salt lake ore into the filtered clear liquid, controlling lithium oxide concentration to 30-48 g/L, purifying mixed liquid with lithium hydroxide mother liquor or calcium oxide, adjusting pH to 9-12, removing impurities containing iron, manganese, aluminum and calcium to filtered clear liquid, further filtering the clear liquid to obtain a purified liquid and a purified filter residue, causticizing purified liquid with 50% alkaline solution or crude mother liquor, filtering to obtain causticizing liquid and causticizing filter residue, controlling equivalent content of lithium hydroxide in the causticizing liquid to 30-75 g/L, filtering causticizing liquid through a precision filter to remove portion of the calcium ions, freezing at -5 degrees C to -20 degrees C, separating sodium sulfate decahydrate and lithium hydroxide solution, concentrating, purifying, removing crystal water by heating at 20-800 degrees C to obtain anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering lithium hydroxide solution through a precision filter to remove portion of the calcium ions, concentrating by evaporation, crystallizing, centrifuging, reheating, filtering by a precision filter, concentrating to obtain a fine lithium hydroxide slurry, centrifugally separating and dissolving refined lithium hydroxide slurry, supplying carbon dioxide gas with a pressure of 0.5 MPa, carbonizing at 0.06-0.08 MPa and 48-52 degrees C, centrifugally separating to produce solid lithium carbonate having water content of 5% or less, conveying centrifugal solid phase to the disc dryer through a closed conveyor, pneumatically conveying to the finished product warehouse of lithium carbonate, transporting resulting material to the raw material distribution system. The controlled magnetic substance of the finished product is 30 ppb or less.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The particle size of the fine ground substance is 200 meshes. The acid is sulfuric acid having concentration of 98%. The solid content of the calcium salt slurry is 10-55%. The filter cake filtered by the filter press is rinsed with tap water or process water, and the filter cake is purged with compressed air to make the water content of the filter cake to 20% or less. The concentration of the lithium hydroxide mother liquor is 10-50%. The calcium oxide is a powder with concentration of more than 75% or a calcium oxide slurry with a solid content of 10-55%. The purified filter residue is mixed with water to form slurry with a solid content of 10-70%. The alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide solution and/or lithium hydroxide solution. The causticizing filter residue is mixed with water to form slurry with a solid content of 10-70%. The centrifugal liquid obtained by centrifugal separation contains a small amount of lithium carbonate. The centrifugal liquid is pumped back to the slurry mixing process of the lithium sulfate production section. The moisture content of the material after drying is less than 0.2%.

PN CN112048386-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of            agilawood essential oil by crushing agilawood to obtain            crushed agilawood powder, drying, sieving agilawood            powder, and extracting.
AU YIN A
   ZHOU T
   QIN Q
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEM TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
   MAOMING QUNXIANGHUI AGARWOOD TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (MAOM-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7257S
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction                of agilawood essential oil comprises crushing                agilawood to obtain crushed agilawood powder,                drying until water content is not greater than 5%,                sieving the agilawood powder of 20-60 mesh, and                extracting at 14-22 MPa and 30-50 degrees C for                0.5-2.5 hours.
   USE - The method is for supercritical CO2 extraction                of agilawood essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The agilawood essential oil extraction rate                reaches 0.688%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                optimization method of supercritical CO2 extraction                of agilawood essential oil comprising testing the                moisture of agilawood powder and ensuring the                moisture mass content of the agilawood powder for                subsequent steps is not greater than 5%; single                factor analysis comprising particle size analysis,                which comprises selecting a determined temperature                at 30-50 degrees C as the extraction temperature,                selecting a determined pressure at 14-22 MPa as the                extraction pressure, selecting a determined time of                0.5-2.5 hours as the extraction time, and obtaining                a relationship between the granularity of 20-60                mesh and agilawood essential oil extraction rate,                so as to determine a contribution degree of the                granularity to the agilawood essential oil                extraction rate; extraction pressure analysis                comprising selecting a determined temperature at                30-50 degrees C as the extraction temperature,                selecting a determined particle size of 20-60 mesh                as the granularity, selecting a determined time of                0.5-2.5 hours as the extraction time, and obtaining                the relation of extraction pressure at 14-22 MPa                and agilawood essential oil extraction rate, so as                to determine the contribution degree of extraction                pressure to the agilawood essential oil extraction                rate, extraction temperature analysis comprising                selecting a determined pressure at 14-22 MPa as the                extraction pressure, selecting a determined time of                0.5-2.5 hours as the extraction time, selecting a                determined particle size of 20-60 mesh as the                granularity, and obtaining a relation between the                extraction temperature of 30-50 degrees C and                agilawood essential oil extraction rate, so as to                determine the contribution degree of the extraction                temperature to the agilawood essential oil                extraction rate, and extraction time analysis                comprising selecting a determined temperature of                30-50 degrees C as the extraction temperature,                selecting a determined pressure at 14-22 MPa as the                extraction pressure, selecting a determined                particle size of 20-60 mesh as the granularity, and                obtaining a relation between the extraction time of                0.5-2.5 hours and agilawood essential oil                extraction rate to determine the contribution                degree of the extraction time to the agilawood                essential oil extraction rate; and analysis of the                response surface comprising selecting granularity,                extracting pressure, extracting temperature, and                extracting time contribution degree of three                factors as the test factor and agilawood essential                oil extraction rate as the evaluation index, and                using DesignExpert software as response surface                analysis on the three-factor multi-level basis to                obtain optimized extraction process                parameter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The granularity of                  agilawood powder is 30-50 mesh, preferably 42.65                  mesh. The extraction is conducted at 16-20 MPa,                  preferably 18.76 MPa and 35-45 degrees C,                  preferably 41.61 degrees C for 1.5-2.5                  hours.

PN IN351428-B
TI Method for performing hydrate based continuous gas separation process (HBCGS), involves circulating cooled gas through heat exchanger to cool hydrate forming chamber such that water soaked packed material comprises of thermodynamic additive.
AU KUMAR R
   KUMAR A
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 2020B59098
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing the feed gas mixture (5) through the fixed bed of water soaked packed material in chamber (3,4) loaded with multiple trays for selective enclathration of carbon dioxide (CO2) to form CO2 rich solid hydrate phase and hydrogen (H2) rich gas phase. The H2 rich gas phase through membrane separator (2) is passed to obtain pure H2 gas and storing in H2 storage tank (1). The CO2 rich solid hydrate containing trays are moved through its pivot to the decomposition chamber and decomposing the solid hydrate at suitable temperature and pressure to recover CO2 rich gas phase. The cooled gas through heat exchanger is circulated to cool the hydrate forming chamber. The water soaked packed material in chamber comprises of metallic packing selected from copper foam or brass packing. The water soaked packed material in chamber comprises of thermodynamic additives and kinetic promoters.
   USE - Method for performing hydrate based continuous gas separation process (HBCGS) for selective separation of gases from their mixtures using multi fixed bed reactors.
   ADVANTAGE - The HBCGS process is suitable for carbon dioxide separation from flue and fuel gas mixture and methane recovery from coal mine gas. The economical, scalable process is provided with improved thermodynamic and kinetics additives which enhances the hydrate formation kinetics at moderate experimental conditions. The fixed bed media is provided for better reaction kinetics and easy scalability. The HBGS process uses water for gas separation. The additives used in the process are mostly biodegradable polymers in less than1% by weight of water. The cost effective and high separation efficiency is achieved. The HBGS process has a very efficient regeneration. The scale of operation is a non-issue since the working fluid is water. A cost effective continuous process works like a tray dryer. Suitable packing material and additives has been suggested and the process is optimized at lab scale setup. The fixed bed approach for HBGS process is economical compared to a stirred tank reactor, fluidized bed reactor or a gas bubbling reactor. The separated CO2 is used for cooling through throttling process so that a reduction in external cooling cost is expected. The gas hydrate separation process is unique in a sense that any mixture of gases is separated based on their size and shape.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY- The thermodynamic additives selected from the group consisting of THF, propane and cyclopentane and kinetic promoters are selected from sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate (SDBS), sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS), dodecyltrimethylammonium chloride (DTACl), Tween-80 (T80), poly-N-vinyl pyrrolidone (PVP), Sodium salt of poly acrylic acid (PAA), cocoamidopropyl betaine (CAPB) and linear sulfated polysaccharide i-carrageenan (CAR).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram for the HBGS process.
   H2 storage tank (1)
   Membrane separator (2)
   Chamber (3,4)
   Feed gas mixture (5)
   Throttling process (6)

PN CN111264769-A
TI Preparing multigrain potato chips comprises e.g. selecting high-quality buckwheat, oats, sorghum, corn kernels and mung beans, cleaning, mixing above raw materials in pressure cooker, adding fresh water, boiling, stirring and grinding.
AU CAI J
   BU X
   CAI R
   CAI P
   XIE Y
AE ANHUI XIAOGANG PANPAN FOOD CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202055614U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multigrain potato chips comprises e.g. (i) selecting high-quality buckwheat, oats, sorghum, corn kernels and mung beans, cleaning them and placing aside; (ii) mixing the above raw materials in a pressure cooker, adding fresh water, boiling, taking out, mixing and stirring into a mixed slurry; (iii) grinding the above mixed slurry, adding it in a frying pan and stirring; (iv) adding the above mixed powder into egg white, milk and water, mixing and stirring, adding lactic acid bacteria and Bifidobacterium, mixing and placing in a constant temperature box at 35-40 degrees C to obtain fermentation slurry; (v) adding the above fermentation slurry into a high-pressure reaction kettle, pressurizing, heating, filling the reaction kettle with carbon dioxide, and taking it out to get the treated slurry; (vi) pouring the treated slurry into the mold tray, baking and cooling; and (vii) mixing the prepared seasoning agent and spices, sprinkling the seasoning powder and baking.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing multigrain potato chips.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can enhance the nutritional value of potato chips, absorption efficacy of nutrients through fermentation and other methods, and enriches the taste and flavor of potato chips.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing multigrain potato chips comprises (i) selecting high-quality buckwheat, oats, sorghum, corn kernels and mung beans, cleaning them and placing aside; (ii) mixing the above raw materials in a pressure cooker, adding fresh water, boiling at high temperature to 40-50 minutes, taking out, mixing and stirring into a mixed slurry; (iii) grinding the above mixed slurry at high temperature and grinding it into a powder, adding it in a frying pan and stirring to fry until the surface changes color; (iv) adding the above mixed powder into egg white, milk and water, mixing and stirring to form paste, adding lactic acid bacteria and Bifidobacterium, mixing evenly and placing in a constant temperature box at 35-40 degrees C for 10-12 hours to obtain fermentation slurry; (v) adding the above fermentation slurry into a high-pressure reaction kettle, pressurizing it to 16-18 MPa, heating to 80-100 degrees C, filling the reaction kettle with carbon dioxide, allowing it to stand for 20-30 minutes and taking it out to get the treated slurry; (vi) pouring the treated slurry into the mold tray, placing it in the oven at 180 degrees C, baking it for 2-3 minutes, taking it out and cooling it to obtain the initial shaped potato chips for use; and (vii) mixing the prepared seasoning agent and spices in advance to grind into seasoning powder, sprinkling the seasoning powder evenly on both sides of the initial shaped potato chips, and placing it in the oven and baking at 150 degrees C for 10-12 minutes
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of buckwheat, oats, sorghum, corn kernels and mung beans is 3:1:2:1:0.6. In the step (ii), the volume of fresh water added is 2-3 times the volume of the substrate. In the step (iv), the mass ratio of the mixed powder, egg white, milk, water, lactic acid bacteria and Bifidobacterium is 6:2:3:3:0.01:0.01. In the step (v), the volume of carbon dioxide filled in the reaction kettle is 2-3 times the volume of the fermentation slurry. In the step (vi), the thickness of the first shaped potato chips is 2-3 mm. In the step (vii), the amount of adding seasoning powder into each potato chip is 10-15% of the quality of the potato chips. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the method comprises drying mixed slurry at high temperature, grinding into powder and sieving through a 120-meshes sieve.
