TI New lentiviral expression vector useful e.g. in preparation of medicament for performing prevention, treatment or adjuvant treatment of malignant tumors, comprises gene encoding chimeric antigen receptor, and interleukin (IL)-6R blocker.
AU TAN Y
   ZHANG H
   CHEN Q
   HAN Z
AE SHANDONG QILU CELL THERAPY ENG TECHNOLOG (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202019934P
AB    NOVELTY - A lentiviral expression vector comprising a gene encoding a chimeric antigen receptor, and an interleukin (IL)-6R blocker i.e. tocilizumab, is new. The nucleotide sequence of the gene encoding the chimeric antigen receptor comprises a 1197 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 11) fully defined in the specification. The IL-6R blocking agent Tocilizumab has a nucleotide sequence comprising a 1500 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1) fully defined in the specification.
   USE - The lentiviral expression vector is useful producing chimeric antigen receptor T cell targeting CD19 expressing an IL-6R blocking antibody and in preparation of a medicament for performing prevention, treatment or adjuvant treatment of malignant tumors chosen from B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia, B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukemia and lymphoma (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The expression vector does not affect its phenotype of cell when transduced to T cells by a lentivirus-mediated manner, controls occurrence of cytokine release syndrome (CRS) side effects and increases safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) kit comprising the lentiviral expression vector; and (2) method for preparing a chimeric antigen receptor T cell targeting CD19 expressing an IL-6R blocking antibody by using the lentiviral expression vector involves (a) sequentially cloning cluster of differentiation (CD)8 transmembrane signal peptide, anti-CD19 single chain variable fragment (Scfv), CD8 transmembrane region, 4-1BB costimulatory signal region and CD3 Zeta T-cell receptor (TCR) activation region, internal ribosome entry site (IRES) and tocilizumab into lentivirus backbone plasmid pHR to obtain pHR-antiCD19CAR-Tocilizumab plasmid, (b) mixing the lentiviral expression vector pHR-antiCD19CAR-tocilizumab, pCMV vector and pMD.2G vector carrying the gene of interest, transfecting into 293FT cells, after 6-8 hours of transfection, replacing the medium with complete culture medium, after 48 hours, collecting culture solution, retaining supernatant after centrifugation, and filtering the supernatant with a 0.45 mu m filter, (c) taking 50 ml fresh blood, separating mononuclear cells by density gradient centrifugation through lymphocyte separation solution, resuspending the mononuclear cells in CTS AIM V (RTM: Not defined) serum-free medium (SFM), adding CD3 monoclonal antibody and CD28 monoclonal antibody at the same time to activate T lymphocytes, and culturing at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 48 hours, (d) taking 2x 106 cells, adding concentrated recombinant lentivirus solution with multiplicity of infection (MOI) of 5, adding IL-2 and polybrene, mixing well, culturing at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 6-8 hours, changing 300 g medium to fresh CTS AIM V (RTM: Not defined) SFM medium, adding fresh CTS AIM V (RTM: Not defined) SFM every 2-3 days, maintaining cell density at 1x 106 cells/ ml, expanding for 10-12 days and obtaining a CD19-targeting chimeric antigen receptor T cell expressing IL-6R blocking antibody, pHR-antiCD19CAR-tocilizumab-T.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecules: The structure of the lentivirus expression vector is pHR-antiCD19CAR-Tocilizumab, and the nucleotide sequence of the expressed gene comprises a 3593 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 9) fully defined in the specification. The IL-6R blocker comprises KOZAK sequence, a signal peptide, a light chain variable region, a hinge region, a heavy chain variable region and immunoglobulin (Ig)G1-crystallizable fragment (Fc). Preferred Medium: The CTS AIM V (RTM: Not defined) SFM culture medium further comprises 5% intracellular cytokine stain and 200 international unit (IU)/ml IL-2.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PT P
PN CN110512202-A; CN110512202-B
TI Preparing surface coating used for improving            corrosion resistance of medical magnesium alloy,            involves using magnesium alloy as base material, and            after grinding with sandpaper, and put it in the            cleaning mixture for ultrasonic cleaning.
AU YANG J
AE SUZHOU MEIXINDISI MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019A1448L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing surface coating involves using                magnesium alloy as the base material. After                grinding with sandpaper, put it in the cleaning                mixture for ultrasonic cleaning, and then place it                in deionized water for ultrasonic cleaning. The                high-purity carbon dioxide (CO2) gas is passed into                deionized water at room temperature, and                continuously passed in 5-10 minutes to make a                carbonic acid aqueous solution. The magnesium alloy                substrate is placed in a carbonic acid aqueous                solution for ultrasonic treatment. The temperature                control is 35-45 degrees C, the ultrasonic                frequency is 40-50 kilohertz, the power is 110-120                watt, and the ultrasonic time is 2-4 hours. After                the ultrasonic treatment, a basic magnesium                carbonate film is formed on the surface of the                magnesium alloy substrate. The magnesium alloy                substrate is placed in a water bath, and heat                treatment is performed. The heat treatment process                involves heating water bath to 350-420 degrees C,                and then maintaining the temperature.
   USE - Method for preparing surface coating used for                improving corrosion resistance of medical magnesium                alloy (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple and easy to                operate, the preparation cost is low, the                preparation process does not produce substances                that are liable to pollute the environment, and the                prepared coating is relatively uniform and dense,                has an ideal corrosion resistance effect, good                performance stability, and good                biocompatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing surface coating involves using                magnesium alloy as the base material. After                grinding with sandpaper, put it in the cleaning                mixture for ultrasonic cleaning, and then place it                in deionized water for ultrasonic cleaning. The                high-purity carbon dioxide (CO2) gas is passed into                deionized water at room temperature, and                continuously passed in 5-10 minutes to make a                carbonic acid aqueous solution. The magnesium alloy                substrate is placed in a carbonic acid aqueous                solution for ultrasonic treatment. The temperature                control is 35-45 degrees C, the ultrasonic                frequency is 40-50 kilohertz, the power is 110-120                watt, and the ultrasonic time is 2-4 hours. After                the ultrasonic treatment, a basic magnesium                carbonate film is formed on the surface of the                magnesium alloy substrate. The magnesium alloy                substrate is placed in a water bath, and heat                treatment is performed. The heat treatment process                involves heating water bath to 350-420 degrees C,                and then maintaining the temperature. After the                heat treatment of the magnesium alloy substrate is                completed, the magnesium alloy substrate is taken                out and cooled to room temperature, and a dense                magnesium oxide layer is formed on the surface of                the magnesium alloy substrate. The magnesium alloy                substrate is placed in a sodium silicate solution                for sealing treatment. The temperature of the                sodium silicate solution is 85-90 degrees C, and                the erosion time is 10-15 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium alloy is                  a magnesium-zinc (Mg-Zn)-based alloy, including,                  but not limited to, one of Mg-Zn,                  magnesium-zinc-calcium (Mg-Zn-Ca), Mg-Ca, and                  magnesium-strontium (Mg-Sr) alloys. The cleaning                  mixed liquid is a mixed liquid of ethanol and                  acetone. The sodium silicate solution is a                  saturated sodium silicate solution. Preferred                  Conditions: In the cleaning mixture, the volume                  ratio of ethanol and acetone is 1:1. The flow of                  CO2 gas is 1-2 L/minute, and the pH of the                  prepared carbonic acid aqueous solution is                  4.0-4.5. During the heat treatment, the                  temperature rising rate of the water bath is 15                  degrees C/minute, and after the temperature is                  raised to 350-420 degrees C, the holding time is                  11-15 hours.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).); M14 (Other chemical surface treatments - including etching, brightening, forming non-metallic layers, passivation, cathodic protection and corrosion inhibitors, but not processes specifically for semiconductor production (C23F, C25). PN CN110423631-A
TI Method for preparing high cetane number diesel oil by direct hydrogenation of animal and vegetable oils, involves entering discarded animal and vegetable oil to flash tank and entering oil phase to low pressure separator.
AU QI H
   ZHAO K
   LI K
   WANG J
AE ECO ENVIRONMENTAL ENERGY RES INST LTD (ECOE-Non-standard)
   UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019955407
AB    NOVELTY - A high cetane number diesel oil preparing method involves entering the discarded animal and vegetable oil to the flash tank, and entering the animal and vegetable oil after the water removal to the adsorption tower containing the adsorbent, and removing the impurities and metal ions by adsorption. The treated animals and plants are treated, the mixture is pressurized and mixed with hydrogen to enter the hydrotreating tower, the upper layer of the hydrotreating tower is filled with a refining catalyst, and the lower layer is filled with a refining catalyst. The product enters the high pressure separator for gas-liquid separation, the gas phase is treated and returned to the inlet of the hydrotreating tower to adjust the carbon dioxide concentration and effectively increase the liquid hydrocarbon yield. The oil phase enters the distillation column to separate gasoline and diesel oil, and part of the diesel oil is re-reacted as a mixture of circulating oil and raw materials.
   USE - Method for preparing high cetane number diesel oil by direct hydrogenation of animal and vegetable oils.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing high cetane number diesel oil with enhanced yield, high catalyst activity, better stability, low cold filter plugging point, and better economical benefit and industrial application prospect, in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A high cetane number diesel oil preparing method involves entering the discarded animal and vegetable oil to the flash tank and flashes to remove water, and entering the animal and vegetable oil after the water removal to the adsorption tower containing the adsorbent, and removing the impurities and metal ions by adsorption. The treated animals and plants are treated, the mixture is pressurized and mixed with hydrogen to enter the hydrotreating tower, the upper layer of the hydrotreating tower is filled with a refining catalyst with a heterogeneous function, and the lower layer is filled with a refining catalyst. The operating conditions of the hydrorefining tower are temperature at 300-380 degrees C, pressure is 3-8 MPa, space velocity of 0.3-1.0 h-1 and hydrogen oil ratio of 200-1000:1. The product enters the high pressure separator for gas-liquid separation in the aqueous phase, oil phase and gas phase, the gas phase is treated and returned to the inlet of the hydrotreating tower to adjust the carbon dioxide concentration and effectively increase the liquid hydrocarbon yield. The oil phase enters the low pressure separator, and further flashes off the gas dissolved in the oil phase and the oil and water are separated. The water phase is directly discharged. The oil phase enters the distillation column to separate gasoline and diesel oil, and part of the diesel oil is re-reacted as a mixture of circulating oil and raw materials after adsorption treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The sulfur content in the sulfur-suppressing agent is 0.1-1.5% of the total mass of the raw material oil, and the sulfur-suppressing agent is carbon disulfide, hydrogen sulfide or dimethyl disulfide. The adsorbent is a mixture of a humic acid substance and a clay or aluminum oxide, where the humic acid substance accounts for 20-40% of the total mass of the adsorbent, and the clay or aluminum oxide accounts for 60-80% of the total mass of the adsorbent; The refined catalyst is an oxide-supported fluorine and rhodium-modified nickel molybdenum sulfide catalyst, where the oxide is aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide or aluminum oxide-titanium dioxide. The catalyst before pre-sulfidation, nickel oxide accounts for 3-5% of the refined catalyst, molybdenum trioxide accounts for 16-25% of the refined catalyst, lanthanum accounts for 1-2% of the refined catalyst, fluoride accounts for 0.5-1% of the refined catalyst and the remaining oxide carriers The supplementary refining catalyst is an oxide-supported nickel-tungsten sulfide catalyst, where the oxide is aluminum oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The discarded animal and vegetable fats and oils are raw materials containing glycerides and free fatty acids, and fatty acid methyl esters or fatty acid ethyl esters prepared by trans-esterification of animal and vegetable oils and fats.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN110330023-A; CN110330023-B
TI Preparation and drying of magnesium trisilicate involves pretreating boiler scale, pulverizing, adding calcium disodium edetate, acid-dissolving, filtering, preparing solution of sodium silicate and sodium hydroxide, washing and drying.
AU LIU X
AE LIU X (LIUX-Individual)
   YUNPENG PHARM GROUP CO LTD (YUNP-Non-standard)
GA 201986841R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation and drying of magnesium trisilicate involves pretreating boiler scale, screening, crushing, pulverizing to form boiler scale powder, adding aqueous solution to form boiler scale powder slurry, adding calcium disodium edetate to form stable soluble metal chelate compound, adding acidic solution, acid-dissolving magnesium hydroxide, calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate to form product solution after acid hydrolysis, where acid hydrolysis involves reacting magnesium hydroxide and sulfuric acid to form magnesium sulfate, calcium carbonate dihydrate and sulfuric acid, further reacting to form calcium sulfate, magnesium carbonate monohydrate and sulfuric acid and further reacting to form magnesium sulfate, water and carbon dioxide, filtering, adding water to magnesium sulfate solution, preparing solution of sodium silicate and sodium hydroxide, adding magnesium sulfate solution to reaction kettle, adding sodium silicate and sodium hydroxide mixed solution, washing and drying.
   USE - Preparation and drying of magnesium trisilicate.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation is economical, has mild reaction condition, and provides magnesium trisilicate with uniform shape, high acidity and no sand property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation and drying of magnesium trisilicate involves pretreating 1000-1500 pts. wt. boiler scale, screening, drying, crushing to 3-5 cm pellets, pulverizing to 100 mesh size to form boiler scale powder, placing boiler scale powder in water storage tank, adding aqueous solution, stirring to form boiler scale powder slurry, adding 30-60 pts. wt. calcium disodium edetate, neutralizing heavy metal ion of cadmium, aluminum, arsenic and mercury in scale powder slurry to form stable soluble metal chelate compound, encapsulating metal ions inside chelate to form stable, larger molecular weight compound thereby preventing metal ions from functioning in subsequent reactions and also avoiding influence of heavy metal substances on human body, adding 50-100 pts. wt. acidic solution, acid-dissolving magnesium hydroxide, calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate to form product solution after acid hydrolysis, where acidic solution is concentrated sulfuric acid and acid hydrolysis involves reacting magnesium hydroxide and sulfuric acid to form magnesium sulfate, calcium carbonate dihydrate and sulfuric acid, further reacting to form calcium sulfate, magnesium carbonate monohydrate and sulfuric acid and further reacting to form magnesium sulfate, water and carbon dioxide, filtering product solution to remove calcium sulfate to form magnesium sulfate solution containing stable metal chelate, adding water to magnesium sulfate solution to form magnesium sulfate solution in accordance with preparation concentration of magnesium trisilicate, preparing solution of 60-120 pts. wt. sodium silicate and 90-180 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, leaving still sodium silicate solution, mixing with solution of sodium hydroxide, adding magnesium sulfate solution to reaction kettle, adding sodium silicate and sodium hydroxide mixed solution, stirring, driving reaction liquid into plate frame of plate and frame filter, washing with purified water, discharging plate to be beaten, driving slurry into clean zone plate frame, washing, discharging press plate, performing sampling control and drying after passing free alkali test.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the sodium silicate solution is 15-25%, preferably 25-30%, the concentration of the magnesium sulfate solution is 15-20%, preferably 20-30%, and the concentration of sodium hydroxide is 15-20%, preferably 20-30%. The molar ratio of magnesium sulfate, sodium silicate and sodium hydroxide is 1:(1.5-2):(2.5-3), preferably 1:1.8:2.7. Preferred Property: The medium-controlled free base has a pH control range of 8-12, preferably 9-10. Preferred Condition: The reaction is carried out at 10-60 degrees C, preferably 30-40 degrees C. The drying temperature of the vacuum drying, blast drying and double cone drying is 30-80 degrees C. The flash drying air inlet temperature is 200-260 degrees C, the outlet air temperature is 60-110 degrees C, and the feeding frequency is 5-25 Hz, preferably, the inlet air temperature is 240-250 degrees C, the outlet air temperature is 95-105 degrees C, and the feed frequency is 15-25 Hz. Preferred Process: The drying method is vacuum drying, blast drying, double cone drying or flash drying.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN109956757-A
TI Preparing ceramic material comprises placing preformed body in muffle furnace and pre-firing, performing calcination, continuing firing.
AU TANG S
AE TANG S (TANG-Individual)
GA 201959878J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ceramic material comprises placing the preformed body in a muffle furnace and pre-firing, performing calcinations to obtain a high temperature thermal shock resistant ceramic material, where preformed body preparation comprising placing colloidal product in oven, drying, transferring into a carbonization furnace, where colloidal product preparation comprising mixing a reaction liquid and a mixture, loading into reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide gas, heating, where reaction solution preparation comprising (a) crushing rice hull, sieving, placing in pyrolysis furnace for pyrolysis, cooling and discharging and (b) placing rice hull pyrolysis ash into a stainless steel reaction vessel, adding sodium hydroxide solution, stirring, where mixture preparation comprising crushing fresh sugar cane to obtain sugar cane crushed material, mixing the above material with aluminum and magnesium powder, placing in a mixer, stirring and mixing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ceramic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good high temperature resistance, excellent thermal shock resistance and wide application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ceramic material comprises placing the preformed body in a muffle furnace and pre-firing for 2-3 hours, performing calcination, programming the temperature to 1800-2000 degrees C at a rate of 20 degrees C/min, continuing the firing for 3-5 hours to obtain a high temperature thermal shock resistant ceramic material, where the preparation of the preformed body comprising placing the colloidal product in an oven, drying at 105-110 degrees C for 1-2 hours, then transferring into a carbonization furnace for carbonization for 3-4 hours under nitrogen protection to obtain the preformed body, where the preparation of the colloidal product comprising mixing a reaction liquid and a mixture to obtain a substrate, loading the substrate into a reaction vessel with an aeration device at the bottom, introducing the carbon dioxide gas at the rate of 1-2 l/minutes through an aeration device into the substrate, heating until the substrate is boiled for 2-3 hours to obtain the colloidal product, where the preparation of the reaction solution comprising (a) crushing the rice hull for 1-2 hours, sieving through a 100 mesh sieve to obtain rice husk powder, placing the rice hull powder in a pyrolysis furnace, carrying out the pyrolysis for 1-2 hours under an argon atmosphere, naturally cooling to room temperature after the end of the pyrolysis and discharging to obtain a rice husk ash and (b) placing rice hull pyrolysis ash into a stainless steel reaction vessel, adding 30 wt.% sodium hydroxide solution to the reaction vessel, stirring the reaction at 90-100 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain a reaction liquid, where the preparation of the mixture comprising crushing fresh sugar cane into a tissue crusher for 40-50 minutes to obtain sugar cane crushed material, mixing the sugar cane crushed material with aluminum powder and magnesium powder, placing in a mixer, stirring and mixing for 15-20 minutes at a speed of 200-300 revolutions/minutes to obtain the mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: The calcination is carried out at 800-900 degrees C. The temperature of the heat insulating carbonization treatment in preformed body preparation is 300-400 degrees C. The temperature of the heat preservation pyrolysis in reaction liquid preparation of the step (a) is 600-700 degrees C. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the reaction liquid to the mixture is 10:1. In the preparation step (b) of the reaction liquid, the sodium hydroxide solution having a mass fraction of 30% is added in an amount of 5-8 times the mass of the pyrolysis rice hull ash. The mass ratio of the sugar cane crushed material to the aluminum powder and the magnesium powder is 10:2:1.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27))

PN CN109535375-A; CN109535375-B
TI Preparing highly exfoliated two-dimensional nano-sheet reinforced polyurethane composite material comprises heating isocyanate, dissolving modified layered particle flocculent mixture in a metered polyol, vacuum defoaming and curing.
AU XU J
   ZHANG L
   LI C
   YANG H
   WU H
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
   UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEM TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
GA 201931030T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly exfoliated two-dimensional nano-sheet reinforced polyurethane composite material comprises (i) stirring nano-sized, sub-micron or micron-sized layered inorganic particles and dispersing in an anhydrous solvent, adding excess isocyanate, mixing uniformly, heating to 80 degrees C until the system does not produce carbon dioxide gas to obtain a mixture of isocyanate-modified layered particles, and removing the upper solvent by filtration; and (ii) dissolving modified layered particle flocculent mixture in a metered polyol, and adding the metered isocyanate at 80 degrees C to continue the stirring reaction, vacuum defoaming after the depolymerization of the prepolymer, adding a metered chain extender, stirring, pouring into a mold preheated, and placing in flat vulcanizer for pressure curing, placing the sample, curing in a forced air oven, allowing to stand for one week to obtain a thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer nano-composite.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing highly exfoliated two-dimensional nano-sheet reinforced polyurethane composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material enhances the mechanical properties of the polyurethane material and has a good toughening effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing highly exfoliated two-dimensional nano-sheet reinforced polyurethane composite material comprises (i) stirring nano-sized, sub-micron or micron-sized layered inorganic particles and dispersing in an anhydrous solvent in a ratio of 3-8 g: 100 ml, adding excess isocyanate, mixing uniformly, heating to 80 degrees C until the system does not produce carbon dioxide gas to obtain a mixture of isocyanate-modified layered particles, allowing to stand for 0.5-1 hour, and removing the upper solvent by filtration to obtain modified layered particle flocculent mixture; and (ii) dissolving the modified layered particle flocculent mixture obtained in the step (i) in a metered polyol, and adding the metered isocyanate at 80 degrees C to continue the stirring reaction for 1.5-3 hours to obtain a prepolymer, vacuum defoaming after the depolymerization of the prepolymer, adding a metered chain extender, stirring for 30-90 seconds, pouring into a mold preheated to 60 degrees C, and placing in a 120 degrees C flat vulcanizer for pressure curing for 30 minutes, placing the sample, curing at 100 degrees C for 24 hours in a forced air oven, allowing to stand at room temperature for one week to obtain a thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer nano-composite, where the polyol, the chain extender and the isocyanate is separately pretreated and the pre-treating method comprises vacuum dehydrating the polyol and chain extender at 100-110 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours, slowly melting the isocyanate at 45-60 degrees C in a vacuum oven, where the ratio of the mass ratio of the polyol, isocyanate ad chain extender after pretreatment is 100:25-60:10-35 and the mass ratio of the modified layered particle flocculent mixture to the total mass of the polyol, the isocyanate and the chain extender after pretreatment is 0.5-5:100.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the sheet inorganic particle is hydrotalcite, montmorillonite, graphene, graphene oxide or mica. In the step (i) and step (ii), the isocyanate is hexamethylene diisocyanate or lysine diisocyanate in an aliphatic isocyanate, polymethylene polyphenyl diisocyanate, trimethyl-1,6-hexamethylene diisocyanate, tetramethyldimethylene diisocyanate, dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, benzyl dimethyl diisocyanate in 1,4-cyclohexane diisocyanate and aromatic isocyanate, 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, toluene diisocyanate and/or 1,5-naphthyl diisocyanate. The anhydrous solvent is dehydrated dimethylformamide or dehydrated dimethylacetamide. In the step (ii), the polyol is polycaprolactone diol, polyethylene adipate, polyether polyoxypropylene ether, polytetrahydrofuran, hydroxy terminated polybutadiene, hydroxy-terminated polybutadiene-acrylonitrile and/or hydroxy-terminated polyisoprene polyester and polytetrahydrofuran is polytetrahydrofuran diol PTMG-1000 or polytetrahydrofuran diol PTMG-2000. The chain extender is 1,4-butanediol, hydroquinone bis(2-hydroxyethyl)ether, hydrogenated bisphenol A, resorcinol dihydroxyethyl ether, 3, 3'-dichloro-4,4'-diphenylmethanediamine, 3,5-dimethylthiotoluenediamine or isobutyl 3,5-diaminop-chlorobenzoate.

PT P
PN CN109137512-A
TI Preparation of wool antibacterial deodorant finishing agent, involves adding dried orange peel powder to distilled water, dripping hydrochloric acid solution, heating, filtering, cooling, washing, drying, freezing tangerine peel, and drying.
AU LI D
   CHEN Z
AE SUZHOU TIANAO SPECIAL EMBROIDERY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201905429L
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of wool antibacterial deodorant finishing agent involves adding dried orange peel powder to distilled water, dripping hydrochloric acid solution, heating, hot filtering, cooling, washing, drying, freezing tangerine peel, freeze-drying, taking out, and grinding to obtain porous dried orange peel powder, washing silkworm with deionized water, drying, placing in a tubular furnace, introducing nitrogen, raising the temperature, increasing temperature, heat-preserving and carbonizing, grinding and sieving to obtain carbonized silkworm, activating by carbon dioxide to obtain activated silkworm, adding to hydrogen peroxide solution, refluxing and reacting, washing silkworm, and drying to obtain modified porous silkworm, dissolving chitosan in acetic acid solution, adding 1,2,3,4-tetracarboxylic acid butane, sodium dodecyl sulfate, porous dried orange peel powder, modified porous silkworm and sodium hypophosphite to chitosan solution, and stirring.
   USE - Preparation method of wool antibacterial deodorant finishing agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of wool antibacterial deodorant finishing agent involves (S1) adding dried orange peel powder to distilled water, dripping hydrochloric acid solution to adjust pH to 4-6, heating at 66-85 degrees C in a water bath for 15-30 minutes, hot filtering the resulting mixture by gauze, cooling to room temperature, washing, naturally drying, freezing the tangerine peel at -25 degrees C for 2-4 hours, placing the frozen sample in a desktop freeze drier, freeze-drying at -50 to -80 degrees C for 12-24 hours, taking out, and grinding into 400-800-mesh powder to obtain porous dried orange peel powder, (S2) selecting two years old silkworm, washing the silkworm with deionized water, drying at 100 degrees C, placing the silkworm in a tubular furnace, introducing nitrogen at rate to 3 degrees C/minute, raising the temperature to 300 degrees C, increasing the temperature to 600 degrees C at rate of 5 degrees C/minute, heat-preserving and carbonizing for 2 hours, grinding and sieving by 100-150-mesh sieve to obtain carbonized silkworm, (S3) activating the carbonized silkworm by carbon dioxide at 400-600 degrees C and 10 degrees C/minute to obtain activated silkworm, (S4) adding the activated silkworm to hydrogen peroxide solution in a mass ratio of 5:(3-4), refluxing and reacting for 3-6 hours at 65-80 degrees C, after finishing reaction, washing the silkworm until neutral, and drying to obtain modified porous silkworm, and (S5) dissolving chitosan in 1-2 wt.% acetic acid solution to obtain chitosan solution with concentration of 2-5%, adding 45-80 g/L 1,2,3,4-tetracarboxylic acid butane, 1-8 g/L sodium dodecyl sulfate, 25-90 g/L porous dried orange peel powder, 45-70 g/L modified porous silkworm and 15-30 g/L sodium hypophosphite to chitosan solution, and stirring at 30-50 degrees C of constant temperature for 2-4 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The step (S5) comprises 55-70 g/L 1,2,3,4-tetracarboxylic acid butane, 2-5 g/L sodium dodecyl sulfate, 45-60 g/L porous dried orange peel powder, 52-65 g/L modified porous silkworm and 15-25 g/L sodium hypophosphite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the freeze-drying in step (S1) is -55 to -70 degrees C, and the drying time is 15-20 hours. The temperature of the reflux reaction in step (S4) is 70-75 degrees C and the time is 4-5 hours.
DC A87 (Textile auxiliaries.); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.);

PT P
PN CN108748517-A; CN108748517-B
TI Ceramic wood is based on oxime blocking and comprises wood, where gaps in the wood are filled with a filler that combines with the wood fibers and forms a network with the wood fibers, and the filler comprises polyisocyanate.
AU HUANG Y
   WANG R
   XIE H
   YANG H
AE GUANGZHOU JINGDIAN POLYMER MATERIAL PROD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201888644D
AB    NOVELTY - Ceramic wood is based on oxime blocking and comprises wood, where gaps in the wood are filled with a filler that combines with the wood fibers and forms a network with the wood fibers, and the filler comprises 10-15 wt.% polyisocyanate, 8 to 12 wt.% polyester polyol, 10-15 wt.% aldoxime blocking agent, 1 to 2 wt.% olefin polymerization catalyst, 16 to 25 wt.% amino acids, and 40 to 50 wt.% water.
   USE - Ceramic wood.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic wood is anticorrosive, waterproof, anti-mite, stable in size, long in service life and good in performance, wood is not easily deformed, moldy, and durable, and the inner and outer layers of the ceramic wood are the same, and is not different from the inner ceramic wood because the outer material is polished.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing ceramic wood, which involves:
   (A) preparing a prepolymer, reacting 10-15 wt.% polyisocyanate and 8 to 12 wt.% a polyester polyol;
   (B) adding 10-15 wt.% an aldoxime blocking agent to the prepolymer, and blocking the NCO group with an aldoxime blocking agent to form a closed prepolymer, in which carbon dioxide, olefin and primary are decomposed, where primary amine blocks the free or free NCO group;
   (C) adding 1 to 2 wt.% an olefin polymerization catalyst, 16 to 25 wt.% amino acids, and 40 to 50 wt.% water to form a immersion liquid in the closed prepolymer;
   (D) immersing the wood in the immersion liquid, and the immersion liquid enters the wood through the pressure of 1-10 MPa for 12-24 hours; and
   (E) taking out the impregnated wood, and then putting into a heating kettle, heating the impregnated wood for 10-20 hours, and heating at a temperature of 100-120 degrees C, during the heating process, the NCO group blocked by the aldoxime blocking agent is deblocked, and at the same time, the hydroxyl group in the amino acid acts on the blocked prepolymer which is blocked by the primary amine, where NCO group blocked by the primary amine is deblocked and a chemical reaction occurs at the same time, after the reaction is completed, the wood fiber is formed into a network of the polymer in the wood and the ceramic wood is obtained, where main reaction is carbonyl derivative (I) reacts to form HB and carbonyl derivative (II), which reacts with water to form carbonyl derivative (III), which forms amine derivative (IV) and carbon dioxide, carbonyl derivative (II) reacts with amine derivative (IV) to form carbonyl derivative (V), carbonyl derivative (II) reacts with hydroxyl derivative (VI) to form carbonyl derivative (VII), carbonyl derivative (II) reacts with carbonyl derivative (VII) to form carbonyl derivative (VIII), where olefin undergoes addition polymerization under the action of an olefin polymerization catalyst.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The olefin polymerization catalyst is a chromium-based catalyst. The polyisocyanate is one or more of TDI, MDI, HDI, IPDI. The polyester polyol is one of ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, diethylene glycol, trimethylolpropane, and pentaerythritol.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); P63 (Working, preserving wood (B27).)

PT P
PN CN108579798-A
TI Preparing tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide/zeolite Socony Mobil-5 double core shell catalyst, comprises e.g. dropping copper-zinc mixed solution and sodium carbonate solution into tetragonal zirconium dioxide.
AU LIU F
   CAO J
   WANG G
   WANG X
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018797919
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide/zeolite Socony Mobil-5 double core shell catalyst, comprises e.g. (1) preparing copper-zinc mixed solution and sodium carbonate solution, under stirring condition, dropping copper-zinc mixed solution and sodium carbonate solution into tetragonal zirconium dioxide suspension, stirring, performing ultrasonic treatment, filtering, washing, drying, roasting, then milling to obtain tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide powder, and (2) adding sodium aluminate, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and tetraethyl orthosilicate into deionized water, stirring and aging to obtain a sol system, according to core-shell mass ratio of tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide/zeolite Socony Mobil-5 of 1:2-2:1, placing tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide powder into system, stirring, carrying out hydrothermal reaction, cooling, centrifuging, washing, then drying, and roasting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide/zeolite Socony Mobil-5 double core shell catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improves carbon dioxide conversion rate and low carbon olefin selectivity in a two-step process of hydrogenating carbon dioxide via methanol to produce low carbon olefins.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide/zeolite Socony Mobil-5 (ZSM-5) double core shell catalyst, comprises (1) preparing 0.2 mol x l(-1) copper-zinc mixed solution and 1 mol x l(-1) sodium carbonate solution, where the molar ratio between sodium carbonate, copper(II) nitrate trihydrate, zinc nitrate hexahydrate is 3:2:1, under stirring condition, according to core-shell mass ratio of tetragonal zirconium dioxide, copper(II) oxide and zinc oxide of 1:3-1:9, dropping copper-zinc mixed solution and sodium carbonate solution into tetragonal zirconium dioxide suspension, stirring at 80 degrees C for 2 hours, performing ultrasonic treatment for 10 minutes, filtering, washing, drying at 110 degrees C for 12 hours, roasting at 400 degrees C for 4 hours, then milling to obtain tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide powder, and (2) under stirring condition, adding sodium aluminate, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and tetraethyl orthosilicate into deionized water, where the molar ratio between tetraethyl orthosilicate, sodium aluminate, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and water is 40-360:1:19:4015, then stirring and aging at room temperature for 3 hours to obtain a sol system, according to core-shell mass ratio of tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide/zeolite Socony Mobil-5 of 1:2-2:1, placing tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide powder into the system, stirring uniformly in homogeneous reactor, carrying out hydrothermal reaction at 170-190 degrees C for 24-48 hours (at a speed of 4 revolutions per minute), cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, washing with deionized water, washing with anhydrous ethanol, then drying at 120 degrees C for 12 hours, and roasting at 500-600 degrees C for 3 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The tetragonal zirconium dioxide/copper(II) oxide-zinc oxide powder is 200-400 mesh.

PT P
PN US2018245108-A1; US11274321-B2
TI Biological and chemical method for capture and conversion of e.g. inorganic carbon compound into organic chemical product, by introducing inorganic carbon compound into bioreactor at environment for maintaining oxyhydrogen microorganisms.
AU REED J S
   DYSON L
AE KIVERDI INC (KIVE-Non-standard)
   KIVERDI INC (KIVE-Non-standard)
GA 201867499E
AB    NOVELTY - Biological and chemical method for capture and conversion of an inorganic carbon compound and/or an organic compound containing only one carbon atom into an organic chemical product, involves introducing an inorganic carbon compound and/or an organic compound containing only one carbon atom into a bioreactor comprising an environment suitable for maintaining oxyhydrogen microorganisms and/or capable of maintaining extracts of oxyhydrogen microorganisms; and converting the inorganic carbon compound and/or the organic compound containing only one carbon atom into the organic chemical product and/or its precursor within the environment via chemosynthetic carbon-fixing reaction utilizing oxyhydrogen microorganisms and/or cell extracts containing enzymes from the oxyhydrogen microorganisms. The chemosynthetic fixing reaction is partially driven by chemical and/or electrochemical energy provided by electron donors and electron acceptors.
   USE - The biological and chemical method is useful for capture and conversion of an inorganic carbon compound and/or an organic compound containing only one carbon atom into an organic chemical product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can enable the effective and economic capture of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere or from a point source of carbon dioxide emissions as well as the economic use of waste energy sources and/or renewable energy sources and/or low carbon emission energy sources, for the production of liquid transportation fuel and/or other organic chemical products, which will help address greenhouse gas induced climate change and contribute to the domestic production of renewable liquid transportation fuels and/or other organic chemicals without any dependence upon agriculture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biological and chemical method for capture and conversion of an inorganic carbon compound and/or an organic compound containing only one carbon atom into an organic chemical product, involves introducing an inorganic carbon compound and/or an organic compound containing only one carbon atom into a bioreactor comprising an environment suitable for maintaining oxyhydrogen microorganisms and/or capable of maintaining extracts of oxyhydrogen microorganisms; and converting the inorganic carbon compound and/or the organic compound containing only one carbon atom into the organic chemical product and/or its precursor within the environment via chemosynthetic carbon-fixing reaction utilizing oxyhydrogen microorganisms and/or cell extracts containing enzymes from the oxyhydrogen microorganisms. The chemosynthetic fixing reaction is partially driven by chemical and/or electrochemical energy provided by electron donors and electron acceptors that have been generated chemically and/or electrochemically and/or thermochemically and/or are introduced into the environment from source external to the environment. Biomass and/or biochemicals are produced by the chemosynthetic carbon-fixing reaction. The biomass and/or biochemicals are separated from the environment and are processed into a product comprising an animal feed, a fertilizer, a soil additive, a soil stabilizer, a carbon source for large scale fermentations, and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Processes: The electron donors and/or organic compound containing only one carbon atom are generated through the gasification and/or pyrolysis of organic matter or through methane steam reforming and provided as a syngas to the oxyhydrogen microorganisms. The ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas is adjusted via the water gas shift reaction prior to delivery of the syngas to the oxyhydrogen microorganisms. The thermochemical and/or electrochemical generation of electron donors and/or electron acceptors is powered by a carbon dioxide emission-free or low-carbon emission and/or a renewable source of power, and/or have been generated by gasification, pyrolysis, or steam reforming of a waste or biomass feedstock or biogas. The carbon dioxide emission-free or low-carbon dioxide emission and/or renewable source of power are utilized for the electrochemical production of the electron donor. Electrochemical production of the electron donor involves electrolysis. The electron donors are generated from pollutants or waste products selected from process gas; refinery gas; enhanced oil recovery vent gas; biogas; acid mine drainage; landfill leachate; landfill gas; geothermal gas; geothermal sludge or brine; metal contaminants; gangue; tailings; sulfides; disulfides; mercaptans selected from one or more of methyl and dimethyl mercaptan and ethyl mercaptan; carbonyl sulfide; carbon disulfide; alkanesulfonates; dialkyl sulfides; thiosulfate; thiofurans; thiocyanates; isothiocyanates; thioureas; thiols; thiophenols; thioethers; thiophene; dibenzothiophene; tetrathionate; dithionite; thionate; dialkyl disulfides; sulfones; sulfoxides; sulfolanes; sulfonic acid; dimethylsulfoniopropionate; sulfonic esters; hydrogen sulfide; sulfate esters; organic sulfur; sulfur dioxide and all other sour gases. Converting step is preceded by chemical preprocessing steps in which the electron donors and/or the electron acceptors are generated and/or refined from input chemical and/or are recycled from chemicals produced during the chemosynthetic carbon-fixing reaction and/or chemicals derived from waste streams from other industrial, mining, agricultural, sewage or waste generating processes. Converting step is followed by process steps in which any unused nutrients and/or process water left after removal of oxyhydrogen cell mass and/or chemical co-products of chemosynthesis and/or waste products or contaminants of the process stream produced during the fixing step are recycled back into the environment to support further chemosynthesis. The carbon fixing reaction is conducted under aerobic, microaerobic, or facultative conditions. The chemosynthetic reaction is performed by oxyhydrogen microorganisms that have been improved, optimized or engineered for the fixation of an inorganic carbon compound and/or an organic compound containing only one carbon atom and the production of organic compounds. Energy for carbon fixation is provided by an abiotic process. The chemosynthetic carbon-fixing reaction utilizes ATP produced through the oxyhydrogen reaction. Preferred Microorganism: The oxyhydrogen microorganisms comprise Rhosopseudomonas; Rhodobacter; Rhodospirillum; Rhodococcus; Rhizobium; Thiocapsa; Pseudomonas; Hydrogenomonas; Hydrogenobacter; Hydrogenovibrio; Helicobacter; Xanthobacter; Hydrogenophaga; Bradyrhizobium; Ralstonia; Alcaligenes; Variovorax; Acidovorax; Anabaena; Scenedesmus; Chlamydomonas; Ankistrodesmus; and Rhaphidium microorganisms; and/or a purple non-sulfur bacterium, cyanobacterium, or green algae. Preferably, the oxyhydrogen microorganisms comprise Rhodococcus; Hydrogenovibrio; Rhodopseudomonas; Rhodobacter; Hydrogenobacter; and Xanthobacter microorganisms; and/or a purple non-sulfur bacterium. The oxyhydrogen microorganisms comprise Rhodococcus opacus; Hydrogenovibrio marinus; Rhodopseudomonas capsulata; Hydrogenobacter thermophilus; and Rhodobacter sphaeroides. Specifically, the oxyhydrogen microorganisms comprise Cupriavidus necator DSM 531.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Processes: The electron donors and/or electron acceptors are generated or recycled using renewable, alternative, or conventional sources of power that are low in greenhouse gas emissions, where the sources of power are selected from photovoltaics; solar thermal; wind power; hydroelectric; nuclear; geothermal; enhanced geothermal; ocean thermal; ocean wave power; and tidal power. The thermochemical and/or electrochemical generation of electron donors and/or electron acceptors involves electrolysis of water by approaches including proton exchange membranes (PEM), liquid electrolytes such as potassium hydroxide (KOH), high-pressure electrolysis, high temperature electrolysis of steam (HTES); thermochemical splitting of water through one or more of the iron oxide cycle, cerium(IV) oxide-cerium(III) oxide cycle, zinc zinc-oxide cycle, sulfur-iodine cycle, copper-chlorine cycle, calcium-bromine-iron cycle, hybrid sulfur cycle; electrolysis of hydrogen sulfide; thermochemical splitting of hydrogen sulfide; other electrochemical or thermochemical processes known to produce hydrogen with low- or no-carbon dioxide emissions including but not limited to: carbon capture and sequestration enabled methane reforming; carbon capture and sequestration enabled coal gasification; the Kvaerner-process and other processes generating a carbon-black product; carbon capture and sequestration enabled gasification or pyrolysis of biomass. Preferred Device: The bioreactor does not comprise transparent materials that expose the oxyhydrogen microorganisms and/or cell extracts containing enzymes from the oxyhydrogen microorganisms to light.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic carbon compound comprises carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide comprises carbon dioxide gas, either alone and/or dissolved in a mixture or solution further comprising carbonate ion and/or bicarbonate ion. The inorganic carbon compound comprises carbon contained in a solid phase. The electron donors comprise ammonia; ammonium; carbon monoxide; dithionate; elemental sulfur; hydrocarbons; hydrogen; metabisulfites; nitric oxide; nitrites; sulfates; thiosulfates; sulfides; sulfites; thionate; thionite; transition metals or their sulfides; oxides; chalcogenides; halides; hydroxides; oxyhydroxides; phosphates; sulfates; carbonates, in dissolved or solid phases; and conduction or valence band electrons in solid state electrode materials. The electron acceptors comprise carbon dioxide; oxygen; nitrites; nitrates; ferric iron or other transition metal ions; sulfates; or valence or conduction band holes in solid state electrode materials.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic compound containing only one carbon atom comprises carbon monoxide, methane, methanol, formate, and/or formic acid. The biochemicals comprise a carbon chain length of C5 or greater. The organic chemical product comprises compounds with carbon chain lengths between C5 and C30.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)
MC D05-A02; E10-C04J1P; E10-E04E; E10-J02D1; E10-J02D3; E11-M; E31-N05B1; H09-F03; J03-B
IP C12M-001/00; C12N-001/12; C12N-001/20; C12P-007/62; C25B-001/04; C25B-015/02; C12P-007/625; C12P-007/6463

PT P
PN CN108330708-A
TI Preparing purple hygroscopic acrylic fiber comprises e.g. adding acrylonitrile and ethylenediamine into flask, then adding deionized water, stirring, heat-refluxing, washing and placing in vacuum drying box to obtain aminated acrylonitrile.
AU XIE X
   HE W
   CHEN K
AE XIE X (XIEX-Individual)
GA 201860706W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing purple hygroscopic acrylic fiber comprises e.g. adding acrylonitrile and ethylenediamine into a three-mouth flask with reflux device, then adding deionized water, stirring, heating, refluxing, collecting acrylonitrile, washing and placing in a vacuum drying box to obtain aminated acrylonitrile, placing the bamboo in the muffle furnace, taking out air in a muffle furnace, heating up, sintering to obtain bamboo charcoal, crushing the bamboo charcoal to obtain active carbon powder and mixing the acrylonitrile and active carbon powder to obtain pre-dispersed material, adding the pre-dispersed material into a double-screw extruder, melting and extruding the melt pump for adjusting kettle to obtain aminated acrylonitrile melt, taking violet petals, adding distilled water, placing in a mortar, mixing, grinding, filtering and separating to obtain the filtrate, adding polyether diol into the filter liquid, washing with carbon dioxide to obtain mother liquor of carbon dichloride.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a purple hygroscopic acrylic fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The used raw materials improves the moisture absorption performance of acrylic fiber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a purple hygroscopic acrylic fiber comprises (i) adding 40-50 pts. wt. acrylonitrile and 20-30 pts. wt. ethylenediamine into a three-mouth flask with reflux device, then adding 20-25 pts. wt. deionized water, stirring, heating, refluxing, collecting acrylonitrile, washing with deionized water until the solution is neutral, washing the acrylonitrile and placing in a vacuum drying box to obtain aminated acrylonitrile; (ii) placing the bamboo in the muffle furnace, taking out air in a muffle furnace, heating up, sintering to obtain bamboo charcoal, crushing the bamboo charcoal by an airflow crusher to obtain active carbon powder and mixing the acrylonitrile and active carbon powder to obtain pre-dispersed material; (iii) adding the pre-dispersed material into a double-screw extruder, melting and extruding the melt pump for adjusting kettle to obtain aminated acrylonitrile melt; (iv) taking 200-300 g violet petals, adding 300-400 ml distilled water, placing in a mortar, mixing, grinding, filtering and separating to obtain the filtrate, adding 200-300 ml polyether diol into the filter liquid, washing with carbon dioxide for 3-5 times to obtain mother liquor of carbon dichloride, adding the mother liquor of carbon dichloride into rotary evaporator for rotary evaporation to constant weight to obtain purple powder, mixing the purple powder with dimethyl sulfoxide to obtain dyeing agent; (v) passing the aminated acrylonitrile melt into the spinning box, spraying the nascent fibers and washing the nascent fibers with water and then drawn by a drafting machine; and (vi) immersing the water washed nascent fiber, drawing into the dipping tank containing the dyeing agent to obtain colored tow and coloring the yarn, drying the bundle by a hot roll, cooling, crimpling, then passing the tow into a shaping pot for heat setting treatment and passing the shaped tow through a steam box to stretch the tow.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (i) the stirring speed is 200-400 rotations/minute, heating temperature is 80-100 degrees C, refluxing time is 4-5 hours, washing with de-ionized water at temperature of 60-70 degrees C and the setting temperature is 70-80 degrees C in the vacuum drying box. In step (ii) heating temperature is 1000-1100 degrees C, the sintering time is 3-4 hours, active carbon powder grain diameter is 400-500 nm and the aminated acrylonitrile is mixed with active carbon powder with mass ratio of 3:2. In step (iii) the control head temperature is 200-250 degrees C, controlling the melt viscosity reaction residence time to 30-50 minutes and adjusting the vacuum degree of the reaction kettle to 100-200 Pa. In step (iv) the carbon chloride mother solution rotary evaporation temperature is 50-55 degrees C, rotating and evaporating speed is 90-100 rotations/minute and the purple powder is mixed with dimethyl sulfoxide with mass ratio of 1:5. In step (v) the spinning temperature is 230-250 degrees C, water washing tank temperature is 80-90 degrees C, water washing flow rate is 20 30 l/minutes, the drafting multiple of drafting for 3-6 times and the drafting speed is 40-50 m/minute. In step (vi) the hot roller drying temperature is 120-150 degrees C, the hot roller drying time is 5-20 minutes, the heat-setting process time is 20-40 minutes, controlling the heat setting temperature to 110-150 degrees C, the pressure is 5-10 MPa, the setting temperature in steam box is 100-150 degrees C and the stretching ratio is 2:1.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)

PT P
PN KR2022122083-A
TI Manufacturing copper nano-wrinkle structure useful            as an electrocatalyst to convert carbon dioxide into            carbon material, comprises e.g. generating graphene on            copper substrate using chemical vapor deposition.
AU JUNG H T
   KIM J Y
   JUNG W B
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2022B5853H
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing copper nano-wrinkle structure                comprises (a) generating graphene on a copper                substrate using chemical vapor deposition (CVD);                and (b) cooling the copper substrate on which                graphene is generated to form nano-wrinkles on the                surface of the substrate by the difference in                thermal expansion energy between graphene and                copper.
   USE - The copper nano-wrinkle structure is useful as                an electrocatalyst to convert carbon dioxide into                2C material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper nano-wrinkle structure can be                applied to fields requiring step sites, high-plane                atomic arrangement, increase in surface area, or                regular surface arrangement at the nanoscale, can                be widely used in the field of catalysis, and has                improved stability from oxidation and surface                changes through graphene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method further                  comprises (c) removing the resulting graphene by                  electrolysis or etching. In the step (b), cooling                  is carried out at 20-30&#176; C. The step (a)                  is performed at 850-1000&#176; C. The method                  further comprises pre-treating the copper                  substrate with methane before step (a). The                  pretreatment time is 20 minutes to 2 hours, and                  the width of the nano-wrinkle is controlled                  according to the time. In the copper grain                  boundary, the nano-wrinkle direction is formed in                  the same direction, the width of the nano-wrinkle                  is 280-400 nm, the depth is 16-22 nm, and the                  wrinkle inclination consists of 200, 210, 310                  high plane atomic arrangement and the density of                  step sites is 2-3/nm.
   EXAMPLE - Plain copper film was transferred to a 4-inch                CVD quartz tube. The temperature was raised in a                hydrogen atmosphere (1000&#176; C), and when                the temperature was reached, a pretreatment was                performed in a methane atmosphere to form a uniform                monolayer graphene. After that, graphene was grown                and cooled again to room temperature in a hydrogen                atmosphere. During this cooling process,                nano-wrinkled structures were formed by the                difference in thermal expansion energy between the                graphene and copper surfaces. The process was                carried out over a large area (74.48 cm 2), and due                to the effect of methane pretreatment, a uniform                graphene monolayer was formed and thus receives a                similar level of difference in thermal expansion                energy, resulting in a uniform copper nano-wrinkle                shape.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Preparing aluminum polychloride water purifying agent comprises adding aluminum hydroxide powder into sodium hydroxide solution, heating, cooling, adding hydrochloric acid to neutralize, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting, filtering, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust basicity, heating and aging.
AU RUAN J
AE GUANGZHOU LUWEI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202245594E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aluminum polychloride water purifying agent comprises: (i) adding aluminum hydroxide powder into sodium hydroxide solution, and heating to obtain sodium aluminate solution; (ii) cooling sodium aluminate solution, adding hydrochloric acid to neutralize, adding hydrochloric acid while introducing carbon dioxide, and reacting to obtain mixed solution containing aluminum hydroxide and aluminum polychloride; and (iii) filtering mixed solution, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust basicity, heating and aging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aluminum polychloride water purifying agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared aluminum polychloride water purifying agent has low impurity content, high purity and good water purifying effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: A molar ratio of the aluminum hydroxide and sodium hydroxide is 1:1.1-1.3.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the heating process is carried out at 80-90degrees Celsius for 15-30 minutes. In the step (ii), the cooling process is carried out at 30-40degrees Celsius, the neutralization is carried out by adding 10-15 mol/L hydrochloric acid, the carbon dioxide is introduced into solution upward from bottom of solution under flow rate of 50-200 mL/minute, and the reacting process is carried out for 10-15 minutes by adding 5-8 mol/L hydrochloric acid. In the step (iii), the filtering process is carried out through 50-100 mesh gauze, the basicity of hydroxide/aluminum is 2-2.5, the heating process is carried out at 60-75degrees Celsius for 10-20 minutes, and the aging heating process is carried out at 20-30degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours.

TI Preparing dimethyl carbonate comprises reacting            methanol and carbon dioxide under catalyst catalysis,            where catalyst comprises tin(IV) oxide, and further            comprises cerium(IV) oxide and/or zirconium            dioxide.
AU HE X
   LING Y
   LI W
   WEI J
   SONG S
   ZENG W
   SHI M
AE UNIV XIANGNAN (UYXI-Non-standard)
GA 2021D18264
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dimethyl carbonate comprises                reacting methanol and carbon dioxide under catalyst                catalysis to prepare the dimethyl carbonate, where                the catalyst comprises tin(IV) oxide, and further                comprises cerium(IV) oxide and/or zirconium                dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dimethyl                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes tin dioxide as a                catalyst, which is easy to separate and has high                reaction activity and stability at the same time;                improves recovery rate of dimethyl carbonate; has                excellent economic benefits; is simple, and                economical; easily prepares dimethyl carbonate;                saves production costs; improves economic benefits;                has excellent production and application prospects;                synergistically increases the catalytic reaction                rate; increases the stability of the catalyst, so                as to make more reusable; and improves economic                benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the catalyst comprises: taking the solution                  containing the template and the precipitating                  agent, slowly adding to solution containing                  tin(IV) ion to form a turbid solution of the                  catalyst precursor, making the turbid liquid of                  the catalyst precursor, reacting, centrifuging                  and drying, then calcinating to obtain a                  catalyst. The pressure of carbon dioxide is 2-6                  MPa, preferably 4-5 MPa. The step of reaction is                  carried out at 10-160degrees Celsius, preferably                  140-150degrees Celsius for 8-16 hours, preferably                  12-16 hours.Preferred Components: The tin(IV) ion                  containing solution further comprises cerium(IV)                  ion and/or zirconium(IV) ion, preferably, the                  template is hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide                  or polyethylene glycol-400, preferably the                  precipitating agent is urea, ethylenediamine                  and/or aqueous ammonia. The catalyst is a mixture                  of cerium(IV) oxide and tin(IV) oxide in a molar                  ratio of 1-9:1-6, preferably 1-5:2-6, preferably                  the catalyst is mixture of zirconium dioxide and                  tin(IV) oxide in a mol ratio of 1-6:1-6,                  preferably 3-6:1-3. The catalyst comprises                  cerium(IV) oxide, zirconium dioxide and tin(IV)                  oxide in a mol ratio of 1-9:1-6:1-6, preferably                  1-5:3-6:1-3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises adding methyl formate to methanol and                  carbon dioxide, preferably, the added amount of                  methyl formate does not exceed 1/3 of the mass of                  methanol, and more preferably the mass ratio of                  methyl formate and methanol is 2-4:15. The method                  further comprises mixing methanol, methyl formate                  and catalyst, sealing, filling with carbon                  dioxide, and reacting to obtain the                  product.Preferred Components: The methanol and                  methyl formate are both liquid, and carbon                  dioxide is gaseous.

The reactor                  core shell and the side wall of the active area                  are provided with a control drum. The control                  drum is coated with neutron absorber. PT P
PN CN112920187-A; CN112920187-B
TI Simultaneously removing formaldehyde and synthetic metal complex used for catalyzing epoxide comprises adding ammonium salt and soluble metal salt introducing formaldehyde-containing gas, stirring, reacting, crystallizing, and purifying.
AU LAN D
   CHEN K
   YI B
   ZHU H
   PU L
   SHEN J
   TAN N
   OU Z
AE HUNAN INST ENG (HNIE-C)
GA 2021655666
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneously removing formaldehyde and synthetic metal complex comprises adding ammonium salt and soluble metal salt into formaldehyde-containing solution, introducing formaldehyde-containing gas into the mixed solution of ammonium salt and soluble metal salt, stirring and reacting, crystallizing, and purifying.
   USE - The metal complex is useful for catalyzing epoxide, preferably epoxy ethane, epoxy chloropropane, propylene oxide, epoxy butane, epoxy cyclohexene, oxidized cyclopentene and oxidized styrene, and carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction to synthesize cyclic carbonic ester (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: firstly uses ammonium salt to recycle the formaldehyde into hexamethylene tetramine, then uses coordination effect of metal salt and hexamethylenetetramine to promote formaldehyde conversion and form stable metal complex for efficient catalytic carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ammonium salt comprises ammonium nitrate, ammonium chloride, ammonium bromide, ammonium iodide, ammonium fluoride, ammonium phosphate, ammonium hydrogen phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate and/or ammonium acetate. The soluble metal salt comprises nitrate of zinc, iron, calcium or magnesium, chlorine salt, bromine salt, iodine salt, fluorine salt, phosphate, hydrophosphate and/or dihydric phosphate. The mol ratio of the ammonium salt and the soluble metal salt is 4-20:1. The concentration of the formaldehyde in formaldehyde-containing solution is 0.1-10 g/l, preferably 5-100 mg/l. The mol ratio of the ammonium salt and the formaldehyde is 1-10:1. The mol ratio of the metal complex to the epoxide is 0.005-0.05:1. Preferred Method: The stirring reaction is carried out for 10 minute to 6 hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN WO2021100971-A1; KR2021062528-A; KR2364604-B1
TI Composition comprises manganese-enzyme hybrid cascade system containing Clostridium carboxidivorans-derived formate dehydrogenase, Saccharomyces cerevisiae-derived alcohol dehydrogenase, and manganese ions.
AU LEE J
   PATEL S
   CHOI H
   JOO J
   LEE J G
   SANJAY K S P
AE UNIV KONKUK IND COOP CORP (UKUK-C)
GA 202157298R
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises a manganese-enzyme hybrid cascade system composed of Clostridium carboxidivorans-derived formate dehydrogenase (CcFDH), Pseudomonas cepacia genomvar II-derived formate dehydrogenase (PcFalDH), Saccharomyces cerevisiae-derived alcohol dehydrogenase (ScADH), and manganese ions.
   USE - The composition is useful for producing methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition enables to produce methanol from carbon dioxide more stably, simply and effectively, and exhibits much higher methanol productivity than complex enzyme immobilized in the existing one.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing methanol from carbon dioxide, which involves reacting Mn-enzyme hybrid cascade system composed of Clostridium carboxidivorans-derived formate dehydrogenase (CcFDH), Pseudomonas cepacia genomvar II-derived formate dehydrogenase (PcFalDH), Saccharomyces cerevisiae-derived alcohol dehydrogenase (ScADH), and Mn ions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises NADH and sodium bicarbonate. Preferred Component: The manganese-enzyme hybrid is characterized in that manganese(II) phosphate-CcFDH-PcFaldDH-ScADH (manganese-ME) form, where the manganese-enzyme hybrid is formed at 0.1-10 degrees C for 2-48 hours. The enzyme is CcFDH, PcFaldDH and ScADH which are each mixed in a ratio of 0.1-3:0.1-5:0.1-2. The manganese ion is manganese(II) sulfate, where the concentration of manganese ion is 0.5-2.5 mM. Preferred Method: The manganese-enzyme hybrid is cross-linked by glutaraldehyde. The method involves performing crosslinking after addition of glutaraldehyde, and incubating at 0.1-10 degrees C for 0.5-8 hours. The method involves performing reaction at 25-60 degrees C, preferably 30 degrees C under a pH of 5-9, where the reaction is a reduction reaction of carbon dioxide by a cascade reaction.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN112647090-A
TI Preparing hydrophobic high molecular film load            evaporation metal gas diffusion electrode in            electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, involves            purging surface of hydrophobic high molecular film with            nitrogen or argon.
AU YANG H
   CAO L
   LIU P
   CHENG J
   WU X
   MAO F
   YUAN H
   YANG X
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202143459P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrophobic high molecular film load                evaporation metal gas diffusion electrode, involves                cleaning surface of hydrophobic high molecular film                with nitrogen or argon, and evaporating layer of                metal with thickness of 1-1000 nm on hydrophobic                high molecular film.
   USE - Method for preparing a hydrophobic polymer                film supported vapor-deposited metal gas diffusion                electrode for use in the field of electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables prepare a hydrophobic                polymer film supported vapor-deposited metal gas                diffusion electrode, which has high selectivity for                the multi-carbon product, plays an important role                in pushing the electro-catalysis material to                further realize the device, and has very wide                application prospect in the environment science and                new energy field.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrophobic                  polymer membrane is polytetrafluoroethylene. The                  hydrophobic polymer membrane also has a                  supporting material, and the supporting material                  is polypropylene or polyethylene terephthalate.                  The metal material is copper, silver, gold or                  zinc.
