PN CN112479696-A
TI Production principle of fracturing propping agent            by performing high-temperature composite reaction of            silicon dioxide, sodium carbonate and calcium carbonate            to prepare calcium silicate and sodium silicate and            carbon dioxide gas.
AU LIU Z
   WANG S
   ZENG J
   LIU X
AE SICHUAN PETROLEUM ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202130761B
AB    NOVELTY - Production principle of fracturing propping                agent involves performing high-temperature                composite reaction of silicon dioxide, sodium                carbonate and calcium carbonate to prepare calcium                silicate and sodium silicate, and carbon dioxide                gas as by-product.
   USE - Production principle of fracturing propping                agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The production principle provides fracturing                propping agent with low crushing rate, and                excellent spherical shape, compressive resistance,                fluidity, mechanical strength, electrical                insulation property, chemical property and heat                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of fracturing propping agent, which                involves: (1) pre-processing raw materials; (2)                preparing batch materials; (3) melting at high                temperature; (4) processing and forming; (5) heat                treating; (6) crushing cloth; (7) heating and                melting; (8) cooling and recycling; and (9)                collecting in stages.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The chemical reactions are                  all carried out in a high temperature                  environment. The step (1) involves drying the raw                  materials containing 35-60 pts. wt. quartz sand                  powder, 20-42 pts. wt. soda ash, 30-45 pts. wt.                  limestone and 10-20 pts. wt. feldspar powder,                  crushing, and then subjecting to iron removal                  treatment to ensure the quality of the raw                  materials. The step (3) involves heating the                  prepared propping agent batch in a tank kiln or                  crucible kiln at high temperature of 1550-1600                  degrees C to form a uniform, bubble-free liquid                  propping agent that meets molding requirement.                  The heat treatment step in step (5) involves                  annealing the propping agent after high                  temperature processing and molding at 700-800                  degrees C for 10-15 minutes, quenching, cleaning                  or generating internal stress, performing phase                  separation or crystallization of the propping                  agent, and changing the structural state of the                  propping agent. The step (6) involves crushing                  the molded propping agent to specified particle                  size, and evenly pouring the powder into a                  special beading furnace through a distributor.                  The step (7) involves heating and melting in hot                  high-temperature air flow whose temperature is                  1750-1850 degrees C to form a spherical propping                  agent under the action of the propping agent                  surface tension. The propping agent is in a                  floating state while being heated and melted, and                  then cooled, recovered, sieved and classified to                  finally obtain the required special fracturing                  propping agent of different particle                  diameters.

TI Devolatilizing trans-butadiene rubber by loading            trans-butadiene rubber into extraction tank,            introducing supercritical fluid comprising carbon            dioxide, extracting trans-butadiene rubber, and            reducing extracted rubber under pressure.
AU DI J
   CUI L
   WU W
   WANG Q
   LI L
   LIU N
   XU J
AE CHAMBROAD CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (CHAM-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG HUAJU HIGH POLYMER MATERIAL CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202088429U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for devolatilizing trans-butadiene                rubber, involves (a) loading trans-butadiene rubber                into an extraction tank, introducing supercritical                fluid comprising carbon dioxide, and increasing the                extraction pressure to 5-35 MPa, (b) extracting the                trans-butadiene rubber under extraction pressure by                flowing the supercritical fluid to obtain the                supercritical fluid containing volatile substance                and extracted trans-butadiene rubber, and (c)                reducing the extracted trans-butadiene rubber under                pressure to obtain the devolatilized                trans-butadiene rubber.
   USE - The method is useful for devolatilizing                trans-butadiene rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves excellent solubility and                diffusion properties of the supercritical fluid,                increases the solubility of volatiles in the fluid,                and increases the mass transfer rate of volatiles                in the polymer to achieve deep                devolatilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the total                  volatile content of the trans-butadiene rubber is                  1-5 wt.%, the content of isoprene is 2000-6000                  part per million (ppm), and the content of dimer                  is 2000-6000 ppm, and the filling amount of the                  extraction tank is 100-500 g, and the temperature                  is 30-60&#176; C, and the extraction pressure                  is 10-15 MPa. In step (b), the extraction                  temperature is 30-60&#176; C, the flow rate                  is 5-8 kg/hour, and the time is 0.5-4 hours. In                  step (c), the reduced pressure is 0 MPa, and the                  pressure reduction time is 5-20 minutes.                  Preferred Method: The step (b) involves                  discharging the supercritical fluid containing                  volatiles, separating, and adsorbing under                  reduced pressure to obtain a purified                  supercritical fluid, where the derived frequency                  is 3-5 times/hour. The method involves                  vacuum-separating at 40-50&#176; C under a                  pressure of 4.5-5 MPa. The adsorption method is                  activated carbon adsorption.

The organic                  template comprises polyoxyethylene                  polyoxypropylene block copolymer and/or                  hexamethylene tetramine, preferably F127, F108,                  hexamethylenetetramine, P123 and/or P104. The                  extracting agent is ether.

PN CN110327988-A
TI Preparing PCN-222 (Cu)/titanium dioxide composite photocatalyst useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises dissolving titanium oxide nanoparticles in dimethylformamide, sonicating, reacting, cooling washing and drying.
AU WANG L
   HUANG G
   DUAN S
   HUANG J
   WANG Q
AE UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
GA 201986889P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing PCN-222 (Cu)/titanium dioxide composite photocatalyst, comprises stirring titanium oxide nanoparticles and dissolving in dimethylformamide solution, adding PCN-222 (Cu), stirring at room temperature for 20-30 minutes, sonicating for 5-10 minutes, placing mixed solution in an autoclave, reacting at 110-125 degrees C for 20-24 hours, completing reaction, naturally cooling to room temperature and centrifuging, washing collected product and drying.
   USE - The PCN-222(Cu)/titanium oxide composite photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves synergistic effect of PCN-222(Cu) and titanium oxide effectively the photocatalytic activity of composite, improves dispersibility of titanium oxide nanoparticles, providing more active sites and provides higher photocatalytic activity and catalytic stability to PCN-222(Cu)titanium oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The addition amount of PCN-222 (Cu) is 5-15% (mass of titanium dioxide nanoparticles). Preferred Method: The drying is dried at 60-80 degrees C for 10-12 hours.

PN CN110255819-A
TI Producing vehicle diesel, by adding landfill extract into open-air pond, adjusting pH, absorbing carbon dioxide in atmosphere by photosynthesis carbon of algal microorganisms, transporting and dividing landfill extract.
AU TONG B
AE TONG B (TONG-Individual)
GA 2019921049
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing vehicle diesel using landfill extract, involves adding landfill extract into open-air pond, adjusting pH of the landfill extract to 3-10, absorbing carbon dioxide in the atmosphere by photosynthesis carbon of the algal microorganisms in the landfill extract, transporting landfill extract to high temperature autoclave, heating landfill extract at 380-400 degrees C, pressurizing the extract at 1.6-2.5 MPa, after 8-12 minutes of distillation, and dividing landfill extract into upper oil phase and lower layer of the precipitate, which is cooled by the upper oil phase to obtain diesel fuel for vehicle.
   USE - The method is useful for producing vehicle diesel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The landfill extract heating temperature is 390 degrees C. The algal microorganisms in the landfill extract include salt algae, Chlamydomonas, algae, Spirulina, Brachypodium fuliginea, Chlorella pyrenoidosa and Aeromonas aeruginosa. The heating is electric heating and the pressure is pressurized by a steam compressor. In photosynthesis carbon fixation of the algal microorganisms in the landfill extract, the illumination time of the landfill extract is controlled for 8-12 hours.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. TI Preparation of molybdenum disulfide/tin(IV) sulfide/reduced graphene oxide composite photo-catalyst for reduction of carbon dioxide comprises hydrothermal-treating thioacetamide, sodium molybdate, reduced graphene oxide and tin(IV) sulfide.
AU YIN S
   WANG H
   SUN L
   ZHOU Y
   GUAN J
   LI J
   LI X
   HUO P
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
   UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201992008A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of molybdenum disulfide (MoS2)/tin(IV) sulfide (SnS2)/reduced graphene oxide (r-GO) composite photo-catalyst comprises preparing SnS2 precursor; placing thioacetamide and sodium molybdate in deionized water, adding r-GO to form mixed solution; adding SnS2 precursor into mixed solution, stirring, pouring into vacuum reaction kettle into drying box for hydrothermal treatment, taking out and cooling naturally to room temperature, washing with deionized water and ethanol and drying.
   USE - The method is preparation of molybdenum disulfide (MoS2)/tin(IV) sulfide (SnS2)/reduced graphene oxide (r-GO) composite photo-catalyst for reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and does not cause waste resource and formation of secondary pollutants. It is environment-friendly and a high-efficient pollution treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The SnS2 precursor is prepared by mixing tin chloride pentahydrate, L-cysteine and sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, stirring for 0.5 hour, pouring into high pressure reactor, subjecting to hydrothermal treatment at 160 degrees C for 24 hours and centrifuging to obtain solid. Preferred Components: The tin chloride pentahydrate, L-cysteine and sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate has mass ratio of 0.0877:0.2423:0.5645. The mass ratio of sodium molybdate to reduced graphene oxide is 4.62-6.35:1. The ratio of thioacetamide, sodium molybdate and deionized water is 1.56 mmol:1 mmol:30 mL. The mass ratio of SnS2 to MoS2/r-GO in MoS2/SnS2/r-GO composite photocatalyst is 1:2.49-4.03. The MoS2/SnS2/r-GO composite photocatalyst has morphology having flower-like structure with size of 40-500 nm.

TI Semiconductor processing equipment, has optical system for delivering laser beam in radiation source for lithographic apparatus employed to fabricate integrated circuit chips, where laser system comprises carbon dioxide laser.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 201923844Q
AB    NOVELTY - The equipment has an optical system for delivering laser beam in a radiation source for a lithographic apparatus employed to fabricate integrated circuit chips. The laser system comprises a carbon dioxide laser that is used to convert fuel droplet into plasma to generate radiation such as EUV radiation, for the lithographic apparatus. The laser system generates laser pre-pulse (PP) to condition the fuel droplet for receipt of a laser main pulse (MP), where the laser MP hits the fuel droplet and converts the fuel droplet into plasma to generate the radiation.

TI Preserving fresh fruits and vegetables by controlling Botrytis cinerea damage by air-conditioning method comprises placing fruits and vegetables in sealed container, and replacing gas in container with mixed gas of carbon dioxide and oxygen.
AU GUO Y
   ZHANG Y
   WANG L
   HUANG X
   LI T
   CHEN Y
   LIU S
   YUN L
AE UNIV SHANDONG TECHNOLOGY (SDUT-C)
   ZIBO VEGETABLE OFFICE (ZIBO-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG QIHE BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201918550S
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving fresh fruits and vegetables by controlling Botrytis cinerea damage by air-conditioning method comprises placing fruits and vegetables in a sealed container, replacing the gas in the sealed container with mixed gas of more than 10 vol.% carbon dioxide, and oxygen (remaining amount) having specific volume ratio, and sealing the container after replacement with the mixed gas for preserving the fresh fruits and vegetables.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving fresh fruits and vegetables from Botrytis cinerea damage by air-conditioning method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has excellent fresh keeping effect on fruits and vegetables; and greatly reduces the harm of B. cinerea on fruits and vegetables.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method comprises placing the fruits and vegetables along with ethylene absorbent and drying agent in the sealed container, and maintaining relative humidity of air in the sealed container at 75-95%.
   ACTIVITY - Fungicide.

PT P
PN WO2019030192-A1; TW201920066-A; KR2020036926-A; CN111032614-A; EP3665150-A1; US2020199060-A1; BR112020002460-A2; US10793511-B2; JP2020529454-W; IN202017008228-A; IN390333-B; TW761571-B1
TI Converting cyclic alkyleneureas into their            corresponding alkyleneamines by reacting feedstock            comprising cyclic alkyleneureas in liquid phase with            water, with removal of carbon dioxide.
AU TEN KATE A J B
   VENEMAN R
   RAAIJMAKERS M J T
   JOVIC S
   EDVINSSON R K
   VAN DAM H
   KANTZER E N
   EHLERS I
   SKANSEN B P
   SARNING M B E
   LAKE K F
   WERNERSSON S M
   TENKATE A J B
   VANDAM H
   PATRICK S
   WERNERSONSTIGMICAEL
   ANDERSSON-SARNING M B
   KATE A J B T
   DAM H V
AE AKZO NOBEL CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (NOUR-Non-standard)
   AKZO NOBEL CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (NOUR-Non-standard)
GA 201915326G
AB    NOVELTY - Cyclic alkyleneureas are converted into their                corresponding alkyleneamines by reacting feedstock                comprising cyclic alkyleneureas in liquid phase                with water in an amount of 0.1-20 mole water per                mole urea moiety, at greater than or equal to 230                degrees C, with removal of carbon dioxide                (CO2).
   USE - Conversion of cyclic alkyleneureas into their                corresponding alkyleneamines.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows the efficient conversion of                alkyleneureas into the corresponding alkyleneamines                with high yield and low side product generation.                The process yields less side product cyclic                alkyleneureas, where the alkyleneurea moiety is                present between 2 further alkyleneamine moieties on                the one hand, and at the same time, avoids the use                of a strong base and large amounts of aqueous                solvent, avoiding or at least minimizing salt waste                streams, corrosion, and product degradation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Cyclic alkyleneurea of                  formula (I) reacts to an alkyleneamine in                  accordance with reaction scheme (RS1). Water is                  added in multiple dosings or continuously during                  the process. CO2 is removed during the                  reaction.R1, R2=H, alkylene amine of formula                  X-R3-(NH-R3-)p-, or alkoxy of formula                  X-R3-(O-R3-)n-, or group combining such alkylene                  amine and alkoxy units p and n, where optionally                  greater than or equal to 1 units -N-R3-N- may be                  present as either one of the rings of formulae                  (III) or (IV);R3=optionally substituted alkylene;X=OH, amine, 1-20C hydroxyalkyl, or 1-20C                  aminoalkyl; andn, p=2-20, optionally containing greater                  than or equal to 1 piperazine, or alkylene urea                  groups, or when p or n is 0, may be a 1-20C                  hydroxyalkyl or 1-20C aminoalkyl.Preferred Compositions: Cyclic alkyleneurea                  comprises ethyleneurea, urea derivative of                  ethylenediamine (EDA), urea derivative of                  diethylenetriamine (DETA), group of urea                  derivatives of triethylenetetraamine (TETA),                  diurea derivative of triethylenetetramine, urea                  derivatives of tetraethylenpentamine (TEPA),                  diurea derivatives of TEPA, urea derivatives of                  pentaethylenehexamine (PEHA) and higher analogs,                  urea derivative of aminoethylethanolamine, urea                  derivative of hydroxyethyl diethylenetriamine,                  urea derivative of hydroxyethyl                  triethylenetetraamine, and/or diurea derivative                  of hydroxyethyl triethylenetetraamine. Feedstock                  comprises greater than or equal to 10 mol.%,                  preferably greater than or equal to 20 mol.%                  cyclic urea derivatives of alkyleneamine                  compounds comprising -NH-R3-NH-R3-NH-R3-NH-                  moiety, calculated on the total of cyclic urea                  compounds present in the mixture. Molar ratio of                  water to urea moieties is less than or equal to                  15 mole water per mole urea moiety, preferably                  less than or equal to 5 mole water per mole urea                  moiety. Preferred Conditions: Reaction is carried                  out at 240-400 degrees C, preferably 250-320                  degrees C. Reaction is carried out for a reaction                  time of 15 minutes to 24 hours, preferably 1-6                  hours.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R2=H; and
   R3=ethylene, propylene, or isopropylene,                preferably ethylene.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN108854520-A
TI Method for treating factory exhaust gas, involves collecting dust from exhaust gas by static electricity, performing catalytic reduction of nitrogen monoxide, fully burning and oxidizing by flame, converting and recovering carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG R
AE ZHANG R (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2018957082
AB    NOVELTY - A factory exhaust gas treating method involves collecting dust from the exhaust gas by static electricity and performing catalytic reduction of nitrogen monoxide to form nitrogen. The reduced exhaust gas is fully burned and oxidized by a flame, converted and recovered the carbon dioxide, entered the exhaust gas to the absorption device and absorbed. The exhaust gas of the absorption device is finally discharged directly.
   USE - Method for treating factory exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables treating the factory exhaust gas with better effect of reducing the discharge of the harmful gas, meeting the national gas discharge standard and the harmful gas converting application.

PN WO2018213821-A1; US2020171474-A1
TI New catalyst comprising catalytically active            compound used to transform glycerol and carbon dioxide,            carbonate salt and bicarbonate salt, and make formic            acid and lactic acid.
AU HELTZEL J M
   FINN M T
   VOUTCHKOVA-KOSTAL A
AE UNIV WASHINGTON GEORGE (WAOR-C)
   UNIV WASHINGTON GEORGE (WAOR-C)
GA 2018930010
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprising a catalytically active                compound (I) is new.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for: transforming                glycerol and any one carbon dioxide, a carbonate                salt and a bicarbonate salt; and making formic acid                and lactic acid (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprising a catalytically active                compound of formula (M1(NHC-R-linker)aLbXc) (I) is                new.
   M1 = transition metal;
   NHC = N-heterocyclic carbene ligand;
   R = alkyl or aryl;
   linker = polar group;
   L = neutral ligand;
   X = anionic ligand;
   a = 1-3;
   b = 0-3; and
   c = 0-3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) making formic acid and lactic acid                comprising reacting in a reaction solution, a                carbon feedstock and glycerol in the presence of                the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is given.                  Preferred Components: The catalyst further                  comprises solid support, where (I) is immobilized                  on the solid support via ionic or columbic                  interactions, or via a covalent bond. (I) is                  indirectly bonded to the solid support via a                  secondary linking compound, where the secondary                  linking compound covalently bonded with both of                  the linker and the solid support. The solid                  support has any one of a 3-dimensional structure,                  a 2 dimensional structure, a 1 dimensional                  structure, and a 0 dimensional structure. The                  solid support is non-porous, porous or a                  hydrotalcite material including any one of copper                  ions (preferred), zinc, iron, chromium, nickel,                  cobalt and/or iridium. (I) is in the form of a                  sodium or potassium salt. The carbon feedstock is                  in carbon dioxide, where the carbon feedstock in                  a carbonate salt or a bicarbonate salt. The                  carbonate salt or bicarbonate salt is any one of                  potassium carbonate (preferred), lithium                  carbonate, lithium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate,                  sodium bicarbonate, sodium sesquicarbonate,                  potassium bicarbonate, magnesium carbonate,                  magnesium bicarbonate iron carbonate, calcium                  carbonate, strontium carbonate or barium                  carbonate. The reaction solution further                  comprises water or base, where the base is                  potassium hydroxide (preferred), sodium                  hydroxide, lithium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide,                  magnesium hydroxide, strontium hydroxide or                  barium hydroxide, sodium tert-butoxide, potassium                  tert-butylate, potassium carbonate or sodium                  carbonate. Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises solid support. The method is performed                  under batch conditions, or under continuous flow                  conditions. The method is performed in a reaction                  vessel configured to be pressurized. The method                  is performed in: an autoclave, the autoclave                  comprising a glass insert, a means for agitating                  a reaction solution within the autoclave, and a                  means for removing a sample of the reaction                  solution from the autoclave; performed in a                  microwave reactor; and performed in a continuous                  flow reactor The method is performed at a                  temperature of 25-400 degrees C, preferably                  150-225 degrees C. The method is performed using                  (I) at a concentration of 0.25-4 (preferably 1)                  mol.% relative to glycerol. The reaction solution                  has a glycerol concentration of 1-15 (preferably                  6-7) M. The method takes place in the presence of                  at least one gas and a pressure of 1-100                  (preferably 25-50) bar, where the gas comprises                  carbon dioxide, argon or nitrogen. The takes                  place in the presence of a flow of carbon                  dioxide, where the flow of carbon dioxide is at a                  flow rate of 10-50 (preferably 30-35)                  ml/minutes.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M1 = Rh, Fe, Co, Ru (Ru+2) or Ir (Ir+ or                Ir+3);
   NHC = e.g. 2,6-bis(1-imidazolyl)pyridine,                1,1'-methylenebis(imidazole),                2-(imidazol-1-yl)pyridine, 1-methylimidazole,                N-trimethylsilylimidazole,                1-(2-chlorophenyl)imidazole, 1-(3-bromobenzyl)-1H-                imidazole, 1-(3-chlorophenyl)imidazole,                1-(3-fluorophenyl)imidazole, 1-(4-                chlorophenyl)imidazole and                1-(4-fluorophenyl)imidazole;
   R = propyl, butyl, 1,8-naphthyl, phenyl or                benzyl;
   L = CO, p-cymene, 1,5-cyclooctadiene, 1,3-                cyclohexadiene, 1,4-cyclohexadiene, ethylene,                pyridine, a thiol, an alcohol, a phosphine, a                trialkyl phosphine, a triaryl phosphine or                phosphine oxide;
   X = pentamethylcyclopentadienyl, NO, CN,                acetate, allyl, a halide, carbonate, triflate or                trifluoroacetate; and
   linker = sulfonate, carboxylate, an acyl                halide, hydroxylamine or a trialkoxysilyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparation method of m-xylene diisocyanate, involves performing salt formation reaction of m-xylylenediamine with carbon dioxide, reacting with phosgene, and performing cryogenic crystallization and high-vacuum distillation combined method.
AU QIAN S
   HAN B
   LI W
   ZHOU Q
   FU J
   XU X
   LU D
   LI Q
   SUN Y
AE JIANGSU KUAIDA AGROCHEMICAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201883636B
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of m-xylene diisocyanate involves (1) using m-xylylenediamine as raw materials, and performing salt formation reaction with carbon dioxide under the presence of solvent to obtain amino formate, (2) reacting the amino formate with phosgene to prepare m-xylene diisocyanate, and (3) performing cryogenic crystallization and high-vacuum distillation combined method of m-xylene diisocyanate.
   USE - Preparation method of m-xylene diisocyanate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of m-xylene diisocyanate with high content, yield and purity, which does not contain large amount of waste acid, and avoids the formation of by-product urea and the occurrence of polymerization by high-temperature continuous distillation to reduce the product content, by economical and environmentally-friendly process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The salt formation reaction temperature is 25-30 degrees C. In the step (2), the phosgene is introduced to carry out the photochemical reaction at 60-135 degrees C. In step (3), the vacuum degree of high vacuum distillation is 20-100 Pa, preferably 50 Pa. In step (3), the crystallization and recrystallization temperature is -10 to -40 degrees C, preferably -22 to -35 degrees C. Preferred Components: In step (1), the solvent is o-dichlorobenzene or chlorobenzene. Preferred Composition: The concentration of m-xylylenediamine and solvent is 5-15 %mass, preferably 7-8 %mass. The molar ratio of m-xylylenediamine to phosgene is 1:5, and the optimum ratio is 1:(2.5-3). Preferred Property: In the step (3), the technical index of the m-xylene diisocyanate product has printed content at 99.6% and chromaticity of less than 20. Preferred Process: When m-xylene diisocyanate is stored, the polymerization inhibitor is added, and an inert gas is filled to isolate air and avoid deterioration caused by oxidation.

AU RAHBAR N

   ROSEWITZ J A
AE WORCESTER POLYTECHNIC INST (WORE-C)
   WORCESTER POLYTECHNIC INST (WORE-C)
GA 2018675020
AB    NOVELTY - Sealing of cementitious surface involves contacting at least one opening or fissure of the cementitious surface with an aqueous solution of carbonic anhydrase, calcium chloride, and carbon dioxide, forming calcium carbonate and precipitating on the opening or fissure.
   USE - Sealing of cementitious surface of concrete structure such as buildings that are in direct contact with humans.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient and inexpensive, and generates less heat and avoids using toxic or bioactive materials. The use of carbonic anhydrase enzymes provides rapid, strong and environmentally safe repair of concrete structure. The generation of calcium carbonate improves compressive strength of concrete having smaller flaw sizes occurred over a longer time period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solution is exposed to an ambient atmosphere including carbon dioxide or ambient air. Preferred Properties: The aqueous solution has pH of 3-9.5, preferably 6.5-9.5. Preferred Composition: The carbonic anhydrase has molar concentration of 0.5 mu M -1 mM, preferably 1-10 mu M. The calcium chloride has molar concentration of 0.5-5 M, preferably 1.5-2.5 M.

PN CN114849654-A
TI Preparing oxalic acid intercalated expanded kaolin            comprises e.g. preparing expanded kaolin, preparing            lanthanum(III) and yttrium(III) solution, adding            expanded kaolin to the solution, performing static            adsorption, adjusting the pH of solution with            hydrochloric acid.
AU FAN J
   DOU W
   DENG Z
AE UNIV CHENGDU TECHNOLOGY (UYCT-C)
GA 2022A47935
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oxalic acid intercalated expanded                kaolin, comprises e.g. (i) mixing kaolin, dimethyl                sulfoxide, and ultrapure water, remove the milky                white viscosity using ethanol, filtering the thick                solution, drying the filter cake to obtain                kaolin-dimethyl sulfoxide intercalated complex,                (ii) mixing kaolin-dimethyl sulfoxide intercalated                complex and oxalic acid solution, filtering the                milky white solution, drying the filter cake to                obtain kaolin-oxalic acid intercalated complex,                (iii) mixing kaolin-oxalic acid intercalated,                sodium carbonate and oxalic acid, calcining in a                muffle furnace, washing to obtain expanded kaolin,                and (iv) preparing a certain concentration of                lanthanum(III) and yttrium(III) solution and adding                expanded kaolin to the solution, performing static                adsorption, adjusting the pH of solution with                hydrochloric acid, then reaching the adsorption                equilibrium, taking the supernatant and measuring                the concentrations of lanthanum(III) and                yttrium(III) in the solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing oxalic acid                intercalated expanded kaolin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: overcomes the problem of low                adsorption efficiency due to strong hydrogen                bonding between kaolin layers and significantly                improves the adsorption performance of                kaolin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing oxalic acid intercalated expanded                kaolin, comprises (i) mixing kaolin, dimethyl                sulfoxide, and ultrapure water in a certain                proportion, stirring magnetically for a certain                period of time at a suitable temperature, remove                the milky white viscosity using ethanol, then                suction filtering the thick solution, and drying                the filter cake in an oven at 60&#8451; to                obtain kaolin-dimethyl sulfoxide intercalated                complex, (ii) mixing kaolin-dimethyl sulfoxide                intercalated complex and oxalic acid solution                (ethanol as solvent) in a certain proportion,                stirring magnetically for a certain period of time                at room temperature, and filtering the milky white                solution with ethanol and drying the filter cake in                an oven at 60&#8451; to obtain a kaolin -oxalic                acid intercalated complex, and (iii) mixing                kaolin-oxalic acid intercalated, sodium carbonate,                oxalic acid in an agate mortar and grinding for 30                minutes in a certain proportion, adding it into a                ceramic crucible and calcining in a muffle furnace                for a certain period of time (the heating rate is                5&#8451;/minute), cooling to room temperature,                and washing with water for 2-3 times to obtain                expanded kaolin, the reactions involved in the                calcination process includes reacting oxalic acid                with sodium carbonate to obtain sodium oxalate and                liberates carbon dioxide and water, and converting                oxalic acid into carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide                and water, and (iv) preparing a certain                concentration of lanthanum(III) and yttrium(III)                solution and adding expanded kaolin to the solution                and placing it in the solution, performing static                adsorption in a constant temperature water bath,                and adjusting the pH of the solution with 0.1                mol/litter hydrochloric acid, then reaching the                adsorption equilibrium, taking the supernatant and                measuring the concentrations of lanthanum(III) and                yttrium(III) in the solution by inductively coupled                plasma-optical emission spectroscopy                (ICP-OES).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the mass                  volume ratio of kaolin, dimethyl sulfoxide, and                  ultrapure water is 5-10g: 50-100ml: 4.5-9 (g: ml:                  ml), and the magnetically stirring is carried out                  at 60-90&#8451; for 12-48 hours, the drying                  time is 6-12 hours. In step (ii), the mass volume                  ratio of kaolin dimethyl sulfoxide precursor and                  oxalic acid solution (ethanol is used as solvent)                  is 1-1.5:25 (1-2mol/l) (g: ml), the magnetically                  stirring is carried out for 24-48 hours, the                  drying is carried out for 6- 12 hours. In step                  (iii), the mass ratio of kaolin-oxalic acid                  intercalation complex, sodium carbonate and                  oxalic acid is 1:0.8-1:1-1.2 and calcination is                  carried out at 300-400&#8451; for 10-30                  minutes. In step (iv), the concentration range of                  lanthanum (III) and yttrium (III) solution is                  20-500mg/l, constant temperature water bath                  temperature is 25-30&#8451;, and pH of the                  solution is 5.

PN US2022154453-A1
TI Construction board for use in roofing system, has foam body having first and second planar surfaces, facer arranged on one of first and second planar surfaces of foam body, first coating arranged on external planar surface, and second coating including polymeric binder.
AU HUBBARD M J
   YAO C
   LETTS J B
AE FIRESTONE BUILDING PROD CO LLC (FIRE-C)
GA 202268652M
AB    NOVELTY - Construction board has a foam body having first and second planar surfaces. The foam body includes a polyisocyanurate foam matrix defining a set of closed cells. The closed cell includes carbon dioxide, air, or a partial vacuum. A facer is arranged on one of the first and second planar surfaces of foam body. The facer includes a glass substrate having an internal planar surface proximate to said foam body and an external planar surface opposite foam body. A first coating is arranged on said external planar surface. A second coating is arranged on or proximate to said internal planar surface. The first coating is arranged on external planar surface and includes filler. The filler consists inert filler. The inert filler is 3-30 wt.% with respect to the entire weight of first coating. The second coating is arranged on or proximate to internal planar surface that includes intumescent material. The second coating includes a polymeric binder in which intumescent material is arranged.
   USE - Construction board for use in roofing system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The construction board improves fire properties and flame resistance, and achieves frequency modulation class 1 approvals for steel deck insulated roofs without the use of gypsum-based coverboard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Construction board has a foam body (12) having first and second planar surfaces. The foam body includes a polyisocyanurate foam matrix defining a set of closed cells. The closed cell includes carbon dioxide, air, or a partial vacuum and less than 0.5 vol.% hydrocarbon blowing agents. The foam body is characterized by a density, pursuant to ASTM C303, of 2.5 lbs/ft3. The foam is characterized by an ISO Index of from 220 to less than 360. A facer (14) is arranged on one of the first and second planar surfaces of foam body. The facer includes a glass substrate (18) having an internal planar surface proximate to said foam body and an external planar surface opposite foam body. A first coating is arranged on said external planar surface. A second coating is arranged on or proximate to said internal planar surface. The first coating is arranged on external planar surface and includes filler. The filler consists inert filler. The inert filler is present in an amount of 3-30 wt.% with respect to the entire weight of the first coating. The second coating is arranged on or proximate to internal planar surface that includes intumescent material. The second coating includes a polymeric binder in which intumescent material is arranged.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a roofing system, which comprises: a roof deck, the construction board arranged on the roof deck, and a water-protective layer arranged on top or the construction board.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of construction board.
   Foam body (12)
   Facer (14)
   External coating (16)
   Substrate (18)
   Interfacial coating (20)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The inert filler includes calcium carbonate. The intumescent material includes expandable graphite. The first coating includes a polymeric binder in which inert filler is arranged. The facer is a first facer. The construction board includes a second facer arranged on one of the first and second planar surfaces of foam body opposite first facer. The polymeric binder for second coating is selected from natural rubber, waxes, and starches. The polymeric binder for second coating is selected from polyolefin, styrene-butadiene copolymers, acrylic polymers, methacrylic polymers, silicones, and its copolymers. The polymeric binder for second coating includes styrene-butadiene polymers bearing of hydrophobic moieties for repelling water, where the hydrophobic moieties for repelling water are fluorine-containing groups. The polymeric binder for second coating is selected from polyurethane coating compositions, polymeric resin coating compositions, siloxane coating compositions, polymer-modified asphalt coating compositions, and polymer-modified bitumen coating compositions. The inert filler is 3-30 wt.% with respect to the entire weight of the first coating. The construction board has zero flame spread and zero smoke development when tested in accordance with ASTM E84. The roofing system meets the requirements of UL 790 without the use of gypsum-based construction boards.

PN CN114506856-A
TI Preparation of ZSM-5 molecular sieve used in e.g.            ion exchange application, by ball-milling silicon            residue, silica fume and sodium carbonate, mixing,            pressing, crushing, leaching with sodium hydroxide to            obtain filter residue and leaching solution,            crystallizing solution, filtering, and calcining.
AU LI H
   JIANG A
   CHEN F
   LONG S
   YANG L
   LV J
   LONG X
   LUO W
AE GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
GA 202272354Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ZSM-5 molecular sieve involves                ball-milling silicon residue, silica fume and                sodium carbonate, mixing, pressing into balls, and                calcining; crushing the calcined balls, adding                sodium hydroxide solution for leaching to obtain                filter residue and a leaching solution, and washing                the residue; introducing carbon dioxide into the                residue to perform carbon separation, slowly                neutralizing the solution, mixing sodium silicate                and sodium aluminate in the leaching solution to                generate a precipitate of silicic acid and aluminum                hydroxide, mixing, precipitating, washing, taking                the precipitate as a silicon source and an aluminum                source for preparing ZSM-5 molecular sieve,                evaporating and crystallizing the solution to                obtain sodium carbonate; and adding a template                agent, distilled water and sodium hydroxide to the                mixed precipitate of silicic acid and aluminum                hydroxide, mixing, stirring, aging, crystallizing,                filtering, drying, and calcining.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ZSM-5                molecular sieve used in adsorption and separation,                catalysis and ion exchange applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares high-quality ZSM-5                molecular sieves with various silicon/aluminum                ratio, uses waste as raw material, and reduces the                production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of ZSM-5 molecular sieve involves                ball-milling silicon residue, silica fumes, and                sodium carbonate at a silicon/aluminum molar ratio                of 10-500 and silicon/sodium molar ratio of 0.2-3,                mixing, pressing into balls, calcining in a muffle                furnace at 400-1200degrees Celsius for 0.5-6 hours,                crushing, adding 20-300 g/L sodium hydroxide                solution at a balls/sodium hydroxide solution ratio                of 2:1-15:1 for leaching at 20-100degrees Celsius                for 0.5-5 hours to obtain filter residue and                leaching solution, which is a mixed solution of                sodium silicate and sodium aluminate, washing the                filter residue, introducing 0.2-10 L/minute carbon                dioxide into the residue for carbon separation at                an end point pH of 4.5-7, slowly neutralizing,                mixing sodium silicate and sodium aluminate in the                solution at a sodium/silicon molar ratio of                0.05-0.5 to generate a precipitate of silicic acid                and aluminum hydroxide, mixing, precipitating,                washing, taking the precipitate as a silicon source                and an aluminum source for preparing ZSM-5                molecular sieve, evaporating and crystallizing the                solution after carbon separation to obtain sodium                carbonate, adding a template agent, distilled water                and sodium hydroxide to the mixed precipitate of                silicic acid and aluminum hydroxide, mixing,                stirring, aging at 20-90degrees Celsius for 0.5-10                hours, crystallizing in a hydrothermal kettle at                100-220degrees Celsius for 6-48 hours, filtering,                drying, and calcining. The molar ratio of template                agent/silicon is 0.05-0.4. The molar ratio of                water/silicon is 15-200.

TI Air separating system for producing e.g. liquid            oxygen comprises rectifying tower, air compression            purifying system, air separating system, liquid            hydrogen-air heat exchanger system and circulating            chilled water system.
AU WU D
   MA D
   ZHANG Z
   LUO C
   LIU Y
   WANG X
   YANG J
   CHEN X
AE HUALU ENG & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 2022107815
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises rectifying tower, air                compression purifying system, air separating                system, liquid hydrogen-air heat exchanger system                and circulating chilled water system. The air                compression and purification system comprises air                filter, raw material air compressor, air                pre-cooling system and air purification system. The                air filter is used for filtering dust and                mechanical impurities in the raw material air. The                air pre-cooling system comprises air cooling tower,                water cooling tower, cooling water pump, freezing                water pump and cold water heat exchanger, for                cooling and washing the compressed raw material                air. The air purification system comprises purifier                and regeneration heater for absorbing and removing                water, carbon dioxide, and part of hydrocarbon. The                air separating system comprises main heat                exchanger, rectifying tower lower tower, rectifying                tower upper tower, and condensing evaporator.
   USE - The air separating system using liquid                hydrogen cold energy is used for producing liquid                oxygen, liquid nitrogen, liquid argon, partially                pressurized oxygen, gas nitrogen and partial air                liquid product.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses the low temperature cold                energy of the liquid hydrogen for the air                separation device, which can greatly reduce the                energy consumption of the device, and has great                energy-saving effect, simple process and low                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system comprises rectifying tower, air                compression purifying system, air separating                system, liquid hydrogen-air heat exchanger system                and circulating chilled water system. The air                compression and purification system comprises air                filter, raw material air compressor, air                pre-cooling system and air purification system. The                air filter is used for filtering dust and                mechanical impurities in the raw material air. The                raw material air compressor is used for performing                multistage compression to the filtered raw material                air. The air pre-cooling system comprises air                cooling tower, water cooling tower, cooling water                pump, freezing water pump and cold water heat                exchanger, for cooling and washing the compressed                raw material air. The air purification system                comprises purifier and regeneration heater for                absorbing and removing water, carbon dioxide, and                part of hydrocarbon. The liquid hydrogen-air heat                exchanger system comprises liquid hydrogen-air heat                exchanger used for further cooling the raw material                air. The liquid hydrogen gasification after heat                exchange is low temperature hydrogen. The                circulating chilled water system comprises                hydrogen-water heat exchanger and circulating water                pump. The hydrogen-water heat exchanger is used for                obtaining the low-temperature hydrogen cold energy.                The low-temperature hydrogen is heated to the pipe                temperature and sent to the gas pipeline. The air                separating system comprises main heat exchanger,                rectifying tower lower tower, rectifying tower                upper tower, and condensing evaporator. The                rectifying tower and condensing evaporator are used                for rectifying the raw material air to obtain pure                nitrogen. The main heat exchanger is used for                cooling and reheating of polluted nitrogen.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                separation method of air separation system using                liquid hydrogen cold energy.

TI Surface processing for realizing low adsorption plastic moving liquid suction head comprises e.g. mixing hydrophobically modified material and polypropylene base material in molten state, injection-molding to obtain moving liquid suction head, surface etching, surface modifying and drying.
AU ZHANG H
   DING X
AE SAINING SUZHOU LIFE SCI CO LTD (SAIN-Non-standard)
GA 202204603U
AB    NOVELTY - Surface processing for realizing low adsorption plastic moving liquid suction head comprises mixing the hydrophobically modified material and the polypropylene base material uniformly in a molten state and injection-molded to obtain a plastic moving liquid suction head, delivering the first gas to the reaction chamber of the plasma processing equipment by using the air pressure buffer control device, performing a plasma surface etching process on the plastic moving liquid suction head in a first atmosphere formed by a first gas, the first gas comprising an inert gas, inputting a second gas into the first atmosphere using the gas pressure buffer control device to form a second atmosphere including the first gas and the second gas, performing plasma surface modification treatment on the plastic moving liquid suction head in the second atmosphere, where the second atmosphere includes a mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide with a volume ratio of 1-1.5:4, and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for surface processing for realizing low adsorption plastic moving liquid suction head.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the hydrophobic modified material to evenly embed the hydrophobic component inside the base material of the plastic pipetting tip, etching and cleaning are used to expose more hydrophobic components, which ensures the low adsorption performance of the entire surface of the pipette tip with high aspect ratio and small diameter.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Surface processing for realizing low adsorption plastic moving liquid suction head comprises providing a hydrophobic modified material and a polypropylene substrate, where the hydrophobically modified material includes a hydrophobic component including polyalkylsiloxane, polytetrafluoroethylene and nano-silica particles, mixing the hydrophobically modified material and the polypropylene base material uniformly in a molten state and injection-molded to obtain a plasticmoving liquid suction head, and the maximum diameter of the moving liquid suction head is less than 7 mm, and the ratio of the length of the plastic moving liquid suction head to the maximum diameter is more than 10, delivering the first gas to the reaction chamber of the plasma processing equipment by using the air pressure buffer control device, performing a plasma surface etching process on the plastic moving liquid suction head in a first atmosphere formed by a first gas, the first gas comprising an inert gas, inputting a second gas into the first atmosphere using the gas pressure buffer control device to form a second atmosphere including the first gas and the second gas, performing plasma surface modification treatment on the plastic moving liquid suction head after plasma surface etching treatment in the second atmosphere, where the second atmosphere includes a mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide with a volume ratio of 1-1.5:4, and drying the plastic moving liquid suction head after the plasma surface modification treatment at 50-70degrees Celsius for 20-40 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hydrophobic component includes 60-80% polydimethylsiloxane with a degree of polymerization of 2000-4000, 10-20% polytetrafluoroethylene and 10-20% nano-silica particles in the size range 50-200 nm. The roughness of the molding surface of the mold for injection molding is mirror surface grade. The auxiliary agent is a mixture of polyethylene wax, antioxidant and colorant in a mass ratio of (0.8-1.2):(2.5-3.5):(4.5-5.5). The filler includes talc, wollastonite, graphite, kaolin and mica powder.Preferred Method: The technical conditions of the plasma surface etching treatment comprise: the temperature of the reaction chamber is 20-30degrees Celsius, the vacuum degree is 0.3-0.4 mbar, the power is 1.5-2 kW, and the processing time is 10-15 minutes, the process conditions of the plasma surface modification treatment include: the temperature of the reaction chamber is 20-30degrees Celsius, the degree of vacuum is 0.3-0.4 mbar, the power is 1.5-2 kW, and the treatment time is 25-30 minutes. The drying treatment is carried out by means of infrared radiation and hot air circulation synergistic treatment. The hydrophobic component is co-mixed with polypropylene material, nucleating agent, filler and auxiliary agent at 200-250degrees Celsius to form a hydrophobically modified masterbatch, which is used as the hydrophobically modified material. The injection molding comprises mixing the hydrophobically modified masterbatch and the polypropylene base material with a mass ratio of 1:20-1:100 at 200-230degrees Celsius, melt co-extruding 3-8 times to obtain a mixed base material, injection molding the mixed substrate at 220-280degrees Celsius in an injection mold with a mirror surface roughness level.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The air pressure buffer control device is configured to include a plurality of air pressure spray heads arranged in the plasma surface treatment device and a flow chamber connected to each of the air pressure spray heads. A regulating chamber placed in the overflow chamber, a control chamber connected with the regulating chamber, a first piston arranged in the regulating chamber. A return spring arranged in the rodless cavity of the regulating cavity, a second piston arranged in the control cavity, a connecting rod connecting the first and second pistons in sequence. An overflow channel arranged on the inner wall of the overflow chamber and a main plug body that is arranged on the connecting rod and blocks the overflow channel when the return spring is not stressed, under the pushing action of the first gas or the second gas, the main plug body moves in the opposite direction of blocking the flow passage, and compresses the return spring, so that the first gas or the second gas can enter the plasma surface treatment device through the flow passage and the gas pressure shower head. The air pressure buffer control device also includes a one-way valve disposed in the rodless cavity of the control cavity, a connecting channel disposed in the connecting rod and sequentially connecting the adjustment cavity and the control cavity, a radial channel arranged on the first piston and communicated with the connecting channel, an installation groove arranged in the radial channel, a resistance spring and a resistance block arranged in the installation groove. The interference block is adapted to the adjustment cavity, and the first piston is provided with a vertical air outlet hole that communicates with the rod cavity of the adjustment cavity and the radial channel. The rod cavity of the regulating cavity is provided with an outlet air hole; when the main plug body is impacted by the air pressure of the first gas or the second gas. The gas in the rodless chamber of the control chamber enters the radial channel through the connecting channel, and pushes the abutting block against the inner wall of the adjusting chamber.

GA 2021C7085A
AB    NOVELTY - Use of Eucommia bark saponin for promoting proliferation and chondrogenic differentiation of fat mesenchymal stem cells.
   USE - Eucommia bark saponin is useful for promoting proliferation and chondrogenic differentiation of fat mesenchymal stem cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can promote the proliferation and chondrogenic differentiation of fat mesenchymal stem cells while reducing its cost and has excellent safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) in vitro culture system for promoting proliferation and chondrogenic differentiation of fat mesenchymal stem cells, comprising Eucommia bark saponin; and
   (2) promoting proliferation and chondrogenic differentiation of fat mesenchymal stem cells, comprising (1) (1-1) obtaining the discarded lower patellar fat pad after the artificial knee joint replacement surgery under aseptic conditions with the informed consent of the surgical patient, moving it to sterile operating table, and using sterile ophthalmic scissors to cut off the red blood vessels and white connective tissue that are visible to the naked eye, cutting the remaining adipose tissue into 1-2 mm3 pieces, and washing twice with Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline (DPBS) for 5 minutes each time, (1-2) digesting with 0.25% trypsin at 37degrees Celsius for 1 hour, removing 0.25% trypsin, adding 0.2% type I collagenase solution prepared with serum-free high-sugar DMEM medium, and digesting overnight, and (1-3) transferring to 50 ml centrifuge tube on the second day, carefully absorbing the lower layer of liquid, filtering it through 40 microm cell strainer, transferring to 15 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute, dissolving the precipitate with 2 ml basal medium and transferring it to cell culture flask middle, placing it in 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator, adhering the cells will to the wall and grow after 1-2 days, and obtaining primary fat mesenchymal stem cells, (2) culturing the obtained primary fat mesenchymal stem cells for 24 hours, changing half of the medium, changing the medium in full after 48 hours, changing the medium every 2 days, growing the primary fat mesenchymal stem cells and converge to more than 90% after culturing for 5-7 days, inoculating into cell culture flask after digesting with 0.25% trypsin according to 1:3, and continuously culturing in vitro to obtain the second-generation fat mesenchymal stem cells, (3) taking the second generation of overgrown fat mesenchymal stem cells, digesting with 0.25% trypsin, waiting for the cells to grow, adding 10 ng/ml Eucommia bark saponin to the basal medium when the degree of fusion reaches 80%, and placing it in 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide incubator to continue culturing for 24 hours, and (4) taking the second generation of overgrown fat mesenchymal stem cells and digesting with 0.25% trypsin, adding 10 ng/ml Eucommia bark saponin, 10 ng/ml transforming growth factor beta-3 (TGFbeta-3), 100 nmol/l dexamethasone, 1% gibco insulin-transferrin-selenium (ITS-G), 50 microg/ml vitamin C and 40 microg/ml proline to the basic medium, changing the medium every 2 days, and continuously culturing the for 14 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In vitro culture system is added with Eucommia bark to promote the proliferation and chondrogenic differentiation of fat mesenchymal stem cells. The 10 ng/ml Eucommia bark saponin is added to the in vitro culture system, and the fat mesenchymal stem cells were cultured.Preferred Components: The in vitro culture system comprises high-sugar Dulbeccos modified eagle medium (DMEM) medium, 10% fetal bovine serum and 1% double antibody solution. The culture condition is 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and the culture temperature is 37degrees Celsius. The added amount of Eucommia bark saponin is 10 ng/ml. The basic medium comprises high-sugar DMEM medium, 10% fetal bovine serum and 1% double antibody solution.

TI Method for recycling biomass of hydrogen production catalytic system, involves putting biomass or domestic waste in sulfuric acid, adding sodium metavanadate and dimethyl sulfoxide, hydrolyzing, and vacuum-distilling obtained hydrolysate.
AU LI Y
   HU R
   GUO Y
   SUN Y
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021A0214T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling biomass of high-efficient hydrogen production catalytic system involves putting biomass or domestic waste in dilute sulfuric acid, adding sodium metavanadate and dimethyl sulfoxide, performing oxidative hydrolysis at 140-170 degrees C, quantitatively converting cellulose and hemicellulose into formic acid to obtain first biomass hydrolysate, adding dilute sulfuric acid, sodium metavanadate and dimethyl sulfoxide, separating first biomass hydrolysate by vacuum distillation method to obtain second fraction and third mother liquor, adding 50-80 ppm hydrogen-producing catalyst to second fraction, subjecting mixed gas produced by reaction system to potassium hydroxide solution absorption treatment to obtain pure hydrogen, decomposing formic acid in second fraction, removing water and other volatile substances to obtain a yellow liquid, taking other batch of second fraction, heating to 70-100 degrees C, and adding recovered catalyst to collect hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for recycling biomass of high-efficient hydrogen production catalytic system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of improving stability of the catalyst, realizing recycling of the catalyst, combining biomass hydrolysis hydrogen production catalytic system for recycling, finishing circulation of two-step catalytic system using biomass to produce hydrogen, reducing catalytic cost of biomass hydrogen production, and laying solid experimental basis for industrial application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for recycling biomass of high-efficient hydrogen production catalytic system involves putting biomass or domestic waste in dilute sulfuric acid with concentration of 0.5-1.5 wt.%, adding sodium metavanadate and dimethyl sulfoxide, performing oxidative hydrolysis at 140-170 degrees C under pressure of 2-7 MPa, quantitatively converting cellulose and hemicellulose into formic acid to obtain first biomass hydrolysate, adding 15-45 mL dilute sulfuric acid, 0.05-0.15 g sodium metavanadate and 0.5-2 mL dimethyl sulfoxide for every 1 g of biomass or domestic waste, separating first biomass hydrolysate at 45-65 degrees C vacuum distillation method to obtain second fraction and third mother liquor, where second fraction is distillate of biomass hydrolysate and its components include aqueous formic acid and small amount of organic matter, the second fraction is used for subsequent hydrogen production conversion, and the third mother liquor is a catalytic system for concentrated sulfuric acid and sodium metavanadate, and continues to be used in the biomass oxidation reaction step, adding 50-80 ppm hydrogen-producing catalyst to second fraction, heating to 70-100 degrees C to generate large number of bubbles, subjecting mixed gas produced by reaction system to potassium hydroxide solution absorption treatment to obtain pure hydrogen, decomposing formic acid in second fraction, distilling the reaction liquid at 45-65 degrees C, removing water and other volatile substances to obtain a yellow liquid, which is used as a recovered catalyst, taking other batch of second fraction, heating to 70-100 degrees C, adding recovered catalyst to collect hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and repeating above steps to realize the cycle of the system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass is wheat straw, maize straw, rice straw or reed rod. The domestic waste is bagasse, box-board paper or waste newspaper.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen-producing catalyst is iridium complex chosen from 9 complexes e.g. complexes of formulae (I)-(V).

PN CN113198510-A
TI Preparing graphite-phase carbon nitride micron tube/nickel cobalt layered double metal oxide grade hollow heterojunction useful on photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, comprises performing hydrothermal reaction and ultrasonic dispersion.
AU YU Y
   LIU H
   CHEN J
   WANG X
   CHEN F
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2021919605
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphite-phase carbon nitride micron tube/nickel cobalt layered double metal oxide graded hollow heterojunction comprises e.g. dissolving nitrogen source in the de-ionized water, heating under oil bath and stirring until the solution is clear to obtain solution A, pouring the solution A into the reaction kettle, performing hydrothermal reaction, then cooling to room temperature along with the furnace, centrifuging, washing, drying to obtain white powder B, taking light yellow powder C into round bottom flask, dissolving in de-ionized water for ultrasonic dispersion, then magnetically stirring to form solution D, adding hexahydrate nickel chloride, hexahydrate cobalt chloride into solution D, adding urotropine, continuously stirring to obtain solution E, placing the solution E on oil bath pot low temperature oil bath and magnetic stirring, centrifuging, washing, drying obtain green powder F, and adding obtained green powder F in the muffle furnace to uniform heating speed.
   USE - The graphite-phase carbon nitride micron tube/nickel cobalt layered double metal oxide graded hollow heterojunction is useful on the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graphite-phase carbon nitride micron tube/nickel cobalt layered double metal oxide graded hollow heterojunction: can promote the separation of photogenerated electron-hole pairs, has simple and efficient preparation process and excellent photocatalytic CO2 reduction performance; and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing graphite-phase carbon nitride micron tube/nickel cobalt layered double metal oxide graded hollow heterojunction comprises (i) dissolving nitrogen source in the de-ionized water, heating under oil bath and stirring until the solution is clear to obtain solution A; (ii) pouring the solution A obtained in the step (i) into the reaction kettle, performing hydrothermal reaction at 180 degrees C for 24 hours, then cooling to room temperature along with the furnace, centrifuging, washing, drying to obtain white powder B; (iii) taking white powder B obtained in step (ii) to calcine under the atmosphere of nitrogen to obtain light yellow powder C; (iv) taking light yellow powder C obtained in step (iii) into the round bottom flask, dissolving in de-ionized water for ultrasonic dispersion, then magnetic stirring to form solution D; (v) adding hexahydrate nickel chloride, hexahydrate cobalt chloride into solution D obtained in step (iv), stirring for 30 minutes at room temperature, then adding urotropine, continuously stirring for 60 minutes to obtain solution E; (vi) placing the solution E obtained in the step (v) on the oil bath pot low temperature oil bath and magnetic stirring, centrifuging, washing, drying to obtain green powder F; and (vii) adding obtained green powder F in the muffle furnace to uniform heating speed. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for graphite-phase carbon nitride micron tube/nickel cobalt layered double metal oxide graded hollow heterojunction, is constructed by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the nitrogen source is 1-4 g melamine. In the step (v, hexahydrated nickel chloride, cobalt chloride hexahydrate and urotropine at the molar ratio of 1:1:8.75. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the heating temperature is 90 degrees C, and the added amount of deionized water is 60-240 ml. In the step (i), the calcining parameters are: the heating speed is 2 degrees C/min, the calcining temperature is 520 degrees C and the heat preservation time is 4 hours. In the step (iv), the volume of the de-ionized water in is 60 ml, the stirring speed is 300 rpm, and the stirring time is 30 minutes. In the step (vi), the oil bath parameter is specifically as: the oil bath temperature is 95 degrees C and the heating time is 5 hours. The stirring speed is 300 rpm, the washing order is water and ethanol and each washing is three times and then vacuum drying. In the step (vii), the calcining parameters are: the heating speed is 2 degrees C/min, the calcining temperature is 350 degrees C and heat preservation time is 2 hours.

GA 2021717891
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic framework comprises organic                linking ligand of (I). The metal ion is bonded to                organic linking ligand, metal-organic framework                (MOF) is obtained by performing protection step,                cross-coupling step, cleavage step, and                deprotection step. The process is configured to                enable organic linking ligand to bond with group of                metal ions includes Mg, Co, Ni, Zn, Fe, and Cu to                form MOF, when MOF is capable of bonding with zinc                ion to form Zn-MOF. The Zn-MOF is characterized by                BET surface area of 2,790 m^2g^-^1, pore volume of                0.23 cm^3g^-^1 which is capable of adsorbing carbon                dioxide at 39.4 cm^3g^-^1.
   USE - Metal-organic framework.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-organic framework enhances CO_2                storage and catalytic conversion of CO_2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal-organic framework comprises organic                linking ligand of formula (I). The metal ion is                bonded to organic linking ligand, metal-organic                framework (MOF) is obtained by performing                protection step, cross-coupling step, cleavage                step, and deprotection step. The process is                configured to enable organic linking ligand to bond                with group of metal ions includes Mg, Co, Ni, Zn,                Fe, and Cu to form MOF, when MOF is capable of                bonding with zinc ion to form Zn-MOF. The Zn-MOF is                characterized by BET surface area of 2,790                m^2g^-^1, pore volume of 0.23 cm^3g^-^1 which is                capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide at 39.4                cm^3g^-^1. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: a method for synthesizing metal-organic                framework, which involves protecting phenolic                hydroxyl group, using benzylic ether (OBn) through                reaction of hydroxy iodobenzoate and benzyl bromide                (BnBr) for producing benzyloxy iodobenzoate                compound, producing ethyne bis(benzyloxybenzoate)                compound by Sonogashira cross-coupling of benzyloxy                iodobenzoate compound obtained, hydroxylating                benzylic ether of bis(benzyloxybenzoate) compound                obtained, reacting with organosilicon compound for                producing bis(hydroxybenzoate) compound,                saponificating bis(hydroxybenzoate) in strong base                solution for producing bis(hydroxybenzoic acid)                compound, heating at elevated temperature liquid                medium containing metal salt, bis(hydroxybenzoic                acid) compound, and mixture of solvents in sealed                vial or air-free tube under inert atmosphere                producing synthesized metal-organic framework,                cooling liquid medium to room temperature,                decanting the mother solution and washing with                anhydrous alcohol or anhydrous solvents for                producing solvent-exchanged Metal-Organic Framework                (MOF) and drying solvent-exchange Metal-Organic                Framework (MOF) by vacuum under room temperature                followed by heating at elevated temperature for                producing the solvent-free Metal-Organic Framework                (MOF). The bis(hydroxybenzoic acid) compound is                4,4-(ethyne-1,2-diyl)bis(2-hydroxybenzoic acid);                and#a compound, which comprises bis(hydroxybenzoic                acid) compound operable to bond organic compound                with metal in carbon-carbon bond, metal node and                multiple of three-dimensional mesopores, each                having diameter ranging from 22.6-24.8                angstroms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organosilicon                  compound is trimethylsilyl iodide (TMSI). The                  bis(hydroxybenzoic acid) compound is                  bis(2-hydroxybenzoic acid). Preferred Conditions:                  The metal-organic framework further comprises                  multiple of three-dimensional mesopores has                  diameter ranging from 2-50 nanometer. The Zn-MOF                  has molecular formula of Zn_2C_1_6H_6O_6                  (Zn_2C_1_6H_6O_6 MOF). Preferred Process: The                  method involves synthesizing linking ligand to                  bond with zinc, adding solution of                  Zn(NO_3)_2.6H_2O into N,N-dimethylformmaide (DMF)                  and preloading with ethyl 3,4-dihydroxybenzoic                  (EDHB) linker to obtain first solution. The                  mixture of DMF and water are added with 3:1                  volume ratio into first solution to obtain second                  solution, sonicated and heated second solution at                  120?oC to obtain light beige solid, then decanted                  and washed light beige solid with DMF to obtain                  powder. The powder is soaked in anhydrous                  methanol (MeOH) to obtain solvent and desolvated                  solvent under vacuum at ambient temperature and                  then heated at 100?oC. The Zn_2C_1_6H_6O_6 MOF                  has density of 0.7785 g/cm^3. The Zn_2C_1_6H_6O_6                  MOF is characterized by void volume of 70.2% of                  the total volume of Zn_2C_1_6H_6O_6 MOF. The                  metal node comprises metal-oxo clusters which is                  (M_3((O)_3(CO_2)_3)), and M_3 comprises                  magnesium, cobalt, nickel, zinc, copper, and                  iron.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Detecting classical swine fever virus immunoperoxidase monolayer assay neutralizing antibody comprises e.g. digesting and centrifuging PK15 cells to prepare cell suspension, spreading per well on 96-well plate, and setting up control hole.
AU LI P
   WANG L
   LIU X
   WANG X
   LIU C
   WEI X
   SUN G
   YUE F
   LI H
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV XINXIANG (UNXX-C)
GA 202138280P
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting classical swine fever virus (CSFV) immunoperoxidase monolayer assay (IPMA) neutralizing antibody comprises (1) digesting and centrifuging PK15 cells to prepare cell suspension containing 1x 105 cells/ml, spreading 100 mu l per well on 96-well plate, placing it in 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 12 hours, incubating equal volume of CSFV virus and clinical serum at 37 degrees C for 1 hour after the cells are fully attached, inoculating on the PK15 cells in 96-well plate, and at the same time, setting up control hole for normal toxin cells, (2) culturing for 48 hours, taking out the 96-well plate when the cells are overgrown, and fixing it with pre-cooled absolute ethanol, (3) washing the 96-well plate with phosphate buffered saline for 2-3 times, adding 100 mu l of CSFV positive serum diluted 1:400 with 0.01 M phosphate buffered saline, and placing it at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, and (4) adding 100 mu l of goat anti-pig IgG-horse radish peroxidase secondary antibody diluted 1000 times.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting CSFV IPMA neutralizing antibody.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses the interaction between the whole virus and the antibody to infect the target cell; truly reflects the interaction between the virus and the neutralizing antibody; and has strong specificity, high sensitivity and simple operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting classical swine fever virus (CSFV) immunoperoxidase monolayer assay (IPMA) neutralizing antibody comprises (1) digesting and centrifuging the PK15 cells to prepare cell suspension containing 1x 105 cells/ml, spreading 100 mu l per well on 96-well plate, placing it in 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 12 hours, incubating equal volume of CSFV virus and clinical serum at 37 degrees C for 1 hour after the cells are fully attached, inoculating on the PK15 cells in 96-well plate, and at the same time, setting up control hole for normal toxin cells, (2) culturing for 48 hours, taking out the 96-well plate when the cells are overgrown, and fixing it with pre-cooled absolute ethanol, (3) washing the 96-well plate with phosphate buffered saline for 2-3 times, adding 100 mu l of CSFV positive serum diluted 1:400 with 0.01 M phosphate buffered saline, and placing it at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, (4) adding 100 mu l of goat anti-pig IgG-horse radish peroxidase secondary antibody diluted 1000 times with 0.01 M phosphate buffered saline (pH 7.4), and acting at 37 degrees C for 1 hours, and (5) 3,3-diaminobenzidine developing color for 5 minutes, terminating the reaction, and observing the color result under optical microscope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of the CSFV virus liquid comprises grinding the clinically collected disease material tissue, adding 1 ml phosphate buffered saline to dissolve 1 mg tissue sample, freezing and thawing repeatedly 3 times, taking the supernatant, centrifuging at 12000 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes and filtering with 0.22 mu m filter to obtain CSFV virus solution. The preparation method of the clinical serum is centrifuging the collected blood to collect serum for later clinical serum testing.
