PN CN212777623-U; CN113899011-A
TI Fresh air system for air conditioner, has indoor            air quality sensor and outdoor air quality sensor that            are electrically connected with purifying device, where            purifying device is used for purifying air entering            room.
AU HUANG T
   LAI M
   HU R
   YAN L
AE NINGBO AUX ELECTRIC CO LTD (AUXS-C)
   AUX AIR CONDITIONING CO LTD (AUXS-C)
   AUX AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (AUXS-C)
GA 202132186D
AB    (CN212777623-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a fresh air system                and air conditioner; the fresh air system comprises                an indoor air quality sensor, an outdoor air                quality sensor and a purifying device; the indoor                air quality sensor and the outdoor air quality                sensor are respectively electrically connected with                the purifying device. detecting indoor and outdoor                air quality CA and CR through the indoor air                quality sensor and the outdoor air quality sensor,                controlling whether the purifying device is started                according to the value of CA and CR; purifying the                waste gas and pollutant in the fresh air after                starting the purifying device, keeping the air                entering the chamber fresh; so as to accurately                control the indoor air quality; when CA and CR do                not reach the condition of starting the purifying                device, the purifying device is not started; the                service life of the purifying device is                prolonged.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an air conditioner;
   (2) a control method of fresh air                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a fresh                air system.
   Indoor air quality sensor (110)
   Outdoor air quality sensor (120)
   Purifying device (130)
   Air channel (140)
   Air inlet (141)
   Air outlet (142)
EA    NOVELTY - The fresh air system comprises an indoor air                quality sensor (110), an outdoor air quality                sensors (120) and a purification device (130). The                indoor air-quality sensor and the outdoor air-                quality sensor are respectively connected to the                purification equipment, and the indoor air quality                sensor is located in the room for detecting indoor                air quality. The outdoor air quality sensor is                positioned outside the room, and is used to detect                outdoor air quality. The purification devices are                used to purify the air entering the room. The                outdoor air quality sensor is set at the outdoor                air inlet (141).
   USE - Fresh air system for use in an air conditioner                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The purifying device purifies the air entering                the chamber when the concentration of indoor carbon                dioxide is too high, so as to reduce concentration                of carbon dioxide in the indoor air, thus reducing                concentration of the pollutant in the chamber, and                hence reducing the pollution of the chamber and                prolonging service life of the air conditioner. The                system allows the air to enter the chamber to be                kept fresh, thus accurately controlling indoor air                quality, when the indoor and the outdoor air                quality do not reach the condition of starting the                purifier. The purifier is used for purifying the                waste gas and pollutants in the fresh air after                starting the air purifier, thus keeping the air                inside the chamber fresh, and accurately                controlling the air quality of the room.

PT P
PN CN112300397-A; CN112300397-B
TI Manufacture of high-performance polycarbosilane used in manufacture of silicon carbide fibers, involves forming crude product, dissolving crude product in solvent of polydimethylsilane, filtering, reducing pressure, and distilling.
AU WU B
   HOU Z
   WU D
AE JIANGXI XINDAHANGKE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGXI XINDAHANGKE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOL (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202116373Y
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of high-performance polycarbosilane involves (i) taking a certain mass of nano aluminum powder, nano magnesium oxide, nano chromium oxide and titanium carbide, washing in an organic solvent, and placing into muffle furnace, heating, taking out, placing in organic solvent for further washing and drying to obtain mixture (M1), (ii) immersing the mixture (M1) in KH-550 (RTM: gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane) solution for 15-30 minutes, with constant stirring, taking out, drying in muffle furnace, and taking out to obtain mixture (M2), (iii) adding solvent to the polydimethylsilane, and adding mixture (M2), heating the water bath at 40-60 degrees C, stirring to obtain mixture (M3), (iv) placing the mixture (M3) into the reaction kettle, and passing carbon dioxide, heating according to the temperature rise procedure, and cooling to obtain the crude product, and (v) dissolving the crude product in the solvent of polydimethylsilane, filtering, reducing pressure, and distilling.
   USE - Manufacture of high-performance polycarbosilane used in manufacture of silicon carbide fibers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of high-performance polycarbosilane with ceramic yield of more than 90%. The silicon carbide fiber has strength of more than 2.9 GPa and strength retention rate of more than 70% after 400 hours of working in air atmosphere of 1200 degrees C. The additives are first cleaned and dried to remove surface impurities, and then treated with KH-550 (RTM: gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane) coupling agent to enhance its binding performance with polydimethylsilane, and improve dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of high-performance polycarbosilane involves (i) taking a certain mass of nano aluminum powder, nano magnesium oxide, nano chromium oxide and titanium carbide, washing in an organic solvent and placing into a muffle furnace, and heating in an air environment at 260-320 degrees C for 2-4 hours, taking out, placing in an organic solvent for further washing and drying to obtain mixture (M1), (ii) immersing the obtained mixture (M1) in KH-550 (RTM: gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane) solution for surface modification, for 15-30 minutes, with constant stirring during the period, taking out, drying in a muffle furnace at 120-130 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and taking out to obtain mixture (M2), (iii) adding 5-10 %mass solvent to the polydimethylsilane, and adding mixture (M2), heating the water bath at 40-60 degrees C, stirring until the solvent is completely volatilized to obtain mixture (M3), (iv) placing the mixture (M3) into the reaction kettle, and passing carbon dioxide to replace the gas in the kettle 3-5 times, setting the pressure to 1-5 MPa, heating to 420-460 degrees C according to the temperature rise procedure, for 2-4 hours, and cooling to room temperature with the furnace to obtain the crude product, and (v) dissolving the crude product in the solvent of polydimethylsilane, filtering, reducing pressure, and distilling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the mass ratio of nano aluminum powder, nano magnesium oxide, nano chromium oxide and titanium carbide is (5-15):(1-2):(2-5):1, and the organic solvent is ethanol or xylene. In step (iii), the mass ratio of mixture (M2) to polydimethylsilane is (3-10):100, and the solvent for polydimethylsilane is isopropanol, acetone, n-hexane, or n-heptane. Preferred Process: In step (ii), the KH-550 (RTM: gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane) solution is obtained by placing KH-550 (RTM: gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane) in a mixed solvent of water and ethanol, and dripping acetic acid to adjust the pH to 4-6, in which the mass ratio of KH-550 (RTM: gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane) to water and ethanol is (15-20):(5-10):(70-80). Preferred Conditions : The temperature increase procedure is carried out at a rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute to 80-100 degrees C, and holding for 0.5-1 hour, (ii) 2-4 degrees C/minute to 200-220 degrees C and holding for 1-2 hours, (iii) 2-3 degrees C/minute increase to 420-460 degrees C and maintaining for 2-4 hours, and cooling to room temperature along with the furnace.

PT P
PN CN112158865-A
TI Recycling and recovering the lithium element in lithium precipitation mother liquor in the lithium carbonate production process comprises e.g. using sodium carbonate solution and lithium chloride solution for stirring and mixing reaction.
AU YANG F
   LIU B
   LIU Y
   LI N
   ZHAO X
   ZHANG F
AE QINGHAI HENGXINRONG LITHIUM IND TECHNOLO (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2021040225
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling and recovering the lithium element in lithium precipitation mother liquor in the lithium carbonate production process comprises e.g. (1) using sodium carbonate solution and lithium chloride solution for stirring and mixing reaction in a reaction crystallizer, and controlling the reaction temperature at 80-100 degrees C, filtering the reacted slurry under normal pressure, washing 1-3 times with process water, drying at 150-300 degrees C to obtain lithium carbonate, and the filtrate is the mother liquor of lithium precipitation, (2) adding hydrochloric acid to the lithium precipitation mother liquor to control the pH value of the mother liquor in the range of 4-7, where the molar amount of chloride ion added to hydrochloric acid is 1.5-3 times the molar amount of carbonate ion in the solution, mixing the lithium-precipitating mother liquor and hydrochloric acid, stirring in a buffer tank heated while stirring, and discharging the carbon dioxide generated in the solution.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling and recovering the lithium element in lithium precipitation mother liquor in the lithium carbonate production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the subsequent renewed lithium chloride in the blending solution without affecting the quality of the product lithium carbonate in the lithium carbide precipitation step; and solves the product quality problems that have plagued the industry for many years.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling and recovering the lithium element in lithium precipitation mother liquor in the lithium carbonate production process comprises (1) using sodium carbonate solution and lithium chloride solution for stirring and mixing reaction in a reaction crystallizer, and controlling the reaction temperature at 80-100 degrees C, filtering the reacted slurry under normal pressure, washing 1-3 times with process water, drying at 150-300 degrees C to obtain lithium carbonate, and the filtrate is the mother liquor of lithium precipitation, (2) adding hydrochloric acid to the lithium precipitation mother liquor to control the pH value of the mother liquor in the range of 4-7, where the molar amount of chloride ion added to hydrochloric acid is 1.5-3 times the molar amount of carbonate ion in the solution, mixing the lithium-precipitating mother liquor and hydrochloric acid, stirring in a buffer tank and heated while stirring, discharging the carbon dioxide generated in the solution and removing the carbonate, (3) mixing the lithium precipitation mother liquor from which carbonate has been removed and old Bittern at a weight ratio of 3:6.5-8, stirring, reacting for 25-40 minutes in the pH range of 4-7, removing the solid phase crystals by filtration or precipitation, and leaving the blending liquid, (4) concentrating the blending solution by a nanofiltration membrane to remove impurity ions, and preparing blending solution into the lithium chloride solution required for the lithium carbide precipitation reaction, and continuing the lithium carbide precipitation to obtain lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the stirring speed in the buffer tank stirring process is 80-120 revolutions/minute, preferably 100 revolutions/minute, the stirring is 30-45 minutes, preferably 38 minutes, and the heating temperature during the stirring process is 70-90 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C. In the step (3), the carbonate-removed lithium precipitation mother liquor and the salt field brine are mixed in a weight ratio of 3:7, and the reaction is stirred, and the pH value is in the range of 4-7 for 30 minutes.

PN CN112042791-A
TI Kumquat lemon passion fruit freeze-dried tea slice useful for e.g. whitening skin, contains passion fruit pulp, white granulated sugar, honey, lemon juice, kumquat juice, potato starch, water, kumquat, lemon, and green banana peel.
AU CHEN J
   YAO G
   ZHOU H
   GUO X
AE ZHONGBAO FUJIAN FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4945N
AB    NOVELTY - Kumquat lemon passion fruit freeze-dried tea slice comprises 8-12 pts. wt. passion fruit pulp, 6-8 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, 4-6 pts. wt. honey, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. lemon juice, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. kumquat juice, 2-3 pts. wt. potato starch, 60-63 pts. wt. water, 4-8 pts. wt. kumquat, 4-8 pts. wt. lemon, 3-5 pts. wt. green banana peel, 3-5 pts. wt. cactus pulp, and 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. Plectranthus leaf.
   USE - The kumquat lemon passion fruit freeze-dried tea slice is useful for clearing away heat, moisturizing throat, removing dryness and whitening skin.
   ADVANTAGE - The freeze-dried tea slice has high vitamin C amount, sweet and sour taste, and excellent color and shape, retains more than 95% nutrition of fruit, and rapidly restores appearance of fresh fruit after absorbing water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing kumquat lemon passion fruit freeze-dried tea slice, which involves (a) preparing kumquat and lemon by (i) screening raw material, (ii) cleaning qualified raw material including kumquat and lemon, and (iii) slicing cleaned kumquat and lemon to obtain kumquat slices and lemon slices, (b) preparing green banana peel by (i) selecting green banana and rinsing, (ii) putting green banana peel in 90-100 degrees hot water and cooking for 5 minutes, and (iii) pulverizing boiled green banana peel to obtain a banana peel mixture, (c) preparing cactus fruit pulp by (i) peeling cactus fruit by cutting and taking the pulp of the cactus fruit out, and (ii) separating cactus fruit pulp from the seeds, and pulverizing cactus fruit pulp after the seeds are taken to obtain the cactus fruit pulp mixture, (d) preparing Plectranthus leaf by (i) placing Plectranthus plant in a space with a high concentration of carbon dioxide, where high concentration of carbon dioxide quickly closes stomata on Plectranthus leaf, and rapidly cutting Plectranthus leaf in this space, and (ii) cleaning the cut Plectranthus leaf with closed pores, and (e) preparing freeze-dried tea slice by (i) cooking the passion fruit pulp for 5 minutes under temperature control of 85 degrees C, adding water, white granulated sugar, honey, lemon juice, kumquat juice, potato starch, banana peel and cactus fruit pulp mixture, stirring, and mixed thoroughly to form a mixture, (ii) shaping mixture based on brewing ratio of each cup, and adding 2 kumquat slices and 1 lemon slice, (iii) passing freeze-drying and fresh-keeping technology of Plectranthus leaves, impregnating kumquat slices and lemon slices with mixture, rapid freezing at -45 degrees C, and vacuum freeze-drying, and (iv) combining and packing dehydrated and dried leaves, kumquat slices and lemon slices impregnated with the mixture, where each individual package contains 2 kumquat slices, 1 lemon slice, and 2 Plectranthus leaves.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The freeze-dried tea slice comprises 10 pts. wt., 8 pts. wt. or 12 pts. wt. passion fruit pulp, 7 pts. wt., 6 pts. wt. or 8 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, 5 pts. wt., 4 pts. wt. or 6 pts. wt. honey, 1 pt. wt., 0.5 pt. wt. or 1.5 pts. wt. lemon juice, 1 pt. wt., 0.5 pt. wt. or 1.5 pts. wt. kumquat juice, 2.5 pts. wt., 2 pts. wt. or 3 pts. wt. potato starch, 61.5 pts. wt., 60 pts. wt. or 63 pts. wt. water, 6 pts. wt., 4 pts. wt. or 8 pts. wt. kumquat, 6 pts. wt., 4 pts. wt. or 8 pts. wt. lemon, 4 pts. wt., 3 pts. wt. or 5 pts. wt. green banana peel, 4 pts. wt., 3 pts. wt. or 5 pts. wt. cactus pulp, and 1 pt. wt., 0.5 pt. wt. or 1.5 pts. wt. Plectranthus leaf. The white granulated sugar is imported from South Korea. The honey is jujube flower honey.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PN CN111837956-A
TI Rapidly producing konjac tissue culture in            mini-potato bottles comprises e.g. transferring            embryogenic callus of konjac to cluster bud induction            and differentiation medium, and transferring to rooting            and strong seedling medium.
AU CAI Y
   DUAN L
   QIN J
   CHEN G
   ZHANG B
   LIU C
   YANG L
   WANG Z
AE ANKANG AGRIC SCI RES INST (ANKA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B16851
AB    NOVELTY - Rapidly producing konjac tissue culture in                mini-potato bottles comprises (i) transferring                embryogenic callus of konjac to cluster bud                induction and differentiation medium at 22-25                degrees C, humidity of 50-60%, light intensity of                3500-5000 lx and illumination time of 8/16 hours,                and cultivating for 35-45 days to obtain                differentiated cluster seedlings, (ii) transferring                the clustered seedlings differentiated in the step                (i) to the rooting and strong seedling medium at                26-28 degrees C, humidity of 60-80%, light                intensity of 5000-6000 lx and light time of 8/16                hours, and culturing for 12-18 days to obtain                rooted and strong seedlings, (iii) transferring                rooting and strong seedlings from step (ii) to                culture bottle containing a mini-potato medium at                26-30 degrees C, humidity of 60-80% and ambient                carbon dioxide concentration of 5-8%, using blue                light for 2-4 hours, red light for 1-2 hours and                red and blue mixed light for 6-7 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for rapidly producing                konjac tissue culture in mini-potato bottles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: grows tissue culture seedlings new                roots in 4-6 days and small seed potatoes in 15-20                days; produces 8-14 g seed potatoes in 40-55 days                and an average of 6.4 potatoes per bottle; omits                many links in the process of transplanting konjac                seedlings outside the bottle; saves production                costs; and improves the production efficiency of                the original seeds of konjac tissue culture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rapidly producing konjac tissue culture in                mini-potato bottles comprises (i) transferring                embryogenic callus of konjac to cluster bud                induction and differentiation medium at 22-25                degrees C, humidity of 50-60%, light intensity of                3500-5000 lx and illumination time of 8/16 hours,                and cultivating for 35-45 days to obtain                differentiated cluster seedlings, (ii) transferring                the clustered seedlings differentiated in the step                (i) to the rooting and strong seedling medium at                26-28 degrees C, humidity of 60-80%, light                intensity of 5000-6000 lx and light time of 8/16                hours, and culturing for 12-18 days to obtain                rooted and strong seedlings, (iii) transferring                rooting and strong seedlings from step (ii) to                culture bottle containing a mini-potato medium at                26-30 degrees C, humidity of 60-80% and ambient                carbon dioxide concentration of 5-8%, using blue                light for 2-4 hours, red light for 1-2 hours and                red and blue mixed light for 6-7 hours, where light                intensity is 6000-7500 lx, light time of 12/12                hours, culturing for 15-20 days, where the blue                light has a wavelength of 440-480 nm, the red light                has a wavelength of 640-680 nm, and the                chromatographic ratio of the red and blue mixed                light is 5-10:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The composition of the                  medium for inducing differentiation of clumping                  buds in step (i) is: Murashige and Skoog (MS),                  2-4 mg/l kinetin (KT), 0.5-1 mg/l 6-benzyladenine                  (BA), 0.2-0.5 mg/l 2, 4-dichlorophenoxyacetic                  acid (2,4-D) and 0.1-0.2 mg/l naphthaleneacetic                  acid (NAA). The callus in step (i) is soaked for                  15-30 seconds with a mixture of 0.1-0.3% ascorbic                  acid and 5-10% citric acid in a mass ratio of 2:1                  before transferred. The seedling rooting and                  strong seedling medium composition in the step                  (ii) is: 1/2 MS, 1.5-3 mg/l NAA, 0.5-1 mg/l 2,4-D                  and 20-40 mg/l uniconazole. The composition of                  the micro-tuber culture medium is: 3-6 mg/l 6-BA,                  526-630 mg/l potassium nitrate, 204-287 mg/l                  monopotassium phosphate, 132-248 mg/l dipotassium                  phosphate, 318-394 mg/l ammonium nitrate, 219-421                  mg/l salicylic acid, 4-8% sucrose, 5.42-10.77                  mg/l boric acid, 85-125 mg/l uniconazole,                  0.2-0.4% activated carbon, and MS, mixing, and                  adding distilled water to make the volume to 1                  l.
DC C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

TI Solar energy wind energy and gas complementary combined hydrogen production methane circulation thermal power generation device comprises a groove type solar thermal power generation system, a wind power system, and a photovoltaic power generation system.
AU ZHANG J
AE ZHANG J (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 202080258G

PN CN111485100-A; CN111485100-B
TI Enhancing gold leaching rate by suspension roasting of carbon-containing gold ore involves grinding carbon-containing gold ore, heating for arsenic removal roasting, oxidizing, grinding slag powder, cyanide leaching, and filtering.
AU LI Y
   HAN Y
   XIAO H
   YUAN S
   GAO P
   SUN Y
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 2020759536
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing gold leaching rate by suspension roasting of carbon-containing gold ore involves crushing carbon-containing gold ore, grinding to obtain fine ore, transporting to pre-oxidation suspension roaster, igniting gas and air through burner to generate high-temperature flue gas, passing flue gas to pre-oxidation suspension roaster, suspending fine ore under negative pressure, heating for arsenic removal roasting, where arsenic in fine ore is converted into arsenic trioxide and remaining solid material is used as pre-oxidized roasting powder, passing pre-oxidized roasting powder into strong oxidation suspension roasting furnace, suspending pre-oxidized roasting powder under action of airflow, roasting, oxidizing carbon and sulfur to produce sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide, where remaining solid material is used as strong oxidation slag powder, grinding portion of slag powder to obtain fine powder, cyanide leaching, and filtering to obtain gold-containing cyanide leaching solution.
   USE - Enhancing gold leaching rate by suspension roasting of carbon-containing gold ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has excellent production continuity and strong adaptability to different ores.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhancing gold leaching rate by suspension roasting of carbon-containing gold ore involves crushing carbon-containing gold ore to particle size of 5-15 mm, grinding to obtain fine ore, where 80 %mass or more of fine ore has particle size of -0.074 mm, amount of gold in carbon-containing gold ore is 5-30 g/t and carbon content is 0.2-20%, transporting fine ore to pre-oxidation suspension roaster with burner at bottom, where top of pre-oxidation suspension roaster is connected to induced draft fan through pipeline, turning on induced draft fan, igniting gas and air through burner to generate high-temperature flue gas, passing high-temperature flue gas to pre-oxidation suspension roaster, suspending fine ore under action of airflow and negative pressure, heating to 650-700 degrees C for arsenic removal roasting, where arsenic in fine ore is converted into arsenic trioxide and remaining solid material is used as pre-oxidized roasting powder, passing pre-oxidized roasting powder into strong oxidation suspension roasting furnace, where top of strong oxidation suspension roasting furnace is connected with induced draft fan through pipeline, turning on induced draft fan, introducing oxygen and nitrogen from bottom of strong oxidation suspension roaster, suspending pre-oxidized roasting powder under action of airflow, strong oxidation roasting at 550-600 degrees C, oxidizing carbon and sulfur to produce sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide, where remaining solid material is used as strong oxidation slag powder, cooling strong oxidation slag powder to normal temperature, grinding portion of slag powder to particle size of -0.074 mm which accounts for 90 %mass or more to obtain fine powder, adding water to make 35-45 %mass slurry, adding 0.5-8 kg/t sodium cyanide, stirring, cyanide leaching for 20-24 hours, and filtering to obtain gold-containing cyanide leaching solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of oxygen in the strong oxidation suspension roaster is 20-50 vol.%. The leaching rate of gold in the gold-containing cyanide leaching solution is 95% or more.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN CN111268783-A
TI Cyclic degradation of organic materials comprises introducing water into reactor, passing feed stream and feed material into the reactor and degrading, where the feed stream includes an initial material stream and an oxidant material stream.
AU ZHANG D
   QI Z
   ZHOU W
   LIN W
AE CHINESE ACAD AGRIC SCI URBAN AGRIC RES (CAGS-C)
GA 202054993K
AB    NOVELTY - Cyclic degradation of organic materials comprises (i) introducing water into the reactor and making the water in the reactor enter the supercritical water state; (ii) passing feed stream and feed material continuously into the reactor and degrading, where the feed stream includes an initial material stream, a secondary material stream, and an oxidant material stream, and stream material that has not undergone degradation treatment by the reactor contains organic matter to be degraded, the secondary material stream has undergone at least one degradation treatment in the reactor and contains incompletely degraded organic matter, and the oxidant material stream contains an oxidant that degrades organic matter; (iii) separating the partially degraded material stream containing incompletely degraded organic matter from the reactor into a gas product containing carbon dioxide gas, a liquid product containing water and a secondary stream.
   USE - The method is useful for cyclic degradation of organic materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cyclic degradation of organic materials comprises (i) introducing water into the reactor and making the water in the reactor enter the supercritical water state; (ii) passing feed stream and feed material continuously into the reactor and degrading, where the feed stream includes an initial material stream, a secondary material stream, and an oxidant material stream, and stream material that has not undergone degradation treatment by the reactor contains organic matter to be degraded, the secondary material stream has undergone at least one degradation treatment in the reactor and contains incompletely degraded organic matter, and the oxidant material stream contains an oxidant that degrades organic matter; (iii) separating the partially degraded material stream containing incompletely degraded organic matter from the reactor into a gas product containing carbon dioxide gas, a liquid product containing water and a secondary stream; (iv) discharging the gas product and the liquid product, and supplying the secondary stream to the reactor as part of the feed stream; (v) continuing to repeat steps (ii) to (iv) so that the amount of organic matter that is completely degraded and the amount of organic matter brought in by the initial material flow reach equilibrium; (vi) continuing to repeat steps (ii) to (iv) until the expected amount of initial material stream degradation treatment is completed; where, in step (ii), the feed rates of the initial material stream and the oxidant material stream are set, the carbon to oxygen molar ratio of the carbon in the initial stream and the oxygen in the oxidant stream is less than 0.5; before the secondary stream is generated in step (ii), the feed stream contains 0% by weight Secondary stream; after the secondary stream is generated in step (ii), the feed stream supplied to the reactor is calculated based on the total of the initial stream and the secondary stream, and the amount is controlled to 1-99 wt.%.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN110150023-A
TI Method for cultivating fungus basswood, involves selecting tree, cutting selected linden wood, filling through hole of wood section, and filling wood section and culture material, followed by placing packaged bacteria bag in sterilizing pot.
AU LIU S
   MA W
AE LIU S (LIUS-Individual)
GA 201977428X
AB    NOVELTY - A fungus basswood cultivating method involves selecting a tree, cutting the selected linden wood and peer through the wooden section, filling the through hole of the wood section, filling the wood section into a high temperature resistant polyethylene bag and filling the culture material with the void to form a bacteria package, placing the packaged bacteria bag in a sterilizing pot for sterilization, transferring the bacteria bag to the buffer room, extracting the hot gas, and transferring to the cooling room for cooling, inoculating the strains, transferring an inoculated bacteria bag to the culture rack of the culture chamber, ventilating the medium and ventilating to discharge carbon dioxide, reaching an effective accumulated temperature of the ear fungus package, transferring the bacteria bag into the ear shed, removing an ear stick from the bag and carrying the hanging stick, leaving a distance between two ear sticks and controlling the temperature and humidity.
   USE - Method for cultivating fungus basswood.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating fungus basswood with improved yield and quality, high use value and ensured nutrition value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fungus basswood cultivating method involves selecting a tree for 5-15 years old and has a diameter of 5-8 cm, cutting the selected linden wood into a 25 cm long wooden section and peer through the wooden section, filling the through hole of the wood section with a uniformly stirred culture material having a water content of 40-80%, filling the wood section into a high temperature resistant polyethylene bag and filling the culture material with the void to form a bacteria package, placing the packaged bacteria bag in a sterilizing pot for sterilization, after the sterilization is completed, transferring the bacteria bag to the buffer room for 15 minutes, extracting the hot gas, and transferring to the cooling room for cooling, and cooling time for 12 hours, inoculating the strains that have passed the test, and inoculating an each strain with 100-120 ml or 8% of the total weight of the bacteria package, transferring an inoculated bacteria bag to the culture rack of the culture chamber under the initial control temperature at 26-28 degrees C, the medium-term control temperature at 23 degrees C, and the later control temperature at 18 degrees C, ventilating the medium and ventilating to discharge carbon dioxide during the cultivation process, reaching an effective accumulated temperature of the ear fungus package at 1800 degrees C, transferring the bacteria bag into the ear shed at a temperature of 15 degrees C for ear culture, when the formation rate of the fungus on the surface of the ear fungus is 2-3%, removing an ear stick from the bag and carrying the hanging stick, leaving a distance of 15 cm between each two ear sticks and controlling the temperature and humidity to culture until the ear is mature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The tree comprises Quercus, chestnut, birch, elm and linden tree.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The culture material comprises 76% broadleaf wooden meal, 20% wheat bran, 2% bean cake powder, 1% calcium superphosphate, 0.5% gypsum powder and 0.5% quicklime.

PN CN109006093-A
TI Relieving peach tree dormancy, by constructing greenhouse in garden, fertilizing microelement fertilizer e.g. calcium, spraying dormancy-breaking agent e.g. Salvia miltiorrhiza extract to peach tree and wrapping with plastic wrap.
AU SUN Y
AE JIESHOU TIANRUNFA HOME FARM (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4589H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for relieving peach tree dormancy involves constructing the greenhouse in garden in the beginning of January, illuminating everyday for 7-9 hours, increasing the peach temperature to 15-18 degrees C, increasing the concentration of carbon dioxide in the night shed to 1500-1600 ppm and fertilizing microelement fertilizer the micro liquid jet wet-prepared on the surface of soil at the dosage is 40-50/acre and spraying dormancy-breaking agent to the peach tree in the evening during mid-January, wrapping with plastic wrap for 12-15 hours, removing the wrapped material and continuously using the dormancy-breaking agent for 2 times (each interval for 4-5 days), where the dormancy-breaking agent is prepared by using Salvia miltiorrhiza extract, linoleic acid, phosphate buffer solution, chitosan, tannic acid, dextran, phenylalanine, potassium phosphate, hydrogen peroxide solution and water, adding the organic fertilizer and performing irrigation after 1 week.
   USE - The method is useful for relieving peach tree dormancy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accelerates the respiratory metabolism of peach trees, regulates the activity of the enzyme in the cell, movement of the cell, balance of the hormone and transmission of the neurotransmitter and development of the flower bud, improves the germination rate, utilization of soil resources and germination uniformity of the peach tree, exhibits rapid performance and solves the problem of quality deterioration and periodicity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for relieving peach tree dormancy involves (a) constructing the greenhouse in garden in the beginning of January, illuminating everyday for 7-9 hours, increasing the peach temperature to 15-18 degrees C, increasing the concentration of carbon dioxide in the night shed to 1500-1600 ppm and fertilizing microelement fertilizer the micro liquid jet wet-prepared on the surface of soil at the dosage is 40-50/acre, where the micro-fertilizer comprises 1.5-1.6 g/kg calcium, 1.2-1.3 g/kg zinc, 0.8-0.9 g/kg iron, 0.3-0.4 g/kg magnesium, 0.2-0.3 g/kg copper and 0.1-0.2 g/kg manganese and (b) spraying dormancy-breaking agent to the peach tree in the evening during mid-January, wrapping with plastic wrap for 12-15 hours, removing the wrapped material and continuously using the dormancy-breaking agent for 2 times (each interval of 4-5 days), where the dormancy-breaking agent is prepared by using 70-80 pts. wt. Salvia miltiorrhiza extract, 3-5 pts. wt. linoleic acid, 2-3 pts. wt. phosphate buffer solution, 1.3-1.5 pts. wt. chitosan, 1-1.3 pts. wt. tannic acid, 0.8-1 pt. wt. dextran, 0.5-0.6 pt. wt. phenylalanine, 0.3-0.4 pt. wt. potassium phosphate, 1.5-2 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide solution and 150-160 pts. wt. water, adding the organic fertilizer and performing irrigation after 1 week.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Property: The artificially supplemented light intensity in step (a) is 400-450 lx. The daytime temperature difference is controlled not less than 12-14 degrees C. The pH of the phosphate buffer is 7.2-7.4. Preferred Method: The S.miltiorrhiza extract is prepared by crushing the S.miltiorrhiza, sieving the crushed material using a sieve of 30-40 mesh sieve, adding 6-8 times water, ultrasonically dispersing for 30-40 minutes, adding 2-2.3 mol/l acetic acid solution to adjust the pH of the ultrasonic dispersion to 5.5-5.8, boiling the material for 1-1.2 hours, cooling the boiled material in an ice bath, filtering the cooled material in ice bath, adding 0.2-0.3 %mass bamboo salt for 20-30 minutes and storing at 0-5 degrees C for refrigeration. Preferred Composition: The amount of the hydrogen peroxide solution is 25-30 %mass.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN108987805-A; CN108987805-B
TI Method for preparing lithium ion battery electrode material for preventing corrosion, involves preparing lithium ion battery anode material and detecting residual lithium on surface of lithium ion battery anode material.
AU LI Y
   XUE L
   ZHANG J
   LI W
   LEI T
   CHEN Y
   LI P
   LI H
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2018A19124
AB    NOVELTY - A lithium ion battery electrode material processing method involves preparing a lithium ion battery anode material, and detecting residual lithium on the surface of the lithium ion battery anode material, reacting the residual lithium on the surface of the anode material of the lithium ion battery with carbon dioxide and water, assembling a lithium ion battery with the lithium ion battery cathode material, the prepared anode material and the prepared electrolyte, forming lithium carbonate and/or lithium hydroxide on the surface of the anode material of the lithium ion battery and adding an oxide and/or a metal salt reacted with hydrogen fluoride. The oxide and/or metal salt is added in the form of fine particles, followed by reacting a part of the additive with hydrogen fluoride generated in the electrolyte to form a fluoride, migrating a part of the additive to the positive and/or negative surface of the lithium ion battery to form a denser solid electrolyte interface film.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium ion battery electrode material for preventing corrosion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the lithium ion battery electrode material with less dosage and convenient storage, and effectively improving the cycle stability of the battery, electrochemical performance and service life, in simple, convenient and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A lithium ion battery electrode material preparing a lithium ion battery anode material, and detecting residual lithium on the surface of the lithium ion battery anode material, reacting the residual lithium on the surface of the anode material of the lithium ion battery with carbon dioxide and water in air to form lithium carbonate and/or lithium hydroxide, assembling a lithium ion battery with the lithium ion battery cathode material, the prepared anode material and the prepared electrolyte, forming lithium carbonate and/or lithium hydroxide on the surface of the anode material of the lithium ion battery by reacting with the lithium salt in the electrolyte to form hydrogen fluoride during the assembly of the battery and/or after the assembly is completed or reacting traces of water and electrolyte in the battery of the lithium salt to form hydrogen fluoride and adding an oxide and/or a metal salt reacted with hydrogen fluoride in the process of formulating the electrolyte. The oxide and/or metal salt is added in excess and added in the form of fine particles, followed by reacting a part of the additive with hydrogen fluoride generated in the electrolyte to form a fluoride, migrating and/or diffusing part of the additive to the positive and/or negative surface of the lithium ion battery under the influence of electric field and/or concentration difference to enter the pore of the solid electrolyte interface film formed on the surface of the positive electrode and/or the negative electrode medium to form a denser solid electrolyte interface film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium ion battery anode material is ternary anode material. The ternary anode material is nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary anode material and/or nickel-cobalt-aluminum ternary anode material. The lithium salt in the electrolyte is a fluorine-containing lithium salt. The fluorine-containing lithium salt is lithium hexafluorophosphate. The cation in the oxide and/or metal salt is selected from anion of lithium, sodium, potassium, calcium, titanium, silicon, zirconium, magnesium, aluminum, zinc, nickel, cobalt, manganese, molybdenum and vanadium. The oxide and/or the metal salt are selected from cobalt carbonate, sodium carbonate, nickel fluoride, manganese carbonate, zirconium tetrafluoride, titanium dioxide and alumina.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN WO2018206850-A1; CA3062730-A1; CN110740801-A; US2020078730-A1; EP3634606-A1; BR112019023517-A2
TI Purifying gas involves e.g. treating gas in            multistage treatment, injecting motive medium            comprising liquid, steam or gaseous agent to ejector,            mixing treated gas with motive medium, and supplying            gas and/or liquid phases to ejector stage.
AU MELIN K
   SIMELL P
   KURKELA E
   SUOMALAINEN M
   MERLIN C
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VTT TECH RES CENT FINLAND LTD (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
GA 2018897248
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying gas comprises treating a gas in a                multistage treatment comprising at least two                ejector stages, injecting a motive medium                comprising liquid, steam or gaseous agent at high                pressure to at least one ejector of the ejector                stage, and sucking the gas into the same ejector in                which the gas is mixed with the motive medium for                forming a mixture, supplying at least a part of gas                phase and/or liquid phase of the mixture to a                second or later ejector stage which comprises at                least one ejector so that a second or later motive                medium comprising liquid, steam or gaseous agent is                injected to the ejector and the gas phase and/or                the liquid phase is sucked into the same ejector in                which the gas phase and/or liquid phase is mixed                with the second or later motive medium for forming                a second or later mixture, where at least one of                the mixtures comprises an additive for removing                impurities of the gas and a purified gas is                formed.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying gas which                is in apparatus and in a chemical synthesis,                synthesis gas production, biofuel production,                hydrogen production, methanol production, biogas                upgrading process, pyrolysis process, LNG                production, purification of flue gases, carbon                dioxide post capture of flue gases, gas                purification of chemical synthesis, biogas                purification process and/or gas fermentation                process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: removes impurities, e.g. carbon                dioxide or other impurities, from the gas;                pressurizes the gas economically; offers a                possibility to effectively and energy-effectively;                and is industrially applicable, simple and                affordable way to pressurize and purify the                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                apparatus for purifying gas in a multistage                treatment, comprising at least two ejector stages                and each ejector stage comprises at least one                ejector, at least one feeding device for injecting                a motive medium which comprises liquid, steam or                gaseous agent at high pressure to at least one                ejector of the ejector stage and at least one                supplying device for sucking the gas into the same                ejector in which the gas is mixed with the motive                medium for forming a mixture, at least one second                feeding device for injecting a second or later                motive medium which comprises liquid, steam or                gaseous agent to a second or later ejector stage                which comprises at least one ejector and at least                one gas phase or liquid phase supplying device for                supplying at least a part of the gas phase and/or                liquid phase of the mixture to the same ejector in                which the gas phase and/or liquid phase is mixed                with the second or later motive medium for forming                a second or later mixture, and at least one                addition device for adding an additive for removing                impurities of the gas to at least one of the                mixtures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The method comprises 2-8                  ejector stages. The high pressure liquid is used                  as motive medium in the first ejector stage                  and/or in the second or later ejector stage. The                  high pressure steam is used as motive medium in                  the first ejector stage and/or in the second or                  later ejector stage. The pressure of the motive                  medium is: 1.3-50 bar if the motive medium is                  liquid; and 3-300 bar if the motive medium is                  steam. The gas is compressed by the motive medium                  in the ejectors so that pressure of the gas is                  increased to a first pressure level in the first                  ejector stage and pressure of the gas is                  increased from the previous pressure level to a                  second or later pressure level in the second or                  later ejector stage. The pressure of the gas is                  increased from a starting pressure to 1.1-3 bar                  in the first ejector stage. The pressure of the                  gas is increased from the first pressure level to                  the final pressure level, which is 2-150 bar, in                  the second or later ejector stage. The part of                  the gas phase of the mixture is separated in the                  separation stage. The part of the liquid phase                  separated in the separation stage is fed as the                  motive medium to the ejector stage. The additive                  is added for increasing a bonding of desired                  impurities to a liquid and/or increasing a                  capture of desired impurities in each separation                  stage. The liquid phase is treated in an                  additional treatment stage after the separation                  stage for removing and/or recovering a desired                  compound from the liquid phase. The purified gas                  is treated, post-treated or refined. Preferred                  Components: The ejector is a liquid motivated                  ejector, or a vapor ejector. The ejector of the                  first ejector stage is a liquid motivated ejector                  and the ejector of the second or later ejector                  stage is a vapor ejector. The apparatus comprises                  at least one separation device for separating at                  least a part of the gas phase from the mixture.                  The apparatus comprises at least one reactor for                  treating the two-phase mixture of the liquid                  phase and gas phase or the gas phase or the                  liquid phase in order to form a chemical compound                  and/or improve a recovery of a desired                  component.
   EXAMPLE - Gas was treated in a multistage treatment                comprising at least two ejector stages. Motive                medium was high pressure liquid e.g. water, was                injected to a liquid motivated ejector of the first                ejector stage and the gas was sucked into the same                ejector in which the gas was mixed with the motive                medium for forming a mixture of liquid phase and                gas phase. The mixture comprises 40 vol.% vapor and                60 vol.% liquid. At least a part of the gas phase                was separated from the mixture in the separation                stage and at least a part of the gas phase was                supplied to a vapor ejector of the second ejector                stage. Liquid phase was recovered. Second motive                medium was high pressure steam e.g. water steam,                was injected to the ejector and the gas phase was                sucked into the same ejector in which the gas phase                was mixed with the second motive medium for forming                a second mixture comprising at least gas phase. The                second motive medium was vaporized before the                second ejector stage. The pressure of the motive                medium was 1.3-50 bar in the first ejector stage.                The pressure of the motive medium was 3-300 bar in                the second ejector stage. The apparatus comprises a                long pipe or tube which has enough residence time                to allow dissolving a desired part of the gas into                the liquid and through which the mixture flows                after the ejector stage. Sodium carbonate or other                suitable additive was added as an additive for                increasing a bonding of desired impurities to a                liquor and/or increasing a capture of desired                impurities in the separation stage. The additive                may be added the mixture before the separation                stage. Alternatively, the liquid phase comprises                the additive and a make-up additive may be added                before the separation stage. In the process, the                gas was compressed and purified by the motive                medium in the ejectors so that pressure of the gas                was increased from a starting pressure to a first                pressure level in the first ejector stage and                pressure of the gas is increased from the first                pressure level to a final pressure level, such as                to a synthesis gas pressure, in the second ejector                stage. The pressure of the gas was increased from                the starting pressure to 1.1-3 bar in the first                ejector stage. The pressure of the gas was                increased from the first pressure level to the                final pressure level was 2-150 bar. Preferably, the                gas or alternatively the motive medium comprises                carbon dioxide as an impurity. The carbon dioxide                can be removed from the gas in the process.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. PN CN108293597-A
TI Black fungus bag-material cultivation method by mixing corn core, wood chips, rice bran, corn powder, gypsum, sugar and water, adding water, adjusting pH value, piling, fermenting, and taking polypropylene or polyethylene plastic bag.
AU HE X
AE HE X (HEXX-Individual)
GA 201858331R
AB    NOVELTY - Black fungus bag-material cultivation method by uniformly mixing and stirring corn core, wood chips, rice bran, corn powder, gypsum, sugar and water, adding water, adjusting pH value, piling, and fermenting to obtain cultivation material; taking polypropylene or polyethylene plastic bag, dividing cultivation material into cultivation bag, compacting while charging, tying bag mouth by rope, sterilizing cultivation material, moving into inoculation room or inoculation tank, and inoculating good strain; and cultivation managing, shading culture room door and window during cultivation, keeping dark environment, cultivating, preventing air vibration, entering bacteria spore from bag opening, extending hypha, checking, removing contaminated bacteria bag, keeping cultivation room ventilation, reducing carbon dioxide concentration, keeping air fresh, growing hypha, continuously culturing, fully absorbing and accumulating hypha nutrient substance to physiological maturity, and transferring.
   USE - Method for black fungus bag-material cultivating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extends industrial chain, increases agricultural resources value in multiple levels, industrial added value and farmer's income, protects forest resources and environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Black fungus bag-material cultivation method involves:
   (A) uniformly mixing and stirring 55-58 wt.% corn core, 22-25 wt.% wood chips, 13 wt.% rice bran, 1 wt.% corn powder, 5 wt.% gypsum, 1 wt.% sugar and water to form mixed material, adding water to water content of 60-65%, adjusting pH value to 7.5-8, piling, and fermenting for 5-8 days to obtain cultivation material;
   (B) taking 17 asterisk 33 cm polypropylene or polyethylene plastic bag, dividing cultivation material into cultivation bag, compacting while charging, tying bag mouth by rope, sterilizing cultivation material, moving into inoculation room or inoculation tank, and inoculating good strain under sterile environment;
   (C) cultivation managing, shading culture room door and window during cultivation, keeping dark environment at 15-30 degrees C at relative air humidity of 60%, cultivating for 5-8 days, preventing air vibration, entering bacteria spore from bag opening, extending hypha after 5-8 days, checking, removing contaminated bacteria bag from cultivation room, keeping cultivation room ventilation, reducing carbon dioxide concentration, keeping air fresh, growing hypha, continuously culturing for 7-10 days, fully absorbing and accumulating hypha nutrient substance to physiological maturity, and transferring;
   (D) using blade around plastic bag when hyphae reach physiological maturity, dividing into 4 rows, opening, stacking by layout, putting film on bacteria stack, hanging bag in greenhouse, keeping field temperature at 15-23 degrees C at relative humidity of more than 85%, improving space humidity in greenhouse space if the air humidity is low, scattering light on field, and fresh-maintaining; and
   (E) harvesting when having soft and thick meat quality, generating white powder arm spore, drying bacteria bag, recovering growth of hypha in bag, and promoting formation of next stubble of black fungus and prevent bacteria growth according to management method.

TI Combined flame extinguishing agent comprises propellant and material liquid (comprised of e.g. surfactant, foaming agent, thickening agent, stabilizing agent, inhibitor, flame retardant, foam enhancer, penetrating agent and solvent).
AU REN Z
   QIU Y
   LAI S
   YANG Q
AE GUANGDONG THEAOSON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201835772V
AB    NOVELTY - Combined flame extinguishing agent comprises propellant and material liquid in weight ratio of (2-15):100, the material liquid comprises 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. surfactant, 3-10 pts. wt. foaming agent, 0.1-3.5 pts. wt. thickening agent, 1-5 pts. wt. stabilizing agent, 0.05-2 pts. wt. inhibitor, 1-5 pts. wt. flame retardant, 0.1-1 pts. wt. foam enhancer, 1-5 pts. wt. penetrating agent, 60-90 pts. wt. solvent, 0.01-1 pts. wt. indicator and 0-5 pts. wt. pH regulator, the propellant comprises nitrogen and carbon dioxide in weight ratio of (40-70):(30-60).
   USE - Used as combined flame extinguishing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The extinguishing agent: meet the jet effects; contains raw materials, which are are non-phosphorus, and do not cause eutrophication of the water body; is economical and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Combined flame extinguishing agent comprises propellant and material liquid in weight ratio of (2-15):100, the material liquid comprises 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. surfactant, 3-10 pts. wt. foaming agent, 0.1-3.5 pts. wt. thickening agent, 1-5 pts. wt. stabilizing agent, 0.05-2 pts. wt. inhibitor, 1-5 pts. wt. flame retardant, 0.1-1 pts. wt. foam enhancer, 1-5 pts. wt. penetrating agent, 60-90 pts. wt. solvent, 0.01-1 pts. wt. indicator and 0-5 pts. wt. pH regulator, the propellant comprises nitrogen and carbon dioxide in weight ratio of (40-70):(30-60), where the surfactant is perfluoroalkyl betaine, perfluoroalkyl glycoside, perfluoroalkyl polyoxyethylene ether, perfluoroalkyl phosphate, perfluoroalkyl phenol vinyl ether or perfluoroalkyl glucosamines, the foaming agent is at least four of sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, dodecylamine oxide, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene 9 ether, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene 7 ether, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene 12 ether, alkyl glycosides, coconut oil fatty amide, sodium oleate, sodium stearate, cocamidopropyl betaine and sodium xylene sulfonate, the foam stabilizer is polyether silicone emulsion, the corrosion inhibitor is sodium silicate, sodium citrate, disodium edentate and sodium nitrite in equal weight ratios. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing combined flame extinguishing agent, comprising (1) adding solvent into sealed reaction kettle A, stirring and adding corrosion inhibitor during stirring, (2) adding flame retardant, performing high speed stirring process, (3) improving stirring speed, adding surfactant and foaming agent, and stirring for 1 hour, (4) into the sealed reaction kettle B, slowly adding stabilizing agent, and adding penetrating agent and foam stabilizer while stirring, (5) slowly adding the mixed liquid in the reaction kettle A into in the reaction kettle B, accompanied by stirring, and (6) adding thickening agent, pH regulator and indicator into reaction kettle A, adding slowly and stirring fastly, waiting for defoaming and cooling to the room temperature to obtain the flame-extinguishing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The flame retardant is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate or sodium chloride, the thickener is polyacrylamide, yellow gum, guar gum, hydroxypropylmethylcellulose, hydroxyethyl cellulose or xanthan gum, the foam enhancer is 8C fatty alcohol, 10C fatty alcohol, 12C fatty alcohol, 14C fatty alcohol or 16C fatty alcohol, the penetrant is JFC, JFC-1, JFC-2, JFC-E or JFC-M, the indicator is methyl orange, methyl red or bromothymolphthalein. The solvent is de-ionized water, the pH regulator sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the stirring treatment conditions are, the temperature is 30 plus minus 2 degrees C, the stirring speed is 1000 revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is 5 minutes. In the step (2), the condition of the high speed stirring process is, stirring speed is 2000 revolutions/minute, temperature is 25 plus minus 2 degrees C, and the stirring time is 15 minutes. In the step (3), the increased stirring speed is 3500 turns/second, temperature is 35 plus minus 2 degrees C. In the step (4), the stirring treatment conditions are, the temperature is 25 plus minus 2 degrees C, stirring speed is 1500 revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is 10 minutes. In the step (5), the stirring temperature is 25 degrees C, the stirring speed is 2000 revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is 30 minutes. In the step (6), the conditions of the rapid stirring processing are, the temperature is 30 degrees C, the stirring speed is 4000 revolutions/minute and stirring time is 5 minutes.

PT P
PN CN107841556-A; CN107841556-B
TI New kit useful for detecting developmental toxicity of drugs or other compounds based on C/EBP-alpha and IGF1R genes.
AU WANG H
   WEN Y
   QI Y
   HE B
   WANG G
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 201828692J
AB    NOVELTY - Kit, is new. The kit comprises primers for detecting mRNA expression of C/EBP alpha and IGF1R genes. A mammalian cell line or differentiated cell to subject a drug or other compound and cell lines or differentiated cells transfected with plasmid containing IGFIR promoter and a luciferase reporter gene in the cell, cell lysate and luciferase.
   USE - The kit is useful for detecting developmental toxicity of drugs or other compounds based on C/EBP alpha and IGF1R genes (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of kit for detecting developmental toxicity of drugs or other compounds based on C/EBP alpha and IGF1R genes, comprising (i) inoculating cell lines or differentiated cells at 5 /asterisk 105 cells/well in 6 well plates containing 10% serum in complete medium at 37 degrees C and incubating in 5% carbon dioxide saturated incubator; (ii) selecting the drug or other compound, screening, and dissolving to obtain 1nM-1M solution, adding the dissolved drug or other compound into the cultured cells according to their respective concentration gradients, setting the control group and processing for 24 hours; (iii) discarding the cell culture medium from each well, washing once with phosphate buffer saline solution, extracting trizol 500-1000 mu l per well and total cellular RNA; (iv) adding 200 mu l chloroform into the EP tube, adding trizol, vibrating vigorously for 30 seconds, centrifuging at 12000 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes, aspirating the upper supernatant, adding iso-propanol, mixing, allowing to stand for 10 minutes, centrifuging at 12000 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, washing twice with 75% pre-cooled ethanol to remove ions or salt, detecting RNA concentration and purity on photometer, performing reverse transcription PCR and detecting mRNA expression of C/EBP alpha and IGF1R; (v) selecting cell lines or differentiated cells, transfecting with step (i) containing the IGF1R promoter-luciferase reporter gene, inoculating 5/asterisk105 cells/well in 6-well plate containing 10% serum in complete medium, incubating in 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide saturated incubator; (vi) selecting the drug or other compound, dissolving the solution in a concentration of 1 nM-1M, adding the dissolved drug or other compound into the cells cultured in step (v) and setting the control group for 24 hours; (vii) discarding the cell culture medium in each well, washing with phosphate buffer saline solution, adding 200-500 mu l cell lysate and detecting luciferase reporter gene; (viii) lysing the lysed cells, transferring the cell debris and the liquid into the centrifuge tube, and centrifuging at 12000 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, taking the supernatant, adding luciferase, mixing, reacting and measuring with fluorophotometer; and (ix) treating the increased expression of C/EBP alpha gene in cells with drugs or other compounds to be screened, reducing expression of the IGF1R gene, decreasing activity of the luciferase reporter gene in cells containing the IGF1R promoter-reporter gene and indicating the drug or other compound to be screened is developmentally toxic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The sequence of the primer pair for C/EBP alpha is fully defined 38 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 1-2). The sequence of the primer pair for IGF1R is fully defined 40 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 3-4). The plasmid containing the IGFIR promoter and luciferase reporter gene is GV238 vector and digested with MluI/BglII. The sequence containing the IGFIR promoter is inserted and the sequence is fully defined 2161 base pairs (SEQ ID NO:5). The GV238 vector is purchased from Shanghai GKN Chemical Technology Co., Ltd. The drug or other compound includes synthetic compound, natural product, organic molecules, inorganic small molecules, lipids and/or sugars. The criteria for the kit are that the group to be screened for drugs or other compounds meets the following three indicators at the same time as the control group. The increased expression of C/EBP alpha gene in cells of the drug or other compound to be screened, reduced IGF1R gene expression and decreased activity of the luciferase reporter gene in cells containing the IGF1R promoter-reporter gene suggests developmental toxicity of the drug or other compound to be screened.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN107723485-A; CN107723485-B
TI Recovering and recycling of manganese from waste residue of magnesium alloy flux, involves crushing manganese-magnesium alloy flux slag, mixing waste flux slag powder and water, extracting and stripping zinc and iron in filtrate.
AU JI Z
   ZHU G
   JI J
AE WENXI YUANHUA METALLURGICAL MATERIALS CO (WENX-Non-standard)
   ZHU G (ZHUG-Individual)
GA 201817923L
AB    NOVELTY - A manganese-magnesium alloy flux slag is crushed. The waste flux slag powder and water are mixed, reacted, filtered and centrifuged, to obtain filter residue. The filter residue is dehydrated to obtain magnesium products. The filter residue and water are added to carbonation equipment. Carbon dioxide is added to mixture, acidified and carbonated to obtain acidified mixture. The filtrate is extracted with P204 extractant and flux kerosene. Zinc and iron in filtrate are extracted and stripped to obtain ferrous sulfate and zinc sulfate as raw material for compound fertilizer.
   USE - Recovering and recycling of manganese from waste residue of magnesium alloy flux.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A manganese-magnesium alloy flux slag is crushed to 30-40 mm. Coarse residue of 30-40 mm is crushed to 60-80 mesh. The waste flux slag powder of 60-80 mesh and water in solid-liquid ratio of 1:5 are mixed and reacted for 30 minutes. The mixed liquid is filtered through aperture of 2 mm and centrifuged, to obtain filter residue comprising magnesium alloy particles. The filter residue comprising magnesium alloy particles is dehydrated to obtain less than 1% magnesium products. The filtrate is recycled to belt vacuum filter with hole of 80 mesh. The filtrate is used to extract chlorides. The filter residue and water in solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:5 are added to carbonation reaction equipment. Carbon dioxide is added to mixture, acidified, carbonated and filtered using belt vacuum filter with hole of 80 mesh to obtain acidified mixture. The filter residue comprises coarse fluorite powder. The filtrate is treated to extract magnesium carbonate. The content of crude fluorite powder is 60-85% during sulfation reaction. The crude fluorite powder and filtrate in solid ratio of 1:4 are acidified and reacted for 100 minutes. The acidified reaction solution with pH value of 6-7 is filtered with belt vacuum filter. When the moisture content is less than 1%, the filter is discharged to fluorite powder product. The filtrate is extracted with P204 extractant and flux kerosene. The zinc and iron in filtrate are extracted and stripped with sulfuric acid to obtain ferrous sulfate and zinc sulfate as raw material for compound fertilizer. The remaining solution comprises manganese extraction material. The remaining solution is extracted with P204 extractant and flux kerosene for secondary extraction. The secondary extract is stripped with 35-37% hydrochloric acid. When the manganese content in residual solution is less than 0.02 g/L, the reaction is stopped. The stripping solution is evaporated. When solid-liquid ratio of solution is 50%, the solution is evaporative cooled and crystallized, to obtain manganese chloride tetrahydrate. The manganese chloride tetrahydrate is heated, dried and dehydrated at 250-300 degrees C to obtain anhydrous manganese chloride product. The manganese-magnesium alloy containing manganese element is added as alloying element.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-E02; M25-G05; M25-G16; M25-G17

PN WO2018013388-A1; EP3320032-A1; CN109312095-A; US2019218356-A1; US10745533-B2; EP3320032-B1; CN109312095-B
TI Foamed thermoplastic material used for coating conductor wire, comprises thermoplastic material and cells present in amount to provide foamed thermoplastic material with preset density of unfoamed thermoplastic material density.
AU MA S
   SHETH K C
   ZHOU J
   YANG J
   MADDIKERI R R
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MA S (MASS-Individual)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201805616B
AB    NOVELTY - A foamed thermoplastic material comprises a thermoplastic material and cells which are present in an amount to provide foamed thermoplastic material with density which is 10-90% of the density of the unfoamed thermoplastic material. The thermoplastic material has, in its unfoamed state, flammability index of 0.1-15 determined by method (A) according to ASTM D7309 at heating rate of 1 degrees C per second under nitrogen atmosphere of 25-750 degrees C, and has, in its foamed state, tensile elongation of 100-500% measured at 23 degrees C using a coated wire test sample and dielectric constant of 1.4-4.
   USE - Foamed thermoplastic material is used for coating conductor wire for primary insulating core transmission medium of plenum cable (all claimed) used as data and voice transmission cables installed in building plenum.
   ADVANTAGE - The foamed thermoplastic material has excellent flame retardance and mechanical property, and low smoke generation, and is lightweight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A foamed thermoplastic material comprises a thermoplastic material and cells having number average mean diameter of 5-150 mu m. The cells are present in an amount to provide the foamed thermoplastic material with density which is 10-90% of the density of the unfoamed thermoplastic material. The thermoplastic material has, in its unfoamed state, flammability index of 0.1-15 determined by method (A) according to ASTM D7309 at heating rate of 1 degrees C per second under nitrogen atmosphere of 25-750 degrees C, flame growth rate of 0.1-1.5 kW/m2.second determined according to ASTM E1354 or ISO 5660 using applied heat flux of 35 kW per square meter, specific extinction area of 300-1200 square meters per kg determined according to ASTM E1354 or ISO 5660 using applied heat flux of 35 kW per square meter, tensile elongation of more than 100% measured according to UL 1581 at 23 degrees C using a coated wire test sample and dielectric constant of 2-4 determined according to ASTM D 150-11 at 1 MHz, and has, in its foamed state, tensile elongation of 100-500% measured according to UL 1581 at 23 degrees C using a coated wire test sample and dielectric constant of 1.4-4 determined according to ASTM D 150-11 at 1 MHz. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of foamed thermoplastic material, which involves melt-kneading the unfoamed thermoplastic material and a carbon dioxide-generating blowing agent; and
   (2) coating of conductor wire, which involves preheating the conductor wire to temperature of 150-220 degrees C and contacting the preheated conductor wire with the unfoamed thermoplastic material and the carbon dioxide-generating blowing agent to provide the foamed thermoplastic material surrounding the conductor wire.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the plenum cable.
   Plenum cable (10)
   Jacket (20)
   Twisted conductor pairs (30)
   Separator spline (70)
   Conductor (80)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide-generating blowing agent is a chemical blowing agent containing sodium bicarbonate and having decomposition temperature of 150-250 degrees C, and is present in an amount of 0.1-5 pts. wt.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The thermoplastic material comprises a thermoplastic polymer chosen from poly(phenylene ether) and polysulfone containing a repeating unit of formula (I). The foamed thermoplastic material further comprises 25-40 pts. wt. poly(2,6-dimethyl-1,4-phenylene ether) having intrinsic viscosity of 0.3-0.6 dl/g or 20-30 pts. wt. poly(phenylene ether-siloxane) copolymer, 30-45 pts. wt. impact modifier comprising a hydrogenated block copolymer (C1) of an alkenyl aromatic monomer and a conjugated diene containing 30-50 pts. wt. poly(alkenyl aromatic) and having melt flow index of 5-10 g/10 minutes measured at 260 degrees C and 5 kg load according to ASTM D 1238, and a hydrogenated block copolymer (C2) of an alkenyl aromatic monomer and a conjugated diene containing 20-40 pts. wt. poly(alkenyl aromatic) and having melt flow index of 2-8 g/10 minutes measured at 230 degrees C and 5 kg load according to ASTM D1238, 10-20 pts. wt. polyolefin comprising a polybutene, ethylene copolymer and/or a propylene homopolymer, 10-15 pts. wt. organophosphate flame retardant comprising a phosphoric acid salt and a metal dialkyl phosphinate, and 25-35 pts. wt. surface-treated metal hydroxide. Preferred Property: The foamed thermoplastic material further has foaming extent of 5-80%.Preferred Conditions: The composition is melt-kneaded at maximum temperature of 220-280 degrees C. The process of contacting is carried out in an extruder having feed zone temperature of 180-200 degrees C and exit die temperature of 220-280 degrees C.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A12 (Polymers of di-and higher olefins; acetylenics; nitroso compounds.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. PN CN113666360-A
TI Preparing nanometer carbon ball based on mixed            phenol used in carbon dioxide absorbing material,            involves dispersing mixed phenol in mixed solution of            water, absolute ethanol, ammonia water,            ultrasonicating, adding formaldehyde solution.
AU HOU H
   CUI Y
   GAO X
   NIU Y
   LIU Q
   HUANG R
   WANG J
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2021D73722
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nanometer-carbon ball based on mixed                phenol involves dispersing mixed phenols into mixed                solution of water, absolute ethanol and ammonia,                ultrasonically processing to obtain uniform                solution, adding formaldehyde aqueous solution to                uniform solution and stirring at certain                temperature for period of time, placing in reaction                kettle, performing hydrothermal for multiple hours                at high temperature, centrifuging and washing                sample, drying, calcining at high-temperature under                nitrogen dioxide atmosphere to obtain nano-carbon                ball.
   USE - Method for preparing nanometer carbon ball                based on mixed phenol used in carbon dioxide                absorbing material preparation field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to provide high specific                surface area, good hole structure, excellent                chemical stability and uniform shape of nanometer                carbon ball.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed phenol                  comprises any two or more than two of phenol,                  resorcinol, m-cresol, alpha-naphthol and 3 amino                  phenol.Preferred Conditions: The mol ratio of                  phenol to resorcinol to m-cresol to                  alpha-naphthol to 3-amino-phenol is                  0-1.8:0-1.8:0-1.7:0-1.4:0-0.18. The volume ratio                  of water to absolute ethyl alcohol is 20:1:1:1-4.                  The ratio of adding ammonia water to carbon                  source quality provided by mixed phenol is 0.1-8                  mL:0.1-2.0 g. The stirring is to stir for 12-24                  hours at 0-90degrees Celsius; hydro-thermal is                  hydro-thermal for 12-36 hours at 100-250degrees                  Celsius. The high temperature calcining comprises                  pyrolyzing for 2 hour at heating rate of 1-5                  minute at 300-350degrees Celsius, then                  carbonizing for 3 hours at 700-800degrees                  Celsius, raising speed of 1-5 minute.Preferred Method: The method for preparing                  nanometer carbon ball based on mixed phenol                  involves dissolving mixed phenol to water, before                  mixing solution of absolute ethyl alcohol and                  ammonia water, dispersing hard template silicon                  dioxide (SiO2) ball and cationic surfactant into                  mixed solution and then adding mixed phenol,                  ultrasonic processing, obtaining uniform                  solution.
