The nickel oxide present at between about 5-40 wt.% of the modified red mud catalyst composition.
   USE - Method for reforming over modified red mud catalyst composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reform over modified red mud catalyst composition that improves non-modified red mud catalytic activity for autothermal reforming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition includes component selected from Fe_2O_3, Al_2O_3, SiO_2, Na_2O, CaO, and TiO_2. Preferred Conditions: The gas hourly space velocity of the methane feed with oxygen and carbon dioxide is between about 1000-10000 h^-^1. The majority of the particles of the composition has particle size of less than about 70 micrometer. The nickel oxide is present at between about 10-30 wt.% of the modified red mud catalyst composition. The nickel oxide is present at between about 15-25 wt.% of the modified red mud catalyst composition. The methane conversion is greater than 40% for 6 hours. The molar ratio of methane, carbon dioxide and oxygen is 2:1:1. The Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of the modified red mud catalyst composition is between 50-90 m^2/g. Preferred Compositions: The composition includes 5-20 wt.% Al_2O_3, 2-10 wt.% CaO, 20-40 wt.% Fe_2O_3, 5-20 wt.% SiO_2, and 10-20 wt.% TiO_2.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN US2021229073-A1; WO2021154759-A1; US11396006-B2
TI Performing bi-reforming over modified red mud            catalyst composition involves providing methane feed in            the presence of carbon dioxide and steam to react over            the modified red mud catalyst composition to produce            synthesis gas.
AU BAMAGAIN R
   ALBUALI M A
   FADHEL B A
   ALSOLAMI B H
   ALSOLAMI B
   FADHEL B
   ALBUALI M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202187088B
AB    NOVELTY - Performing bi-reforming over modified red mud                catalyst composition involves providing methane                feed in the presence of carbon dioxide and steam to                react over the modified red mud catalyst                composition at increased temperature and increased                pressure to produce synthesis gas comprises                hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The composition                comprises red mud material produced from alumina                extraction process from bauxite ore and nickel                oxide, nickel oxide present at between about 5-40                wt.% of the modified red mud catalyst                composition.
   USE - Method for performing bi-reforming over                modified red mud catalyst composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform bi-reforming                over modified red mud catalyst composition that                improves catalytic activity for bi-reforming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition                  includes component selected from Fe_2O_3,                  Al_2O_3, SiO_2, Na_2O, CaO, and TiO_2. Preferred                  Conditions: The majority of the particles of the                  composition has particle size of less than about                  70 micrometer. The nickel oxide is present at                  between 10-30 wt.% of the modified red mud                  catalyst composition. The conversion percentage                  of methane reaches up to about 40%. The molar                  ratio of methane, carbon dioxide, steam is about                  3:1:2. The produced H_2 is 10 mol.% of produced                  products from the reaction for 5 hours. The                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of the                  modified red mud catalyst composition is between                  50-90 m^2/g. The composition includes 5-20 wt.%                  Al_2O_3, 2-10 wt.% calcium oxide, 20-40 wt.%                  Fe_2O_3, 5-20 wt.% SiO_2, and 10-20 wt.%                  TiO_2.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Frost-resistant concrete is prepared by using dry mixture containing ordinary Portland cement, river sand coarse aggregate and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, mixing with water and Bacillus cereus suspension and steaming with carbon dioxide.
AU LEI X
AE CHENGDU JINLUHAI CONSTR TECHNOLOGY CO (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202136806E
AB    NOVELTY - A frost-resistant concrete is prepared by mixing dry mixture with water and Bacillus cereus suspension and steaming with carbon dioxide. The dry mixture comprises ordinary Portland cement, river sand coarse aggregate, modified pumice, antifreeze buffer filler and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose. The modified pumice is prepared by mixing pulverized pumice with ethyl orthosilicate and water and reacting at high temperature and high pressure. The frost-resistant buffer filler is prepared by mixing and reacting starch, sodium silicate solution and hydrochloric acid solution through high temperature and high pressure reaction.
   USE - Frost-resistant concrete.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the frost-resistant concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The dry mixture comprises 60-70 pts. wt. ordinary Portland cement, 20-30 pts. wt. river sand coarse aggregate, 20-30 pts. wt. modified pumice, 10-15 pts. wt. frost-resistant buffer filler and 3-5 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl cellulose. Preferred Property: The mass ratio of dry mixture, water and B.cereus suspension with a concentration of 106 cfu/mL is 30:10:1.

PT P
PN CN112474025-A
TI Performing calcite acid trigger-hydraulic power flotation involves reacting calcite with acid solution to generate carbon dioxide bubble, reducing calcite apparent specific gravity, and separating two minerals.
AU DENG R
   XING D
   DUAN W
   WANG Y
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202130619K
AB    NOVELTY - Performing calcite acid trigger-hydraulic power flotation involves using calcite to react with acid solution to generate carbon dioxide bubble. The bubble and calcite form gas-solid combination. The calcite apparent specific gravity is reduced. The non-carbonate mineral apparent specific gravity is almost unchanged. The mineral apparent specific gravity difference is increased. The two minerals are separated through the interference bed hydraulic separator.
   USE - Method for performing calcite acid trigger-hydraulic power flotation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively solve the problem of difficult separation caused by the small density difference of the calcite and non-carbonate mineral, and the surface property is similar, which can reach the purpose of improving the ore dressing efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves crushing and sieving the ores containing calcite to obtain mixed ore particles of 0.1~2mm, and sieving the mixed ore particles to obtain multiple narrow-grained products, turning on the rising water flow switch of the interference bed hydraulic separator, passing in a certain concentration of acid solution, and adjust the liquid distributor, making the water flow steadily rise in the sorting machine at a certain speed, and add a certain amount of sodium dodecyl sulfonate to the acid solution at the same time. A certain narrow-grained product is continuously and added to the interference bed hydraulic separator through the feeding port, fine-tuning the rising acid solution speed again, the fine-tuning range is between 0.001~0.005m/s, so that calcite and non-carbonate minerals are stratified in the interference bed. The calcite flowing out with the rising water flow is passed through a fine rubber sieve to achieve solid-liquid separation to obtain a calcite product. The dosage of sodium dodecyl sulfonate is 100~500g/t raw ore. The acid solution is a sulfuric acid solution, and the sulfuric acid concentration is 0.5-2 wt.%.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).)

PN CN112403470-A
TI Catalyst for catalyzing reforming of methane carbon dioxide to syngas, is prepared by taking bis(dimethylamino-2-methyl-2-butoxy) nickel as precursor, loading nickel particles and iron particles on titanium dioxide nanotubes and annealing.
AU GAO L
   PANG Y
   SONG X
AE UNIV YULIN (UNYL-C)
GA 2021236103
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises titanium dioxide nanotubes loaded with nickel particles and iron particles, and is prepared by taking bis(dimethylamino-2-methyl-2-butoxy) nickel as precursor, and ammonia plasma or hydrogen plasma as reactant, loading the nickel particles on the titanium dioxide nanotubes by atomic layer deposition, loading the iron particles on the titanium dioxide nanotubes with nickel particles by pulse electrodeposition, and annealing to remove impurities.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalyzing reforming of methane carbon dioxide to syngas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has conversion rate of methane as high as 80% or more at higher temperature, and exhibits high catalytic activity during the continuous operation of more than 400 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The atomic layer deposition is performed by loading nickel particles on the titanium dioxide nanotubes under the condition of substrate temperature at 220-380 degrees C, preferably 270-280 degrees C and precipitation period of 45-55 cycles, preferably 50-55 cycles. The pulse electrodeposition involves using an aqueous solution containing ferrous ammonium sulfate, sodium citrate, citric acid, and boric acid as the electrolyte, maintaining the temperature of the electrolyte at 50-90 degrees C, preferably 70-80 degrees C, maintaining the temperature of the reaction chamber at 150-200 degrees C, preferably 170-180 degrees C, taking continuous pulse, holding and purging as a cycle period, where the time ratio of continuous pulse, holding and purging on the electrolyte is 1:15:15, and the number of cycles is 400-600, preferably 500-550, the content of ferrous ammonia sulfate in the electrolyte is 0.2-0.9 %mass, preferably 0.5-0.7 %mass, the mass content of sodium citrate is 0.15-0.35 %mass, preferably 0.2-0.3 %mass, the mass content of citric acid is 0.25-0.45 %mass, preferably 0.3-0.35 %mass, and the mass content of boric acid is 4.25-5.4 %mass, preferably 4.5-5 %mass. Preferred Conditions: The annealing for removing impurities is carried out at 600-800 degrees C for 30 seconds to 1 minute. Preferred Composition: The loading amount of nickel particles in the catalyst is 4-6 %mass, and the loading amount of iron particles is 10-30 %mass.

PT P
PN WO2021018753-A1; CN114144451-A; EP4004083-A1; US2022315698-A1
TI Producing polyether carbonate polyol, by adding            alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide to H-functional            starter substance in presence of double metal cyanide            or metal complex catalyst based on zinc and/or cobalt            and adding buffer system.
AU HOFMANN J
   LAEMMERHOLD K
   HILKEN P
   WELSCH N
   NEFZGER H
   HOFFMANN J
   HOFMAN J
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO GERMANY AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2021130172
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing polyether carbonate                polyol, involves (i) adding alkylene oxide and                carbon dioxide to an H-functional starter substance                in presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC)                catalyst or a metal complex catalyst based on the                metals zinc and/or cobalt to obtain a reaction                mixture containing the polyether carbonate polyol,                and (ii) adding at least one component K to the                reaction mixture containing the polyether carbonate                polyol, where a buffer system suitable for                buffering a pH of 3-9 is used as component K, and                the component K is free of compounds containing                phosphorus-hydroxy groups.
   USE - The process is used for producing polyether                carbonate polyol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The buffer system is                  used as component K chosen from mixtures of                  carboxylic acids and their alkali metal salts,                  aqueous solutions of alkali metal salts of                  carboxylic acids, 2-(N-morpholino) ethanesulfonic                  acid (MES) and                  3-(4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazinyl)                  propanesulfonic acid (HEPPS), preferably malic                  acid/sodium salt of malic acid, MES and                  HEPPS.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The component K                  comprises neither compounds which contain a                  phosphorus-oxygen bond nor compounds of                  phosphorus which can form one or more                  phosphorus-oxygen bonds through reaction with                  hydroxy-functional compounds. The buffer system                  is suitable for buffering a pH of 3-7.5.                  Preferred Method: The process involves thermally                  reducing the content of highly volatile                  constituents in the polyether carbonate polyol                  obtained step (i) at 80-200 degrees C before step                  (ii), (iii) thermally reducing the content of                  volatile constituents in the reaction mixture                  obtained in step (ii) at 80-200 degrees C, and                  (iv) adding at least one component K to the                  reaction mixture containing the polyether                  carbonate polyol obtained in step (iii). The step                  (i) involves (a) taking an H-functional starter                  substance or a mixture of at least two                  H-functional starter substances or a suspending                  agent, if appropriate, removing water and/or                  other highly volatile compounds by increased                  temperature and/or reduced pressure (drying), and                  adding the catalyst to the H-functional starter                  substance or to the mixture of at least two                  H-functional starter substances and the                  suspending agent before or after drying, (b) if                  appropriate, activating DMC catalyst by adding a                  partial amount (based on the total amount of the                  amount of alkylene oxides used in the activation                  and copolymerization) of alkylene oxide to the                  mixture obtained in step (a), where the addition                  of a partial amount of alkylene oxide optionally                  can take place in the presence of carbon dioxide,                  and leaving still for the temperature peak                  (hotspot) and/or a pressure drop in the reactor                  that occurs due to the exothermic chemical                  reaction, and performing activation for several                  times, and (c) adding alkylene oxide, carbon                  dioxide and optionally an H-functional starter                  substance to the mixture obtained in step (b),                  where at least one H-functional starter substance                  is added in at least one of steps (a) or (c), and                  removing the reaction mixture obtained in step                  (c) from the reactor. In step (c), DMC catalyst                  is continuously added to the reactor. Preferred                  Composition: The component K in step (iv) is                  added in an amount of 5-2000 parts per million                  (ppm), preferably 30-500 ppm. The component K in                  step (ii) is added in an amount of 5-2000 ppm,                  preferably 30-500 ppm.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN KR2020142608-A
TI Recycling organic waste used for feed and fertilizer, comprises e.g. adding mixed drying agent into organic waste to produce intermediate product through lime stabilization reaction, and adding carbon dioxide into intermediate product.
AU JUNGSANGOG
   LIM J
   KIM S J
   YOUNG P S
AE NANOTECHCERAMICS CO LTD (NANO-Non-standard)
   JUNGSANGOG (JUNG-Individual)
GA 2020D01539
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling organic waste, comprises (a) adding a mixed drying agent into organic waste to produce an intermediate product through a lime stabilization reaction, and (b) adding carbon dioxide into the intermediate product to obtain a final product having a pH of 7-9 through a neutralization reaction.
   USE - The organic material is useful for feed (claimed) and as weak base raw material for sterilized neutral fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method protects environment by preventing inflow of leachate generated during treatment of various water-soluble organic wastes including livestock waste, food waste, and sewage sludge into environment; and effectively removes odor peculiar from organic waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for organic material prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed drying agent contains 10-200 pts. wt. oxidizing agent and 10-400 pts. wt. coagulant based on 100 pts. wt. quicklime. The oxidizing agent includes sodium percarbonate, potassium permanganate, chlorine dioxide, ozone, hydrogen peroxide and/or hypochlorous acid. The coagulant includes aluminum sulfate, iron sulfate, aluminum ammonium sulfate, iron chloride, potassium aluminum sulfate, sodium aluminate, sodium silicate and/or bentonite. Preferred method: The method satisfies the following conditions (a) and (b), as given in specification.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC C04-D02; C05-A01; C05-A03A1; C05-A03A2; C05-B02C; C05-C01; C05-C04; C05-C05; C05-C07; C14-S18; C14-T; C14-Y; D03-G; D03-P; D04-A01B; D04-A01K; D04-A01P1; D04-A02; D04-B06

PT P
PN KR2020141798-A; KR2240348-B1
TI Producing high-purity aragonite-type calcium carbonate comprises preparing suspension by adding calcium raw materials to seawater, adding magnesium raw material, and performing indirect carbonation reaction by injecting carbon dioxide.
AU JIN K M
   JEONJUNHYUCK
AE UNIV KOREA MARITIME IND-ACADEMIC COOP (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA MARITIME IND ACADEMIC COOP (UYKR-C)
GA 2021010125
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high-purity aragonite-type calcium carbonate, comprises (a) preparing suspension by adding calcium raw materials to seawater; (b) adding magnesium raw material to the solution to adjust magnesium/calcium molar ratio to 0.5-5; and (c) performing indirect carbonation reaction by injecting and stirring carbon dioxide into the solution while adjusting molar ratio of magnesium/calcium to obtain aragonite-type calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing aragonite-type calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces aragonite-type calcium carbonate in economical manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for producing aragonite-type calcium carbonate (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) is carried out by adjusting molar ratio of magnesium/calcium to 0.5-1. In the step (c), the stirring is performed at the same time as injecting carbon dioxide at greater than or equal to 0.15 L/minute into the solution. Preferred Components: The seawater includes general seawater, seawater desalination concentrated water, brine, bittern, and/or artificial seawater. The calcium raw material is calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide. The magnesium raw material is magnesium chloride hexahydrate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E34-D03A
IP C01F-011/18

PT P
PN CN111908511-A; CN111908511-B
TI Removing calcium and magnesium ions in produced            high-purity manganese sulfate, comprises dispersing            high-purity manganese sulfate precursor in water,            mixing with soluble carbonate, passing carbon dioxide            gas, and solid-liquid separating.
AU WANG Y
   LI Y
   AI Y
   WANG B
   JIA M
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2020B3990B
AB    NOVELTY - Removing calcium and magnesium ions in                produced high-purity manganese sulfate, comprises                (i) dispersing high-purity manganese sulfate                precursor in water to obtain slurry, where the                high-purity manganese sulfate precursor is crude                manganese carbonate and/or crude manganese                hydroxide, and the high-purity manganese sulfate                precursor comprises calcium and magnesium elements,                and (ii) mixing the slurry and soluble carbonate,                then introducing carbon dioxide gas into the                system, and solid-liquid separating.
   USE - The method is useful for removing calcium and                magnesium ions in produced high-purity manganese                sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple steps and low cost; is                easy to carryout; and has excellent calcium and                magnesium ions removal effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the mass                  ratio between high-purity manganese sulfate                  precursor and water is 1:5-8. In step (ii), the                  dosage ratio between soluble carbonate and slurry                  is 0.1-0.2:1 (mol:l). The soluble carbonate                  includes sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate                  and/or ammonium carbonate. In step (ii): the pH                  value of the system when the carbon dioxide is                  introduced is less than or equal to 5. Preferred                  Method: The step (ii) comprises adding soluble                  carbonate by stirring and continuously stirring                  for 1-3 hours. The solid-liquid separation                  further includes washing the obtained solid                  product. The washing detergent is water, the                  number of washings is 4-5 times, and the mass                  ratio between water and solid product for single                  washing is 5-8:1.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.)
MC E31-N05B; E31-N05C; E32-A02; E33-D; E34-B04; E34-D03B; E35-S

TI Medicinal product, preferably sterile medicinal            product useful for treating liquid losses of humans,            comprises flexible mono-chamber container and aqueous            liquid containing bicarbonate.
AU ADAMO V
   SCHWEBEL H J
AE BRAUN MELSUNGEN AG B (BINT-C)
   BRAUN MELSUNGEN AG B (BINT-C)
   BRAUN MELSUNGEN AG B (BINT-C)
GA 2020A2536W
AB    NOVELTY - Medicinal product, preferably sterile                medicinal product, comprises a flexible                mono-chamber container and an aqueous liquid                containing bicarbonate and having a physiological                pH value, where the container comprises a first                side wall and a second side wall, where the first                side wall and the second side wall comprise a                barrier material, where the barrier material is                capable of preventing or retarding escape of carbon                dioxide from the container and/or intake of carbon                dioxide into the container such that the pH-value                of the aqueous liquid is maintained or                substantially maintained during a shelf life at                room temperature of the medicinal product for at                least 12 months.
   USE - The medicinal product is useful for treating                liquid losses of humans and/or used in the dialysis                treatment of humans.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred System: The first side wall and                  the second side wall are arranged opposite each                  other and are connected, preferably cohesively                  connected, at the edges, thus forming a storage                  volume. The first side wall and the second side                  wall comprise the same barrier material or the                  first side wall and the second side wall comprise                  a different barrier material.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The pH-value of the                  aqueous liquid is maintained or substantially                  maintained during a shelf life at room                  temperature of the medicinal product for 24, 30                  or 36 months. The pH-value of the aqueous liquid                  is not greater than 7.8, preferably 7-7.5, during                  the shelf life at room temperature of the                  medicinal product. The aqueous liquid contains                  130-150 mmol/l sodium, 0-5 mmol/l potassium, 0-2                  mmol/l calcium, 0-2 mmol/l magnesium, 90-150                  mmol/l chloride, 10-40 mmol/l bicarbonate, 0-30                  mmol/l gluconate, 0-10 mmol/l citrate and 0-60                  mmol/l glucose, preferably 135-145 mmol/l sodium,                  0-4 mmol/l potassium, 0-1.5 mmol/l calcium, 0-1.5                  mmol/l magnesium, 95-125 mmol/l chloride, 20-35                  mmol/l bicarbonate and 10-25 mmol/l gluconate,                  0-10 mmol/l citrate and 0-60 mmol/l glucose, more                  preferably of 135-145 mmol/l sodium, 4 mmol/l                  potassium, 0.5-1 mmol/l calcium, 0-1.5 mmol/l                  magnesium, 100-120 mmol/l chloride, 24-35 mmol/l                  bicarbonate, 15-20 mmol/l gluconate, 0-5 mmol/l                  citrate and 0-60 mmol/l glucose.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN IN202041044259-A; IN398788-B
TI Nanocomposite material used for sequestering carbon dioxide from fluid stream, comprises graphene oxide nanoparticles, hardener, casting molds, and polyester.
AU SEENUVASAN M
   SRIDHAR N
   JEEVARATHINAM G
   M S
   N S
   G J
AE HINDUSTHAN ENG & TECHNOLOGY COLLEGE (HIND-Non-standard)
GA 2020A40941
AB    NOVELTY - A nanocomposite material comprises polyester, graphene oxide nanoparticles, hardener, and casting molds. The polyester is synthesized from jojoba oil containing tricaprylin, myristate, and esters of fatty acid by mixing glycerol with jojoba oil in a ratio of at least 1:3, adding methanol and phthalic anhydride to the mixture, and heating the mixture at 60-120 degrees C to obtain a homogeneous solution. The polyester comprises 20 vol.% methanol and phthalic anhydride.
   USE - Nanocomposite material used for capturing and sequestering carbon dioxide from fluid stream. Uses include but are not limited to power generation, adsorption, carbon recovery, sensors, batteries, super capacitors, hydrogen storage systems and reinforcement fillers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the nanocomposite material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the synthesis method of resin loaded nanocomposite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The nanocomposite material has a size of 20-40 nm, and shape of well dispersed stacked sheets. The graphene oxide nanoparticles have a size of 10-30 nm. Preferred Components: The graphene oxide nanoparticles are synthesized from precursors such as graphite, sugar, sugar alcohol and polymers.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The esters of fatty acid comprise di-, tri-, tetra-, penta- and octa-decanoic acid. The hardener comprises methyl ethyl ketone peroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The casting molds comprise permanent molds comprising Teflon (RTM: PTFE).
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC A04-E08B; A05-E01D; A12-H05; A12-W14; Q49-A

PT P
PN CN111763560-A
TI Extracting Acer truncatum oil by obtaining samples, drying, crushing, sieving Acer truncatum seeds, adding sample to supercritical fluid separation column, controlling pressure in extraction kettle, and maintaining carbon dioxide flow rate.
AU ZHOU B
AE SHENZHEN JINFENG BIOMEDICAL TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A4806V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting Acer truncatum oil, involves (a) obtaining samples, drying, crushing and sieving the A.truncatum seeds, (b) adding the sample to a supercritical fluid separation column for extraction, controlling the pressure in the extraction kettle to be 23-30 MPa, controlling the separation pressure in the separation kettle to be 5-8 MPa, and maintaining the carbon dioxide flow rate at 20-30 kg/hour at 30-35 degrees C, and (c) obtaining the yellow transparent A.truncatum seed oil from the separation column.
   USE - The method is used for extracting Acer truncatum oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation, and has high extraction rate and high oil quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extraction temperature is increased step by step in the process of step (b), where the method comprises controlling appropriate carrier gas flow rate and split ratio, and extracting A.truncatum seeds. The step (a) comprises: drying A.truncatum seeds in an electric heating constant temperature blast drying oven at 40 degrees C, and the moisture content is less than 5%; and using a high-speed crusher to crush the A.truncatum seeds, and passing through a 40-mesh sieve for use. Preferred Conditions: The gas chromatography condition of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extract involves performing reaction at a programmed temperature of 30-35 degrees C and initial temperature of 30 degrees C, and holding for 3 minutes, increasing to 35 degrees C at 0.5 degrees C/minute, maintaining for 3 minutes, and solvent delaying for 3 minutes. The carrier gas is high purity helium, the carrier gas flow rate is 1 ml/minute, the injection volume is 1 mu l, and the split ratio is 5:1.

PT P
PN CN111701423-A
TI Recycling and reusing tail gas of ozone oxidation reaction by sequentially subjecting ozone oxidation reaction tail gas to alkali washing, carbon dioxide removing, deep cooling dewatering, and activated carbon purifying to remove ozone.
AU LI L
   QIU Y
   XU D
   LIU Z
   WU H
   ZHOU Z
   CHEN L
   LIN Z
AE SHANDONG HOLLY PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202096903V
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling and reusing tail gas of ozone oxidation reaction comprises sequentially subjecting ozone oxidation reaction tail gas to alkali washing, carbon dioxide removing, deep cooling dewatering, activated carbon purifying to remove ozone, and concentrated sulfuric acid drying, and adding the obtained gas into ozone generator to prepare ozone.
   USE - The method is for recycling and reusing tail gas of ozone oxidation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves oxygen purity of more than 99%, satisfies the requirements of preparing ozone raw material gas, recycles the useful resources in the tail gas, realizes the green cycle economy, saves energy consumption, greatly reduces the ozonization reaction cost, has simple recycling process, is safe and reliable, and greatly reduces the three-waste discharge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ozonation reaction tail gas is generated in the quinoline ozonization-decarboxylation synthesis of nicotinic acid. The washing liquid for alkali washing is 5-30 mass% sodium hydroxide solution. The alkali washing is spraying using liquid ring pump. The deep-cooling dewatering uses heat exchanger to remove water, where the temperature of cooling medium is - 5 to 0 degrees C, and the water content of the gas after removing water is less than or equal to 0.3%. The concentration of ozone in the gas is less than 0.15 ppm after the activated carbon is purified and deodorized. The concentrated sulfuric acid is at 98 mass%, and the volume content of the water in the gas after concentrated sulfuric acid drying is less than or equal to 0.01%. The concentrated sulfuric acid drying is spraying using liquid ring pump, which has exhaust pressure of 0.05-0.2 MPa. After the concentrated sulfuric acid is dried, the method further comprises removing the micro-scale sulfuric acid mist in the gas by a demister.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN111592022-A
TI Producing pseudoboehmite involves using secondary washing liquid in pseudoboehmite washing process to prepare sodium aluminate solution, and pouring carbon dioxide into sodium aluminate solution to obtain carbonized pseudoboehmite slurry.
AU ZHANG H
   MA X
   LI W
   ZOU Y
   GUO C
   XIA Q
AE CHALCO SHANDONG ADVANCED MATERIAL CO LTD (CNAL-C)
GA 202085781P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing pseudoboehmite involves using the secondary washing liquid in the pseudoboehmite washing process to prepare the sodium aluminate solution meeting the preset index, and pouring carbon dioxide into the sodium aluminate solution to obtain carbonized pseudoboehmite slurry. The rapid aging of the carbonized pseudoboehmite slurry is carried out to obtain aged pseudoboehmite slurry. The aged pseudoboehmite slurry is washed and dried to obtain a finished product of pseudoboehmite whose peptization index meets the preset range. The secondary washing liquid is recovered in the washing process to be applied to the preparation of the next batch of sodium aluminate solution.
   USE - Method for producing pseudoboehmite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the recycling and comprehensive utilization of the secondary washing liquid in the pseudo-boehmite production process.

PN PH2201950269-U
TI Collapsible incinerator, has upper cylindrical case element which is wider than lower cylindrical case element, and that comprises multiple filter stages for treating carbon dioxide into cleaned gases before exhausted to atmosphere.
AU PARRENAS R A
AE PARRENAS R A (PARR-Individual)
GA 2020500886

PT P
PN CN110465671-A; CN110465671-B
TI Preparation of flake silver powder involves mixing solvent, silver powder and coating agent, introducing carbon dioxide for coating treatment, separating product, ball milling, sintering, washing, adding to dispersant, stirring and drying.
AU LI Y
   SHI W
AE HUNAN NUOERDE MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019990671
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of flake silver powder involves adding solvent to silver powder, mixing, adding coating agent, introducing carbon dioxide into mixture for coating treatment, subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain solid product, subjecting the solid product to ball milling, sintering and washing the product, adding to dispersant, stirring and drying. The solvent is an alcohol solution containing halogen bismuth compound.
   USE - Preparation of flake silver powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts dispersing and coating silver powder and then ball milling which effectively prevents the agglomeration of silver powder particles during ball milling process to produce flake silver powder having smaller particle size with high tap density.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of solvent and silver powder is 120-150:1. The mass ratio of silver powder raw material and coating agent is 1:30-50. The concentration of halogen bismuth compound in solvent is 200-400 ppm. The ball-to-mass ratio of grinding ball and silver powder is (20-30):1. Preferred Components: The coating agent comprises at least one of sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide. The halogen bismuth compound comprises one of bismuth iodide, bismuth chloride and bismuth bromide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solution is one of methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol and propylene glycol. The dispersant is at least one of n-octanoic acid, triethanolamine, oleic acid, stearic acid and hexadecanoic acid.

PT P
PN CN209637686-U
TI Drilling tool for multi-year frozen earth area frozen earth tunnel, has drill rod whose top part is provided with water swivel that is connected with liquid carbon dioxide storage tank through infusion hose, where rod is provided with drill head.
AU HAN C
   SHI Y
   CAO X
   YANG S
   XU P
AE CCCC FIRST HIGHWAY ENG CO LTD (CCCC-C)
GA 2019976924
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a drilling tool suitable for multi-year frozen earth area frozen earth tunnel, the drilling tool comprises a drill rod, a drill rod top part is provided with a water swivel, swivel through the infusion hose into the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, bottom of the drill rod is a drill head; the water swivel, a drill stem, a drill bit is provided with through holes connected up and down. The utility model can near the drill bit cool the frozen, reduce melting of the frozen, so as to improve the drilling efficiency.
DC Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC Q49-A01; Q49-A10
IP E21B-007/00; E21B-010/60
PD CN209637686-U   15 Nov 2019   E21B-007/00   201990   Pages: 7   Chinese
AD CN209637686-U    CN20071940    16 Jan 2019

ER

PT P
PN US2019345089-A1; US10941101-B2
TI Producing an alpha, beta unsaturated carboxylic acid or a salt involves contacting in any order a group 8-11 transition metal precursor compound comprising at least one first ligand, at least one second ligand, an olefin and carbon dioxide.
AU IACONO P
   HLAVINKA M L
AE CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
GA 2019953041
AB    NOVELTY - Producing an alpha, beta unsaturated carboxylic acid or a salt involves contacting in any order (i) a group 8-11 transition metal precursor compound comprising at least one first ligand, (ii) at least one second ligand; (iii) an olefin; (iv) carbon dioxide (CO2); (v) a diluent; and (vi) an anionic polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations to provide a reaction mixture; and applying conditions to the reaction mixture suitable to produce the alpha, beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid or a salt.
   USE - Method for producing an alpha, beta unsaturated carboxylic acid or a salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anionic polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations comprises a metallated phenol-formaldehyde resin, a metallated polyhydroxyarene-formaldehyde resin, or a metallated polyhydroxyarene- and fluorophenol-formaldehyde resin. The anionic polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations comprises a sodium phenol-formaldehyde resin, a potassium phenol-formaldehyde resin, a sodium resorcinol- and 2-fluorophenol-formaldehyde resin, or a potassium resorcinol- and 2-fluorophenol-formaldehyde resin. The anionic polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations is insoluble in the diluent or the reaction mixture. The first ligand, the second ligand, or both the first ligand and the second ligand comprise(s) a diphosphine ligand, a diamine ligand, a diene ligand, a diether ligand, or dithioether ligand. The first ligand, the second ligand, or both the first ligand and the second ligand comprise(s) an ether ligand, an organic carbonyl ligand, a thioether ligand, an amine ligand, a nitrile ligand, a phosphine ligand, a diene ligand, or a carbene ligand. The first ligand is a diene ligand and the second ligand is a diphosphine ligand. The first ligand is 1,5-cyclooctadiene or TMEDA, and the second ligand is 1,2-bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)ethane or TMEDA. The first ligand or the second ligand is selected from trimethylphosphine, triethylphosphine, tripropylphosphine, tributylphosphine, phenylphosphine, tolylphosphine, diphenylphosphine, ditolylphosphine, triphenylphosphine, tritolylphosphine, methyldiphenylphosphine, dimethylphenylphosphine, ethyldiphenylphosphine, diethylphenylphosphine, tricyclohexylphosphine, trimethyl phosphite, triethyl phosphite, tripropyl phosphite, triisopropyl phosphite, tributyl phosphite and tricyclohexyl phosphite, 2-(di-t-butylphosphino)biphenyl, 2-di-t-butylphosphino-1,1 -binaphthyl, 2-(di-t-butylphosphino)-3,6-dimethoxy-2 ,4 ,6 -tri-i-propyl-1,1 -biphenyl, 2-di-t-butylphosphino-2 -methylbiphenyl, 2-(di-t-butylphosphinomethyl)pyridine, 2-di-t-butylphosphino-2 ,4 ,6 -tri-i-propyl-1,1 -biphenyl, 2-(dicyclohexylphosphino)biphenyl, (S)-(+)-(3,5-dioxa-4-phospha-cyclohepta(2,1-a;3,4-a )dinaphthalen-4-yl)dimethylamine, 2-(diphenylphosphino)-2 -methoxy-1,1 -binaphthyl, 1,2,3,4,5-pentaphenyl-1 -(di-t-butylphosphino)ferrocene, 2,2 -bis(diphenylphosphino)-1,1 -binaphthyl (BINAP), 1,2-bis(dimethylphosphino)ethane, 1,2-bis(diethylpho sphino)ethane, 1,2-bis(dipropylphosphino)-ethane, 1,2-bis(diisopropylpho sphino)ethane, 1,2-bis(dibutyl-phosphino)ethane, 1,2-bis(di-t-butyl-phosphino)ethane, 1,2-bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)ethane, 1,3-bis(dicyclohexylpho sphino)propane, 1,3-bis(diisopropylphosphino)propane, 1,3-bis(diphenylpho sphino)propane, 1,3-bis(di-t-butylphosphino)propane, 1,4-bis(diisopropylpho sphino)butane, 1,4-bis(diphenylphosphino)butane, 2,2 -bis(bis(3,5-dimethylphenyl)phosphino)-4,4 ,6,6 -tetramethoxybiphenyl, 2,6-bis(di-t-butylphosphinomethyl)pyridine, 2,2 -bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)-1,1 -biphenyl, bis(2-dicyclohexylphosphinophenyl)ether, 5,5 -bis(diphenylphosphino)-4,4 -bi-1,3-benzodioxole, 2-t-butylpho sphinomethylpyridine, bis(diphenylphosphino)ferrocene, bis(diphenylphosphino)methane, bis(dicyclohexylphosphino)methane, bis(di-t-butylpho sphino)methane, or tetramethylethylenediamine. The reaction mixture comprises a metalalactone compound, the metalalactone compound comprising a group 8-11 transition metal metalalactone moiety and (a) at least one first ligand or (b) at least one second ligand. The reaction mixture comprises an adduct of a metalalactone compound and the anionic polyaromatic resin with associated metal cations. The associated metal cations are selected from a Group 1, 2, 12, or 13 metal cation. The associated metal cations are lithium, sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, zinc or aluminum cations. The metal-containing base comprises sodium carbonate, caesium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, aluminium isopropoxide or magnesium ethylate. The metal-containing base is selected from an alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal oxide, hydroxide, alkoxide, aryloxide, amide, alkyl amide, arylamide, or carbonate. The olefin comprises ethylene, propylene, butene, pentene, hexene, heptene, octene, or styrene. Preferred Conditions: The molar yield of the alpha, beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid or a salt based on the transition metal precursor compound is at least 100%.

PT P
PN CN110280283-A
TI Catalyst used for electrocatalytic reducing carbon dioxide comprises vulcanized indium-zinc-MXene composite material.
AU ZHAO Y
   WANG J
AE UNIV ZHAOQING EAST CHINA NORMAL PHOTOELE (UYZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019841915
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises vulcanized indium-zinc-MXene composite material.
   USE - The catalyst is used for electrocatalytic reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high electrocatalysis activity and selectivity and improves energy efficiency of carbon utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the catalyst comprising adding milled MAX phase ceramic powder in hydrogen fluoride solution, immersing, heating to 50-90 degrees C, magnetically stirring for 12-24 hours, centrifuging, washing with deionized water, and drying at 60-80 degrees C for 12-24 hours; taking indium nitrate, thiourea, zinc nitrate, and MXene, adding into deionized water, stirring, transferring to reaction kettle, reacting at 150-200 degrees C for 12-24 hours, centrifuging, collecting product, washing with deionized water and ethanol for 3 times, and drying at 60 degrees C for 24 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The MAX phase ceramic powder is titanium aluminum carbides and/or chromium aluminum carbide. The ratio of MAX phase ceramic powder to hydrogen fluoride solution is 1 g:20 mL. The raw materials added in second step comprises 0.5-1 g indium nitrate, 1-2 g thiourea, 1-2 g zinc nitrate, 0.1-0.5 g MXene, and 50-100 mL deionized water.

TI Method for power generation for solid alkaline fuel cell, involves exposing cathode-side surface of inorganic solid electrolyte to atmosphere containing carbon dioxide.
AU TAKEUCHI Y
   KAN H
   NAKAMURA T
   OHMORI M
   OMORI M
   JIAN B
AE NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
   NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
   NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
   NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
GA 201954026G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing the solid alkaline fuel cell (10) having a cathode (12), an anode (14) and an inorganic solid electrolyte (16) arranged between the anode and the cathode and exhibiting hydroxide ion conductivity. The cathode-side surface (16S) of the inorganic solid electrolyte is exposed to an atmosphere containing carbon dioxide of greater than or equal to 600 ppm and less than or equal to 20000 ppm.
   USE - Method for power generation for solid alkaline fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The power generation capacity is enhanced by the solid alkaline fuel cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of the solid alkaline fuel cell. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN109880032-A
TI New functional group-containing nitrogen-rich microporous organic polymer used for e.g. carbon dioxide and volatile organic vapor capture, heterogeneous catalyst matrix materials and heavy metal recovery.
AU WANG Z
   ZHOU X
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019549450
AB    NOVELTY - A functional group-containing nitrogen-rich microporous organic polymer (I), is new.
   USE - Functional group-containing nitrogen-rich microporous organic polymer used for carbon dioxide and volatile organic vapor capture, heterogeneous catalyst matrix materials, heavy metal recovery and removal of organic dye contaminants in water systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The functional group-containing nitrogen-rich microporous organic polymer has large specific surface area, and can be prepared economically without using the catalyst by simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A functional group-containing nitrogen-rich microporous organic polymer having structural formula (I), is new.
   R = halo, hydroxy, carboxy, sulfonic acid group or nitro.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of functional group-containing nitrogen-rich microporous organic polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the melamine and the functional benzaldehyde monomer to the organic solvent (a) is 1 g/100 ml to 30 g/100 ml. Preferred Components: The solvent (a) is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide and/or diphenyl sulfone. The organic solvent (b) is fatty alcohol, fatty ketone, alkane, halogenated hydrocarbon, tetrahydrofuran, dioxane, N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide and/or N,N-dimethylacetamide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): A functionalized benzaldehyde monomer of formula (B) and melamine of formula (A) are dissolved in an organic solvent (a) in ratio of 1:3-3:2, and heated at 150-250 degrees C for 1-6 days. The temperature is lowered to room temperature, the obtained solid is extracted with the organic solvent (b) for 2-48 hours and dried at 30-150 degrees C to produce polymer (I).

PT P
PN CN109769912-A
TI Freezing seafood by screening and grading seafoods, and carrying out chlorine dioxide treatment by mixing chlorine dioxide and citric acid, diluting mixture with water, soaking seafood and draining under carbon dioxide gas atmosphere.
AU TANG W
AE SICHUAN HAISHENGJIE CRYOGENIC TECHNOLOGY (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201947082T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for freezing seafood using a freezing device involves (a) screening and grading seafood products that satisfy the standards, and (b) carrying out chlorine dioxide treatment by mixing 2% concentration of chlorine dioxide and citric acid in a volume ratio of 50 ml:1 g by volume to make 20000 parts per million (ppm) chlorine dioxide solution, diluting the mixture with water to become 25-30 ppm chlorine dioxide solution, soaking the seafood for 20-30 minutes, taking out and draining, where the seafood is protected with carbon dioxide gas during soaking and draining.
   USE - The method is useful for freezing seafood.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of seafood demand.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (b) further involves diluting 2% concentration chlorine dioxide into a 1% aqueous solution and freezing into ice particles (I), freezing 1% concentration aqueous citric acid solution into ice particles (II) and combining the ice particles (I) and (II) in a weight ratio of 1:1 after mixing with seafood in a ratio of 1:1. The method further involves weighing the frozen seafood, packaging, sealing and storing or transporting in a fresh-keeping or refrigerating truck at -1 to 0 degrees C, where carbon dioxide shielding gas is filled during packaging process.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

TI Foamed silicone rubber blowing agent comprises organic blowing agent, inorganic blowing agent, protein blowing agent, foaming aid, and film-forming substance.
AU YIN Z
AE ANHUI XUCHUAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019350005
AB    NOVELTY - Foamed silicone rubber blowing agent comprises 5-15 pts. wt. organic blowing agent, 10-25 pts. wt. inorganic blowing agent, 8-20 pts. wt. protein blowing agent, 3-9 pts. wt. foaming aid, and 8-18 pts. wt. film-forming substance.
   USE - Used as foamed silicone rubber blowing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The foamed silicone rubber blowing agent has foaming stability and excellent controllability and matching properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing foamed silicone rubber blowing agent comprising ball-milling organic foaming agent, inorganic foaming agent and protein foaming agent and uniformly mixing, and adding film-forming substance and mixing uniformly, placing mixture in a reaction kettle for pre-reaction of supercritical carbon-dioxide, adding co-blowing agent to carry out supercritical carbon-dioxide reaction to obtain blowing agent granule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Foamed silicone rubber blowing agent comprises 12 pts. wt. organic blowing agent, 18.5 pts. wt. inorganic blowing agent, 15.5 pts. wt. protein blowing agent, 6 pts. wt. foaming aid, and 15 pts. wt. film-forming substance. Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon-dioxide reaction is carried out under the conditions of temperature of 30-45 degrees C and pressure of 15-25 MPa. The pre-reaction conditions of supercritical carbon-dioxide temperature is 20-35 degrees C and pressure is 8-20 MPa. Preferred Components: The film-forming material is sodium alginate. The foaming aid is urea, urea fat, glycerin, zinc oxide, stearic acid or zinc stearate. The protein blowing agent is composed of animal protein blowing agent and vegetable protein blowing agent in a mass ratio of 1:0.2-0.8. The organic blowing agent comprises 8-18 pts. wt. blowing agent azobisisobutyronitrile, 5-10 pts. wt. blowing agent OBSH (4,4'-oxydibenzenesulfonyl hydrazide), 4-8 pts. wt. blowing agent H (N,N'-dinitrosopentamethylene tetramine), and 3-8 pts. wt. blowing agent azodicarbonamide. The inorganic blowing agent has particle diameter of 200-300 mesh and is composed of sodium bicarbonate and ammonium bicarbonate in a mass ratio of 1:0.3-2.5.

TI New porous coordination polymer used in agent for reducing carbon dioxide and fossil fuel, comprises hydride donating group.
AU KAJIWARA T
   HIGUCHI M
   TANAKA K
   KITAGAWA S
AE UNIV KYOTO (KYOU-C)
GA 201926273M
AB    NOVELTY - A new porous coordination polymer comprises a hydride donating group (I).
   USE - New porous coordination polymer used in agent (all claimed) for reducing carbon dioxide and fossil fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous coordination polymer has excellent stability and hydride donation capability (reduction ability).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A new porous coordination polymer comprises a hydride donating group of formula (I).R = H, CH3, CONH2, COOH or COCH3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) organic linker of formula (II);
   (2) organic linker precursor (III); and
   (3) oxidized-type porous coordination polymer.
   R1-R9 = H, CO2-, CS2-, PO32-, PO42-, AsO32-, AsO42-, SO3-, SO4-, SeO3-, SeO4-, TeO3- or TeO4-, in which at least one of R1-R4 and at least one of R5-R9 is CO2-, CS2-, PO32-, PO42-, AsO32-, AsO42-, SO3-, SO4-, SeO3-, SeO4-, TeO3- or TeO4-;and
   m = 0-3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation: No general preparation is given. Preferred Composition: The porous coordination polymer includes an organic linker (II) integrated in skeleton of the polymer. The porous coordination polymer is preferably of formula: (Zr6O4(OH)4(bpdc)6) or (ZrO(bpdc)).
   EXAMPLE - 5 mL Dimethylformamide and 254 mg biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylato-zirconium complex (Zr6O4(OH)4(bpdc)6), and 444 mu ol biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate were mixed, 15 ml lithium-2-((3-carbamoyl-1,4-dihydropyridin-1-yl)methyl)biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate aqueous solution was added, deaerated, left still for three days at room temperature, the mixture was centrifuged, the supernatant liquid was removed, mixture of dimethylformamide and water was added, centrifuged, 11 ml acetonitrile was added, centrifuged, and vacuum-dried to obtain biphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylato-zirconium complex-based porous coordination polymer.

PN CN109364933-A
TI Copper-bismuth/vanadium composite photocatalyst useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, obtained by coating copper nano-sheet on the surface of bismuth/vanadium composite nano-particle having mesh structure.
AU CHEN L
   FENG X
   DUAN Z
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201920520C
AB    NOVELTY - Copper-bismuth/vanadium composite photocatalyst, obtained by coating copper nano-sheet on the surface of bismuth/vanadium composite nano-particle having mesh structure.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the photocatalyst, comprising adding vanadium source and bismuth source-containing glycol solution, mixing, performing solvent thermal reaction at 120-180 degrees C degrees to obtain a network nanostructure composed of elemental bismuth and amorphous bismuth vanadate, dispersing bismuth/vanadium composite nano-particle in aqueous solution containing a copper source and reducing copper source using elemental bismuth under hydrothermal conditions at 120-160 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of bismuth/vanadium composite nano-particle is 30-100 nm and the copper nano-sheet diameter is 30-100 nm. The mass percentage content of the copper nano-sheet in the copper-bismuth/vanadium composite photocatalyst is adjusted to 5-50%. The molar ratio of vanadium source-containing ethylene glycol solution and bismuth-containing ethylene glycol solution is 1:1. The molar concentration of the bismuth /vanadium dispersant is 0.02-0.08 mol/l. The molar concentration of the copper source is 0.1-8 mmol/l. The bismuth source is bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate. The vanadium source is ammonium metavanadate. The copper source is copper(II) acetate monohydrate. The time of solvent thermal reaction is 8-12 hours. The hydro-thermal reaction time is 2-10 hours. The copper-bismuth/vanadium composite photocatalyst catalyzes the reduction of carbon dioxide under visible light conditions.

AU WANG M
   ZHANG L
   SHI J
AE SHANGHAI INST CERAMICS CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGU-C)
GA 2018928852
AB    NOVELTY - A manganese dioxide-loaded graphitic                carbonitride nanosheet material comprises graphitic                carbonitride nanosheets, and 2-23 wt.%, preferably                2-20 wt.% manganese dioxide dispersed in the                nanosheets.
   USE - Manganese dioxide-loaded graphitic carbon                nitride (g-C3N4) nanosheet material for carbon                dioxide photoreduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The light absorption capacity is enhanced,                thus forming a strong heterojunction structure. The                photocatalytic efficiency is greatly enhanced,                which facilitates the catalytic oxidation                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the manganese dioxide-loaded                graphitic carbonitride nanosheet material, which                involves adding a divalent manganese salt to an                aqueous solution containing graphitic carbonitride                (g-C3N4) nanosheets to obtain a mixed solution,                adding potassium permanganate to the obtained mixed                solution, reacting at 30-60 degrees C for 12-78                hours, and washing centrifugally with water and                drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the                  g-C3N4 nanosheet to the divalent manganese salt                  is (2-85):1, preferably (8-85):1. Preferred                  Components: The manganese dioxide is a ( delta                  )-manganese dioxide nanosheet. The                  nitrogen-containing organic molecule is selected                  from at least one of monocyanamide,                  dicyandiamide, melamine, and urea. The divalent                  manganese salt is at least one selected from                  manganese sulfate, manganese nitrate, manganese                  chloride, and manganese oxalate. Preferred                  Properties: The manganese dioxide-loaded g-C3N4                  nanosheet material has mesoporous pores with a                  pore diameter of 18-27 nm and a specific surface                  area of 40-110 m2/g. The nitrogen-containing                  organic molecules are heated to 450-600 degrees C                  at a rate of 5-20 degrees C /minute, and held for                  2-6 hours to obtain g-C3N4 nanosheets.

PN JP2018167493-A; JP6948137-B2
TI Manufacture of flame-retardant wooden material for e.g. fence, involves dissolving sodium aluminate and borate in water, injecting resultant into wooden material, contacting with carbon dioxide/acid and generating aluminum hydroxide.
AU NAKAI D
AE KANEMATSU SUSTECH CORP (KANE-Non-standard)
GA 201885606S
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of flame-retardant wooden material involves dissolving sodium aluminate and a dissolution stabilizer in water, obtaining sodium aluminate aqueous solution, injecting resultant solution into a wooden material, contacting resultant wooden material with carbon dioxide or an acid and generating aluminum hydroxide in the wooden material. The dissolution stabilizer is borate.
   USE - Manufacture of flame-retardant wooden material. Uses include but are not limited to cladding material and interior material for residence, cabinet, wood deck and wooden fence.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and simple manufacture of wooden material having excellent external appearance and surface crack resistance, and suppressed deterioration in flame retardance, with reduced generation of waste material and suppressed precipitation of the aqueous solution.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacture of flame-retardant wooden material further involves processing the wooden material with a component (c1) chosen from preservative, ant-repellent and anti-mold agent by coating, spraying, immersion coating or injecting, before injecting the sodium aluminate aqueous solution. The flow speed of carbon dioxide is 15-28 l/hour. The first stage of separation is carried out at 35-45 degrees C under pressure of 8-12 MPa and the second stage separation is carried out at 26-37 degrees C under pressure of 5-8 MPa. The total sesquiterpene extract from ragweed comprises 55-80 wt.% sesquiterpene.

PN CN108077549-A
TI Preparing apple seed protein comprises e.g. carrying supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to obtain apple seed residue after apple seed oil, grinding, extracting with alkaline solution, and slowly stirring in extraction process.
AU AO Y
AE AO Y (AOYY-Individual)
GA 201843599A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing apple seed protein comprises carrying supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction to obtain apple seed residue after apple seed oil, grinding, passing through 150 meshes, extracting with alkaline solution for 2 times, slowly stirring in the extraction process, centrifuging, carrying acid precipitation, re-centrifuging, adding water to the acid precipitate obtained by centrifugation in the ratio of 1:2 g/ml, adjusting the pH to 7, spray drying under constant stirring to obtain apple seed protein.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing apple seed protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can effectively extract the apple seed protein; and has apple seed protein yield produced rate of 50.41%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction on raw material to obtain apple seed residue after apple seed oil. In the method, the optimal process conditions for the alkali extraction includes: pH is 11, temperature is 50 degrees C, time is 40 minutes, solid-liquid ratio is 1:40 g/ml, extracted twice, and slowly stirred during the extraction process. In the method, the optimal acid precipitation condition includes slowly adding saturated citric acid solution while constantly stirring, adjusting the pH to 4, and allowing to stand at 4 degrees C for 60 minutes. In the method, the optimum spray drying conditions includes: feed pH adjusted to 7, preheated to 60 degrees C, inlet air temperature is 180 degrees C, and outlet air temperature is 70 degrees C. In the method, two centrifugal separations are performed at speed of 6000 revolutions/minute, and each centrifugation time is 15 minutes.

The ice whose carbon dioxide content rate is greater than or equal to 3% by weight is compressed carbon dioxide hydrate. Preferred Conditions: The ice whose carbon dioxide content rate of greater than or equal to 3% by weight has a maximum length of greater than or equal to 3 milli-meter. The body-cooling agent containing the ice which can be made to generate an ultra-fine bubble in water so that the density or concentration of an ultra-fine bubble when the ice whose carbon dioxide contentrate is greater than or equal to 3% by weight measures by the following measurement method R1 may be greater than or equal to 5 million pieces/mL. (Measurement method R1) In 25 degrees C water, is (-)80 degrees C to 0 degrees C and adds 300 mg/mL of ice which has the carbon dioxide content rate of greater than or equal to 3% by weight, set it as the 0-2 degrees C ice water containing the ice which has the carbon dioxide content rate of greater than or equal to 3% by weight, then, the density or concentration (piece/mL) of the ultra-fine bubble in the water is measured by Malvern nano-site NS300. Preferred Process: The manufacturing method of the cooling fluid for bodies for applying to skin involves the process which makes the body-cooling agent to contact with a liquid.

AE UNIV NORTH CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNNS-C)
GA 201920701E
AB    NOVELTY - An argon oxygen decarburization steel slag carbon sequestration method comprises crushing argon oxygen decarburization steel slag, sieving, drying, cooling, adding deionized water according to dosage of (8-10) L/Kg, introducing carbon dioxide under 7-9 bar, heating at 120-140 degrees C, discharging gas, cooling, filtering, taking filter residues, and drying.

PN CN108525676-A
TI Preparation of composite oxide catalyst for carbon dioxide methanation, involves mixing aqueous solution of nickel salt, zirconium salt, cerium salt and ruthenium salt, with ammonium carbonate, performing metathesis reaction, and calcining.
AU NI W
   WANG X
   CHENG Y
   ZOU X
   SHANG X
AE NANTONG LONGXIANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2018742581
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ruthenium-nickel/cerium-zirconium composite oxide catalyst involves mixing mixed aqueous solution comprising nickel salt, zirconium salt, cerium salt and ruthenium salt, with ammonium carbonate solution, performing metathesis reaction to obtain a precursor, and calcining the precursor.
   USE - Preparation of composite oxide catalyst (claimed) for carbon dioxide methanation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces catalyst which enables methanation reaction with carbon dioxide conversion rate of 98%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of ruthenium in the ruthenium salt, to nickel in the nickel salt, is 1-6:10-40. The mass ratio of the nickel in nickel salt, to sum of mass of zirconium in zirconium salt and cerium in cerium salt, is 0.3-0.5. The mass of cerium salt is 0.3-0.95 of the sum of the mass of cerium salt and zirconium salt. The ratio of sum of mass of nickel salt, zirconium salt, cerium salt and ruthenium salt, to mass of ammonium carbonate is 1-6:4-24. Preferred Components: The composite oxide catalyst comprises cerium-zirconium composite oxide support, and ruthenium oxide and nickel oxide which are dispersed on the surface of the composite oxide support. Preferred Properties: The nickel oxide has particle diameter of 9-10 nm. The composite oxide catalyst has specific surface area of 50-100 m2/g, pore volume of 0.05-0.1 cm3/g, and pore diameter of 5-9 nm. Preferred Conditions: The metathesis reaction is carried out at 70-90 degrees C for 3-5 hours. The calcination of the precursor is carried out at 400-600 degrees C for 3-8 hours.

PN CN114957355-A
TI Preparing natural capsaicin glycosides with            capsicum fruit as raw material comprises e.g.            dissolving product A in dichloromethane-methanol,            applying it to chromatography column, eluting with            dichloromethane-methanol, collecting capsaicin            glycoside eluate, and drying to obtain product.
AU XIA K
   WEN H
AE CHENGDU HERBSOURCE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B7138M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing natural capsaicin glycosides with                capsicum fruit as raw material comprises (1) drying                and crushing the raw materials to obtain pepper                granules, (2) loading the pepper granules into the                extractor, then heating the extractor and the                separation kettle, injecting the carbon dioxide                fluid containing 0.1-2% methanol into the                refrigerator extractor, and controlling the                temperature of the extractor at 30-60&#8451;,                where the pressure of the extractor at 15-30 Mpa,                the temperature of the separation kettle at                10-20&#8451;, and the separating still pressure                is at 5-10 Mpa, keeping above-mentioned temperature                and pressure for 30-60 minutes, and obtaining                product A, (3) dissolving product A in                dichloromethane-methanol, applying it to a                chromatography column, eluting with                dichloromethane-methanol, collecting capsaicin                glycoside eluate, and drying to obtain product B,                and (4) dissolving product B in a solvent, and                recrystallizing to obtain a solid product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing natural                capsaicin glycosides with capsicum fruit as raw                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the raw                  materials are dried at 60-100&#176;C, and                  crushing to obtain pepper particles with a                  particle size of 0.4-0.6 mm. In the step (4), the                  conditions of recrystallization are: temperature:                  -5 to 15&#8451;, placing for 3-30 hours for                  recrystallization, and the crystals are white or                  off-white after recrystallization.Preferred Components: In the step (3), the                  dosage ratio of product A and                  dichloromethane-methanol is 1-10:1, the ratio of                  dichloromethane and methanol in                  dichloromethane-methanol is 3-1:1, and the                  temperature during dissolving is not higher than                  60&#176;C. In the step (3), the column                  chromatography is alumina column chromatography                  and/or silica gel chromatography. In the step                  (3), the ratio of dichloromethane to methanol in                  dichloromethane-methanol is 4-1:1-10, the                  concentration temperature is not higher than                  60&#8451;, the concentration pressure is not                  higher than -0.05 Mpa, the concentration time is                  not higher than 60 minutes, the product B is                  obtained as a light yellow to light brown solid                  powder. In the step (4), the solvent adopts                  water-ethanol solution, the ratio of water to                  ethanol in the water-ethanol solution is 1:3-8,                  the ratio of solvent to product B is 1:10, and                  the dissolving temperature is not higher than                  50&#176;C.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing a copper-silver                sheet-like nanocomposite material involves                dispersing the template and the copper source in a                solvent to disperse them uniformly to obtain a                first dispersion, mixing the reducing agent and the                first dispersion and heating successively to obtain                copper nanomaterials, separating and washing the                prepared copper nanomaterials, dispersing in a                solvent, and adding a certain amount of template                agent to obtain a second dispersion, dispersing the                silver source in a solvent to form a silver source                dispersion liquid, and adding the silver source                dispersion liquid into the second dispersion liquid                and continuously stirred to obtain a copper-silver                flake nanocomposite material.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly defective boron nitride                supported atomically dispersed metal catalyst                comprises mixing and grinding urea, boric acid and                carbon nanotubes, reacting at 800-1100degreesC in a                mixed atmosphere of ammonia and argon, mixing the                obtained powder with boric acid solution and                heating, then washing using water to obtain a                defect-rich boron nitride composite material,                mixing the defect-rich boron nitride composite                material with metal salt solution, ultrasonically                treating, freeze-drying, and heat treating at                650-850degreesC in a mixed atmosphere of ammonia                and argon to obtain an highly defective boron                nitride supported atomically dispersed metal                catalyst.
   USE - The highly defective boron nitride supported                atomically dispersed metal catalyst is used in                electrocatalytic water splitting reaction, oxygen                reduction reaction (ORR), carbon dioxide reduction                reaction (CO2RR), organic catalytic reaction or                zinc-air battery.
