PT P
PN IN202241021829-A
TI Recovering calcium as precipitated calcium            carbonate and iron as iron oxide from calcium            containing feedstock including waste generates in steel            making process comprises e.g. extracting calcium from            feedstock, neutralizing and carbonizing.
AU NANDEESWARAN N K
   JAYAPAL D
   SAMBANDAM M
AE JSW STEEL LTD (JSWS-Non-standard)
GA 2022792984
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering calcium as precipitated calcium                carbonate (PCC) and iron as iron oxide from calcium                containing feedstock including waste which                generated in steel making processes comprises:                extracting the calcium from the calcium containing                feedstock following treating the same with 0.1-1.5                M hydrochloride (HCL) solution to recover dissolved                calcium in an acidic calcium containing solution by                filtration; neutralizing the thus obtained acidic                calcium containing solution with a base to generate                a basic calcium containing solution to precipitate                out the iron content which is converted to iron                oxide by roasting; and carbonizing the basic                calcium containing solution with carbon dioxide                which reacts to form bicarbonate which in turn                reacts with some of dissolved calcium to form                calcium carbonate precipitate and finally                recovering therefrom the calcium carbonate                precipitate by filtration.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering calcium as                PCC and iron as iron oxide from calcium containing                feedstock including waste generates in steel making                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces calcium products which is                eco-friendly, techno-economically attractive and                takes care of the waste generated in the process of                steel making process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the process: the                  calcium containing feedstock is waste comprising                  steel converter slag preferably energy optimizing                  furnace (EOF) slag, ladle furnace (LF) slag,                  electric arc furnace (EAF) slag, the HCL                  comprises industrial waste HCL and the base may,                  preferably alkali, or hydroxide base, and the                  base is ammonium hydroxide; the calcium                  containing feedstock include steel converter slag                  preferably EOF slag of size of less than 1 mm;                  the EOF slag included has the composition range                  comprising 20-35 wt.% silicon dioxide, 35-50 wt.%                  calcium oxide, 5-10 wt.% magnesium oxide, 2-10                  wt.% manganese(II) oxide, 2-8 wt.% aluminum oxide                  and 25-40 wt.% ferrous oxide; the liquid to solid                  ratio maintained in the dissolution step is 6-7                  ml/g of steel slag; the product PCC is obtained                  in size of less than 2 mu m which contributes                  90%; and the product iron oxide recovery                  percentage is 4-8%. Preferred Process: In the                  process: the base used in neutralization step is                  ammonium hydroxide which is provided to increase                  the pH of 4-10 for removing the impurities iron                  oxide; the carbon dioxide used with the flow rate                  of 0.3-4 l/minute in the carbonation process                  which is obtained from flue gas generated in iron                  and steel making process; and the precipitated                  iron is subjected to roasting at 400-500 degrees                  C to obtain iron oxide.
   EXAMPLE - The different solution was stirred by a                magnetic stirrer (650 plus minus 50 rpm) in 1000 ml                glass reactor. The glass reactor was heated by                using a hot plate along with stirrer. 150 g Steel                converter slag was added to the reactor containing                1.5 M hydrochloride at 30 degrees C, 40 degrees C,                50 degrees C were tried. To observe the progression                of the dissolution, the residue samples were taken                from the solution 5, 10, 20, 30, 45,60, 90, and 120                minutes after the slag was added. To analyze the                composition of the residual (i.e., undissolved)                slag, the residue was dried in the oven at ~120                degrees C and sent to X-ray fluorescence (XRF). The                solution samples were filtered and analyzed for                calcium and iron by titration method. The maximum                dissolution of calcium from the steel slag was                observed in 1.5 M hydrochloride solution, nearly                calcium oxide % in slag reduced to 26.69% from                38.1% and the rate of dissolution achieved is                0.026. The filtration solution was analyzed to find                out the amount of calcium and iron dissolved in the                solution. The filtration carried out by 50 ml                filtrate solution, indicator-patent rador in                solution, 10 ml sodium hydroxide solution was added                to adjust pH to 7, 10 ml 8.5 Ml ammonia-ammonium                chloride was added, titrate solution with the EDTA                solution until the color changes from wine red,                through purple, to a pure rich blue color. At the                end point, the last traces of purple in the                solution will have just disappeared. By the use of                end point value, and the dissolved calcium was                calculated.

PN CN114479394-A
TI Preparing full biodegradable suction pipe,            comprises mixing raw materials, adding double-screw            extruder to charging barrel, mixing and extruding and            granulating, cooling, using pipe drawing machine to            pull pipe, and heat treating.
AU WANG Q
   XIANG H
   ZHONG C
AE SUZHOU ZDAIR MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202270814N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a biodegradable suction pipe                involves mixing 3-8 pts. wt. carbon dioxide based                polymer, 52-65 pts. wt. modified polylactic acid,                30-40 pts. wt. polybutylene succinate, 0.3-0.5 pts.                wt. ethylene disstearate amide, 0-0.3 pts. wt.                compatibilizer 4468, and 0.05 -0.3 pts. wt. stearic                acid amide, adding to the charging barrel at                160-180degrees Celsius, maintaining mold head                temperature at 170 degrees Celsius of double-screw                extruder, mixing and extruding and granulating,                cooling to 18-25 degrees Celsius, using a pipe                drawing machine to pull the pipe, then heat                treating for 5 minutes at 105 degrees Celsius to                obtain the suction pipe.
   USE - Preparation method of full biodegradable                sucker used in disposable tableware plastic                product. Uses include but are not limited to                coffee, milk tea drink and take-out.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the full biodegradation                suction pipe with high phase continuity, high heat                stability, high temperature resistance and moisture                resistance, high crystallization performance and                high thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                full biodegradation suction pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  based polymer is carbon dioxide-based                  polyurethane and carbon dioxide based PPC in a                  weight ratio of 1:9. In the formic acid solution                  of polybutyrolactam, the molar ratio of                  polybutyrolactam and formic acid is 5: 83; in the                  trioxymethylene mixed solution, the molar ratio                  of trioxymethylene and formic acid is (2-3): 70;                  the mass ratio of the hydroxylated                  polybutyrolactam to polylactic acid is 1: 8. In                  zinc sulfate solution, the volume ratio of the                  deionized water, tertiary butanol is 2:1; carbon                  dioxide polyol, isophorone diisocyanate, 1,4                  butanediol, 3-di-amino-1,4-propylene glycol mass                  ratio is 50: 22: (1.8- 6.3): (0.8-6.6). Preferred                  Method: The carbon dioxide-based polyurethane                  preparation method comprises: (1) mixing                  deionized water, zinc sulfate, and tertiary                  butanol at 40degrees Celsius to obtain zinc                  sulfate solution, dropping cobalt potassium                  cyanide aqueous solution into the zinc sulfate                  solution, after ultrasonically stirring,                  centrifuging suspension at 5500                  revolutions/minute (rpm) for 2 minutes, washing                  the separated slurry by deionized aqueous                  solution, then centrifuging the suspension for 2                  minutes at 5500 rpm centrifugal speed,                  continuously washing the separated slurry with                  the mixed solution of deionized water and                  tertiary butanol, and gradually increasing the                  ratio of tertiary butanol to water in the mixed                  solution, until using pure tert-butanol as                  detergent, centrifuging the solid and dry                  zinc-cobalt-based double metal cyanide under                  vacuum at 40degrees Celsius; (2) adding                  zinc-cobalt-base double metal cyanide, epoxy                  pentane and isophthalic acid in a reaction                  kettle, filling carbon dioxide gas, heating for                  75degrees Celsius to react for 7 hours, after                  finishing reaction, cooling reaction kettle to                  20-25degrees Celsius and releasing the gas in the                  kettle, dissolving the product in the deionized                  water, and filtering to obtain the carbon dioxide                  polyol; (3) vacuum dehydrating the carbon dioxide                  polyol in 80degrees Celsius oil bath for 2 hours,                  under the nitrogen atmosphere, adding isophorone                  diisocyanate and 1,4 butanediol, mixing and                  stirring, then adding 3-di-amino-1, 4 propylene                  glycol, dibutyl tin laurate, 2-butanone, mixing                  and stirring, neutralizing with triethylamine for                  30 minutes at 30degrees Celsius, obtaining carbon                  dioxide-based polyurethane by vacuum distillation                  in a water bath at 40degrees Celsius. The                  pressure of the filled carbon dioxide gas is                  2-2.2 MPa. The preparation method of polylactic                  acid comprises: heating polybutyrolactam and                  formic acid to 60degrees Celsius and obtaining                  the formic acid solution of polybutyrolactam,                  mixing trioxymethylene and formic acid to obtain                  trioxymethylene mixed solution, adding into                  formic acid solution of polybutyrolactam, heating                  at 60degrees Celsius for 1 hour, using acetone to                  precipitate, pumping and filtering for 3-5 times                  and then vacuum drying to obtain hydroxylated                  polybutyrolactam; blending and extruding to                  obtain the modified polylactic acid. The                  preparation method of biodegradation suction pipe                  comprises: (S1) dividing carbon dioxide-based                  polymer into 2 parts according to the weight                  ratio of 99:1, and is counted as carbon                  dioxide-based polymer A, and carbon dioxide-based                  polymer B; (S2) melt-blending carbon                  dioxide-based polymer B, modified polylactic acid                  through a twin-screw extruder, extruding,                  granulating and drying to obtain material A;                  melt-blending carbon dioxide-based polymer A,                  polybutylene succinate, ethylene disstearate                  amide, and stearic acid amide, extruding,                  granulating and drying through a twin-screw                  extruder to obtain material B; (S3) granulating                  material A and material B by micro-nano                  co-extrusion, cooling to 18-25degrees Celsius,                  and then drawing by a pipe drawing machine to                  obtain a fully biodegradable suction pipe. The                  temperature from the double-screw extruder                  cylinder area to the extrusion die is 175 degrees                  Celsius, 185 degrees Celsius, 185 degrees                  Celsius, 195 degrees Celsius, 195 degrees Celsius                  and 185 degrees Celsius.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a porous aerogel scaffold                involves (a) adding photoinitiator and polyethylene                glycol diacrylate to buffer, heating to dissolve,                mixing well, adding Pluronic F127 (RTM: Poloxamer                407) to the mixture, and maintaining at low                temperature to obtain aerogel scaffold material,                and (b) printing hydrogel scaffold by three                dimensional (3D) printing technology and                irradiating with UV light to make it cross-linked,                forming three-dimensional scaffold with stable                structure, removing Pluronic F127 (RTM: Poloxamer                407) by low-temperature immersion, and                freeze-drying aerogel support.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The buffer is phosphate                  buffer. The photoinitiator lithium                  phenyl-2,4,6-trimethylbenzoylphosphinate (LAP)                  photoinitiator. The buffer comprises 0.1-0.25                  wt.% LAP photoinitiator and 5-15 wt.%                  polyethylene glycol diacrylate. The aerogel                  scaffold material comprises 15-25 wt.% Pluronic                  F127 (RTM: Poloxamer 407).Preferred Method: The method involves                  heating mixture in water bath at 35-40degreesC,                  preferably 37degreesC for 5-15 minutes,                  preferably 10 minutes with oscillated interval,                  and shaking mixture every 1-3 minutes during the                  heating period of the water bath. The aerogel                  scaffold material is maintained at 0-5degreesC,                  preferably 4 for 2-5 days, preferably 3 days. The                  step (b) involves irradiating with UV light                  having a wavelength of 405 nm and light intensity                  of 20-30 mW/cm2 for 5-15 minutes. The                  three-dimensional scaffold is soaked in                  phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) at low                  temperature i.e. 0-5degreesC, preferably                  4degreesC for 20-30 hours, preferably 24 hours to                  remove Pluronic F127 (RTM: Poloxamer 407). The                  freeze-drying is performed at -60degreesC to                  -50degreesC for 20-30 hours. The method further                  involves sterilizing the prepared aerogel                  scaffold by immersing in ethanol solution.

TI Detection method of hardened and tempered steel            grain size involves measuring crystal grain degree of            surface of sample to be tested after corrosion, and            obtaining grain size of quenched/tempered steel            crystal.
AU HU Y
   YAN J
   ZHEN L
   JIANG G
   DAI G
   LIU S
   DUAN Z
   ZHANG J
   BI M
   GONG Y
   WANG H
AE DONGFANG GUANGZHOU HEAVY MACHINERY CO (DFEC-C)
GA 202210799A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating to-be-tested                sample to below 20-40?? C below a steel seed                critical point AC1 temperature, where the AC1 is a                critical temperature of tissue transformation in                steel heating process. A carbon dioxide layer is                oxidized and diffused on a surface of the sample.                An oxide layer is removed until a surface is                bright. A decarburization layer is polished and                exposed. A corrosive agent is used to corrode the                decarburization layer to obtain a sorbite                interface. A crystal grain degree of the surface of                a sample to be tested is measured after corrosion.                A grain size of quenched and tempered steel crystal                is obtained.
   USE - Detection method of hardened and tempered                steel grain size.
   ADVANTAGE - The detection method clearly and completely                displays grain size of grain boundary and backfire                sorbite, the grain size evaluation result is real                and effective, the accuracy is high, the detection                process is simple, the material consumption is low,                and the safety is high.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating detection method of hardened and                tempered steel grain size.

TI Photo-responsive metal organic framework filling            mixed matrix film used in gas storage, separation and            catalysis applications, comprises block polyether amide            polymer base film and metal organic frameworks filled            in base film.
AU OTHER I R N

   XIN Q
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 202210509C
AB    NOVELTY - Photo-responsive metal organic framework                filling mixed matrix film comprises block polyether                amide polymer base film and metal-organic framework                filled in the base film, where the framework                material (i.e., cerium-zirconium                1,4-dicarboxybenzene-amino (CE-Uio-66-amino)) is a                porous framework material with high specific                surface area and high porosity, where the azo group                enhances the adsorption of sulfur dioxide in the                metal-organic framework material, and                simultaneously makes the metal-organic framework                have photo-responsive properties.
   USE - The photo-responsive metal organic framework                filling mixed matrix film is used for applying to a                membrane contactor for separation and absorption of                gases such as sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide                (all claimed). The photo-responsive metal organic                framework filling mixed matrix film is useful in a                gas storage, separation and catalysis applications,                and a membrane separation application.
   ADVANTAGE - The photo-responsive metal organic framework                filling mixed matrix film has excellent water                stability, acid and alkali resistance, and improved                selectivity, and enhances sulfur dioxide adsorption                effect, and light response characteristic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer in the                  base film is to select the polymer base film                  material based on to the conditions required by                  the application, such as polyethylene oxide,                  polyethersulfone, PTFE, and polyimide. The form                  of the photo-responsive metal-organic                  framework-filled mixed matrix membrane can be a                  flat membrane, a hollow fiber membrane or a                  tubular membrane.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  polymer (block polyetheramide), ethanol and                  ultrapure water in the photo-responsive                  metal-organic framework filled mixed matrix film                  is (1-8):(64-69):(28-30), and the mass ratio of                  the photo-responsive metal-organic framework                  material to the block polyetheramide is                  (1.1-6.8):(0.23-1.8).Preferred Method: The source of the                  photo-responsive metal-organic framework material                  is synthesized by an existing method. The mixing                  method and film-forming method of the                  photo-responsive metal-organic framework-filled                  mixed matrix film are an existing method, and the                  mixing method is an existing method. The                  photo-responsive metal organic framework filling                  mixed matrix film is synthesized by (a)                  preparation of metal organic framework materials                  denoted as Uio-66-amino by preparing Uio-66-amino                  by hydrothermal synthesis, using N-N                  dimethylformamide as solvent, dissolving                  zirconium tetrachloride in N-N dimethylformamide,                  adding 2-aminoterephthalic acid, where the mass                  ratio of zirconium tetrachloride to terephthalic                  acid is (4.5-5):(1.6-2), and the concentration                  range of zirconium tetrachloride in N-N                  dimethylformamide is 15-17% g/ml, stirring to                  dissolve completely, adding glacial acetic acid                  and evenly mixing, where the mass range ratio of                  glacial acetic acid to N-N dimethylformamide is                  (3-4):(25-35), transferring the solution to a                  stainless steel reaction kettle, placing in a                  muffle furnace, keeping at a constant temperature                  of 120degrees Celsius for 20 hours, after                  reacting, cooling the product to room                  temperature, washing by centrifugation with                  N,N-dimethylformamide and methanol, and then                  performing vacuum-drying at 45degrees Celsius to                  obtain Uio-66-amino powder, (b) preparing a                  photoresponsive metal-organic framework material                  namely CE-Azo-Uio-66 by performing                  vacuum-activating the Uio-66-amino powder                  obtained in step (a) at 60degrees Celsius for 12                  hours, adding then Uio-66-amino powder and                  4'-nitrobenzo-18-crown 6-ether with glacial                  acetic acid, heating at 60degrees Celsius, and                  stirring for 24 hours, after cooling the reactant                  to room temperature, purifying it by methanol                  centrifugation, washing the product to                  neutrality, and finally performing vacuum-drying                  the product at 45degrees Celsius to obtain                  CE-Azo-Uio-66 powder with a particle size of                  200-1 microm, and (c) preparation of the                  photoresponsive polymer-metal organic framework                  mixed matrix film using the casting method to                  prepare the mixed matrix film, by where the                  polymer metal-organic framework mixed matrix film                  includes CE-Azo-Uio-66, block polyetheramide                  (Pebax-1657 (RTM: Polyetheramide)), ultrapure                  water, and ethanol, weighing the mass ratio of                  Pebax-1657 (RTM: Polyetheramide) particles,                  ethanol and ultrapure water as                  (1-8):(64-69):(28-30), stirring the mixture and                  refluxing at 80degrees Celsius for 2 hours to                  obtain a Pebax (RTM: Polyetheramide) solution                  with a mass fraction of (1-8)%, after cooling to                  room temperature, taking the mass ratio of                  CE-Azo-Uio-66 powder and Pebax-1657 (RTM:                  Polyetheramide) as (1.1-6.8):(0.23-1.8), and                  taking the mass ratio of ethanol and ultrapure                  water as (6-8):(2-4), firstly dissolving                  CE-Azo-Uio-66 powder in a mixture of appropriate                  amount of ethanol and ultrapure water,                  ultrasonically treating for 3 hours, adding it to                  Pebax (RTM: Polyetheramide) solution, stirring                  and ultrasonically processing to make it evenly                  mixed, finally and slowly pouring the casting                  liquid into the PTFE mold, drying at room                  temperature for 48 hours, then putting the film                  put into a vacuum oven together with the mold,                  after drying at 45degrees Celsius for 24 hours,                  and finnaly obtaining the photoresponsive                  polymer-metal organic framework mixed matrix                  film, and this method was used to prepare Pebax                  (RTM: Polyetheramide)/Uio-66-amino mixed matrix                  membrane.Preferred Properties: The particle size of                  the photo-responsive metal-organic framework                  material is 200 nm to 1 microm.

PN CN113549035-A
TI Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid from furfural involves mixing acid-base regulator, nano copper oxide powder and water, adding furfural, injecting continuously air, filtering to removing nano-copper oxide after reaction.
AU WANG J
   LIU J
   WANG R
   LI Y
   ZHOU G
AE JILIN ZHONGKE POLYMER ENG PLASTIC CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4039F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid from furfural involves mixing acid-base regulator, nano copper oxide powder and water, adding furfural at 60-100degrees Celsius, injecting continuously air, filtering to removing nano-copper oxide after the reaction, cooling the mother liquor to 0-10degrees Celsius to precipitate solid, filtering to obtain potassium furoate, filtering the precipitated solid, adding continuously mother liquor to potassium carbonate and the nano-copper oxide removed by filtration, repeating above process 0-n times, combining the final potassium furoate, reacting potassium furoate, basic compound and solvent under the condition of passing carbon dioxide to obtain 2,5-furandimethic salt, and post-processing to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   USE - Method for preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid from furfural.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enbles to prepare 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid by using solvent and raw material are cheap, the solvent and the catalyst can be recycled, cost-effective, the waste water is small, the process is simple, the process is efficient, and eco-friendly, and it is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is aprotic compound selected from diphenyl ether, sulfolane, dimethyl sulfone, biphenyl, naphthalene, xylene, trimethylbenzene, N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethylacetamide, and hexamethylphosphoramide, preferably, diphenyl ether, sulfolane, and xylene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid-base regulator includes potassium carbonate and potassium bicarbonate. The basic compound is selected from potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, cesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, sodium acetate, potassium acetate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, calcium bicarbonate, potassium tert-butoxide, and sodium tert-butoxide, preferably, potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate. Preferred Conditions: The furfural is added at 70-90degrees Celsius. The acid-base regulator, nano copper oxide powder and water are mixing, adding furfural at 60-100degrees Celsius, injecting continuously air, filtering to removing nano-copper oxide after the reaction, the reaction time is 0.5-72 hours, preferably, 0.5-2 hours. The molar ratio of acid-base regulator, nano-copper oxide and furfural is 0.25-0.75:0.05-10:1. The molar ratio of nano-copper oxide to furfural is 0.4-0.6:0.05-0.1:1. The mother liquor is adding continuously to potassium carbonate and the nano-copper oxide removed by filtration, repeating 0-n times, where n is an integer greater than 1, when n is 3-4, the nano-copper oxide filtered and removed is activated, tthe activation treatment method is to heating the nano-copper oxide to 450-550degrees Celsius for 3-5 hours, and the treated nano-copper oxide can continue to be used without loss. The potassium furoate, basic compound and solvent are reacting at temperature of 150-350degrees Celsius, preferably, 200-265degrees Celsius, the carbon dioxide is introduced to making the reaction pressure 0.1-25 megapascal, preferably, 0.8-22 megapascal. The molar ratio of potassium furoate to the basic compound is 1:0.1-50, preferably, 1:1-5. The mass ratio of potassium furoate to solvent is 1-99:99-1, preferably, 25-75:75-25.

PT P
PN CN113372934-A
TI Decarbonization and hydrogen production by potassium-calcium co-catalysis of biomass pyrolysis by dissolving potassium hydroxide and calcium hydroxide in water, placing in immersion treatment, dehydrating and milling biomass sample, placing in fixed bed reactor and quickly pyrolyzing.
AU HU M
   YE Z
   ZHANG H
   PAN Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2021A8141L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for decarbonization and hydrogen production by potassium-calcium co-catalysis of biomass pyrolysis involves (a) dissolving potassium hydroxide and calcium hydroxide in water together to form a potassium-calcium mixed solution, placing the biomass waste powder in the potassium-calcium mixed solution for immersion treatment, (b) dehydrating and milling the biomass sample after immersion treatment, and obtaining the pyrolysis precursor raw material after sieving, (c) placing the pyrolysis precursor raw material obtained in step (b) in a fixed bed reactor and quickly pyrolyzing under the protection of argon atmosphere, and producing the pyrolysis gas by pyrolysis is the hydrogen-rich product after decarburization.
   USE - Method used for decarbonization and hydrogen production by potassium-calcium co-catalysis of biomass pyrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the hydrogen content in the biomass pyrolysis gas product, increases hydrogen volume percentage in the pyrolysis gas while achieving the effect of completely removing carbon dioxide, obtains the raw materials more easily, has high proportion of hydrogen in the pyrolysis gas, and adjusts the proportion of gas components artificially.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves using the fixed bed reactor, the operation steps of pyrolyzing the pyrolysis precursor raw materials as (i) placing the pyrolysis precursor raw materials in a porcelain boat, and first adjusting the porcelain boat to the inside of the upper end of the quartz tube fixed bed reactor, so that the porcelain boat is set above the vertical tube furnace, pouring argon into the quartz tube fixed bed reactor, replacing and exhausting the air in the quartz tube fixed bed reactor, and heating the quartz tube fixed bed reactor through a vertical tube furnace, (ii) after the temperature of the quartz tube fixed bed reactor rises to the set temperature, passing the porcelain boat into the middle of the quartz tube fixed bed reactor, placing the porcelain boat in the high temperature section of the quartz tube fixed bed reactor, and carrying-out pyrolysis under the protection of argon atmosphere, and collecting the gas discharged from the bottom of the quartz tube fixed bed reactor by a gas collection device to obtain the pyrolysis gas product. The process of immersion treatment in step (a) involves placing the mixed liquid in a constant temperature shaker and shaking at room temperature for 10-15 hours, leaving still to remove the supernatant, and the resulting product is a biomass sample after immersion treatment. In step (b), the dehydration and milling process of the biomass sample after the immersion treatment is: after placing the sample in a blast drying oven at 100-120 degrees C for 10-15 hours, the product obtained by grinding through a 100-mesh sieve is the pyrolysis precursor raw material. Preferred Conditions: In step (c), the pyrolysis temperature is 580-620 degrees C. Preferred Components: In step (a), the biomass waste powder is soybean straw powder with a moisture content of not less than 10% and a particle size of not less than 0.15 mm. In step (a), the mass ratio of calcium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and biomass waste powder is 0.1-2.2:1:2, preferably 2.0:1:2. The pyrolysis gas produced by pyrolysis comprises four components: hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and carbon dioxide, and the total integral number of the four components in the pyrolysis gas is more than 85%, the volume concentration of hydrogen in the four components of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and carbon dioxide is more than 40%. When the mass ratio of calcium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and biomass waste powder in step (a) is 2.0:1:2, and the pyrolysis temperature in step (c) is 580-620 degrees C, The hydrogen component in the pyrolysis gas has a volume percentage content of more than 70% in the four components of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane and carbon dioxide, and the volume content of carbon dioxide in the four gas products accounts for less than 0.1%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The fixed bed reactor in step (c) comprises a quartz tube fixed bed reactor and a vertical tube furnace for heating the quartz tube. The quartz tube fixed bed reactor is vertically arranged in the vertical tube furnace, and the upper end of the quartz tube fixed bed reactor passes through the vertical tube furnace. A porcelain boat with an open upper end is suspended in the quartz tube fixed bed reactor. The porcelain boat is connected to the molybdenum wire, and the upper end of the molybdenum wire penetrates upward from the top of the quartz tube fixed bed reactor. The upper and lower positions of the porcelain boat in the quartz tube fixed bed reactor can be adjusted by adjusting the molybdenum wire. The top of the quartz tube fixed-bed reactor is connected with a gas inlet pipe for passing argon. The bottom of the quartz tube fixed bed reactor is connected with a gas collection device through a pipeline.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC H09-F03; J04-E01; J04-E04

PN CN113234931-A
TI Comprehensively recycling platinum and aluminum from spent platinum-alumina catalyst by e.g. fine grinding and sieving spent platinum-alumina catalyst, adsorbing and refining platinum-containing enriched slag, and calcining the precipitate.
AU LI B
   PU Q
   CUI H
   DONG H
   LI Y
   ZHAO J
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202197165N
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensively recycling platinum and aluminum from spent platinum/alumina catalyst comprises fine grinding and sieving the spent platinum/alumina catalyst to obtain the catalyst powder; adding the catalyst powder into the sodium hydroxide solution; carrying out high temperature and high pressure leaching in the high pressure reaction kettle; separating solid and liquid; obtaining the platinum-containing insoluble slag and aluminum-containing solution; adsorbing the platinum-containing insoluble slag to obtain the platinum-containing enriched slag; refining the platinum-containing enriched slag to obtain platinum metal; introducing the aluminum-containing solution into excessive carbon dioxide to convert the sodium meta-aluminate into aluminum hydroxide precipitate; and calcining the aluminum hydroxide precipitate to obtain the metal alumina, where 20-30 wt.% ammonium sulfate solution is used as an ionic liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensively recycling platinum and aluminum from spent platinum/alumina catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for processing alumina-based catalyst, has good carrier dissolving effect, makes high content of platinum group metal in the enriched slag, and realizes the efficient comprehensive recycling of alumina and platinum in the spent catalyst.

PN KR2021092177-A; KR2334430-B1
TI Biodegradable mulching film with excellent water resistance comprises antioxidant comprising biodegradable resin and chain extender, hydrolysis resistance agent, primary antioxidant including phenolic antioxidant.
AU HOON S J
   WON K C
   HEE J H
   YOO D J
AE FREX CO LTD (FREX-Non-standard)
GA 202186427W
AB    NOVELTY - Biodegradable mulching film with excellent water resistance comprises (i) an antioxidant comprising a biodegradable resin and a chain extender, a hydrolysis resistance agent, a primary antioxidant including a phenolic antioxidant, and a secondary antioxidant including a phosphorus antioxidant, blending at least one additives of UV absorbers and light stabilizers; (ii) melt extruding to form pellets; (iii) annealing the extruded pellets; and (iv) preparing through the film extrusion step after the annealing. The biodegradable resin is made of poly(butylene adipate-co-terephthalate), and polylactic acid (PLA), and annealing step comprises agitating the extruded pellets in a high temperature atmosphere of 80-120 degrees C for 30-50 minutes to solve the residual stress inside the resin and to minimize the active site that microorganisms and enzymes can access.
   USE - Used as biodegradable mulching film with excellent water resistance.
   ADVANTAGE - The film maximizes expression of physical properties and increase degree of crystallinity, has improved water resistance compared to existing biodegradable mulching film by minimizing active site and shortens dehumidification drying time by more than 70%; is environmentally friendly, reduces generation of carbon dioxide which is one of causes of global warming by more than 30%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The film comprises 0.5-3 pts. wt. chain extender blended, 0.5-3 pts. wt. hydrolysis resistant agent, 0.05-0.5 pts. wt. antioxidant including primary antioxidant and secondary antioxidant, 0.1-0.9 pts. wt. UV absorber and 0.1-0.9 pts. wt. light stabilizer blended with respect to 100 pts. wt. biodegradable resin. Preferred Components: The biodegradable resin, a chain extender, a hydrolysis resistance agent, a primary antioxidant including a phenolic antioxidant, and a phosphorus antioxidant containing antioxidants consisting of secondary antioxidants, UV absorbers, and at least one additive of light stabilizer is blended with the biodegradable resin to satisfy an acid value of 0.5-2.
   EXAMPLE - Typical biodegradable mulching film composition comprised of: 74 wt.% polybutylene adipate terephthalate, 8 wt.% polylactic acid, 15 wt.% calcium carbonate, 3 wt.% carbon black, 0.1 pts. wt. primary antioxidant (A0-60), 0.15 pts. wt. secondary antioxidant (A0-2112), 0.2 pts. wt. lubricant and slip agent (ethylene bis stearamide), and 0.5 pts. wt. light stabilizer (Tinuvin 770 (RTM: Low molecular weight hindered amine light stabilizer)).

PN CN112858111-A; CN112858111-B
TI Measuring interfacial tension between polymer            melts in high pressure gas, involves preparing polymer            A into short fiber and polymer B into sheet, and            calculating shape-related factor in short fiber            retraction process by combining slope.
AU LIAO X
   ZOU F
   LI S
   TANG W
   LIU F
   LI G
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202161775V
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring interfacial tension between polymer                melts in high pressure gas involves preparing a                polymer A into short fiber and performing annealing                treatment to eliminate internal stress. A polymer B                is prepared into sheet. The sample to be tested is                placed in a high pressure cavity of a high pressure                visual device. The temperature of the high pressure                cavity is adjusted to a saturation temperature. At                least 5 pieces of short fiber shot at different                time points is selected to shrink into a picture                before spherical shape from the data with a change                of the shape of a short fiber recorded in time. A                shape-related factor in short fiber retraction                process is calculated by combining slope of linear                equation obtained by fitting. The test gas is                carbon dioxide, nitrogen, hydrogen, air, methane,                propane, butane pentane, helium gas or argon                gas.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring interfacial                tension between polymer melts in high pressure                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low requirement on a measuring                condition, good applicability, which can improve                the accuracy of a measuring result.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: A polymer A and a                  polymer B are chosen from polypropylene,                  polyethylene, polystyrene, polyvinyl chloride,                  polyethylene glycol terephthalate, polymethyl                  methacrylate, polycarbonate and polyamide.                  polylactic acid, polycaprolactone and polyacrylic                  acid.

   LAI H
   DONG N
   YAN Y
   YANG X
AE HUANONG ZHAOQING BIOLOGICAL IND TECHNOLO (HUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202148265F
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises taking Madin-Darby canine                kidney cell suspension after propagation subculture                using initial viable cell inoculation density of 1x                106 cells/mL, temperature of 37 degrees C,                introducing 5 wt.% carbon dioxide, performing                serum-free full suspension culture in 30 mL culture                system in 125 mL shaking bottle, propagating and                passaging, when the cell density reaches the                logarithmic growth phase density and total amount                reaches a certain scale, separating culture bottle,                culturing cell for 48 hour, designing multiplicity                of infection is 0.01, calculating the needed                inoculation of avian influenza virus volume                according to the inoculating virus content and cell                number ratio relation, adding tosyl phenylalanyl                chloromethyl ketone-pancreatin, transferring to 5                wt.% cabon dioxide incubator to continuously                culture, setting temperature to 33 degrees C, and                harvesting the virus for 36 hours or 48 hours after                infecting virus.
   USE - The method is useful for proliferating H9N2                subtype avian influenza virus in fully suspended                serum-free Madin-Darby canine kidney cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method optimizes production process,                expands culture, and produces high quality and                high-titer H9N2 subtype avian influenza                virus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The final                  concentration of tosyl phenylalanyl chloromethyl                  ketone-pancreatin is adjusted to-11 mu g/mL,                  preferably 7 mu g/mL after inoculating avian                  influenza virus. Preferred Method: The culture                  system is added while the virus is inoculated,                  and the supplemented culture system is equal to                  2/3 of the volume of the original culture                  system.

Preferred Method: The temperature of the heating reaction is 80-100 degrees C, and the time of the heating reaction is 0.8-1.5 hours. PT P
PN CN112429769-A; CN112429769-B
TI Preparing ultra-low density titanium dioxide foam by putting low-density metal foam as substrate for atomic layer deposition into reaction chamber, turning on cyclic deposition, soaking in dilute nitric acid, washing and drying.
AU NIU G
   YUAN L
   XU X
   YANG R
   TAN X
   DONG Y
AE LASER FUSION RES CENT CHINA ACAD ENG PHY (CAEP-C)
GA 202123155M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of ultra-low density titanium dioxide foam, involves (i) putting low-density metal foam as substrate for atomic layer deposition into reaction chamber of atomic layer deposition equipment, setting the temperature of the reaction chamber at 120-200 degrees C, using titanium source and oxygen source as precursor to generate titanium dioxide respectively, turning on atomic layer deposition equipment for cyclic deposition, and stop heating after the cyclic deposition is completed, and (ii) lowering the temperature of the reaction chamber to room temperature, taking out low-density metal foam and titanium dioxide foam composite block, soaking in dilute nitric acid of 5-20 vol.% to remove low-density metal foam to obtain a titanium dioxide block material, washing the titanium dioxide bulk material and drying with supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ultra-low density titanium dioxide foam, which is used in the fields of laser inertial confinement fusion, hydrogen storage, purification, filtration, and catalytic support.
   ADVANTAGE - The titanium dioxide foam has low density.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The low-density metal foam is foamed silver, prepared by adding sulfide alcohol solution to polyvinylpyrrolidone alcohol solution in the dark, uniformly stirring, adding silver nitrate alcohol solution, uniformly stirring, adding to autoclave, reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 5-12 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain silver nanowire foam suspended in alcohol solution, putting silver nanowire foam in ultrapure water to exchange, washing several times, exchanging with acetone several times until the moisture content is lower than 4000 parts per million after the solution is transparent and clear, and freeze-drying to obtain foamed silver, (or) adding sulfide alcohol solution to polyvinylpyrrolidone alcohol solution in the dark, pressing and ultrasonically treating for 15-30 minutes, adding silver nitrate alcohol solution, uniformly stirring, adding to supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, sealing, feeding in carbon dioxide at 15-20 MPa, maintaining the temperature at 45-60 degrees C for 45-60 minutes, releasing the pressure, adding to the autoclave, and reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 3-8 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain silver nanowire foam suspended in alcohol solution, putting silver nanowire foam in ultrapure water to exchange, washing several times, exchanging with acetone several times until the moisture content is lower than 4000 parts per million after the solution is transparent and clear, and freeze-drying to obtain foamed silver. In step (i), the one cycle of cyclic deposition of the atomic layer deposition equipment involves (a) subjecting pulsed titanium source precursor to reaction chamber and chemically adsorbing on substrate, (b) purging and flushing the unreacted titanium source and the by-products produced by reaction between titanium source and substrate with nitrogen, (c) pulsing oxygen source and reacting with titanium tetrachloride, and (d) using nitrogen purge gas to purge unreacted water and by-products. Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the pressurized ultrasonic treatment is 0.2-0.4 MPa, and the frequency is 120-150 kHz. The pulse time for the deposition of the titanium source precursor in the reaction chamber time is 1 second, and the time for nitrogen purge and flushing is 10 seconds. The pulse time of the oxygen source deposition in the reaction chamber is 0.1 second, and the nitrogen purge and flushing time is 10 seconds. The step (ii) involves immersing in dilute nitric acid with content of 5-20 vol.%, and processing at 50 KHz, 100 KHz, 180 KHz, and 260 KHz in sequence, each frequency processing time is 5-10 minutes. The number of cycles of the cyclic deposition is 500-2000, and the drying temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide drying is 40 degrees C. The polyvinylpyrrolidone alcohol solution is heated to 120-160 degrees C before use, and maintained for 10-15 minutes to fully remove excess water and dissolved oxygen. Preferred Components: The solute sulfide of the sulfide alcohol solution is sodium sulfide or potassium sulfide. The titanium source is titanium tetrachloride. The oxygen source is water or ozone. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the sulfide alcohol solution is 0.02-0.05 mol/l. The concentration of the silver nitrate alcohol solution is 0.5-2 mol/l. The volume ratio of the sulfide alcohol solution and the polyvinylpyrrolidone alcohol solution is 1:(45-55). The volume ratio of the silver nitrate alcohol solution to the polyvinylpyrrolidone alcohol solution is 1:(15-25).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvents of the sulfide alcohol solution, polyvinylpyrrolidone alcohol solution and silver nitrate alcohol solution are ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, glycerol and/or butylene glycol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the polyvinylpyrrolidone alcohol solution is 0.1-1 mol/l. Preferred Properties: The solute polyvinylpyrrolidone in the polyvinylpyrrolidone alcohol solution has a molecular weight of 54000-1300000.

TI Hydroxyl non-isocyanate polyurethane prepolymer used for curable formulation, is copolymer of precursor cyclic carbonate having preset residual epoxy equivalent weight, and polyamines in preset molar ratio.
AU ALLAUDDIN S
   NEHETE K K
   SHYAMROY S
AE ASIAN PAINTS LTD (ASPA-Non-standard)
GA 2021118803
AB    NOVELTY - A hydroxyl non-isocyanate polyurethane (HNIPU) prepolymer comprises stable cyclic carbonate backbone based amine terminated HNIPU prepolymer. The HNIPU prepolymer is a copolymer of precursor cyclic carbonate having residual epoxy equivalent weight of 800-4000, and polyamines. The molar ratio of precursor cyclic carbonate to polyamine is 1:1.1-1:2. The copolymer has amine hydrogen equivalent level of 190-600.
   USE - Hydroxyl non-isocyanate polyurethane prepolymer used for curable formulations for forming coating film.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydroxyl non-isocyanate polyurethane prepolymer has excellent shelf stability and does not contain catalyst and provides the coating film having excellent sandability only after 3-5 hours, mechanical and flow leveling properties, glossiness, drying property, curing efficiency, and re-coatability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of HNIPU prepolymer, which involves (i) providing epoxy precursor and partially reacting with carbon dioxide to obtain precursor cyclic carbonate, and (ii) reacting the precursor cyclic carbonate with polyamine which is preferably cycloaliphatic amine and/or linear aliphatic amine in molar ratio of 1:1.1-1:2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The stable cyclic carbonate backbone is preferably a hybrid cyclic carbonate backbone based amine terminated HNIPU prepolymer having functional groups including amines and hydroxyl apart from the carbamate linkages. The amines are curable with epoxy moieties adapted for epoxy amine coatings or with isocyanates adapted for hybrid polyurethane urea coatings. The pendant-hydroxyl functionality with each carbamate group is adapted for baking to provide one component thermoset systems with the hydroxyl groups curing by themselves. The precursor cyclic carbonate comprises partially carbonated different backbone based precursors as blend cyclic carbonate sourced of epoxy precursors including bisphenol A diglycidyl ether (BADGE) and epoxidized soybean oil (ESBO) in molar ratio of 1:99-75:25. The weight ratio of blend of partially carbonated ESBO and partially carbonated BADGE is 60:40 with epoxy equivalent levels of 1300-2000 corresponding to 40-80% of epoxy converted carbonate. When ratio of cyclic carbonate and polyamine is 1:1.1-1:2, the amine terminated HNIPU prepolymer is stable and includes excess amine that passes accelerated stability test at 55 degrees C for at least 30 days, and does not self cure at the temperature. The molar ratio of cycloaliphatic amine and linear aliphatic amine is 0.45-0.9:0.2-1.1. The BADGE has epoxy equivalent of 170-300 which enables faster drying and diglycidyl ether of hydrogenated bisphenol-A has same epoxy equivalent of 170-300 which provides for lower drying characteristics upon curing with epoxy resin. The epoxy precursor includes 0.1-3% diglycidyl ether of hydrogenated bisphenol-A with epoxy equivalent weight of 450-3000 adapted for improved flow leveling of films. The copolymer has amine hydrogen equivalent of 190-600 adapted for faster drying in the range of 3-8 hours and solid content of prepolymer and binder of 65-70%. The HNIPU prepolymer is curable/cross-likable with epoxy moieties sourced of epoxy monomers/polymers/resins, and free of any catalyst adapted for curable formulations/thermosets/coating films including ambient temperature cured films with sandability of the film only after 3-5 hours, improved mechanical and flow leveling properties, gloss, drying property, curing efficiency, and re-coatability. Preferred Process: The manufacture of HNIPU prepolymer involves (i) providing blend of BADGE and ESBO as epoxy precursors in ratio of 1:99-75:25 and partially reacting with carbon dioxide in the presence of tetrabutylammonium bromide with initial carbon dioxide supply pressure of 18 bar in organic solvent, preferably o-xylene at 150 degrees C by maintaining the carbon dioxide supply pressure at 38-40 bar for 9.30 hours, to obtain the partially carbonated epoxy based cyclic carbonate blend adapted for hybrid cyclic carbonate backbone based amine terminated HNIPU prepolymers and (ii) reacting the epoxy precursor based cyclic carbonate blend that is partially carbonated, with polyamines including isophoronediamine in the temperature of 40-120 degrees C preferably 80 degrees C for 2-5 hours, preferably 2.30 hours, and adding triethylenetetramine and continuing for 2-8 hours, preferably 4.30 hours until amine hydrogen equivalent levels in the range of 190-600, preferably 272-350 is reached and obtaining the hybrid cyclic carbonate backbone based amine terminated HNIPU prepolymers, which is free of any catalyst.

PT P
PN CN112111414-A; CN112111414-B
TI Preparing white rot fungus solid culture medium, by adding calcium oxide and deionized water to waste chrome shavings of tanning, reacting, performing centrifugal suction filtration, and sterilizing culture medium.
AU LIU J
   ZHANG F
   XU Z
   LI Y
   LI X
   ZHOU Y
AE UNIV QILU TECHNOLOGY (UYQQ-C)
   UNIV YIBIN (UYYI-Non-standard)
GA 2020D0076D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing white rot fungus solid culture medium, involves adding 6-9% calcium oxide and 600-1000% deionized water of the weight of the chrome shavings to the waste chrome shavings of tanning at 60-80 degrees C, stirring the mixture, reacting the mixture, performing centrifugal suction filtration, collecting the filtrate, treating with carbon dioxide, removing the precipitate by suction filtration, collecting the filtrate, centrifuging, discarding the precipitate, collecting the liquid, adding endohydrolyzing protease to the liquid, stirring to obtain small molecule enzymatic hydrolyzed protein, mixing raw materials comprising glucose, agar, peptone, trace element stock solution, vitamin B1, small molecule enzyme hydrolyzed protein and purified water, and sterilizing the culture medium and inoculation tools.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing white rot fungus solid culture medium (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method replaces traditional peptone resource, and is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) solid medium prepared by the method; and
   (2) use of the solid medium, in fermentation and cultivation of white-rot fungal microorganisms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The shavings are sheepskin shavings, cowhide shavings or pigskin shavings. The chromium content of the chrome shavings is 2.5-5% chromium(III) oxide. The chrome leather shavings are added in batches, respectively in the reaction 0 hour, 0.5 hour and 1 hour, each adding amount is 1/2 and 1/4 of the total amount of chrome shavings. The chromium content in the liquid is 0.06-3 mg/l. The amount of endohydrolytic protease is that amount of the endohydrolytic protease of 1% of hydrolyzed protein content. The composition of the trace element stock solution comprises 6 g/l cobaltous sulfate heptahydrate, 6 g/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 6 g/l ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, 0.6 g/l copper sulfate pentahydrate and 0.6 g/l boric acid. The medium comprises 20 g/l glucose, 20 g/l agar, 0.5 g/l peptone, 16.7 ml/l trace element liquid stock solution, 0.4 g/l vitamin B1, and 2-25 g/l enzymatically hydrolyzed protein.

PT P
PN CN111944073-A
TI Extracting active ingredients of Chinese medicinal materials by washing radix codonopsis, crushing, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis using cellulose and pectinase, concentrating under vacuum and freeze-drying.
AU LI B
   TIAN P
AE LI B (LIBB-Individual)
GA 2020B71562
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting active ingredients of Chinese medicinal materials involves (a) washing radix codonopsis, crushing the slices for 3 minutes at -15 to 0 degrees C under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, raising the temperature to 80 degrees C and crushing for 2 minutes to obtain radix codonopsis powder, (b) placing the obtained radix codonopsis powder in an enzyme hydrolysis tank, adjusting the pH to 2.5-5.5, adding cellulase and pectinase, placing in a constant temperature water bath shaker at 60-80 degrees C, rotating at 100-200 rpm, carrying out enzymolysis treatment for 30-80 minutes, inactivating the enzyme to obtain an enzyme inactivation solution, (c) adding the obtained enzyme inactivation solution to an autoclave, adding purified water, carrying out subcritical water extraction treatment for 30-50 minutes at 100-200 degrees C, carrying out centrifugal separation, concentrating the resulting supernatant under vacuum and freeze-drying to obtain the finished product.
   USE - The method is used for extracting active ingredients of Chinese medicinal materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the extraction time and increases the extraction rate of radix codonopsis polysaccharide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises pulverizing for 3 minutes at -15 to 0 degrees C under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, increasing the temperature to 80 degrees C at a rate of 100 degrees C/minute, and pulverizing for 2 minutes to obtain radix codonopsis powder. The supernatant in the step (c), is concentrated by rotary evaporation to 1/5-1/15 of the original volume under the conditions of 40-70 degrees C and 0.08-0.1 MPa to obtain a concentrated solution. Preferred Components: The addition amount of cellulase in the step (b) is 1-6% and the addition amount of pectinase is 0.3-5% of the mass of radix codonopsis powder. The material-to-liquid ratio of the subcritical water extraction in step (c) is 12 ml/g.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN JP6782506-B1; JP2021172903-A
TI Biodegradable mask comprise a portion of fibers made of a biodegradable resin composition, where the biodegradable resin composition contains a resin component containing polylactic acid, lactic acid, contains a plastic agent.
AU NAKAMURA H
   MIZUNO E
AE TBM CO LTD (TBMT-Non-standard)
GA 2020A91842
AB    NOVELTY - Biodegradable mask comprise a portion of fibers made of a biodegradable resin composition, where the biodegradable resin composition contains a resin component containing polylactic acid, lactic acid, and weight average molecular weight of 3,000, contains a plastic agent selected from lactic acid oligomer and a branched polylactic acid, and heavy calcium carbonate particles, and the plastic agent has a total mass of the biodegradable resin composition of 100. The resin component and the heavy calcium carbonate particles are contained in a proportion of 1-5 wt.%, the proportion of the heavy calcium carbonate particles is 50:50-10:90. The average particle diameter of the heavy calcium carbonate particles contained and calculated from the measurement result of the specific surface area by the air permeation method according to JISM-8511 is 1.0-10.0 micrometer, and the heavy carbon dioxide is contained. The BET specific surface area of calcium particles is 0.1-10.0 m2/g.
   USE - Biodegradable mask.
   ADVANTAGE - The biodegradable mask is cost-effective, has flexible mounting|wearing property and excellent also in the wear feeling and improves biodegradability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The mask comprises at least a portion of the woven fabric or non-woven fabric of the fiber. Preferred Conditions: The roundness of the heavy calcium carbonate particles is 0.50-0.95. The woven fabric or non-woven fabric of the first fiber and the woven fabric or non-woven fabric of the second fiber are laminated, the first fiber is made of the biodegradable resin composition, and the second fiber is non-heavy calcium carbonate particles. The heavy calcium carbonate particle-free resin composition comprises resin-containing composition, resin component containing polylactic acid, lactic acid, a lactic acid oligomer having a weight average molecular weight of 3,000 or less, and branched polylactic acid, plasticizer, where the content of the plasticizer in the heavy calcium carbonate particle-free resin composition is 1-5 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Horizontal well low-temperature fluid enhanced            injection ice plugging and fracturing method, involves            adjusting sealing ball to seal and fix drilling            plugging device at position, and completing cracking            process of horizontal drilling.
AU DI C
   CONG Y
   ZHENG Y
   XU J
   SUN Y
   TANG W
   LI Y
   CHEN Z
   HE S
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2020B3041V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves extending a hydraulic                cutting device into a horizontal well drilling from                ground drilling. A low-temperature fluid pipe is                selected form a low-temperature fluid passage. A                drilling plugging device is placed in the                horizontal drilling. A control valve on a water                injection pipe is opened. The control valve on a                low temperature fluid passage and a low temperature                fluid pump is opened. Gasification fracturing of a                sealed fracturing chamber is realized along with                low temperature fluid. Low temperature fluid cold                impact and gasification cracking process is                performed. A sealing ball is adjusted to seal and                fix the drilling plugging device at a position.                Cracking process of entire horizontal drilling is                completed, where low-temperature fluid is liquid                nitrogen and liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - Horizontal well low-temperature fluid enhanced                injection ice plugging and fracturing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing rapid injection                of low-temperature fluid so as to ensure fracturing                effect after fracturing low-temperature fluid                circulation by ice blocking and avoid surrounding                environment pollution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a                horizontal well.

PT P
PN CN111821327-A
TI Processing and storing traditional Chinese medicine raw materials by selecting fresh traditional Chinese medicine raw material, cutting, drying in microwave drier, placing in insulation box, packaging and storing in warehouse.
AU ZHONG W
   ZHANG L
   HE X
AE DESHENGTANG JIANGSU PHARM CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
GA 2020A73836
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing and storing traditional Chinese medicine raw materials involves (a) selecting fresh traditional Chinese medicine raw materials, removing rotten and moth-eaten part, and cleaning fresh traditional Chinese medicine raw materials, (b) cutting the cleaned traditional Chinese medicine raw material into required size, (c) adding the cut raw material in a microwave drier to dry, (d) adding the dried traditional Chinese medicine raw material in insulation box at 25-30 degrees C, maintaining for 2 days, and placing in UV lamp for killing insect, (e) packaging the traditional Chinese medicine material after killing insect by packaging machine for different weight specifications, where the packaging adopts packaging bag for vacuum inflation sealing packaging and the packaging bag is filled with mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide and (f) placing in warehouse for storage and maintaining temperature in warehouse at 10 degrees C or less.
   USE - The method is useful for processing and storing traditional Chinese medicine raw materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts standardized equipment to process traditional Chinese medicine, prevents deterioration of traditional Chinese medicine and ensures medicinal quality of traditional Chinese medicine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves drying the cut raw material in a microwave drier with wavelength of 50-500 mm. The step (c) involves drying the cut raw material at 80-90 degrees C for 6-15 minutes. The step (d) involves placing the traditional Chinese medicine raw material in UV lamp with wavelength of 265-350 nm for killing insect and irradiating with UV lamp for 15-20 minutes. The step (e) involves packaging the traditional Chinese medicine raw material with weight packaging specifications of each bag includes 1 g, 2 g, 5 g, 10 g, 50 g and 100 g. The step (e) involves filling packaging bag with mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide at 1:1-1.5 ratio. The packaging bag is made of polyphenylene ether (PPE) plastic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); P14 (Animal management and care (A01K, L, M).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)

PN US2020318900-A1
TI Separation of components e.g. hydrocarbon, involves cooling process liquid stream comprising components, expanding process liquid stream into vessel, and passing process vapor stream and solid product stream out of vessel.
AU BAXTER L
   BURT S
AE SUSTAINABLE ENERGY SOLUTIONS LLC (SUST-Non-standard)
GA 2020981803
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of component involves cooling a process liquid stream comprising a component (c1) and a component (c2) to near a temperature at which the component (c2) forms a solid, expanding the process liquid stream into a vessel such that the component (c1) and a portion (p1) of the component (c2) vaporize to form a process vapor stream and a portion (p2) of the component (c2) forms a solid to form a solid product stream, passing the process vapor stream out of the vessel, and passing the solid product stream out of the vessel.
   USE - Separation of component e.g. hydrocarbon such as methane, ethane, propane, isobutane, n-butane, 5C hydrocarbons, 6C or more hydrocarbons, nitrogen, and helium, and acid gas such as carbon dioxide, sulfur oxides, nitrogen oxides, and carbon monoxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and enables thermodynamically efficient separation of component from process liquid stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of separation of components.
   Cyclone (12)
   Exchanger (14)
   Compressor (16)
   Pump (20)
   Liquid product stream (52)

PT P
PN WO2020206058-A1; CN113853438-A; EP3947701-A1; BR112021017085-A2; US2022186266-A1
TI Producing fermentation product from starch            containing material involves e.g. forming slurry            comprising starch and water, converting            starch-containing material into dextrins, saccharifying            dextrins, hydrolyzing and recycle.
AU KREEL N E
AE NOVOZYMES AS (NOVO-C)
   KREEL N E (KREE-Individual)
   NOVOZYMES AS (NOVO-C)
   NOVOZYMES AS (NOVO-C)
GA 202098113T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a fermentation product from starch                containing material comprises (a) forming a slurry                comprising the starch-containing material and                water, (b) converting the starch-containing                material into dextrins with alpha -amylase, (c)                saccharifying the dextrins using a carbohydrate                source generating enzyme to form sugars, (d)                fermenting sugars using a fermenting organism, (e)                recovering the fermentation product to form whole                stillage, (f) separating the whole stillage into a                liquid fraction thin stillage and solid fraction                wet cake, (g) hydrolyzing the thin stillage, (h)                recycle a portion of the hydrolyzed thin stillage                to steps (a), where the thin stillage in step (g)                is hydrolyzed using a glucoamylase and/or                polygalactorunase.
   USE - The process is useful for producing                fermentation product from starch containing                material, where the fermentation product is e.g.                alcohol (ethanol, methanol, butanol,                1,3-propanediol), organic acids (citric acid,                acetic acid, itaconic acid, lactic acid, gluconic                acid, gluconate, lactic acid, succinic acid,                2,5-diketo-D-gluconic acid), ketones (acetone),                amino acids (glutamic acid), gases (hydrogen and                carbon dioxide), antibiotics (penicillin and                tetracycline), enzymes, vitamins (riboflavin,                vitaminB12 beta -carotene) and hormones.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The portion of the                  hydrolyzed thin stillage that is not recycled                  (i.e. as backset) is evaporated to syrup and                  condensate. The condensate is recycled to step                  (a). The 5-90 vol.%, preferably 20-60 vol.%                  hydrolyzed thin stillage is recycled as backset                  to step (a). The steps (c) and (d) are carried                  out simultaneously or sequentially. The alpha                  -amylase is added in step (a). The thin stillage                  is hydrolyzed in step (g) treated with a alpha                  -glucoamylase (E.C. 3.2.1.3), preferably a                  glycoside hydrolase family (GH)15 enzyme, derived                  from Trametes cingulata comprising SEQ ID NO: 1.                  The thin stillage is hydrolyzed in step (g)                  further hydrolyzed with fungal acid alpha                  -amylase activity i.e. Rhizomucor alpha -amylase,                  i.e. Rhizomucor pusillus alpha -amylase with a                  starch-binding domain (SBD) with linker and SBD,                  preferably Aspergillus niger glucoamylase linker                  and SBD and the alpha -amylase comprises SEQ ID                  NO: 2. The polygalacturonase (EC 3.2.1.15) used                  for hydrolyzing the thin stillage in step (g)                  derived from Aspergillus aculeatus, Aspergillus                  fumigatus, Aspergillus kawachii or Aspergillus                  niger or Aspergillus tubingensis or derived from                  Thermoascus crustaceus. The thin stillage is                  further hydrolyzed in step (g) with a pullulanase                  (E.C.3.2.1.41) derived from Bacillus deramificans                  comprising SEQ ID NO: 3. The thin stillage is                  further hydrolyzed in step (g) with a                  laminarinase (E.C.3.2.1.6), derived from                  Aspergillus aculeatus. The thin stillage is                  hydrolyzed in step (g) with a combination of:                  glucoamylase and alpha -amylase; glucoamylase and                  pullulanase; polygalacturonase and laminarinase.                  The thin stillage is hydrolyzed in step (g) at                  20-80 degrees C, preferably 50 degrees C for                  0.1-10 hours, preferably 2 hours. The process                  flow is similar or identical to that shown in                  Figure 1 of the specification. The protease is                  present in and/or added in steps (a) and/or (b).                  Preferred Components: The recycled hydrolyzed                  thin stillage (backset) constitutes 1-70 vol.%,                  preferably 30-50 vol.%. The glucoamylase has at                  least 60%, preferably 100% sequence identity to                  SEQ ID NO: 1. The fungal acid alpha -amylase has                  at least 60%, preferably 100% sequence identity                  to SEQ ID NO: 2. The pullulanase has at least                  60%, preferably 100% sequence identity to SEQ ID                  NO: 3 (Sequences not defined here may be found at                  ftp://ftp.wipo.int/pub/published_pct_sequences/publication).                  The thin silage comprises 10-50 wt.%, preferably                  35 wt.% the dry solids.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of syngas involves reacting a                catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture                (10) in a CPO reactor to produce the syngas (15).                The CPO reactant mixture comprises hydrocarbons and                oxygen. The CPO reactor comprises a CPO catalyst.                The portion of the hydrocarbons in the CPO reactant                mixture undergoes decomposition to carbon and                hydrogen. The syngas comprises hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, and unreacted                hydrocarbons. The CPO catalyst has a catalyst                productivity variation of 20% of target catalyst                productivity over a time period of 500 hours or                more. The catalyst productivity is defined as the                ratio of the amount of syngas recovered from the                CPO reactor to the amount of hydrocarbons                introduced to the CPO reactor.

Preferred Components: The                  hydrocarbons comprise one or more of methane,                  natural gas, natural gas liquids, associated gas,                  well head gas, enriched gas, paraffins, shale                  gas, shale liquids, fluid catalytic cracking                  (FCC) off gas, refinery process gases, stack                  gases, and fuel gas from fuel gas header. The CPO                  reactant mixture further comprises water and                  carbon dioxide. The methanol reactor                  effluent stream comprises methanol, water,                  hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and                  hydrocarbons. The crude methanol stream comprises                  methanol and water. The purge gas stream                  comprises carbon monoxide and hydrocarbons. The                  carbon dioxide stream comprises at least a                  portion of the carbon dioxide of the methanol                  reactor effluent stream. Preferred Composition:                  The molar ratio of steam to the carbon is                  (0.01:1)-(2.4:1). The CPO reactant mixture                  comprises 0.1-7 mol% carbon dioxide.

TI Treating acute respiratory distress syndrome in patients with acute ischemic stroke with established hypoxemia by inhaling mixture containing helium and oxygen, while monitoring e.g. blood pressure, heart rate and blood saturation.
AU PANIN A A
   CHUCHALIN A G
   GUSEV E I
   KIM T G
   MARTYNOV M Y
   TSAI S T
   SHOGENOVA L V
   MARTYNOV M YU
   TSAY S T
AE PANIN A A (PANI-Individual)
GA 2020571949
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating acute respiratory distress syndrome in patients with acute ischemic stroke with established hypoxemia involves (a) inhaling a mixture (heated to t of 45-60 degrees C) containing 65-75% helium (He) and 25-35% oxygen (O2) daily 3-5 times per day, with duration of inhalation gradually increased daily from 7-10 to 15-20 minutes taking into consideration for the patient's tolerance of procedure during acute phase of stroke, while monitoring blood pressure, heart rate, blood saturation, partial pressure of oxygen (PaO2), partial pressure of carbon dioxide (PCO2), lactate, ECG, electroencephalography (EEG), body temperature and internal respiratory function parameters. The He-O2 mixture is inhaled in conjunction with standard drug therapy, including reperfusion therapy where indicated.
   USE - The method is useful for treating acute respiratory distress syndrome in patients with acute ischemic stroke with established hypoxemia, and preventing hypoxia and hypoxemia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables a genuine acceleration of neurological recovery on National Institutes of health stroke scale (NIHSS), and daily function on modified Rankin score (mRS) scale in patients with ischemic stroke and respiratory impairment.
