PN CN111320190-A; CN111320190-B
TI Waste polishing powder recycling system comprises material conveying unit, roasting unit, extracting unit, carbonation unit and sodium carbonate recovery unit.
AU WANG Y
   XIAO X
   YANG G
   AN X
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2020596266
AB    NOVELTY - A waste polishing powder recycling system comprises material conveying unit, roasting unit, extracting unit, carbonation unit and sodium carbonate recovery unit. The carbonation unit is arranged with first material pump and first filter. The carbonation unit and sodium carbonate recovery unit are arranged with second material pump and second filter. The first filter filter residue outlet is connected to first dryer. The second filter second residue outlet is connected to second dryer.
   USE - Waste polishing powder recycling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently recovers the rare earth, sodium, aluminum, silicon and other elements in the waste polishing powder, and the sodium carbonate medium can be recycled without secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the waste polishing powder resource utilization method comprises taking waste polishing powder and sodium carbonate, mixing and pulverizing, roasting to obtain roasted material, mixing with leaching agent, leaching, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining filtrate and rare earth solid, passing filtrate into carbonation unit, feeding carbon dioxide, carbonating, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining treatment liquid and silicon-aluminum-containing material, concentrating treatment liquid, and drying to obtain sodium carbonate solid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The waste polishing powder resource utilization method comprises taking waste polishing powder and sodium carbonate, mixing and pulverizing by ball milling to particle size of less than or equal to 74 mu m, roasting to obtain roasted material, mixing with water leaching agent, leaching under 0.3-0.5 MPa at 120-150 degrees C for 60-90 minutes, using steam as heating medium at 140-160 degrees C under 0.5-0.6 MPa, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining filtrate and rare earth solid, passing filtrate into carbonation unit, feeding carbon dioxide under 0.4-0.6 MPa, carbonating at 850-950 degrees C for 60-90 minutes, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining treatment liquid with pH value of 7-7.5 and silicon-aluminum-containing material comprising silica and alumina mixture, concentrating treatment liquid via evaporating, and drying to obtain sodium carbonate solid. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of sodium carbonate to the total molar amount of aluminum and silicon in the waste polishing powder is (1.3-1.5):1. The mass concentration of the mixed solution of sodium aluminate and sodium silicate in the filtrate is 15-20 wt.%.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of use of the guinea pig myocardial cell line in the construction of a young animal myocarditis cell model comprising (i) inoculating foot-and-mouth disease virus into the cell line, inoculating 1 ml of foot-and-mouth disease virus liquid in a 25 cm2/bottle, adding 10 ml of cardiomyocyte culture medium CMM after 10-30 minutes of adsorption, adjusting the pH value to 7-7.2, and adding 100 IU/ml of penicillin and culturing streptomycin in an incubator at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide concentration, and (ii) culturing in the step (i) until the cytopathy reaches 75% or more, keeping freezing and thawing twice at -20 degrees C, after the sterility test, qualifying mycoplasma test and foreign virus test, and completing the construction of the young animal myocarditis cell model.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the foot-and-mouth disease virus is type O foot-and-mouth disease virus, type A foot-and-mouth disease virus or AsiaI type foot-and-mouth disease virus. In the step (ii), the virus TCID50 of the young animal myocarditis model is greater than or equal to 6. The concentrations of troponin I and myoglobin in the young animal myocarditis cell model were more than twice that of the uninoculated cell line.

AU JIA W
   WANG B
   SONG Y
   LIN F
AE EVEN RUBBER INST CO LTD (EVEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020380309
AB    NOVELTY - N-thiomorpholino-terminated mercaptosilane                derivative (I) is new.
   USE - N-thiomorpholino-terminated mercaptosilane                coupling agent used in rubber compounding or                vulcanization to improve the interaction force                between rubber and filler or promote the                vulcanization of rubber, where                N-thiomorpholino-terminated mercaptosilane coupling                agent is applied to raw rubber isoprene or                styrene-butadiene rubber, and mixed to get                vulcanized rubber after vulcanization (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The N-thiomorpholino-terminated mercaptosilane                derivative has the effect of promoting                vulcanization, reduces the energy loss in the                vulcanization process and fuel consumption and                carbon dioxide emissions during tire travel, and                enhances the wear resistance of tires.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - N-thiomorpholino-terminated mercaptosilane                derivative of formula(I) is new.
   R1 and R2=H, 1-18C chain alkyl, aromatic or                cycloalkyl;
   X=1-3;
   R3=1-18C chain alkyl, aromatic, cycloalkyl,                alkenyl or alkynyl; and
   R4, R5 and R6=hydrolyzable chlorine, bromine,                alkoxy or ester group, and the rest are selected                from hydrogen, chain alkyl, aromatic group or                cycloalkyl group.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing N-thiomorpholino-terminated                mercaptosilane derivative as coupling agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The                  N-thiomorpholino-terminated mercaptosilane                  derivative is prepared by reacting a                  dithiodimorpholine compound and a                  mercaptoalkylsilane compound. Preferred                  Components: The N-thiomorpholino-terminated                  mercaptosilane derivative is represented by                  formula (II). The dithiodimorpholine compound is                  4,4'-dithiodimorpholine or 4,4'-dithio-bis (                  2,6-dimethylmorpholine). The mercaptoalkylsilane                  compound is mercaptopropyltriethoxysilane.                  Preferred Process: The ratio of the                  dithiodimorpholine compound to the                  mercaptoalkylsilane compound is 1: 1.0 to 1.2.                  The reaction time is 6 to 12 hours, and the                  reaction temperature is 25 to 40 degrees C. The                  reaction involves dissolving the                  dithiodimorpholine compound in an organic solvent                  under an inert gas atmosphere, and preparing a                  concentration of 0.4 to 0.8 mol/L reaction                  solution, the mercaptoalkylsilane compound is                  added dropwise to the reaction solution. The                  reaction temperature is 25-40 degrees C, the                  magneton rotation speed is 500-800 revolutions                  per minute, and the reaction time is 6-12                  hours.

   (4) article for producing polymers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The feedstock                  comprises 50-100 wt.%, preferably 70-85 wt.%                  renewable isomeric paraffin composition and 0-50                  wt.%, preferably 15-30 wt.% fossil naphtha. The                  feedstock comprises 95 wt.% or more, preferably                  99 wt.% or more renewable isomeric paraffin                  composition and fossil naphtha in total. The                  weight ratio of isoparaffins with more than three                  branches with respect to all isoparaffins is less                  than 0.12, preferably less than 0.05. The                  renewable isomeric paraffin composition comprises                  80 wt.% or more, preferably 95 wt.% or more                  14-18C isoparaffins. The renewable isomeric                  paraffin composition comprises 60-95 wt.%,                  preferably 90 wt.% or more paraffins. The fossil                  naphtha comprises 20-85 wt.% paraffins, 0-35 wt.%                  olefins, 10-30 wt.% naphthenes, 0-30 wt.%                  aromatics, and 95 wt.% or more, preferably 99                  wt.% or more hydrocarbons. The feedstock                  comprises 20-300 weight ppm, preferably 50-65                  weight ppm sulfur. Preferred Process: The process                  further involves subjecting the cracking product                  to purification treatment to remove carbon                  monoxide, carbon dioxide and/or ethylene, and/or                  to polymerization treatment to produce                  polymers.

PT P
PN CN111007824-A
TI Intelligent agricultural greenhouse management system, has monitoring module comprising sensor module, and simulation module comprising data collecting module, simulation and modeling module and model storage module.
AU ZHANG T
AE XUZHOU SAICHENG CYBER SERVICE CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202032218L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a monitoring module comprising a sensor module, a camera shooting module and an acousto-optic alarm module. The sensor module comprises a temperature sensor unit, a humidity sensor unit, an illumination sensor unit and a carbon dioxide concentration sensor unit. A drive module comprises a motor module and a storage module. A main control module comprises an integrated information processing module, an IDC connection module, a water pump spraying module, an illumination module, a fan module, a heating module and a gateway module. A user end module comprises a registering module, an information transceiving module, an information display module and an alarm prompting module. A simulation module comprises a data collecting module, a simulation and modeling module and a model storage module.
   USE - Intelligent agricultural greenhouse management system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can realize real-time monitoring of environment state in an agricultural greenhouse and integrated management so as to improve automation level and production efficiency and reduces staff, and is clear and complete, and has simple structural design and wide practicability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of an intelligent agricultural greenhouse management system (Drawing includes Non-English language text).
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); T01 (Digital Computers); T06 (Process and Machine Control)

PT P
PN CN110993461-A
TI Automatic electric leakage power cut-off switch, has shell provided with transmission chamber that is provided with transmission mechanism, and pin column provided with rotating block that is provided with metal block.
AU 
AE PUJIANG TANPU ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (PUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202031865B
AB    NOVELTY - The switch has a shell, provided with a connecting cavity that is fixed with a moving mechanism. A rotating shaft is fixed with a take-up wheel and a friction wheel. A rear end wall of the connecting cavity is fixed with a fixing block. A live wire is fixed with a connecting block that is connected with the fixing block. A right end wall of the connecting cavity is fixed with a telescopic spring. The shell is provided with a transmission chamber that is provided with a transmission mechanism. A pin column is provided with a rotating block that is provided with a metal block.
   USE - Automatic electric leakage power cut-off switch.

   USE - Used as hospital urine in-situ separation, collection and processing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device generates high oxidizing active free radicals, oxidatively degrades and removes antibiotics in urine, generates carbon dioxide and water, achieves deep removal of antibiotics, ensures safety of discharged urine entering into the natural circulation and avoids the cyclic accumulation of antibiotics.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the hospital urine in-situ separation, collection and processing device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The automatic dosing unit is capable of automatically dropping hydrogen peroxide solution, or titanium dioxide suspension device, and passed hydrogen oxide solution, or titanium dioxide suspension reacts with ultraviolet light to generate active agents with active oxygen radicals. The urine processing unit includes a containing body constituting a main body space, a partition for separating urine flow, and an ultraviolet lamp for urine processing. The inside of the containing body is a main body space that contains raw urine. Multiple partitions are arranged in an array in the main body space and the top surface of the adjacent partition and the containing body or the bottom surface forms a misaligned opening structure. A side wall of the main body between adjacent partitions is provided with an ultraviolet lamp for performing ultraviolet irradiation on the raw urine in the main body space. The wavelength emitted by the ultraviolet lamp is 220-380 nm. The partition divides the main body space into a relatively independent processing chamber and adjacent processing chambers form continuous flow channels through the opening structure. One ultraviolet lamp is arranged in each chamber, and the light intensity of the ultraviolet lamp is not lower than 2 mV/cm2. The raw urine enters the body space and is oxidatively degraded along the re-entrant flow path of the processing chamber one by one. An automatic dosing unit is connected above the processing chamber. The automatic dosing unit sets an introduction tube for dosing according to the entrance position of a single processing chamber. A hydrogen peroxide solution or a titanium dioxide suspension is added through the introduction tube. The concentration of the active agent in the separate processing chamber is not less than 10 mM. One end of the urine collection channel communicates with the station urinal, and the other end is connected with an electronic shunt valve. The electronic diverter valve is a three-way electronic diverter valve, and one side of the electronic diverter valve is connected to a urine collection channel. One side is connected to the flushing channel and the other is connected to the urine transport channel. The side of the urine conveying channel opposite to the electronic diverter valve communicates with the urine processing unit. One end of the urine collection channel is connected to a squat toilet, and the other end is connected to a urine processing unit. The urine collection channel is provided with an electronic one-way valve that controls the opening and closing of the second urine collection channel. The stand urinal or the squat urinal is provided with an infrared sensor for sensing the distance of the human body. The infrared sensor is connected to the electronic diverter valve or the electronic check valve through a control unit.

Preferred                  Composition: The active solution comprises 1-3                  %mass potassium carbonate, 2-3 %mass copper                  chloride and 0.1-0.15 %mass palladium chloride.                  The activated carbon comprises 5-10 %mass                  aluminum hydroxide.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The purifying process further comprises before introducing carbon dioxide, concentrating the lithium ion-containing solution to make the lithium ion concentration in the lithium ion-containing solution greater than or equal to 25 g/l, or adjusting the lithium ion-containing solution to alkaline. In the process, applying the potential to the electrochemical reaction is 0.2-1.5 V, and the time for applying the potential is 1.5-8 hours, in the purification process, the lithium ion-containing solution further comprises the nickel ions, the cobalt ions, and the manganese ions, performing the electrochemical reaction, adjusting the applied potential, and leaching lithium ions, nickel ions, cobalt ions, and manganese ions. In the process, performing the electrochemical reaction, adjusting the applied potential to 0.9-1.4 V to leach the lithium ions, and adjusting the applied potential to 0.2-0.5 V to leach cobalt ions and nickel ions. The purification further comprises crystallizing, washing, and drying the obtained lithium salt precipitate in order to obtain purified lithium salt, using the remaining solution cyclically as the electrolytic solution, or adjusting the molar ratio of the cobalt ion, the nickel ion, and the manganese ion in the remaining solution to 1:1:1, preferably 8:1:1, introducing carbon dioxide and subjecting carbon dioxide to pressure and heat treatment. The manufacture of cathode comprises taking the waste lithium battery electrode material, disassembling, calcining, washing, drying, grinding and pressing to obtain the cathode, where the thickness of the cathode is 5-10 mm, and the compacted density of the cathode is 5-10 g/cm3, where the cathode is lithium battery anode material, used lithium battery cathode material, and the inert electrode is graphite electrode or conductive inert metal electrode, the electrolyte is an inorganic salt containing sodium ion, ammonium ion, potassium ion, lithium ion, ferrous ion, manganese ion, nickel ion, cobalt ions, aluminum ion, and the concentration of the inorganic salt in the electrolyte is 0.1-1.0 mol/l. Preferred Components: The lithium ion-containing solution further comprises nickel ion, cobalt ion, and/or manganese ion.

The dried red mud                  has 2-20 wt.% water content and a temperature of                  50-80 degrees C. Preferred Device: The disperser                  is arranged in the reaction tower, and the                  disperser is a high speed rotating blade                  structure or a dent structure. The dust removing                  device is a bag filter.

PN CN110283787-A
TI Preparation of dendritic cell tumor vaccine used            e.g. for preventing tumor, by adding tumor cell body to            serum-containing Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium or            RPMI-1640 cell culture medium, culturing, centrifuging,            re-suspending and sorting.
AU WANG J
   FU Y
   LI Y
   LAN H
   ZHANG W
AE UNIV GUANGZHOU CHINESE MEDICINE (UYGC-C)
GA 201984111Y
AB    NOVELTY - A dendritic cell tumor vaccine is prepared by                adding tumor cell body to serum-containing                Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM) or                RPMI-1640 cell culture medium, adding 1-30 mu M                platycodin D, placing into culture tank with 5%                carbon dioxide, culturing at 37 degrees C for more                than 2 hours, trypsin digesting cultured tumor                cells, adding dendritic cells for more than 2 hours                according to the number of tumor cells and number                of dendritic cells at 1:5-5:1, continuously                culturing by adding LPS with 100-300 ng/ml                lipopolysaccharide, changing dendritic cells from                adherent state to suspended state, absorbing                dendritic cell suspension, centrifuging at 1000 g,                removing culture liquid, adding DMEM,                re-suspending, sorting and purifying by using                magnetic cell sorting or flow-sorting to obtain                mature dendritic cell, and adding isotonic saline                or phosphate buffer or serum-free DMEM cell culture                solution. The concentration of mature dendritic                cells is 50-500 thousand/ml.
   USE - The method is used for preparing dendritic                cell tumor vaccine for promoting tumor antigen and                preventing tumor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The dendritic cell is                  prepared by taking bone marrow-derived or                  spleen-derived or hematogenous dendritic cell in                  vitro culture, and amplifying using                  granulocyte-macrophage colony stimulating factor                  (GM-CSF) and interleukin-4 (IL-4).
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Vaccine.

   WU Y
   LI M

GA 201991671L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves extending an end of a lead wire from a liquid storage tube. The lead wire is connected with a heating device. A constant shear sheet is completely entered into the liquid storage tube. A filling valve is screwed. The filling valve is tightened by disassembling a release pipe to measure resistance. Carbon dioxide is filled into an induced cracking device. A down-the-hole is drilled by a drilling machine, where depth of the down-the-hole is controlled at 1-3 m. The induced cracking device is inserted into a blasting hole. The filling valve is detachably connected with the release pipe.

PN CN110028761-A
TI Preparing composite microporous dielectric material useful for electronic device packaging, comprises e.g. stirring epoxy resin and acetone, diluting, stirring and mixing concentrated liquid and the self-made block copolymer and drying.
AU YIN K
AE CHANGZHOU XUNWANDE ELECTRONICS CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019651261
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite microporous dielectric material comprises (i) stirring epoxy resin and acetone and diluting at a mass ratio of 1:4-1:6 to obtain a diluent; (ii) taking 80-100 diluent, 4-6 modified graphene oxide, 8-10 glycerol, 8-10 oleic acid, 10-15 aluminum alkoxide, 10-15 silicate, ultrasonically dispersing, heating and stirring, and concentrating to obtain a concentrated solution; (iii) stirring and mixing the concentrated liquid and the self-made block copolymer in a mass ratio of 5:1-10:1, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas during the stirring process to obtain a concentrated liquid; (iv) mixing the obtained concentrated solution and dilute acid solution at a mass ratio of 1:1-3:1, and shearing at a high speed to obtain an emulsion; and (v) placing the obtained emulsion in a closed container, heating and maintaining the reaction, allowing layer to stand, removing aqueous phase, and drying.
   USE - The preparing composite microporous dielectric material is useful for electronic device packaging (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The epoxy resin is epoxy resin E-41, epoxy resin E-42, or epoxy resin E-44. The aluminum alkoxide is aluminum ethoxide or aluminum isopropoxide. The silicate is methyl orthosilicate, tetraethyl orthosilicate, or propyl orthosilicate. In the step (iv), the dilute acid solution is 3-5% dilute hydrochloric acid, 8-10% dilute acetic acid, 3-5% dilute nitric acid, 3-5% dilute sulfuric acid, 8-10% dilute citric acid, 8-10% dilute phosphoric acid, and 8-10% dilute oxalic acid. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the modified graphene oxide is modified by ultrasonically dispersing graphene oxide and acetone in a mass ratio of 1:8-1:10, adding 8-10% isocyanate, stirring under heating, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain a modified graphene oxide. The self-made block copolymer is prepared by mixing (N-fluorenyl)dodecyl acrylamide and polyethylene glycol phosphate in a mass ratio of 10:1 to 20:1, adding a mass ratio of polyethylene glycol phosphate of 0.1-0.2 times p-dichlorobenzene and polyethylene glycol phosphate mass of 0.07-0.10 times ferrocene and stirring. In the step (v), the heating and holding reaction is carried out in high pressure reactor, the pressure is maintained for 2-3 hours under the conditions of a temperature of 135-140 degrees C and a pressure of 1.6-2 MPa. In the step (iv), the high speed shear dispersion is carried out in mixer at a speed of 8000-12000 revolutions/minute and the high-speed shear dispersion for 20-30 minutes.

PT P
PN CN109833872-A; CN109833872-B
TI Preparation of cobalt oxide bulk catalyst of regulating product distribution for carbon monoxide production involves dissolving cobalt acetate in ethylene glycol, heating, adding aqueous potassium carbonate, washing and drying.
AU GONG J
   YANG C
   MU R
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019524757
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cobalt oxide bulk catalyst of regulating product distribution involves dissolving the cobalt acetate in ethylene glycol to form a 0.5-1.5 M/L mixed solution and heating the mixed solution at 150-200 degrees C, dissolving a 0.5-1 M/L potassium carbonate aqueous solution drop wise to a mixed solution of cobalt acetate and ethylene glycol at a rate of 20-50 drops/min, the mass ratio of the potassium carbonate solution and the mixed solution of cobalt acetate and ethylene glycol is 1:5-1:3, hydrothermal reaction at 150-200 degrees C for 0-3 hours to form suspension, centrifuging, washing and drying under vacuum at 80-100 degrees C, calcining at 400-500 degrees C for 2-6 hours.
   USE - Preparation of cobalt oxide bulk catalyst of regulating product distribution for the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide and/or methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The cobalt oxide bulk catalyst has simple structure, convenient preparation, low operating pressure, and long-term stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred composition: The concentration of the mixed solution of cobalt acetate and ethylene glycol is preferably 1 M/L. The concentration of the aqueous potassium carbonate solution is preferably 0.5 M/L. Preferred Conditions: The heating temperature is 180 degrees C. The temperature of vacuum drying is 80 degrees C. The vacuum drying is carrying out in vacuum oven. The calcining temperature is 450 degrees C for 4 hours. Preferred Process: Production of carbon monoxide and/or methane involves subjecting the cobalt oxide catalyst to tableting to form 20-40 mesh particulate catalyst, charging the particulate into a fixed bed reactor, heating the fixed bed reactor gas at 250-350 degrees C, passing the reaction gas, the molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 2-3, the equilibrium gas is nitrogen, and the reaction space velocity based on carbon dioxide is 1-1 hour-1.

PT P
PN CN109796003-A; CN109796003-B
TI Controlling directionally coal-based hard carbon            surface oxygen functional groups useful for storing            sodium negative electrodes comprises carbonizing thin            coal powder, ball milling carbonization coke, acid            cleaning and drying.
AU SUN F
   WANG L
   WANG H
   GAO J
   PI X
   QU Z
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201947442X
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling directionally coal-based hard                carbon surface oxygen functional groups comprises                (i) refining the raw material, crushing coal raw                material, grinding and sieving to obtain the                refined coal powder, (ii) carbonizing thin coal                powder at high temperature, placing in the                atmosphere furnace, performing carbonization                treatment in an inert atmosphere and naturally                cooling to room temperature to obtain high                temperature carbonization coke, (iii) ball milling                the high temperature carbonization coke in ball                mill and nitrogen atmosphere or carbon dioxide                atmosphere to obtain ball milled product, (iv) acid                cleaning and drying the ball milling for 2~5 times                and water washing for 2-5 times to obtain the ball                milling product washing and drying.
   USE - The controlling directionally coal-based hard                carbon surface oxygen functional groups is useful                for storing sodium negative electrodes                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adjusts the content and type of                oxygen functional group, realizes the                high-efficiency loading of oxygen-containing group                without causing waste liquid pollution and is                suitable for the orientation control of oxygen                functional groups on coal-based hard carbon                surface.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  particle size of the refined coal powder in the                  first step is 20 to 100 mesh. In the step (i) ,                  the coal raw material is lignite, bituminous                  coal, sub-bituminous coal and/or anthracite coal.                  In the step (ii) the inert atmosphere is high                  purity nitrogen or high purity argon. In the step                  (iv) , the wash liquid in wash liquid is dilute                  hydrochloric acid or nitric acid and the                  concentration of the wash liquid is 0.01-2 mol/l.                  Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  carbonization is one-step carbonization or                  two-step carbonization. In the step (i), the                  carbonization comprises heating at 1000-1700                  degrees C in speed of 0.1-30 degrees C/minutes                  and maintaining to 0.5-10 hours. In the step                  (ii), the carbonization comprises heating at                  80-600 degrees C and maintaining for 0.1-50                  degrees C/minutes to 24 hours, heating to                  1000-1700 degrees C in speed of 0.1-30 degrees                  c/minutes and maintaining for 0.5-10 hours .In                  the step (iii) the ball milling rotating speed of                  the ball mill is 100-1000 revolutions/minute for                  6-100 hours, ball milling time is made from                  agate, corundum or stainless steel. In the step                  (iii) the atmosphere in the ball mill tank is                  obtained by directly introducing gas or adding                  corresponding liquid or solid phase materials,                  when the atmosphere in the ball milling tank is                  nitrogen atmosphere, adding liquid phase is                  liquid nitrogen, the atmosphere of ball milling                  tank is carbon dioxide atmosphere, adding liquid                  phase substance is dry ice. In the step (iv) ,                  drying temperature is 60~100 degrees C and drying                  time is 5-12 hours.

PN CN109504130-A; CN109504130-B
TI Preparation of nano calcium carbonate for            silane-terminated polyurethane sealant, involves            dehydrating modified calcium carbonate suspension by            pressure filtration, drying, and pulverizing to obtain            nano calcium carbonate product.
AU LAN Q
   ZHU Y
   LIU L
   LIAO J
AE ANHUI XUANCHENG HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHN (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201928640N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano calcium carbonate involves                adjusting the specific gravity of the refined lime                milk to 1.060-1.080, introducing into the bubbling                carbonation reactor, introducing a carbon dioxide                mixed gas for carbonation reaction, but stopping                the reaction in the reaction solution at pH of 7.0                to obtain a nano calcium carbonate suspension,                heating (2) nano-calcium carbonate suspension to                75-85 degrees C, and preparing surface treatment                agent by compounding trimethylsilanol,                dimethylformamide and a fatty acid salt, and adding                surface treatment agent in an amount of 4.0-5.0% of                the dry mass of the calcium carbonate in the                suspension, and completing surface modification                after stirring for 30-60 minutes to obtain a                modified calcium carbonate suspension, dehydrating                (3) modified calcium carbonate suspension by                pressure filtration, drying, and pulverizing to                obtain a nano calcium carbonate product.
   USE - Preparation of nano calcium carbonate for                silane-terminated polyurethane sealant.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate can solve the problem of                dispersibility of in the silane-terminated                polyurethane sealant after filling the SPU. The                elongation at break and storage stability of SPU                sealant can be significantly improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The temperature of                  the calcium carbonate reaction is 30-35 degrees                  C, and the concentration of carbon dioxide in the                  mixed gas is 30-35%. Preferred Conditions: The                  drying is carried out in an oven at a temperature                  of 100-110 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN109380762-A
TI New Pectobacterium carotovorum subspecies carotovorum useful for comprehensively extracting passion fruit peel volatile oil and polysaccharide for preparing cigarette product.
AU LI J
   LI R
   WU H
   ZHANG K
AE UNIV YUNNAN (UYUN-C)
GA 2019208660
AB    NOVELTY - A Pectobacterium carotovorum subsp. carotovorum (Pcc) 047 (CCTCC M 2018607), is new, where the Pcc is a Gram-negative bacterium, uniflagellate or has 2-6 flagella, does not contain capsule and its colony is grayish white on lysogeny broth (LB) medium, surface is smooth, slightly convex and translucent and edges are regular.
   USE - The Pcc is useful for comprehensively extracting passion fruit peel volatile oil and polysaccharide for preparing cigarette product.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared volatile oil and polysaccharide improves sensory comfort of cigarette and ensures that the cigarette is pleasant and has natural sweetness and fragrant smoke.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for comprehensively extracting passion fruit peel volatile oil and polysaccharide, which involves (a) cutting fresh passion fruit peel into slices having thickness of less than 2 mm, (b) subjecting the passion fruit peel to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, obtaining passion fruit peel crude oil and residue, dehydrating and dewaxing crude oil to obtain passion fruit peel volatile oil, (c) fermenting residue obtained in step (b) using Pcc and (d) extracting fermentation broth obtained in step (c) to obtain passion fruit peel polysaccharides; and #use of the passion fruit peel volatile oil in preparing cigarette product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction at 50-70 degrees C and 25-35 MPa for 2-3 hours. The step (c) involves mixing passion fruit peel residue with water in a mass ratio of 1:6-1:10, sterilizing, inoculating 0.5-1.5 vol.% Pcc having viable count of 104-106 colony-forming unit (CFU)/ml, fermenting at 35-39 degrees C for 24-48 hours, inactivating the strain, filtering, and centrifuging the filtrate to collect the supernatant and obtain the fermentation broth. The step (d) involves concentrating the fermentation broth to a density of 1.15-1.25 g/ml, adding 3-5 times 95% ethanol, performing alcohol precipitation for 12-20 hours, centrifuging, collecting the precipitate, washing the precipitate with absolute ethanol and drying at 70-80 degrees C to obtain the polysaccharide of passion fruit peel. The cigarette is prepared by diluting volatile oil of the passion fruit peel with a glyceryl succinate to obtain 0.1-1 %mass solution, forming the beads, filling into a filter rod and then preparing cigarette. The cigarette is prepared by formulating polysaccharide with water into 0.001-0.01 %mass solution, uniformly spraying on the shredded tobacco and preparing cigarette.

   USE - The method is useful for smelting of quickly                dephosphorizing molten iron by spraying iron oxide                red at the bottom of converter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solve the technical problems of                slow dephosphorization rate and low material                utilization rate in the dephosphorization process                of the converter, reduces the amount of calcium                oxide by more than 25%, increases the                dephosphorization rate, shortens the                dephosphorization cycle by 15-40%, has obvious                significance for changing the existing                dephosphorization process and improving the                production efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the smelting of quickly dephosphorizing molten                iron by spraying iron oxide red at the bottom of                converter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The smelting of quickly                  dephosphorizing molten iron by spraying iron                  oxide red at the bottom of converter comprising                  (i) selecting spray gun according to furnace                  capacity of converter, vertically arranging spray                  gun at bottom of converter in penetrating mode,                  vertically arranging spray gun nozzle in                  converter melting tank to upwards, (ii) during                  converter smelting process, using spray gun for                  blowing nitrogen for 30-45 seconds, spraying iron                  oxide red powder upward from the bottom of the                  converter to the inside of the converter pool                  through a spray gun, when phosphorus content of                  molten iron is more than 0.09%, where flow rate                  of spraying is 40-45 kgx minute/t for 1-2                  minutes, spraying powder of iron oxide red and                  passivated lime powder, when the phosphorus                  content of the molten iron is 0.06-0.09%, where                  flow rate of spraying is 30-40 kg/minute-1/t for                  4-5 minutes, modifying the passivated lime powder                  when the phosphorus content in molten iron is                  less than 0.06%, where the flow rate of spraying                  is 20-30 kgx minute/t, using nitrogen as carrier                  gas in powder spraying process, and carbon                  dioxide as protective gas, where the nitrogen                  flow is 220-240 Nm3/hour, and the carbon dioxide                  flow is 100-180 Nm3/hour and (iii) spraying                  passivated ash powder for 8-15 minutes to stop                  powder spraying, where the carrier gas and                  protective gas flow remained unchanged, reducing                  the flow of carrier gas and flow of the                  protective gas to 40% until the end of smelting,                  tapping is completed and recovering the gas flow                  to normal.

PN CN109320754-A
TI Preparing porous polyurethane carrier involves adding polyurethane to high temperature reaction kettle, and recting microwave for two to four hours to obtain polyurethane liquid, and adding polyurethane liquid to spray device.
AU ZHANG J
AE ZHANG J (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201917412B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous polyurethane carrier involves adding polyurethane to the high temperature reaction kettle, and recting microwave for 2-4 hours to obtain a polyurethane liquid, adding polyurethane liquid to the spray device, and then uniformly spraying into supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to obtain a suspension, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone to anhydrous ethanol for ultrasonic reaction for 2-4 hours to obtain a dispersed alcohol liquid, uniformly spraying dispersed alcohol solution in the suspension, and then ultrasonically reacting for 2-4 hours to obtain a suspension mixture. The suspension mixture is placed in a vacuum distillation reaction kettle to carry out a gradient pressure reduction reaction until completely evaporated to obtain a precipitate, and then the precipitate is placed in a mold at a constant temperature and constant pressure reaction for 0.5-1 hour to obtain polyurethane precursor.
   USE - Method for preparing porous polyurethane carrier.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare porous polyurethane carrier which has good uniformity of pores.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The polyurethane precursor is subjected to ultrasonic reaction in anhydrous ethanol for 2-3 hours, and then taken out and dried to obtain a porous polyurethane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The microwave reaction has a power of 800-1200 watts and a temperature of 140-160 degrees C. The mass ratio of the polyurethane to the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is 1:5-10, speed is 10-15 mL/minute and the temperature is 140-150 degrees C. The concentration of the polyvinylpyrrolidone in anhydrous ethanol is 40-80 g/L, and ultrasonic frequency is 20-40 kilo Hertz and the temperature is 40-50 degrees C. The volume ratio of the dispersed alcohol liquid to the suspension is 3:2-7, and the uniform spraying spray rate of 1-4mL / minute, the ultrasound frequency ultrasound reaction is 20-40 kilo Hertz, a temperature of 40-60 degrees C. The gradient pressure reduction reaction includes temperature of 50-70 degrees C, 70-80 degrees C, 100-105 degrees C , ratio of pressure and atmospheric pressure 80-90%, 70-80%, and 65% for 2-3 hours, 1-2 hours, 1-2 hours, respectively. The temperature of the constant temperature and constant pressure reaction is 2-4 mega Pasclas, and the pressure is 140-160 degrees C. The cooling rate of the cooling is 5-10 degrees C / minute. The anhydrous ethanol in the step 6 immerses the polyurethane precursor, and the frequency of the ultrasonic reaction is 40-80 kilo Hertz. The temperature is 30-50 degrees C, and the drying temperature is 100-110 degrees C.

PT P
PN US2019023974-A1; US10287486-B2; WO2020068443-A1; CA3112221-A1; CN112752825-A; EP3850054-A1
TI Enhancing oil recovery in hydrocarbon of carbonate            reservoir formation, comprises injecting slug of oil            recovery composition into carbonate reservoir formation            and injecting second solution into formation after            injecting slug of composition.
AU AYIRALA S C
   AL-YOUSEF A A
   AL-SOFI A M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201907762Y
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing oil recovery in a hydrocarbon                containing carbonate reservoir formation,                comprises: injecting a slug of an oil recovery                composition into the carbonate reservoir formation;                and injecting a second solution into the carbonate                reservoir formation after injecting the slug of the                oil recovery composition, where the oil recovery                composition comprises an aqueous solution of at                least one salt having a salinity of 4000-8000 ppm                TDS, the salt comprises at least one of sodium                chloride, calcium chloride, magnesium chloride,                sodium sulfate or magnesium sulfate, the aqueous                solution comprises at least 400 ppm sulfate ions                and 300 ppm or less divalent cations, the divalent                cations comprise calcium and/or magnesium, a                polymer having a concentration of 2500500 ppm, many                metal oxide nanoparticles having a concentration of                0.5-0.1 wt.% and dissolved carbon dioxide in the                aqueous solution.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing oil                recovery in a hydrocarbon containing carbonate                reservoir formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the polymer in the                composition that increases in viscosity of the                aqueous solution, thus providing mobility control                and improving the macroscopic sweep efficiency at                reservoir scale.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide                  nanoparticles comprise silicon dioxide and/or                  aluminum oxide. The aqueous solution comprises at                  least one ion, where the ion comprises at least                  one of calcium, magnesium or sulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  recovering displaced hydrocarbon from the                  carbonate reservoir formation. Injecting a second                  solution into the carbonate reservoir formation                  comprises continuously injecting the second                  solution at an injection rate. The carbonate                  reservoir formation has an in situ oil viscosity                  of less than 3 centipoise. Preferred Components:                  The oil recovery composition comprises aqueous                  solution of the salt having a salinity of                  4000-8000 ppm TDS, and the polymer having a                  concentration of 250-500 ppm, metal oxide                  nanoparticles having a concentration of 0.5-0.1                  wt.%, and dissolved carbon dioxide in the aqueous                  solution. The slug of the oil recovery                  composition has a pore volume of at least 0.3 of                  the carbonate reservoir formation. The second                  solution comprises seawater or the aqueous                  solution. The aqueous solution is a first aqueous                  solution the salt comprises first at least one                  salt, and the second solution comprises a second                  aqueous solution of at least one second salt for                  enhancing oil recovery from the carbonate                  reservoir formation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer comprises                  a copolymer of acrylamide and acrylamido tertiary                  butyl sulfonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109250919-A; CN109250919-B
TI Preparation of thin oxynitride foam glass using laser used e.g. as conventional insulation material, by uniformly mixing E glass powder and nitride powder, melting, pouring in mold with heating function, annealing and cutting.
AU DONG L
   CUI Y
   CHEN C
   BAO S
   JIANG H
AE SUZHOU NORTH AMERICA HIGH SCHOOL (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201910872D
AB    NOVELTY - A thin oxynitride foam glass is prepared using laser by uniformly mixing 80-95 wt.% E glass powder and nitride powder (5-20 wt.% aluminum nitride, silicon nitride and boron nitride), melting under nitrogen or ammonia gas atmosphere at 1500-1700 degrees C, pouring under nitrogen protection in mold with heating function, annealing at 800-950 degrees C to obtain basic oxynitride glass, cutting to thickness of 0.2-10 mm, placing in oxygen partial pressure of 0.02-0.5 MPa, and dynamically scanning glass sheet by laser beam at laser beam diameter of 0.1-10 mm and laser output power of 10-1500 W.
   USE - Method for preparing thin oxynitride foam glass using laser used as conventional insulation material, plasma display barrier wall and high temperature resistant electronic insulation sheets (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has fast process speed and carbon dioxide emission-free, does not add foaming agent, and uniformly distributes nitrogen atom level. The product has uniform small bubbles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The glass has closed cell structure, nitrogen content of 1.70-9.10 wt.%, aperture size of 1-150 mu m, porosity of 30-60% and compressive strength of 2.8-20 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The laser is generated by carbon dioxide laser, solid laser, excimer laser and fiber laser.

PN CN109232923-A
TI Method for dispersing two-dimensional nanomaterials by supercritical fluid, involves using double plunger micropump, inserting nozzle, scanning electron microscopy and projection electron microscopy.
AU ZHAO J
   ZHANG W
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201913308R
AB    NOVELTY - A method involves (1) weighing 1.5g of two-dimensional nanomaterial and put it into a 25mL high pressure reactor, (2) using a double plunger micropump to pass carbon dioxide into the kettle, and using a heating jacket to raise the temperature, raise the temperature to 50 degrees C, increase the pressure to 10 MPa, keep the reaction conditions unchanged, and terminate the reaction after 2 hours, (3) inserting the nozzle into the molten polymethylmethacrylate (PMMA) after the dispersion of the boron nitride nanosheet is completed by the supercritical rapid expansion method (RESS), (4) scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and projection electron microscopy (TEM) were used to characterize the dispersion of two-dimensional nanomaterials before and after RESS treatment and the dispersion of boron nitride nanosheets in PMMA matrix, (5) analyzing and discussing the characterization results of the two-dimensional nanomaterial with similar properties such as boron nitride or molybdenum sulfide.
   USE - Method for dispersing two-dimensional nanomaterials by supercritical fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The structural integrity of boron nitride is very good, and the RESS treatment does not cause great damage to the morphology of boron nitride nanosheets.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); V05 (Valves, Discharge Tubes and CRTs)
MC A04-F06C; A08-M; V05-F01A1B; V05-F01B5

PN WO2019012474-A1; CA3068161-A1; EP3652109-A1; CN111148718-A; JP2020527534-W; IN202027005960-A; EP3652109-A4; US2021147239-A1; IN385242-B; EP4049976-A1
TI Mechanochemically producing exfoliated            nanoparticles for use as e.g., additive for polymers,            by mechanically agitating solid feedstock comprising            carbonaceous and/or mineral-based material and            oxidizing gas in a mechanical agitation unit.
AU SINHA A
AE CARBON UPCYCLING TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON UPCYCLING TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON UPCYCLING TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON UPCYCLING TECHNOLOGIES INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 201907087E
AB    NOVELTY - Exfoliated nanoparticles are mechanochemically                produced by providing solid feedstock comprising                carbonaceous and/or mineral-based material;                providing flow of oxidizing gas; introducing solid                feedstock and flow of oxidizing gas into mechanical                agitation unit; and subjecting material of solid                feedstock in the presence of oxidizing gas to                mechanical agitation operation in mechanical                agitation unit at a pressure of greater than or                equal to 1 atm.
   USE - A mechanochemical process to produce                exfoliated nanoparticles for use as additive for                polymers, additive in coatings, additive in                adhesives, additive in inks, additive in membranes                or in coatings of membranes, in biomedical                applications, in energy storage applications, to                provide quantum dots, in spraying applications, as                adsorbent or absorbent material, or as coating (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be upscaled, does not require                toxic chemicals, allows to sequester carbon dioxide                (CO2) in a stable solid form that is suitable in                various applications, and creates nanoparticles                using waste CO2 and cheap solid feedstock.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                nanoparticles which have Brunauer-Emmett-Teller                (BET) surface area of 10-1000 m2/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Carbonaceous                  material comprises fly ash, bottom ash,                  incinerator ash, graphite, petroleum coke,                  anthracite coke, bituminous coal, and/or                  activated charcoal. Mineral-based material                  comprises olivine, talc, yellowstone, serpentine,                  saw dust, and/or amorphous powder. Oxidizing gas                  comprises oxygen, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen                  dioxide, and/or carbon dioxide. Catalyst                  comprises metal oxide selected from iron oxides,                  cobalt oxides, rhenium oxides, and/or titanium                  oxides. Nanoparticles have a carbon/oxygen (C/O)                  ratio of 1-40. Preferred Processes:                  Mechanochemical agitation operation comprises                  mixing, stirring (low-speed stirring or                  high-speed stirring), shearing (high-torque                  shearing), shaking, blending, or ultrasonication.                  The process further comprises addition of a                  catalyst to solid feedstock. The process further                  comprises introducing greater than or equal to 1                  intercalant agent comprising an acid selected                  from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, and/or                  nitric acid. Preferred Properties: Nanoparticles                  have a D50 of 20 nm to 10 mu m. Nanoparticles                  have a bimodal distribution with a 1st set of                  nanoparticles having a particle size of 100-300                  nm and a 2nd set of nanoparticles having a                  particle size of 1000-5000 nm. Nanoparticles show                  a release of carbon dioxide (CO2) of 5-35 wt.% of                  the mass of the nanoparticles.
DC A60 (Additives and Compounding Agents If the usage is very restricted it may be classified under the individual polymer or process involved.); G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).); G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. PN CN109049206-A
TI Method for preparing rattan vase, involves utilizing fresh willow wood followed by processing into board, naturally drying freshly harvested rattan, processing, placing treated board and processed rattan in dryer and knitting into vase.
AU NI H
AE NI H (NIHH-Individual)
GA 201901016N
AB    NOVELTY - A rattan vase preparation method involves utilizing fresh willow wood, processing the willow wood into round or rectangular wooden board, opening a through hole around the board, soaking the board in preservative solution for 1-2 hours, naturally drying freshly harvested rattan, polishing to obtain smooth surface, placing in pressure tank, pressurizing at 3-3.5 MPa, along with mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide, slowly reducing pressure in the tank to standard atmospheric pressure within 10 minutes, pressurizing at 0.13-0.18 MPa using hot steam, opening the tank, after 20 minutes, removing the rattan, placing the treated wooden board and processed rattan in dryer, knitting into vase on the wooden board using rattan, spraying distilled water to wet the vase and drying using dryer.
   USE - Method for preparing rattan vase.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing rattan vase with improved corrosion resistance and oxidation resistance and suitability for utilizing near equator, withstanding long-term continuity of high-temperature baking and high intensity UV radiation and controlling humus bacteria in soil, when utilized as flowering vase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The preservative solution (1 kg) is prepared by adding 20 g kale, 20 g wild Chrysanthemum, 5 g Eucommia bark, 5 g goldthread root, 8 g fried fennel, 16 g myrtle, 12 g mint leaves, 13.5 g Acorus gramineus, 13.5 g Glochidion eriocarpum roots, 22 g papaya leaf, 4.4 g Lysimachia alfredii, 4.4 g vinegar processed cinnamon, 27 g fructus viticis extract, 27 g gecko extract, 5 g mustard seeds and 5 g cotton seeds to 1000 g yellow wine, processing for 1-2 hours, filtering and adding distilled water to the filtrate. The hot steam is produced by steaming 2 g Corydalis turtschaninovii, 4 g Andrographis paniculata, 5 g Eclipta prostrata, 5 g hawthorn, 3 g dodder and 8 g Radix isatidis.
DC P63 (Working, preserving wood (B27).)

PN CN109025938-A; CN109025938-B
TI Coal mine underground stage combustion shock induced cracking coal body intensified gas extraction method, involves closing gas extraction pipe, and performing impact compression process such that coal forms fracture network.
AU LIN B
   ZHAO Y
   KONG J
   SONG H
   LIU T
   LI Q
   ZHENG Y
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2018A5057N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting combustible gas and auxiliary gas into a combustion chamber. The combustible gas in the combustion chamber is ignited by a control system. Shock wave produced by burning of the combustible gas is introduced into a perforated cylinder by a shock wave leading-in pipe. A piston is moved along a gas injection pipe. A gas extraction pipe is opened to continuously inject a nitrogen or carbon dioxide extrusion piston into an impact drilling hole to reset the piston. The gas extraction pipe is closed. Multi-stage impact compression process is performed such that coal around the drilling hole forms a fracture network.
   USE - Coal mine underground stage combustion shock induced cracking coal body intensified gas extraction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables promoting desorption of coal by high-temperature and high-pressure heat shock wave residual heat and improving gas extraction efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a coal mine underground stage combustion shock induced cracking coal body intensified gas extraction system.

PT P
PN DE102017209848-A1
TI Thermal decomposition of gaseous oxide for forming gaseous product e.g. hydrogen, involves contacting reduced metal oxide with gaseous oxide, oxidizing metal oxide, and reducing gaseous oxide to form product with reduced partial pressure.
AU LANGE M
   BULFIN B
   ROEB M
   VON STORCH H DE
AE DEUT ZENT LUFT & RAUMFAHRT EV (DELF-C)
GA 201898709M
AB    NOVELTY - Thermal decomposition of gaseous oxide in formation of gaseous product at process temperature in thermochemical cycle involves thermally-reducing (i) a metal oxide to release oxygen, and contacting (ii) the reduced metal oxide with a gaseous oxide, oxidizing the metal oxide to absorb oxygen, and reducing the gaseous oxide to release oxygen and to form a gaseous product. The partial pressure of the gaseous product relative to the partial pressure of the gaseous oxide in the oxidation process is reduced.
   USE - Thermal decomposition of gaseous oxide e.g. water, carbon dioxide and sulfur trioxide, for forming gaseous product e.g. hydrogen, carbon monoxide and sulfur dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical thermal decomposition of gaseous oxide with improved efficiency by reducing partial pressure of the gaseous product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) reactor; and
   (2) use of device for reducing hydrogen partial pressure in thermochemical cycle for thermal decomposition of water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the reactor for thermal decomposition of gaseous oxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The oxygen partial pressure resulting from process (i) is reduced by release of oxygen. The partial pressure of the gaseous product is reduced in proportion to the partial pressure of the gaseous oxide in the oxidation process, by increasing the partial pressure of the gaseous oxide or lowering the partial pressure of the gaseous product, preferably using a membrane, a vacuum pump and/or a material capable of binding the gaseous product. The oxidation process is performed using a hydride-forming material, preferably hydride-forming metal, as material for binding the gaseous product. The ratio of the partial pressure of the gaseous product with respect to the partial pressure of the gaseous oxide in the oxidation process is 10 to 10-10. The ratio of the partial pressure of the gaseous product with respect to the oxygen partial pressure in the reduction process is 10 to 10. The process (i) is performed at 900-2000 degrees C. The process (ii) is performed at 200-1500 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The metal oxide comprises element(s) chosen from calcium, manganese, magnesium, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc, strontium, tin, barium, lead, and rare earth metal.

PT P
PN CN108975334-A
TI Preparing S-doped microporous activated carbon, by carbonizing polystyrene sulfonate, washing with hydrochloric acid to remove acid-soluble impurities, drying, dissolving zinc chloride in deionized water, immersing, activating and drying.
AU INVENTOR U
AE QIDONG CHUANGLV LANDSCAPE ENG CO LTD (QIDO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A2643R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing S-doped microporous activated carbon, involves (i) carbonizing a polystyrene sulfonate at 780-790 degrees C for 50-60 minutes under the protection of nitrogen, washing the carbonized material with 8 vol.% hydrochloric acid to remove acid-soluble impurities, washing using deionized water to be neutral, drying at 60-70 degrees C to obtain a sample and recording the sample as a carbon source, and (ii) dissolving 20-30 pts. wt. zinc chloride in 65-75 pts. wt. deionized water, after dissolving, adding 7-9 pts. wt. carbon source, stirring, immersing for 11-13 hours, then putting the materials into the tubular furnace, activating at 785-795 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours, washing the activated materials using 8% hydrochloric acid and deionized water and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing the S-doped microporous activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides S-doped activated carbon, which is prepared by simple and rapid method on a large scale under easy operation, has a microporous structure and exhibits high adsorption performance for carbon dioxide and methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The immersing step is carried out for 12 hours. The activating step is carried out at 790 degrees C for 2 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The polystyrene sulfonate is carbonized at 785 degrees C for 55 minutes under the protection of nitrogen. The drying of step (i) is carried out at 65 degrees C.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

   ONEAL E J
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)

GA 201891141R
AB    NOVELTY - Metal monolith comprises a monolithic support constructed via additive manufacturing comprising a metal or metal alloy, where the monolithic support comprises a multiple of cells with channels extending, and where the monolithic support has a melting point greater than 1200 degrees C.
   USE - Metal monolith.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for activating the metal monolith, which involves exposing the metal monolith to a cyclic oxidative and reducing environment for a sufficient number of cycles to activate the metal monolith; and
   (2) a method for reforming a feed, which involves activating the metal monolith of claim 1 by either coating with a catalyst or exposing the metal monolith to a cyclic oxidative and reducing environment for a sufficient number of cycles to activate the metal monolith and introducing a reforming feed to the metal monolith in the presence of heat, where the reforming feed comprises hydrocarbons and steam, carbon dioxide, or a combination producing CO and H2; and introducing a combustion feed to the metal monolith comprising O2 or a combination of O2 and N2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The multiple of cells with channels form squares, circles, ovals, hexagons, trilobes, quadrolobes, fractals, or a combination. The multiple of cells with channels comprises channels with non-linear pathways through the monolith. The catalyst is a zeolite. The catalyst comprises at least one of metal nanoparticles and microparticles. The metal or metal alloy comprises a Group 10 element. The Group 10 element comprises one of nickel, platinum, and palladium. The monolithic support comprises a metal alloy and the metal alloy is one of an Inconel, Hastelloy, and Monel variant. The oxidative environment comprises at least one of oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, water, combustion byproducts, peroxide, ozone, permanganate, organic acids, halides, or combinations. The reducing environment comprises at least one of methane, ethane, propane, butane, higher C number paraffins, ethylene, propylene, butylene, higher C number olefins, acetylene, methylacetylene-propadiene (MAPD), hydrogen, carbon monoxide, hydrides, hydrogen sulfide, or combinations. The reducing environment comprises at least one of methane, ethane, propane, butane, higher C number paraffins, ethylene, propylene, butylene, higher C number olefins, acetylene, methylacetylene-propadiene (MAPD), hydrogen, carbon monoxide, hydrides, hydrogen sulfide, or combinations. The reducing environment comprises steam and hydrocarbons. The reforming feed comprises methane. Preferred Compositions: The monolith further comprises void spaces interrupting the channels forming chambers within the monolith and a catalyst coating. Preferred Conditions; The monolithic support has a volumetric heat capacity greater than 3.5 J/cc/K. The metal component is exposed to a to a cyclic oxidative and reducing environment includes 5 to 300 cycles.

PN CN108822304-A
TI Classifying lignin in gama-valerolactone/water            system, involves weighing certain mass of lignin having            high molecular weight dispersibility according to            solid-liquid ratio which is added to            gama-valerolactone/aqueous solution.
AU WANG G
   SI C
   LIU X
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 201894698D
AB    NOVELTY - Classifying lignin involves weighing a certain                mass of lignin having a high molecular weight                dispersibility according to a solid-liquid ratio of                1:15-1:30 which is added to a gamma                -valerolactone/aqueous solution having a volume                concentration of 60-80 wt.% to dissolved all                lignin. The water is added to the lignin solution                to a volume concentration of 45-40 wt.% of                gama-valerolactone, and precipitated first fraction                (F1), after centrifugation, and added water to the                gamma -valerolactone volume concentration of 30-25                wt.%, sinked second fraction which is the medium                molecular weight lignin fraction. The remaining                supernatant is continuously added to the gamma                -valerolactone volume concentration of 5-3 wt.%,                and the third grade is precipitated in a small                molecular weight lignin fraction, after                classification. The gamma -valerolactone is                recovered by steaming off water, or recovered by                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   USE - Method for classifying lignin in                gama-valerolactone/water system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to classifying lignin which                is helps to realizes effective classification of                lignin having high molecular weight dispersibility,                and improves uniformity of molecular weight.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high molecular                  weight dispersible high lignin extraction raw                  material is a dilute acid pretreated                  lignocellulosic raw material which includes steam                  explosion pretreatment, dilute acid pretreatment                  and steam prehydrolysis treatment. The extraction                  of high molecular weight dispersibility high                  lignin includes alkali cooking extraction and                  ethanol cooking extraction.

PN CN108782047-A
TI Promoting rapid increase in weight of saffron bulb, by selecting saffron bulb without mildew and lesions, sterilizing using formaldehyde solution, planting in greenhouse, subjecting to semi-light treatment, and spraying nutrient solution.
AU CHEN D
   LIN X
   YANG H
   MA X
   HE Z
AE WUHAN YEDONGLI BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201892986V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for promoting a rapid increase in the weight of a saffron bulb, involves (a) selecting the saffron bulb and removing saffron with mildew and lesions, where the saffron bulb are chosen from small bulbs formed by propagation of the saffron bulb after picking process, (b) sterilizing using 0.15% formaldehyde solution and placing the bulb in the planting greenhouse and (c) subjecting the sterilized material in the greenhouse to the semi-light treatment at 18 degrees C under the light condition and 12 degrees C under without light condition, maintaining the ventilation in the greenhouse, applying carbon dioxide at a concentration of not greater than 600 ppm for 0.2-0.4 hour, spraying the standard test nutrient solution to the bulb once a week until the end of the flowering period of the saffron bulb.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting a rapid increase in the weight of a saffron bulb.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides enriched nutrients, increases the reproduction rate and quality of saffron bulb.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves applying 80-100 kg/acre prepared field cultivation base fertilizer in the field, where the composition is prepared by mixing 1-3 pts. wt. bean cake, 8-10 pts. wt. silkworm feces and 5-8 pts. wt. rabbit feces and the moisture content of the field cultivation base fertilizer is 3-7%. The step (c) involves performing semi-light treatment for 12 hours under light conditions and without light conditions and spraying the distilled water of the safflower bulb in the greenhouse by using an air mist spray method and maintaining the humidity in the box to 75-85%. In step (d), the pre-treatment of the selected saffron seed ball is performed using 25% carbendazim and 1000 times liquid mist spray. The saffron bulb are placed in the planting greenhouse at a line spacing of 2x 2 cm.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PT P
PN CN108455598-A
TI Preparing high performance activated carbon rich            in micropores using antibiotic bacterial slag comprises            e.g. providing to be treated antibiotic bacterial slag            with water, adjusting the pH with lye, stirring,            allowing to stand and filtering.
AU HONG C
   XING Y
   LI Y
   WANG Z
   FENG L
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201869170B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high performance activated carbon                rich in micropores using antibiotic bacterial slag                comprises (i) providing to be treated antibiotic                bacterial slag with water content of 70-90%,                adjusting the pH to 8-10 with lye, stirring for                0.5-2 hours, allowing to stand for 6-10 hours, and                filtering, (ii) vacuum drying filtered solid                substance at 30-70 degrees C to obtain dry powder                of bacterial residue, (iii) grinding bacterial                residue to powder of 0.1-2 mm particles, and (iv)                heating dry powder particles of the slag to 400-550                degrees C in a tubular furnace at a heating rate of                10-20 degrees C/minute, pyrolyzing for 3-6 hours in                an carbon dioxide atmosphere, and obtaining                activated carbon rich in micropores.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high                performance activated carbon rich in micropores                using antibiotic bacterial slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the                preparation of high performance activated carbon                rich in micropores using antibiotic bacterial slag                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i)                  antibiotic bacteria slag is penicillin V                  potassium, gentamicin, or streptomycin antibiotic                  bacteria slag.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lye is sodium                  hydroxide, or potassium hydroxide. In the step                  (i), lye is calcium hydroxide, sodium chloride or                  potassium chloride with mol ratio of 0.5-0.6:1.                  The lye concentration is 0.2-2 mol/l in terms of                  hydroxy-concentration.

PN WO2018144840-A1; AR111149-A1; CA3052619-A1; CN110234619-A; EP3577096-A1; BR112019015047-A2; US2020123078-A1; US10703689-B2; ZA201905747-A; EP3577096-B1; CN110234619-B; ES2908727-T3
TI Converting feed stream into hydrocarbons by            contacting feed stream comprising hydrogen and carbon            containing component with hybrid catalyst comprising            methanol synthesis component and solid microporous acid            material and increasing pressure.
AU NIESKENS D L S
   SANDIKCI A C
   GROENENDIJK P E
   MALEK A
   MAREK A
   NIESKENS D L
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 201861977K
AB    NOVELTY - Process for converting a feed stream into 2-5C                hydrocarbons involves (i) introducing a feed stream                comprising hydrogen and at least one carbon                containing component chosen from carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide and their mixture into a reaction                zone at an initial reactor pressure and an initial                reactor temperature, (ii) contacting the feed                stream with a hybrid catalyst positioned in the                reaction zone, where the hybrid catalyst comprises                a methanol synthesis component and a solid                microporous acid material and (iii) increasing a                pressure within the reaction zone during the step                (ii) from the initial reactor pressure to a final                reactor pressure, where the temperature within the                reaction zone at any time during the step (ii) is                within plus minus 20 degrees C of the initial                reactor temperature.
   USE - The process is used for converting the feed                stream into 2-5C hydrocarbons (claimed), which is                used as starting materials to produce plastics,                fuels and various downstream chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves activity of hybrid                catalyst to convert carbon in carbon-containing                feed streams, such as syngas, into light                hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The methanol synthesis                  component comprises copper, Zinc, chromium and                  aluminum in either metallic or oxidic form. The                  solid microporous acid material is chosen from                  molecular sieves having 8-MR access and a                  framework type chosen from framework types CHA,                  AET, AFX, ERI, LTA, UFI, RTH and their                  combinations, where the framework types                  correspond to the naming convention of the                  International Zeolite Association.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The final reactor                  pressure is at least 1 bar (0.1 MPa), preferably                  1-60 bar (0.1-6.0 MPa) greater than the initial                  reactor pressure. The initial reactor pressure is                  15-60 bar (1.5-6.0 MPa) and the final reactor                  pressure is 16-120 bar (1.6-12.0 MPa). The                  initial reactor temperature is 350-450 degrees C.                  The feed stream has a flow rate of greater than                  500 per hour. Preferred Condition: The                  temperature within the reaction zone at any time                  during the step (ii) is within plus minus 10                  degrees C of the initial reactor temperature.                  Preferred Method: The process involves increasing                  the initial reactor pressure to the final reactor                  pressure by at least 1 bar (0.1 MPa) at intervals                  of at least 12 hours, preferably 12-48 hours                  within the reaction zone during the step (ii).                  The process involves increasing the pressure by                  at least 5 bar (0.5 MPa) within the reaction zone                  during the step (ii) when a percentage yield of                  desired products reaches a predetermined                  threshold value.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108315501-A
TI Sulfur-free sugarcane clarification by pressing            mixed juice fine filtrate, adding in waste molasses            clarification reflow tank, filtering, heating,            precipitating, and mixing mixed juice with lime milk            and calcium hydrogen phosphate.
AU BAI J
AE BAI J (BAIJ-Individual)
GA 201859612W
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfur-free sugarcane clarification comprises                adding a mixed juice fine filtrate in a pressing                section of a clarifying equipment, and adding in a                waste molasses clarification reflow tank; and                pressing mixed juice at a pH value of 6.8-7.3,                filtering, heating to 40-55 degrees C, adding the                juice in precipitation reaction container tank,                mixing the mixed juice with a lime milk and calcium                hydrogen phosphate at a pH of 9.5-11, sending the                juice in a first reaction vessel, subjecting to                multi-layer and multi-stage dicalcium phosphate                under the effect of forming nano-hydroxy calcium                phosphate particles, controlling pH value at 10-11,                sending the juice to a second reaction vessel,                controlling pH value at 10-11, sending the juice to                a carbon dioxide inflator to obtain a pH of                7.2-7.5, heating, precipitating, and controlling pH                value at 6.9-7.2.
   USE - Method for sulfur-free sugarcane                clarification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is inexpensive, improves sugar                yield at 0.2-0.45%.
