PN CN108318100-A; CN207703293-U
TI System for measuring decomposed releasing gas volume of liquid nitrogen freezing hydrate sample, has temperature measuring device are utilized for detecting sample chamber pressure, and air inlet passage connected with sealing device.
AU CHENG S
   JI C
   PANG Y
   FU X
   DENG J
AE GUANGZHOU MARINE GEOLOGICAL SURVEY (CGSU-C)
GA 2018600125
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a sealing device provided with a sample cabin for storing natural gas hydrate sample and dissolving liquid solution of carbon dioxide gas in the sealing device. The sample cabin and a liquid chamber are provided with a gas-liquid passage. The sample cabin is provided with an air inlet passage and an exhaust passage. The sample cabin is utilized for detecting methane content of a methane detector. A pressure measuring device and a temperature measuring device are utilized for detecting sample chamber pressure and temperature. The air inlet passage is externally connected with the sealing device.

The gas used in the gas cluster ion beam is argon, nitrogen, oxygen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitric oxide, nitrogen dioxide, dinitrogen monoxide, azane, hydrogen, helium, neon, xenon, carbon tetrafluoride, sulfur hexafluoride, nitrogen trifluoride, trifluoromethane, difluoromethane or methane. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The natural gas metal                  cutting gas comprises 1.5 pts. wt. natural gas                  combustion-supporting additive and 98.5 pts. wt.                  liquefied natural gas.Preferred Method: The method comprises (i)                  injecting the natural gas combustion-supporting                  additive into LNG tank containing liquefied                  natural gas, the two are dissolving and placing                  for 10-15 hours to obtain liquid-phase natural                  gas metal cutting gas (ii) converting liquefied                  natural gas into compressed natural gas,                  liquid-phase natural gas metal cutting gas is                  converted into gas-phase natural gas metal                  cutting gas and (iii) supplying the natural gas                  metal cutting gas to the station by the LNG                  low-temperature steel bottle.

AU PAREZ K
   DODDS D
   ELBL P M
AE GALY CO (GALY-Non-standard)
GA 2022935932
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing cotton fiber involves                modifying cotton cells comprising at least one                differently expressed gene, where the differently                expressed gene affects growth of the modified                cotton cells and/or improves and/or modulates                cotton fiber development, culturing the modified                cotton cells in vitro and harvesting cotton fiber                from the cultured cells.
   USE - The method is used for producing cotton fiber                using cotton cells in vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is reliable, energy-efficient, and                eco-friendly alternative to traditional                agriculture, produces greater than or equal to 77%                less water, 80% less land and greater than or equal                to 84% less carbon dioxide emissions than                traditional in planta methods, produces cotton                fiber much faster in greater than or equal to 45                days than traditional method requiring 5-6 months                from planting to harvest, and do not require the                growth of entire cotton plants for modifying cotton                cells. The method reduce propensity for failed,                mistimed, or excess crops, if not completely,                eliminated and allows quick and efficient                cultivation of cotton fiber in a controlled                environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The modifying involves                  introducing the transgene and promoter via                  transformation. In the method, prior to                  modifying, the cotton cells are obtained or are                  the progeny of cotton cells obtained from at                  least one cotton plant. The modifying involves                  contacting the cotton cell with at least one                  mutagen, where the mutagen comprises an                  endonuclease, a chemical mutagen, and/or                  radiation. Preferred Components: The cotton fiber                  is harvested directly from the cultured cells.                  The cotton fiber is harvested from the cells                  without growing a cotton plant. The differently                  expressed gene is a gene selected for improvement                  and/or modulation of cotton fiber development or                  a developmental regulatory gene. The differently                  expressed gene is a transgene. The transgene is                  operably connected to a promoter. The promoter is                  selected from a D113 promoter, a GbPDF1 promoter,                  a GhMYB109 promoter, a Stunt7 (S7) promoter, a                  floral binding protein 7 (FBP7) promoter, a TA29                  promoter, and a CaMV 35S promoter, a cotton                  fiber-specific GhSCFP promoter, an actin                  promoter, such a GhACT1 promoter, a petunia MADS                  box gene floral binding protein 7 (FBP7)                  promoter. The differently expressed gene is                  expressed at a higher level compared with a                  cotton cell prior to the modifying. The                  differently expressed gene is a developmental                  regulatory gene. The differently expressed gene                  decreases doubling time of the cells during the                  culturing. The gene is selected from maize                  WUSCHEL gene Wuschel2 (Wus2), an Arabidopsis                  WUSCHEL gene (AtWUS), a cotton WUSCHEL gene                  (GhWus), a maize BABY BOOM gene (Bbm). The                  differently expressed gene is a gene selected for                  improvement and/or modulation of cotton fiber                  development. The differently expressed gene                  increases cotton fiber yield, increases cotton                  fiber length, and/or increases cotton fiber                  strength. The differently expressed gene is                  selected from a homeodomain-leucine zipper                  transcription factor GhHOX3 gene, a putative                  homeodomain leucine zipper transcription factor                  GhHD-1 gene, an SBP gene family of transcription                  factors gene, a SQUASOMA promoter-binding                  protein-like transcription factor 5 (SPL5) gene,                  a GL1-like MYB protein encoding gene GaMYB2, a                  GhACT gene, a GhACT1 gene, an indole-3 -acetic                  acid (IAA) biosynthetic gene iaaM, an auxin                  efflux protein gene GhPIN3, a cotton LIM protein                  gene GhWlim5, a basic helix-loop-helix protein                  encoding gene GhFP1, a cotton sucrose synthase                  gene (GhSus), a cotton protodermal factor-1                  (GbPDF1) gene1, a cotton fiber specific RDR3 MYB                  gene GhMYB109 and a sucrose-synthesis related                  gene. The modified cotton cells are not                  transgenic. The differently expressed gene is                  endogenous to the modified cotton cells. The                  cotton cells are obtained or are the progeny of                  cotton cells obtained from cotton plant or                  explant apical meristems, cotyledons, young                  leaves, hypocotyls, ovules, stems, mature leaves,                  flower, flower stalks, floral whorls, roots,                  bulbs, germinated seeds, somatic and zygotic                  embryo, and/or cambial meristematic cells                  (CMC).

PN CN114162840-A
TI Method for preferentially extracting lithium from expired ternary lithium battery material involves mixing expired ternary lithium battery material and ternary acid leaching residue, reducing, carbonizing, filtering, heating carbonized liquid, separating and drying to obtain lithium carbonate.
AU WU S
   PENG L
   HUANG B
   PENG A
   CAI D
   WU Q
   JIANG T
   ZHANG X
   WANG C
   LI L
AE JIANGXI GANFENG RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202242137L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preferentially extracting lithium from expired ternary lithium battery material involves mixing expired ternary lithium battery material with ternary acid leaching residue, adding magnesium chloride, stirring to obtain mixture, reducing and roasting the mixture, crushing and sieving to obtain screening material, adding screening material and pure water into the carbonization kettle for slurry mixing, feeding carbon dioxide to carry out carbonization reaction, filtering and separating to obtain carbonized liquid and nickel-cobalt-manganese slag, heating the carbonized liquid to separate out lithium carbonate, and returning separated mother liquor to the carbonization reaction, centrifuging the precipitated lithium carbonate for separation, purifying, and drying to obtain battery-grade lithium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preferentially extracting lithium from expired ternary lithium battery material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly and recycles the ternary acid leaching residue and uses magnesium chloride to recycle the lithium in the slag and produces battery-grade lithium carbonate with high lithium recovery rate and low recycling cost, and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The ternary acid leaching residue is slag produced by existing wet reduction acid leaching. The slag comprises graphite powder and electrolyte lithium hexafluorophosphate decomposition product lithium fluoride.Preferred Composition: The slag comprises 0.1-0.3 wt.% lithium. The mass ratio of added ternary acid leaching residue to the expired ternary lithium battery material is 0.8-15:1. The ratio of magnesium chloride to lithium in the ternary acid leaching residue is 7-10:1. The mass ratio of the screening material to the water is 1:3-5.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E11-A01; E33-G; X16-B01F1
IP C01D-015/08

PN US2022055113-A1
TI Producing sintered material, comprises contacting at least one microparticle of material with binder to form first mixture, and exposing microparticle to energy source under conditions sufficient to cause sintering of mixture.
AU TAI K Y
   JIAN C
   FERRALIS N
   ZANG X
   GROSSMAN J
AE MASSACHUSETTS TECHNOLOGY INST (MASI-C)
GA 202227599Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a sintered material, comprises: contacting at least one microparticle of a material with a binder to form a first mixture; and exposing the microparticle to an energy source under conditions sufficient to cause sintering of the mixture to form a sintered material. The energy source is a carbon dioxide laser. The material comprises at least one of aluminum, cobalt, nickel, molybdenum or tungsten. The binder includes at least one of tar, steam cracker tar, mesophase pitch, or asphaltene.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sintered material e.g. refractory metals including molybdenum and tungsten carbide used in machine tools and armor-piercing shells and aeronautical engines including nozzles and turbine blades.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing a controller that includes a feedback loop that is configured to minimize errors pertaining to mixture that is ejected from the nozzles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system (100) for printing in three dimensions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows three-dimensional printing system set-up for additive manufacturing.
